My boyfriend told me to call and ask, days later, if they found it in the parking lot... and they had it in their lost and found! I'm so grateful. It just goes to show they really take care of this location.

this starbucks sucks. i was in the drive threw line for what feels like forever when only like 4 cars are in front of me. i do not know what takes so long but i've never been in a slower starbucks line in my life

Great NOLA-inspired interior with trumpet-lamps and whatnot (1 star). Coffee is good as expected (1 star). But don't try to talk or work here, they love to play ear-battering punkrock music at noon (They dimmed it a little bit after we asked twice).

Compared to others this one misses the mark. The brand is supposed to be consistent and nothing could be further from the truth. Do yourself a favor and go 1 mile east on Watson Rd to the one in Crestwood. This matches what can be found through the country. No comparison! Traveled 150k air miles last year and have been to hundreds of locations. Sunset Hills is the bottom of the barrel.

I can always count on Starbucks when the little shops let me down!   I changed my mind several times, even gave back a drink, and the staff worked with a smile the entire time!  Not only were they nice, but they were FAST!  The last thing I want is a SLOW coffee shop.

This Starbucks is minutes from my place and although that sounds nice..it's really a dangerous thing. Haha. Service here is consistently good and eventually the baristas will get to know your order. Only had one or two drinks here that weren't great but that is the barista making them and not a reflection of the location overall. Not a whole lot of parking here, but it is very convenient for those on the lrt.
Standard Starbucks. Has a drive thru that moves quickly, free wifi and outdoor patio. The drive thru line gets quite long in the morning rush.

I reaaaaally love this place!! I've been to dozens of Starbucks in my life and the service at this one is my all time favorite. They offer me new suggestions of drinks I never even though of on the regular, and they make my reg. lattes so yum yum yummy! The outside space is especially really nice in spring and summer to have a little date or just get some fresh air. Also, love the free wifi ;-)

I love the Starbucks in my neighbourhood. The are super friendly and the store is always clean. Coffee is great, you cam veganize your drink which is awesome. The only bad thing here is the lack on vegan food. They only offer a bagel with avocado spread (which is way too spicy) and a platter they never ever have. I hope they realize this and get More savoury stuff for all the vegans in town.

Glad there is finally a Starbucks close to me! (My only reason to go to these outlets.) Typical Starbucks. Have been here once a week during the summer months. They have always had my order - iced mocha - and never had a long wait. I recommend the cake pops here. I think it's an unappreciated sweet here that I can never seem to pass up!

We pulled in the drive-thru line and the line was not moving for a solid 5 minute. We parked and went in but waited 15 minutes for our drinks and 7 minutes more for simple egg bites. The people were very nice but awful service, and the cold foam on my drink was not made properly.

I'm not a fan of Starbucks. However... From what I had it was pretty good! I ate a cake pop and it was one of the best I've ever had! The reason for a 4 star is that the coffee smell was so strong I wanted to get out of there immediately!

Stopped in today around 530/6 and there was a deaf family ordering lots of frapps and goodies. One guy was writing down all his questions and drink orders and she was writing back the answers to his questions. The barista was SO patient and undeterred and totally not flustered by the growing line, it was great.  With Sbux new push to make their stores accessible by all, this was a great example. I was really, really, REALLY impressed. You go girl. AND, this store is always great with my stupid drink. :) Love it!

The customer service is on point and I was here at 7 am! I made the mistake ordering a super sweet drink whilst I'm an espresso or Summer frap drinker.  I called Gina and told her I made the mistake (the drink was still great to anyone else.) she told me to come in and get some thing new.  a Ashley the barista was concerned and cared.  100 percent and when it could be busy, they all took their time.  Thank you.

If certain people are working, this is a pleasant and quick experience. When other, certain people are working, the line gets lengthy and you will repeat your order more than once. Not the end of the world, but you may want to give yourself some room on time at this locale.

Worst customer service and cleanliness in a Starbucks even compared to busy ones in New York and San Francisco. No one acknowledges your existence, there is no friendly presence, and it seemed like there was no leader or manager in duty to give direction. A mess.
Little nosey to do work, not alot of places to sit.

Drinks are decent, but expensive. Not many outlets to plug in phone/tablet/laptop chargers. Also, they ALWAYS have the air conditioning on full blast in there. It's way too cold. I can't stay there longer than a few minutes unless I have a jacket on.

This is by far the best Starbucks experience I've ever had. The new manager Emma is really turning things around. She is so nice and friendly and made my friends and I amazing drinks based on the request to 'be awake enough to go out tonight'. She is a genius!!! The drinks she made us were amazing and totally gave me the boost of energy I needed. What a wonderful Starbucks staff. So rare to see such great customer service these days! Definitely returning and I hope Emma is working. I would recommend this Starbucks over any other coffee shops (or Starbucks) in the area!! Ask for Emma.

I'm new to the area and I've been craving my starbucks! Little did I know it's literally 2 mini from me!!!  I stopped by and got my usual caramel macchiato with an extra shot and it's like heaven in my mouth!!!!! Super fast service and awesome employees!!! I definitely will be back!

Excellent service!  I love when I don't have to wait more than 10 min. . It's an enjoyable experience when the staff is  working hard and engaging with the customer. .  This Starbucks is consistent and I  go out of me way to get to this location.
Starbucks is awesome. I love the food. And drink. They have all the new stuff. I wish there was a more local feel but I understand that's hard.
Starbucks Haiku:  Burnt beans yet none care Restroom code is 12-60 Guy always hogs chair

Always enjoy a treat from Starbucks although the prices are getting out of hand- I actually had a reward on my card and it was only good for 3 days- so people watch your free rewards because I honestly think they put them out for so little time they hope you don't see it and it would be lost!

I like this Starbucks for a couple of reasons - they never get my drink wrong (and they always spell my name right), they have a drive through when I am too lazy to get out of the car, the staff is always super friendly.  The only negative about it is the parking sitch... which gets worse if there are a ton of cars in the drive through.  But they are pretty darn efficient at this location, so it is not that much of a negative for me.

Currently on minute 25 of waiting in line at this place. This has to be the worst Starbucks in the history of the world.   We finally made it to the window and one of our drinks is wrong and they're out of lemon cake, blueberry scones and basically everything on the menu. Only come here if you want to make yourself late for something.

I agree with the other reviewers...not very friendly...no friendly greeting. My girl and I had coffees (although they filled hers halfway and then said they were out of the blonde roast (??). And we had no idea our hot breakfast sandwich was made. They let it sit on their counter without telling us. And no comfortable chairs...not the nice leather ones like the others, despite the space here.

I came to the Wayne store on a Sunday afternoon. I ordered a chili mocha Frappuccino, it was wonderful! I came back the following Friday and ordered the same thing in a larger size. The first mistake was that they made it hot. So I had her remake my frozen cappuccino, hence the term Frappuccino!! She proceeded to dump the contents over ice and throw some whipped cream on top. I paid almost $6 for that fucking drink. I am extremely disappointed. If I wanted cold coffee over ice cubes I could have done that at home myself for a lot less. I know that is not how it is done in that store. Big thumbs down

I've been here three times in the last 24 hours.  Two of the three times they got my drink wrong.  One drink they had to make three times. Each visit I had to wait a crazy amount of time.  I'm going to look for another location.
Great Starbucks! Super fast baristas and my drink is always right! The only drawback is no drivethru and sometimes parking is a little crowded.

Love this place... I hate the limited seating though. Line moves quickly but you always have to take it to go because many seniors come and take over the place and stay forever. I can't complain about my drinks though, always on point

The place was clean, good coffee, but... Service awful, cashier made fun about my nationality he though I don't speak English or I don't understand, bad experience, just not comments, I hope all the franchise are like that

This is my favorite Starbucks!!!! Sandy is awesome! She came up with the Birthday gift idea "gift card in a Starbucks cup" Looooove it! Bought it for my employees every year; plus she always takes good care of her customer. Sandy is the best!

I don't make reviews often but I had to today with the service I had. Was my second time going to this specific location and it was horrible service each time. They got my order wrong today and the girl acted like it was a hassle and my fault when it was clearly her fault. She gave me a hot coffee when I had ordered an iced coffee. I order this at least once daily and this did not taste right. It was so sloppy when she handed it to me, it had caramel all over the sides and it  wasn't even filled up all the way like she had poured the messed up hot coffee into some ice to "fix" the problem. On top of being rude and then getting a coffee tasting like shit I was very unhappy customer today and will never go to this location again! Usually customer service is great at Starbucks but not this one. I think they need some new employees. All my coffee junkies know how disappointing it is to get a crappy tasting coffee when you are craving your favorite Starbucks coffee. :(   - very unhappy customer

Still running slow at drive thru...people are friendly and seem to be trying to give good service, but it just takes forever to get thru the drive up. I also wish there were some big improvement in the breakfast sandwich offerings...it sounds great to offer a bacon, cheese, egg sandwich, but, when the roll is funky, semi stale tasting, the egg is a pre cooked, maybe frozen disc...yuk!  Bacon was ok on taste, terribly undercooked and not crisp at all.  Combination just totally bad and at the price, really, really bad.

Every single visit I've made here since it opened (which have been several), has been fantastic.  The drive-through is speedy, orders have been accurate, and the best part is the friendliness of the staff.  They actually SMILE when they talk to you and seem pleased to be there, a very welcome change from the Robb Dr. location just down the road.  Hands-down, this is my new go-to morning coffee stop on my way to work in NW Reno.

While the service is pleasant, especially for the high volume of customers, there is a severe shortage of products, even in the crucial morning hours. If you have a specific drink you're craving, you may do better going down the road to a standalone Starbucks.

Please make the "no smoking signs" bigger, and more visible during the night. Right now the signs are too small and not visible at all. At the streets, in the parking, please make those arrows more visible to indicate right traffic way.

One thing Starbucks does well is brand recognition. This was my first time to this location and it looks like all the others so I felt like I had been there a million times before. This can be seen as a positive and a negative - it is great because you are familiar with the layout and the menus etc but not so great because there is no individuality, uniqueness, or more personal feel to the place.  The drinks and treats are exactly what is expected from a Starbucks location (any Starbucks location). What separates the good from the bad is location and service. Unfortunately this particular Starbucks fails on both accounts.  I went at 4:30pm on a Saturday afternoon and the place was not busy however finding a parking spot and negotiating the parking lot was a feat all on its own. Unfortunately there is a T&T Supermarket directly across the parking lot and all of these customers were consuming the parking and walking haphazardly in the paved lot. This made it a unique challenge even to get in the door.  Once I had made it in I was able to walk right up to the counter and attempt to order. The baristas were all busy cleaning and so it took a while to get their attention but eventually I placed my order. The friend I was meeting had arrived before me so instead of waiting at the counter for my drink I sat with her and chatted for a while. Next thing I realize that ten minutes had gone by and my drink was still not prepared so back up to the counter I went. Again all of the baristas were cleaning machines and organizing items behind the counter - they all had forgotten about my drink. Luckily I was not in a rush because from the time I walked in until I received my beverage was over 15 minutes.   There are two other Starbucks' at the intersection of 97th Street and 137th Ave and (without being to either) I would recommend trying both of them over this location.

Out of most of the Starbucks I go to in the Brandon/Tampa area this is one of my favorites. EVERYTIME I come here the staff is very friendly. And they always get my drink right, especially when I order DECAF. This is very important to me because I cannot handle caffeine. So why drink it then, you say? Because, I love the taste of coffee!  The store is hardly crowded when I come in and they are pretty quick to make your drinks.
Has always been my fave of the many I frequent to,study, work or just unwind. The staff there are so friendly and entertaining. :)

Starbucks!  Need I say more?  Well, maybe!  Pumpkin Spiced Latte, Iced Caramel Macchiato, Bacon & Gouda breakfast sandwich... Mmm... I'm already drooling.  But really, this is a great location with ample seating and inside Indigo Bookstore which gives it a great vibe for sitting back and relaxing with a cup 'o joe over a good book!  Be careful about the parking as another reviewer noted!  2 hour limit - keep your receipt to prove you were actually there!  And the North Town Parking Lot SUCKS - sorry, but no bones about it.  The worst parking lot in the city by a mile!  Downside to an Indigo Starbucks?  No drive-thru, but there is another one just down the road if you need the drive-thru.  Coffee on!  PS - Don't forget to download the SB app to your phone to earn free drinks and discounts!!!

This is a pretty nice Starbucks. The staff are friendly, the drinks are made well, and there is a seating area upstairs that's away from the noise of the espresso machine and endless small talk, so it's a great place to study.

Parking is MEH, but besides that it has a drive through that is normally very quick, workers are always chipper and fun to deal with and the coffee lives up to Starbucks standards.  Will be here often!

I don't go to Starbucks often because it's so ridiculously expensive. This location is nice, clean, and the staff were friendly. There was a wait both inside and even longer at the drive thru. To be honest the wait wasnt as bad as it usually is at Starbucks. I am sure i will be at starbucks again every once in a while since i do enjoy their iced mocha frappachino but not regularly since i cant spend $4 on a coffee all the time.

Came to this location for the first time today. Manger was awesome and super helpful getting my app and gift card balance to transfer. The staff were also very friendly and the coffee was perfectly made. The inside was awesome as well with a cozy fireplace to warm up next to. I will definitely make this a weekly spot.
2 stars for coffee.. sometimes it's very weak and flaw. 5 stars for customer service. Polite & friendly  baristas.

This is the most inefficient and just plain awful Starbucks I have ever been to. They've never gotten my order correct and all it is only an iced vanilla coffee with heavy cream. I went there once and I had to go back in the store THREE different times and I still left with a drink that didn't taste how it normally does from all other locations. I only go here when it is convenient as far as location. Don't even consider the drive thru...unless you're prepared to wait thirty minutes for your incorrect order.

My brother and I went here and our coffee was just okay. I had better a different locations. It was almost empty inside but the guy making our coffees was taking a very long time. Then a lady he was friends with came in and he took even longer to make them. He forgot to put the vanilla syrup in one coffee but corrected this. He also almost spilt the second one. We probably won't be returning to this location.

It's interesting to see some rough reviews on here but I just went for the first time and enjoyed it! The woman working the register was very friendly in an auntie or mom kind of way, saying "you're welcome, baby" and stuff. That made me feel a bit more at home. I ordered the Sumatra but they ran out halfway through the the cup. She asked if I wanted to get topped off with something else and I said the Veranda, a bit sad since I couldn't get a full cup of what I wanted. Then she asked if I just wanted all Veranda, I said yes and she dumped out that Sumatra and told me that the next time I come she'll give me a free cup. So, yay!

Love his location. The staff is amazing. Love Trey and his staff!! They are amazing at remembering your order after just a few visits and are so happy to see you. The other locations around can at times feel like they are too busy to notice you. Not here. They may not always remember your name but your drink and size of cup... spot on!! Go team. You keep it up.

came into starbucks with my girlfriend. A bigger young women with purple lipstick and glasses was extremely rude to her coworkers. made our drink the wrong size and was disrespectful to us when we asked her to change it.  wont ever be coming back again.
This Starbucks is very inconsistent between shifts! The early morning barista gets it right every time. Weekends and afternoons are always a chance to what the flavor profile will be
This Starbucks was ok. Small, cramped and people lounge everywhere so there was no where to sit. The staff seemed gossipy and distracted. Not my favorite one! I will stop here on an as needed basis.

I enjoy going to any Starbucks.  The atmosphere is casual and relaxing.  My only complaints are,  Starbucks doesn't serve any organic coffee, and when I come in I try to take a bistro box home for later but they never seem to have what I want.  I don't know if it's because they don't order enough or if they actually sell out before I get there.  I usually go around 3 in the afternoon.
No drive-thru available and limited parking.  Staff is friendly and location is well kept.

This stores is very inconsistent.  I purchased the clear double-insulated cup that last year ice tea refills were always 50 cents each.  This year, this specific store will charge 50 cents for ice tea refills one day and then the next they try to charge full price less 10 cents.  They need to come up with a store policy and stick to it.  Ironically, they actually sell the clear double-insulated cups at this location, so it's not like they are unaware of the cup and the concept behind it.    In the future, I'll visit the many other locations around Tucson, which are much more customer service friendly and consistent.

Honestly the worst service ever. Does it make sense to take someone else's order when they're behind you? I dont care if they are a regular at that location, its first come first serve. Not first serve regulars then others.

My first time at this Starbucks.  No big complaints, but the staff seemed like they were more concerned with talking about what they did lat night then paying attention to the customers.  They were not ride, but certainly not paying attention.

My go to every morning every here knows what I get ..... Excellent service here ...... But Dixie needs to stop talking so much sometimes holding a 5-10 conversation.. people people like me gots places to be and things to do

Griffin Quay is by far the BEST employee there. He is so kind and made my experience beyond enjoyable. Even with a big rush, he made sure that every customer was catered to and taken care of. He was so sweet and you could tell he loved his job. No surprise, he got employee of the quarter- he rocks! Hope this Starbucks gives him a raise because he is running the show! Such a great barista, if you ever go here make sure you get your drink made by Griffin. He makes it right every single time, best barista ever!

Joel thinks he is on a pedastool. He believes he is better than others, and can disrespect anyone. He is a piece of ugly shit that thinks he can kick people out upon sitting down, and force people to buy immediately. He is a donkey that believes he can rush people, and time their presence in the establishment before ordering. I pissed all on their entry door due to a lack of respect.

Feel like I'm in a homeless shelter.  Was quite disruptive to hear someone mumbling and see him staring at me. There was another homeless man too although not disturbing anyone. Staff does not clean up after people leaving tables dirty.

My home away from home. I love you Starbucks, let me count the ways!  This place is always hopping, especially in the mornings. It's busy, and usually understaffed. Just come prepared to wait. and then wait some more. The staff is super friendly and the store is clean. Above all else, the COFFEE AND ESPRESSO is YUMMY.  Other than the mall, this is my fave Starbucks around this side of town. Shots are milky-ish and perfect, especially when ordered ON TOP of my iced coffee. YUMM.  I would marry this spot if I could.
If I could give this place 10, I would. I walk past 2 Starbucks just to go to this one. Great staff, great service, fast, reliable. Wish all Starbucks (especially NY) were like this one.

Best Starbucks I live in fishers and will always stop at this one on my way to work. Fast service, drinks are always right and everyone is always so friendly!!!! Probably my favorite Starbucks on the north side!!!
This is one of nicest locations of Starbucks in downtown Philadelphia - nice seating arrangement, two bathrooms, cool background music, free wifi -

Don't come here unless you are using the drive-thru!  I have been here twice in the last 3 days and both times I had to wait over 20 minutes for a toasted bagel!  Terrible in-store service.  Coffee also inconsistent in quality and flavor.  I observed the staff frantically getting orders out to drive-thru customers while ignoring in-store clients.  Bad form , Bux!

Horrible service! Overworked and not happy people working here...Our 2 drink order took 20 minutes.  It was made twice, they attempted to scrape off whip cream on a vanilla bean,  add java chip,  no whip... really.  Please retrain!!
Very busy every time we go by this place.  We stopped in when we saw the drive through line was almost to the street.  The wait on the inside was just as long.  The staff was friendly and helpful.

Up until the last few months, I have LOVED this Starbucks.  I am downgrading to 2 stars based on on consistently poor experiences that are driven by the staff.  Let me say, if you get some of the more 'tenured' Baristas you will get top notch service and drinks, but there are a couple of newer (newer to me) Baristas that seemingly don't know what they are doing.  If I am paying a chunk of change for a specialty drink with extra shots -- have the courtesy to pay attention and make it correctly.  The last four visits, one Barista in particular doesn't pay attention, leaves out shots, pumps the wrong syrup, gives me whole milk instead of NF... the list goes on.  I started actually watching him from the side after the first 2 issues I had with my drinks... he gets it wrong every.single.time.  When I see him working, I feel as though someone in this cosmic world hates me.  I have written two letters thus far to customer service... hopefully they will get this guy some training.

This is my preferred Starbucks to go to as the staff is always super friendly and polite. They have never made me feel like I needed to feel honoured to have them acknowledge me, which is a common trend I find at most of the chain locations.   I do not drink coffee, I am not a Starbucks addict and I just am not a frequent customer by any means (with my lack of coffee drinking) but I have recently discovered a drink I love! A vanilla frappucino with raspberry syrup and whipped. I order it when my Starbucks junkie boyfriend drags me along to satisfy his withdrawals.   I know very little of the lingo and I order small, medium or large, and the staff do not try to correct me or lecture me on the language of the coffee addict world... and I like that. Most other locations have refused to really help me when they find out I am not one of them. So a big thank you to the wonderful staff that has never shamed me as an outcast!

I like to come here once in a while when I'm getting a massage at the salon next door. Nothing hits you like a double espresso after a massage!  But, today, I arrived and found out they removed all the counter stools because it's healthier to stand. I exercise every day and if I want to sit down, I'm going somewhere where they have a chair for me!   Am I unreasonable!
Standard Starbucks experience! Plenty of seating area both indoors and outdoors. Service was quick and ample parking available.

This is my go-to, neighborhood Starbucks. I'm here a few times a week and love the coffee.   The staff is friendly and they really get the job done.  This has to be one of the busier Starbucks, and it runs like a well oiled machine most days.  The lines are long at peak times, but I think that's to be expected. The employees never stop moving, so the wait times aren't bad.

I come here 3/7 days a week and have NEVER had a bad experience.  If they get my order wrong ( which they never do) they quickly correct it.  If they feel I have waited in the drive-thru too long, I get a free coffee.  Talk about being pro-active rather than re-active.  This is the way every business should be run.

On my way out of Target and wanted to try a new fall flavor brew. Selected a salted caramel mocha,hot. It was quiet so I was waited on promptly by two very personable and friendly barristas. The drink was great, mocha with a hint of salt and caramel. I think non chocolate lovers would even enjoy this.

Horrible customer service!! Literally stood there for a minute while 5 employees all talked to each other and the cashier lady just stared right through me! I wasn't sure wether to order or leave. Then she finally came back to earth and asked me if i wanted to order. Pretty sure that's why I'm standing there?! Got my drink and the barista made zero eye contact and didn't thank me. As a past Starbucks employee myself, they didn't do anything to standard and was the worst customer service I've encountered in a long time! Will not be back!
Been here twice, and both times, the wait was extremely long.  The employees are very friendly, but there is definitely not enough of them at this location.
All I have to say is nicest barista EVER at the drive-thru window! Excellent service and the drinks were great.
Close to home but they close too early, I think this location will not remain open much longer because of lack of business. I hope I'm wrong because it's one of my favorite locations!
Friendly staff. Great coffee. Recently renovated which gave this location a bit more seating. Still find  it hard to find a seat when it's busy.

Its Starbucks. This is the only Starbucks for miles around this area so they are often vert busy. Secondly, the service is slow. Never order a frappacino at the drive through...itll defiantly be melted by the time you get it. The service is nice however, slow slow slow. They need another Starbucks in the area to help with these issues.
The employees are friendly but they need to move faster. They work to slow which makes long lines. You also need a code to get in the bathroom which makes another line for the bathroom.
This place is easy to get to in center city. The staffs are friendly and drinks are well made. I was able to get in and out quickly, not bad!

This Starbucks is the closest to me, but I don't frequent it to often since it's in the opposite direction of my work. When I do visit, I always go through the drive through. It can get congested but goes quickly. I'm not a fan of Starbucks coffee. I do love the black tea here though, which I always get. I also get the butter croissant at times, which is delicious!

It's a poorly designed (drive thru) and run Starbucks. Because of this they are constantly behind on orders and routinely taken 15-20 minutes per order. They need a new location to cut down on the drive thru bottleneck.
Slowest starbucks in the United States! Least proactive staff, unfortunately only coffee at terminal
I go to the Ridgemoor Starbucks quite often on the way to work and am always impressed with the fast service and friendly faces.
Super sweet employees that are helpful. I just started using the mobile order application and it's so fast and simple. Love this place

The best Starbucks I know. The batistas here are the best. I've asked them to just make something up for me several times and each time they've given me something that I thoroughly enjoyed. So I trust them enough to just pay and let them surprise me. Not to mention the energetic and friendly vibes they're always radiating. They'll also always make sure that they get your order right. If you don't like it, they're more than happy to redo it to your liking. And this isn't some corporately enforced attitude. They are genuinely willing to redo your order and make it right.   The store itself is setup well. It's nice and cozy in the corner and also has good spots to just study or to do work. People are always here chillin. The store is always kept clean. Almost every time I come in, someone is cleaning some part of the store. Out of all the Starbucks that I've been too, this is definitely the most well run. It'll continue to be the one I'll always go to.

Not my favorite location, but not the worst that I have been in. Stopped in on a Friday afternoon before heading up to the Northside for an afternoon of shopping and doing pretty much whatever we wanted. They were busy when we arrived. Normally I would have stuck with the drive-thru, but for some reason decided to go in. The inside as busy just like the drive thru. When we got into line, we had about 4 people a head of us.  Surprisingly for as busy as they were, they got us through pretty quickly. They all seemed to work well together to get people through and on their way.  Some of the furniture looked a little beat up like perhaps it may need to be replaced soon. Other than that the overall location seemed to be clean with several places to sit. Perhaps this was because everyone was sitting outside, but still...lots of places to sit.

When I'm up north, this is my favorite Starbucks. It's a little neighborhood joint, so you don't have anything too loud, too obnoxious or too much in general. Great meeting place, great working place. It has somehow even missed that after school hang out from all the kids.   The employees here always seem to remember me, even though I keep changing my drink order on them. It's just a nice, friendly, neighborhood coffee shop.

I love Stabucks but this is my least favorite location in South Tampa.  It is one of the dirtiest Stabucks I've ever been at.  I've been here a few times but just strictly for location.  I don't ever linger since it's so dirty.  If you want to sit for awhile go to the one on Westshore.  The last time I was here there were 2 employees behind the counter and neither knew how to make my tea latte.  One was new so I'll give him a break.  Then another employee was out sweeping (surprising!) and yelling to them how to make my drink when there was a line of people.  Why not get behind the counter while its slammed and then sweep?

Ok, as a long time Starbucks fan, I am a bit bias. Boise is where I began my addiction to coffee, and this "open late" Starbucks definitely had a hand. The baristas here are nice, and this location has a drive thru. Lately they re-decorated for BSU studiers, with more plugs and lone seats. I personally miss the comfy chairs, but am happy it is open late.

Unlike Los Angeles, there aren't too many Starbucks here in New Orleans. I'm glad to say that this has been my go to coffee place in the mornings for this whole week while I was here in NOLA.   Yes, I bypassed the other hipster coffee house inside the lobby of this high rise (sorry) as much as I wanted to try it.  I just didn't have the time every morning as I was in a rush. And convenience is the major contributor.  The coffee here is very consistent as it's not water compared to other Sbux's in California to not be mentioned geographically. My bagels were delicious, my cinnamon buns was fresh, my bananas were decent, however the egg muffin wasn't as good as I thought it would be. I'll stay with the golden arches for that item.  Anyhow, I'm still happy about this Sbux as it was convenient for my walk to the convention center every morning. It's never too crowded, and the employees are also very friendly.  People basically come and go at this location without really hanging around too long. Nobody smokes outside which is a major plus.   I wish a Sbux similar to this location was around my neighborhood, that's never too crowded.

There are over 1200 cafés in Canada sporting the twin-tailed siren, but none are staffed with such friendly, authentic, and professional staff as this location. Most Starbucks do a decent job of hiring good employees, but someone did an exceptionally good job here. I visit this location a few times a week to read and write; after a couple weeks most of the staff took the time to learn my name!  This location is very busy during the day with office workers taking beverages to go and students studying at tables. Patrons come in waves, so if you can't find a spot, just wait five minutes. In the evenings the students are still there, but it seems to also be a popular place for "we haven't talked in a while, lets grab a coffee" meetings. Being so busy it's not quite as tidy as the Starbucks at The Matrix hotel (only several blocks away), but it is maintained quite well.  The space is quite large with many tables with power plug access. Unlike the internet at The Matrix location which can be spotty, the wifi here is quite reliable. There are also large east and north facing windows for natural light.  Other than that, the beverage and food quality is exactly what you would expect from a Starbucks. I leave it to your own preference to judge that!

The beverages are standard and many of the baristas do their job well and algae great customer service. however it's quite irritating to hear all the employees boisterous goofing around constantly. Also I thought store cleanup sweeping and mopping was to be done after hours, not at a quarter to nine when the shop is still full of customers. I understand the need for cleanliness often but it's not looking like it's helping the infestation of fruit flies.
Great people work here . Plus come on great Coffees . I love the old time feel of this place . If it was easier to get to I would give it 5 stars
My favorite place in Hendersonville, the hand crafted drinks are always perfect, very friendly staff

This is a great location in the Boise Towne Square mall. I almost always order ahead via the app, and from the time it takes me to walk from my car to the store, my order is always ready and waiting for me. This is a busy location, the line gets long (like, outside the store long), so ORDER AHEAD and save some time! I've also had some great interactions with employees, who have filled me in on specials and just chatted me up. Overall, this is an excellent location with consistently good drinks and service.

My fiancé and I visit this location every time we visit my in-laws. Over the past 3 years the service has never changed. There are usually 5-6 employees working but there is no sense of urgency. It takes 3-4 times as log to get our drinks than the Starbucks standard. The employees are all over the place and do not seem to have any specific assigned station to keep the store running smoothly. If there was another Starbucks closer, I would be more than happy to avoid this one and drive a little further for better service.

It's always busy but there's lots of comfortable seating the baristas do the best they can but it's the only coffee shop in the busy Grand Sierra casino. It's a great place to work because of all the comfortable seating and you get to watch people play slots.
Great place with amazing coffee! Great service and they always have a long line but get it moving fast. Plus, it's totally worth it to stand in line for 3 mins for great coffee and lattes.

I love starbucks. and I don't drink coffee. at least not straight. that's where starbucks won me over, they create these delicious fraps and teas that are loaded with enough calories for a meal!!!! but I validate it with its coffee...this location has super friendly employees and is rather quick. they lose two stars simply bc whoever thought of this location was going for drive thru only, its not a location one can really sit at and enjoy, weird set up

Barista crew is efficient and polite. If you come here during the day, expect tables to be taken up by McEwan students and business meetings. Not a lot of seating, but a nice space none-the-less. Large windows let in a lot of natural light, which makes this location welcoming.

i absolutely LOVE this Starbucks compared to others in the area. Its much newer, the crowd is good, WiFi is usually running on decent speed and the Barista make a pretty design on your Latte if you ask them LOL! I just LOVE the outside seating for nice sunny days.

Average to below average Starbucks. Two stars because the bar never has milk (skim or whole) and if you need some you have to ask the Baristas who subtly let you know they don't like you asking for milk in your coffee as it gives them extra work. For some reason, if you ask, you're given a really tiny amount of milk in a separate cup, not the usual carafe used by other Starbucks.
Cleaner tables & cleaner bathrooms then hwy 70 location. This one also has drive thru. Hwy 70 does not. The servers here seem to be on their game better. More attentive.

Unfortunately this location has pissed my family of Starbucks regulars off.   Brownsburg is the busiest Starbucks in the state--not a surprising fact if you know it well.   They have taken advantage of this fact by increasing prices at this location, so all the drinks now cost more than elsewhere.  I appreciate the fact that you can come here on a Sunday and not have your order murdered by the third-string crew as seems to happen everywhere else on the weekends, but come on. Gouging us because we come here all the time!

Why does this place have such a low rating? This place treats me well on weekday mornings. I just mobile order and in less than 5 minutes my food or drink is ready to go! There are times my drink I order is a bit complicated but they make it well anyway. A few times in the past someone has said it was "too many things going on in the drink" which I didn't take as a good thing. You just don't say that. I like my drink the way it is. But otherwise, it is good.

Awful. Waited in line 40 minutes for a TERRIBLE latte. I go to Starbucks 4 times a week or more. All over the place. Very very rarely do I have anything negative to say. This was rough. Long line, new cashier, couldn't figure out the reward even without a manager and a crappy cup of coffee

Starbucks is Starbucks. This store has been pretty decent. MAJOR problem - this location is too small. They parking lot constantly backs up and the drive thru blocks cars in their spots because it routes right through the parking lot. The drinks has always been really good coming out of this location though and the staff has always been really friendly. I hope maybe they expand this store or move it to a bigger location with a better design for a drive thru.

This is probably my favorite Starbucks in the Tucson area. The staff is awesome, the service is fast and the seating is the best. Lots of space for studying and relaxing (I never feel cramped unlike other Starbucks in the area). I would totally recommend this Starbucks!

Great Starbucks. Decent size with some seating. Right in the heart of downtown by Macy's and City Hall. They made a great cinnamon dolce latte, but my favorite thing is that they said my name right when they said my coffee was ready! For someone with a unique name I never wait for my name to be said correctly! I was so excited! Employees were super nice.
It's a Starbucks in kop what do you expect ... I like the one on gateway shopping center but that's me. Love the mobile app,

It's unbelievable. My wife came here 10 min ago and asked for a cup of warm water for baby formula. They said 54 cents. She said I left the wallet with my husband on the first floor and Starbucks is on the third. They said no. I don't know what's wrong with this particular location when the refuse mother with a baby a warm cup of water. We will follow up with a complain.

This  Westshore & Kennedy location right next to Westshore Plaza has small outdoor seating, TWO long tables to study on, 1 desk-like table in the back, multiple small tables for two, multiple multi-plugs, comfy furniture and great music. I was a bit excited they had Motown and some chill classics on. The atmosphere here is inviting and the staff is friendly and servicial.  The Grande Vanilla Late and Venti Unsweetened Passion Tea we ordered were on point. They have many choices for snacks from croissants, cookies, pound cake to greek yogurt, fruit salad, biscotti, almonds, etc... i however went with the Fruit & Nut snack.  If i am ever in Westshore area, i would probably stop here to study.

The one thing I love about Starbucks is that I can know what to expect from their coffee. It was good as usual. I tried their new drink Carmel roasted something and it was really took sweet for me. I should have stuck with a cup of regular joe.

I have been a regular here for about two years now and have come to count on quick and friendly service.  Yes, as others have mentioned the parking lot is small and spaces are rather narrow.  If you drive a truck or large SUV, I imagine maneuvering during rush hour could be kind of a hassle. Typically, I enter the lot from Horatio so I can scout out the best parking.  Many of the baristas have been here for 2+ years but lately there have been a few new faces.  For such a great/busy location it's unfortunate that there is no drive through but that's a limitation of having a Starbucks in a strip mall. As for me, I'll keep coming back for a reliable and quick fix of caffeine as I head off to work.

(4.5) ~ Very Good Overall Food & Service!  ** My wish finally came true...A Starbucks with a "Clover Brewing System" close to home!:-)  Read about the Clover Brewing System online and order your favorite Coffee Bean either Hot -or- Iced.  My Fav is their Aged Sumatra:-)

This coffee shop now deserves 5 stars. There service is absolutely amazing!   Going in for my normal coffee run, I ended up in line behind a deaf person. The staff could not have been more patient or friendly with the customer. At the register, the cashier was very attentitive and signed thank you to him after he paid. The barista, who was making his drink, diligently came over to him and asked him if he wanted room for milk. At first he didn't understand and many people would have just given up. She, however, perservered until he understood her. Witnessing this Starbucks staff be kind and helpful to this man makes me want to give this place 10 stars - which I would if I could.

This is the closest Starbucks to me and it is on my way to work so I have been here multiple times.  I have never gone in as this is a drive-thru location and I am always on my way somewhere.  They are fast, very nice and efficient every time.  I have never had any issues with my service or what I have gotten.  The location can be tricky to get out of just due to the volume of cars at the intersection.  Parking is also a negative, but that has never been an issue for me.  I will continue to get my coffee here!

This Starbucks is conveniently located nearby and has a drive thru yet every time I go there the line is always way too long inside and out and the baristas constantly mess up my drink, and it's not even that complicated. I'm always disappointed and shocked they don't recognize me or my order for how often I go there.
This is the store that banned a customer for life for confronting dbags who were not handicapped but parking in the handicapped spots. Never reward this kind of stupidity.
I like this Starbucks because it has a drive-through which is usually run prety efficiently and quickly!

Best customer service I've ever had at a Starbucks! Doesn't matter what day or what time I go I've always had the best, most friendly employees help me. We go out of our way to come to this store because of that.

Hardly ever a wait here. I just moved to this area and was one day craving my peach green tea. I parked to walk inside which I hate. I was wishing it had a drive through and low and behold it did but the staff said it was hidden and it darn sure was. That's the only flaw of this place.
This is by far the best Starbucks location in all of University City. I am so pleased with the service. The baristas are friendly and prompt. They make their drinks to perfection!
The staff is friendly enough.  Waiting is kept to a minimum and they make a great iced cinnamon dolce latte.
Are there any adults that work here and can help these kids out? 20 minutes for a ice tea and panini... The drink wasn't even correct.   Parking lot is a nightmare.

Update: this is literally the worst Starbucks ever with the absolute most awful staff to exist. I just waited in a 4 person line for 10 minutes watching an employee use his bare hands to maneuver pastries. Save yourself your time and money and go to united by Blue for better coffee and nicer people.
Great sbux, always fast service, although, no perfect rating because twice they haven't had my favorite sandwich feta and spinach wrap in stock, otherwise they're great.

I really enjoy going here, because it is less than a mile away from my house. The service is very good, they are pretty quick about getting orders out, and they have never made a mistake about my order.

Went to this location three days in a row, and the barista knew my order by the third day.  And almost everyone else's in the (very long) line.  Very busy location in the mornings, but understandably so.  Its prime location and delightful service make the delicious coffee served there seem even better.
By far the slowest Starbucks I've ever experienced. Just waited 20 minutes for two cappuccinos, amidst a crowd of other customers waiting. And it seemed to be business as usual.
Ridiculously slow. 1 cashier for 8 people in line and one clerk is doing NOTHING. At least two staff on break just sitting in area beyond shop. They need to respond to the line!

Great outdoor seating area but everything else sucks. Very few places to sit and spread out inside with a wifi that goes up and down all day long. The drinks are good but unless you like to sit in the heat. They don't even have the elevator music which I like to listen to from time to time. This location was very cheaply built and needs improvement.

The weekday morning crew at this store is absolutely the best. They are always friendly and quick, using a style that makes you feel invited and not rushed. They have offered excellent suggestions every time I have asked for ways to tweak any of my favorite drinks.  I pass by 2 other Starbucks on my way into the office because the service at this location is truly the best.

Although they've moved (they used to be next to the Apple Store), they've still kept their quality service intact. Pretty much every time I go here, I get fast and friendly service. I did have an issue once with an unfriendly worker, but I'm going to consider that a one-off.  Definitely gets busy quick, but they do their best to get you in and out with the right drink and a smile!

This coffee shop is by far is the best around! The staff is awesome and super friendly. Thank you Ronni, Abby, Krissy, Brett, Annie and sara and issac. I know dealing with people can be different but you guys to a superb job at keeping your customer's happy! Thanks for your hard work. I appreciate you!!!
I enjoy this location because it is quieter than others further downtown. The staff was friendly, the lines were not too long and they made my drink right!!

Spanking new Starbucks, open less the a week. It has everything you expect from a Starbucks, and that's good... people are friendly and helpful  Coffee is good, cold brew is on tap..... Big store with plenty of seating both inside and outdoors They have a drive thru as well...

Love the coffee but the employees at this location are horrible. Every time I have the misfortune of needing to stop at this location they act like they're doing you a favor by waiting on you. Terrible customer service

This was the last location I will try. Whoever owns the Starbucks on dale mabry I'm sorry but y'all slacking. I've contacted corporate and managers. Nothing works. All I want is pike roast with cream and sugar stirred. Since they can't handle a simple order like that I'll take my business elsewhere. Gold member since 2009. I will never spend another dime at any location.
The bathroom was really dirty. The customer service was poor and the forgot to add some parts of my order.

Nashville is unusual in the number of Starbucks it has WITHOUT a Drive Thru.  Fortunately this one has a Drive Thru, as well as being a nice new location.  We recently stopped in and the better half even ordered one of the recently and popularly discussed unofficial "Rainbow Drinks". The Barista was very kind and she and her manager helped to make "Orange Drink".  It isn't a large Starbucks, but the service is fast and friendly.

Nestled and small, I didn't expect much from this Starbucks. Granted I'm not a big fan but 1) It was raining and I was cold and 2) I had recently broken my large coffee cup and needed a replacement. So while waiting for a prescription at CVS next door I went in for a basic Cafe Mocha. They don't short you on whipped cream either.   The atmosphere is calm,  baristas friendly. Today I'm filling a prescription and plan on grabbing a coffee simply to enjoy the quiet. I would never say that of the other Starbucks locations I've visited.

I kind of hate this Starbucks but also not really?   Pros:  - it is close to my home and also on the way to the highway (65).   Cons: - The interior layout is dreadful. In regards environment, it is just not a cozy space. The Windows face west and north, and let in harsh light that (if you are bringing a laptop) makes it impossible to sit it (but pull the blinds and it's too dark). When sitting with folks you're liable to have sunlight stab you in the eye if you don't face in a weird direction. There's one large long table where there should be multiple small tables pushed together - rarely do large parties sit there so instead a couple of folks on laptops or talking will take up a huge space. There's one good table by the coffee shelves that's ok. They have another corner table that is accessible to folks in a wheelchair.  - omg the parking and drive-thru is terrible. NEVER PARK IN THE DRIVE THRU AREAS. Often the drive-thru gets backed up and then angry jerks in the drive-thru line are too rude to give space to folks backing out so they can leave. Multiple times I have gotten stuck there for a period of time. It's just the dumbest set up for a drive thru. They should have taken the lane that's between them and LA Fitness.  - the people working here are ok. Sometimes I am just not impressed with service and other times they are fine.   Overall, it is a Starbucks. It has stuff you probably want. I go here for convenience because the south side doesn't have a lot of coffee options. Sometimes it is actually nice. But usually I come here and am fairly unhappy, but glad to have coffee on my way to work.

I'm surprised that during a busy time there is only one cashier. The person before me had an item return and it took such a long time. No one said anything to me while I waited and waited. I come here a lot and just expected to be welcomed and not ignored.   Today Austin made my day! He gave me lots of ideas on how to enhance my drink. Nice! I feel like an important customer and will be back soon. My cascara latte with an extra shot is perfect! Thanks Austin!   David took my order and he was just wonderful! He was helpful, friendly and made my customer experience amazing. I'm now giving this location a top rating because of David's efforts! Keep it up David and I hope others follow suit!

I love this Starbucks. The baristas are so nice and it is usually pretty quiet. Though this location is small, there is always somewhere for me to sit. I go on my lunch break a few times a week and it seems like the staff genuinely care about the customers. This location is definitely a wonderful escape from my office!

This is your typical Starbucks. There's nothing special about it.   The location is only a few blocks from where I work and they get me in and out of there quickly. Like every Starbucks, everything is expensive, so to order anything from here is a treat. The customer service could use some work but it isn't too bad. Some of the employees could at least smile when asking for your order. Also, when ordering a pastry, don't expect it to be fresh 100% of the time. I have received a few stale lemon cakes from here.   Again, 3 stars.

I can't dislike a Starbucks with a drive-thru, and so I shan't. My visit consisted entirely of ordering at the drive-thru, and there were many cars and the line was long, but the queue went relatively fast. Good coffee, great iced tea, breakfast sandwiches all day ... yup, this Starbucks is good.

This location usually very busy. Seating is decent. The problem is for some reason the customers here all think they live in Beverly Hills and shouldn't have to wait or suffer the fools behind the counters. The employees here have always treated me well.

Same good Starbucks quality; however, the interior design (layout of tables and chairs) leaves much to be desired. It's hard to believe this was the best that could be done with a place built from scratch. I've been in Starbucks converted from gas stations that had a warmer feel inside.
Turn up the heat! It is winter. Doesn't have to be 80 degrees in here but cutting off the cool air coming from the vent would be a nice touch for patrons.

This location has a small parking lot that on a normal workday morning the drive thru is completely jam up! I prefer to recommend you the Starbucks on Anderson Rd.  - by AMC 24. Due to very narrow direction of car flow it sometime can be HARD to access this location.  Neither less to say it's always busy. Drive thru is quick so I do suggest it unless your lucky enough to find a parking space.
Incredibly rude and incompetent staff! We've been Starbucks members for a very long time and have never experienced such a terrible service. I would highly stay away from this location.

worst experience ever! i would give them zero stars if i could. please be aware (especially people with allergies)! these people at this particular location do not pay attention at all, i came to this location on friday may 20,2016 at 1:37 pm through the drive thru, i always order the same exact thing everytime which is a vanilla bean  frap, with soy milk, one shot, and extra whip. well they ended up giving me whole milk (which i am highly allergic too). this made me extremely sick, i was headed to work and happened to take a sip and soon after was extremely sick, i had to leave my job and go to an urgent care, the district manager has yet to call me. the 1800 starbucks number told me that she would give me a call by this morning.the 1800 starbucks number also tried to just compensate me with a starbucks gift card, how degrading?! i could have lost my life!!!!!! why would i go back?! please beware, i havent even recieved an apology! how tacky?!

Pros: it's a drive-thru Their outside menu is pretty cool Not to many cars were in line @ 10am on a weekday morning!!  Cons: rude employees who like to come into your car with your drinks  They are out of ice coffee((really)) and cookies and protein plates!!!  If your not watching you will miss the driveway to turn into and will have to drive down the street make a u- turn go back down the street make another u-turn then make a quick right!! :p

Stopped by this Starbucks before a trip to the DMV. My order was made perfectly and the girls that worked there were nice. The only problem was the cleanliness. I went later in the day so I understand it being a bit messy but it wouldn't hurt to wipe down your tables and check your bathroom every hour or so.  I did like that this location had ample outside seating, as well as a counter top seating section. Overall the look was very modern. Definitely better than other Starbucks I frequent.

This Starbucks is inside Target, I had gotten a wrong order from another store and was in Target, I mentioned it and Andrew working was incredibly polite and corrected it for me. Would absolutely return to this location. Not to mention it was delicious when I did get what I wanted!

Love Starbucks! I go close to every day. Excellent service, but they are slower during certain times of the day. If they could find the groove like they have in the mornings, they'd have 5 stars from me. Unfortunately, I've experienced the slow service during dinner and later at night and that's when they seem to be the least busy.

Honestly? I try to stay away from here if I can. The store is just too small for the amount of customers who come through. My suggestion is to grab a drink and leave because the seats are way too cramped.

This Starbucks is inside Harrah's which made it convenient for us since we were staying there.  However, our lattes were weak even with extra espresso, so much do that my husband had to go back down to have them remake his as it tasted like cream only.  They did not offer the breakfast selections we are used to at our various local Starbucks.  A near fail for Starbucks.
I ordered a pumpkin spice latte for my mum. The barista was so kind to write MUM on her cup.
The worst latte I've had in a ling time. Looks like just coffee with milk. Tastes ok, but no foam. Ive been here previously and it was better
It is an small place but has the most friendly staff!! Frapuccinos, lattes, coffees, and delicious sandwiches are on the menu.

It's Starbucks. Just as good, or mediocre, as all the rest. My pet peeve is that when you order a cafe au lait, a coffee drink that's twice as old and known in NOLA than Starbucks, at THIS location, the barista MUST correct you that it's a Misto.  No, Starbucks, it is not. At least not in New Orleans. While in Rome, learn the vernacular. Or at least keep your snobbish better-than-thou attitude to thyself.
Friendly baristas, quick service and plenty of seating. Nice spot to recharge with your favorite caffeinated beverage and a quick bite.
A typical starbucks, however less stars due to the lack of comfortable, cozy seating.
The the sugar free syrups taste jute as good in a latte as the regular.

Hey Starbucks! A big wag of the finger!! I bring my own personal cup to get my iced coffee and yet, your staff thinks that it is okay to use a plastic cup to simply write what my drink was and then that cup gets discarded. Chuck You #Starbucks. #wasteful

I need my Starbucks. I been to Reno before and always look for a Starbucks in Circus Circus, El Dorado and Silver Legacy and NOTHING. The closest one is in the Harrahs. It's very easy to find and spot... its on the 2nd floor in the West Tower. The staff is very nice and friendly. The coffee is OK, especially its not the normal Starbucks prices either. Its actually more... I think its because its located inside Harrahs, but there always a line out the door. There only a few chairs and table, but usually people don't sit around. People get in line and purchase their coffee and leave.  They don't give Internet access either... SUCKY!!! I thought they don't offer internet to everyone, but I have Starbucks Rewards, so with my rewards... I'm suppose to get free internet access, but I don't at this location.

Starbucks as usual but one of the biggest locations I've been to in awhile. I'm a sucker for local coffee shops but there's a serious lack of "local" in Haddonfield with Starbucks and Saxby's on King's Highway.   Nice seating area. I've always had a big problem with cafes not having enough seating for customers. There's a nice line of high chairs and bar-like seating while also having your tables by a fireplace on the other side without the bustle of the people ordering. Wi-Fi is great - I've had issues at Saxby's down the street and other shops before. Plenty of outlets too (at least, at the bar-like seating). Also probably one of the very few places open past 8pm during the weekdays which is helpful when you're a working professional who also wants to study for grad school exams and future grad school!   The weird? They are open until 10pm but were cleaning when I arrived at 8pm and mopping around 8:30 when there were still people walking in and out. I ordered a decaf coffee when I arrived and there wasn't any brewed but they gladly brewed a fresh batch for me and told me it was a short wait.   Overall, one of the better Starbucks I've been to.

OK, OK it's Starbucks! But on that note, this location very efficient, clean & fast!! It was extremely Brick outside, so running into the city Sunday I stopped to get a skinny macchiatio-& it delivered. Yummmmm. The employees were quick, sits on corner, & like this one.

Okay this is by far my favorite location. The management is wonderful, the staff amazing, and they even make great drinks!!! I love everything about them and they are always there with a smile. Drive-thru takes a little long but I don't mind since I'm just too lazy to walk my butt in there sometimes  I have messed up ordering on the app I don't know how many times and they just wave at me and say no worries we got you.  If I had any new complaint it would be the price and they can't help that  Love this place.

Not enough seating for normal folks wanting to sit and enjoy a coffee with a date. Is taken over by students using Starbucks for a study hall. Funny cause some of them don't have a purchase that I have noticed?  Sorry but I pay good money for Starbucks coffee .
love Starbucks but the Egyptian taking all over the place and the fight in it wallah... I come here everyday because I love seeing them fighting lol

Can't say I'm too impressed with today's visit. I received my latte and it was just a bit more than half full, we waited in the drive thru for almost 20 minutes, I was OVERCHARGED, and the barista who took our order didn't seem to know very much about the items Starbucks serves. I can't say I've ever had a bad experience at Starbucks; however, this visit was the worst on my list and I'm a gold member so I visit Starbucks a lot! I originally wasn't too worried about the service and the wait time - the thing that got me was my latte being more of a tall than the grande that I ordered. And mind you, I did not ask for it with room. The picture along with this review was taken before I took one sip of my latte that wasn't even prepared correctly. Really hate having to write these reviews, but just like for others to be informed. I called the store after getting down the road and realizing I was overcharged. I called and asked to speak to a manager and they don't even have a manager in today, how unprofessional!!  I had the nice girl confirm how much my order would be and I was right, I was overcharged a few dollars for two lattes and a sandwhich. Very very annoyed.

Starbucks is Starbucks, BUT the people who work here really make it. It feels familial when the employees get to know you. I'm a regular in the morning during the weekdays, and there are a lot of other regulars who make it feel warm and friendly.

This review has been a long time coming. I am a previous employee of Starbucks so I feel like I have a pretty good idea on policies, procedures and drink quality. The customer service and drink quality lacks consistently here.   I want to like this store so bad as it is near my home and on my way to work. But they have messed up one too many times. The other day I ordered a venti iced americano in the drive thru and when I pulled around to receive and pay for my drink the employee let me know they just ran out of cold cups. That's fine. I'm not one of those Starbucks snobs who freaks out. However, I did want my normal amount of espresso shots, strength of the drink, etc. She asked if I would like a different size and I asked if she could just dump the drink in a Trenta size cup. I didn't expect anything extra or even for the cup to be filled up- and I let her know that. She shortly replied "no we're not allowed". If there's anything I know about working at Starbucks or any business- the customers come first. Especially when the business makes a mistake- like not ordering enough cups. She could have very easily given me a larger cup- I know from personal experience. Another time I was told I could not have my free refill on iced tea and coffee for the gold star membership and the manager stood right there and agreed with it. The employees here are very often short and rude in general. However, there is one guy- a younger, taller, thin guy with facial hair that I usually see in the drive thru and at times the register who has always been more than willing to provide excellent service well. As well there is a woman with short blonde hair who is usually making drinks and if I could find out her schedule and only come when she is making drinks- I would. She always provides me with excellent drinks just how I like it. Those two are the only reason I'm giving this place 2 stars instead of 1.  I wish I could like this place- messing up a drink a couple times or having a slow drive thru is one thing- but I just can't seem to get over the poor service. Sorry, Starbucks.

This is a great Starbucks right in the heart of town. One of the few you see anymore that isn't a drivethru. It really keeps hold of the old ways of the neighborhood store. I moved away from Florida 3.5 years ago and just came back for a vacation for the first time since I left. And while there has been some turnover there were still a few baristas (and the manager) who remember me by name. It's nice to still feel like a regular in a town that has changed so much in the last few years.   Outside of that this store while busy can usually get you through the line quickly and is kept clean and comfortable. There is ample seating inside and out and the music is kept low enough that you can easily read, study, or work if you want to camp out for a while.
I love the service I get from this Starbucks I like the management ,& workers are excellent especially the morning staff could not be happier

I'm a Starbucks fan, but I think I was at this location at a bad time.  I was there around lunchtime on a Sunday.  I was able to place my order pretty quickly, but I had a long wait time for pick up (and I had friends sitting in the car waiting for me).  I like the architecture of the building, but parking is problematic.  There are some spaces in the back and the side of the building, but it was cramped.  Also perhaps because it was the weekend, there were more teenagers in here than I usually see at Starbucks.  I think they could have used another staff member behind the counter making drinks to handle the volume of people.  I hope they do on busy weekday mornings.

Horribly slow service every time I come here no matter what time it is! Drinks are inconsistent but still better than any other coffee around big bend, and they have a drive thru, unlike most other sb in Riverview. Been waiting over 5 min in drive thru with one car in front of me for a grande coffee.

Like wow!! Who does the training in this staff!!?? This is like the 5th time my drink it been messed up and the 1st time I come back... the remake the drink and still wrong!!!! Wtf!? And I spend $8 on a drink and for my inconvenience they give me $4!? Dude this is a joke of Starbucks.... I hate that this is he only Starbucks that is close to my job... Do I have to quit coffee in the mornings!!?? I guess if the staff are the same...
This place gives excellent service. Employees are super nice and helpful. I will drive a great distance to visit the Starbucks versus others.

Today is the grand opening of this store - Ferguson, MO. They have a new concept at this location (2nd store to have, 1st was in Jamaica Queens) ....a community room that the Urban League will be able to help better the community. The room can also be booked by other organizations and it is free. They have a video screen, separate coffee/drink station, tables with outlets.  A lot of the workers live within a 5 mile radius. It is a very large store. I love it. Thanks Starbucks

If parking doesn't shy you away, this location is quite quaint.  It has enough little tables for seating outside and inside to allow for a great business meeting or a coffee with a friend.  Staff is pretty friendly and they  haven't got my order wrong yet so got to give it four stars for that!  Good selection of gift ideas too for that coffee or now available Tea connoisseurs!!

Whomever was paid off to put in their review that the drive thru line was "the fastest in the nation" I encourage you to share what you're smoking with the rest of us still waiting in the drive thru line...  It doesnt matter what time you come here, 6am or 10pm, the line wraps the building. Go inside and you will be ignored for drive thru orders that are timed so it makes sense why the drive thru is always busy.   Unless you get simple drinks here, your coffee will be incorrect. I used to drink a quad espresso and after chewing the ice block they provided, I switched to Nitro with sweet cream. Depending on the day, the sweet cream is not refrigerated and may curdle on the top of the cup.   If you can, order ahead, risk your life trying to find a parking spot and get out of the parking lot and you should be in and out in 10 minutes and not 20 as compared to the drive thru.
Had to wait 16 mins in line for 3 shots of espresso... Didnt get a "hello" "sorry for the wait" or acknowledgement for that matter at the window. Rude staff. Unnecessarily long wait.
I love Starbucks Coffee it brighten up my day and it help me stay up to read more books like always. :D and watch more anime.

Our friendly neighborhood Starbucks... good place to meet friends OK for business associates ... She's been there FOREVER and survived the downsizing from a few years ago (thank God)... but her age is showing, could use some new furniture or even a remodel, but we LOVE her    7/31/18  She was FINALLY remodeled a few weeks ago, looks really great and much larger (took over the veterinarian office space that was next door and added to their own).  She needed the space to handle the crowds.  Unfortunately 90% of the chairs have no cushion and are uncomfortable to sit in after about 15 min or so ... maybe that is by design?  Probably won't be meeting friends here to chat... too bad, she used to be such a cozy place to gather for a visit.

I have been to many Starbucks Coffee places in many states and I loved the familiar smell when you first walk in. My drink was perfect. There was some nice out door seating. I was disappointed at the very few selection of coffee mugs and also that the table was pushed directly up to the shelve where you had to interrupt people sitting to browse. There is a refrigerated section that takes up the rest of the wall and gives this location a super market feel. Tucson is over 100 degrees out and most of the seating is outside. It's just not a relaxing atmosphere. I worked at a much nicer location several years ago and the retail selection was just as important as the drinks. I'm happy that they have the classics still!

My review is not for the staff but rather the clientele and owners. Why do people always use the handicap parking spots to pick up mobile orders? Most stores only have 1 mobile parking spot if any at all. Are people so narcissistic that basic laws and human decency are cast aside for a cup of gourmet coffee? Maybe the management can start enforcing the parking or add more mobile spots. I have found this to be true at every Starbucks I have visited in 6 different states.

This is the best Starbucks in Reno. The staff are friendly and speedy, the space is well laid out for staying and reading, and it has a drive-thru.  Our running group usually crashes the place on Saturday mornings, and we're always met with cheerfulness and welcome.
I'm surprised that there are only two stars. Yeah, the parking situation isn't the most convenient, but it's a good place to study and the staff is kind and helpful.

Date night, Apparently there are 2 locations in Oldsmar and I was at one while the beau was at another. While I waited for him to join me at this location, I ordered my hot chai Tea. Jonah was absolutely wonderful. He was personable and friendly and exceptionally warm. We chatted about how our names are different (my alternate Id) and how we don't often hear them, though mine is growing popular due to Anime.   Once my order was placed, it took only a few minutes to have it in my hands so I could wait for Bruce to join me. I'd come back.
The staff is uniformly great.  Warm, helpful, welcoming.  Parking lot is better than the one in Havertown and it feels a bit less crowded.  Too bad it's not open a bit later at night.

Do not go here unless you want to wait 45 minutes for a drink that's made wrong. Sorry but this store needs to get rid of the old lady in drive thru if she can't take orders the right way and in a timely manner.

Is it too much to ask that the cashier have some sort of personality and acknowledge the customer in front of her?  No acknowledgement, no hello, no attempted add ons and no thank you!  Everyone else working was very nice but first impression is everything and this one sucked.

Really limited seating with not a lot of charging ports. I had a lady flip out on me because she wanted a seat from my table, but I needed it to put stuff on it. My table was crowded with drinks, a large laptop and food....Can't really do much, lady! I wish the staff would have asked her to please keep it down when her children were throwing huge tantrums for a good hour.

Very generic Starbucks with a small parking lot.  The staff when I visited did not have the coffee roast I normally drink and wanted to charge me extra for a pour-over.  From my knowledge, pour-overs do not cost more than regular drip coffee.  I will not be returning to this location.

I would give this location five stars if they would figure out the drive-thru process. Making people circle back around the building to exit the drive thru is less than efficient (and safe). However, the folks who work there are generally pleasant, service is quick and we're always happy with our drinks!

I love starbucks. It's my go to coffee destination. I won't, however, be coming back to this location. I'm lactose intolerant and love the bux for the delicious soy milk. I ordered a soy latte. The barista confirmed that it was soy. I've not eaten anything else all day and have been violently ill. Clearly the barista put milk in my drink. I called the location to complain and they couldn't be bothered other than "let us know if you have to go to the emergency room"... really?? I'm so disappointed and still sick.

I am SO PISSED OFF @ this store!!!!!! I did a mobile order which I typically do and my app tells me my order is ready. Get to Starbucks and I wait for 20 mins with no coffee and the baristas making other drinks. Finally I get someone's attention and she informs me that they don't have my coffee and that was if. No apologize, no problem solving. Just flat out screw your mobile order. I will NEVER go to this Starbucks again. Lost a customer.

Finally, it opens!  The shell of this Starbucks had mocked the better half and I for at least a year. Driving past it twice going to visit her folks "Opening soon" it said for ever and ever.  Then, suddenly it was open and operating!  The staff seems more than excited about the new location. My barista, while making my Grande Double Blended Caramel Frappucino, was filled with so much zeal that he must have put almost the entire can of whipped cream on the top.  When he went to put the lid on, an almost obscene display of it ejected from the hole where the straw goes!  I'm looking forward to many future visits to this location, and I hope they keep putting on just the same amount of whipped cream!

The guy who was at the front was having a bad day. I just got two waters around 7pm, so it wasn't busy at all. He just acted like he was having a crappy day and didn't want to deal with customers. Okay, I get it. So I left a tip anyways. But when I got my water, one had a long light brownish gold hair, I thought it was from the girl who got the waters. I told him and he rolled his eyes at me, told me "Uh. That's weird because she doesn't touch the waters at all." I know, it comes from a fountain.  But dude, there was a hair in my water, INSIDE the cup when I just got it, and I have jet black hair. No way it was mine.  Still sitting here, sipping on my new water ‍ He needs an attitude check.

Friendly staff and quick service. I appreciate that they still have breakfast sandwiches near noon. Today is Black Friday and they're keeping everything moving smooth. They have a unique selection of Starbucks coffee cups that I haven't noticed in others. Very clean as well.
