I love this Starbucks.  My favorite place to study.  The baristas are so friendly/happy and frequently give discounts and freebies to regulars (and free samples of food to everyone).  Also, I've never not been able to find a place to sit.  Outlets are usually available, only a handful of times have I had to wait about half an hour for one to open up.  Also, when the weather's nice, there's a great covered porch outside with lights and ceiling fans (AND outlets).  It's true that their internet is restricted to T-Mobile/AT&T/SBC customers, but I think that actually keeps the place from getting overcrowded with students during peak study times (I suppose they go to PJs or Rue instead).  However, if you know someone with a password for one of those providers, you can use it unlimitedly (I've shared my password with a friend there and we were both able to log-on using it at the same time).  I would love to support "local" coffee shops, and I've tried, but all the places close to me have fatal flaws: --too crowded and no tables during peak hours/times (Rue on Carrollton) --too few outlets and trash all over the place (PJs on Maple) --apathetic (bordering on rude sometimes) baristas, terribly low lighting, too few outlets (Rue on Magazine) --Zots (WAY too dark)

Among Tampa area Starbucks, this has shown to be one of the least accessible.  The entrance and exit are difficult to access.  There are about 5 parking spots available.  (The other 5 belong to the Game Stop next door.)  When the drive thru is full, you might as well not try parking for either one of those businesses.

This is my go-to Starbucks while I am at work. Their employees are ALWAYS friendly and accurately take your order. No matter how long the drive-thru line the employees handle it pretty quickly. I have on occasion tried their pre-made sandwiches. My favorite is the egg salad, the arugula adds a nice depth to this classic. There is outside seating. Overall a great location.

I love this Starbucks..I love their flat white!! Rich and sweet! I have been to all of the other ones this is the only place that gets it right!.. However... I would give five stars but I feel like I'm at the bank on Friday at noon.. 20 people in line and one teller The wait is ridiculous!!

I never write reviews for corporate chains but this Starbucks is the second worst Starbucks I have ever been to anywhere in the world.  (The worst: Starbucks right across the street from the British Museum in London)   The service at this Starbucks is horrendous.  I work on Murfreesboro Pk so I stop in for a coffee at least twice a month...the service is ALWAYS bad.  The baristas seem unaware or uncaring about other people's time.  When your establishment is filled with people in business attire at 12:30 PM, you should assume that they're probably on their lunch break and they don't have 20 minutes to wait for you to make a latte.  Heed my warning: if you order anything other than a plain coffee, it will take between 15 and 20 minutes to get your drink.  There are 4 people working behind the bar.  It should never take 20 minutes to make a drink regardless of how many orders there are.  There are 4 of you.  I worked at a much busier Starbucks in college and if it took 4 of us 20 minutes to deliver a customer's drink we'd all be fired at the end of the day.  Not only is the staff lackadaisical in delivering orders, a handful of the baristas are extremely rude and inconsiderate.  I came in today after lunch to pick up a coffee for the afternoon and in the middle of my order (while I was still speaking) another barista offered the cashier an erroneous latte.  The cashier stopped taking my order, asked me to "hold on" and turned her attention to the barista.  "What is this?" "I used skim milk instead of whole.  Do you want it?" "Um, no let me ask this other barista chick if she wants it." "Just put it over there." "I don't want them to think it's a to-go order though."  The conversation continued while my boyfriend and I literally stood there and just stared at the guy making coffee and the cashier banter about what to do with this erroneous order.  The cashier interrupted me mid-sentence and then proceeded to have a 3 minute conversation about what to do with an erroneous order.  This is unacceptable.  THERE ARE CUSTOMERS IN FRONT OF YOU.  A customer is ordering.  Take the effing order and then figure out what to do with the mistake latte!   The Starbucks brand is all about providing you a good cup of coffee with the (HUGE) added bonus of great convenience.  If I wanted to be treated like an afterthought and wait around for 20 minutes to get a cup of coffee, I'd head to one of the hundreds of independent coffee shops for a far superior brew.   NEVER AGAIN.
Fast service through drive-through even during rush hour each morning. Friendly staff. Convenient location.

I have been there several times, walk-in, ignored, drive thru, wrong order! I am a basic latte drinker, no funny order requests.  Burned coffee I will not abide! Being ignored, no more! Get a dedicated cashier with no headset these folks can't multi-task!
Can't really think of anything bad to say. The store is brand new so it looked super clean. Ambiance was very nice. Very relaxed atmosphere. Workers kept bringing me samples of drinks.
This is a really nice Starbucks.   The wifi is strong and there is ample comfortable seating.   I like to wait here while my car is in for service at the Firestone across the parking lot.

I like this Starbucks.  It's practically across the street from my house and it's never really that busy.  The service is great and fast.  Also, it's next door to a Brueggers if you're not feelin' the Starbucks breakfast choices.

This location is so hit or miss. More miss than hit. Their are a few baristas in there that need attitude adjustments. Being nice is not hard. I understand we all have our days but their drive thru skills are pathetic. Need to take a lesson from Chick fil A. I do enjoy Sandy (I think that's her name). She always is pleasant and there is a younger guy that works there that takes his job really serious and aims for excellent customer service. Great job to those two employees. The others just need to be a bit more friendlier.

I travel all over the world and often visit Starbucks. I've probably been to Starbucks in more than 20 countries. This is my absolute favorite location. They have really taken the time to get to know me, and they always treat me well.  In addition, the drink quality is always spot on. Sometimes, the taste can be off at a Starbucks where the baristas are in a rush or not trained properly, but I have never had a bad drink here.  Rock on, 19th and Chestnut!

This place is FREEZING. Yes, in Tucson even, this place is uncomfortably cold. The building benefits from shade so it shouldn't need too much AC, but they blast it as if they're on the sun. Much warmer and more comfortable outside somehow.
This Starbucks is great, fast service and very convenient. I'm a guest here and I appreciate the staff and the manager.

This is THE slowest Starbucks I have ever been to. If you are in a rush and need coffee and WANT to be late to wherever you're going, go to this Starbucks. They don't know how to work efficiently and there is always a line to order and a line waiting to get there coffee. I live one block away it takes me 2 minutes to get there. I wait in line for 10 minutes then wait for my drink for 20 minutes. Thumbs down.

A little nasty and testy when you ask for refill. The workers automatically take for granted that you walked off the premises when you've been sitting here for 45 minutes working on your computer. This is not good business practices by the employees of Starbucks.

It's 6:53 am supposed to be open at 6:00am no staff in site not the first time they have not been open on time. Heading across the street to the Diner for my go-go juice at least I know someone will be there to make it.
Very quick service. Customer service was great. I would recommend going back to this Starbucks.
This is the worst Starbucks out there! Second day in a row no vanilla in a vanilla latte! I waited five minutes as three employees watched coffee pour into a cup. Worst tasting coffee.

I've been to probably over 100 different Starbucks.  This particular Starbucks is the most friendly and consistent I've been to.  The other day they gave me my order for free for a Christmas present.  That's great customer service.  It's located in the Hyatt so it's  quite busy during conventions,  but the speed of service is great.  They always have enough staff,  and they are all friendly.

This is an awesome location on Harrison Avenue at Memphis Street. The building is small and tan with the line on the depth of the Katrina water on the side. The staff and baristas are sweet and friendly. The coffee is hot and awesome! The WiFi is free and called Google Starbucks. To log in click the screen on a green box that says "accept & connect".  Open the same hours as Canal at St. Charles (05:30-21:00). This location has plenty of outdoor seating, as much or more than indoor! Some decent bicycle parking is also available. Nearby to the lake and City Park! You'll enjoy this location out of the main hustle and bustle of the CBD and French Quarter. :) Muchlove y'all!
I love how friendly the staff is. Went here multiple times already and the experience is always good. They serve fast and accurate. Though parking lot is quite small.

We stop at this Starbucks usually on the way to my husband's parents' home. Service is fine, although they don't put sprinkles on my skinny cinnamon dolce latte unless I ask (unless the stores on Binford and 96th St). The odd thing is that there is always something weird going on in the parking lot. This last time, a guy was driving in reverse across the lot; another time, a car was trying to exit while another car was trying to enter and neither car would move. I ended up having to get up out of the car to order while my husband waited to get into the lot!

In general, slow service, some unhappy workers, little teamwork. Store can be messy when busy. Parking lot too small that you may be blocked by people sitting in the drive thru. Falling short of expected Starbucks standards sadly and the reason why i brew more at home.  Too bad it's the only Starbucks on my way to work when i don't wake up in time to brew and ironically still cutting it close to clock-in time...

Everyone who works at this Starbucks seems to be having the worst day of their life every time I come here. I've had nothing but bad experiences every time, but I keep coming back because it's closest to my apartment. I've never had a drive thru experience where they didn't get something wrong and then get mad because I asked them to fix it. Seriously, it's happened like six times and I have a very common, uncomplicated drink order.
Super slow on the mobile order.  Also, I order the same Frappuccino every time I go to a Starbucks.  This is the sweetest one I've ever tasted and not in a good way.

Drive-thru is perpetually slow, sometimes waiting 10 minutes plus. Not sure if they are understaffed or staff is just slow. Other Tampa Starbucks the staff is hustling during busy times. It's too bad because it's such a convenient location.

Greatest staff of any Starbucks in the area.   I've been going to this Starbucks for about ten years and I'm not exaggerating when I say this place has seen me grow up. They have the best baristas in the game. If you're new: they'll make you feel welcome and if you're a regular: you can be sure they'll make you feel like family. In fact, when I left for college I told my mom they would be some of the people I missed most.   Since the location change and addition of the drive thru, new hires were made and they're the same type of wonderful as the veteran baristas.   Speaking of the move, the place now boasts a larger, shiny, new interior (although if you roll through at a busy time you may be hard-pressed to find a seat), wonderful outdoor seating area, and the benefit of being a test location for Starbucks Evenings- a development that offers wine, craft beer, and small plates in addition to the Starbucks coffee drinks and treats (the truffle popcorn is a must-have.)  The drive thru is a great addition although, if I'm being honest, I hardly ever use it because I don't want to miss out on the great experience of talking to my baristas.   Basically, if you're in the area, make this your go-to Starbucks and you won't regret it.
As frequently as I stop here, it's just a stop. I get my grande bold and move on. This Starbucks is indicative of MOST locations, so there's not much to add to the conversation.

Very cool, low-key easy in and out not crowded most mornings- plus all of the workers are great weekends are a lot busier but hey it' worth the wait not as many seats as I have seen at most, being that it's a community college across the street it could use a little more seating, very limited outside seating ( but great place to stop buy

All Starbucks are not the same. This one is top notch! The staff is very quick and efficient, and has never made a mistake in my app orders... not even once. And after a few days, they even greeted me by name when they realized which order matched me. Honestly, this is the best Starbucks I've visited in a long while. Very happy customer.
This location has a consistently friendly staff. Makes my Monday's a lot easier when I get to interact with people that are nice, quick, and knowledgeable of the product offerings!

I am very upset with the service at this Starbucks. When I ordered a pink drink through the drive through, they got my order wrong, so naturally I go back inside and ask that they remake my drink. The employees smirk at my request and proceed to make me a drink, and the drink they made me was basically just coconut milk. These employees were rude, incompetent and careless. If I could give this Starbucks a lower rating I would, but it isn't possible. This is the drink the Starbucks employees gave me versus what the drink is supposed to look like.

We frequent this location. (3) times this week we have sat in a very long line only to find out they ran out of Tea Refreshers.  Could you possibly put up a stationary sign indicating this? So frustrating & waste of time.  Even before the holidays, this location frequently runs out of bagged coffee & stoppers.  This location could be managed better.

Any place where I can sit for more than two hours with my cup of coffee and zero disturbance is my kind of coffee shop. This morning was very busy and as busy as it got I was still able to surf the web in peace. Free wifi, drive thru, and friendly service. This SB is popping so whether you're reading this review or not, chances are you'll stop by this one if you're around the area.

Absolutely LOVE this new location!  It has just recently opened and is easily my favorite one to study at!  It is actually a little further of a drive from my house than other Starbucks locations, but I choose to make the drive every time!  First of all, I have to complement the staff!  The employees are so kind and are eager to please their customers.  During one of my visits, I ordered a drink that they were out of and was completely refunded and given my "second choice" at a discounted rate.  I thought this was so kind.  Next, the atmosphere is great!  There are many tables of all shapes and sizes, so I am convinced there is a place for everyone to get "comfy" here!  They also offer nice furniture in the front of the store by the windows, which is not something all locations have.  The restrooms are impressively clean and are very well-maintained; really, the whole place is!  If you are looking for a place to be productive or just meet with some friends over a cup of coffee/tea, I highly suggest this Starbucks location!

I came here ordered a drink, ran to the bathroom real quick since it was a bit busy and I had just ordered... I came back waited but then realized someone took my drink I ordered . So I let the barista know so I could get a new one , he then said "okay I'll make you another". Right after the barista I told then went to the back for like 15 minutes I have no idea why. Long story short I had to remind the other baristas like twice to remake my drink since I paid already, took at least 30 min for me to get just one drink.
I love this drive through Starbucks! I come here all the time. Sometimes I order ahead of time and others I just use my Starbucks app to pay. They always have what I want and it tastes amazing!

They do a good job keeping the line moving.  Sometimes the staff gets obviously overwhelmed and passive-aggressive with one another which can be a bit uncomfortable.  The food selection isn't as stocked as some other locations I've been to.  But overall...It's a Starbucks.
Great hidden place to relax and enjoy a cup of coffee with friends. Great staff and a great little patio.

This place is horrible, I've gone maybe 3 times hoping it would get better since it's close to my house. The coffee is always watered down, I'll usually get an iced coffee, and for whatever reason it may be this location always seems to fill the entire cup to the brim with scoops of ice. They need to properly train their employees, they lack courteous customer service skills and are oblivious when it comes to making coffee, if I wanted this trash I'd go to some gas station on Milpas but yet again that would be much much better than this. Save your time and drive too montecito they never disappoint, best of all they know how to treat their customers and most importantly the coffee is the best of all the Starbucks (la cumbre is a close 2nd) in this city.

On my way into this facility, someone did not hold the door open for me. This set the vibe I got from this disgrace of a store. Everyone was staring at me for wearing my "I'm an Hispanic doctor.. keep your eyes off ladies" shirt. I felt so uncomfortable and targeted. This establishment refused to serve me do to me being barefoot and tracking in a bunch of dirt.. this is why I hate Luna Y Sol.  also the bacon and Gouda sandwich was not good but good but not good for sure.
I love this Starbucks and location.  The staff are always super nice and the coffee's are on point.  This is a great chill place too. I am always ready for my latte with extra foam yummm

Our third attempy, as we are new to area....today 10 min to order and almost 25 min to get husband drink.  I had a coffee he a lattee, mine virtually gone by time we received his.  Asked and said sorry someone may have taken, didnt readily offer to make immediately...guy next to me had 40 min wait.  Unbelievable  need to find another coffee place.  Your brand wont withstand horrible service

2 stars. 6 stars less than the amount of people working. I come in, primed for joe, ready for bean juice. 8 humans, 16 eyes (lenses) focused on me. I greet each pair with my own. "Hello," I say behind my eyes. Accept me, I'm only here to pay. So I pray, I pray that someone will help me. A simple cup of joe on ice would suffice. I know I'm not elite yet, but I long for the day. Shorter hair glare comes forward, and I wait for confirmation. Affirmation. Acceptance. "Hi, i'd like a small iced joe please". I hear my words enter the void, and soon I see them wave back at me. I did not ask for tall, I asked for small. Forgive me father for I have sinned.

The Starbucks here in the mall is one of the best in boise. Every time I come here my coffee is fantastic and made in a timely manner. All the employees are friendly and talkative. I normally order a venti iced caramel macchiato. Best drink Starbucks has to offer. Next time you are in the mall stop in and grab a little something.

Don't waste your time or money at this place. Will take at least 10 minutes in the drive thru and that's if you're the only car. I have left multiple times due to the wait. Terrible service and unfriendly staff. There's other local coffee shops in the area that are much better and faster
My favorite Starbucks!!! Stellar service and drinks made perfectly every time. The best staff of any Starbucks I have been to.Always pleasant, always friendly, always warm. :)

I frequent this Starbucks location often on my way to work. Can I just say, that besides the obviously good coffee, the food is pretty scrumptious as well! I recently tried a piping hot tomato & mozzarella panini that was incredible, along with a satisfying piece of banana bread. My absolute favorite drink is a chai tea latte -milky ,creamy, sweet, with some spice. Yum. Pumpkin spice lattes are up there too :)
This Starbucks is a pretty good place to hang out & get some work done.  Upstairs seating area is comfy.

This location sometimes runs out of soy milk and bacon gouda sandwiches, but the employees are very friendly. They used to mess up my drink sometimes but they've gotten much better. It sort of depends on when you go, I guess.

Excellent, fast and friendly service every single time. They get my mocha light frap right every time I go. I only go on my pay day, otherwise I would have gone bankrupt by now. BUT it is a nice treat, and the price tag is easier to swallow when my frap is delivered with a smile :)

If you are using the drive through be prepared to wait in line for long time since this seems to be a very popular drive-through location. And unfortunately this location has really bad and sloooooow service.  I have been inside many times and 90% of the time had to just wait for someone to actually take my order. There are always employees walking around but dis-interested in actually taking an order. They only know what they do walking around. Since I have to wait to get my drink for a long time as well. This location really needs to improve on the service.

My favorite Starbucks location in Reno. The baristas are friendly and my order is always correct. Easy to get through the drive-thru or inside. Always clean inside too. Nice seating for sitting with friends.

This Starbucks location is really cool.  Since they've been open,  the tall gentleman barista (don't know his name but he reminds me of David from QVC)  has been so nice and helpful in my indecisive choices lol.... Though this spot has little and tight parking spots, it's best you park across in the median area, and it gets really crowded at different times of the busy day.   Also the location is easy without having to drive deep into Metairie or downtown if you are near the Lakefront, N.O.East, or Mid City areas.

This is my favorite Starbucks in the area. I've got a lot of choices and this one isn't the closest, but I really love this one and will go out of my way to choose this Starbucks over the others. The people are very nice and make delicious beverages (everyone makes them a little different, but this is the most consistent and delicious!). It doesn't ever seem too crowded and the service is very quick!

I went in to pick up some coffee for the morning, coarse ground for my Chemex. I picked my coffee, asked for it to be coarse ground for my Chemex. I'm okay with her not knowing what a Chemex is, but to give me the finest grind, the opposite of coarse, is pretty bad. Working in a coffee house, you should know the difference of different grinds. To top it off, she was minimally apologetic for her error and made it seem more like I was bothering her than she was keen on pleasing me, the customer.

Starbucks at this location is great. The staff is awesome, the drinks are great (cider and vanilla chi tea are my favorite) and the place is relaxing with a great hangout area with free wifi. The downside is that the parking lot is very small and a pain to get into. With GameStop next door, the lot seems always full but I ride a bike there so this isn't an issue for myself. Also the interselection is quite brutal between memorial highway and hillsborough ave. Also remember that Starbucks isn't the cheapest but in the end, it's better than anything Dunkin can produce.
Large and crowded.  Not much in the way of "outstanding".  Starbucks is becoming just "ok" now.  Honestly, give some locals a try.  I be the WiFi is just as good.
It's a beautiful day in paradise but a hot one familia. I have to take time and thank good and the person who invented air conditioning better known as "a/c"!

This particular Starbucks location really gets the job done and pushes their customers through with ease. Ordered a cinnamon chai tea latte, it was on point and tasted delicious. Major knock on this place is if your ordering inside, parking can be tough because the lot is small and the drive thru traffic makes it difficult for you to pull out. Aside from that, solid location.   Pros- Drinks Service  Taste  Cleanliness  Cons- Parking  Crowded  Drive thru traffic

I have always raved about this store. Today was a very SLOW day with drive thru...waited at least 25 minutes. I would've left but I was boxed in.  Not sure why the wait was so long! But I am writing this review while I wait in line. Please help this store....Terribly SLOW
literally so slow, you would think they would be a little faster with orders. i should not be waiting 30 minutes for a drink.

Their drive thru is super slow, even when I was the only car. I ordered, she made my drink, I payed, a few other cars came behind me and I sat in my car and stared at my drink on the counter for 5 minutes listening to this lady take orders from the other cars. I guess she couldn't multitask and just hand me my drink while taking orders. Oh well, at least she apologized for the wait lol.

The staff in this Starbucks is very nice. They smile and are always polite. My coffee is always the way I like it. It does get quite crowed but the staff handles it well and gets me out quickly. I love having this Starbucks in my neighborhood. Hannah, Nikki and Justin are doing a great job.
Easily the best SBUX in downtown Philly. Friendly, quick & clean. What else can you ask for. Tasha & Jarrett are the best.

I'm surprised that during a busy time there is only one cashier. The person before me had an item return and it took such a long time. No one said anything to me while I waited and waited. I come here a lot and just expected to be welcomed and not ignored.   Today Austin made my day! He gave me lots of ideas on how to enhance my drink. Nice! I feel like an important customer and will be back soon. My cascara latte with an extra shot is perfect! Thanks Austin!
Unfriendly.  Forgot part of order. Half full drinks. Asked for extra hot - didn't get that either.

While I was a little put off that on occasion the lines go bit longer than I felt they should, then I had occasion to sit down and complete some work I Hadn't finished. That moment gave me a chance to appreciate truly relaxed atmosphere, as well as the friendly hard-working staff! It has become my favorite spot to just sit and collect myself and enjoy a nice atmosphere.

I really enjoy Starbucks.  I enjoy stopping off at them from time to time to relax and clear my mind. Although, trying to find a parking space at this location is almost impossible sometimes.  With that is added stress which I don't need.  What's sad is the location is very convenient for me. I'll be craving a cup of Jo as I pass, look at the lot and decide to keep going.
These directions led us to a cow field, no word on whether his name is Starbucks, but he didn't have any coffee.

I feel sad for the other employees. Shannon at Starbucks was rolling her eyes at them, yelling at one of the younger girl(Isabella I believe) for asking her "too many questions" and kept raising her voice saying "OH MY GOSHHHHH" towards her   She was also ignoring the customers because she was so flustered with the other employees (who were not doing anything wrong)  It costs $0 to be a kind human.
Always consistently good service; however, exceptionally friendly service at the drive-thru this morning, without being phony.  Great job!  Oh, and great coffee :)

Person is refusing to serve me because they are out of the cups needed to make my drink. But I can see her literally making other people's orders in similar cup? I asked why and am told it's against their policy?

Always a line, but the service is great.  I'm there every morning for a to-go coffee and bagel.  How come they never remember my name or order yet they remember all of the other regular customers?  Am I that forgettable?  Regardless, I still stop by hoping that they'll remember my name.  Oh well.
Very nice Starbucks - lots of outdoor seating & the baristas have always been pleasant.  Drinks & food are consistent with Starbucks quality.

This is my least favorite Starbucks to visit.  The only person that is professional is the worker named Kareema.   Everyone else is very rude.  They talk to you in a very exasperated tone.   They act as if you are bothering them by wanting to put in your order.

Came to this Starbucks and I will definitely be coming back. I frequent Starbucks in different cities vet often. I love that they have a larger patio to sit outside, a drive thru, and a very convenient location to easily get in and out. Ample parking as well. The service could of been quicker on a not too busy Saturday mid-afternoon. But I didn't mind waiting a little bit longer. But my dog thoroughly enjoyed the Puppychino from the barista and meeting new customers that walked by. I will be coming back soon! Great experience here at his location

So excited to try the newly renovated Starbucks in Safety Harbor! This is where my excitement ended.  Newly renovated, but for some reason it doesn't seem like the whole "Design and Research" department at Starbucks tested the seating and comfort designs on humans.    Let's get started with the HUGE bench in the new part of this Starbucks, why is it so high up?  Why is it so uncomfortable? Did the designer actually SIT on this?  Nice plugs right by your feet, but why?  No Human could ever sit here and plug away at studying or work while enjoying Coffee because the tiny tables in front of the bench is LOWER than the bench itself.  What? Yah.  This would be torture to try to use these little tiny tables even for setting a coffee on.    OK, so what about the chairs?  Very odd wooden bottomed chairs with either a strange padded back or NO BACK AT ALL.  What the heck?  I wonder how many back injuries are going to happen after sitting in here over a cup of coffee and a scone.  Bench along the window.  Good idea I guess, but I can't get over the horrible chairs.   To the designer, are you a reincarnated Medieval torture device engineer?  What on earth are you thinking!?   As to the coffee, yah it's good and the service is still on point.  But the Baristas were even complaining about back pain because they lowered the counter they have to work on by 4 inches and over the course of the day they are hurting.  Starbucks, you might want to go back to the drawing board on this design.

Well as Comcast did not yet connect Internet to my new Apt. I became Internet-Less person, it is harsh, unbelievable sad reality ! I never thought I can live 2 days without Internet, after long angry calls with Comcast, I started to analysis my situation, looking around me for wifi places I saw this Starbucks, I know other branches in the city but this one open late on Saturday. I went there with my laptop looking for Internet shelter after Comcast turn us to Internet homeless! With my laptop in my arms I entered the shop and I liked the design, yes it is Starbucks should all look the same but this one look more special to me, more detailed with good finishing touches. the Register was friendly and smiley  asked me what I want, I said caramel macchiato for me  and Internet for my "dog" oops my laptop, to my surprise she told me they have sitting upstairs devoted for Internet hungry laptops, yay! here i go, here i come, finally getting my Internet dose and enjoying my caramel macchiato. The place is clean, very quiet and to my surprise empty! Good privacy and no snoopy eyes into your laptop. I do not usually review brand names but this place definitely deserve every word i wrote it!

around 12:02 to 12:24 friday nov 4 2016 1) i was 2nd in line, and it took over 20 minutes. 2), after saying order and handing 2 bags of coffee to grind, a tall african american female (i say this for owner of the franchise to identify crappy employee) in the line behind me who turned out to be an employee (supervisor?  she was bossing everyone) purchasing stuff after her shift who was getting impatient!!! (because they're so slow!) - repeatedly pressured me to put my chip credit card in before the baristo rang me up and before i even knew the amount (he was off putting coffee in grinder).  She came at me from the side and told me, not asked me, repeatedly.  i asked her to back off because i'm head injured and noise sensitive, and then she stepped more in my space and told me to put my card in the reader again!  until i said, keep it up, and you're going to cost [the barista] his tip, because i felt so powerless and overwhelmed, and she wouldn't back off. (i had two orders on two cards, but she didn't know this - i wasn't sure which one he was going to put through first.)   she then flew in a huff, and said loudly twice to the barista (baristo?)  - "i'm going to ring myself up!"  and went around the counter and did so, and kept picking at me, saying "it's fine lady" (uh, no, it's not).  and said something to barista as she passed by him. 3) she then told the baristo to give the man behind her his order for free (but then another employee rung it up -not sure if he paid, fyi franchise owner). 4) i tipped the baristo, only to find he had wrinkled and not sealed the bags so they leaked all over (incompetent or on purpose? because i had then asked him (not her) if i could put the tip on the card, and she interjected "no," so maybe he thought he wasn't getting a tip). 5.  i've been to many starbucks.  i always tip, and i always feel they're wonderful.  until now.  unbelievable.   worst. starbucks. ever. you know it's slow when the employees so can't bear to wait in their own line they become aggressive toward their customers in front of them.

Pretty average Starbucks as far as service and food goes. Staff was relatively friendly and the location is great. Only wish the tables were bigger because the tight space isn't ideal for studying which is usually the reason I come to Starbucks.
This location makes my drinks right most of the time.   Unfortunately getting in and out of the parking lot when busy is not the easiest.
It seems every time I go to this Starbucks they short me on my drink :( just like this morning, I ordered a venti but when I opened the lid it was the amount of a grande...

Save your money and go to a local cafe. Don't support this toxic work environment. I worked here for over 6 months and it was the absolute worst experience I've ever had working anywhere. Each employee here is overworked, overwhelmed, & underpaid.   Starbucks is a mega corporation thats more than capable of paying their employees more. Not only that, but dear lord, please check up on your management. Creepy bosses, district people coming in a watching you while you work, at least one employee leaves crying everyday. It's ridiculous. If you ever consider working here; don't.

I have never been to this Starbucks where they get my o e drink correct. I drink a vanilla sweet cream cold brew. I ordered just off the menu, nothing special and thus group of idiots can't do it right, NOT EVER.

Hit Reverse & go to the Starbucks in Target!  This is practically impossible for a wheelchair to get through, due to the close high top tables I usually go the Wrong direction & turn into the line there. I think I've seen one chair by the fireplace empty, why are people camping out there??  If you order anything slightly off the menu, they don't handle it well?

Actually, this store is at 1490, not 1468.  I found the crew in a nasty mood this morning....overweight women with bad attitudes and a chip on their shoulders, just going through the motions.  No music and the bathroom was a mess.  This store is a LONG way from Seattle, figuratively and literally!
I like the atmosphere of this starbucks. Plenty of different options for seating- comfy chairs, high top, long tables, small tables!

This Starbucks is on my way to work but I'd rather go anywhere else. Takes absolutely forever and the staff will literally ignore you in line or at the drive thru, no acknowledgement that they even know you're there.

I've been coming to this location for 2 weeks.  Each time I come here the employees spend more time talking to each other rather than to customers.  Not friendly.  I'm here early in the morning right as they open yet it always looks dirty.  Doesn't seem like they clean at closing time and that milk and sugar area is always dirty. This morning the employee used the same towel to clean that area and the rest of the bar areas without rinsing the towel. Yuk.  Time to move on to another coffee shop,  one that isn't so popular where they have pride in their shops.  Starbucks you need to check your shops.
Love the coffee don't go to this location if you are in any hurry you'll be waiting awhile.

I love Starbucks. I love this Starbucks. Best part...the drive thru!! 9 times out of 10 I have the boys with me. I love that I can get my Starbucks on the go. The baristas are great. They are always super friendly. I have also enjoyed coffee inside on a couple occasions. There is plenty of room for everyone. It was very comfortable. My drinks always taste perfect!
The baristas are great! Service is prompt, accurate and consistent. Drive thru operates smoothly with little delay
It's a typical Starscmucks. Nothing really outstanding or disappointing about this place. The area where you pick up your beverages is a bit squishy though.

This place hasn't been reviewed in ages. I've come here quite a bit and they are fabulous. Some Starbucks lack quick service and pleasantries. Not this one. The staff are stupid fast and very nice.   The location itself is well maintained and has tons of seating to accommodate people watching, eavesdropping, networking, coffee breaks and working. This is one of my fave stops on the north side of yeg.   The only issue you may find is parking. The lot can get really busy with the mall patrons. So be on the look out for DB parkers and clueless drivers. It gets hairy in this location.

Super friendly service and made great suggestions for drinks for my wife (who cannot have caffeine due to her pregnancy).  I go to a lot of Starbucks, and this one is a great example of customer service.

This is a busy little Starbucks, but a fun one. Good music is always playing, strong WiFi, and nine out of ten times they spell my name correctly on the cups (haha).  The patio out on the side is a nice touch on the warm days, a good place to have a snack with your dog.  I like it here, and I will be back.

Me like Starbucks, yes I said Me... just because!!!!  This is my Starbucks location that I frequent when I can't go to the location near Lake Avenue.  I do like it here as I pass this way weekly, sometimes 2 or even 3 times a week.  The drive thru, while small & kinda off the beaten path, it is very useful.   Friendly baristas, outside seating & a WalMart nearby, make this a great location.  I especially like the outdoor seating as there are beach sized umbrellas that are affixed to the table that provide shade of a uber hot & sunny day.  It would be super, duper nice to have power strips in this area; I hope the Starbucks-wish-granting-fairies are listening to (or reading) this review.  Spacing inside is fairly nice as well.  A nice sitting area to the left & right, then straight ahead is the main, cafeteria style table & chair setups.    I love that there's always something on sale, albeit coffee mugs, drinking straws, coffee etc; makes me feel like buying another mug/cup that I really don't need... Great marketing you guys. ( I know this is probably the norm at all Starbucks, but this location always seems to have the cutest little finds)  Anywho, I love the soy, skinny etc options; but  must admit I often forget the tall, grande, venti, trenta designations & I normally result to saying small, medium... My bad, I know, oh well, I'll try harder.  Anyway, I love it here, so do be surprised if you're passing thru & you see me posted up...

I gets coffee here on the reg. Service is tight. Food is grrrd. Drive thru is sick. Other patrons are legit. I like this store. Just one mega ish that my wife got is their inability to keep cold brew flowin. What's up with that?! Besides that, I consider the Irvington Starbucks to be the best in Indiana and I've been to several other ones.

I really love this Starbucks!  1. It has a drive through which I feel is essential for busy days on the run and to not have to drag my cranky or sleepy kids in. Thank you for working the drive thru into your design. It definitely gives me an excuse to stop by more!   2. I feel like almost every Starbucks team is genuinely helpful and has excellent customer service...but I really like the team here. They crack jokes with customers and can carry on a good conversation. They are sweet and courteous. They make the atmosphere of this Starbucks more enjoyable.    3. The manager, Mel, took the time to find out exactly how I wanted my coffee ground for my cold brew. She even ground two samples so I could check out the coarseness before grinding the whole bag. She offered suggestions on other Starbucks coffee she has tried cold brew with and had good results. I can't wait to try the Kenya roast she recommended. I feel like personalizing the customers experience goes beyond wanting to give good customer service; it shows you care about your job and are passionate sharing it with others. I LOVE that.  4. The place is always spotless. I always see the employees making sure everything is neat, organized and filled. Thank you for that!  I truly feel that employees at this location love life and love people and that just naturally radiates from them. I say ... give them all a raise!

Comfortable Star$$$ in the Acme Shopping Center just south of Bordentown off Route 130.    Not a fan of their burnt approach to java, but sometimes it is the only option in the vicinity.  The Flat White was fine, but the prices are designed to make the stockholders happier than the customers.    This shop was well laid out with ample table and comfortable seating around.  No problem with the staff.  Everyone was in a good mood this mid-week late morning.  Several quiet conversations and solitary students around and about.

This Starbucks is absolutely disappointing. I live in Carpinteria and dread every time I have to go to this Starbucks when I leave to go down south in the morning. I go to this place hoping it will be better than the last time I went but it never ceases to disappoint me. I went recently this time I mobile ordered. I ordered a Venti Iced Coffee with 2 added shots, light ice, and half and half but what I got was extra ice and no half and half. Not only that but my second order I ordered a grande double shot with light ice, pumps of white mocha and half and half. What I got back was a double shot with the pumps but Extra ice and no half and half. Again once disappointed. Never going back

Went in around 10 and had the worst service . Waited 30 minutes for an iced green tea . Watched workers laugh and talk while slowly making drinks . They should have called in more people if they were  short handed .

This is a refreshing change from the Starbucks in Safeway at 97 St. and 137 Ave. Overall a pretty nice place to work, but there are only two tables conducive to setting up a laptop and doing some work.  While there aren't many tables inside, if you like working outside on a patio, this Starbucks has one of the best I've seen.  They have five tables on a large patio and a couple of big umbrellas. The road next to it isn't that noisy and the neighbourhood is relatively quiet. A nice setting to work or relax in.   I've been to this Starbucks a few times now and it's one of my favourites. Doesn't hurt that it's not too far from home.  Busyness - Early afternoon here means the traffic is slow and the store is pretty well empty, leaving a good choice of seats. The drive-thru seems to be pretty busy though.  Noisiness - The music, as in many Starbucks, is ever-present, but it's not an especially noisy place. Today the choice is latin music. I don't know whether to work or take a siesta. I'm sure on a hot day the blenders would be humming and really make the place noisy.  Ambience - I like the layout and colour scheme of this store. It's relaxing and if you like cool, the air conditioning setting is low here. It's like a refrigerator.   Power - Plenty of choices for seating and outlets available.  Free wifi - It's available and works well.  Staff - Lots of friendly staff at this location.  Seating - There are 17 seats inside, with four comfortable armchairs available. There are only two tables inside that are worth using a laptop on. There are seven bar seats that you can set up your laptop at, if you like working that way.  Security - It seems to be a pretty good neighbourhood.

The only thing good about this location was that the two ladies working it were very friendly. Long lines and slow service. The prices were so much higher than any other Starbucks. They crossed out the prices with black sharpie in the display case and increased them when putting in the register. An egg wrap and a latte was $20.

I used to spend a lot of money here. Worst experience today at this Starbucks at Hillsborough & Memorial in Tampa. They ground my coffee incorrectly, argued with me, and refused to correct it unless I bought something else. Blew my mind. They were incompetent, rude, condescending, disrespectful and greedy.

The best customer service ever!!! Ms.Debra, Marty, Kelly, and Greg are awesome!!! Everyone knows what I drink and it's ready at checkout! They always greet everyone with a smile and a pleasant greeting!!
Always clean, very friendly service and conveniently located. Every time I stopped in , the staff is fast and helpful. They also love pets in the drive through.

As I write this review I'm starting to get concerned about the amount of these stores I patronize locally.  Maybe I should cut back a little (and perhaps the caffeine is why I am writing this at 11:45PM?).  FIrst off, these guys are quicker than many of the other SBUXs in town. I love it. Secondly, they have a drive through which is a big plus.  It's great if you work in that center because you can pop off to your office.  However, if you are trying to get back on S. McCarran you need to make a tight u-turn to pull it off (or drive up to Lakeside).  One thing I'd like to mention.  There are several businesses over here and people conduct a lot of business at this SBUX.  It's not a place i would go to talk about confidential matters but you'll hear it all the time.  The seating area in the back is somewhat 'detached' from the front of the store so any conversations you have back there are louder.  Just a note.
Always enjoy coffee and pastries. Great staff cleaned facility which is warm and inviting. Heck ya I'll be back

Normally very good service and I have been impressed many times. The service is normally spot on but today the lady that took my order was very abrupt and short. Maybe I caught her on a bad day or maybe she was just in the weeds but it doesn't. Take too long to smile and be a bit more personable.

I'm new to the area, but I travel often around the country and have never had an issue with my normal Starbucks order. Always tastes the same across the board. Except here. 3 times in a row my drink was made and it was gross. They are always SUPER slow. So taste your drink before you leave or drive off. They will probably have to make it again.

Not a huge fan of Starbucks in general, overpriced and sometimes bitter coffee. With that said, I do drop by for a coffee fix and lemon loaf from time to time. This location is close to me and the people are always very nice. Parking is good and traffic getting in and out it smooth.

Great service! It was fast and has a drive though. We ordered multiple drinks and food items and it was all correct! Way better than waiting in lines out the door at other locations as it is off the beaten path.
Great drinks and food. Awesome/ cozy place to come and get work done and even meet up with friends!

This is by far the slowest Starbucks I have been too and after waiting at the drive thru for 11 minutes for the 4 cars in front of me, they inform me that they don't have the gluten free sandwich I ordered.  This is the 2nd time that has happened to me at that location (but didn't wait 11 minutes the first time)

The lesser of the two uptown starbucks locations, based purely on seating.    There are lots of great local alternatives that serve better coffee with FREE wifi.  Local options close by include: PJs (literally two storefronts away toward downtown) CCs (magazine and jefferson) Rue de la Course (oak and carrollton)

It's open, and something happened to it. This has always been my favorite Starbucks, and I frequent many, this one was a little crowded, but everyone was always nice. They closed it and remodeled it, and now it is lovely inside, but the employees all got grumpy.   I've been several times and there is no joy. No one smiles, all the nice people seem to be gone and they took all of the cranky people that don't really want to work and put them all in one Starbucks.   Use your mobile app so that the coffee is ready when you get there and you don't have to talk to the employees. It will work out better that way. The facility is terrific.
Live in Speedway, and been looking for a closer location to use laptop publicly, so Im happy for this location. Employees are nice and friendly!!!

Love the atmosphere at this one especially the upstairs which is rarely so crowded that you can't find some space. You can sit and get work done--plus people watch out the window in one of the best spots in the city. Baristas at this one are pretty nice and some of the quicker ones in the city . My one compliant is that there is NEVER toilet paper in the bathroom, but I think this is just a Starbucks "thang" Girl at counter super nice about it but don't understand why no one stocks this regularly even when they are not busy. I've gotten stuck without several times so I always check now lol!
I come here regularly due to location and staff. I have to sit outside because this is one of the coldest Starbucks ever. I get the corporate controls the AC but it gets ridiculous!
Clean, spacious and quiet. I wish this is just around the block where i live.. Theres also a patio to hangout and breathe the clean air of santa barbara.

This is a large, clean, well-run starbuck on the corner of Marsh & Silverside roads in Wilmington.  We usually stop by in the morning - and even at the height of the AM rush, the lines move quickly.  Fourbucks, Charbucks, whatever yoiu call it - it's nice tohave one around when you need it.  Especially in the Culinary wasteland of North Wilmington.

It's like any other Starbucks. I'm pleased with its service each time I'm there. My go-to drinks are green tea fraps and vanilla ice latte.   The place however is a bit messy. The tables are not wiped down and chairs are all over the place.
Always love to visit this location.  They are always so helpful and accommodating.  They never seems to disappoint.  This is probably my favorite Starbuck locations in Tucson!!!

The new starbucks all seem to be very "open concept" which is a little weird to me...  I would think they'd want more of a cozy ambiance, but instead they build large blocks.  But that's not the end of the world.  I'm just starting to think that Starbucks DOESN'T want me hanging out all day using their free wifi... sheesh... the nerve!  The coffee is what it is... nothing different from any other starbucks.  you either like it or you don't.  The people here don't make it any better or worse.  The people behind the counter could be a little friendlier, but hey... we're outside of philly... I'm not expecting southern hospitality.    I L-O-V-E the tables that have outlets in them.  I might get one for my house.  Or office.  Or both.    The bathrooms are probably my biggest complaint.  First off... they chose to designate the bathrooms (the old broomall sb didn't do so) which I find mildly annoying.  And the bathrooms are just single-person usage... so I've had to wait for a while when someone's been in the restroom for an overly long period of time.  I suppose the caffeine does have that effect on people...  and really... shouldn't that just be common sense?!?  A place that sells caffeinated beverages should design their restrooms to INCREASE waiting times.  Just saying... could lead to a disaster!  I haven't used the drive through.  From what I've heard, it can be hit or miss.

Whatever guy is working right now.. it's 6pm on Wednesday night, 12/6, is the MOST RUDE Starbucks employee I've ever encountered. I can't believe I'm even writing a review but I've never had this happen at Starbucks. I know these jobs are hard to come by as Starbucks is a phenomenal employer, so whoever the dude is working the Starbucks drive thru on Robb needs to at least treat people with some form of kindness. I'm in disbelief..
I will just say if going to this Starbucks location use mobile order and pay. It will save you a lot of aggravation.

Not doing to well. Maybe Starbucks should have some barista training. There's no cleanliness, no pride, and no hurry what so ever. But at least the product was made to my liking and Starbucks standards. Maybe have 3 on duty at all times or train them to care more.

My service was fine but there's a pet store next to it causing the lobby to smell like dog. No one seemed to have a problem with the smell but I don't have a dog which made me nauseous to the smell. Again service was fine.

So I went into this Starbucks on a lovely sunny day making me very parched I ordered a large refresher and a protein box and was hoping for a large water. I was then told by one of the workers that they were only doing grande waters for the day... like honey pls I am not Arianna Grande I need me a big water so that I can play a good day of tennis smh. I was not pleased and wished that they knew the money I spend at this place and that I feel that if I ask for a large water they should give me one because I bought other things... not mad just disappointed

Friendliest service I've ever received at a Starbucks- I was on a long, tiring trip from Texas to Ohio and I stopped by this place to charge my phone.  The barista at the register made sure to put a smile on my face and force a laugh or two, and everyone else seemed to chime in.  I felt like the baristas here were more interested in my life than I was!  I'll definitely remember this stop for future travels.

I used to love coming to this Starbucks but I think with new management, I don't think I'll be coming back to this location too often. The new manager is very hot or cold. He'll be nice one day and the next, he would be extremely rude. The baristas and the atmosphere is wonderful when the manager is not in.
A unique Starbucks. An upstairs and superb people watching. We were on vacation staying at a hotel a few doors down and came here a few times. Really friendly staff.

Just came here recently couple days ago. Wow I am amazed at how fast they serve your drinks! The staff are also nice here and a friend of mine actually works at this location as well.Great teamwork here! Always clean and staff are prompt and ready to take customer orders of all the times I've visited here. Never had a problem with my order or the taste of my coffee!!

I Love this new location! The layout is great, the staff us friendly, and brisk with your order! The chairs and furniture is super comfy as well! I tried the new Passion Fruit Iced Tea Lemonade (shaken) and it was the best thing I have ever drank! I am hooked on them now! I also love the Strawberry Creme Frappuchino!   I also love the patio! It was so nice and warm outside, and it was awesome to sit outside, in the shade with my Lemonade, and visit with my friends!   Also, this location has speakers outside, which plays very nice music, and leaves a nice background noise for customers to enjoy if they are outside sitting at the patio by themselves. The only suggestion I have for them, is to turn down the music a bit, in the cafe, it seems to be a little loud, and makes you have to talk above normal levels for your friends to be able to hear you.  I also must add in that starbucks recently changed 90% of their recipes to take out all artificial flavorings, and preservatives!!! Now their muffins come made with real FRESH berries, and instead of syrup flavorings in their fruit drinks, they now come made with fresh fruit juice! Very nice for people like me who are *trying* to cut back on refined foods, and junk!   Overall, this is my favorite location, and I definentaly plan on coming back!

It's Starbuck's so you know what to expect. The staff are friendly and fast. This seems like a popular location as there is often a line mid-morning. It's comfortable to sit outside as there are lots of spots in the shade and they have fans too. One neat thing about this Starbuck's location is the Katrina water line painted on the outside of the shop.

I admit I occasionally  stop at a Starbucks,  but this is not my norm. And I will even admit I enjoy some of their coffee & tea beverages. The food, I am not very impressed with, as it all appears cookie cutter style to me & that just isn't my thing.  On my last visit I did enjoy my cappuccino, it was hot, frothy and had a robust flavor. I can't fault the beverages. My problem comes with the overabundance of locations. Ok, offer free wifi & a particular  type of crowd will come. All of the restaurants  are basically  the same & just vary in size. Are they really out to take over the entire coffee industry, of enjoying your favorite  brew at a public location?  Personally mass comercialism, is not my style. Yes, I plead guilty to stopping occasionally & am usually furious with myself for giving into convince & paying way too much for a beverage. Give me a family dinner, a good cup of Joe & I am a happy patron. Friends, enough with the gifts of gift cards. If I didn't  have the gift cards, it would be easier to keep on driving. To those who love Starbucks, definitely  your choice, as is mine to keep driving the next time. What's a girl to do?  Too many of the same anything, just screams monopoly, this is still the USA & we have choices.

This is a good location and the folks here do a great job. I've been here when it's busy and when it's not. (Of course, it's difficult to greet and chat with every customer when the line is 15 deep and there are 10 cars in the drive-thru. I say this because I've read other reviews for this location where people were kvetching that they didn't get a back-rub when they walked in...) This location was recently remodeled. It's more open, seems more spacious, with more natural light and has a simpler layout. The new  furniture is not comfortable. It looks like somebody went crazy shopping at IKEA. Gone are the comfy chairs and couches. Doesn't all-powerful Starbucks  want customers to "hang out at Starbucks?"

This location is hit or miss. The morning staff seems to be pretty good, quick and know their stuff. The evening staff are rude, slow and unapologetic. I also believe this location is understaffed. The drinks are also hit or miss, never know if it's going to be made correctly. I can tell when they leave the cup of fresh espresso sitting too long because they get so busy in the evening and can't keep up. I highly recommend ordering ahead on the mobile app if you want your coffee within a timely manner....
Dirty, slow service, rude, and after waiting 15 mins for a frap, they got the order wrong.

I have a good friend who works downtown and when we want to get together for a quick coffee over his lunch break this has been our spot for about a year now. Unlike alot of places downtown it boasts a good sized free parking lot making it easy to get in and out of. Naturally however when you have a good location it draws a crowd and this one can get quite hectic especially in the mornings and at lunch time.  Seating is limited at this location (probably one of the smallest stand alone stores I have seen) which I don't think makes alot of sense for the amount of business they do. It's still a good location though,no matter how busy they get I have always gotten my drink quickly. Drinks are pretty standard no matter which Starbucks you go to and I have no issues with quality here. I come back here due to the warm personal attachment I get from being able to meet with a good friend however there are so many coffee shops downtown you wont miss out if you never make your way down to this one but there is no need to go running and screaming in the other direction either. Just your everyday run of the mill three star experience.

Yes, I hate to admit it but these baristas here- Sure know my order by now. Living from 1600 Walnut to S. Philly, I still come back every night to order my Paleo Version'd Coffee Black with a Stick of Butter. They get it right every time. Why wouldn't they? Many people look at me like I'm nuts but this Starbucks makes me feel at home. My location at best! Great workers, in fact I'm friends with a few now! Drinks expensive? No way. Same price as DD. I'm such a fan of this Starbucks!!

I like the location of this Starbucks, and it holds a lot of memories. Bf and I used to play the game of life on my iPhone after work here before we were dating. The staff are friendly and while it's usually pretty busy I find there are lots of places to sit. The neighbourhood is safe so there are no complaints from me on this location, there is even a decent amount of street parking and parking around back!

A downtown Franklin staple.  Get your coffee and relax with friends or walk to the theater, Sweet CeCe's for some frozen yogurt, or down to the square for shopping, or drive to the Factory.  On the first Friday night of each month, get your coffee and head out to see the Art Scene displays in a variety of venues around the downtown area--great night treat.

Standard issue starbucks.  Coffee tasted about the same as most of the other starbucks I've been to.  The seating area inside and outside is relatively large.  No drive thru, prices were about the same, and the friendliness factor at starbucks is generally bordering weird as it is.

If I could put zero stars I would. Normally I go to the Starbucks on 42 (almost everyday) and they have always had outstanding service. On the other hand, today my boyfriend and I were closer to this location, even though I've been to this one and didn't care for the service before, i decided to give it another shot. We go to the drive thru and I order my usual, a warmed butter croissant and a venti iced coffee with cream and sugar..  The croissant was Luke warm and the iced coffee was filled with no sugar. So I decide to go inside and have them correct the mistake. The gentlemen who took care of us at the window quickly approached me along with another young man who I assumed must've been the manager. I let them know there was no sugar in my coffee, also the label said with milk so I questioned that as well.. Instead of saying sorry about that, let's make you a new one, I got nothing but a cocky attitude and excuses; again I thought nothing of it.. Then the guy that's remaking my coffee is done quickly hands me the drink and walks away.. I immediately notice that not only is my cup is completely filled with ice and the coffee is an inch away from the top.. I was so frustrated that I just walked out, knowing that I just spent $4 on ice.. Very disappointing.. Will never come here again, Two thumbs down for sure

There's a consistent taste at this Starbucks. It would do better with a drive thru, especially when there are parking restrictions in Haddonfield. I do like stopping here when I'm not busy. They've never screwed up my drink and the baristas are always caffeinated, happy, and friendly.
No one behind the counter for more than 5 minutes. So, I went next door to panera. Has Starbucks gone humanless already? Not ready for that yet Starbucks :/

Though I usually avoid Starbucks in loyalty to my other fave coffeehouses, the sheer convenience of its close proximity to my office and its early morning hours make it tempting for me to stop in when I'm running supremely late to work.  Without this branch, I'd be just another super java-deprived grump at the office, givin' my coworkers hell.  In all honesty, this place does stand out from the rest.  Every other location should be modeled on the awesomeness of this store -- great placement on the corner of a gorgeous building in Old City, staffed by some super friendly folks with remarkable coffee-to-person matching skills, and a clean and well-decorated environment with local florist offerings at the windows.  Difficult to find parking at times, being Old City parking, but don't try to park illegally for even five minutes since the PPA is always on the take here.
Love Starbucks coffee. Can spend hours reading and doing my work on laptop. Free wifi..! And yes nothing like a great cup of coffee along with it..!

I find myself more critical of Starbucks, or national coffee chains than local.  So I need to be thoroughly impressed to give Starbucks five stars and I just wasn't on this visit.  There was an uncommonly long line and the drinks seemed to take longer than I come to expect at other locations.  A major plus to this location is that there is plenty of seating, a lot more than other locations, which is great, because Starbucks are always packed.  The noise level was proper as I was able to converse with my business contact with ease.  Again they didn't wow me like I require of Starbucks for five stars, but they did not disappoint, so I will continue to meet here and at other locations throughout our wonderful city!
Worst Starbucks in the world.  Cold sandwiches, Slow, dirty, rude and always mess ups.

I live and work around several Starbucks and although it may be slightly out of my way at the time, I will ALWAYS come to the Starbucks on 5520 Calgary Trail. Bren, the barista, is an absolute treat. I feel working at a coffee shop you need to be in a positive mood dealing with grumpy bears before their morning coffee and Bren really sets the bar.  He takes the time to get to know you and always calls me 'friend' which I find makes it more personal! Haha...  My coffee is always done proper and in a timely manner. Not an overly busy coffee shop either so to sit down and get work done isn't much of an issue.
Excelent service  good caffe I will go here all the time Now this will my my library  And caffe of course  Thank you

Just one visit here, but the staff is not friendly at all. Most Sbux have reasonably friendly Baristas who make eye contact and smile. Not this location. The attitude is just, 'give me your order, get it, get out!". We found another location the following day with people that enjoy life!

Every time I come to this Starbucks they always screw my order up and the funny thing is I'm there daily.   My boyfriend goes in as well but they happen to always give it to him for free.  I always order no whip somehow they always put whip. This last time they put soy milk and whip. There employees need to start repeating orders to there customers.  Drive through is always busy around 7am-10pm so that could be why. I see it's a great place for meetings due to the business area

Same great coffee as every other Starbucks. The location is great, right smack dab in the middle of suburbia. They recently expanded the dining area which made it nearly double the size it originally was, which is awesome; however, there is still a horrible parking lot situation and drive thru. I often place my order on the app and like to run in and grab my drink off the counter (usually much faster than the drive thru) but the problem is there's never anywhere to park to run inside. It's a shame because I really like this location. The drive thru doesn't help either, it often wraps around the building through the parking lot, only to block more of the parking spaces. I think the solution is building another location on Hazel Dell, north of 146th St. if anyone from Starbucks Corp. wants to chat about exactly where I'd like them to build, let me know!

I stopped here and got a refreshing cold teavanna iced tea (peach citrus white tea) based on the recommendation of the staff member who was working here when we visited (part of the reason for the visit was that my companion needed to use the restroom). The beverage was really quite good -- better than I had imagined. It was a good choice for a hot day. I'm thankful for the beverage recommendation.

If I can give a zero I would the employees here were horrible. I am here for a work trip and the cashier named "Frida" and the barista "Steve" were extremely rude and mocked me. Never have I had such horrible customer service at Starbucks. I simply asked for a cool lime refresher with extra limes she kept going back and forth with me. Then later I hear her and Steve mocking me and laughing. Disgusting service!

I come here because it's the closest thing I got to my work place at the moment. It does the job, you know what to expect because it's Starbucks.  However, I don't think this is one of the better locations.  I mean, I've been to busier locations with fewer staff that move the drinks faster, and I'm taking into account the drive thru line too. The staff are generally very nice and accommodating, but not so much when they are busy, that leads to grouchiness and sometimes rudeness. This location has very nice indoor seating and the perk is a drive thru, but I go inside more often.  Downside, slow in delivering drinks, even when it's a downtime, and they've also gotten my drink wrong on several occasions.
many peaple just come use the wifi end didn't buy any thats crazy stupid.they ocuped nice seat n buy nothing .

I love the Pumpkin Spice Latte and it always comes out a week before my birthday! It is the Epidemy of fall! As soon as Pumpkin spice latte comes out you know fall is coming and the flavor is rich sweet and pumpkin! The thing about Starbucks' Pumpkin Spice latte that makes it so special is that they use real pumpkin in their latte. I have to say it's as good as it gets and at this location they are quick and accurate in getting you your order the way you want with a smile and friendly face to greet you! What more could you want!?

The slowest Starbucks I have ever been to. Always 10 cars in line and it takes a good 20 mins to get to the window. And once at the window, my drink still isn't ready. I use to work at Starbucks and making these drinks isn't a difficult task.
Love my Skinny Vanilla Latte.  Always have quick, friendly and accurate service. I also love the outside seating.  One of the better Starbucks locations.  The new decor is nice.

I come to this Starbucks a lot because of it's convenient location. However, it seems as though the service gets slower and slower every time. The baristas here would never survive at the on campus Starbucks at university's like FSU that I go to most often.  They were out of the food I wanted but it was late afternoon so that is understandable. They got my drink right once it finally came out. Overall a pretty average Starbucks experience.
The workers are very nice but sooooo slow.  It is consistently the slowest Starbucks I've ever been to.

Most Starbucks are fairly standard since it's a chain but this is the one I seem to frequent the most. There's nothing about this Starbucks that makes it stand out aside from its location (right on the corner of Jasper and 109ave). In the summers they set out patio sets, which makes it a great place to just hang out, have a coffee/drink and just people watch or read.   The menu is the same as any other Starbucks and they have the same products. The barristas have always been super friendly and willing to fix a drink if there is something wrong. I think the only thing I would change about this Starbucks is that I wish it was bigger and had more spaces inside to sit. Since its so central, it's usually busy and at peak times it's quite difficult to find somewhere to sit.

This Starbucks need more employees . They are short staffed and take forever to get a drink out. Also they don't make the drinks and they are. They taste burnt and nasty ! This is the worst Starbucks ever !! I hate coming here but it's the closest to my house ! Not happy at all .

I've been going to Starbucks for a long long time. I even went to the original in Seattle just because I could. Tonight, I found my ideal treat!  The store was mostly empty near closing time I suspect so we had a relaxed time chatting with the barista. My wonderful wife ordered a chili mocha with whipped cream. As I watched her make it, my my mind was thinking "Mmmmm. Whipped cream. Good. Must have some now."  Never being one to resist an impulse, I asked "Could I have a small cup of whipped cream?" She said, "Sure, you can order it any time! It's called a puppaccino!"   I was kinda stunned having never heard this ersatz-Italian-word before. She explained, people order this for their puppy! Not really being a dog-person, I said "I'm going to call it a Daveaccino instead!"  Mmmmm Whipped cream Good

Who doesn't love coffee??? We visited today because it was the first day of the "holiday" seasonal drinks and I LOvE the peppermint mocha & couldn't wait to get my hands on one! Then, to my surprise they had free reusable holiday cups today! This location has a drive through and is right off of Eastlake road making it the perfect pit stop to get your energy fix. The drive through had a long line but, the coffee was totally worth the wait!  The seasonal offerings provided by the baristas at Starbucks help to keep things new and exciting. Just when you think, how sad no more pumpkin spice lattes.... bam peppermint mochas and toasted whites chocolate lattes!! They also have delicious seasonal cookies with this amazing icing that my kids just can't get enough of!

This location is my absolute favorite Starbucks! I am a coffee fanatic and this one provide consistently good coffees, excellent, fast service with a smile! I prefer to go through the drive-thru because I am on the go, I think it's fab! I will be going again!

Everytime i come to this location, Staff are extremely rude. There is no courtesy. Its like a  like a bar.  the only sound I hear in this coffee shop are the employees constantly talking to each other. So loud! Nonstop loud talking amongst each other! Very unprofessional.   I go to starbucks everyday and this is by far the worst experience. Had to post after several bad experience at the same store.

The drive thru takes an eternity to get through.  When I got to the counter to pay, I noted how long I had been waiting (just for a tea!).  The girl who was there said "it's happy hour, so it going to take a long time."  Does the manager not know that it's happy hour?  Can they bring in extra staff?  I should note that it was last day of a month of every day happy hours, so they should be prepared by now.  I usually get this attitude from anyone working at this location.  I don't go on a good day, it will ruin my mood.

Thanks for being open at 515. Good to know as we were headed to the Airport. When asked about the protein packs they were eagerly making them in the back. Quick to help out. Clean place & good seating options available.
Always get my drink right. Always with a smile. It makes my day when I get my tea in the morning from this place! It is a fave! Do recommend it highly!
Love this store. We often in warm weather ride our horses there for coffee in the afternoon!  Fun!  Staff and guests love the surprise guests!

Extraordinary slow. I counted 7 people working and only one person was making drinks. They were having a great time laughing and talking to each other while ignoring the customers in line. It took 15 minutes from the time I ordered for them to make a latte.
Quick, friendly baristas make your drink right and get you on your way. If you're drinking your coffee here, parking can be a beast.
The most convenient Starbucks on campus. You'll have to try to avoid the crowds though.

This Starbucks is a bit different from the others I have been to. The staff is super friendly and put forth an effort to learn your name and what you like quickly.  They are not 100% perfect but they do everything they can to make it right when they do make a mistake.     It's usually a quick visit, even during the busy times. The team works expeditiously to get all orders out.

Ok so I am a diehard Starbucks fan. I was way excited when they opened this one because it's super close to me and now i don't have to go inside target to get one of my fav morning pleasures. That said, I am really sad to say this Starbucks has consistently fallen a short of the excellence Starbucks is known for. There are usually extra long wait times (regardless what time you come in), the last 4 of 5 times I've come here they got my order wrong or just given me someone else's order accidentally. I've ordered via the app, walk in and drive thru but it doesn't matter - they are consistently inaccurate. Literally every time I've come inside, they were out of raw sugar at the bar, so I have to go to the counter and ask for it. I hope they turn it around because the way it's going now, I prob won't keep coming back much longer. I am not one to complain and I don't put in difficult orders - but there comes a point when even the most faithful customer will get tired and walk away. I'm almost to this point and it sucks.  PLEASE GET IT TOGETHER Starbucks. Please.

This is my favorite Starbucks location in all of SJ and Philadelphia. Outdoor seating is a very enjoyable feature, and the interior is spacious and inviting. Have never experienced a bad barista here. There are two bathrooms with average cleanliness. Speed of drive through seems on par with other Starbucks.

I was looking for a comfortable spot to have a coffee and do some work on my computer, which meant I was looking specifically for a place with a lot of plugins as I have low battery power on the laptop.  As I was close to the new Starbucks on Calgary Trail, I decided to pop in.  The first impression was great as they were pumping their music to the outside seats, making for a lively parking lot!   The space is fairly small though with a community table, a handful of smaller tables and six seats up at the bar by the window. It looks like all of the community seats, along with the bar seats have great access to not only power plugs but also USB portals! I definitely feel like I got the best seat in the house as I pulled up to the bar and plugged in my computer and phone at the same time.   The staff were super pleasant and I can see why a lot of people would pop in here. It was 3/4 full when I got there around 3pm on a Sunday but by the time I left two hours later, I think there were only three or four of us there, making for a quiet space. Drive thru was constantly busy as it's in a great location.
