I am completely addicted to Starbucks- especially since they launched their mobile ordering. My weekly purchases must have increased 200% as a result of mobile ordering. However - I've had the throw out 3 breakfast sandwiches in the past 3 weeks from this location. Although I indicate NO CHEESE on my mobile order - they sometimes do not remove the cheese. I am highly ALLERGIC to daily due to a serious medical condition (not just lactose intolerant - a true allergy). Disappointed that this location does not consider serious food allergies in their preparation.

Came here for the first time today and went through the drive thru, the wait was about 10 minutes after ordering, but this is not uncommon for any Starbucks location. I ordered two drinks, one came out perfect and the other was incorrect, but I was already home before I noticed and I was too far to go back.  I would ordinarily give less stars for this, but the staff at this location are SO FRIENDLY! Both baristas I spoke with seemed really happy as did the person who spoke to me from the drive thru speaker. The owners must be doing something right to have such happy people working for them, it's really nice to see workers enjoying what they do.

This is an awful Starbucks. It's consistently terrible service with rude employees and the drinks are never correct. I was in there today and they didn't have any blonde Espresso. Really? I also asked for whipped cream and when I got my drink there was no whipped cream. So, I sent the drink back asking for whipped cream. The barista said "well, it does not say that on the cup".  And I was like I know, that's why am telling you now. Because they weren't listening to me up there. And you can lose the attitude. Why?!! Why are all of the employees here so rude? Why can't they also just get a drink correct?  The line is also always way too long for the drive-through.  It's pretty bad when there is a Starbucks inside the Target next-door and the employees there tell me they have people bringing drinks to them to be fixed that they got from this Starbucks.
I go to this location when I am in the 109 Street area. I wish it was a bit larger as it is difficult to find a place to sit when it is busy.

I want to point out that I am a coffee lover first and a loyal Starbucks customer.This Starbucks is one of the best around. They have employees that have been working there for years and treat you like family. Sandy, whom you can almost always find at the cash register always greets you with a smile, she is one of the most pleasant persons you could ever meet. Don't forget about the manager Isa, he always goes the extra mile for his customers. If Howard Schultz, the Starbucks founder stopped by this Starbucks location, even he would be impressed with the quality and service.

This is one of my favourite Starbucks in the city. It is also my most frequented since it is on my way to and from work. I stop here usually 2-3 times a week for a snack or great coffee. I only usually go through the drive thru but the staff is really good at taking accurate and quick orders. The wait time is very minimal most of the time.   The staff are really friendly and I have yet to have a bad drink here. The prices and selection is the standard Starbucks selection. On the inside they have a "Bar" seating area by the windows so you can look out at the Calgary Trail traffic. There is a large community table with power outlets for electronics and they have free wifi which is nice. The store isn't usually super busy but it depends on the time of day you go.   In the summer time they have tables and chairs outside on their small patio area so customers can enjoy the weather.   They also have a twitter account and usually tweet about specials or sales going on in the store which I think is a great way to reach out to their customers.   I am a Starbucks addict and I will be back again. Probably after I finish this review.
They've always gotten my order right, but for a Starbucks they're a little slow. Their staff all seem to be nice though. No real complaints :)

Enjoying a sbux iced "reserve" blend - Dragon, I believe. Super dark, plenty robust.....apparently this new "line" is only available at certain locations (I work in Wayne, PA, and they informed me there was none in that area that carried the Reserve line. Tastes like REAL coffee!!!!

Seriously worst Starbucks experience so far. Ordered a pour over, the male cashier didn't have proper change, 3 other employees just standing there... I asked if someone else could start the pour over while he saw to the change. I was given just a regular coffee being told it was a blonde... this is ten minutes in. Then I have to wait for 1/2 and 1/2. 15 minutes in no change yet OLD COFFEE THAT turned grey when adding the cream. My son comes in to look for me because It has been so long. I tell them the coffee is old, I get told it's not, bologna, smelled old, looked old... none of them looked like they were going to do anything but let me leave with old coffee, I asked for the originals nail order of a pour over... but from how slow it had already been and how long it had already taken I asked for my money back. Girls leaning against the register... waiting for change for ten minutes! Manager aloof and childishly laughing about it with employees.  Not good Starbucks! No wonder at all why this is rated a TWO!!!

Renovation is a positive , my only negative is that there is always a long line during peak times and there is no urgency of the team that people have things to do. Moving at a glacial pace. I've been to many SB in NYC and Phila and they run like a German train. Not sure what the issue is ?

Hands down, this is the WORST sb ever!  My husband and I always get the same drinks... this place NEVER...EVER gets it right!  It's either too bitter, too diluted, or too much breve. I've even received my ICED coffee WITHOUT ICE!! After many... MANY complaints to the customer service department ... even an in-person meeting with the AREA MANAGER, we have officially given up on this place. We have been very patient and they just can't get it right. There have been numerous times that we've even brought our drinks up the street to the SB on Robb & Mae Ann for (what I have defined as) "coffee repairs".  Simply awful :(

consistently the slowest service and lackadaisical staff of any starbucks. even when a line of 5-8 people are present they did not open a second register. strangely  there were at least 5 people behind the counter

They are always busy. Most of the time you don't wait but sometimes they can be VERY slow. The drive through is kinda behind the building. One time I left my purse at home but they insisted giving me the coffee without paying which was very nice of them.

Do you want to be late for work? Do you want to wait 30 damn minutes for a single cup of coffee? Look no further than the absolute worst Starbucks in Reno! Shoutout to you guys for mixing my walk-in order with the drive-thru orders! Making me wait 30 minutes for a single cup of coffee! I'm now late to work thanks to you guys! I came thirty minutes early specifically to give myself time for coffee and still be on time and if not, early. I'm now late thanks to you! I will be telling everyone I know to NEVER go to this store again. There wasn't even a line. Just lazy workers!

Incredibly rude barista, never really experienced that at a Starbucks. After ordering my drink I asked him where the restroom was and he pointed in a general direction and said "over there". He attempted to give me the wrong drink and after saying it wasn't mine he told me it must have been someone else with my name. I was the only person in the drink que... After telling my it was the wrong drink i responded with an "ok cool", and he said "wow, you're sassy" which was very interesting. His friend was standing nearby likely waiting for him to get off work which I'm sure is the reason he was trying to show off. No big issue but I really don't like when people act super rude for no reason and get away with it. He said his name was Tyler, but his co-worker Sam looked at him weird when he said that. Was probably not a real name.

Very convenient location in Town & Country, so this place gets very busy since early morning. Nice interior remodeling, new look but not so comfortable furniture, who knows why..? Some staff members are lacking basic knowledge about standard gift card activation procedures, with an attitude against customer, as it happened in my case. The good part is that there are so many other stores to go to get the gift cards. No big deal.

The staff here is super chill. It gets a little snug in here when it's busy which can be overwhelming, but besides that I've never had a problem.   I also like Starbucks because they carry a few GLUTEN FREE snacks like blueberry granola (yum!) and some other things. It's a little pricey, but worth it every once in awhile.

Terribly slow service. They had 2 people working. I got there around 9:20 am on a Saturday and didn't get to order my drink until 9:35. I have no clue why there was so little staff in a cafe in a heavily populated area on a Saturday morning.

I'm a Starbucks fan, I just am.  This one of all of them near the Franklin area, is the worst. They always have not enough people working and the service is so slow. Unfortunately this one is most convenient and the drive thru, who doesn't love the drive thru.  Only a couple times that I've been here have the staff been friendly or helpful, usually I have to check for a pulse.  Of all the locations nearby this one the iced coffee & espresso shots taste the most burnt, which is annoying.  It's also LOUD. I came once to get some work done, and the speakers were SO LOUD I had to leave. Maybe that's the goal?? Plus the internet is real choppy here.  Not my fave...

First impression was that it is has a unique layout, and it a pretty good size for a downtown location. There was plenty of seating, and we were able to get a table right away. Impacts on the experience were the spotty wifi; poor acoustics - the ambient noise was more overbearing than in most locations, conversation really travels here; and lastly, though it is a matter of opinion, it was pretty cold as well.

That's right...locals beware I am up-ing a star for a corporate MONSTER!!!  But for good reason. First off. I have a friend that works here. So it's nice that I can go and work and catch up with her when she has some down time. That's really the only reason I hit up this Starbucks. Anyway, now that I feel justified...I went in today wanting some tea, but the register was down. The cashier tried to fiddle with a bunch of things, pressed buttons galore, but nothing seemed to work. Rather than have me wait for a manager to come over, she politely gave me my tea, sent me on my way, and said it was on the house. I would have waited. I was so impressed and appreciative of that small gesture. Now it's time to pay it forward.   Know Before You Go:  It's in a strip with a bunch of other things so the parking situation can be dicey. I don't mind parking across the way near Ross and walking, which is probably a good idea if you're planting a seat at Starbucks for hours on end.

I've been here on a work trip. Walked over every morning. The staff are so slow. Jasmine was excellent. She was running bar and register while all her other co-workers stood around and ate a bagel. Now her 3 co-workers have gone to the back. Jasmine was very nice and remained calm throughout the rush. As a former shift supervisor for Starbucks, it's sad to see this kind of behavior. If it weren't for Jasmine I would give this Starbucks a 1 star.

This Starbucks is just.... a disgrace to all Starbucks. And I LOVE Starbies!!   Firstly, the layout is weird and it's always dirty in there.   Secondly, the girl who took our order could not comprehend anything I was saying. I'm usually quite patient with people, but after the 4th time of repeating myself I was quickly losing my patience. Especially since I had somewhere to be.   Thirdly, the girl who made my drinks made me a hot latte when I asked for an iced one. I noticed she messed up the girl's order, who was in front of me as well.   Maybe if they weren't so busy talking amongst themselves then they'll be focused on taking correct orders, and not have to do extra work...  Surprisingly enough the drinks I got turned out to taste well.   There's another Starbucks a few blocks away that's much better than this one. I wouldn't recommend this location at all.

Least favorite Sbux. The staff looks at me like I'm crazy/dirty/cheap when I ask for a refill on my iced coffee. I never know if they will be charging me for the cream in my americano. I no longer order bar drinks here because it totally depends who is making them. My order is right half the time I go thru the drive up window. I'm ignored for a few mins at the counter when I walk in b/c everyone is focused on the drive up. Yup. I think that's about it.

Before I asked for non fat frappuccino and no whip but they put whip cream on it and then they restarted it but ended up giving me light frappuccino which had the light syrup and tasted really bad I just threw it away.  Today I asked for non fat latte with no whip the same barista added whip and when I told him I don't want whip he did not do the latte again just scooped some of the whip off the top. When I asked him are you sure this has no whip cream in it he said it is a drink that has a lot of chocolate in it and a lot of fat in it but I assure you it does not have whip cream in it. I know what I'm doing and I calculate the calories of chocolate and everything else. Wow lots of attitude for so much money put into a cup of coffee.

I like stopping here.   It's just off the freeway and back on.   I don't have to be here in town long and I can hit the road.   The store is clean The employees are nice  My coffee is hot and yum!   I'll be back,  I feel like I can recommend.   This store has plenty of parking and seating both.

The best Bucks. Hands down.  People only get mad because they are terrible at time management, and can't tell themselves no when they are already late, and then blame it on the minimum wage earning baristas, because the thought of being even remotely in the wrong upends their entire worldview and goes against everything that Mommy and Daddy told them About how special and important they are.  Just be nice people. Dang.

Every time I go in here, generally when I have errands in the area, I have consistently been treated rude by the barista. Also they have a bad tenancy to mess up my drink. Most recently I went in and asked for a passion tea lem...because thats all the girl let me get out before snapping that they were out of lemonade. I ordered a passion tea with raspberry sweetener instead. I watched one barista make it and she completely skipped the sweetener. When I asked if she could put some in she rolled her eyes, grabbed my cup and put a few pumps in then set on the counter rather hard without saying a word.I'll have to start making the drive to keystone to avoid the attitude and death stares.

I stopped here with the intention of getting in a little reading before I headed home. And although the people that worked there were friendly, and my drink was made correctly, the drink station was dirty, and trash was spilling out of it. The sugar packets seemed like they had been wadded up and shoved in the container. They were completely out of vanilla powder, and there were no chocolate powder or nutmeg bottles at all, just cinnamon. And for some reason, for a Tuesday afternoon, it was sooooo super crowded that the introvert in me didn't want to have to sit that close to strangers. Unfortunately, late December doesn't usually lend itself to sitting outside. So, I was mildly displeased with my experience.
Great service at this location and they always get my order correct despite the large surplus of customers this location seems to have.
Absolutely the worst starbucks. Service is ridiculously slow. Don't come here in a rush!!

Ordered a frap with coconut milk no whip! They put whip. When they re did it they took it around the corner and took the whip off. I trashed it. Unacceptable if you have a dairy allergy. I will go to another location for now on.

I really like the Starbucks. I am in there every single day seven days a week. They are always polite and the one time they got my drink wrong the manager gave me a free voucher for a drink next time I came in. The only thing I will say is there is not enough parking there's always a long line in the drive-through and sometimes you can't even find a place to park.

I'm writing this review while sitting in a line that hasn't moved in 8-10 minutes. I'll give them one thing, they're consistent..consistently slow. The workers are always very nice and drinks orders are correct. They are just not efficient which is resulting in many unhappy customers. Block off 30 mins if you plan to grab a drink from this Starbucks.

I've never had a problem with this Starbucks location. They, like their other nearby location off of Bruce B Downs, always have very friendly baristas, who always make sure you have a great experience, and a correct and tasty drink/order. I pass by this location on the way to work in the morning, which makes it convenient to pickup a mobile order. They always have everything ready and waiting when I get there, all freshly made and/or heated up as well (as far as food goes). I would say the only downside is that this location does not have a drive-thru, but even when ordering inside, they are usually pretty quick, from taking your order to preparing and serving your order.

Staff is great and drinks are made well. But the WiFi constantly disconnects and there is not enough plug ins around the cafe. I spoke with the manager about a month ago about these issues. She said the would be fixed, but no improvements so far

What can you say about another Starbucks? This one is a popular joint for the Lakeview crowd even though NOLA Beans is just a few doors down.  The locals seem to like this place for its coffee and outdoor seating. If it gets too crowded, just try NOLA Beans... or just go ahead and try NOLA Beans first.

This Starbucks brings me back to the old school days. Me and hubby used to come to this location all the time. Our all time favorite is the White Chocolate Mocha. We used to sip on that and chat upstairs at their lounge section very often. This is probably my favorite Starbucks since it brung me so much memories.  Since is my favorite, why 3 stars???? I've been checking in quite a bit lately. Their drinks are either hit or miss. For example, I've been getting the Matcha Green Tea Latte. Sometimes the drink is just right but other times, the barista probably didn't shake it enough...... resulting in "chunks" of powder at the bottom of the cup. Sometimes the whipped cream looks so nice while others "cheap/stingy" looking.
Very friendly staff and the drink quality is up to par as opposed to other locations. The wifi speed and range were better than other locations that I have been to.

Not a big fan of 'bucks but one thing they do well is consistency and efficiency - except at this place. But  OMG the wait here is ungodly........... You WILL WAIT at least 15-30 min for coffee.   Just go into Kroger and buy the coffee - take it home and brew it yourself because you will get it quicker than this location.
They have google fiber - gotta love the internet speed here. Good table space too for those looking to study with a laptop & book(s).

This is the worst Starbucks I've ever been to.  The girl working there tonight at 8pm on 8/14/17 was so lazy and slow!!  She didn't feel like doing a pour over so she served me coffee out of a coffee urn that wasn't even turned on.  I tasted it and it was room temp.  I've never experienced that at a Starbucks.  I returned it and she did make a pour over.  It only took her 15 minutes to figure out how to make it.  Come on Starbucks.  Your customers expect better.  Your employee was way more interested in talking with her coworker than doing her job.
I'm a Starbucks fiend. I go at least once daily. I have my regular, and visit many others on my trips.   This one was no different than any other, and I have no complaints.   Five stars in my book!

Old City Starbucks is my go-to Starbucks.  This spot just got renovated so the design is new and much easier to maneuver around in.  There isn't much of a seating area here though, and finding a seat throughout the busy times usually doesn't happen.   This place can get busy due to it's location, and sometimes the staff isn't the quickest.  Most of the time there is a line, but it doesn't take too long to make your way through.  Pick a different spot if you're in a rush and there is a line.  This Starbucks moves at tourist speed.
I've consistently received wonderful service at this Starbucks. The lines are crazy long, but they deliver quality drinks each time!

Cute location not far from WestShore Mall.  Parking lot is not the best and it is limited on decent parking and crazy when too many people trying to find a spot.  Location is medium sized and had a nice look to it.  Grab the comfortable chairs if you can.  This is typical of any starbucks where you are going to find the same drinks and delicacies.  The staff was really nice and fun to talk to.  Normally I do not get a horrid barista in a Starbucks.  I would suggest this being a good spot if you are in the WestShore area and need a little break and some coffee.  If you are a college student and need to come here to study or write a paper.  Get here as soon as possibly to get an outlet and a good space.

They do what they can, but definitely not the best location. The drive thru situation is horribly long and congested due to the poor design. Now with the lack of employees they've decided to close the walk up window making it even worse.

This is one of my favorite Starbucks in Reno. I like Starbucks because of the consistency. I work from home and head out for coffee at least once a day so I can have a break for about an hour.   If I am going for a great coffee experience, this won't be the place. If I am going somewhere in order to check email, have a clean place to work, know that the internet connection is up and working, I'll come here. Starbucks is amazingly average but is always average. Sometimes, that's a good thing.

After waiting several minutes to even be acknowledged, Polly tried to charge me double for a tea without ice "because it's like getting two drinks." $7.50-something is what she tried to charge me, but in the app with mobile order it's $3.22. I might have laughed in surprise, explained that I know that's not how that works since I'm a partner at a corporate store, and told her there was no way I was paying for that and walked out. Extremely disappointed and will not be returning. I also will be telling anyone who asks not to go there and to go to a real corporate Starbucks, not this licensed store. This isn't the first time I've received as service here, but this is the first time it was so bad I walked out empty handed.

I love Starbucks. It is a wonderful treat every once in a while in the afternoon. But out of all the Starbucks I visit this is the slowest by far. I appreciate it when they make drinks correctly and understand this takes time. But this Starbucks is beyond just making the perfect drink. It seems to be a popular stop so you would think there would be more than one person making drinks and one person running the drive thru. If there were more hands on deck they wouldn't have people pulling out of the drive thru line after placing their orders. Unfortunately it takes just as long if you go in.
One of my fave locations. Always available parking, the baristas are awesome, and this one is perfect to come to and get some work done or meet up with a friend.
Worst Starbucks ever. Horrible customer service. Very unorganized and nobody can make a drink correctly. Stay away.
This Starbucks is cramped.  Every time I have been in there, the coffee doesn't taste as fresh as other Starbucks.  The staff is friendly, but it's certainly not a first choice for me.
they are RUDE 3/4 of the time if they even stirred your drink but hey $7 for a latte what else should i expect?

Ugh...this place just keeps getting worse. Whether it's the summertime fly infested dining area, the grime everywhere or the fact they never have half the food on the menu, this place makes a strong argument for going to McDonalds for your coffee. Today's passive aggressive 'screw you' to patrons was setting the thermostat to 60 degrees. People were wearing full out jackets inside. Seriously, Everytime I think 'maybe there a new manager' and give it a try, I wish I hit a drive thru anywhere else. Close. Just close.
Located at the corner of 232 and 132.  Access from either street.  Convenient location.

Despite the staff's cold exterior my order was completed correctly and super fast! There's no room to sit in this place.Tourists hangout due to the Betsy Ross House being across the street and if you're a native philadelphian like myself that is annoying making it very loud and cramped. Like I've said, not a bad cup of coffee but not a place to sit and enjoy it.

This is by far the worst Starbucks ever. I normally bypass this location (despite it being the closest) due to their terrible service. Today was no different. I ordered a grande Mocha Latte. It was ready when I got there. Fabulous. I (stupidly) got in my car and drove off. About a mile up the road I pick up my cup and feel like it's a little light. Open the lid...its 3/4 FULL. Look at the photo. I didn't take a SINGLE SIP. Like this is what I'm talking about with this location. Either one of three things is going to happen. 1. You'll wait forever. 2. Your drink will be made wrong (in taste or just a completely different order than what you asked for) 3. You'll pay for a grande and get a tall.

This Starbucks is top notch. Some of the best baristas I've seen work. Not much seating inside but lots outside. This is a very popular place after school hours. Peak runs through 8am-11am and 3:30pm-5pm so if you want to have really quick service, go there between those times. Many regulars come to this Starbucks and you can see why, welcoming baristas, fast service, and always willing to remake something for you (my personal favorite is Bryce).
Don't assume that this is worth a stop off to get a sandwich to take on your flight out of PIE.  Out of sandwiches (except breakfast sandwiches, really?) at 3:30 pm on a Monday.

This line is always long (but it moves rather quickly)!!!  However, they are open very late, and it helps to be able to grab a cup of coffee when you need a boost in the middle of your studying!  Love this place.... despite the line!

This is a pretty good SBUX. Long but swiftly moving line. Accurate order fulfillment. Ample condiment station. Plenty of comfy shmumfy leather armchairs, footrests, and power outlets. What more could you ask for? Maybe a lil of that free SBUX Wi-Fi. But no, its in the USF Library and guess what? You need a visitor code. Oh well. Everything else is great.

Drive thru is always quick & efficient at this location, baristas are super nice here, and even though the interior is closed during the corona virus outbreak, the staff is still welcoming when you head through the drive thru. I've never had an issue with my drink here, and I've been upgraded a time or two when the wait was just a touch longer than usual. I've also never encountered them being out of any items either; maybe that's luck on my part & maybe that's just good stocking in their part. Location is convenient too; traffic light right outside the side exit, so it's easy to get in & out of this specific spot.

Coming from NY and being around a multitude of coffee shops, I know a good one when I see one.   They are incredibly efficient when a lot of these people order weird crazy drinks, and I just get an iced red eye and call it a day.  The drive thru is actually amazing.  I never heard of a drive thru starbucks before, and this is the only one I know like that.  It's really quick and everyone is so friendly.  The only thing that kind of is annoying is the parking lot and the location of it, how it's not really easy to get into or out of.

This particular Starbucks has the typical food and fare you would find at other locations. Could possibly benefit from a bigger food display case, but decent size. I would say this Starbucks location is above average because it is in a cozy attractive stand alone building with ample parking and easy access from Street Road. Patrons are usually respectful quiet locals.Not too many noisy kids thankfully.  Friendly smiling help for the most part. Could use a couple more soft cushioned seats instead of those hard wooden tables and chairs, but there may be a space issue I guess. Has outdoor seating if you want to enjoy the Java outside on pleasant days and evenings.Nice cold AC if you want to escape the heat for a couple of hours. Very good place to meet a friend, or go on a blind date, so all in all, one of the better Starbucks in Bucks in my opinion.

Horrible customer service!  Besides taking forever to be served,  I went in with an ecard from Starbucks and they wouldn't honor it, despite it showing on the printout that there was $5 on it and it was never used.  They had me call an 800 # and I was on hold and bounced around to different people, so I just left.  Honor your ecards!
Very friendly and nothing out of the ordinary from a Starbucks. Shame about the poor quality coffee though. This post maybe more positive because I just walked away with a free cup
Never a problem and my drink is correct staff has been friendly too!  Only sometimes they rush you through drive thru  and then can't seem to get your app to scan.

Love Starbucks. My cramel frappe is amazing. The only two complaints is the parking the the way they call your order when done. There is not nearly enough parking. Then once my order was done I did not hear the girl call out my order that was ready.
Started going to the Starbucks by my work and never get a bad drink there. This one literally gives the worst drinks...... I don't understand isn't their a recipe your supposed to follow????

I've never had any issues with this location, but the staff is now advised by management to go against Starbuck policy and when someone orders an iced coffee, light ice, they are not allowed to fill it to the top. I order a large size and all I want is more coffee than cream, that extra 1/2 a cup of coffee makes the difference, but what I received was a milky white with a hint of tan drink. When I told then that was way too much cream they poured a little out, and then splashed in a little more coffee, which made really no difference to the white color whatsoever.  It sounds like management is trying to save a couple pennies and instead of acting like a real Starbucks they're rationing out the amount of coffee one can have. Luckily this isn't the only starbucks near me, or even the only starbucks on campus!! I advise finding another one.

I work on Broadway we usually come here all the time and spend a lot of money but lately this Starbucks has been slacking. Drinks made wrong and long wait times. Make sure you have extra time in your schedule if you come here. One iced latte takes 10 minutes and there was hardly anyone in the place! My drink wasn't even mixed, just thrown together. Last time they were out of ice and I waited over 10 mins for a drink. For the volume in that area they need more experienced employees. Don't know if I'll be back. There's a few other great local coffee shops in the area. Try elsewhere.

For the first time in my life a Starbucks barista actually asked me how to spell my name instead of writing something incorrect and acting indifferent (although it was always funny to see what they would put), and I appreciated the fact the gentleman took the time to ask me! I had a delicious frappacino - toasted white chocolate mocha - it was prepared quickly and was delicious! This location also has plenty of seating and lots of convenient parking.

I stopped in this Starbucks on my way from Delaware County to New York City (all the way up from Florida) and the Barista, her name was Armani and a target employee named Brenda were awesome. They were fast, friendly, helpful and professional. This was the kind of Customer service I ensure with my own business so it was good to see it in the hustle and bustle of the city. Thanks ladies!
Staff is super friendly, fast, and efficient! I go there regularly for my afternoon coffee, and no matter what I order, it's always consistent from one time to the next.

Time to update past review.    The service had definitely improved!    Being on the way home every day, I pass by and have been swinging in for a quick drink.   Much quicker than the past 3 months!!    The afternoon barista's are getting better.   The large selection of holiday beverages keep me wanting to go back and try new things.    Make sure you ask for it to be extra hot if you want to feel some warmth going down your throat.   The new gingerbread latte has just the right amount of ginger and cinnamon. They have no problems giving your pup a cup of whipped cream "pupachino".   Just ask when you get to the drive up.

I frequent this Starbucks as it's closest to my job, however the staff at this particular store are not very knowledgeable when it comes to specific drinks (even though I had the drink with the ingredients pulled up on my phone.  Candy Cane Frappuccino: Grande= vanilla bean frappuccino w/ 1.5 pumps of peppermint). I also ordered a Puppaccino for my dog and both of them looked at me with this look of pure loss as to what on earth I was talking about.  Then when I explained to them what a Puppaccino is they looked at me like I totally inconvenienced them although it was just myself and one other customer in there.  Pretty sad that I had to explain what both items I ordered contained and I'm not even a barista. And to top it off, the store was out of peppermint syrup so I had to order something different.

Great customer service!! Polly is always happy and cheerful. There's great teamwork in there. A young lady named Haley appeared to be under the influence of some type of drug or alcohol or I would have given them 5 stars...
Great service! Jeff attended to me and was quick and even offered his extensive knowledge

By far the best Starbucks in Florida!  I travel a lot and there are good and bad Starbucks......  and then there is Starbucks at Suncoast and 54. The employees are friendly and hold conversations.  The bar moves much faster than any other Starbucks I have ever been to.  Thank you Joey, Christine, Sherry, Demi, Just Jake, Phoebe, Kevin, Chelsea , Sania and anyone I missed!

By far my favorite Starbucks location! The staff are always friendly, and my order is never wrong. This location is typically very busy, but for the volume of people in and out the door, not to mention the location right next to Rittenhouse Square, they are great with keeping the line short while maintaining composure. I highly recommend this Starbucks...I even go out of my way from a closer location (on 18th & Market) to enjoy the atmosphere here.  I can't forget to mention the awesome loft upstairs where I can enjoy my beverage whilst people watching! :)

I can't believe I created a yelp account to review this terrible Starbucks. I've been to hundreds of restaurants across the country and this is the one that caused me to create a yelp account. This is my 3rd and final time ever stepping foot into this piece of crap. My most recent attempt at ordering a drink in this craphouse failed miserably twice. I ordered a Salted Caramel Mocha Frappaccino and got a Hot Mocha coffee they put in ice (this after they tried to give me a salted caramal hot coffee. This piece of crap was luke warm by the time I took my first sip and tossed it out at the first trashcan I saw. My Previous experiences here were just as bad. With 2 customers before me, I waited 10-15 minutes for a freaking coffee. I am not sure if the management at this Starbucks enjoys hiring 50 IQ Baristas (someone needs to support the idiots), but this is not acceptable. Not only can these Baristas not give you what you ordered, they are rude. You must avoid going to this Starbucks at all costs. If this Starbucks is any indication of the health of this organization, start shorting the stock now and thank me later. This company and location is complete garbage.   I only wish 0 or negative stars were an option. I hope corporate realizes the management at this location is an embarrassment to the brand. Fire everyone and start over.

The worst Starbucks ever!   I see from other reviews that the drive through is a problem. One would think that after 6 months they would have their act together.  After a 20 minute wait in line for a Grande Cappuccino (couldn't get a pound of Expresso too, as they were out) I drove away with ...TEA. There I was navigating the rush hour traffic, pulled the cup to my mouth and discovered that coffee was tea. How could they mess up a single order ? It wasn't worth my time to battle traffic to go back so, I made note, never to go to this Starbucks again.

Went in for a coffee, while there were 3 people behind the counter, besides the drive thru person,  I was left standing there for over 3 min.  If you are going to stand in a circle engaged in conversation while clearly seeing a patron waiting then I will drive out of my way to Fenton, never an issue there.

So I wanted to use my free drink that expires in a few days but the manager Ristia who took my order didn't listen to me twice and charged my card. I then went up to the counter and I told her that I wanted to use my free drink and told her the code but she used my stars instead. Ristia was then unable to give me the stars back and wanted to give me 4$ off for next time which is half of what I paid for my drink. She was also rude  and just had a bad face on the whole time. I love Starbucks but there is no way I'm returning to this location.

I go to this Starbucks fairly frequently, and the drinks are fine but I can't stand that if you just walk in and up to the counter sometimes it takes a couple minutes until you are greeted, let alone have your order taken even though there are a ton of people behind the counter.

The service at this location was awful and so was the drink they tried to pass off as coffee.  I ordered a short decaf Americano, I basically received a warm cup of water.  I gave it back and told them about it.  So they offered to put in another shot.  But they didn't.  They just gave me back the same cup of disgusting coffee.  Perhaps if you want a fancy coffee drink they'll get it right.  But it seems they cannot get a simple cup of coffee correct.  On top of that I had to wait a long time because she was training a young man how to actually make the coffee but did she say "thank you for waiting" or apologize for the wait, NOPE!!

Disappointed. Went in based on the great deal for my free grande coffee with purchase of a breakfast item. I made sure to mention it to the cashier and she acknowledged the deal. Scanned my card on the iphone application and noticed it was almost $10. I asked the lady why it was so much she said that it was only good for a regulars brewed coffee. Then handed me my skinny coffee and another regular brew coffee. I was in a hurry and told her I didn't need two coffees and I thought it was for my skinny. She explained the offer after the fact/transaction and proceeded to tell me to give the other coffee to my boss. I was not happy about this and did not have time to discuss it further. While I did re-read the offer later and realized it was specific to a regular brewed coffee, I was disappointed that it was not explained further while placing my order. The free coffee went to waste and the breakfast sandwich was hard, eclectically the bread. My skinny was good as usual.

This Starbucks is super convenient to where I work. I have been in a few times in the last two weeks and the barista, Matt, has brought a friendly face to this location. Helped two of my friends with specializing their orders and they loved them!

OM ... this Starbucks has a private meeting room! We all know I love Starbucks, but the decorations and decor is very urban and fresh!  This is my first visit to this location in Ferguson, Missouri! The managers/ staff are happy and accommodating while whipping up my fall favorite drink!  Well, this will be I must stop for me when I am in this area. I was here for one of our member of my nonprofit organization first book signing! ( we are very proud of her accomplishment)  It's definitely a fun place to hang out for 3 hours while meeting old and new people at this location! I can see lots of networking going on here!  Thank you to the Starbucks Corporation for opening of this very beautiful Starbucks in Ferguson, Missouri!   Everyone was so nice and friendly to me! I will return!  Gloria~

1-1-15 Just went in for a chi ice tea to start the new year. Asked for soy milk and extra cream. Once i got my order i asked for a bit more cream since the server squirted a little cream. She then gave me the worst attitude you can give a customer. Then i asked for a staw and she tossed it at me like i was a dog. They need to retrain this girl on service. Never going to thos starbucks again. Extreemly rude girl employee.

I'm a fan of Starbucks, like any other red blooded American.  But this Starbucks, is my little piece of heaven.  It's not the biggest, the location is not the most ideal and the parking sucks, but the people are extremely nice.  This is my go-to location for my Starbucks.  B. Major Grade:  B

First time in Idaho and everyone here is soooo nice and friendly! This Starbucks is in the mall and we stopped here after getting a pedicure. The service in this place is incredible, associates were super nice!

Super busy here everytime I come by to grab some coffee. Saturday mornings are just ridiculous I can see why the staff is not all smiley and sunny days but hey at least it's busy. It's walking distance for me so hey.

Consistently great service . Clean bathrooms and cafe area. They do seem to run out of bottled water and pastries pretty often ,though.  I had a meeting and was starving so imagine my delight when I noticed they had a vegan(minus dressing) veggie and brown rice salad bowl! It was layers of yummy , well cooked brown rice, butternut squash , broccoli , world's tinniest(but tasty)peas, toasted beets, kale, red cabbage , and roasted tomatoes. The side tahini dress is not vegan ,unfortunately, it contains honey. . Otherwise, it's a great meal if you're in a pinch for a healthy, delicious and well portioned dinner .

Came here on a Friday night. Not terribly busy. The staffs are busy talking among themselves so the service is slow and chaotic. No thank yous no hellos. Drinks are meh.  Nice location. Decent number of seats. Tables are mostly kept clean.

My 2nd time at this location and 2nd time, my Iced Coffee tasted like it had been sitting in a pot all day. Small location , parking is cramped as well as seating is limited. Not my idea of a relaxing experience at $5 a cup.

Horrible! the guy working the register was an ahole. Didnt even say thanks for buying his over priced expresso. It was like I was doing him a favor. This is the reason I dont go to starbucks, snobby, liberals.   I hope this place goes out of business.

Every time I order from them they always mess part of the order up. The first time they gave me the wrong sandwich. The second time someone stole my drink so I asked them to remake it and they made me the wrong drink lol. I'm just trying to have breakfast before class and they mess me up every time.

Dropped in for a short time to chill and catch up on stuff. The staff, especially Keith, are friendly and bus. This place is in a strip mall, but nice inside. A ton of seating and is this place ever popular with younger and older. It before noon on a Tuesday and there are people doing business, computer time, chatting, reading, and even some knitting time. Awesome that we have places to be where we are welcome to hang out and be with people. The store is clean and love the center area with two leather couches and four leather chairs.

This is my hometown Starbucks and I visit about 3 times a week.  The staff is really friendly and the drinks are pretty much always good.  You know you visit too much when they know what drink you get. They do have a drive-thru here, so that is convenient, but sometimes when you go inside, it feels like they're only taking care of making the drive-thru drinks, but I get why they would not want those people waiting long.   The atmosphere is good and I sometimes go there to study to get out of the house.  Compared to other Starbucks, they don't have too much seating though.

One of the employees here - Oak I think his name tag read - is literally one of the most pleasant people I've ever encountered in customer service, even under pressure of another teammate messing up which is going to happen sometimes. He always makes me smile!

I love this Starbucks the people here are friendly the managers are always training their stuff correctly. Sure it's a little difficult to get in and out of parking lot and there's no drive-through, but that makes zero difference compared to the quality customer service that you get from the Starbucks amazing people here!!!
This location only fills their drinks up 2/3 the way. This has happened both times I've  visited. What a rip off. I'll take the extra min to go to the one on rt 42 where you get a better value.

This Starbucks was conveniently located off the interstate. Everywhere is out of almost every syrup made because of shortages in production. I went with the only thing available. Vanilla latte. Bathrooms were clean. It was a little slow getting the coffee and they weren't that busy.

I like Starbucks because of the consistency between stores all over the country. This one is close to my house and the drive thru is always fairly quick! I get the Strawberry Açaí refresher with lemonade instead of water  with a chocolate croissant!! Yum!! The guy working the drive thru this morning was super friendly and upbeat, which really gave a great start to my day!
A totally fine/normal Starbucks. Do you have a gift card? Why not go here and spend it?

I always come to this location after a workout at the L.A. Fitness for a Venti Iced Green Tea with 2 Splenda.  The employees are always nice, professional and kind.  My Iced Green Tea is always made to perfection.  What more could a man ask for?  There is seldom a wait, yet the place is frequented by lots of the locals to study and catch up with friends.  B. Major Grade:  A+

I've only ever been inside this location once. And I found it a bit of an odd experience if only because it was more lined up than the average Starbucks location usually is. Perhaps this is because they seemed to be training new staff, or rather because the staff they had were busy asking questions about things that are normally rather commonplace at the other Starbucks locations I normally visit. Regardless, my food was delicious, and I didn't need to spend much time inside, so I'll call this visit a success! :)

I love Starbucks, but this location is bad. They messed up my drink and didn't even apologize. They fixed me new coffee- but it was very sloppy. Everytime we go there they give us dirty cups- coffee spilled over the lid. The stuff isn't very polite and the place wasn't very clean- including aprons of the stuff. Bathrooms were very dirty.
This is a fantastic Starbucks!!! 10/10 would recommend. The coffee is great as always and it is very cozy inside. The baristas there are all super awesome as well.
Terrible service. 15 minute wait after placing an order. Plenty of people to help, but they were sweeping the floor at 9am.

Service was fast and friendly. My order was correct, I ordered a grande citrus defender tea and a chorizo breakfast sandwich. The drive thru moved quickly.  I'll definitely return to this location. Oh, and it was busy and looked pretty spacious on the inside too.
Great place to study. Lots of background noise but comfortable environment and friendly staff. Only thing is they don't have late hours, which isn't that bad. Has outside area too.

They need to improve their customer service. The store was not busy and yet I stood in the cash register for 3 mins with the Starbucks employee in front of me until she even took my order. I get it, she was doing some cash inventory-but a paying customer is standing right in front of you and you're making her/him wait for 3 mins so you can finish what you're doing. A good customer service would drop whatever you were doing to serve the customer first.   To make things even better, I had to wait for another 10 mins to get my drink because the barista accidentally put my drink in the drive through section. Again, the store was dead- and it took me almost a total of 15 mins to order my drink and get served. I definitely would have gotten my drink faster in a busy NYC Starbucks!   Very poor customer service - doesn't deserve a star.
Stopped by for a cup of coffee. Couldn't wait for Pike coffee to brew so I chose the other type. A sip as soon as I drove off, coffee is warm to cool. So disappointed. Thanks for ruining my morning

This is a smaller Starbucks than one usually sees, but since this is in Old City, I can't really blame the place. There's plenty of space for the merch and the little refrigerated sections, and a small cordoned off area for the line. There's hardly space for the five or so tables that dot the establishment, but if you can get a seat, they're great for people-watching because this one is on a busy corner.  Drinks are much the same as every other Starbucks and the baristas here remain chirpier than any of the myriad of indie places in the neighborhood. The "bakery" area is weaker than other Starbucks--I visited around noon and all of the display food was already looking very wilted and sad.

I very rarely go to Starbucks because I don't typically drink coffee or tea.  After visiting this location, I thought that maybe I have been missing out.  There is something to be said about enjoying a delicious beverage with your girlfriends while sitting in a comfy chair and having a gab fest!  The staff here was very friendly and helpful albeit rather confused on how to work the register, resulting in the preparation of an incorrect beverage.  Nonetheless, they were very happy to correct the mistake and didn't act at all upset or annoyed.  During their down time, the staff was busy cleaning and restocking and all were pleasant toward each other.  Overall an enjoyable visit.  I will be back to branch out and try something other than a hot passion fruit tea!

I visited this Starbucks today and just know that you will WAIT! I ordered two sandwiches and a drink and I waited inside for about 15-20 minutes. The drive thru is clearly their priority. That's your best bet at this location. I felt so bad for a girl that had a mobile order and waited longer than me.. They might need more staff. Sweet people though.

This Starbucks is an embarrassment to the brand.  Consistently bad service often accompanied with a bad attitude and not clean.  I'll take the extra time from now on to go up to the Bloomingdale store where it seems they train their staff.

Busy, busy, busy...it is a Friday though...at the mall.  The line is a little disorganized - not sure what can be done about that.  Would've left 4 star, but they FORGOT to make out drinks from mobile order.  Mine and a lady next to me.  We stood there and watched 10 customers go by that ordered after we were waiting.

Great Starbucks location. Very close to downtown Santa Barbara yet easy to get in and out of anytime of the day or night. I have always received great service and drinks here. The interior is spacious and there's usually plenty of parking.

The cashier was so nice! We wanted a hot chocolate and the girl literally mentioned every single line they have and could do! It was nice and sincere, something you don't always get when ordering such a simple drink.

This is probably the Starbucks location I've visited the most frequently. Service here is rather consistent, which I love. Although it's a very busy location, meaning that some food items are sold out if one visits late in the day, it consistently performs when it comes to beverage options and definitely provides a quick caffeine fix for those who need it. As a tea drinker, I've been reasonably happy with the chai offerings (though I prefer the Oprah Chai Tea Latte cold, and the regular one hot). The spinach and feta breakfast wrap is one of my favourite food options!   The patio is rather small but great for people watching and certainly for watching the street. The indoor seating area is usually good and one can often find a seat. The wifi is occasionally difficult to access consistently but is available if one needs it.

While they focus on getting the cars through the drive-through in a timely manner, they lose focus on what a five dollar cup of coffee should be. I gave them two tries and I got quite possibly the two worst cups of coffee I've ever had. A call to the store with my frustration was met by a very young and sharpie voice telling me to come back and let them remake my cup of coffee. Doesn't that defeat the purpose? The bottom line isif you're looking for a good cup of Starbucks coffee and have a few extra moments make the drive to Winthrop.

From an avid Starbucks customer, this location is one of the worst. Make sure to have plenty of time to wait for your order to come out. It took just a few minutes for my breakfast to come out, but another 25 minutes for my coffee to be made.
Soooooo slow, over priced, undermanned, and chaotic environment inside.  Poor parking, minimal seating, and odd location.
The staff is very friendly however the young man making the orders did give me the worst VBM i have ever had. I guess he was having a bad day, it is a simple drink to make so I'm not sure.

My love affair with Starbucks began in college, when a budgeted weekend-only soy latte remedied any hangover, amped me up, and helped forge my way to a 4.0. Now, 5 years later, my latte love-turned-lifeline has spiraled and my discretionary income takes a hit more mornings than not. There's something very special about a beverage that is a symbol for industriousness and also the reason why I hike out in two feet of snow, in freezing weather, sometimes with the flu, to get a beloved boisson.   As an aside, this location is the best S-Bux ever! The team always manage to exceed my expectations.

Filthy! I would rarely leave a negative review , but this place looks like it hasn't been cleaned in months. The floors look absolutely disgusting, everywhere ! Dust and hair balls built up under tables. The scum and junk has built up on the the floor edges. I have never in my life seen a Starbucks in this state, I was shocked honestly. I asked for the manager. He said 'I've been gone a few days , yeah , I noticed it too.'.   No way this got like this in days , you can tell it's just the way this store is run. Badly.
Super nice people, always get my order right and they're fast as hell in the morning. Totally my preferred 'bucks!
can someone tell me why would you change trash cans while there is a line out the door?? and aren't refill suppose to get new cups?? infectious control!!!
This is my go to study place. Everyone here is very nice and the location is close to my house so its very convienent I cant live without my coffee!
This location is busy so be prepare to wait 10 minutes. I wished this cafe hired alittle more baristas. I always see the same 2 baristas covering all the tasks.
This place sucks. Go to Nono's or zen den or the other place up the street.

Terrible service asked for a iced London fog tea. Tried explaining what is was because she didn't know, at mid sentence she stopped me and said she'd figure it out. Got my drink and it was just 2 bags of tea in a overfilled cup. Didn't even attempt to try to get my order right

I'm always confused why Starbucks is so busy.  Stopped by this morning as this was the closet coffee, and waited 10 minutes.  Coffee is mediocre compared to other local coffee shops.  Rather get better coffee and support local business vs. this Corp giant.

Rude and bad service.  Left my drink on the table and never called out my name. Even when I stood in front of the girl she continued to ignore and failed to mention my drink was waiting.  I hated spending a single cent at *$ but got a gift card to burn.  Would not go back to this store.

GREAT GREAT GREAT !!!!  Im very picky about starbucks i used to be a starbucks barista  and this location rocks always friendly always the correct drink ( or fixed right away ) they really show what most starbucks used to be and now this kind of quality is few and far between.

I still love coming to this Starbucks because it is near my job. It tends to get busy and slammed by tourist because it is located in Old City. The staff has gone through some new faces but they are all wonderful and friendly.   I love the new color and style, very warm and welcoming. Great product placement to capture your attention reminding you to pick up another bag of beans or another cute/fun mug that you really don't have a place in your cupboard for but oh well you buy it anyway.
For those who work on the run, which often means local coffee shops-this is not your typical Sbux. Much more room and comfortable seating than the norm. My new fabrite.

Quiet little starbucks, which is usually not the case in most Starbucks. The two baristas here are very nice and attentive. Got my cafe americano, and it's pretty good. What more can I say? Good service here.

Employee friendliness was great and all but when I see THREE people get their drinks even though I ordered before them, then I ask where my drink is. Fourth person gets their drink and I don't have my drink. Not Cool Beans! Fifth person that ordered after me gets their drink.... oh now sixth. How unfortunate I had to be somewhere soon. Disappointing service today especially when I spend about $15.

There is nothing wrong with the coffee, but the attitude that was displayed today was not very friendly.  I stop here now and again and everyone tends to be pleasant.  I made a mistake on my order...it was my fault and I was apologetic for my mistake, and the order was caught before the drinks were made.  The girl who was making them gave me an eye roll and a snide remark.  When she paced over the drinks I said sorry again and I got an unpleasant "whatever" in return.  Everyone makes a mistake sometimes, like messing up while ordering and I am going to take it that she messed up with the attitude.

Amazing Staff! So very busy and packed BUT quick, efficient service with smiles! Nice sitting areas and attached to the Hilton. NOT just serving Starbucks, but a REAL Starbucks! Probably the best Starbucks I've ever visited since they are so fast and correct with orders! Go here for your morning Latte! I go every morning when I'm at the Hilton, which is a lot.

I like all Starbucks in general, so even though this one is inside the mall = no seats, always long lines, I still like it. I'm taking 1 star away because sometimes I do have to wait a long time for my drink. As long as the drinks are made right, I'm not complaining.

8:30 at night and you ask for a decaf coffee and they're not Brewing it and act like it's something foreign that you're asking for... Decaf coffee is what people drink at night so that they can sleep but still also have coffee not sure how many people are drinking decaf coffee in the morning to keep them sleeping when I go to work just a really weird way to operate. Off to Dunkin Donuts I guess...

If you put a mobile order in here I would just suggest checking in with the barista where you would normally place your order, which defeats the purpose of mobile ordering. Waited patiently for my mobile order for 15 minutes. I had placed the order while sitting in my car in the parking lot and went right in, never heard anyone call my name. Went to the pick up area stood there for 10 min to let them know I was there to pick up my mobile order, was not acknowledged by anyone. Checked my app to make sure purchase went through, sure enough it did. Checked with ordering barista it had been sitting behind the counter the whole time. All other Starbucks locations that I have visited will just put the drink out so you can grab it, I understand that sometimes people take the wrong drink so that's why this location does not do that, however, not being acknowledged by anyone was very frustrating.

Tried to swing through the drive they for a quick refresher and once again it was not quick.   Only one other car in line 5 minutes later we get to the window and they did not have our order right.   Had to wait while they made it.    The other  employees were standing  around  talking.   Stay away from this Starbucks on the weekends.
I've been here a few times even though I forget to check in. The staff is extremely friendly and knows evrryone by drink and name. Very welcoming for a chain coffee shop :) my new second work spot!
These people actually know how to make an extra dry cappuccino. I'll go to this location over the other Springfield Starbucks location any day.

This place is a joke. The staff is completely unfriendly and unhelpful. It's taken me many small annoyances such as not getting what I ordered or waiting 15 minutes to finally write a review. I just placed an order on my mobile app and they claimed they never received an order and didn't offer to do anything to fix it like...I don't know...make the coffees I ordered and paid for? And I'm a neighbor and frequent  customer. Not anymore. Avoid.

One of the worst Starbucks I went too. They messed up, not one...but TWO of my hot coffee orders. It wasn't until later (since the drinks were hot) that the screw up was found.   Ordered a grande pumpkin spice latte and a cinnamon dolce latte. Pumpkin spice had absolutely no flavor, and cinnamon dolce tasted like it had all of the flavor. In addition, they left about an inch of space from each coffee.  No wonder though, seven employees all standing around bullshitting....and only one customer in front of me. It still took 10 min.   Avoid this Starbucks if you want any service, and if you  actually want your drink made properly the first time.

I usually come to this Starbucks because it's in my neighborhood. I must say the location is nice sice it's right on Harrison. However, the inside space is quite small, even though there is plenty of outdoor space (which is really unpleasant during the summer months).  I must say though that the staff is really friendly. I remember one time I went there, I wasn't sure what I wanted and the barista guided me in the right direction.  All in all, this is a good place to come to study, catch up with friends, or just get your daily caffeine fix.

I love the teas but it's confusing to order. Every Starbucks serves them differently. Some add sweetener and syrup; some don't.  If I order a shaken tea with passion fruit it's one price; with peach it's more money.  Lid I thought syrups were free with gold membership.

I stopped by for a cup of coffee, but all of the handicapped places were taken, so I had to park further. As I was entering the store, I noticed that all of the cars parked in the handicap space WEREN'T HANDICAPPED. No placard, nothing on the license plate. I talked to the manager about it. She was very rude. She said, "Tough s**t, I don't care who parks where as long as I get their money." I left immediately and took my order of ~$20 in frappuccinos elsewhere. Would not recommend.

I've been coming to this location almost everyday for the past two years.  I really miss the old crew, It's just not the same.  It used to be one nice cooperative happy team.  No more smiles, no more heys, it's just simply order, grab, and go, which is fine too but only if my drinks are prepped the right way. I want my coffees fresh not burnt.  My iced teas should be shaken all the time not only upon request, right? my drinks should be served in clean cups, not all sticky and overflowing right? No syrup means No I do not want my drink sweetened! No whip means please save your time and whip. No cheese means, please do not add in the damn cheese! What the heck! No customer service is fine but please read the orders and prep them the right way! If you can't follow simple instructions, why bother work here?

I have the unfortunate pleasure of visiting this Starbucks at least 2x weekly as I work part time in the mall. The service here is constantly BAD.  The employees here do not provide good customer service. Most times the are too busy tasking to pay attention to their customers. The worst part is that it never seems too clean, meaning trash bin is full, I have observed gnats in the pastry case. The cold case isn't fully stocked...The order takers and baristas don't appear to be a team. I tend to stick with just purchasing tea as anything outside of that the wait is FOREVER or the don't have the item. For example their have been at least 10 times this summer that cold brew coffee was not available. No in e offer to instead make a iced coffee. The attitude was pretty much we ain't got it prefer something else and move along. I only provided one star because I'm kinda forced to go here.

This is quiet place to unwind, great place to grab a cup of coffee before a hectic day. Only reason it didn't earn 5 stars is that they were out of soy milk at 8:15 in the morning. Staff here are extremely friendly and cheery and the barrister crafted my drink to perfection. Prices are the same among the Starbucks franchises. The seat at this location was very comfortable and nicely lit. Will definitely return to this location in the future.

It was 2 pm and I waited 20 minutes for a coffee and was late back from my break .  CLEARLY UNDERSTAFFED, which equals poor service and unclean areas. The parking lot is about impossible, with the drive-thru blocking the diner's spaces.  Since this is an obvious issue, one would think to prioritize a solution- which tells me the management doesn't care, is non-motivated, and assumes the establishment makes enough profits as is.

I am a starbucks coffee lover and im always going in any locations here in Edmonton. But this location at 109 st. jasper is very dissapointing!!!! There washrooms are locked!!! 2 hours before they close and the washrooms are locked! this is not acceptable! and they are not even close yet.. I ask one of the barista and she told me that the Ladies washroom are OUT of ORDER... even the Mens is locked too... and the two baristas are smiling while talking to each other....I don't understand why they locked it too early and when the customer are asking why its locked they are saying that is both OUT of ORDER.... and i dont think so that is both "out of order"...the way they answer us! this is not the starbucks experience that i always expected! I am not coming back on this location anymore!!!!!!!!!

Someone please explain to me the presence of so many disgustingly large flies here-- preferably one amongst the staff, as I am positive many other patrons know them more than plenty well, having swiped at most of them. Maybe they are attracted by one of the Insect Wonderlands that is almost any given table top.  Seriously, one day I killed at least nine of them in a two hour sitting. Why on earth was I at Starbucks, you ask? Well, we hadn't had internet installed yet, that's why!

One of my favorite Starbucks! Although I think Starbucks as a company should allow their employees to express themselves and not have to hide tattooes. Its a coffee shop its all about embracing the different in everyone.

As much as I try and support smaller coffee shops more often than not, I can't help but crave my standard Starbucks treat on busy days.  Generally the lines aren't too bad and I really have to respect the fact they are offering healthier options in the showcase. Some newer additions would include a protein plate and paninis. I believe availability differs from store to store. My top 2 drinks: A simple skinny vanilla latte half sweet with a triple shot of espresso. Gets the job done.  And more often lately- a skinny chai latte. I usually get it a bit on the stronger side. It has a good kick of spice and as one of the Baristas told me pretty healthy for you since it's mainly real tea with a small amount of sugar added to the mix. It's more of a subtle caffeine fix than the usual coffee fix and doesn't give u the typical coffee breath. Their vivanno smoothies are really good as well. They are made with real banana and whey protein. And the instant flavored coffee packs available are really a good investment. Super convenient when on the go, and good paired with cold water or steamed hot milk.

This Starbucks has gone way down hill in the past year. Mobile orders are often not ready or wrong. Younger baristas often seem confused. Coworker got curdled milk in her coffee this week. Today I was given a cup that basically disintegrated in my hand burning me - BEFORE I even made it to the door.
Typical Starbucks although this one always seems to take a little bit longer than most.  Still, great service and same great coffee and snacks.

What an absolute pleasure going through this drive-thru this morning! The gentleman passing our coffee out the window and taking payment was such a sweetheart. You know it only takes a moment to be really nice, make a joke or two and completely make someone's morning. This guy knows it.   Plus it's just a short jaunt from the ridiculously busy drive-thru at the Speedway/Wilmot store where I sometimes worry I might run out of gas waiting in line because the line is So. Freakin'. Long.  Not an issue at Broadway/Kolb plus they're super nice and speedy!

This is the BEST Starbucks in town and we have been to them all. The employees are very dedicated and although they are a training store the MANY steady employees have consistently offered excellent personable service. Also the cleanest bathrooms out of all the area Starbucks!

UPDATE: I've not been to this location in a while. Customer service has greatly declined. I waited at the menu/order intercom for a minute before anyone said anything to me. Long waits, friendly staff-most of them-I do not think are here anymore :(

GOD, these people are slow and incompetent. But since they are dealing with tourists and others with no choice, they can be as nasty as they wanna be.  My suggestion?  Do a little search on your phone and find an independent coffee house nearby. You will get much better quality coffee and drinks, and much better service.
Starbucks gravois bluffs is my place ! Every morn they keep me going w my flat white I love Austin

I stop here almost every time I take a flight. The reason? The service is the fastest I have ever seen for a Starbucks. The owner works along with the other baristas. She is taking drink orders 2-3 people back in line and has them ready sometimes even before you finish putting your credit card away. Their food is fresh. The staff is a bit quiet but friendly. 5 stars compared to other Starbucks I have visited.   Other facts: -No tables/chairs -Your typical "compact" airport Starbucks

Such a great experience! Very helpful and knowledgeable of the menu and answered all questions I had. Easy and fun people to interact with. They have it together! I wish more Starbucks interactions could be like this one. While here, try Natalie's caramel cake. Very good!

The drive-thru line wait is always so ridiculous. Also, make sure you're ready with a second option for your drink or meal , because it seems like they're always out of something. The staff is friendly. The inside is clean. But if you're running late to work and need a quick coffee, stop at the McDonald's next door.

I am not a fan of this Starbucks location due to their slow and snobby service.  They do have a drive through so that's convenient, but I find that it's a bit faster to just park and go in.  This time I came at 4 on a Saturday afternoon, was the only person in line, and service was SUPER SLOOOOOOOW.  UGHS.  Come ON.  OK, fine, there were a few people in line at the drive through but still. =P  Lastly, I had asked for extra caramel sauce on my caramel frapp but they didn't do it so I went back and asked for it.  The barista glared at me and as he added the sauce, went jabbering on about how he followed the recipe and didn't leave anything out.  Come ON, no need for attitude.  Just squeeze the bottle of caramel sauce and I'll be on my way!  I didn't blame him for doing anything wrong even though I had asked for it upon ordering.  He DID miss something but I wasn't upset.  SHEESH.
Love this location always friendly. If the line is long no worries they move fast. I will be going back to this location for sure.

Worst Starbucks drink I've EVER had. Ordered the Nitro Cold Brew with cold foam. First off the coffee was warm. Normally there's ice in my cold brew or it's extremely cold. This was unacceptable! It was warm and the "cold foam" was warm as well and very thin. Just terrible. My fiancé ordered it and when I realized the disaster we didn't feel like going back in traffic to get back to the location. Disappointed. Spent almost $6 on one drink and can't even finish it because it's not very good at all.

I couldn't give this place one star because I normally get decent service here. I am not sure if this is a holiday staff issue or what, but they were way off today. The line was not super long, but it definitely was not being managed well... By the time I finally got to the front of the line, I was looking at the cashier's back. There was no acknowledgement of me at all. I know the cashiers are generally charged with the task of preparing the baked goods, so I was prepared to let this slide. Either way, it would have been nice to get a smile, a "Hi, sorry can you give me just a second...?" or something along those lines. But I got her back. Then another employee comes to the cash register. I didn't assume she was taking over and she wasn't... she was just clocking in or out I believe. Either way, she also did not offer any type of acknowledgement to me, the customer, and when she was done with whatever she was doing at the register, she also turned her back to me a proceeded to do something other than provide customer service. Then the two of them started talking about topics that had nothing to do with their job, which would have been fine, had they offered some level of customer service. There were two other young ladies working on what looked like the drive thru orders since they were at the other end of the bar. Although they offered a few glances my way, they did not smile or speak to me or any of the other customers who were standing in a line that was really starting to build up at this point. I took a few minutes to take in what was happening, then I gathered my things and left. I am sure the two young ladies are completely oblivious to the fact that they just lost a customer because they didn't acknowledge me in the first place.

Went through the drive-thru and ordered iced tea and an iced mocha. Sat in the drive-thru for 15 minutes, 10 of them waiting for my iced mocha to be made. 7 staff behind the counter, 2 actually making drinks, several playing on their phones, and then after waiting for 7 minutes they asked if it was ok if my iced mocha did not have whipped cream as they were still making it. At that point I needed to leave so I said fine, but it still took another 3 minutes for my drink to come out. As my drink was handed to me I watched a barista put whipped cream on another drink. Many young kids playing around and chit chatting, not working. Definitely not going back to this location, poor service!

This location is actually the location right across the street from West Edmonton Mall, right beside Boston Pizza.; It is not located inside the mall.  I've gone to this location a few times and to be honest, it lacks in customer service on the occasion.  There are a few barista's that not only provide a quick service, but also provide a friendly attitude while on other days the service is extremely slow coupled with a rude attitude.  Depends on the day.  There is a drive thru at this location as well, which is convenient.

I come to this Starbucks often because I'm frequently in the area.  I really want to be a fan of this Starbucks, after all they do have friendly baristas but here's what annoys me:  They almost never have decaf brewing so it's impossible to get a decaf unless it's a decaf Americano or on the clover press- I've asked for a pour over and they don't have a pour over system- what?!    The seating is very limited here and one person, an older man sleeps at a table in the corner repeatedly day after day for hours on end while other people don't have a place to sit or have to share a table- I even complained about it to the baristas and they defend him- seriously?  No apologies or asking the man to please leave after a half hour or hour so other people have a chance to sit down.  I even called customer service who finally was apologetic and I hope the issue finally gets addressed!    I really hope that things can improve because I want to be more of a fan of this Starbucks but these issues really get on my nerves...

This is the worst Starbucks I have ever been to. The lady at the register was so rude right from the start of us beginning to order. The rest of staff was just standing around not doing anything while there were 10 people waiting in line to order.

I'm slightly confused - all the other reviews for this location seem to indicate that it's IN West Ed mall, when it's actually across the street.  In any case, it's close to my place of employment, which is highly convenient when I'm craving a mocha.  It's also nice to be able to grab a hot chocolate to sip on the drive home after a grueling night shift.  Other than that, it's Starbucks.  What else needs to be said?

This is my absolute favorite Starbucks. I feel like the staff always is accommodating and my drinks are always consistent. You can see their prep area which is always clean. Bathrooms are clean and the "partners" always greet me with a smile.
One of my favorite Starbucks in the city. The staff are always friendly and coffe always fresh n hot.
Nothing like driving to get a $5 cup of coffee to get it only half filled. I wish I noticed before I got home. What a waste.

This place is ridiculous. Seriously why pay 5 bucks for coffee when you can get it for a dollar at McDonald's. Not to mention snobby people. Some customers and some employees. I don't care if you are called a barista or not to me you are a coffee pourer. Don't make me feel like a low ball because you sell expensive coffee!! (End rant)
Does drinks perfectly most of the time and good customer service. This Starbucks is also in a nice neighborhood! I wish there was more parking space though.
