Hit or miss depending on the barista that day if you're ordering a complicated drink like a frap. Always really busy in the mornings and on weekends. Can be slow.

We went to this Starbucks on a Tuesday around 9 am. While they were busy we waited a long time for sandwiches that were just reheated. My husband ordered coffee and sandwiches. The coffee was ready right away. He then ordered a couple of more sandwiches. The first order of sandwiches took 10 minutes. The second order took 30 minutes! Ridiculous!

Usually a long wait since it's the only location in the area. Service has always been polite and efficient but make sure they repeat your order back to you. I've received wrong orders a couple of times and unfortunately did not realize until I was down the street.
it just took them nearly 20 mins to make 2 iced tea drinks. that is just ridiculous, mcdonald's would have been faster
best costumer service ever! super quick and accurate with orders. everyone is so cheerful there. definitely my favorite starbucks
Ordered thru app. Reserve coffee I had ordered thru the app was out of stock. Informed only when I showed up. No phone call, message or email. Ended up wasting 20 minutes.
Usually Starbucks is spot on, but this time my order got completely messed up. I'm finding Starbucks to be over priced, and a corporate bore. I'd rather go spend coffee money elsewhere.

Best baristas in town. They can make whatever you want however you want it. They also make wonderful suggestions if you're looking to try something new. Never had an issue with an order. When going through the drive thru the line always moves fast. The team is so pleasant and welcoming. It's definitely my favorite Starbucks by far, and I've visited MANY up and down the east coast!
Fast friendly and as always Starbucks. I was told my review was alittle on the shortside so I added this. Good, friendly and as always Starbucks.

Prices in Reno Nevada are higher than they are in the Bay Area for a cup of starbucks. The line for this Starbucks was about a 20 minute wait. If your working in this city of at the hotels I don't think you would be able to get Starbucks on your 10 minute break without coming back late.  After ordering it took about 10 minutes to receive our breakfast sandwiches and 10 more minutes to receive our drinks. There were about 20 other customers in the store with only 2 employees. We came early January so it could still be high tourist season but the Farrah hotel should have a large enough staff capable or making coffee in a more efficient manner.  I ordered a caramel macchiato and it was cold, tasted like pre coffee and did not taste like what I am used to buying in San Jose CA. It could be a slow Starbucks and not during winter or high travel season do they ever have to make drinks, but it's Starbucks and Starbucks is always jam packed no matter where you go. Conclusion is try to find a bette estate bucks in Reno if you can.

Ever since I moved to bordentown this was one of my favorite things. The Starbucks is less than 5 minutes away from my apartment. (Can be a bad thing too haha) usually the service is always great; however, the only huge complaint I have is they don't check their mobile orders. On multiple occasions I've placed a mobile order and it won't be ready when I get there, nor did they start it. Today, I arrived 10 minutes after I placed the order and they hadn't checked. I can understand if it's busy, but it wasn't today. This won't make me stop coming here, but maybe they should have a different system to know to check mobile orders. A lot of times I'm in a rush and then have to wait another 5-10minutes.

Gosh darn lousy experience...dirty interior, xtremely slow drive-thru, no smile employees...nine of them behind the counter.  What a disaster of an operation and certainly not well managed.  Tried the drive-thru first and line wasn't moving, so we went inside.  Chaotic looking scene, with full garbage bin overflowing the counter top of the cream/sugar area, dirty floor, littered table tops. Two employees at drive-thru window busy talking to each other, not doing anything?!  Employee at inside register must have been new.  Hesitant on questions, taking our order, etc. Asked for no whip on cold drink, got about half normal amount on top anyway?    All this at 11AM on a Sunday.  We were not impressed.  Too bad they've got a monopoly here...a little competition would do us all good.
Out of many ingredients. Frappuccino has texture of iced coffee. Out of whipped cream last minute. Took 10 minutes for a frappaccino

Starbucks always leaves a good impression on me. The service is as good as to be expected (sometimes there is a line). There are usually good sale items. Everyone is pleasant and the quality is always good.
Great place with super good customer service! Always friendly staff and always accommodating.

Wish it was inside the 3 connecting hotels, but it was a short (cold) walk away from El Dorado. Located inside Harrah's, on the 2nd floor in the West Tower. It was an easy find with signs everywhere. The staff was nice and the hot latte came out quickly!  There were a few chairs and tables but no couch, which would have made my little pit stop a bit more comfortable.  Glad to have a Starbucks nearby!

A pretty average Starbucks if you ask me. There're plenty of seats but because it's connected to a Macy's, I can see this place getting a lot of foot traffic during rush hour and wkends.   The service when I went was just ok. One person, who was obviously still working ignored me for about 3 minutes then told me someone else would help me. That other person was very helpful.

Worst Starbucks EVER and Iv been to many..They never greet you ..they always get your order wrong ..then when they have to remake it they're upset. I love Starbucks but this location needs real help!!

Very good service and friendly staff. I was happy to find a Starbucks with good service here in Tucson.Had several unpleasant experiences with the drive thru on Speedway so this was a welcome find.  At times the drive through seems to get priority leaving only one server for customers in the store.But all in all a good place to visit.
Stopped here on the way, transporting a car down to FL.  It was a great pick me up that energized me 3/4 down the east coast.  The coffee was fresh, tasty and well put together.

Nice Starbucks inside Harrah's Casino. Only problem, you've got to walk through a cigarette-smoke-filled casino to get to it. Harrah's is an old-school, dirty, smelly, old casino. To my surprise, the Starbucks was clean and relatively fresh.

There are two Starbucks on my way in to the office. This is my Starbucks of choice despite not having a drive thru. It's still much faster to do mobile order, park and walk in than wait in the drive thru lane at the other location. Occasionally, I'll still have to wait a few minutes and for a brief stretch over the last couple months that wait became pretty consistent and a time or too way longer than I think it should have been. Today, I mobile ordered a little later than I like to, drove by, saw it was packed, and thought "oh dang I'm fixing to be late to work for sure..." but I walked in and they were throwing out mobile orders like the T-shirt cannons at the titans games! Super quick and my sandwich (reduced fat turkey bacon egg white sandwich) was great and thoughtfully assembled. I usually get mochas but today I had a star reward so I got something a little special - white chocolate mocha, light whip, nonfat. Yum! There's also a lady that works here who is just the absolute nicest - always so helpful and so friendly and cheerful even when I know she's been there since the crack of dawn. I really appreciate her.  Tips: no drive thru! There's street parking for free or a parking lot behind the building that's paid (ridiculous - in my 22 years of living here that lot hasn't been a paid lot til a few months ago...) but if you're mobile ordering and running out I honestly never pay bc I'm there less than 5 min most occasions.

Today this is my rating because the rude blonde with the weird makeup was once again rude.  She really makes me hate this Starbucks.  Asked for splash stick and that seemed like alot and she laid it on the dirty counter that's goin in my drink lady give me me a break!
Sitting for the outside is offered, but we can't smoke after making a purchase?  Very wrong and I won't be back.

Your friendly neighborhood Spiderman, I mean Starbucks. This place is constantly packed any time of the day which is not a bad thing. I've had no problems with any drinks i've ordered or the service. There is plenty of seating available inside and out, so you can bring your furry animals out for some coffee as well. If you're familiar with Harrison, there is plenty of parking everywhere if you're willing to make a few extra steps to get in.

Never see a manager in store. Customer service was fair. Forget drive-up window. Simple Drink orders occasionally wrong. Online orders are not ready because they are out of ingredients. Trash bins are overflowing and tables are dirty. Emailed Starbucks Headquarters Customer Service with no feedback.

I moved to STL from SoCal two years ago.  Started going to the Starbucks at Chippewa and Landsdowne because it is within walking distance of my home.  For 1 1/2 years, I put up with the poor customer experience because I figured that's just the way it is here in STL.  Then, I happened to drive by this Crestwood store and tried it out.  I couldn't believe how clean, friendly and professionally trained the staff is and the store is very comfortable.  So, now I get in my car, drive by the Chippewa store and head to the Crestwood store each morning.  Believe me, it's worth the drive to start my morning with a friendly store with exceptional service.
Slowest drive through service East of the Mississippi! Don't order a frozen drink because it will be a room temperature mess by the time you get to the service window!

Let me start off by saying this place is very very busy. You will need to bring your patience with you. They are understaffed, not the fault of the workers. Hopefully someone will see this and do something about it. Waited 30 minutes in line for coffee. Yes that's a long time. They need more staff. The staff they have for the volume of people they get doesn't match. Staff is amazing and friendly(with the exception of the two ladies that just left when their teammates needed them the most...sMH.  We watched them just walk away and never communicate to their teammates where they were going). We have been at the hotel for three days now and they work non stop alllllll day! Probably the hardest working folks I've seen at a Starbucks! These lines are crazy. Their little shop is clean and organized. People walk away happy with their coffee and no one in line has complained about the long wait. I hope these folks are getting some amazing stock options. Someone needs to acknowledge this store and staff. I can't say that enough. Special shout out to Gracie that was making all these drinks alone and Rachel for holding down the front and orders. The other two chicks...well they would be better off counting stars for NASA. I feel sooo bad for these two girls. Starbucks, time for a secret shopper at this place!
Rude staff and drinks are not made properly. I have thrown out my drink on multiple occasions.
It's fucking Starbucks, duh. They gave my dog a cool little pup cup for his Frappuccino.

This is one of the worst places I have ever been to . They are so slow always seems to be understaffed . One thing I would positively say that they fix my drink properly . Staff seems overwhelmed all the time . It does not matter what time of the day you go it's always slow . They have to fix their drive through path , It's narrow and can't accommodate enough cars.

No matter the time of day I come to this location there is always a super long wait!  It seems they have to brew the coffee fresh for each customer. Lol. After about a 20 minute wait, the host was apologetic for the wait and took my order. My Pumpkin Spice Latte was hot, flavorful and delicious. And my Berry Açaí drink was perfect for later.

I placed a mobile order for a venti iced coconut milk macchiato and a berry trio yogurt. The employees ignored my order for 45 minutes. When I went to ask, they rudely said that I probably ordered it at a different location and demanded to see my receipt. After showing them, they made my drink and forgot about my yogurt. Worst starbucks experience ever and I'm a gold member. I go here all the time, but this was the worst service I've ever had.

This is by far my favorite Starbucks! Mainly because of the baristas compared to other Starbucks. It is also at a good location where you can seat outside on a nice sunny day. I love the peach green tea lemonade! Defiantly will recommend to others

Got some drip coffee here and it was so incredibly burnt tasting, I ended up throwing it out.  The next day I went to get some breakfast... Even though they told me two days before that they had "just" run out of the two gluten free breakfast items (sous vide and gluten free breakfast sandwich), it turns out that they don't actually carry either.  I probably wouldn't have been so irritated had I not waited in the huge line, but there you go.  Do yourself a favor and walk ten minutes to stumptown.

This particular Starbucks should remove the "star" from their name because they just want your "bucks". Sat in line 30 on a Saturday afternoon then treated extremely rude by the staff.   They have a great location and awesome fireplace but there is no warmth in the customer service

I needed to buy breakfast treats for the office, so I went inside this new location one weekday morning at 6:30 am. There were 4 or 5 people working already, and the drive-thru area was very busy, although there were only a couple of us inside.  I was greeted promptly at the register, and in short order had my Pumpkin Spice Latte (venti, cold, with almond milk, no whip), and breakfast for the office.  I bought an assortment of breads: iced lemon pound cake, pumpkin bread, and banana bread, and I love that they were all individually packaged.  Each one was fresh, moist, and very delicious - I highly recommend treating yourself to the pound cake!  The interior is clean, large, and nicely decorated with plenty of seating. I would definitely come to this location again to hang out.
Always has my coffee and breakfast snacks made right. This Starbucks has more comfy seating than a lot of other Starbucks do.  Staff is friendly also.

I called this location with a complaint and instead of listening to my concern, the manager simply placed the phone down and never came back.   I heard her telling customers about how she "needed to take a shower" and "place money into her bank account". She also said something about how annoying customers were which I assume was her way of passive aggressively attacking me.  Poorly run, poorly managed. Starbucks needs to crack down on this location or close it.

What can you say, the ubiquitous Starbuck's.  They cut their own throat by going for quantity (of stores) over quality.  Definitely  have had better espresso at independents and other Starbuck's, but the view is quite lovely if seated just right on the patio, otherwise it's just another parking lot.  Access to go back South on Campbell is kinda tough, but doable.

Pretty disappointed in this Starbucks. I never go to Starbucks because they are always overly packed and I could easily go to DD. Well decided to treat myself and well the drink they gave me was disgusting mind you I order the same thing for probably 10 years now and know what it should taste like. They also didn't put the Carmel that I paid extra for in it which says on the side of the cup. So I turned around and decided to get it fixed I get it mess ups happen, they go ahead and tell me they are remaking it. I spent FIFTEEN minutes waiting for my new drink which at first I was like ok they are obviously going to get the couple before me...... No they got about 5 cars and 7 orders before I got mine which I have already waited patiently for my first one. Will now go eat my cold breakfast sandwich with my ok remake drink on my way to work. Will save my time and money next time I want to treat myself.

Standard Starbucks store. Nothing to stand above, beside great location. On the other side, as I pointed out earlier, more and more stores are lagging in cleanness. This store is one of them. Hope that Starbucks Managment will consider and make some changes in the near future.

I love this starbucks because there's no drive thru which means there's no backup in the parking lot! Yes, you do have to get out of your car and inside to get your coffee. But it's way faster than any drive thru and the service is always awesome! I will be back!

I have been to this location a few times..all I can say is when it is crowded u risk the chance of being cheated.. I ordered a Frapp.. large.. my mistake, I drove off should have gone back..was pressed for time.. ended up with nothing but chocolate syrup and ice!! .almost 6 dollars wasted!!! because they want to rush your drink!!

my favorite of the local starbucks's! i love coming to sit and do work, or meet up with a friend. at this location there is such a great and friendly staff, you always feel welcomed when you walk in and there's such a strong sense of community, off the top of my head i can recall the workers Alexa, Mallory, Remegio, Clecy, Arianna, Mike, and Rebekah as people who have gone out of their way to make sure I have a pleasant experience, from remembering my regular order, to talking to me when i'm having a not so great day, they always go above and beyond for their customers!
HORRIBLE SERVICE. THATS ALL I HAVE TO SAY. But anyways the way they take order and do those orders before your mobile order while they say you are next. Stupid.

Typical Starbucks in the heart of Philadelphia's tourist area, however during this visit... the baristas were slow and unfriendly. There are still customers out there that are unfamiliar with ordering at Starbucks. A gentlemen in front of me seemed nervous with ordering and when the barista asked for what size he wanted, he waited a few seconds said he wasn't familiar with ordering, but wanted a medium. The barista seemed to be annoyed with the customer. The gentlemen had to go through the same ordeal with the cashier as well.  My drink order was correct though... I'll give them that.

Excellent Starbucks location.  I visit a couple times a week and everyone is always nice to me.  Some even know my name and know what I order each time.  It makes me feel like a celebrity.  From time to time, they have let me try new menu item samples, which is always a plus to me (I mean, come on...free food and drink!).   The facility is normally pretty clean and the staff, although busy, seem to stay on top of it.   My only very tiny con is that it seems they don't offer the free coffee grounds anymore.  Years ago, they would have packages of it by the door to take home for composting.  I guess it was too big of a pain in the butt to maintain.
Dirtiest Starbucks ever.  Got my drink wrong.  Barista not friendly.  No sense of urgency, I ng line at drive thru.  Manager appeared from back and seemed oblivious and uninterested.

Hot coffee served like warm.  Could not take Starbucks card.  Had to Apple Pay, then said I could go to website with receipt later to try to get the stars they owe me.  Manager not very friendly. Ultra caramel frappaccino was good.  Vanilla sweet cream cold brew did not seem to have any sweetness or vanilla. Had to add sugar packets in order to drink it.
Great staff. They move quickly and keep the place cheerful and optimistic. The location is convenient and the facility is comfortable and light. Very relaxing and great vibes.

The only bad Starbucks experience I've ever had  1. The coffee was incredibly bitter and had a burnt after taste. I was only able to take a few sips before giving up. It's been an hour since I drank it and the after taste is still there  2. I asked to have room for cream but they filled it nearly to the brim so I ended up having to poor some out on my own which wasn't ideal.  3. They served the coffee without a sleeve, apparently they were all out by 11:30 AM on a Sunday morning.   At this point I was pretty annoyed and asked if they could replace it with a fresh cup and I was told "they don't let us do that". I ended up just throwing out 95% of a large coffee, I just couldn't drink it.    3. We also got a chocolate chip cookie but the center was basically uncooked, had to throw that away too.  I've never had a bad experience at Starbucks so I felt compelled to share my experience. Hopefully the location was just having an off day but if not, the franchise owners will need to make a couple of tweaks.
The service was great met my friend there for coffee. It has be come my favorite meeting place. My coffee was very good. Smooth and full of flavor.

I've been here many times and never had a bad experience. Service and product are always good. Today I placed my order through the app and it was waiting when I arrived about 5 mins later. The barista was super friendly too!

I like local. But, it's the only option out here other than PJ's. PJ's is local-ish, but their coffee isn't to my liking, so here I am. It's easy to sit here and grab a few moments of zen before my evening class.

Have been several times as my office is nearby.  Service is generally friendly but slow.  I would love to get coffee before my early trains to New York but they are almost never open on time.  Great location, they just need execution help in a big way.

This is my neighborhood Starbucks and I frequent it often. Very often. Of course they can make every drink to perfection except the Flat White. It's supposed to be with steamed milk. Not a cappuccino or a latte but steamed milk.  I hate complaining about things but .... Why can't they make a flat white?!  The ONLY person that can a proper Flat White is Hannah. She ROCKS!  She's awesome!!  ....but she works mornings. So if I go on a day she's off or go later in the day, I shouldn't order a drink for my husband. Check out the pic of the drink we got today.

Not sure what happened, but I was given the worst Very Berry Hibiscus I've had in a long time. The drink was ready when I got there but tasted nothing like berries, just hibiscus and watered down juice. I've had this drink maybe a dozen times in my life, and have only had this problem here. It's a relatively new store, so maybe they didn't quite finish teaching the ratios/recipes.
Frequently get lukewarm coffee. They always remake the coffee when I have time to go in and ask, but this is Starbucks...they should have HOT coffee at all times.

I spend plenty of time here but the service varies on the time of the day. Morning hustle is always extremely busy and sometimes slow. Afternoon is pretty fast but most people working not in the best moods. I can't complain about the coffee always deliciously made. The spicy chorizo is probably my favorite and my caramel macchiato I wouldn't change for the world.  Given that it is a drive thru ...I will say I spend way to much time in the in the morning and inside is no better.

Felt like I walked into a Dunkin doughnuts. The girl ringing me gave me the biggest attitude about getting a receipt. (And I needed to get reimbursed from my company) at first she asks me if I need a receipt, then she doesn't give me the recipe just puts it in a pile next to the register and takes the next order. Then I ask her for my receipt and I get a nasty look and she asks me for my card back without any explain actions. At this point I am thinking maybe my card didn't go thru the first time. But hey no communication there. A note to the manager of this store: u have an army of people behind the register, who smile chit chat, talk about life events etc, unfortunately only amongst them selves which again reminds me of Dunkin. however it would be nice if that good energy projected to ur customers; I thought Starbucks brand had a different work culture. To reference my transaction; chk 8516,  7:30 am,  05/27/15

Great service and gray drinks. They always have my order written down before I even get to the register and I always get smiling very nice service. The food is great too!  Now they are very pricey, but well worth it if you are a coffee lover.

Extremely slow!!! 25 minutes in a drive-thru, come on! There is a reason why people want to stay in their cars, we need the time. Def need to improve here. Other than that, coffee was great as always and staff was friendly. Time management is an issue therefore 2 stars.

It's your typical Starbucks. My reason for the 5 star review is bc I witnessed a rich b#%tch just give the poor girl at the counter a hard time bc her drink was "taking too long". Um- I walked in saw it was packed, from there I used deductive reasoning to assume there'd be a wait. There is no shortage of lofty individuals in D-town who think that their time is more precious, she was one of them. The goth-looking counter girl handled herself very, very well. As a person in the food service industry, I frankly, have less and less patience for these people more and more each day. It's really enough to make you run in the back room, cry, even quit. People should just be effin nice. Kudos to the goth girl, I was scared you'd toss the hot coffee on her for a second.

Stopped here on a Tuesday morning around 6am and had some pretty poor service. Me and 2 coworkers made our order and the two of them ordered breakfast sandwiches with their coffee. As soon as the cashier took our order she just left and wasn't seen again. Only one person was left at the counter making our coffees and didn't see the order for our sandwiches. There were no other orders/customers so she was just hanging out while the other girl was nowhere to be found still. After several minutes she realized she missed something because we were still standing there waiting and she finally started heating their food. When they was finally delivered it wasn't even heated all the way through. Not fun.   Coffee was good but the experience left us less impressed. We most likely won't be back here.

Worst Starbucks Ive ever been to. If you can avoid this one and go to the one inside Target next door, you should. The drinks are wrong or gross 4 out of 5 times here. So, make sure you taste it before leaving. It's a reoccurring issue at this location. Also, drink orders take forever sometimes.  There should be one question when hiring applicants for this location...."are you competent?" Because I'm pretty sure most of the employees would have answered no. Also, can you please provide trash cans by the drive thru? When I get just a shot of espresso I drink it before I even leave the window, while I'm waiting on more drinks, and the employees won't/can't  take the empty cup right back through the window to throw it away.

I go to Starbucks at least once a week. I go out of my way to avoid this location though because they are just so slow. 15 minutes from the moment I place my order in drive thru until I receive it is unacceptable. And the staff seem to revel in the fact that they are so incompetently slow. Awful.

Was not impressed with this Starbucks. The space is rather large but the seating is limited. The overall space was kind of a mess and seemed like it needed a deep cleaning. Stuff was just out of place.  They also need better training on making drinks. The first time I went I ordered a brève hot chocolate. It was not made breve, and when I told the barista she started to argue with me that it was instead of just making me a new one. The second time I came I ordered an iced matcha, which is one of my usual orders. It was extremely bland tasting and lacked a sufficient amount of matcha.  Would avoid going to this location unless absolutely necessary.
I usually go drive through and it's usually pretty fast.  I live nearby, so it's on my way home.  I have nothing to complain about. I can't say that the service is slow.

The lines may be long but it's worth it. When I ordered she was so nice. Greeted me with a smile and said "Good morning and how can I help you? " I placed my order but forgot I was hugging a water bottle to purchase. I received my coffee and cinnamon blueberry bagel, toasted of course and this lady too greeted me with a smile. Then I realized I still had the water and had not paid. So I quickly put it back and told the lady and we laughed. She offered me a cup of water and assured me it was filtered and triple filtered. Haha They're busy but not too busy for to give a smile.

Update: I stopped by ( against my better judgement ) and this time the restrooms were out of order and the guy behind the counter yelled at me to go to some market around the corner !  Let me try to describe you the ambiance ( I came here the last 3 days in a row) , I gave up on trying to work so might as well:   1) A homeless guy changing clothes in front of me.  2) Another homeless guy next to me going through his things, he's burping an talking on the phone, it is unclear if there is someone on the other end though.  3) Another homeless guy watching a bootleg of the latest star wars movie (spoilers galore).   Did I mention there are backpacks as far as the eye can see, smelly backpacks and mattresses.  And now a barista comes out behind the bar and gives one of the homeless guys a huge free cup of coffee, and here I am, trying to work to pay for coffee when I could have easily just brought a smelly backpack, not sure how this reward works.  Baristas behind the counter have been either rude or disinterested and the place is generally dirty.

I always receive fast and friendly service at this location. Today Hannah took my order and served me my coffee. she was very pleasant. I noticed she was helping the person in line ahead of me to receive the best value for her request. Not only is the staff friendly, the store and restrooms are clean. These aspects make this my favorite Starbucks location.

Oh man, did these guys get a new staff? Things seem to be served so much slower than in the past. I live across the street so it's super convenient and I wouldn't stop coming back. But man, I have to give myself like 10-15 extra minutes additional now compared with the old staff.

We went here with a big group of girls during a Volleyball Convention. It did not take long to get our order considering we were a big group. Our order was exactly what we wanted. It has  a great location too  if you are staying at Harrahs. If you just want coffee and pastries this a to go place but if you want your typical paninis and salads you won't get it in this Starbucks location.Nothing fancy about this place. This location is smaller than the other Starbucks I've been to. I would definitely come back again.

Enjoying a flat white with friends on this cold January evening.  Love this location. A nice mixture of high school and college kids plus plenty of us more mature crowd.  The baristas are always friendly and always happy to recommend a new item. Parking is very convenient with street  parking and lot parking.

I love Starbucks especially the one on Harrison! I love the frappachinos...my favorites being the green tea and the pumkin spice, which is a seasonal flavor for fall. When I go with my friends, we usually would sit outside since it is usually packed inside and really cold. Even during the winter, we sit outside. A negative is there isn't much seating inside. There also isn't a drive-thru. I love how the workers are really helpful and will even give out free samples of coffee packets. One time they were about to close and gave me a bunch of cookies to take home. Regardless, Starbucks remains one of my favorite coffee shops.

Been here twice now. First time, they messed up my grande black iced coffee. Second time today, also destroyed my grande black iced coffee. Black coffee should not taste like it has 1000 packets of sugar in it. My bagel and cream cheese consisted of a black brick and two tiny packets of butter. I even asked the lady who handed me the bag "this is with cream cheese, right?". Sucks for me I didn't check before I got 10 minutes down the road. So now I'm out $10, with coffee I can't drink and a burnt frisbee. I'll be walking a few extra minutes to go to Dunkin Donuts from now on.

Service was fast and the place if business is spacious BUT the 3 times I've come by this place to sit inside I fiund plenty of seating but knats and small insects on my table and around the place. Just a bit cleaning up or regular inspection would make this place 4.5/5 stars. The staff is nice, service is fast, order has always been accurate but I could do without the critters....
Nice location (inside and out). Very friendly staff. They prepare the drinks quick and are always good quality.

Drive-through was slammed and early-morning took about 15 minutes to get coffee. The coffee was delicious I tried the new Punkin and a salted caramel. Got to the drive-through to pay and the employee was very friendly made the experience worthwhile will come back again soon!

Always cluttered, loud music, and loud people give no courtesy to anyone trying to get work done of any sort. Less clean than most starbucks ive been to.    Ive since stopped going due to their mulitple anti police mentality but have given tbis location a fair review despite peraonal feelings of the company.

If it's one thing the employees of Starbucks can do right it's mess up an easy order. For the third time in a row, I've ordered EGG WHITE BITES. They have given me regular egg with bacon. Save your time and money and go to a local family owned establishment instead.

Stopped in at 9:45 pm when the hours run until 10 pm. The lady inside had locked all the doors and was on the phone not opening. Went out of my way to find an open store for a long drive, just to be sent away.

Service is SO SLOW. I've been there multiple times and end up being in line for longer than 15 minutes each time just for one drink. I use to go to the old one and have incredibly fast service with 6 cars ahead of me. I prefer the original Starbucks in Brownsburg because they are faster when you are in a hurry.

Starbucks is great. I love starbucks. Never had a problem. HOWEVER, I have been ordering the same drink for months, and today was the first day I had a negative comment from one of the baristas. (I usually order a grande cafe americano with three pumps of sugar-free caramel syrup, three packs of Splenda and double-blended with ice. The drink foams up so much it needs to be out into a venti cup, it's all the satisfaction of a frappuccino but with only 15 calories rather than 500.) Then Sarah, the barista, rudely asked me if I had ever had a coffee frappe before, to which I said yes, and she was like "well they're awfully close to being the same thing" YES, I'm AWARE it's almost the SAME THING. ITS ALSO 500 CALORIES AND I KNOW WHAT I ORDERED, SARAH.

This is one of my favourite Starbucks.  The location is close to the mall but away from the mayhem of trying to find parking close by.  There's natural light except a bit darker in the one area that is not by the window.  The service is always efficient and quick, and friendly.  The last visit, they did not have my usual tea that I like to order but I found a good substitute.  There are a few armchair types seats, wood chairs, and a few higher seats.  I've been lucky to always find a table and chairs available.  Right beside the door is a recycle bin labelled for paper, and cups and lids.  I haven't seen this at all Starbucks but they should have them! Some locations might not have room for it but it's better than trying to jam your cup into a full garbage.

I often go to this Starbucks because it is closest to my home.  The service is hit or miss probably because they seem to have new trainees here every time I am here.  The long time (e.g., more than a year) employees are usually very friendly and accurately make my order.  The problem is the trainees are often clueless especially when I redeem a reward and have to ask for advice from a more experienced employee. This tends to slow down the ordering process and results in overcharges.  I often have to repeat my order several times because many of the barista trainees  have short term memory issues which is sad because they are so young.  Given the high customer traffic at this location I think Starbucks should not staff this store with so many trainees. I gave this location two stars mainly because of the good service provided by the few, and ever diminishing, experienced employees; if I were to base this on the trainees, I would give no stars.

This Starbucks was the closest one to the hospital were my mom spent her last days. (sorry to be a downer but this review is a positive one!).  I stopped there almost every day to get a mental break and a strong cup of coffee. It's a beautiful shop, excellent staff, and they all (little do they know) made the two difficult weeks a little easier. Thank you!

Good things about this location Staff is all very nice and friendly Shop is well maintained They've stepped up their game since my original review.  I love the staff here and I'm like Norm from Cheers when I walk in. They all know my name and tray me like family ( actually better than my family )  Once again the staff is all great.  Good job Claudio, Shay NayNay, Vee, Wren, Super Mario (Drew), MarkuMark, Shamir, Aaron, Jamie and to anyone I forgot.  Namaste

This is consistently the slowest Starbucks I have ever been to. Drive through and inside are both painfully slow. And I have tried at all different times - weekdays, weekends, afternoons and evenings. I am always sorry when I decide to stop and grab something. Terrible service
Very nice clean Starbucks. Staff was courteous and made me feel welcomed. Drink was just right, perfect!! Highly recommend this location. A+

I moved here from California.  So it was Starbucks or Petes coffee,,, or perhaps 7-11. Starbucks it was.  This particular store is busy every time I'm there.  Yea,, store security!  Never seem to see the same employee twice however.  Not sure it's because of high turnover or scattered hours.  Always had the same person in CA and my drink was ready for me by the time I got to the register. I miss that.  I also cannot have my favorite Starbucks drink, white chocolate mocha, because I'm diabetic now, but am happy they make so many sugar free syrups.  So it's a "Iced, Venti, Decaf, Skinny Vanilla Latte" for me now. Don't think they've ever gotten my order wrong here,, but I did say ICED !!  That means I want ice in the drink.  I always have to ask for more.  A nice store, friendly employees, good drinks (although they ARE too expensive, but you know that on entry) and awesome blueberry scones!  I'll be back !!

One of the better starbucks in the indy area. They are quick, helpful and overall it has always been a good experience for me. They are colocated with a gas station which makes it loose the high end feel but inside the store it is very nice like a typical starbucks. Dont let this factor scare you away as it is still a corporate location.

Okay so I have been ordering the same coffee the same way for years!!!    So here we go folks:  I go thru the drive thru, I place my order... And what do I get in return, a lecture on how I don't need to say this when I order it, and how I only have to order it this way...and on and on for 2 minutes to only confuse me...I was so irate and confused...I will NOT return here!!!. I think this guy working here either takes his job too serious or needs another job where he can tell people what to do. When I want my coffee, I want it with a total and a smile...no lecture on how to order a Starbucks coffee, when seriously dude, I come from Seattle where Starbucks was established!! Gezzzz...annoying!!!!   FYI: a iced coffee means cold, not luke warm... That means you serve it cold, shall I lecture you on how to make a iced coffee???
My local Starbucks. Good space for some reading or studying or a small meeting. Lines are never too long. Drinks are made in fashionable time, overall great service. Definitely recommend.

Nice Starbucks! Neat location - I should say it's an unusual round building, and that makes it unique.  Seeing a lot of people having lunch meetings here [laptops galore] was reassuring that I had landed at a good Starbucks.   Very quick - in and out in less than 5 minutes!
This Starbucks is the best! Kaitlyn Rocks it out and makes some mmmm mmmm good Coffee!  Hazel Dell crew, salute!
Cold coffee, not the friendliest morning staff. I could have used my index finger to stir in my half n half.
Great location, fast service (even at their peak hours), and friendly employees :) This is usually my go to SB & I'm never disappointed.

I love the staff. The problem is the system of getting mobile drinks out vs. the in house order. Twice I've waited between 15-20 min to get a drink and just walked out(without a drink in hand). I think for the prime hours they should have 2 people working on mobile orders, 2 people working on in house orders, 1 person on food, and 2 people on the register. This way no one gets behind and the flow of getting drinks out would be more efficient.

My local Starbucks is not too shabby. My drinks are always made correctly even when I give them some cray order. The people who work the window are always very nice! I'm giving this place 3 stars because the location really sucks. The parking sucks. The line sucks. The way the Starbucks drive through is positioned, the car line blocks other parking spots and the line comes out in traffic sometimes. When its busy its a PITA to get through.

Wow. I see why I'm not a huge Starbucks fan  Our office closed today due to a random electrical fluke. So one of the other girls and I went out for some coffee before heading home.   This location has a nice small patio with umbrellas for shade. It seemed quite clean inside and the have done some remodeling since I was in last. More shelves for more products. But the gal taking the orders this morning lacked personality and any social skills- I never got a good morning or what can I get you?   She just stared at me until I ordered. Awkward. She did then ask for my name to write on the cup and that was it.  We picked up the drinks at the end of the counter and don't remember hearing a thank you then either.  I realize this could be quite the busy location but really, a smile, hi and thanks isn't too much to expect is it ?  They are consistent on the taste of their drinks, I will give them that.

The drinks are prepared well but the atmosphere is dismal.  I live nearby so I tried this location about 4 times so far.  I would like to stop by after working 8 hours retail on Fri, Sat and Sun but it is an unfriendly place.  I arrived at 7:15 pm but the floors were being mopped so I had to pray that I would not fall as the distance from the sitting area to the front is far.  No greeting; just there to take my order.  I had to clamber back over the wet floor to retrieve my drink and I do have arthritis...no thank you nor a good evening.  Why?  The Starbucks on S Virginia is really friendly but a bit funky at times.  This location is about as cool as a bank.

This is the closest place to my office you can get something to eat, so I eat lunch here once in a while.   My go to is a Cubano sandwich which is pretty good.  And it is small enough to allow a tasty square if you like.     The drinks are standard starbucks fare, all decent but you can taste the corporation in all of them.
worst starbucks i have ever been to.  they made a drink and refused to remake it for it being incorrect.  i paid twice, the second time it took 30 minutes for a cold brew

Absolutely the worst Starbucks I've ever been to! I give it two stars for the two good baristas it has. To give a glimpse of what I frequently go through:  I always ask for soy milk because I don't drink normal milk since It makes me sick but apparently someone thought it was ok (or didn't think at all about the drink they were making) and put whole milk in my drink let me repeat that WHOLE MILK DO YOU KNOW WHAT WHOLE MILK DOES TO SOMEONE WHO DOES NOT DRINK MILK!! none the less that was not the first and last time they have messed up my order. My tummy, toilet and wallet still haven't forgiven them for their service and honestly the only reason I go is because it's inside the library but even then I rather walk a few minutes to get cheaper coffee at the student center.

The worst ever Starbuck you may ever experience. The staff are rude to the customers and one of the staff brought outside food and sat next to us and it was very uncomfortable because of the smell and he was very loud, it doesn't feel like i am in a cafe Very disturbing and unprofessional!  I know many people will agree on this, which is not to allow certain people to come in this Starbucks. Many complaints were made about this but something should be done about this. Woman are being harassed here and no one is doing anything about it.

I'm simple. Grande mocha and a cake pop. It was chilly when I was in Boise, so I sucked down a whole lotta coffee. I only used the drive-thru at this location. Service was friendly and fast. My mocha was a little on the too-hot side, but I got over it.

I've visited the store several times not checking in at the time. I've had service from both inside the store and at the drive through. The service I have received has been on oar with good, friendly, fast service. I only get coffee so don't know anything about their drink making capabilities. From previous reviews by others, seems their service has improved. I'm a fan if this location.

This location makes the drink correct every time, so I cannot complain there. But every single time I order light ice in my drink, they fill it to the brim with ice. Also, the drive-thru takes waaay longer than it needs to.
Great staff, friendly,  gave me an idea for a fun drinks with every little sugar. Clean seating area and overall relaxing atmosphere.
Great pick me up pit stop on a road trip!!! The drinks are on point and the staff is cool.   Gets the job done.

It's a small starbucks that gets very crowded but the staff have been nothing but friendly to me and the drinks were always perfect! I wanted to write though to mention that I was here once on a busy weekday morning and a woman in line in front of me was being absolutely mental. It was driving ME up the wall and i didn't have to serve her! The baristas handled it SO well, very patient and sweet and understanding of her many many many many nitpicky annoyances and explained everything so well to her. I worked in a pizza place myself so i know how it can be sometimes and i really admired their absolute patience with this difficult woman. I actually called back later that day to tell their manager about them!  Definitely a good staff, just definitely plan for things to take a big longer than normal due to its busy location!

If you plan to do a mobile order, this place takes upwards of 20 minutes to get it to you. The app is never correct, and the mobile order situation is a disaster. There's no way to know an accurate time, to cancel your order, or find out where you are in the queue.

My wife and I were treated with blatant prejudice by the manager Elle (who was working in the capacity of barista).  This included her not placing our drinks on the counter, not announcing them, not answering an inquiry about whether or not the products were ours, not greeting or saying thank you to us. Additionally, she treated the other customized, who all appeared to not been people of color, in a professional and kind manner. When I asked her why she was treating us disrespectfully, she literally turned her head and walked to the back. I asked another worker if I could speak with the manager and why the woman treated us so direspectfully and I communicated that she ignored me and gues who came out...yes, Elle the manager. She said, someone wants a manager. I said, Ma'am you just ignored me and treated my wife and I rudely. I was calm and composed and people sitting there watched attentively. I asked for her card and told her that I would be reporting her to Starbucks.   I did so. The initial Starbucks customer service rep tried to credit money to my account as a form of an apology. That is unacceptable. However, video doesn't lie, so I will update this review as the matter is addressed. But the bathroom cleanliness was just as unacceptable as this manager's prejudicial  conduct.

It's all about location here. Located right next to the West Tower elevators and the Conference Center escalator.  It is literally a minute from your room. Much better than hotel room coffee. Service is always swift and friendly. Dropped a star for inflated prices.

Stopped in this new Starbucks for a coffee after visiting my niece. The starbucks is easy in, easy out, and the people that work there are just fine. I ordered a vanilla latte iced, it took about the time I placed the order until I got to the window to pay for my drink to be ready.

Really love this Starbucks the best in the area. Roomy circular building, clean and plenty of seating. Very friendly baristas too. And great coffee. Starbucks is always consistent in how they make their coffee, which I prefer.
This morning I saw a barista add cold water to my drink because she failed to steam enough milk to fill the cup. Appalling. They aren't about the coffee here. They're about moving people through.

This rating is purely based on a experience I had with the store manager.  Starbucks products are consistent across the board and are generally good. The manager at this store was so rude to me recently. The details don't matter, but it was an unfortunate event. There's no need for a manager to be anything other than polite to a customer, no matter the type of day or how busy the store is. A little self awareness goes a long way.

I'm a Starbucks fan.   While I've never been a huge coffee drinker, when Starbucks arrived in town that all changed. Yes, I know they're a tad overpriced. Yes, I know the coffee can be a bit bigger at times (Though I always think this comment is typical of someone who hasn't really experienced the variety that Starbucks offers).   I don't care. I'm a Starbucks fan. I'm a fan of the coffee. I'm a fan of the culture. I'm a fan of their devotion to customer service. I'm a fan of their recent decision to pay for college for employees.   This particular location, however, is not my favorite Starbucks location in Indianapolis. While they can still be mighty friendly at times, especially in the morning, it seems like far too often I've either experienced indifference or employees that lack the knowledge I usually associate with well-trained Starbucks employees. As a member of their "gold club," which you can equate to a frequent flyer in their rewards program, I'm fairly knowledgeable about their offerings and I expect their employees to know more than me. At this location, that doesn't always happen as was the case during my visit this evening.   I arrived to take advantage of a reward that I had received called a "Lucky Dozen." The Starbucks website encourages you to tell the barista you want to use your "Lucky Dozen bonus" so they'll know to look on your card and use it. When I announced this to the barista this evening, I got a puzzled look and a "What?"   While eventually a supervisor did get things figured out and it did work out, this isn't the first time at this location that I've had difficulty receiving rewards. As an added bonus (sarcasm noted), I also received the wrong receipt.   As a chain, I'm a Starbucks fan. In the case of the Starbucks at 56th & Emerson, it unfortunately drops down to a "like" with occasionally great service, occasionally indifferent service, and a lack of knowledge that puts a damper on the usual warm and friendly Starbucks customer service that keeps me coming back.

Very Disappointed of this Starbucks location! Always very slow and they always seem to hire NEW employees all the time. Oh, if you thought mobile ordering will be faster... think twice I waited a long time and decided to have my $ back.

Love my Starbucks, but it seems the drive through has become more of a priority that those who take the time to go in for take out. Love the atmosphere, enjoy my Starbucks coffee, but frustrated waiting for 3 lone staff members to finish serving several cars before pouring me (the only person in the cafe) my one simple regular coffee. Also wish they would change their sizes to small, medium and large I keep ordering a tall and getting a small.   Other than that there is so much potential and I will keep going back.

I went back for a refill of my iced tea small size and the girl in the photo refuse to give it to me after being here close to two and a half hours. She said that my cup look like it went through a dishwasher which was more than rude and unprofessional. Starbucks needs to retrain many of their employees that are absolutely horrible to customers or fire them. Not to mention overflowing trash cans inside and out. Terrible management at this location. I think they forget who the customers are. We're the ones who walk in and pay money for service.

It is not the best coffee in town, but it sure is consistent. I love the fact that I can walk into any Starbucks in the country and get something that I enjoy.  My drink of choice is a tall vanilla bean frappuccino with a shot of espresso, or a grande with two shots if I want a boost.  Give it a shot, it is delicious!  This is my favorite location in town, that fireplace is awesome for late night conversing!

This Starbucks is right where I work so I stop by frequently.  Service is consistently friendly and nice.   Obviously it depends on the hour how busy it is thus how wiped off tables could be, how prepared ya gotta be upon being the next customer helped, etc.  Nothing to complain about. Have always been served well!

Lately, consistently one of the worst Starbucks in the area. Never enough staff and when they are staffed, few baristas seem to have a clue as to what to do. You'll wait 10-12 min minimum for a drink and I've waited as much as 20 minutes.

Not bad, not superb. Just Starbucks. I love that there's a drive-thru but the location is off of the street so it's kind of hard to get here. Yesterday morning, the lid of my frappuccino was not properly applied to the cup, so it created a whole mess after I pulled off. Not cool baristas, not cool. But I've been here several times before then and had no problem.
If you plan to eat/drink inside be prepared to fight/swat at bugs the entire time. Gnats and flies Everywhere ..it's gross
This is the nicest Starbucks Iv been to. They were so friendly and nice. Also catered to my ridiculous coffee request for less calories! Love!

I love the location of this new Starbucks, everyone is super nice.  The past two times I asked for my tea to be sweet and I usually don't drink it until I get home to realize they forgot to put sugar in my tea.  I called the number listed online to speak with a manager and the phone just rings, when I place my order with them I always confirm that they say sweet and the response I received was unless it's mark unsweet it's automatically sweet.  There is nothing marked on my cup telling me it isn't sweet, I'm really disappointed to say the least.  Kaylyn the manager is awesome she resolved my issues without hesitation Thank you!

Terrible customer service. I could feel the thickness of bad attitude immediately. It was cold out & I needed a warm place to wait for my Lyft sooooooo.....I waited in the non-existent line for one of the 3 non-busy employees to acknowledge me. I ordered a Pike because I didn't twist then to craft my usual espresso with care. The young man placed my coffee on the counter & walked away.   I had to ask him, "Excuse me, are you ready for me to scan?"   Nonverbal gesture of a head nod was given.   I scanned and walked away disgusted.   Counter, nasty. Floors, nasty, display case, nasty. Service, nastier.   ~Christy

Yes, this is a busy Starbucks, but even when the line is very long (peak hours) they move people through quickly.  They always prepare my drink exactly the way I ask and I have never had a problem with them getting my order wrong.  There is one male barista (I wish I could remember his name) who is super entertaining and nice to all of the customers and puts a smile on my face every time I come through the drive-thru.  I always hear him say, "THERE she/he is!" when someone comes up to the window.  Even though I am so tired when I roll through at 6 am on Fridays, this guy cracks me up.  I also had a great interaction with a female barista there one time.  She was a very nice young woman who accidentally dropped my credit card on the ground next to the drive-thru window when she was handing it back to me.  I had to get out of my car to get it because it had fallen underneath the car, which was no big deal to me at all.  I kept telling her it was no problem, but she insisted that my venti iced coffee was "on her" that day.    Overall, a decent Starbucks that seems to amazing baristas!

For starters, I ordered my coffee  and pulled to the window, however- I wish I wouldn't have. While waiting for my coffee, I was informed by a disrespectful confrontational employee, claiming that I could not get continue to get marshmallow in my coffee. Knowing this is a lie- due to I get this flavor in other locations literally across the country weekly- got pissed off. She informed me, that they would not be giving me this coffee again. Due to I'm paying 7$ for a frickin coffee I will be getting the flavor I want and that I have no problems with at any other locations, so this chick needs to learn her facts before telling us we can't have a flavor that you offer.
Beautiful location, sweet employees & quality service!  We almost didn't go because of the negative reviews and our expectations were beyond exceeded!

Don't stop here if you are in a hurry! Consistently the slowest moving Starbucks I have ever visited, and I'm not sure why because they seem well-staffer. Expect a 20 minute wait in the drive through (even if there are only 2 or 3 cars in front of you).
They have really improved on their service since they first opened.  They are friendly and fast at the drive through !
Darren and Brianna are very good baristas and very helpful. They always deliver great service every time I go there.

This store is always closed! Or when they are open they are sold out of every item on the menu! It's a real shame living up here in Bordentown we don't have a lot of good stores  and Starbucks is my favorite. But this location is a real let down you never know if it's open or if they will have anything in stock!

If you have to go to a starbucks I would go to this one. It is my local sbux so I am a little bias. That being said, the staff is extremely friendly and never mess up an order which is what keeps me and my coming back to this location instead of any of the other locations that may be closer for them.

I love Starbucks in general, but I particularly like this one.  Mel is knowledgeable about products and externally helpful.  She is a pleasure!   My only complaint is the small parking lot and narrow drive thru, but it doesn't stop us from going there.

Totally worth connecting with everyday Hot Chocolate w/ a shot of espresso Make it a purpose to enjoy the tasteful drinks, snack, and much, much more. I really miss the one on Fall Creak and College. I miss the walk. I  U Starbucks

I frequent this Starbucks a few times a month and I'm always happy. The staff are friendly and the place is clean. So far my orders have been perfect, never messed up. One time they had a young girl singing and playing guitar, which was nice. The place is usually never dead, although never too crowded. I can always manage to find a seat.

This is my local Starbucks, and it's much like any other: smells delicious inside and seating is very limited. The baristas are very nice, but I usually find Starbucks help consistent across the board. I do prefer the stand-alone shops to the ones inside Safeways or bookstores or on the UofA campus. The location of this one is particularly ideal -- at the corner of Jasper and 109th, it's an easy-to-find meeting spot before heading off to explore the rest of downtown. Oh by the way, the new salted caramel mocha is delicious, I definitely recommend it!
Every time I've been to this Starbucks inside Target there's a line and it's understaffed. Every. Single. Time. not quite sure why. The Target Starbucks near my house is great. This one is not.

Trashiest group of employees I've encountered at Starbucks and I've been to some bad ones. Employees refused to give me names when I said I was calling corporate. Their aprons were dirty. One girl tried to shut the drive thru window when I was speaking. All this after waiting in the to go line got almost 30 minutes.

This is your average Starbucks. I went at around noon on a Sunday so of course it was busy as could be! The wait time wasn't too long, maybe about 5 minutes to get my drink. I ordered a Caramel Frappuccino. It wasn't the best one I had ever had from there, unfortunately. It was like it had no ice in it or had melted or something. It wasn't warm or anything, it just didn't have the consistency you'd expect from your typical frappe. My husband ordered his usual cappuccino and he said it was pretty good! I'll definitely be back to give this location another try.

Worst burnt tasting coffee I ever had in my life. Expensive is an understatement. I'm told you have to get one of the flavored coffees or some fancy drink and you'll like it. If you can't just get the base coffee right they shouldn't be selling it. Anyone can dump a bucket of sugar in something to make it taste like desert.

Half an hour in the drive thru for two cups of coffee! 3:00 in the middle of the afternoon,. I don't frequent Starbucks, I honestly don't know what the big draw is but now I have all the more reason to not go there. I have a friend who is homebound and was going to be near her home so I asked if I could get her anything, she asked for Starbucks. It took 32 minutes from the time I got in line until I left the drive thru to get 2 cups of coffee!!! When I pulled up to the window the coffee wasn't even ready! There were about 8 or 9 cars online ahead of me. How freaking long does it take to make and hand out coffee, you are a coffee shop!

This company is doing great considering social distancing. The tables were well spread out and the staff were friendly. My salted caramel cold foam cold brew was fantastic. Served with a smile (under their masks).  Thanks Starbucks for keeping the safety of your customers and staff in mind!

This is the worse Starbucks! I have been twice in the past week and ordered through the app, which stated it was a 1-4 minute ticket time. I live in New Orleans East and I order a latte while I was 20mins away and once I reached the store My coffee wasn't ready. They are extremely slow and should hire more staff for the morning rush! I had to cancel my order twice within the last week. I prefer to order from Starbucks on st Claude!

This is by far the worst Starbucks I've been too. This is the second time I waited about 10-15 minutes waiting for my drink. I had people behind me getting their drinks before I did. Only for them to forget about my drink. Really? Twice!

We live in the condos above this Starbucks and have been over 10 times. However, after this visit we will not be back. Previous reviewers stating that there are long wait times, inexperienced employees, poorly made drinks, and incorrect completions of orders are 100% correct. I asked for a latte with almond milk and it tasted like a hot cup of water. We ordered the everything bantam bagel bites and after two times of explaining our order to the male employee received an everything bagel. Lastly, our egg bites were given to us in an OATMEAL CONTAINER with no lid. Therefore, my egg bites spilled out and were found smushed on top of each other. Terrible service, knowledge of menu items, and overall food quality.   If you are in Nashville from out of town, DO NOT GO TO THIS STARBUCKS and visit the Dunkin Donuts downtown, Downtown Sporting Club café, the diner, or any of the hotels such as Noelle for coffee.

I ordered my coffee and breakfast eggs this morning. Coffee came and then finally 15 minutes after I was able to get someone's attention about my food being skipped. Barista was unhelpful asked me to order again, I showed him my order then proceeded to fill other peoples orders after mine. After 30 minutes left after demanding refund and nothing happening. I go here 6 days a week very disappointed

Horrible experience coming here. I was told I "shouldn't order" my coffee choice because "it takes too long." My reply was then  "oh, is that way there were 6 cars ahead of me because you arnt fast enough?" I will not be coming back. Horrible staff and they are extremely rude!

A continental hotel breakfast in disguise as a Starbucks. No coffee options other than Pike? Really? Really? I'm sorry. I thought this was a coffee shop. I was told that those options were only for breakfast times. Sorry I missed the last call. Then no wifi available for customers unless you're a Hyatt guest. What am I taking crazy pills! They claim to be separate from all other Starbucks since they're inside the Hyatt. Guess that means you should expect less. Right. I was passing by looking for breakfast and coffee before visiting the Archway. Figured Starbucks was a good idea but instead a real disappointment. Guess it's only here as an amenity to the hotel and it's proximity to the Archway. Please get with it.

Multiple workers were rude and unhelpful when I ordered a drink from the Starbucks website that I wanted to try that they weren't familiar with. I understand it can be frustrating if people order difficult drinks from secret menus that the baristas were not taught, but again, the drink I ordered is currently on the Starbucks website. The barista talked about me negatively as if I couldn't hear from the pickup window, and I felt like I did something wrong when I clearly hadn't. No one apologized to me and when I offered to read them the description from the website, I was shut down.
Friendly and kind staff. Mobile order was not available. Always has a bit of a line but it moves. Staff is conscientious

Customer service is horrible. I got a drink I get everyday and it didn't taste as it usually does. They weren't accommodating. I was going to have to pay more fir shots to make it taste stronger, even though 4 shots that I usually is strong enough. I had to complain in order to get it made again. Disappointing.

Wow!  Where do I start?  Hmm.. Let's just start with this.  I've been going to Starbucks nearly ever day of my life for the last.. oh,12 years.  Customer service is something that is a hallmark component of the Starbucks experience, and that being said, this Starbucks is downgright pitiful.  I would have given this 0 stars if I could have.  Besides the location being entirely inconvenient, the tiny parking lot, the poor layout of the store, and lack of seating, I honestly tried so hard to like this Starbucks.  It's the only one on my way to work (besides Park Mall) and if you're like me, a morning without Starbucks makes for a very bad day.  I was never entirely "wowed" by this location... and this past Saturday morning I was absolutely disgusted.  I went in on a Saturday morning.  The line was pretty long, but luckily I left early on my way to work  so I didn't mind the wait.  I have been drinking the same beverage for over 6 years.  At this point, I know exactly what it should look like inside the plastic iced venti cup.  I place my order, and I suppose the barista behind the register forgot her morning coffee, because I had to repeat my order to her 3 times.    After ensuring the order is correct, she rings it up and I pay with my Starbucks Gold Card.    Then I wait.    and wait......   and wait.    After about 10 minutes, I see a male barista holding a venti cup with what looks like espresso in there... Something about espresso, after about 10 seconds of being pulled, if you dont *do* something with it (drink it, pour it over ice, mix it with something), the espresso will "burn".    The barista took his time while reading the cup to try to decode the drink order, then commenced to putting ice inside the cup.  Watching him, the entire time, I already knew it wasn't right.  He then drowned the espresso in milk and put it on the bar and attempted to call out the order.    I approach the bar, and simply tell him 'I'm sorry, that's not right.  Can I please just have a refund?"  The whole time, the store is getting busier and busier.  at this time, the line is to the door.    Barista tells me "you hve to get a refund at the register"....   Did he ask what was wrong with my drink?  No.  Did he offer to fix it?  No.  Did he acknowledge that I may be a little short on time after waiting 10 minutes for a drink and that waiting 10 more minutes in line to get my money back was simlply not an option?  No.  Simply put, this barista could not care less.   Ok I know I'm dragging this on and on.. But I know for a fact that one of Starbucks' goals is to have such consistency with every store that every visit should be just as quality as the other.  This was the breaking point of this store.  I at that point called the Starbucks Customer Service number, lodged a formal complaint, and will be in contact with the district manager regarding this.   So yeah, I waited in line again.  I got my refund, and I will never ever be visiting this store again.  I will tell everyone I know about my horrible experience here relentlessly.    To the barista that attempted to make my drink, I think your name is Brian.  Never have I ever been so disgusted by anyone's lack of customer service.  You deserve a gold star for failure.  If this is the way you treat the customers that give you a paycheck every two weeks, then, my friend, I don't foresee such a bright outlook in your future.     I will continue to go out of my way to work to the Starbucks on Speedway and Country Club--- by far the best in town.

Severely understaffed on many occasions - I waited over 30 minutes for my frap and this is AFTER placing my order. Isn't the purpose for getting coffee, to get a daily boost not a daily depressant. Good place to go if you're waiting on your wife to shop and have hours and hours to kill. Otherwise please avoid. Goto the food court instead for a Java boost.

I like this Starbucks because:  - It's brand new and clean. - It's spacious with plenty of tables and seats to meet up with friends, work related meetings or study. - The high long table has many electrical outlets. You can basically sit anywhere and there'd be an outlet to use. - The Baristas are nice. - There is a drive thru.  I don't like this Starbucks because:  - It shares a parking lot with Chipotle so it's impossible to find parking during the lunch hour.

Not a fan of this Starbucks.   I would go as far as to say it is the worst in in Indy.    The staff is slow and unfriendly and the location always seems drab and dirty.   There are plenty of other locations just wish they would redo the staff and interior of this one because it is closest for me.
Great service, friendly staff, great coffee. Clean store and well organized shelving and displays.
The workers here are friendly and welcoming. They get your order write and make it exactly how you want it.

Anna & Ricky always take good care of my daughter- and Lisa is the fav barista on bar. My daughter has been treating me all week to her obsession! From breakfast sandwiches to coffee and tea! This has become a morning routine. I love the Very Berry Hibiscus Tea (Refresher). Berries and a light refreshing tea is the perfect blend for me! Connor always gets the vanilla sweet cream cold brew and my daughter loves her double tall latte, one pump white mocha and one pump mocha, heavy cream, extra hot, with extra whip in a grande cup (and yes she is that portentous). She's fan-cy. And the breakfast sandwiches! She has me hooked on the bacon gouda- but her fav is the sausage cheddar and the ham and cheese croissant. Drive thru is fast- and given that it's all drive thru at the moment, I am happy they are quick! I have been shotgun on many visits this week!

Good furniture. I like this location because they feature the most comfy chairs and couches of any Starbucks in south Tampa. That makes hanging out here a pleasure. The menu is the same as anywhere else and the staff turns over, so what else can you say? If you want to linger, then this one is your Starbucks location to choose. It's also next to a Panera Bread if you want a pastry or sandwich and don't like any of the Starbucks offerings.

I go to this location at least 3 times a week. Every time I go I have a pleasant and quick experience. My drinks are always spot on. This morning I forgot my wallet and didn't realize until I had already ordered. I informed the cashier and she let me have it for free! I can't remember her name but I see her almost every time I visit. Thank you for the coffee Starbucks! And to this cashier, next time I see you, you will be getting a large tip. Thanks again for being my favorite coffee joint!

This store is dirty and gross. Employees are rude and I even saw water leaking from their ceiling onto their coffee machines for hours. Dirty!! Onto their drinks.   AVOID. Go local!!! So many other cheaper and better local coffee places within the same block.

I've been coming here recently to work "at home" and am enjoying it for the most part. Every time I arrive though, I'm *hoping* there's a seat near the front of the building. If you don't find one there, you're stuck in the back area that is quite dark. It's not bad to be in the back, it's just not as "cozy" to sit for extended periods of time.  Service is normal, and basically it's a convenient Starbucks for all the normal reasons (wifi, location, etc.)

Terrible service. Just went through the drive-thru and they got my order wrong twice. What is complicated about a grande hot latte with skimmed milk and a grande iced coffee with regular milk and 3 Stevia?  First time round I got 2 iced coffees. Second time 'round I got a hot vanilla latte. Of course by the time I discovered this error I was long gone. I can forgive the first mistake but I struggle with the second one.  Definitely will not be going back.

OH MY GOSH!!! I just left this location and got in the car with my husband and said, "if I wasn't married I would marry that Starbucks employee!" I have literally NEVER had such a great experience at Starbucks in my entire life and I've even worked at Starbucks. Whoever the guy running bar on a Tuesday 10/29 was deserves a huge pay bump! Wow. Ok so I'm a super snob about filling up a Grande amount into a Venti cup. Literally every single time I order it that way they always ask what I want with all the extra room. Honestly there's NOT A WHOLE LOTTA EXTRA ROOM. 10 ounces to be exact - so he filled up the grande cup and poured it into the Venti cup and it goes well over the highest top black line. Like a lot. And it's the perfect amount for the pumpkin cream on top and this is the best day of my life. THANK YOU  THANK YOU  THANK YOU!!!!

I love this location. It's just where you need it. Right across the street from a doctor's office. My fav drink is the green tea frap. The rice crispy treat is really good too. Free wifi and excellent customer service at this location.

I frequent this location only because I get a 25%  locals discount.   Their inventory is very minimal and their choices are limited, the customer service is very spotty.  Do not order the Chai Latte here, is hit or miss

A convenient Star$$$$ for this part of the world, with access from both Street Road and Second Street Pike.  Its proximity to some of my favorite restaurants - Aroi Thai, Blue Sage and House of Pho - make it a perfect quick après dinner stop for hot brew on the way to the movies or home.    It is not the most attractive franchise unit in the area, but it serves a functional purpose.   Unlike a box of chocolates, you always know what you are going to get.

Best Starbucks in town ~ where everyone knows your name! I meet friends and family hetr at least three times a week. It where my husband and I stop for coffee before church! It within walking distance from my house. I guess you could call me a "Starbucks Junkie"!

This is a newer location with plenty of seating and friendly employees. Since it is a new location, it has the ability to make a COLD FOAM NITRO, which is an amazingly smooth, lightly sweet cold coffee drink served out of a tap.  My husband tried the COLD FOAM DARK COCOA NITRO, which is similarly excellent. I go miles out of my way to get the cold foam Nitro drinks...they're that good.  This place is definitely worth the stop.

Today was my first visit to this Starbucks. I went to meet a friend, and when she arrived, there was a sign on the door saying they were closed due to safety concerns. I have no idea what that was about, but they were serving via the drive through. By the time I arrived, they'd removed the sign and let people in, so we could still sit and hang out. But I have no idea what that was about. Kind of a weird start to a visit.   The staff was really friendly, though they seemed to be slow with making the drinks. My hot tea took a solid 8-10 minutes to be made, which is unusual. The food and beverage case had a nice variety of food (sandwiches, salads, etc) and bottled drinks that I typically don't find at the Starbucks locations I tend to frequent. Definitely a plus for this one!   The interior has a lot of bar type seating, which made good use of the space. The outdoor has a nice sized patio, too, if it's not super rainy like it was today!

I love my Bordentown Starbucks baristas! Super friendly group and the store is always clean. I go out of my way just to go to this location because they always have my drinks on point. Keep up the good work!
Two perfect drinks after a night of zero sleep.  Soy misto for me, Mango smoothie for the BF.  Quick service too. Thanks Starbucks!

The slowest Starbucks I have ever been in. Ordered an iced coffee while on the go. Stood waiting for 7 minutes waiting for my simple drink while one barista fumbled around steaming milk and pouring it in the sink (what are you doing?????). Not sure why the other 3 workers are on the payroll because they are standing around doing absolutely nothing.   Will not come back, plenty of local coffee shops in the area that are better, faster, and likely cheaper.
My new favorite Starbucks stop every morning on my way to the office. Love the look and staff does a great job!
3 stars because they took away my neighborhood Starbucks, but 4 stars because it's spacious and they have nitro coffee on tap!
