If I could give a zero I'd do that. The chick with the beanie on and nose ring serving drinks is very much a jerk and if she didn't want to come to work today. She should've chosen a stay at home job and let someone more pleasant take her shift. I can see why this location has a 3

Super rude baristas.   This Starbucks is the closest coffee shop to my office, so I occasionally pop in for a cold brew. I regret it every single time. I'd rather drive across town to French Truck or Cherry where I won't be greeted with an eye roll and a snotty attitude.

This is a good Starbucks, but always SOOOO crowded.  The line is always RIDICULOUS, so I honestly avoid it unless I'm desperate for caffeine.   And good luck finding a table, any time of day....  However, they are friendly staff, even though you're surrounded by annoying suburbanites...

I frequent this Starbucks location to study and do work. The layout is okay and the staff is friendly.  Only complaint is the music selection is terrible. This is not a nightclub.  Maybe some 'coffee house' music would be great! Especially in the late mornings.

This place is a bit small, and can be a little cramped with too many people in it. It's conveniently located amongst the historical sites, but this causes many people to come in. The bathroom was really dirty, which was really shocking because it was locked with a code and we expected it to be cleaner from Starbucks. The bathroom smelled and the ground was really wet as if someone had peed in there. The cashier was really nice, but the drinks could have been made better. There were grounds in the coffee for one of our orders as that was slightly disappointing. However, I don't think it's anything that the establishment can't improve on!

I am a big fan of Starbucks even though they are a gross polluter. But this Starbucks is the worst.  It is poorly organized and service times are slow slow slow. Even with mobile order.  They seldom get a drink right even with mobile order.  Average wait times for your drink is about 12 minutes I usually mobile order and that's still about a 8 min wait time after ordering 15 min in advance. This store is too small to accommodate the crowd.
Worst service ever! So freaking slow I would fire all 3 girls that worked today if I were their boss

This Starbucks location is great. It's a lot less crowded than the St Charles x Canal Street location and most patrons are office employees looking to get in and out. The baristas are pretty friendly and know their stuff. I recommend ordering on your app if you're in a rush- I placed my order at the Poydras crosswalk and my iced coffee was ready on the counter by the time I walked into the store. The only reason I'm not giving them 5 out of 5 stars is because there seems to always be one or two fruit flies flying around the pickup counter every time I'm there.

Walked in before they closed at 6:00 pm, there at 5:55 pm and they said they were closed and wouldn't serve me. Stopped at this exit just to go to Starbucks. Need to have a talk with their employees about closing early!

This is my Cheers! The place where everybody knows my name and is happy that I came. The place where my caffeine fix is handled promptly and with a smile. I have been coming to this particular location for about four years now. Although I change up my drink order daily, the baristas are fast. Many times the line is long but they work through orders fast and with a smile. I do tend to avoid the Starbucks with drive thru, it always seems the cars take priority, so this location works for me! A big shout out to Danyelle, Tress, Jamie and Jeremy. Without you, my Good Mornings would just be plain ordinary mornings.
They forgot my cinnamon bun. I had to ask & didn't want to wait for it to be heated. So I couldn't enjoy as I wanted to. My coffee Flat White was good.
Wow, what a great store. There is one employee that is SO nice and good. Grace makes the best drinks EVER. Highly recommend.

I went to Starbucks this morning left my house 30 minutes early to give myself plenty of time to get to work on time there was 5 people in front of me in line and it took them 30 minutes for me to get to the front of the line never go here unless you wanna be late for work
Very friendly staff! The coffee is great(as always).  The only thing I'm confused about is why they have a white marble countertop.  I feel like that would be easy to stain with coffee.  Oh well
Nice place, good coffee. Annoying location, but that's the plaza designers fault.

I used to come to this Starbucks more often with work colleagues but now I'm here occasionally for meetings.  The line ups can be long but they tend to move quickly and the staff are efficient to get my drink fast.  But the spacing is definitely lacking.  There are tables and chairs but the people at the next table are an arm's stretch away so it's not good for any personal or confidential conversation.  Last time I was there, there was a person in a wheelchair and there wasn't enough space for her to maneuver around the people, tables, and chairs.  People also sometimes stand by the area where you get napkins as they wait for their drinks so it gets crowded.  The staff need to buzz people in for the washroom and I have seen people come in just to use the facilities without making a purchase and staff don't seem to mind.   Since most people take their drinks to go, I can usually find a seat but I still find it's not very comfortable compared to another coffee shop half a block away.

The Starbucks on the corner of 109 St. and Jasper Ave is a busy place, both in the restaurant and outside the window. Just a short hop from the Alberta legislature, I'm sure this Starbucks is popular with government types. It's also quite close by another government employee haunt, Ricky's Grill.  The ambience is not too bad. It's nice and bright, with windows going up 15 feet. No worries about a dark, dingy space here.   All in all, it's a pretty solid location. If you're worried about noise, plug in your iPod and pretend the rest of the world doesn't exist. It's fairly laptop friendly here, but there are no large tables, so workspace is a bit limited.   I took transit here, but the parking lot is free, but limited to 90 minutes. I pushed it when I parked the other day, but I don't want the hassle like I had at Northgate. Impark runs the lot.  There is some outdoor seating available in the summer months, but I'm not sure how many seats there are. There's a lot of traffic here, so it'll be noisy!  Busyness - For the early morning, it's not as busy as I'd figured it might be, but it's not far from Grant MacEwan University and the Alberta Legislature, so I'm sure it sees a lot of traffic from there and the other office towers close by  Noisiness - Combine a tile floor with hard surfaces, and you have a loud space. It's not too bad while I'm here, but I'm sure when all the tables are filled it can get quite noisy.  Ambience - It's a comfortable location and not a bad place for a quick meeting.  Power - I'm sitting tucked away in the back corner where there are power outlets directly under the bench seat. Another bench is also suitably equipped. If you're prone to wetting your pants, I wouldn't recommend these seats.  Free wifi - It is available and worked as advertised.  Staff - The staff here seem energetic and friendly, as is usually the case.  Seating - Ample seating is available here. The tables are all on the small side, so if you plan on gathering many people for an all-day, Starbucks fuelled programming session, find somewhere else, or occupy many tables. The tables are small. The seats aren't too bad. There are two benches with power outlets underneath. There are 31 seats, but the benches can squeeze in more (shower first please!).  Security - If you've read my other Starbucks reviews, you've probably gathered I'm a bit paranoid. You're right. Laptop thieves are everywhere! Take the appropriate precautions. I'm kind of a fan of going with an inexpensive laptop for public work. While I may like a Macbook, I'd have it chained to me in public. Could be a fashion statement.

This place was packed in the early morning, everyone getting their coffee and danish fix. I actually came by twice, once in the morning, once in the afternoon before our drive back home. Definitley a slower Starbucks compared to others, but they make my skinny vanilla latte perfectly! Only con is you can't reload your Starbucks card app or use a free bday drink at this location.

This Starbucks is really good.  They are consistently busy, and it's fun to watch the staff bustling around filling their orders.  Today I got my traditional Iced Chai Tea Latte, and my wife got a Skinny Mocha Frappuccino.  It was the perfect pick-me-up while browsing for shoes at the mall.

I went in to the Broadway location to get a Salted Carmel Coldbrew with no ice. The Barist handed it to me full of ice, and I nicely told her I ordered it with no ice. She took it back, scooped out the ice with a spoon and handed it back to me.  The drink was inch below the top of the cup and completely mixed. I asked her if she could add more coffee to fill it to the top. She plopped a scoop of foam on the top and walked away. The drink tasted only like foam. I will never go back there again. ($5.95 for foam, yuck)

It was after lunch and my friend and I needed a "pick me up".  We decided on a coffee from the experts at Starbucks.  The store was actually fairly busy for 1:30pm but we were helped right away.  I had the Pikes Place regular coffee with room for cream.  My friend had the peppermint white mocha in decaf.  The store is very clean and so was the bathroom.  The customer service here is very good and the employees are very professional.  My favorite drink at Starbucks is their Egg Nog latte but I only felt like a cup of plain coffee this visit.  Too much caffeine will keep me awake later.
All staffs who were friendly and helpful with fast order to take out.  Nice services and very good.

This used to be my go-to Starbucks and I'm not sure what has happened as of late but the service is horrendous! The last 4 out of 5 orders have been incorrect. The last order resulting in swallowing a mouthful of coffee grinds only to discover 3 inches of grinds at the bottom of my cup. They are way understaffed and the staff they do have need significantly more training and support. The trash is often overflowing outside.

The staff here is very friendly and polite.  The inside of the store is clean and organized.  With that being said, they are extremely slow.  There's generally a long line in the drive thru and it doesn't move very quickly.    All in all, it's Starbucks and if you're willing to wait a bit then swing by.

MUCH better experience this time - very friendly service and almost NO LINE - but it was 4 PM on a HOT (100 degree) Summer Saturday.  My 11 year old got a TRENTA aka "vat" of green iced tea.  We needed this!
This is a good Starbucks with friendly staff.   And they don't ever seem to get too busy, so no annoying lines.

Fabulous location, amazing staff, and fun music.  The store manager, Irene, is super friendly and makes coming here totally worth wading through tourists. I especially love the local artisan touches like their trombone chandelier and the filigree ironwork. My favorite part of the store is their Hurricane Katrina rebuilding photography. Starbucks is there when it matters!

There's nothing special about this Starbucks other than it is the only Starbucks location on the West Bank west of the Harvey Canal. All of the seats inside were taken by customers who appeared to be studying. I am glad my days of studying are long gone. We sat outside where there were plenty of seats.

Every time I go in the drive thru it's pretty slow, unfortunately. One time they were even closed for cleaning and didn't post any signs to indicate this in their drive thru area so there was a line of cars waiting. When I pulled up to the window to explain this to the employees, they gave me a curt response and apparently could care less. They don't seem to want to retain regulars.   I suggest using pick up when you can to avoid the drive thru and possibly have better luck.
I really enjoy this Starbucks because the staff is so nice and I don't have to worry about my drink being wrong! I've noticed with this one that when the line is long they still are pretty quick!

I wanted some coffee so badly I just pulled up to this Location and I asked the manger she's a girl the one has the scarf on her head . I asked her at 9:23 pm which is right now of the store still open she said no closed with the most bad attitude ever seen

My experience with Starbucks today's was awful. I did a mobile order today thinking I could walk in a pick up my drink cause I was late. Instead of that they made me wait in the long drive thur line to pick up my drink. Took me 30+ minutes get though the line and by the time I get there my drink warm and water down cause all the ice was melted. (never end up drinking it)So I ask the employees for a cup of water, during that process she sneezed and proceeded hand me the cup of water. I could never feel soo disgusting.

This Starbucks (Wingfield location) has the nicest crew!!!!  I am a coffee snob, but I also appreciate nice people.  You want to start your day off right, go out of your way to come to this Starbucks.  They're dog-friendly (outside patio only).    I am updating this review as all employees are new to me.  However, they're still so very nice.   Good place to stop for any kind of break, breakfast, lunch, dinner, etc.

My drink order is pretty simple (iced coffee, light ice), but it's been screwed up in pretty amazing ways.  It's had hazelnut, extra ice, regular sweetener, and various milks put into it.  If you count two drink errors in one trip, I've actually had my drink made wrong more times than I've even been there!  Also, the morning people have a habit of calling everyone "Steve."  I walked through the door, and was greeted with a "hi Steve!"  I figured they just thought I was someone else, until they greeted my friend who came in right behind me ("hi Steve!") and then the woman behind us ("hi Steve!").  I'm not asking for stern professionalism from every barista in the world, but that's just dumb.

What happened to this place?!? It's really gone down the tubes... I used to love coming here. It was home. And now... it's nothing more than a factory of poorly and slowly constructed beverages made by newbies who clearly are not receiving proper training (at no fault of their own). What happened here? It's clear the new hires don't even have a chance since they seem neglected through the training process. And now, there's nothing but employees who are failed by the system.   And not to mention, the manager... She is not approachable at all, and I've never seen manager interacted with employees and customers in such a disrespectful way.

This was one of many coffee stops along my route. Found within a large travel center, this small SB location made some good cold brew. It was a quick stop, but an efficient one. The staff here was friendly, but very busy. I was not the only one in need of a good cup of Joe. After getting my drink, so went on my merry way. Good service, I'm giving it five stars.

This morning while visiting Reno I was surprised to find very few Starbucks offer a drive through.  I finally found this locationthe line was long, but  it moved quickly. The employees were professional and friendly. Thank you!

Starbucks is Starbucks, but this one ALWAYS seems to have a line, and the retail area is hard to get to when there are ten people standing in line...it makes you feel bad that you want to browse the mugs and gifts.  Still, they are a friendly bunch behind the counter, and it's always nice and clean in here, unlike some other Starbucks locations where there never seems to be a clean table.  Lovin' my peppermint mochas this holiday season as always...I'm limiting myself to one per week!

Free parking! Highly convenient location right in the centre of Edmonton's most busy area. Reasonable prices and FREE parking is provided right at the Starbucks. My family and I went here and enjoyed the service. The whole outdoor mall seems well managed. You can get groceries, supper, coffee, ice cream and everything else you need in one strip mall.

This used to be my go-to Starbucks, but since this day I think I'll just have to drive an extra block or two to get my daily cup, or try the new local place around the corner. I was annoyed about the milk ring (so gross), but even more annoyed that my Americano was so poorly made. I would have forgotten all about it but I complained to the supervisor, gave my information, and was told the manager would contact me the following day. Well, that was weeks ago so apparently they didn't care to make things right with one of their regulars. Which sucks because my original barista there, Bailey, is legit one of the best in town. Miss her already   Check your coffee before you pull off people! I know that's easier said than done.

Excellent branch. Not too crowded, decent amount of seating and lots of parking. Thankfully they were not overrun by tens of inconsiderate people doing work all day and taking up every single seat, as often happens in Yardley and Newtown.

This Starbucks is located in Harrahs on the second floor. It is a fairly small Starbucks, and there seems to be wait most of the time. I ordered a medium latte, with nonfat milk at 140 degrees, and it came out so hot. I could not drink it for about ten minutes. I really think they burnt the milk. The service was OK, more like cattle being herded in and out. Will I be back, probably not.

I have went to this location more than enough times that I can say this place is ok. The drinks and food are great but the service is so slow. Workers are friendly but it seems they're short staffed all the time! I have went in and went to the drive thu.  Just be prepared that you may have a long wait and I don't recommend when you are in a hurry

I am usually pleased with AAFES starbucks locations but this is the worst one I've been to . They continually charge for syrup which should come with the coffee.  It makes no sense how on base its tax free and I would be paying less going to an off base location.  If you are going to charge that much at least mix my ice coffee properly so I won't have all the cream at the top.

This isn't my favorite cafe as the selection in the immediate area is too good with the crown going to the -new- Capital One Cafe. This Starbucks is very busy and as a result very loud. It's certainly not a place I personally see myself getting a lot of work done in. Really, I just get my drink and head out. The staff are nice as is expected of Starbucks and they are a saving grace here.
You know what to expect when you walk into any Starbucks in a strip mall...a smallish store with decent seating and pleasant baristas. This one is exactly what you expect.

I stayed at the hotel across from this Starbucks and walked to this Starbucks. I am from the west coast and was looking forward to going to Starbucks and talking to the barista about things to do around town. Well that didn't happen! One, this is a very busy Starbucks even at 830am. Two, not one employee smiled or looks at you. And three, when you finally get to the register, you don't get a greeting, just a ' what can I get for you'. Now let me say, no one is rude but for a southern town I was very disappointed how unfriendly this Starbucks was. I will stick to the hotel coffee for the rest of my trip.

This Starbucks is Great!  The employees are so friendly and attentive and very competent. You get your drink fast and perfect every time.  Maddie, Stephen, Drew, Stacy, and Brad are the best!!  This store is a neighborhood staple. We have been waiting for it forever. Yay!

Stop by for an afternoon ice coffee. Had to wait to order while they fixed online orders and drive thru. Barista was not real friendly, will be sure to go to drive they or order online. Our sweet cream cold brew was ok not great.

As someone who used to work at Starbucks, I feel I have enough authority to say that this is one of the best Starbucks I have been to.   My drink is always made exactly right, and all the employees are very friendly. I am known on a first name basis by several of them, and I visit this location 3-4 times a week.   The store is very clean and spacious. Even when they are busy, there are enough seats to be had and the service maintains a steady 5 out of 5.

This is my office away from home. As others have stated, parking is a mess. But I usually find something. The baristas seem nice enough. I've never had problems. Upstairs is great to kill time or get work done. On occasion, the music is really loud so people have asked to lower it. But overall, a good space.

Horrible customer service in the afternoon. I do not recommend this Starbucks. As the customer I except friendly kind service unfortunately the afternoon crew does not offer this. The tall blonde girl and girl with the nose ring are rude. I called the Starbucks manager and left two messages still no returned phone call. In the future I will go to a different location to get good customer service. Huge let down.

Oh man. I don't normally write reviews for EVERY SINGLE STARBUCKS I go to, but this one was so bad, it warrants a review. While it's super convenient in location, the really young staff could care less about their job. There was one younger gentleman who was basically doing every single thing (running the register, making drinks, etc.) while 2 young teenagers just sat around. The manager needs to seriously review applications for hiring here... :(

If you want to be perpetually late for work every single day, go to this Starbucks. While I have never had any issues with the attitude of the staff, this location just can't seem to operate like the well-oiled machine that other Philadelphia Starbucks pull off without barely breaking a sweat. Regardless of the time of day that I enter this Starbucks to satiate my craving for "fast food coffee," every visit seems like this location's rush hour. When I finally take my place in the long line that snakes through the front of the store... AFTER I walk through the gaggle of angry, caffeine-deprived patrons who seem to have been waiting for their drinks for longer than what it would take to go to South America and harvest the beans themselves, my order is taken by a 'bucks employee. That employee's sole purpose is to play a game of "telephone" with the cashier and the barista. By the time my order makes its way down to the barista, it is so jumbled that it is wrong at least half of the time. To make up for this, I have received more "one free drink, courtesy of us" cards from this location than I have from any other coffee establishment in the city. While I applaud their efforts to make up for their lack of communication, I just wish that I could go here and not only purchase a properly-made beverage, but also make it to work on time without having to practically spend my entire morning shift waiting on my iced soy vanilla latte.

I ordered online 1 grande mango dragonfruit lemonade from this location. When my bf came there to pick up, they said they do not have that, but they still charged my money and just gave me 1 green tea lemonade. Even they gave me that drink, I still feel so bad because why they did not told me when I ordered. I got mad at my bf too. They ruin my day.

Slowest service I have ever experienced. 6+ people waiting for their orders. Witnessed an employee swamped with 5+ drinks he had to make. He asked the manager who else was supposed to be helping him & she said there was no one else and proceeded to be not helpful and argue with the employee. Instead of being a manager & team player she watched him as he struggled. Arguing in front of customers is super unprofessional. I felt bad for the employee. Won't be returning !!

Although the people who staff this location are very friendly and welcoming, the accuracy of the orders is below acceptable. I once ordered a lemon loaf and the label on the bag said lemon loaf and when I checked, it was a banana bread in the bag. Another time, the lemon loaf had a large discolored spot where it looked like something spilled into the sealed bag. Today, I ordered a grande vanilla latte and later found out that the cup was completely just foamed vanilla milk with no coffee at all. Again, in all instances the label was clear as to what the order should have been. It's unfortunate because this is one of the closest Starbucks to me. Can NOT recommend this location sadly.

Stopped at this place when leaving main event. The kid wanted a pink drink so there we went. Ordered a y'all pink drink with no ice and then a venti with ice...simple right? Well, they got the pink drink size correct but gave us an extra large cup of ice! Luckily for the kid, that size sufficed when she poured the pink drink in there!

So I'm traveling from Florida to NY yesterday, left at 5:00am and got to the John Fenwick Service Area Starbucks rest stop and well over due for some caffeineafacation to continue my trek to NY and they were closed already at 9:15.  Is it just me that thinks it's ridiculous at a rest stop to have such limited hours? And on Yelp just looking now it has their posted hours till 10:00 pm, wtf! I find Starbucks to be trending away from customer service, i.e. Products unavailable half way through their day of operations and such limited hours, like who would ever want coffee after 9 pm?
By far my favorite starbucks. Great staff, consistent drinks. Mobile ordering is pretty awesome too. It gets pretty packed, but what starbucks doesnt?
Chai latte tasted horrible, like the syrup was rancid. Asked for a replacement, same thing. Lots of flies on the ceiling. I give two stars because they were helpful in trying to remake the drink.
Great service! Considering the line is generally long (students need their coffee), the staff is still very friendly and extremely quick.
Coffee is good.... Place is comfortable, but loud and wifi is slow.  Service is excellent.

Claire is outstanding. Keep her. Make her your manager. Give her more money. I stop in a few times a week on my way home from work, usually after a long day and Claire is always so patient and friendly. She suggests things when I don't even know what I want. She is a gem.   I have seen quite a few different drive thru attendants, and I usually come thru around the same time. Yesterday I came through and as I was at the window, I could see and hear the manager say she was taking over to get the line down before she sends someone on break. Nice to see management step in and help.
Great service for a busy crowd. Drink was as expected and served with a smile. Great job to the management of this location.
Beautiful new Starbucks with plenty of parking and a fabulous look inside.  Great to have a 3rd Starbucks between Havertown and Broomall that has plenty of seating and the option of a drive through.

Very disappointed in my experience there. I bought two caramel frappuccinos and they were nothing but ice and foam when I got home I had to brew my own coffee  to add to it. I will not go back to that location

I don't normally review Starbucks for the most part they're okay but this location has a few people with attitude problems. The location is good and they're fairly quick but not anything that stands out as far as the Starbucks chain goes.

it was nice at first the people there were nice and almost always got my drink right and then all of a sudden these new employees came around and were just rude. i asked for extra carmel in my drink and it ended up being a third carmel. i miss the way it used to be but i need to move on.

On a cold rainy morning, there's nothing like a Starbucks right down the block at 6 a.m. to really pick you up.  This particular Starbucks was one of the best I've visited in a while.  Not only was the coffee dark roasted and black enough to kill, but the store was very easy to visit and order from, and the orders came out so fast! I went there with two of my friends who were attending an event on Sunday morning with me in the City Plaza, and the time it took us to arrive, order our drinks, and take off was probably less than 5 minutes.   Additionally, the service was simply outstanding! The one-woman show barista- and cashier-in-one in this smaller franchised store, inside the Harrah's Reno, was so nice and pleasant, and had a beautiful smile on her face the whole time we were there. It was just the thing we needed to get our morning off to a great start.  Overall, a great early morning experience and one I would gladly opt for again.

Why does anyone go to Starbucks?  Slow, dirty. The incompetence of the staff oozes throughout the entire store. So many better places close by. If you want long waits, poor service, dirty store, by all means this is your place. Try the Mustache Cafe for the best in SB.
My Favorite Starbucks! The staff ROCKS!  Enjoy a couple lee brew or a hot brew. This is the best Starbucks in the city.    Keep the yummy drinks flowing

The products are always on point. But since this is the only Starbucks within miles, the lines are always long. Never any available tables. You will definitely want to pre-order your stuff if you need it fast.   WE NEED MORE LOCATIONS,CORPORATE!  GIDDY UP!

I go to this Starbucks only because I work right around the corner, so it's based on convenience. I never have an issue with the service or the wait times etc, I just can NEVER seem to get the exact same order twice. I, much like thousands of other coffee drinkers, usually have a regular go-to coffee order, so I basically order the same thing every time, but rarely do I ever get the same quality.  Often times I'll order coffee at 7-7:30am and it's room temperature. I'll ask for cream and sugar and get either barely any sweetness or super sugary, this morning I asked for cream and sugar and it looked like they put no cream in it.  Guys- I understand the struggles, I work in the service industry too, but you're making coffee not building spaceships. I'm sure you have a great training program but PLEASE do better.

I work next door to this Starbucks and come here all the time. I sent my friend in to grab a sandwich for me and she returned with the wrong one, becuase the woman working gave my friend the wrong one. She went back to return it and grab the correct panini and upon returning the woman working, Joy, was extremely rude to my friend. Talking in a disrespectful tone, and giving the worst customer service. This starbucks has ALWAYS messed up my orders anyhow.

MISTAKE STOPPING HERE! Never check reviews before stopping at a STARBUCKS...but I will now! Die hard Starbucks fans on vacation, stopped on a Sunday before noon. Should have realized that the flies and I mean a lot of flies inside (um can you say "dump") was a bad sign. One person in line who took forever to order her tall iced coffee. Ordering was easy - passion ice tea and a decaf iced with cream and pumpkin. Simple drinks.  Tea came out in decent time...coffee try 10 minutes (mind you there was a drive thru, but two baristas running drinks).  Didn't tell us they had no ice decaf and only because we were watching and overheard convo where they were like just make a latte...usually they sub decaf Americano in any other cafe...and they let you know. One person starts it after we correct them and then it sits while they go do other things...a second person picks it up and completes, not before squeezing caramel on top! We question what's on top and while she was nice, after we said we didn't order that way she said "do you want it with it?" - ah we just told you we didn't order it that way! Took another 5 minutes to get it right...meanwhile a father and daughter enter and loudly say "look no line for once!" Clearly there is an issue at this location.

Just got coffee and met the loveliest young woman. Cassie in the drive-thru was a breath of fresh air. She was friendly, mentioned that I had enough points for a free coffee and commented on how good my order looked as she handed it to me. She made eye contact and was very engaged. Mind you, she had already charged me (thru app) but offered to reverse it to let me use points instead. So nice!! This is my regular location, but Cassie is definitely a few steps above the norm. I called the store too and can only hope this review or my call reach the store manager at the least. I will actually look forward to seeing her again.

I like this Starbucks because it's on my way to work early on Saturdays and Sundays. The place is usually empty and I'm always pleasantly greeted. I come almost every week, so the baristas remember me and my order! That has never happened and I love it.   I tried the other Starbucks nearby (the one with the drive thru) for a while, but I like this one more for early morning.   I did come here once in the afternoon and it was beyond crowded... I think I'll stick to the early morning pleasant experiences.

Ridiculous. As a provider of paper, plastic and other items that eventually will need to be disposed of, there are NO TRASH CANS outside of the establishment. On my way into this location, I picked up a paper bag and a couple of cups (not from Starbucks), that some piglets had left in the parking lot. I asked the guy at the front counter if I could drop them in their waste can behind the counter and he said no, they only allow Starbucks trash. When I explained to him that this trash was in the Starbucks parking lot, and that there were no trash cans outside, he shrugged and said it was "company policy". W.t.H.??? So I asked him what he suggested I do with the trash, another shrug. Had no choice but to leave it where I found it outside. Did I say ridiculous?

When it comes to coffee I try my best to stay local but I often find myself craving Starbucks' unbeatable scones. When this happens I drive to this location and indulge. The coffee is good and the pastries are even better. It is always clean and totally friendly!  For those that don't feel like parking and walking 10 - 15 steps to get to the door of Starbucks, this one has a drive-thru. If you see a line don't drive off, the line moves quickly.
Horrible horrible horrible customer service. This location is more worried about pushing customers in and out that they don't care about customer service.

When it comes to quality of products and customer service this Starbucks location is just normal. Most times when you walk in its clean, the bathrooms are decent and the drinks and food are average.   What does really suck about this Starbucks is not the location but how is situated on the edge of Hillsborough and Memorial Highway. I am surprised that there has never been any major accidents during rush hour traffic with people trying to get their morning cup of joe. Even when it's not rush hour the parking lot is ridiculously small and the curve that you have to take to swing into the drive thru is bonkers!   When I'm in a time crunch or just feel like taking the chance and not heading the other direction to another Starbucks I'll shoot to this location. If you're looking for a lot of seating... negative! If you're even looking for a space to park your car...head to another Starbucks! If you don't mind taking a chance on swinging through the drive thru and it's on your way to wherever you're going then this Starbucks is convenient.

usually this starbucks is very good! but my past experience changed my whole mindset. i ordered a grande hot chocolate at kids temperature. when i got to the window the lady gave me a kids sized hot chocolate. i told her i ordered a grande but at kids temperature, not the kids size. and she was so rude, she said she could remake it but i paid for a kids. that's not my fault because i know that i asked for a grande. she gave me the rudest attitude and remade it, she made me feel like i was being so difficult but she is the one who screwed up my order, not me.

This starbucks is always packed and working at its highest potential. Never had an order missed or wrong. Friendly staff that work hard to get things right. For the amount of pain that regular pain in the butt customers give, ya'll always shine through and make it happen.   Know that I hate to see what pain regular customers give to ya'll, but damn if ya'll just put people in their place by ensuring the product you serve is on point! Keep up the good work!

Honestly have no idea why anyone would actually go here based off of what I've seen. The staff here are pretty terrible for the most part, (there's only 1-2 nice ladies that know what they're doing) and they seriously don't know how to make drinks at all. This isn't a real Starbucks as this is privately owned by the school, but can they at least make the products correctly? My friends always go here and complain to me that their drinks are watered down or they forgot to add several things in the drink.  Takes them forever to make them as well! The staff seriously does not give a flying crap as I've seen one of them playing on their phone while I was literally standing there at the register. (I eventually just walked away) Another time, I was with my friend when he went to get an iced caramel macchiato. Took them (no joke) 15 minutes to make.... I think this was due to having 3 employees, one of them walking around and doing nothing (from what I've observed from when I was standing there for 15-20 minutes), the other one standing at the register not bothering to help do anything but stare blankly out the window, and another person slowly making the drinks. There are plenty other stories I can go on about, but I'll just make it easier for you. Just walk the extra 20 minutes and go to the next Starbucks you see. Or maybe, just walk to another cafe that's less than 15 minutes away!

Came in to try out the Mango Dragonfruit at Starbucks. Awsome refreshing drink. This exotic magenta color drink has a bursting sweet tropical flavor you can't resist. I've always heard about the benefits of Dragonfruit, but what I like from this drink is that it comes with real red fleshed dragon fruit (aka Pitaya) that makes it sweet without  needing to add sweetener  on it.  This drink is definitely for the summer! Sweet and Refreshing! Try one out.

I am a semi-regular at this location.  I often end up here when I have some time to kill before or after shopping at Merenda's Italian Specialties, which is one of the most awesome Italian groceries I've ever been to, and which I highly recommend.  I like this place, and I obviously keep coming back.  I like the ambiance, minus the view of the parking lot, and I like the location, again, minus the suburban sprawl that it lives in.  My main complaint about this location is that the staff seem really overworked, and I don't understand why.  I suspect it's the drive through that's the culprit.  I've been in here time and time again, when there is no line (inside, there's usually at least a car or two waiting at the drive through), and it takes quite some time, often over a minute, for someone behind the counter to even come over to me.  For example, today there were FIVE employees behind the counter, no line, very few customers in the shop, and they were all doing things behind the counter in a frenzied / hurried looking fashion.  All five of them!!!  What the heck are they doing?  Cleaning things?  Making drinks for the drive through?  I don't know, but I don't get it.  Maybe I have trouble understanding it because I always order stuff that requires limited effort, like tea, coffee, or a baked good, and maybe other people are ordering ultra-special-involved drinks that require 4 people to expend 5 minutes each just to make the freaking drink.  But I don't know, that just doesn't seem plausible.  I've been to so many other coffee shops where there are fewer employees, yet the employees like, walk right up when a customer comes up to the counter.  Some of my favorite coffee shops in West Philly have only 1 or 2 employees at a given time, and they handled more volume than this shop (no drive through though).  I don't like it.  It creates a stressy vibe.  When I stand at the counter, I feel like I'm absorbing the stress of the employees.  I don't feel like they're really 100% there, when they ask me what I want.  Like, I'm sitting here in the coffee shop as I write this review, and the employees are constantly running back and forth behind the counter in a frenzied fashion, yet there has been a total of one customer at the counter the whole time.  Ahh, here two more customers come in, finally.  I'm watching the interaction.  The employee isn't making much eye contact.  The two customers look so nice.  The guy behind the counter looks so stressed.  Oh, here someone comes out of the kitchen...she looks so stressed out too.  Seriously, Starbucks corporate management, DO something about this location.  The poor stressed, overworked employees...they don't look like they are having a good time, and they're making it a less pleasant place to be for the customers inside the shop.  I really want this place to have a genuinely chill vibe.  That would be like, really chill.  And I'd like that.  Heck yeah.

Starbucks are hard to find in this area. I have to pick a direction and basically go out of my way 30minutes to get to a Starbucks. This is the 3rd time in a row now I've had horrible service plus my drink was wrong. I won't be back to this location. I'm not sure what's happened recently to your customer service but your employees seem very inconveniences with having to do their job. I say job bc my requests are basic uncomplicated drinks usually only one drink.

You know I love SBucks. I mean there are other good coffee bars but none so convenient. If you're on the go or in a hurry this is the choice. They try their hardest to get the drive thru and mobile orders out first but I usually go inside to pick mine up. It is one of the busiest SBucks in FL outside of Disney but give them credit. They do a great job.

ONE star because theres no choice for ZERO .. wanted a nice cold brew drink, they didn't have the white mocha syrup it included, ordered their current special (kiwi star fruit refresher) they didn't have the star fruit syrup for it. Lost my patience, stuck in drive thru, avoid this Starbucks at all costs!!!

Girl at the drive thru window was very attentive and made sure my order was right, with a great smile! You need more partners like her (: Very quick service as well. Happy to have a sbux in the area! From a former employee to you guys, keep it up!

I go after work around 6:30.. even if I'm the only one in line it takes forever to get my drink.  The employees are holding conversations with each other or a friend who came in to say hello and they can't seem to talk and make coffee at the same time.  Very frustrating.  N

they had FIVE OR SIX workers standing in front of us yesterday. when my friend and i were waiting for ten minutes,, a male cashier came up to us and asked if we had been helped. when we said no, he walked away to talk to the others, as he did before. when we finally got our order in (twenty minutes later) , they gave us our drinks and two cake pops. one cake pop didn't even have a stick , and one of our drinks barely had any chocolate chips. it was ridiculous. never going to that starbucks location again.

I come here every morning.   The only time that I do not come is when I'm running late for work. The staff here is marvelous. They know what I want when I come in and they call me by name. Thank you all for being my morning pick up!
Good standard Starbuck's service as usual, with the standard menu.

While driving from Louisville to Chicago, I made a stop over here.  It's conveniently located next to the interstate near the exit.  Quick service, friendly staff and clean store. The coffee tastes as same as every Starbucks coffee tastes. I love Starbucks!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

AM I DREAMING? Starbucks opened less than 5 minutes from my house, and its a drive through!!! I'd been dreaming of this moment for all of my suburban mom life (which has been less than 10 months at this point, but still...). Dragging a baby out of the car to get coffee in the cold or rain is less than ideal, and Dunkin was the only close option. Now there's a starbucks drive through right on Rt 130!   I still miss La Colombe (and being able to walk to get coffee) but this is the best option within 5 minutes of my house.

It's a Starbucks. If you've been to one, you've been to them all. If you want to get into specifics then I suppose you could harp on the service. I've never been blown away by anyone there. They kind of just do their job. It's done right, but nothing to write home about. However, there is barely ever a line. Even in the drive thru I know I rarely have to sit behind more then one car.

I think Sybille is right, we could all go for a midnight frapp. That being said, I commuted here from Boyertown to get coffee. Boyertown only has Dunkin Donuts or Turkey Hill Gas Station. The woman who did monthly updates for her reviews was right, there was an older woman. When we first walked in, the line was about 10 people long with just the older woman. We decided to walk around until the line died down (10 minutes). When we came back, the last lady in line was only 3 away from the front, but a younger woman had come back from a break, I guess. All Starbucks drinks pretty much taste the same. The Madeline cookies we got were stale. Went back 24 hours later, same woman working. Quicker service because there wasn't a long line. Ordered 3 of the same drinks because Monica said she consistently had good drinks. They were all the same. Lol, great work xo
Excellent and fast service. The line was insane long but i didn't wait almost at all. The staff was pleasant despite the crowd and they worked fast.
I'm sorry but the staff here is just rude. I'll just go to the one down the street next time because they are way nicer. Guess I should have known going to a Starbucks inside of a target

I rarely visit this specific Starbucks location, but whenever I do there seems to be an issue that reminds me of why I don't come here! They're always out of something and seem to barely be capable of putting together a drink correctly. Now yesterday was probably my third time experiencing a nasty attitude from one of the Baristas. When I ordered my frappe, I specified that I wanted NO whipped cream. Of course once I received it there was whipped cream on top, (figures). I told the Barista I wanted no whipped cream, as if to only remind her of something she clearly forgot, and she tells me, "ok well next time just let us know" (full blown attitude). I'm over this Starbucks Location!

This place changed my life. I've always thought that yelp was for older woman who leave petty 1 star reviews. I just downloaded the app so i could leave a review to this place. I was going through a messy long term relationship break up. The day after before school I came here, and Deb, the lady at the drive through window was the nicest person i think i have ever met. I was doing poorly financially at the time, so I went here because the water is very good and free. I also ordered a blueberry muffin because i felt bad. Deb noticed this and said that if I just wanted the water that's okay and i shouldn't feel obligated to order something else. This put me in the mood to change my life for the better. Immediately, i made a list of goals that i wanted to accomplish. And since then i have worked out every single day, read more books, became a better brother, friend, and person. The only thing that hasn't changed is that i come here every morning to say hi to Deb. To deb, thank you so much for making the world a better place.

I've never actually been inside this Starbucks. As often as I've gone it's always been the drive through.  So I cannot comment on the interior but I can say the drive-thru Baristas are always friendly and they get my order right 100% of the time. Can't ask for anything more than that.  Honestly the only reason they don't get 5 stars is because this location is so darned inconvenient, which isn't really there fault but it's still a thing so..

I went to the McCarran drive-through Starbucks on a Pumpkin Spice Latte mission the other day. Having recently moved to Reno I found it a bit hard to find. I had to double back and circle around a bit. I know that's my own problem tho. Hopefully I'll figure Reno out eventually.  This Starbucks is located in a business park and seems like a pretty business-y Starbucks. It's super-nice from the outside which is the only part I saw. A nice, new, modern building. Drive through a little confusing too, but hopefully I'll figure it out soon.  Once I got to the drive through the guy who helped me was super-nice. I got a Venti Pumpkin Spice Latte as it was a cold Fall day.   I love Starbucks as a general rule. Knowing Reno had a bunch of Starbucks reassured me that I would be okay living in Reno. This was not my favorite location as it was sort of far away and hard to get into, but it was a good Starbucks experience overall.  When I got to the drive-through window the sweet tattoo-ed hipster handed me my drink. Nice hipsters instead of snotty ones are such a new concept after Los Angeles. This nice hipster neglected to give me a straw. That would have been nice, but wasn't that big of a deal.  My Pumpkin Spice Latte was delicious as usual. I loved it. I gave grandma a little drink of it when I got home and she liked it too.  I'll probably check out the other drive-through Starbucks in Reno before coming back to this one but it's nice to know it's there. Love that pumpkin spice!

I have been going to this Starbucks multiple times a week since it opened.  I have seen so many different staff there and all have been fine, but in the last couple of weeks at least two baristas have been immensely rude. My friends have noticed it too. Something is wrong.
Free wifi, seating inside and out, friendly staff, fast service, and overpriced drinks.

Bright and cheerful with floor to ceiling windows.  Limited match here.   Majority of seating remover for COVID.  A couple parking stalls but hard to come by.   Not my fave location for a leisurely bev but delicious none the less.

This had been my neighborhood Starbucks until just recently. Great staff, well managed and friendly. The decor has an unfortunate "Stalag" feel about it though.  Kudos to Starbucks for it's new policy banning smoking outside it's stores. Nothing ruins a beautiful day visiting with friends at my favorite "Bucks" that some goof ball smoking. Smart, courageous and the right thing to do. Congrats SB

At first I was skeptical about the location of this new Starbucks. But it turned out to be perfect! Looks small from the outside but very spacious (and clean) on the inside with plenty of seating. Also has a very nice outdoor patio. Nicely designed, large drive-thru. Very friendly baristas. Great location & great coffee!
Starbucks is a "treat" coffee for me cuz I usually do DD but when I want my coffee sweet with a lil kick of extra I go here. Love it. And he threw in a cake pop for free

I'm a loyal Starbucks customer, until today because of the staff at this location.  Not only could they not fulfill a basic order, they were COMPLETELY rude. They made it clear that they DIDNT like customers. During the pandemic, customers at food places aren't just there because they are hungry or thirsty, we are supporting the places we like so they can stay in business.  Olivia and the other Starbucks employee were so rude not just to me, but EVERY customer that came in.  I won't be back, and I'm rethinking about how much $ I spend at Starbucks.  Close the store a d scrub the " I hate customers" vibe and retrain EVERYONE

Messed up on three of my drinks. and then I decided to give them another chance but then they messed up in my drink again! and they're pretty slow as well. but once you complain about your drink they make sure it's complete.

This starbucks has the best customer service in the area.   It is true that they are always busy, but they move people through quickly and with a smile on their face.  The manager Jamie is always happy to greet you with a smile
I smellllll.... Cough-feeeee.... Say it with an New York accent, it's fun! =]

I never leave reviews but I needed to for these amazing baristas!   Every single time I visit this location they are so unbelievably kind and helpful. As some one who also works in customer service it's extremely refreshing to visit this store and get treated like a friend. The sweet girl named Sarah Mai, I believe, today asked me my name and was so kind. I told her thank you for always being in such a great mood! She said there's too much darkness in the world so we try to bring some light into your day!   This is how every establishment should be. They are so excellent!

The coffee was made with little to no care at all. I The attitude from the employees there is basic. You'll be lucky to get any eye contact whatsoever. The coffee was so hot that the cup it came in was becoming flimsy. It's just not a pleasant experience   And at the prices they charge...
Staff super friendly here! Good ol' starbucks.  No complaints.  No seating inside as of today. All staff wearing masks. Spacious and clean.

Geesh!  OK the girl was new but the guy working w/her was no help.  It was 8:30ish and there was one person in line in front of us.  We ordered 2 iced teas & 1 order of bacon egg bites.  When we turned around to go wait for our order there were 11 people in line behind us!  The guy told her she needed to multitask as he walked through the door to the back where it sounded like a party.  Turns out at 9:00 "the party" went on shift and the poor girl at the counter got some help.  Out of the then 4 baristas only 1 knew what hustle means.  I get that the new girl hadn't found her groove yet but other than the one who knew what customer service is, the other 2 were just there at their job!

I went to Starbucks this morning. I got an old fashioned donut and a white chocolate mocha. The white chocolate mocha is my absolute favorite drink from Starbucks, and I usually order it with an extra shot or two. The donut was delicious as well. As always, I think that Starbucks is expensive, but well worth the price. I will continue to buy coffee and pastries from Starbucks.

A decently sized Starbucks with an ample amount of seating (with outlets!) upstairs, though you have to come before noon to guarantee a seat on the weekend. Otherwise, pretty standard Starbucks in terms of their drinks, and the baristas were friendly and accommodating.   There is only one bathroom that is not very clean, so I wouldn't recommend staying for too long. I've definitely been to nicer Starbucks in many other cities/locations so this was just an average one.

Three employees chatting. One employee making drinks. Longest wait I've ever had at a Starbucks. Least sense of urgency among baristas. I eventually gave up so I wouldn't miss my flight. $4 bucks so three employees could chat on a Sunday afternoon just before 6 at the Terminal B Starbucks. Really disappointing. Would be faster to walk to Terminal A and back!
Great location.  Friendly baristas,  Coffee made to order.  Would definitely go back there next time I'm in town.
Workers were nice, but didn't really know what they were doing. Lots of little errors that make a big difference.
Anybody Starbucks starts at a very mediocre position, however this one consistently delivers better quality and customer service as opposed to some of the other Tampa Bay locations.

It's another Starbucks, but this location been to handful of times, when i am in the area, quite convenient, stop grab something,I use it as a Landmark as well to meet up with my buddies. .Quite nice inside,sitting area, wii-fii. The outside seating should be happening soon-warm weather is upon us to people watch!! Service has been consistently good, guess the ones I've been to are personable.

The staff at this Starbucks is exceptional; engaging, friendly, knowledgeable & helpful and, very customer centered.  Frankly, today, it was like being at the coffee version of Cheers while I was at the counter .... the whole staff.  It's also more spacious than most Starbucks. So, if you're sticking around, it's a comfortable place to be.

This Starbucks is located very close to where I live, it's only a block or two off Rittenhouse Square.  The baristas are friendly and they know my coffee order which they give me exactly as I like, very nice!  They also have given me advice on how to brew coffee at home with the correct water/coffee ratio.  I'll definitely keep coming here, the convenience is great.

I stop in this Starbucks a couple times a week. The staff is always friendly and efficient, and I'm definitely a sucker for free parking. I love big coffee shops that you can go and sit for hours and feel relaxed..this is one of those. I was pleasantly surprised the first time I went in and now I keep going back!

This Starbucks gets busy as hell during the morning commute. However, they do their best and have a system set up that's as good as can be.  The staff were all nice, and the breakfast sandwich I got were good. Solid 'bucks.
My go to location! Always make my caramel macchiatos perfect. They also always have the reduced fat turkey bacon and cheddar sandwiches which start my day perfect.
Very good food, and drinks. They gave us "drip to hard water" and we will never forgot that. If you ever go to Starbucks rate 5 stars.

Starbucks is my favorite coffee hang out anyway. But I was especially impressed with the great customer service at this location. Speedy service, the flavoring was done perfectly and all of the employees were professional polite and friendly.
Very fast and efficient.  Only thing lacking is space to sit.

This starbucks was the closest one to my friends house so we stopped by in a hurry. The girl behind the counter was very nice, however, when I asked to use the buy one get one free happy hour deal it did not come through and I got charged for both drinks. Although that was bothersome I decided not to say anything after ordering our drinks. I got an iced caramel macchiato with almond milk as I usually do. The drink came out almost white looking like just milk and caramel mixed together. After leaving the store, I went back in and asked for it to be fixed and the barista rudely handed me a shot of espresso and told me to pour it in myself. It is safe to say that I will not be returning to this location and it was not worth the $6 I spent on one drink.
I normally love this Starbucks, but my last visit not so much. Not happy when I get my drink far from full. I will admit I ordered it without ice and using my star points, but a venti is a venti.

As much as one tries to avoid eating at a non-local chain when in New Orleans, sometimes PJs just doesn't cut it. For when that happens, this Starbucks is fine. The Northeasterners from Tulane flock here.

I go specifically to this Starbucks whenever I can because the staff makes the coffee taste even better. I happen to go in on a Friday morning and we all had a big laugh because I was the fourth Mike in a row. They hustle the orders out and do it with a smile... I'll be back for my next cuppa coffee soon. - Mike #4

It's always busy which doesn't bother me, however service over the years as gone down. Yesterday I went in to pickup a mobile order and I saw the man put non fat milk in my drink that was supposed to have almond milk. I informed the barista who looked annoyed and said I was lying. Her co worker who put the milk in told her I was right and he did put the wrong milk in. The manager I assume was sitting on the bar side and went back over to help them and re make my drink. I am just frustrated by the lack of an I'm sorry we will remake it no problem. If I am paying $10 for a coffee it should be made correctly. This location is close to my night class and work or I'd honestly go elsewhere.

My experience at this Starbucks has been fantastic.  True, there are times when the line is very long but it's in downtown Nashville and if they are busy on a regular basis that tells me they must be doing something right.  My drink orders are always filled quickly and I've not had any problems with the quality of my drinks or food.  The baristas at this location are friendly and I always look forward to stopping in when I can.

Horrible Service unable to complete the simplest request without having to repeat it 2 if not 3 times. Apparently a Venti Vanilla Latte no foam and 120 degree causes great vexing on the person working the drive thru. The staff is incredibly slow inefficient and indifferent. The mgr has no more interest in customer service than the staff. It is quite unfortunate and at $5 a cup of coffee it should b an incredibly BIG DEAL .....
great customer service by the lovely lady with glasses.
I am not sure they know what they are doing both in ringing the register or making the drinks. Worst latte macchiato ever.

This is a full-sized Starbucks, not a cart or hut. I love this location! The employees are all super friendly and have been from the get-go. The various batistas quickly learned my order and always make me feel welcome. They have typical Starbucks fare...cups, mugs, snacks. And a pretty good selection of breakfast and lunch foods. When new drinks come out, they are quick to offer samples. There's lots of coffee competition on this block, but if you're a diehard Starbuckser on Poydras...your coffee dreams have finally come true!

This is the slowest Starbucks in existence. During my last visit I ordered a Iced Caramel Macchiato and they forgot the espresso!   This morning:    - We were lined up to the door and I've been waiting in line about 15 minutes.  - Their truck unloaded everything in the front covering up some prime seating.  - There is a man sleeping in the front of the café. I guess they are too busy to eject him.   If this weren't the only Starbucks in Downtown Nashville with any type of seating area (the other two are in hotels)  I would be choosing another location.   Once Starbucks allows their franchise locations to order ahead via the app I think that will help with the speed issues.

Good food - fast service and accurate ordering. Space inside is regular/ clean & friendly. Free WIFI (awesome)! Overall 4 stars to my previous rating bc well deserved. I've been coming here early morning for ah cup of Joe. Enjoy the staff & environment.

Disappointed with the service of this location.  Ordered ahead through the app so I wouldn't have to stand in line. My coffee was ready but the food wasn't. I asked about the food; the response I got was "I just do drinks." Okay, but what about the rest of my order...she walked away.  Finally I see a food item put into the pickup section. It was mine...but where was the rest of it. Sat back down and continued to wait. Finally went back to ask and they said they're working on it. Once the guy came over with my oatmeal he had the wrong kind. Ordered blueberry but got the classic. Guess they figured I didn't want or need agave syrup and the nut medley. At least he tossed in the berries.  Needless to say, I won't be going back if I'm ordering food. Service was slow and my order was wrong.
How could you not love Starbucks! Exceptional staff also! I highly recommend an Iced Caramel macchiato!

Typically don't go to Starbucks, would prefer supporting local businesses. However, I received a gift card and needed some cold brew this last Sunday. Ordered 2 plain cold brew coffees, there were a ton of people waiting for drinks. The place was total chaos. Essentially only 1 person making drinks and multiple people standing around. I ended up waiting 20 minutes and when I asked about my order the cashier said I have 10 drinks ahead of mine. I walked out without my coffees and went to Nola beans. This Starbucks needs to add people or train employees to handle the busy times. No plans on coming back.

I'm from out of town and this was honestly the rudest and worst service ever. They could be less interested in doing anything for you. Try not to interrupt their convo by ordering your drink...ridiculous.

This Starbucks is gorgeous, the renovations in the building are fantastic. I am so happy it is opened as it is only a block from my office. Starbucks is Starbucks is Starbucks. The only thing special about this one is that it is a Starbucks reserve location and the sell the special reserve star blends. The pastry and dessert case is spectacular and the display of the food in there is quite pleasing to the eye, luckily Starbucks likes to post the calorie count on their baked goods which usually causes me to pass up those types of treats, especially when I know my delicious salted caramel mocha has about 500 calories in it. Whatevs! I need it sometimes, peppermint mocha!! Where have you been all my life? Love the holidays at Starbucks!!
Best Starbucks around. They always have a dog biscuit for Piper  super friendly staff

This Starbucks is one of the best ones I've been too! The baristas are always very very nice and I've never had any problems here they get the drinks and the food out fast and always ask if the drink is right and if it's not they'll make you another which is amazing.

I love going into Starbucks and ordering a medium coffee. That look the barista gives you is always fit for framing. They scowl at you like you just walked into a McDonalds and ordered a Whopper.  Starbucks: "We don't have 'medium' here."  Me: "Yeah, you do. It's the size in the middle between the big one and little one."  I was once acquainted with someone who hated the word 'crotchless.' She'd wince and roll her eyes whenever she had to say it (which was surprisingly often). This Starbucks employee said 'medium' that same way my acquaintance said 'crotchless.' I made sure to ask for the medium roast too just for extra effect.  Anyway, after that awkward exchange things flowed smoothly. I can't bring myself to say 'Grande'. (It's my 'crotchless.')  This location on 109 St is always quick and orderly. There have been times I walked in here and it was crazybusy but the employees always managed to herd the coffee cattle in an efficient manner.  I don't think I have to go into detail about what they serve here. Just remember the Denis Leary routine about coffee-flavoured coffee, "They got mochaccino, they got chocaccino, frapaccino, capuccino, rapaccino, alpaccino, wtf?!" And as you already read, I got coffee-flavoured coffee. MEDIUM!  So remember: quick, clean, and (slightly) argumentative. I kind of instigated the latter though so I accept 26.3 % of responsibility.

I prefer to patronize independent businesses, but unfortunately the closest indy coffee spot is a 10-minute drive. On the other hand, this newer Starbucks is about the same distance from my parents' house, only I can walk. So in this case, environmental footprint is more important than independent operation.  Most of the hate in the reviews seems to be reserved for the drive-thru, to which I can't speak. However, the inside is cheery and bright, with plenty of plug-ins and fast wifi. My barista, who apparently normally worked at a different store (Drexel Hill?) and was helping out, was super nice. I wanted a mocha, but when I asked if it was very sweet, he said fairly and offered to cut down the sweetness (halving the pumps of chocolate they put in it). On top of that, he told me to let him know if it still was too sweet. It came out quickly, and while it's not as good as a mocha at Javista in LA, it's a solid mocha.  In any case, I will continue to do my best to seek out independent businesses to support whenever it's environmentally responsible to do so, but this Starbucks, at least from my limited experience, is a perfectly worthy substitute when necessary.
No drive up window. :( However, each time I'm greeted with a warm smile, I don't understand previous reviews.  Coffee quality is the same as other Starbucks.

Nice and convenient Starbucks in the same plaza with Panera Bread. This works out perfectly because I don't like the food at Starbucks and I don't like the coffee at Panera. So I get my coffee to go at Starbucks while I wait for me food at Panera.

Are there any Sbux locales out there that truly stink? This place is close to the office I'm temporarily working at and it is always packed! My only gripe (and why it received 4 stars instead of 5), is the parking. During peak hours, its very difficult to find a space and there is no drive-thru.

Drive Thru Organization:  Thank you for being open during COVID-19 but please (please, oh please!) put some cones up so cutting in line doesn't happen now that it's drive thru only.  Nothing worse than waiting through this line without caffeine to have folks cut in from the other entrance who weren't waiting nearly as long - we are living in a society here people.

Awful customer service. I walked in to grab a cup of faucet water just to take my medication with and the barista (Cameron S. ) was very rude and told me to get in line. That day the line was long and since I did not need to pay for the water I did not see the need to wait in a huge line while they can just grab a cup and run it under the faucet. Instead I had to wait in line for no reason. Won't be back to this location again.

This location is literally next door to where I work so I visit often, haha. Sometimes I work at 6:30am, sometimes I work at 7:45am. Either way, the baristas in the drive thru are helpful and friendly! I believe they recognize me too, haha. Anytime I order a food item, they will ask if I would like it warmed up. I also know that my coworkers are treated well when they go too. Our drinks are made to order.  Depending on what time you go, the drive thru can get pretty busy. I personally have not had a problem, but I have driven by and seen a long line before. It definitely depends on day and hour. The Medicine Ball is a wonderful beverage! Made of hot tea, lemonade (steamed) and other healthy items, it helps when you are sick! Not a cure, of course, but it will ease your symptoms so you can temporarily function for work. One last thing about this location, there is plenty of space to sit with friends or conduct a coffee meeting with peers! Nice little patio in good weather and large sitting room inside! One of Reno's best Starbucks locations.

Ordered three grande chestnut praline drinks.   Two of the three came out ok, but one of them had about an inch and a half less fluid in it than the other two.   The service and the atmosphere were good, but I'm not happy about the drink.  That's what I get for not checking before I leave the store

I have to say of all my experiences as Starbucks, my travel through the south Starbucks have been horrible. The drinks just don't taste the same as up north. They aren't made with that same flavor and they get very frazzled with changes of drinks!

I frequent this Bux location at least three times a week, usually by means of the drive thru. I've gotta say: FANTASTIC service and friendliest cashiers. I highly recommend it if in the Carmel or Zionsville area! :)

I love my Starbucks, but I went to this location around 8:45 AM on a Saturday a few weeks ago and when I ordered the bacon egg and gouda with a grande caramel macchiato the cashier informed me that they don't have anymore bacon egg gouda sandwiches. On a quiet Saturday morning! I must have come on a off day and they clearly had sold out the day before, but that was really odd.   My drink was great though as always so no hard feelings.

I work right around the corner, so i am always going to this Starbucks. Today was the day that made me want to write a bad yelp review. Around 1:30pm went in the drive thru, backed up. took almost 30 mins. I was locked in otherwise i would have backed out and went to pjs. I had to repeat my order 3 times. Grande coffee w/ soy. it shouldn't be that difficult? got my coffee and it was wrong. this happens more than it should. the baristas at the drive thru are never smiling. I will continue going to this Starbucks in hopes they get my coffee with soy right one day... and for the convenience!

Starbucks is not really something a challenge in terms of food. You know what you're going to get. Customer service, however, is very important.   I've been coming to the Egg Harbor & Greentree starbucks for years. As a regular, I can say that the staff here is extremely friendly and helpful.   One issue you may have depends on the time of the day. At some points, high school lets out and this place can be as loud as a school cafeteria. Other times, several parents decided that this Starbucks is a good place to bring their children. Being in Washington Township, they have the "my kid does no wrong" attitude and often the parents will allow their infants to cry, or their younger children to run around as if this were a daycare. It does affect the experience of a nice place to relax with a tasty beverage.   The service gets a 4-star rating. The crowd sometimes kills it though.
This location blows. Customer service was god awful. Everyone working there seemed like they hated their job. Maybe I just came at the wrong time of day (11 am) but I shant be returning ever again.

They were kind and attentive to my needs and wants as a customer! Great spot!   They offered to press instead of brew my coffee. My breakfast sandwich was hot, and I didn't know they had Sriracha!!    Feel bad for their location, but if you're in Millwoods, this is a great location!

It great to have another Starbucks in Delaware County with a drive through.   Went through today and got a VERY hot barista made drink in a reasonable amount of time.  This Starbucks replaced the cute quiet one up the road nearer to Rte 320 that shared the lot with the pretzel company.

AJ has a fantastic persona! She is friendly, informative, and witty. She helped out with my London fog latte (in Proper English :D) and provided a thoroughly good Starbucks experience!   The London Fog Latte is a bit less sweet then I imagined, but still tastes fantastic!

This Starbucks sets the standard for the many Starbucks in Tucson. The friendly Baristas, the quality drinks, and service never disappoint. The location is prime and convenient. The best coffee on the East side of Tucson!

Two words...Barista Baby!  So you think Starbucks coffee is bitter?  Wrong...it's better and you wanna know why?  The obvious of course is that you've gotta start with some pretty awesome coffee beans...after that it's all about the Barista. Doesn't matter which Starbucks I visit...whether it's a drive-thru, in a mall, airport or Target.  Those Baristas are highly trained professionals who know what they're doing.  I've been to many other coffee houses and time and time again, my #1 complaint is lack of consistency.  I mean it's not like they don't have great coffee, but please make me a non-fat latte that tastes the same all the time.  Until then, I remain a Starbucks devotee...peace out.

My friend and I stopped at this Starbucks because she felt her blood sugar dropping. She is a type 1 diabetic and needed something to spike her blood sugar. The line was long but she was willing to wait. We got out of line and were talking. She started to feel dizzy. She checked her pump (monitors her blood sugar levels) and noticed she was below 60. Not good! So we then paid attention to who was receiving their order. Everyone that received their drink was well after us!! I went back in line to get her an apple juice to help with her sugar, because at this point she was dropping lower. The two girls that were in front of me received their drinks BEFORE US!!!! At this point she went up to ask about her drink. They made it immediately. After we left she checked her receipt and when she got her drink and it was 26 minutes !  I get your lines are long but you need a system !

Ordered coffee and a breakfast sandwich. Received my latte in a reasonable time frame. Waited and watched several sandwiches go out via the drive through. Kept waiting for mine - about 5 min. Finally I was given my sandwich with an apology on how long it took ... Started to eat as I drove off and the entire center was still frozen. Not edible. Had to toss it out. So I waited too long - for nothing. Wasted money too.

I witnessed one of the baristas wiping down a toilet(not using gloves) with a wash rag, she lifted the seat and really got it clean. Then, check this out, proceeded to wipe down all the mugs and cups for sale with said pee rag. That's foul.

The drive-thru line at this location is ALWAYS long, but because I expect it at this location and know to give myself the extra 10-12 minutes that part is irrelevant at this point. After waiting a decent amount of time just to get to order a female in an annoyed tone says "what can I get you?" Now it may be petty of me, but at every other Starbucks they usually say "welcome to Starbucks what can I get you?" Or "how are you? What can I get you?".. I felt very rushed to give my order & I barely had time to finish ordering my last drink when a total was being given.. I apologized and asked her if I could change the size on the last one and she recited the total. I verified that she heard my size change and apologized again and once again a very annoyed "yes" thru the mic and that was it. I get up to the window and a girl with a matching voice and name tag that said "Cassie" is at the window.. once again no customer service, no nothing. Recites my total, scans my phone and closes the window without a word being said. I wanted to use a reward (which had she not just scanned and closed the window I would've had the chance to tell her the first time). She opens the window, hands me a drink to which I explain I wanted to use a reward and she says she needs to rescan my phone. Hands me the other 2 drinks and closes the window again.. still with no words exchanged. I didn't even get the chance to tell her one of our drinks was the wrong size and I was so disappointed and frustrated it never got fixed. I get that it can get busy and hectic, but this is the only location I chronically have customer service issues with, BUT this experience was worse than normal. If this location wasn't a block away and most convenient with having a newborn I wouldn't even go to this one. And as a matter of fact.. I feel that after this experience I'll be going back to other coffee shops that appreciate their loyal customers. Unfortunately, I know this review and experience won't be address, but maybe by reading it you can save yourself the long wait (inside and drive they) and the snobby experience.
Really great service every time we go to this location. It's nearly always busy but the employees are always friendly.
New "no straw lid"on my iced coffee doesn't fit!  Way too loose.  Of course drink spilled as the lid fell off.  BS.

This Starbucks is located between Terminal A and Terminal B.  Stopped by on my way to Terminal A for a 6am flight. This location opens super early (around 4:15am). Quick service and polite staff. They had 2 cashiers and 2 baristas. You gotta appreciate that! This was a great stop before heading to the A gates because there were no great food options in that terminal.
