This starbucks is just okay. There isn't really anything special about it. Sometimes they mess up my orders here when I do the online mobile ordering. They have added the wrong milk to my coffee and also gave me the wrong food once. Everytime I order in store my order doesn't come out wrong only when I order on the app. There is always a huge line here hence why I do the online ordering. Sometimes the staff here is in a good mood but other times they aren't. You can tell by the way they are talking to the customers if they are or not.   Overall I will continue to come to this starbucks because its convent for me but I hope that their customer service improves and they stop messing up my order.
I love this Starbucks the girls and sometime guys are always friendly I'm a regular and they are very nice they remember what I get.  If I could I would give them 10  stars

I love Starbucks but this is not one that I would recommend if you can avoid it.  The one good thing is that there is a drive through, not many people like getting out to get coffee so it's nice that it has one. There is plenty of seating indoors and I think when it's nice outside there is also outdoor seating.  They don't really make my coffee that great, they never have. If you get frapps or lattes only then it's not that bad.. you can't mess those up. Like in my other reviews- caramel iced coffee with soy and it sucked almost every time. I stopped going here when the last 3 times they gave me the soy to put in myself, um, somethings wrong there. I was really unhappy with that. The people aren't that great or memorable so wouldn't stop by here again.

Good product, poor customer service. The wife and I came to this particular location on St. Patrick's Day around 1:15pm to order our usual; while we're sitting in line we hear the window attendant taking the lady in front of us' order in what seemed to be a cheery mood. As we pull up to the speaker their tone is completely different, snappy, and pompous as they (oh and their name is Stephen) tell me I can't order a Frap because "they're brand name and call them Frappuccinos" which normally would t bother me because it's a common occurrence when I try to order via shorthand with the Starbucks franchise but Stephen in particular was just seemed to be in a bad mood all of a sudden; seeming like being petty toward his customers made him feel better. Then as we pull up to the window to pay he rips my debit card out of my hand then proceeds to hand me our order and say "here's your stuff" and closes the window.  I've never had an issue with this location before but I hope proper actions are taken to make sure Stephen doesn't feel the need to treat his paying customers like a nuisance anymore.

This is actually the place I tried my first Starbucks coffee, so it holds a special place in my heart. It's not perfect, but really what place is? They do a great job for being constantly so busy, and I appreciate that.
We went through the drive through and it was a great start to our morning, everyone was wonderful and speedy! Not only did our drink look so lovely but it tastes... *chefs kiss*  Thank you :)!!!
Super quick store!  Definitely worth a mobile order to get in and out. Not super busy.

I try to avoid this location as much as possible as I find them slow and they always seem to be out of ingredients. Yesterday was no exception. I was the ONLY person in line and I had to wait of 5 minutes for someone to take my order.  I wanted a Very Berry Refresher nope had to have Cool Lime. I asked for light ice and I got 3/4 of ice. I added a photo showing clearly on the sticker light ice so I clearly didn't stutter.

Everything from my previous review holds true (copy below), but the service here really is stellar so I had to bump them up another star. The issues I have with the location (small venue, horrible parking design, exorbitant profit margins built into upcharges) are all larger company issues...but on the small side, the staff here really does a fantastic job.   I've been in this location enough to see what employees have to deal with. Yes, customer turnover is high, but think about the irritability level of some of those caffeine-addicted customers having to pause in their flurry to get their fix: they want their specific order EXACTLY the way they imagined. Perfectly. Instantly. Even if they didn't say the order correctly (because baristas should also be psychic, too).  I've posted up here with my laptop on occasion. I've never seen a barista lose their cool; they're unflappable. They're deft and efficient speeding orders through both the drive-thru and the counter. They know the menu inside & out (and I'm definitely one of those people who asks a bunch of questions so I can order exactly what I want). And they're smiling, calm, and share that soothing vocal timbre reminiscent of a shrink talking their client off a ledge...probably the best quality for a barista working the a.m. hours. I even trusted my last barista, Josiah, to "surprise me" and have me try something new - if you know me in person, you know I like to do exhaustive research and then make a educated, conclusive decision; unless you're one of my inner circle, surprising me is often a recipe for disaster - but Josiah got it right and I have a new favored drink!  I didn't expect to write this much about a simple chain coffee shop, but I really do think the staff here is undervalued. The service alone would rate this a 5-star location for detail, consistency, and speed. Unfortunately, the other problems at this location are such an issue that I can still only rate it as 3 stars.   Since I referenced it and in case it's no longer available after I post my new review:  MY PAST/PREVIOUS REVIEW I like that the baristas know their coffee culture and can answer any questions.  Whoever set up this drive-thru, however, is an idiot.  If you come from the cross street rather than entering off Brentwood, prepare for some tight finagling (and the blaring horns of irritated, coffee-deprived customers) to get to the lane. And you're going to sneak in a sixty cent surcharge for adding soy milk (no upcharge for dairy) to my coffee?  St. Louis Bread Company is a block away with better coffee & no extra fees for those of us avoiding lactose.  I won't go out of my way for this one.
They have really turned around & they are so personable & know my emotional support animal's Name!!

Been going to this Starbucks for quite some time now. I order the same drink each time and I don't find the drink that I order to be that difficult to understand/make (green tea lemonade no classic syrup). Without a doubt each time I go my drink is ALWAYS made wrong whether it's not the right size cup I ordered or half the time they don't add the lemonade when I specify it 3-4 times prior to paying. I think Starbucks needs to hire more attentive people than what they currently have on staff at this location. Ridiculous.

no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no. no? no.

Every time we go here they forget our order they will serve the people before after and everyone in the drive through. I speak up and they say oh yeah what was your order? Every dang time! 8 people working and they can't manage a black tea?

Let me provide you with a disclaimer that I'm not a huge fan of Starbucks, so you might see some bias in this review. However I happened to find myself here while visiting with friends in the area, and we came here for an afternoon coffee, or in my case, iced tea.  The parking here is atrocious, but what do you want for a business that emphasizes more on the drive through experience? We were however able to obtain a space, despite the fact that it was super busy for some reason.   The inside of this place was a madhouse. It was dirty, and smelled horrible.   People huddled about everywhere, standing shoulder to shoulder, and not wearing masks despite the fact that the city of Tucson had passed an ordinance requiring people indoors to be masked. Unfortunately, Starbucks must be desperate for business since they didn't enforce the masks.   There were people talking loudly on their phones, a group in the corner playing Pokemon Go, people hunched over their computers, and patrons in booths shouting over the din of those on their phones to the point where me, a Deaf man, was able to understand what they were saying.   What an awful experience to behold.   We ended up going through the drive through instead (against my wishes, as I prefered to go elsewhere). However, to Starbucks' credit, they completed my order accurately, and we went through the drive through much quicker than expected.

Why is the music so loud at this location? The acoustics are already awful, so it's noisy from talking, phone conversations, blenders, timers, and chairs being dragged across the floor. The music is just added annoyance.

This location is fine. It's not spectacular but also not the worst. We typically stop in here because its the last stop before we hit Delaware. They don't have any gluten free items and they are a little slow but it gets the job done and you get to have coffee that isn't Dunkin or whatever other brand.

Full Service Starbucks coffee & breakfast sandwiches INSIDE I95 Southbound rest-stop (to your far right.) Clara Barton is LAST I95 southbound rest area before Delaware Memorial al Bridge.   Pls note: Separate entrance into Starbucks from outside of Reston w/ regular & disables parking.  Staff were excellent! 4 Stars (not 5) due to bathrooms being part of rest-stop & not serviced I.e. not clean, hand-towels empty, clothing hooks inside stalls broken.  Starbucks opened at 6:15am today - 9pm (not sure of posted hrs.

I was here last weekend and have been coming to this Starbucks for years now. The quality of the drinks, the building upkeep, and staff has gone extremely sour. The building is covered in weeds and just looks kind of abandoned. My drink had to be made almost 3x because it was made wrong (all I asked for was a French vanilla latte). First I received an iced coffee, then a regular latte, and finally he opened the window told me the price of my latte (which was way less than usual) so I double checked and said "that is for the French vanilla latte, correct?" The response that I received was "OHH, you wanted a French vanilla latte??". At that point I said "never mind" and then received an "OH MY GOD, really?!?!" as funny as it sounds, it was not funny when I was waiting in line for so long for a coffee. Maybe if I was in a better mood that day I would have taken it lighter but I still feel as though this whole situation could have been handled better.
Service is always slow. I went there on a Sunday morning and they were out of everything including bottled water. Will never patronize this place again.
Coffee at not be great and quality questionable but customer service is amazing. The staff was very gracious a made sure they got our large order right and our names correct.

This store is dirty and gross. Employees are rude and I even saw water leaking from their ceiling onto their coffee machines for hours. Dirty!! Onto their drinkAVOID. Go local!!! So many other cheaper and better local coffee places within the same block.  Update: their management is horrible and they're inconsiderate to their neighborhood
My favorite Starbucks by far! Everyone here is always so nice and helpful, EXCEPTIONAL service every visit! I pass 2 other Starbucks store just to come this one.

Stopped in to grab some coffee and banana bread while I waited for an appointment. Seems to be a pretty busy location, so I was really shocked they don't have a drive thru.   There was quite a wait for my Peppermint Mocha, but they seemed pretty busy. Service was friendly. It's a nice, spacious location that looks good for working or casual business meet ups. Having no drive thru is the only downside I see to this location.

I was happily greeted at the door upon my arrival. The staff was very kind and attentive. The establishment was clean and offered a nice atmosphere for enjoying delicacies while using the free WiFi to get work done. There is adequate seating and the tables and lounge area are nicely kept.  My drink was delicious. My favorite beverage is the dragon drink. The staff made it exactly how I wanted it. My son got a birthday cake pop which he loved and didn't waste a single crumb.   I will definitely be back to this location. There are Starbucks on almost every corner but this particular one is at the top of my list to visit while in the area.

This store is clean, organized, and well-maintained. Although drink quality is important, it pales in comparison to the customer service I experienced while I was here. I watched the store manager (Ian) take my order and then clean up around the store when business died down. To top it all off, I told him I was having trouble with my Starbucks account and I hadn't been able to get in contact with a human through the Customer Service line. He wasn't sure how to help me, which was understandable. Two minutes later, while I was sitting drinking my coffee, he approached me with the store phone and told me he had been able to get in contact with someone who could help me with my account. Good service is hard to come by these days. Ian's service was phenomenal and he completely made my day.

It was a cold morning and all I wanted was a white chocolate peppermint mocha. I was on my way to school so I stopped at this location, no drive thru so I was forced to get out in the cold. It was a packed house.  I order my drink and they are out of white choc. Bummer. So I ordered a peppermint mocha=yuck. Tasted like dirt, not sweet and just when I thought they must have given me the wrong order, I tasted the peppermint on my last sip.  Never again will I waste my time getting out the car and paying $8 for a coffee and a danish when I can go to my local drive thru coffee shop and be way happier.    Lets remember why were NOT friends.

Small location inside a rest area but with a full menu. The milk and sweeteners are done by the baristas, not at a station, so make sure to ask for what you need. Service was slower than at dedicated locations but still fairly friendly. Not bad for a quick pick me up on the road.

Placed mobile order. Phone said would be ready at 4:06. When I pulled up, saw mobile line wrapped around the building. No problem, though. I parked in front and waited until 4:08. Imagine my aggravation when I tried to go in to pick up my order and there was a sign saying everyone had to go through the drive-thru. So, now it meant waiting until 4:25 for a coffee I ordered at 3:57 because I had to go sit in that long line.  Had I known, at least I could have gotten in line when I arrived.  If there is a problem at the store, the app should not accept orders! What's the point of a mobile order if you have to wait in a long line? They owe me a free coffee at the VERY least.

One of the worst locations on the north side of Indy. Today there were TWO people working on drinks for over 13 people waiting....it took the cashier over 7 tries to put an iced coffee drink order in correctly. I get these are difficult times, but managers need to schedule enough employees to take care of customers.

Customer service- Not available at this location- I went through the drive thru and waited 15 minutes meanwhile I was the only car then once ny drink is finished the girl puts it by the window- where the cashier and another employee continued to have a conversation while I sat there- with the drive thru window closed- so finally I yelled is there a problem - to high the rudely said what u talking bout - I said I been waiting for 15 minutes I want my drink sitting there- she says I'm talking to my manager closes the window and turns her back on me- so I drove off- calling district tomorrow-

Really great service and employees are always super nice! The inside of this store is so clean and fresh and very welcoming! Would be nice to sit and have coffee once things are open again. Would definitely recommend this store.

My wife and I submitted a mobile order after eating next door at PDQ. It was my birthday and if you have the Starbucks app installed, they give you a free birthday drink. We submitted our order and I was pleased to see that the birthday offer chose the most expensive drink on the order (and didn't really seem to care that I added some extras like mocha drizzle). Anyway, the app said our order would be ready in about ten minutes, at right at ten minutes, we were sitting in the drive through. The person in front of us had a long complicated order (FOR ONE DRINK) and we sat there listening to her chat with the Starbucks employee for about five minutes before finally pulling forward. When I got to the speaker, you could hear the sense of relief in the order taker's voice when I said "Hi there, mobile order for James."  Anyway, we pulled around, and quickly picked up our drinks. They were made exactly as we had specified and were absolutely delicious. Lemmie tell you, Starbucks has a winner with their cold brew lines. I love mine with a bit of sweet cream and some mocha drizzle and this particular location nailed it.   Note, it may be easier to walk inside and pick up your order as the line for the drive through tends to wrap around in weird places and block traffic.

Starbucks is literally out of every single thing I ever order. Today I ordered an oatmilk vanilla latte but of course they had no vanilla syrup, no toffee nut syrup, no caramel syrup and no oatmilk. Like what? I won't be back to Starbucks because they literally never have supplies.

I love Starbucks. I get coffee about every other day. Never have I had to wait 25 minutes for my coffee. 15-20 people behind me got theirs before me. This is after reminding them twice. This store needs to get it together.
Very nice people at this Starbucks and I felt the love with my latte and bagel. Everything was correct temperature and bagel was toasted to perfection. Thanks Again and Happy Valentines Day Y'all!!!

This place is hit or miss but today took the cake for me.. ordered my favorite skinny peppermint mocha and while I was waiting the girl at the window started sneezing and the she began blowing her nose....then proceeded to hand me my drink...so I asked her didn't you just blow your nose...she says to me I'm not sick... then I told her that I wanted my drink remade and someone else to give it to me...so I wait she leaves the window... then I'm watching this other person make my new drink... next thing I know she tried to hand me my new drink and informs me she washed her hands... (don't believe she did)I lost it by then and went off on her and the manager. I just asked the manager for my money back... I won't back to this Starbucks anymore..  that will save me money in the long run....
Waited over 45min to get 2 travel size coffees. They only have a limited amount of coffee machines and were out a bunch of other items...at 8am on week day!

It's frustrating when they say they are open at 5:30am and it's 5:40 and no one is at the drive through. Not the first time it's happen either. Why advertise that the opening is 5:30am if you aren't going to open til almost 6?!
Always great service at this location. People who mark off for humans not being perfect all the time strike me as sad little people. I love this location.

So they have 3 people working.  Only one car in the drive through but I decided to go inside to use the restroom while I waited on my coffee. First their restrooms are closed. Second no one said hi or even acknowledged me when I walked in. Then the two guys stood there with their backs to me talking while girl was on the other side with her back to me. It literally took 6 minutes for them to even notice I was there. Worst Starbucks ever

Since I was in the area and nearby, I had not been here since the summer I wanted to see what I could get from here today. So, here's my review...  Positives Love the location being in Cherry Hill, which is not too far from my house and township. As always, great atmosphere when you enter the place.  Two women conversating in their workout attire, working professionals drinking lattes and specially made drinks, people on their laptops making use of the store's Wi - Fi, barista's taking orders and fixing drinks for the patrons. Very busy this morning but not to where it slowed down customers or orders. Staff was courteous and professional and all had positive attitudes. Ended up getting a caramel coffee, but it took an unexpected turn...  Negative: ... my coffee order was black!!! I requested it with milk and caramel syrup. It was black coffee with only caramel syrup. Very very hot upon my first sip and was unable to drink until I got home and put my own creamer in it. The only thing that went wrong on this particular visit.

As far as Starbucks chain coffee shops go, this one is well managed, maintained, very clean, has friendly and knowledgeable staff, and make your order for a drink fast.  Of course when I have gone to this coffee shop I do it after I make my purchases at Target, not before, and just drink the coffee while driving home.
Sabrina the barista needs a grammy for her fricken awesome personality! Great location! Good service! Clean interior

Been here multiple times due to location. The last two times it has taken the barista over 15 minutes to make an iced coffee with one shot of espresso. The workers are all new and can't take the stress of making multiple drinks at once. Would recommend a different location.
Don't have decaf coffee?  What kind of Starbucks doesn't have decaf drip coffee...this one!!  Don't waste your time stopping. Kong wait line, poor service, poor selection

I gave it one Star because I had to pick at least one. I would have given it 0. Made a mobile order and "pick up in store" was the only option. Not a problem until we arrived and the store was locked and drivethru was 13 cars deep. I tried calling and they wouldn't answer the phone. Since I already paid I had to sit in the line. My order was supposed to be ready at 8:58 and I didn't get to the window for 20 minutes. While in line I watched 4 other people go to the door and get their orders. Apparently they know someone there? At the window they said they were understaffed and can't change anything on the app to show drive thru only. Very inconvenient,  and they were unapologetic. Frappuccinos were melted and coffee was luke warm when they finally gave it to me.  Go to one of the other hundred Starbucks in town. This one is poorly managed.

I have come to Tucson for a week's vacation and have been going to  this Starbucks every morning.  We always take for granted that the coffee and breakfast will be consistent (which it  was), but what I loved about THIS Starbucks was how cheerful and welcoming the staff were and how efficiently they filled orders.  A greaat way to staart the day, thanks!

Service sucks at this location. Not only are they systemically rude (which is a leadership issue) but they are not proactive. Seeming high turn over..today once.again asked for something easy and simple and again it was not done, then the young man handed me the cup.to do it myself. Shame on me for going back.
Avoid this location    Rude employees. Terrible customer service. Got my order wrong. Super slow.

So sad I can't give a good rating but this is the fifth time in a row my order has been messed up.  It's unfortunate because they are also so slow, so when your order comes out wrong you can't even complain because it's likely you are already late to work.  I refuse to go back, especially when there is another Starbucks up the street.
Basic Starbucks , clean , nice area , nice people fair enough , gets the job done. Had to spend a few hours in the area to kill time I relaxed here and worked a bit

Hey Starbucks-  When someone comes in with their four year old and asks about the dragon drinks caffeine content- you should probably give a better answer than "barely any".   You guys served my toddler 45mg if caffeine today and I had NO idea. This could be incredibly dangerous. All dietary questions should be treated with seriousness.   I won't be going back to this location. And maybe Starbucks at all.

Stopped here on a cross country trip needing some Joe for the road.  First negative - no drive through.  Second negative - small parking lot that was very congested.  I think this must be an old Dr. office or something.  The building itself was unique for a Starbucks and was plenty busy but getting in and out kind of a pain.

Conveniently located in the Central Business District.  Good place to get work done and get a drink while you are at it.  Barista wanted to be sure she got my order in correctly and definitely out in the extra effort to make sure it was accurate. Overall a clean location with plenty of seating for a downtown location.

I've been coming here for sometime now. This Starbucks is in a great neighborhood and parking usually isn't that difficult to locate. The employees at this location are very friendly and work efficiently. Theirs seating towards the rear of the store which seats a good amount of customers with  additional seating at the bar area looking out the window. The store always looks clean and well kept. They also have various mugs, instant coffee, coffee beans for sale. They had a 30% sale for Christmas time and ended up purchasing a mug.   I liked the decorations they put up for Christmas and the music being played was great. I'm happy when I come in here it isn't too busy or I probably wouldn't stay. I feel a lot of people will take away from the overall environment. I feel some people talk too loud and I don't enjoy hearing other people's conversation. I would stop by here if your in the area and check it out.
It was ok... aside from the rat maze trying to find the entrance and them being 'out' of the one drink I wanted. Seems I've got popular tastes despite being different a lot...

Perfectly reasonable, perfectly normal Center City Starbucks. They're right across from a Target.  Normal amenities. Free Wi-Fi, they take credit cards, etc. Upstairs seating. Lots of natural light during the day.  Friendly service!  If you're staying, ask for a mug. And be sure to bus your own cups to the counter when you're done; there's no table service, naturally.

This location is always hit or miss. Sometimes my order is great and sometimes my order is wrong. I counted once and figured out they had messed up my order more than 12 times out of maybe 20. And when I say messed up my order I mean either forgetting something, completing giving me the wrong item, messing up a drink order, or something else. It became an ongoing joke anytime I went there. The staff was usually helpful when I reported the mistakes but it just shouldn't happen that often to begin with.

Walked in this past week to the new Starbucks out of morbid curiosity. I was not acknowledged at all while the store was basically empty minus one guy on his laptop. I waited a minute and a half to get service at the counter where I was rudely served.  My regular drip coffee was horrid and the service was even worse...no actually check that. The worst part about this whole thing is that there is another coffee shop in the same building! The guy over there was super nice, helpful and his coffee was outstanding! Apparently, he has been in the building for 4 years when $tarbuck$ moved in...what a bunch of greedy people.   Not only am I never coming here again for my daily coffee, I'll just walk an extra 50 steps to get much better coffee and much better service!   p.s. If you don;t like the other little guy in the building, there are literally 3 other places within a 100 foot radius. This was a poor job in picking out locations...shame ::ding ding::

Every Starbucks is the same bland, boring, sterile yuppie infested, "I'm in a rush, get my latte quick" type place.  Awful Coffee, lousy rude service, terrible sandwiches, overpriced everything.  I avoid it always unless I get a free gift card.

This Starbucks is conveniently located in the Central Business District in Nola. With close proximity to most of the major hotels and business, banks and high rises in the area.     We were famished walking around looking for a breakfast spot.  It was Memorial Day and so many places were closed. We found ourselves lost in the area until we spotted a Starbucks and decided to go in and refuel with a coffee or snack.  My party of four all bought coffee, frappes and I got a new cold brew that just came out with a froth on top. It was so good and I wished I would have bought a Venti but then remembered this was just a quick stop.    Besides the cool drinks what set this Starbucks apart was the service we received. As mentioned earlier, we were lost and wondering the area looking for a place to eat.  Before we left I asked the baristas behind the counter where would be a good homey place to eat.  They stopped what they were doing and literally threw a bunch of options and places to eat that I wasn't aware of and emphasizing on what would be my next stop and review, Mother's Place.   Thanks for being so helpful and nice Starbucks team!

Girl who took my order never greeted me but just stared at me til I ordered. It took her a while to input the order so should've confirmed then that she had the correct order. My chai latte came out a green tea latte. So they fixed it w attitude. My drink cans to me Luke warm and I only got more attitude when I asked them to warm it up. It is now warmer but hardly.  This location is in the heart of the park where locals and tourists alike go and now to enjoy the holiday events. I can deal with an honest mistake but not bad attitude, and it's sad this is what people get coming here.

This Starbucks is located in the 2nd floor west wing of Harrahs casino. Very convenient if you are staying in the casino and it's too cold to go outside. It is decent in size and the staff was very friendly!
Never get an order right. Hot not cold big not small not sweet too sweet. Never fail to get it wrong. Remind me not to come back.  Cute inside but coffee is a disaster.

[780]  The large Starbucks location is located at the Gloucester Premium Outlets.  No drive thru as it's part of the outlets.  It's a busy location but they were able to work the line quickly and prepare the order correctly.  Plenty of indoor and outdoor seating.  We actually spilled our coffee outside before taking a sip and the staff replaced it with a smile.  We will definitely be back.

This Starbucks sucks. When I pulled up to the window, I paid and he handed me my drink which was completely just black iced coffee (not what I ordered) and the worker was trying to argue with me that there was my almond milk, caramel, and vanilla cream in it. How are you gonna argue all that is in there when it was straight up black??? He told me he wouldn't do anything about it and told me to keep driving. I've never been treated so rudely

Went in to get an iced latte and piece of lemon pound cake. Asked the cashier if the cake was fresh she said it was, which was not true.   I then watched as 6 people who ordered after me got their beverages, some of which were quite more elaborate than mine, were given their beverages. As I was the only Caucasian in   In the place, including both employees and customers, one has to wonder if there was any racial overtones involved here. At any rate, I won't be returning to this store.

Customer service is awesome and they are very kind and caring. They are very understanding if you don't have enough money they sometimes help you out a little because they know that these are hard times

Small Starbucks open 24/7 on the rest area stop. Either first or last rest stop area on the turnpike depending on which direction tore traveling.    I wanted one dark and one blonde roast but barista told me all they had was pike. Mind you it was 6.30am.  I was very disappointed.    Bakery items looked good & fresh and the pike was ok.

If they don't want me to take the bakery item I ordered, that clearly had my name on it, then next time don't leave me standing near the mobil orders without acknowledging me. The reason people order on the mobil app is so that they don't have to wait when they get there. If the order isn't ready that's one thing, but if it's ready and sitting there but you have to wait to be acknowledged by employee(s), who are working right near there and just ignoring you, that's different. Get a better system for mobil app order pick up. Someone needs to monitor it. Also, rude female employee, don't tell me for future reference don't touch anything here. Instead, how about you say, "Sorry for the wait." Work on your attitude at work or get another job that you like so you're polite to customers.
I tried to order 3 different items but they were "all out" at 11 a.m.  Said they may have more tomorrow.  Poorly managed & run.  Don't go there unless you want to be unhappy.

What a beautiful, new Starbucks! Very cool design and easy access right off I-65 south. Very friendly staff and they made our coffees perfect! We will add this to 'must stop' list anytime we are trekking south!

I would visit the old location at least twice a week. The staff there was great! They were friendly and always helpful. Most of the drinks I ordered were made perfect. However, I have had quite a different experience at their new location. I often feel like I am bothering the staff when I place an order (even if it is a simple as straight coffee). Most seldom smile and as mentioned in previous post, they mock customers or other employees. I have had positive visits with a couple employees that still work there from their old location.      Not a go to place anymore. It's worth the drive to Main Street Lansdale location where the staff there has always been great!

I go to this Starbucks every single day and I usually order the same drink too. Marley is the best barista because she can memorize your drink! Anytime I've ever had a drink taste funky, she's always remade it PERFECTLY for me! She is so nice and friendly and deserves a raise for doing gods work. I don't usually write reviews but she deserves the hype!

If you go in the morning, it's so much better. I started coming here on my way to work and the morning crew is soooooo much better. I haven't had a problem with my coffee yet and it has been delicious every time. At least someone there follows the lines!!  They are building my confidence back into this place. GO TEAM ICED COFFEE.

Two baristas didn't know how many pumps of sweetener went into a Trenta iced coffee.  I was charged extra for the standard pumps of sweetener.  Keep an eye on your drink as it's being made.  I get the feeling they are trying to short the customer.

The staff are very friendly, though usually very busy.  The new building is very nice.  I like Starbucks coffee (if you don't, well then....).   I have very few complaints.  One is that sometimes (not usually) the music is too loud.  But overall, this is an excellent Starbucks!

I had such a great experience at this Starbucks. The staff was extremely friendly and the place was very clean. I had the best tea of my life at this Starbucks. The girl that made my tea was so nice and accommodating.

How hard is it to follow a THREE ingredient recipe? They consistently use the wrong tea bag and this last visit was "the straw that broke the camels back". I brought my drink and told them it was wrong. Instead of them correcting the issue and sending me on my way, I had to wait for everyone (PLUS the people that came in after me) to get their order. I'm done with the Big Bend Starbucks and will drive those additional miles to get better customer service. (ie, TARGET)

I'm not a big fan of this location overall.  I've interacted with a couple female baristas who were very lovely, but the staff as a whole comes off as very aloof and unprofessional.  I've walked in there on multiple occasions, with no one in line, and had to wait as the baristas finished their personal conversations (clearly not work related) with each other with their backs turned to me before anyone acknowledged that I was there.  This just always feel so rude to me, especially considering that most Starbucks really seem to put a lot of emphasis on great customer service.  Also, not the most amiable bunch. Though I think I'm a bit spoiled by the location over on Coast Village Rd. where the staff are extremely friendly and consideration.  This crew - not so much.
Standard Starbucks fare. Nice to have it at the resort. Good but not amazing. Completely better than the Hoffer served st the resorts Restaurants.

For some reason, this one is always disappointing. The male baristas/cashiers are usually unpleasant/mildly surly. I've repeatedly gotten the bottom of the barrel coffee, meaning the last bit with all the grounds in it and they have made a few busted drinks for my wife as well. The condiment counter is always dirty and the trash is always busting out of the top full.  Now I'm fairly easy to please, and if the coffee was $1, I would have no problem with lousy service and grinds in my coffee (multiple times), but for what is charged, I think things should be better. Honestly, this is the only Starbucks in the city I have a problem with. The rest are all awesome.

Don't waste your time and money. The worst service- slow and don't care. Will ask you to repeat what you ordered several times. And when you get your coffee, you might as well have drank from the 3 day old pot of coffee on your kitchen counter. Avoid!

Warning if you are used to consistency in a chain, this particular location does not provided it.   After living in Seattle where you can go from store to store and everything is the same. You would think that they could do that here. They can't.

A year ago I walked into this Starbucks with my 1 year old.  The barista and I chatted a bit about my daughter while I was ordering and when I was about to pay she told me that she was going to cover my drink that day.  It was such a sweet act of kindness and made my day, which had been a bit stressful a whole lot better.  It made a big impression on me because I still remember it a year later!  I'm sure working in costumer service is very hard, and I really appreciate the kindness!

Very nice Starbucks location.  I love the warm fireplace, and the views of the sierra from the window.  The location is large enough and loaded with seating so that you can find a seat even if it's busy (for the most part) and the service was quicker than most Starbucks I've been to and it was very friendly.  I often work out of coffee shops all around town, being a field sales rep and I got a gift card to Starbucks from my boss this year for Christmas.  This was a nice location to use it in.

The friendliest Starbucks in the city. Always gets my order perfect and always making an effort to ask how customers day is! Super clean and definitely handling all COVID19 precautions as far as I can tell.
The service was amazing and the girl with the blue hair was so nice and make my drink perfectly

This is one of the better Starbucks around. It's busy and the area is nice also. It's conveniently located across the road from Grant MacEwan University which might make it a lot noisier and tough to find a seat once school is in session.   It's a pleasant, spacious location with a good array of indoor and outdoor seating.  I've never noticed the parking sign, but you can bet after getting a parking ticket at North Town Mall last week I am looking at the signs more closely. Unfortunately the lot here is managed by Diamond Parking as well. I have only begun to deal with that. Watch for my review of Diamond Parking and North Town Mall.  It should be obvious that you should keep an eye on your car for the parking Nazis. If you're going to be longer than two hours, you might want to find a better parking spot. I think I saw some free street parking.  Busyness - This is a pretty busy location. It's fairly close to downtown and just across from Grant MacEwan University. I would imagine that it gets even busier once school's back in.  Noisiness - There's a nice high ceiling that seems to absorb the noise reasonably well. The music isn't too loud either.  Ambience - I've been coming to this Starbucks for a couple of years and I've always liked it.  Power - It is available, but the window seats and tables in the centre have an issue. I'd bring an extension cord if you want to use some of the tables in the centre.   Free wifi - Available and works well. Being one of those people who keeps a number of tabs open, you might want to login with a browser which you don't use often, otherwise each page becomes a login page, oddly enough. (In Firefox Windows 7 at least.)  Staff - Always friendly and helpful. I've rarely had a complaint about a Starbucks employee.  Seating - There are 28 seats in this Starbucks, with four armchairs and two good sized tables for laying out a bit of work.  I like the square tables they have here. They're at a nice height and are quite roomy, compared to the dinky round cafe tables I normally see at Starbucks. There are four or five tables outside on the spacious patio. It's also bordered by some hedges to give it a bit of privacy. I don't know if they ever use the patio heaters.  Security - When I first started coming to this location, there was a homeless camp in the empty lot nearby. It's gone now, but the Starbucks is close to downtown. Not too bad of an area but 107 Ave is close by. Take the usual precautions.

In general the move away from the train station was a very positive one. Allowing for a great space to sit and relax or work. Ample parking. They now have a drive through and still seem a little "challenged" with that new addition but all in all a great place.
This location is consistently pretty great.  As a witness, I can tell you that recent media portrayals do not tell the whole story of what happened there.

The problem with this Starbucks is that they know people will still come in no matter what they do. This is the slowest Starbucks I have ever been to, and their drinks are far from the best. If you go through the drive through, you'd better plan to be stuck in place for at least 15 minutes, regardless of how many cars are in line. The DT line goes straight down the middle of the parking lot. The parking lot is tiny, too, so it gets super congested and it's difficult to get out of your parking spot when there are more than 2 cars in line. Also, their coffee and espresso often tastes bitter and slightly burnt. Maybe they don't properly clean their machines? Unfortunately, this location is right next to where I work and I am as caffeine-addicted as the next person, so they still get a decent amount of my paycheck. Yes, I'm aware that I'm part of the problem! Haha

Consistently, the SLOWEST Starbucks I have ever been to. Prepare to spend anywhere from ten to twenty minutes waiting on your order if you go through the drive thru. I don't know who trained these employees but they have absolutely zero sense of urgency. Even when the line is literally wrapped around the building, they will be moving at such a glacial speed that it's quite frankly unnerving. As a former Starbucks employee, I gotta say, this location really needs to step it up a notch! Super disappointing!
Slowest starbucks I've ever been to. Don't use the drive through. You'll be stuck for an hour.

This Starbucks location can be found on the corner, down the street from the Betsy Ross House. My husband and I came by here after visiting the Betsy Ross House last Thursday afternoon, around 4:25 pm.   There were definitely plenty of people inside waiting for their orders and only one person ahead of us in line. We ended up ordering:  Teavana Shaken Iced Passion Fruit Green Tea Lemonade, $2.75 prior to tax- Although my husband asked for a grande size, the employee rang him up for a tall size. I didn't have any since I am not a big fan of passion fruit but he thought this drink was good.   Mango Dragonfruit Starbucks Refreshers, $4.95 prior to tax- The girl at the register didn't ask for the size but automatically rang us up for a grande size. I thought this drink was refreshing, especially on the hot we were there. I liked the pieces of the dragonfruit, it was nice to eat it after I had finished drinking my beverage.  ------------------------ (Total= $8.32 after tax)  The employee taking our order didn't give us a receipt and when I had asked for one, she said she didn't know how to reprint one out. I am assuming she was a new employee since I saw another employee showing her around and training her.   After my husband had initially ordered his iced peach green tea lemonade and paid for our order, the employee told him that they didn't have any more peach tea and to pick another flavor. The employee should have checked beforehand before charging us for the drink. My husband ended up ordering the passion fruit instead. He still thought this drink was good but he had wanted the peach flavor instead.   Wait time for our drinks was over 15 minutes, which seemed like a pretty long time, compared to other Starbucks that we have been to before.   A little later, I asked the barista making our drinks for water. He said okay but never gave it to me. I had to ask him again after 10 minutes and he never did. I was rather annoyed at the point. So my husband went up and asked another employee who got us 2 cups of water right away. No attitude and seemed to have a positive manner.   If you have a choice, avoid this location.   Please note that the unisex restroom was decently clean and adequately stocked with soap and toilet paper.

My drink is a ice soy latte. I've gotten many many times, but never like this at Bordentown location. Twice it's happened to me in a week. The bottom of my drink has a white chalky chunky substance. The first time I did not notice it will I was drinking it and it was off. The second time this morning (7-24-18) I looked before drinking and again the same white chalky substance. That's disgusting!

This was my second time visiting this location and clients were lined up inside waiting for meals to be warmed with only one oven working. The employee who was working the oven was eating a sandwich while cooking sandwiches for guests. If it wasn't Covid maybe it would be OK, if it wasn't a violation of a food prep area maybe it would be OK, unfortunately it's not OK. The owners of this location should frequent to see what occurs during the morning coffee frenzy. I will not return.

Nothing like a vanilla chai tea latte with oatmeal & brown sugar. Hearty and semi-heavy on the tummy. I needed a nap afterwards. Their breakfast cage free egg white chicken sausage or turkey bacon is rather tasty as well. Stole a few bites from my friends. The wraps are light with low calories so there is no guilt associated with eating 'em.  This location is very crowded most times so be prepared for the wait.

This place is busy in the morning, but they kept it moving like a machine. Bathroom was clean and they let me use it before buying something.   Friendly staff and pretty patrons. My drink was made quickly and to my specifications. Good place to sit and get work done in the morning. I would go back again.

Stood in line for 20 minutes before I got to place my order. You would think with a line out the door there would be some sense of urgency to make these drinks, yet this girl thought it would be a good time to check her phone and drink her own coffee. An iced coffee and bagel took THIRTY MINUTES TO GET.

Just left this Starbucks... and it will be the last time. The staff here are unfriendly and unbelievably rude. As I politely asked the barista for no whipped cream, he rolled his eyes and snickered to the guy next to him. There was not one worker who was polite, friendly, or professional. This place needs that show undercover bosses to show up at their doorstep. Horrible experience Starbucks...

This Starbucks location is conveniently located off Route 130 right off a turn. I always seen it and thought it was busy. But this morning I had extra time before I started work so I gave it a try.  There were probably about 5 cars ahead of me but it moved pretty fast. Seemed like the staff knew what they were doing and moving orders right along.  I asked for a white mocha and the barista suggested a grande toasted white mocha. So it was my first time trying it. Pretty good I must say. But if you drink a white mocha you know it's hard to mess it up. Just a whole bunch of sugar :-) I love it tho. I paired it up with one of the breakfast sandwiches on the healthier side: the egg whites, cheese and turkey bacon one. Which was a great compliment to my drink. Now I know where to stop by for my Starbucks fix.

I am a very frequent flyer at Starbucks and I don't write reviews but I just had THE worst experience I have ever had at a Starbucks at this location. The barista was already remaking 2 others drinks when I arrived for my mobile order. My latte was WHITE and although I like cream in my coffee I draw the line at paying $7 for a glass of milk! The barista was rude when I asked for a straw and wanted to argue when my boyfriend went back in to have her remake my drink. Absolutely unacceptable. Never going back to this location!

I ordered hot cocoa and the reindeer cake pop. Unfortunately, they forgot to add the whip cream. The cake pop was delicious and I even shared it to my sister. I recommend to order the reindeer cake pop so you can try it your self.

It's 5PM and they have their order ahead turned off - really irritating. It can't be that busy. Besides, we get a estimated time to pick up and we still have to go through the drive through line anyways. This location also took forever to open the inside compared to other locations. What's going on here?
Really slow service two people working the whole store you expect more from Starbucks . I will say that the staff was nice even under pressure
I will drive passed the other Starbucks location in Brownsburg to this one because they ALWAYS make my drink right.

This Starbucks is a huge disappointment. The baristas are rude, with one or two exceptions. The drinks are sub-par, and not made correctly. Today I asked the barista what flavors of syrup they offer and her reply was "You can check the website for that." As if it would be too much trouble for her to just tell me. I was the only person in line on a slow Sunday afternoon, it's not like there was a mad rush. I'm tired of being treated so rudely. I will not return to this Starbucks.

I love Starbucks but this is the single worst location I have ever encountered by far. Between 300 and 330 on Monday Mar 2 I was trapped in their drive thru line unable to get out during the half hour it took to get to the window and be able to escape. People were actually leaving their cars and clustering to talk while we waited, unable to escape. When we went in to plead just to be able to leave the line, forget the coffee, we were told "they got busy" - but hey looked fully staffed, not abnormally busy, and not edit silly hurried. Awful awful awful.

Starbucks as far as drinks go is... Well.... Starbucks. They're always exactly the same. I know what to expect and that's what I get. So that's the reason for the solid three stars. What baffles me about this location is that I have been going there consistently since I moved to this side of town about three years ago. And by consistently, I mean at least three times a week, every single week. They still do not know my name. Every time I go in, they act like its the first time they have ever seen me. They're never rude, but they're not really that nice and even when they're nice- they definitely don't recognize me. The QT employees next door recognize me more and they have only been seeing me for about 4 months... Step it up Starbucks. Please.
Love the drive through here. Courteous and quick. Friendly staff who allow for just the right amount of conversation while passing out drinks.

This is my go to spot near my house when I need a caffeine fix!  It is a small space in a strip mall in the out lot in front of the Target store.  The space maybe small but they have both indoor and outdoor seating with a fire place.  Makes a great spot for impromptu meetings, and those casual cups of Joe.  I especially love having a drive through!

This Starbucks is located at a rest stop on the NJ turnpike just outside of Delaware.   This Starbucks is more like a kiosk location, it's very small with one cashier & two people fixing drinks.   For the life of me I don't understand why one of the young ladies decided to go on break when the line was long as hell. I waited 15 minutes on line to order and another 3 minutes for my tea. This location is extremely busy and could really use more decks on hand.   I received excellent customer service, although small it was very neat and organized. My tea was made very well without me having to give instructions.   Other than the slow service due to lack of staffing I have no complaints.

We were on our way home from competition in Atlantic City and decided to make a pit stop for some yummy Starbucks. No wait at all to order however after we paid for the drink and waited a few minutes we were told that they did not have two of the ingredients to make our drink. They gave us a refund and then offered a free drink for our trouble but looking back on the reviews for this place it seems as though this is a common issue at this establishment. They have to get their act together in order to satisfy the customers

The service is VERY slow. I waited longer on a brewed coffee than I did for an espresso based drink because baristas are assigned to stations instead of orders. I used mobile order as the line was out the door and still waited longer than if I had been in line.

Something as run of the mill and assembly line as Starbucks I will only review isn't he instance where something is great or terrible.  This time it is great.  The people that work here are very friendly.  They seem personable with many of the customers, and they strive to deliver a positive experience and your food and beverage item exactly as you order it.    It is well maintained.  What else could you ask for?

This Starbucks sucks! You ask for light ice and your cup is 1/3 filled with ice. When you tell them that you asked for light ice, they just look at you. The female employee with really dark hair that's bleached blond doesn't know how to make drinks either. She's the one that put all of the ice in my drink. It tasted funny too, different than any other Starbucks made it. They need to work on teaching employees how to follow instructions and properly make drinks.

Wow- I'm no Starbucks expert, but they're usually nice. This one is gross. For starters, upon entering, at least 7 employees were buzzing around, but no one was taking orders. The woman who finally assisted me was very nice & helpful. Drink was fine.  I needed a space for some computer work. Had to put napkins down as a barrier between my computer and the table. The bench to sit on is completely broken, dented in, and uncomfortable. Only use the bathroom if you are wearing a hazmat suit. I would definitely not recommend this place unless you use the drive-through.

This location is living up to the Starbucks standards for sure ! The only issue is the placement of the building. You can't see it from the road. So you can easily pass. Other than that, two thumbs up.

I've read other reviews of this Starbucks and most are complaining of the lines.  Its important that you remember that this particular location is in a very densely populated section of Tampa.  You have all of Westshore / Parts of South Tampa and the business district coming to this one location.  I've also thought the lines were very long too but just last week I was about 7th in line and 10 people piled in behind me and I was at the register is less than 5 minutes.  I had my 4 very custom drinks in less than another 5 minutes and I was on my way.  Very surprised.    Obviously its not the fastest location out there but its super convenient and in the middle of everything.    Have you gone to a Starbucks in Manhattan or Boston?  This location would feel like a breeze.  I guess its all relative :)
Great and fast service. Their chocolate cake pop is to die for. I can eat at least 3 a day, I'm addicted. Nice and helpful staff

Just went through the drive thru and the young man at the window literally deserves a raise. The small 30 second interaction we had made my day with his positive, go get 'em attitude. A plus costumer service!!!!
Very slow service.  Arrived inside at 1025am, 35 minutes prior to church.  Had to leave without my coffee at 1050am to make church on time.  Out on the $$.
Was good before they hired a bunch of duds. I guess it's because of their lack of pay.

I was really happy that there was a Starbucks in the casino. The barista was very friendly.  The reason I'm only giving 4 stars is because it was really expensive. I guess harrahs hits them with the resort fee. Still love the convenience.

This is not a Starbucks that I frequent because it's not normally on my way to where I'm going.  However I have been here a few times and it's pretty consistent. The staff is friendly and I appreciate that.  The drive thru is kind of a cluster because of the limited space, but so are most Starbucks drive thrus!

So... five employees... One working as a cashier, one making drinks one squatting down I assume looking for some thing, one sticking his butt in her face laughing then going on about "blah blah blah bullshit, blah blah blah bullshit", and the final one stuffing his face with food and visiting each individual employee. On a positive note, they got the order right so I guess that deserves one star at least. ‍

Went in here with some friends around 10 a.m. on a Thursday while on a girls' trip to S.B. and ordered the same drink I always do - a venti iced coffee with half and half.  The drink was $0.60 more than usual.  I asked why the price was different and the unfriendly lady ringing my drink said it was because I asked for half and half.  WHAT??????? I told her that if it was a charge for that, I would've put it in myself (which I used to do before a Starbucks at another location once told me that they would be happy to do it for me) and she annoyingly asked if I wanted a refund.  Obviously, I wasn't going to hold up the line, but now I feel like I should have.  Unbelievable!

I can rate 0 stars if I could. Terrible service and waiting line is always long. I got a gift card and they didn't have an envelope. They told me to go to the Acme and get the envelopes. Really?!!!?!?
Very nice staff! Desi made my day by being so friendly. My iced coffee tasted great.
The service is okay.  The inside is very cold. It was a rainy morning and the weather was in the 50's, but Starbucks was significantly colder inside.
The baristas are all very nice and helpful! If you are looking to enjoy a nice coffee shop atmosphere, this is the place! Absolutely love the barista Ingrid! Good workin girl!
It's Starbucks: good quality, overpriced coffee with code names for the "In Crowd", but the bathroom was filthy. Unless it is inconvenient, go to Wawa and save your money.
They do not take Starbucks rewards.  I'm getting pretty sick of this BS.   Time to cash out the reward as and switch to Dunkin Donuts.

I would not recommend having babies here. The owners were very displeased, everyone kept giving me judgmental looks. and they weren't supportive at all, in this time of great stress. If i could rate 0 stars, i most definitely would. when i asked for assistance they told me i was too young and banned me from all starbucks franchises. don't have babies here!

This Starbucks is awesome. I have no idea why such low stars. The staff is super nice and patient. This is an incredibly busy location so they do get backed up but most do when it's that busy.......and I've never had to wait more than ten minutes.  There's not a ton of seating but they do have outdoor seating which is nice.

Large Starbucks right near 476 - so fairly convenient but the trafic at rush hour is bad.  Drive thru is available but we opted to order inside (and use the restrooms).  No issues.  Got in and out fairly quickly.  Parking lot was PACKED.  Alex (cashier/barista) was very good. Seating area was clean and comfy.

Dropped in here to here a tea. Looks like the drive thru is super busy all the time so I just walked in. Then about six members of staff said hello to me before I got to the counter to order. It was a bit too much! Imagine I hadn't had a coffee yet!!  I ordered a tea and asked him to leave room for milk. I was given a tea latte which might be the best mistake they've ever made because it was damn good. That will be my new got drink of choice. Lots of seating and overzealous staff. Decent Star Bucks.
I  like this location alot. Everyone is really friendly and good customer service.  I go there almost every day.  This is one of my favorite Starbucks Coffee location.

My daughter loves the spinach feta wrap and I like all of the varieties of the sous vide egg bites especially the bacon one. Generally we order a custom fru fru drink and a very classic coffee with 2 breakfast foods and almost all of the time they get it correct. So let's say that we go to Starbucks four times a week. That is approximately $85 a week. I  am not sure why a super simple iced coffee with heavy whipping cream only was incorrect. It had sugar in it. I know the difference when something has sugar in it. My cheeks  get hot and I feel funny. It was so sugary I think I lost a toe over the excessive amount if sugar that pulsed through my body.   OK I understand that Starbucks is like anyplace else understaffed and overly busy.   We almost always preorder and we always have to wait at least 20 minutes, which is fine.   Today we ordered With a person standing outside taking orders in the drive-through.  If  i am able to  correctly navigate the Starbucks app and get my order correct, I would expect that they would be able to do the same as a Starbucks employee taking my order in person.  What is concerning is that when I called to let them know Of their mistake and that I would be back  after dropping kids to school, in about 20 minutes, that I really didn't have time to wait. The person was RUDE and told me they couldn't Guarantee how long it would take and then I may have to wait. I asked  i could give them the order number and have them make it and I would be in shortly,  20 minutes. Again I was told she couldn't guarantee the wait time. I asked if she could just make it and I would be in  and it didn't need to be hot or cold it just needed to be corrected without sugar.    Somehow the phone call was disconnected. It was rude and I haven't ever had this happen at a Starbucks where they custom make your order. Just grumpy that I still don't have my coffee and I have to check my blood sugar and  likely correct it with insulin as I don't consume sugar/sugar substitutes. Thanks Starbucks.     Really it's a blessing because I am going to save my 85 dollars and make my own coffee.  I hope this teaches my daughter that if you pay for a service or product the expectation is that it's correct and if it's not, then advocate as a consumer, to have it corrected without rudeness.

This starbucks is beginning to lose my patronage a little. I mostly come to get work done, and enjoy a nice iced tea, but lately the wifi is becoming very spotty, and I can not get as much work don as I'd like. When i ask about it the staff just shrugs, and says there isn't anything they can do about it. i wish they would try a little harder for someone who buys something every time they visit. I am on the hunt for a new coffee shop to go to.

As packed as this location always is, being in the middle of a business district area, they move remarkably well. The service is as fast as it can be but if you have questions they take the time to answer them. They are kept immaculately clean and as busy as they always are there's always a seat to be found If you want to just take a moment and relax

I never realized there was a Starbucks here until I had a meeting scheduled here a few months ago. This is one of the Starbucks that is on the smaller side, and located around so many striip malls and stores, I somehow hadn't noticed it before. I went inside, ordered a cold pressed juice and sat outside.  The outside - that's the best part. They have outdoor patio seating that made our meeting much more fun. It was great to be in the sun and the wifi also reached out there, allowing me to get a little work done before leaving. Solid Starbs.

This Sbux is the best! The service was super friendly. Since it's in the Hyatt lobby it's not a store that honors gold card rewards but the barista wishes me a very happy birthday and gave me my PSL for free. Now that's customer service!

This is a BEAUTIFUL Starbucks. I have been to a many in my day and this one was a great new building with happy workers.   I got a dirty matcha with egg bites for breakfast. I forgot to ask for Oatmilk and the barista happily changed the milk in my drink. I was so thankful

This is the closest Starbucks to my house, and this visit was pleasant overall! They're nice and up to date on their decor, and the music is GOOD! I visited early in the afternoon and was pleased when she told me they had breakfast sandwiches left since I was there for breakfast and tea.   My tea was flavorless, but IMO the Honey Citrus Mint  (formerly the Medicine Ball from the secret menu) hasn't been right since they added it to the regular menu.

i came to this location expecting a fast and easy process. however i was sadly mistaken. my colleague order a simple black coffee. the location was not crowded whatsoever at the time. we waited an exceptionally long time, and happen to still be waiting. next time i'm going to dunkin. what can i say? america runs i  dunkin for a reason.
Use to like this Starbucks but they have went downhill. Always getting orders wrong. Speak to customers in a condescending tone. Very disappointed. Do better.
Nice coffee, but the customer service is not nice. She rushes me on ordering while I tried to order green tea fraps.

Keeps getting worse. Today, 8:30. They took my order for pike coffee. And I had to wait because it was not brew. It is 8:30 in the morning. Nine people working, it is a coffee shop, who is monitoring this. Not the first time, and who ever is brewing the coffee is also doing a terrible job. Steve, come back please.
Looooove this spot and especially Kelsie's warmth and awesome personality along with her way of rockin' my order to perfection!! Grazie mille!

This is the second time I have ordered a protein box with fruit and cheese and had moldy/mushy fruit. I'd have gone back but the service took too long the first time and we had already hit the interstate. Double check your fruit   And they continue to just mess up: Gave them another chance because of time and it ends up wasting my $$. Lesson Learned (again): This is why I have given up on this Starbucks. When I use the Starbucks on Lebanon Rd (or anywhere else around here aside from this one) my drinks are usually great. This Starbucks continually fails. This is one example. These are both Cold Foam beverages, the one on the left is the CF Cappuccino purchased from the Lebanon Rd store and the one on the right is the CF Cascara from this store. If it is supposed to be a COLD FOAM beverage I think more than a sparse topping of cold foam is appropriate. This is always the standard at this location. Aside from the ridiculously long wait time in line, the beverages just continue to be lacking.
I'm updating my rating 2 days in a row my has been ice cold, seriously if I want cold coffee that's what I'd order. Check your coffee before you leave

Came here for a couple skinny vanilla lattes. It was crazy busy, but they still got everyone through in a timely manner. The lattes were perfect. They now honor the Starbucks rewards here according to a sign.

This is the best Starbucks in Nashville. The location is really large with plenty of seating. The best thing about it is the staffing. They really seem to enjoy their job and its consistently an enjoyable experience. The baristas always know me by name and know my drink order. Casey the store manager does a great job of keeping her employees happy and making it feel like one big family. Also they are impeccably clean. Keep it up Bell Meade!

It's a Starbucks whats not to love. Even my auto spell has it memorized and will correct me if i don't capitalize the "S". this location has fast service and plenty of seating. It even has a drive through window for those on the go. 4 stars because i was terrified of the wasp flying around near my table that day.
Awesome service and great coffee! Matt hooked up my birthday coffee by adding vanilla bean powder to my latte. Yum! Great idea! Thanks everyone there! I'll be back!

I come to Starbucks waaaayyyyy more than I should. I know this location very well. The drive thru is always courteous and prompt but sometimes I like to go in. The restroom is ALWAYS DISGUSTING.  I know these coffees are costly and I know Starbucks can afford a cleaning service to clean their facilities.  Their baristas clearly do not think they get paid enough to keep a clean work environment.  Not only was the restroom disgusting but so was the lobby. The floors had massive amounts of trash along with what looked like weeks of yuck built up on the floor. I'd love to offer my company as some relief. Please contact stellar cleaning services LLC if you need a professional to clean this place up. Now let me start on the baristas... there were some AWESOME  workers about 6 months ago but now they are all young and worried about hanging out and how much fun it would be if they could get another of their friends to work here with them. OMG please split these people up! They do not get the job done well when they are busy talking about the weekend coming instead of listening to the order being placed through drive thru!! They definitely need a class on how to do your job and do less socializing.  Bottom line, starbucks costs too much money to not be taken care of in a top notch manner!

We stopped by here after spending some time at city park which was pretty much a hop, skip, and a jump away. This Starbucks is located right over the little bridge in the Lakeside area. We came around 4 pm for the Meet for Macchiatos event, and apparently so did everyone else because  they were pretty swamped. Anyhow, the service was still prompt, and the barista Cat knows how to make a mean frappe. Our drinks were served up ice cold, tasty and near perfect. We ordered the Coconutmilk Mocha Macchiato, and the Iced Cinnamon Almondmilk Macchiato. I preferred the Iced cinnamon almond milk one and I had this made with sf dolce syrup. The coconut milk mocha we added extra Splenda and stirred it more and it was better after that. Overall our coffee was pretty yummy, especially for the buy 1, share 1 event.

I wanted to take a moment to give a shout out to this location. My daughter and I made a special trip because the staff is so great.  The seating area outside is nice and spread out.  They welcomed our dog and visited with us. We will be back!
This Starbucks is consistently poor. They do not accept the Starbucks Gold Card, which is annoying, and the coffee is almost always bad--very acidic and stale.

This Starbucks is so slow! I got there at 7:40am today and there were about 4 people ahead of me. No big deal, so I thought. I thought 20 minutes was enough time to order and get my drink as I had to be too work across the street by 8am. I thought wrong. The Barista making my drink was on training so I understand she may not be up to speed but her level of slow was darn near reverse.  Needless to say,  I was 5 minutes late getting across the street to work.

Service was fast and friendly enough. But we ordered five drinks - four simple iced coffees - that were all not filled up (about a half inch or more below the lid).   The iced Cloud Macchiato was like 3 inches down and barely cold within a couple minutes. The picture I posted has NOT been drunken out of. The foam didn't last at all. That was a $6 drink. I've ordered these drinks before and they aren't like this. The Cloud Macchiato does have the foam go down, but only after you start drinking. Not so fast on it's own.   Both of these issues aren't normal so I wasn't impressed.

Brownsburg finally has a new Starbucks--which has had no problems finding customers.  The new location on the northern side of the freeway has several noticeable improvements over the old one. First of all, the parking situation is way better, with a circular set up for easy access. Second, there is more space in the interior for hanging out. It is way less cramped. And way less noisy.  The crowd here seems less uptight than the group who frequent the older branch.  Because its new, there is an efficient and fresh-looking checkout area.  No exterior seating, though. I like this option at various times of the year.  Service--friendly and error-free so far

I stopped into this Starbucks near closing time, and was told that they were "out of coffee beverages". I ordered a Frappuccino so I can only assume that they meant THAT and not that they were actually OUT OF COFFEE. It was close to closing time, and its possible that the staff just didn't want to bother, but I had to drive to Mt Juliet and a Frappuccino would have really helped.

I don't see why this Starbucks has only a couple stars. Service was excellent, my order was perfect and the place is clean. I don't live here but it's a normal small store. Bonus is it's in an Albertsons large shopping center.

Shoutout to Dylan for his amazing customer service.   I did a mobile order and drove to this location. When I walked in and sat at the mobile order station. Within a minute Dylan asked me what the name for my order was. I told him and he said he didn't see it, but he could get it started if I told him what it was.   So as I was waiting for my venti green tea latte with soy and a cake pop I double checked my order to make sure that I did pay for it. This is when I realized I ordered at a completely different location. I told him my mistake, and was about to trek on over when Dylan said he would take care of it by making my latte, giving me my cake pop, and that he would call them later to confirm the mishap.   On top of everything he remade my drink because when it was done because he wasn't 100% sure if he made it with soy. The only way to be 100% sure what to start over.   He is amazing!
Quick. Good coffee. Reliable. Just as any Starbucks should be.
Nothing too spectacular about this Starbucks.   However, this Starbucks ALWAYS has a line, a long line and a long wait. By the time you get your coffee, you probably have lost your craving for it.

Small, quiet, attached to Hampton Inns.  All the handicapped sports were taken by enormous pickup truck with no stickers or disabled plates, super frustrating. Coffee was hot and strong  just like my husband likes. I like the free water to drink in the car. Good coffee.  Impossible parking.

Starbucks drive thru in Reno! My review is regarding drive through service only. The line moves quickly, always offering to warm your danish/muffin & perfect drinks on each visit. Although, this 5 star  review is for the drive thru window employee. She always greets you with a smile & good morning, how's your day?  Making everyone's day a bit brighter- Thank you!
Due to covid 19 it's the law to wear masks and whenever I go, there are are few that don't wear them  Drinks aren't made correct ether  Food is uncooked

Ok... lets start off positive. Still the best coffee. Still over priced. But good. Now, onto the problem. If you are selling food then stock food. 9 our of 10 times going to this facility they do not have what is on the menu. They are out. And, most of the time it is 10am in the morning. There is NO way they can run out so please don't give that as an excuse. I will probably still come here.... I just wish they would fix their shiznit!!!

I love Starbucks food & beverage products. I'm a regular at those near my home. Today, out running errands, I stopped at this location for drive thru. Slowest service ever - for those in front of me as well as my wait. When I got to the window (only 2 vehicles in front of me), the window barista asked if I had ordered a peppermint fru-fru drink. In short, they lost track and had not made my drink. A bad day or SOP?  Don't know, but not up to expected performance.

I went here to get my Salted Cold Foam Cold Brew, and the barista said they were all out of Salted Cold Foam Cold Brew. What the heck do you mean you're out of Salted Cold Foam Cold Brew, y'all are advertising it EVERYWHERE. So, they gave me an iced coffee instead of my Salted Cold Foam Cold Brew- and thankfully they put some Salted Cold Foam on top, but the Salted Cold Foam wasn't even Salted!? Wtf is this? Why would I go to get a Salted Cold Foam Cold Brew if the Salted Cold Foam isn't even Salted? UNaCCEPTABLE. This is why people regularly die in the bathrooms.

Worst Starbucks I've ever been to, and I am a frequent Starbucks customer/enthusiast where ever I am traveling.  Employees are slow, disinterested and have absolutely no sense of customer service. I've been twice in 12 months, with equally disappointing customer service. I won't be returning.  The coffee is good and the setting is nice, but the customer service makes this a place to avoid if you can.

I am not sure why this has such low rating. The workers are always over the top friendly. It is a nice area to work either inside or their patio outside that also has a few tables to work on as well. As for the drinks, I've ordered both in person and on the go and never waited for either. I have not had any that I didn't like, but would feel comfortable asking for a redo with how upbeat and personable all the workers always were. I have noticed a great change since this first was opened- they needed to work out the kinks! Most of the workers are new.

Review completely based on using it as a place to study.  I would give it one star but it only has three ratings and I don't want to kill it's rating that bad.  One reason I hate this place and walk to one much further away to study at... NO OUTLETS!  Seriously people... I don't understand what the deal is with places in philadelphia offering free wifi without access to more than one outlet in the whole place... it makes sense if you want one time customers.... but not if you want them to come back...
Always great service when I come here. And the baristas offer free samples to make sure I like the drink. My favorite Starbucks in Reno!

I've had good service at every other Starbucks I've ever been to! At this location the lady who took my order was so snobby and acted like it was a chore to take my order when thats her job and she didn't even get my order correct.
