The cold brew was so bitter it was hard to drink. They should try to work on their friendliness and hospitality skills.

Least efficient SBux I've ever been to. Drip coffee was old. One of the espresso machines was "down" and it took nearly 15 minutes for 2 lattes. Lots of kids running around, in fact, two children were wrestling.
This Starbucks is a nightmare for mobile orders. It is normal to wait 20mins or more sometimes to receive your order. I've had to cancel it many times because I had no more time to wait.
I never review starbucks'. This one was fantastic. My cashier was perky as hell and friendly asf. She really made my day and I hope to spread her joy. My coffee tastes better because if it :)

I always have such a great time at this Starbucks.  The baristas are all super friendly and great to talk to. I usually place my order through the app and pick it up at the drive through.  Most of the time they know who it is as soon as I pull up and say "order for Ryan."  They have a personal touch which has been lost to most other Starbucks locations.

This Starbucks is located just right off 65 and on Southport road so it can be quite busy. The location is relatively easy to get in and out of. I don't stop by here often but when I do the staff are always friendly and helpful. They get through the line pretty quickly and with the drinks made correctly.

Awe Starbucks! I have only visited via the drive though recently to get my warm delightful steaming cup of goodness!  I appreciate the app and the new rewards system that gives you credit even if you don't pay through the app!

This Starbucks is located in a fantastic location. Located at the corner of Linebaugh and Sheldon, this is a great place to go if you are passing through the area. Compared to most Starbucks locations, this place is so much bigger and more spacious. This place has a ton of table seating as well as couch seating. But the centerpeace has to be the coffee bar that allows you to sit there and watch the baristas make your drink for you. The feel of the coffee bar and open coffee and food prep area really gives this Starbucks location a very modern feel. It makes you think of the open kitchen trend that is soaring in popularity at restaurants around the world. The service is also really good. The people working here are really friendly and take their time explaining drinks to you and are patient with you as you customize your order or have other requests. Keep up the good work here! :)

Worst Starbucks I've ever been to! Shouldn't even be called "Starbucks" because it feels like a knock-off! Pink drink didn't taste like strawberries, but more like Red Bull! Iced Chai with almond milk was NOTHING like Chai....no milk color to it at all, very watery looking and tasted like there was seltzer water in it!! Threw the whole thing away. I've been to other Starbucks in reststops and they still had the Starbucks feel and quality, so I know its possible!

Starbucks is my favorite holiday stop!  I always look forward to their new holiday flavors and it doesn't seem like the holidays without stopping at Starbucks to both get one of the holiday flavors as well as look for Christmas stocking stuffers.
It's a Time wasted crew, and the attitude that you can never miss when the Starbucks employees deal with you in 1300 s Dale Mabry is Impressive

Made a mobile order this morning to try their new Pistachio Latte.  My order was ready when I arrived. I was a bit confused so I waited around -- thinking my name would be called, but there is a sign clear as day that says something along the lines of "mention your name for uber eats and mobile orders." Whoops  During my short wait, I ordered Baloo a puppucino (free cup of human-dairy whip cream for dogs)  , because I couldn't come home empty handed.  The line was wrapped around the plaza, so I was so grateful to have a mobile order.  This location has been remodeled, so it's super open in the inside now which is perfect for COVID precautions.  There are no tables inside, you can order inside if you desire. There is one set of chairs outside. The decor is super modern and can't wait to see what the rest of furniture will look like when they decide it is safe to add it.

Just went there this morning and the girl at the window was very rude to me because i didn't know how to use the app correctly. Then, when i received my venti iced coffee it was filled to the top with ice & had barely any coffee in it. I've never had either of these problems at any other location. Very disappointing.

The second I place my 1st Dr Marten boot within this holy espresso bean Temple, I hear John Doe (Starbucks Barista) welcome me as if I was Danny Zuko returning to Rydell High after a long summer vaca. The Barista, if not busy at that time, comes from around the counter just to truly express his feelings towards his customers making me and the other caffeine junkys feel simple at Bliss. Many times I get to the location and my drink has already been started and by the time I lock my noble steed(bike) to the pole outside the drink is awaiting me. All the baristas are swift, social, intelligent, beautiful beings that offer something more than most Baristas. That something is the reason why myself and other colleagues will ONLY go to this location, they are my reason for REASON!!

this is without a doubt the worst starbucks ever  i have yet to ever get a drink order correct. its so simple: venti vanilla latte almond milk no foam   today no vanilla i wa the first or second person thru the drive thru i had to repeat the order twice , we had a whole lol momnet about no vanilla creamer at home i mean seriously at $5.50 a cup get it together. i usually drive tot he 71st and bindford location because the location  by me on 56 st. is so slow and they always alway always mess up something in the order or you have to repeat it 3 times.  get it together

Literally the absolute slowest Starbucks I have ever been to. I am a barista of almost five years. I know when there is a lot of volume and when a set of baristas are just taking their time. There were several men working- very slowly. They were talking and walking around despite the ever growing line of people who had already ordered and were waiting on our drinks. It took us probably 15 to get one medium mocha....

Smaller Starbucks in the Olde City section of Philly. Can get cramped when busy. seating is at a premium. Typical food and drinks here. Service is the usual efficient folks/kids that work at these places. Never any issues. Parking of course is tough in this area. Does the job for Starbucks fans that live and work in the area and want a drink or bite to go,  but if you are looking for place to bring your laptop and sip your latte slowly there are better Starbucks to be found.
Simply put the barista doesn't  really care much about customer service.  Drinks are not made to Starbucks standards.

This Starbucks is a hit or miss! I have gone  there every day for the past 8 years! Sometimes the coffee is good. Sometimes awful! The lines are ridiculous! Weekends I go to Hendersonville as you may as well park at this one! The people there are very friendly but they get my coffee wrong at least 4 days out of 7! If this continues I will start making y on coffee at home!

I was here Saturday to get two iced coffees. The girl messed up my order, at first only charging me for one and not putting down that I wanted vanilla syrup (when I specifically asked for that). She politely fixed it and charged me for another one. I noticed afterwards when checking my bank that one cost about a dollar less than the other, which is kind of odd but oh well. My boyfriend's drink came out fine, but mine had no vanilla. The barista quickly fixed that and added it in for me. I really do appreciate that even though they made a few mistakes, they fixed it with a smile. I probably won't be back just because there are other locations with shorter waits that make my drinks correctly consistently.
Excellent location! The staff is always courteous and never miss any special instructions. The lines generally move very quick and always a great experience. Keep up the great work!

The staff this morning was extremely rude. When asked who was here for mobile orders a few people said their names and the girl shouted one at a time. A gentleman came in without a mask (by mistake) and the girl shouted you can't come in here without a mask so get out !!! Who the hell does she think she is, never going back to this elitist dump !!!

Honestly one of the worst starbucks I've ever been to. Staff isn't friendly the place isn't welcoming and chances are every time you go in here you'll be waiting for 10+ minutes in line and for your drink. Good luck.

I don't know how - but they consistently get my coffee orders correct. I have patroned the Rock Hill location several dozen times since my morning commute changed about six months ago and while their staff is always ultra friendly - they do not get the exact same order correct. I cannot figure out how the same order between two Starbucks in the same zip code (? I think?) can be interpreted so wildly... and this one gets it correct so in the mornings I have rerouted to drive from Webster to Crestwood because they get it correct...   They don't ever act like my drink is irritating to them to help reduce the amount of sugar I am taking in but still getting what I want.  They drew a rainbow on my coffee sleeve on NYE and it made my day.  Everyone that has ever met me at the window has always been so nice and pleasant and made conversation if we had to wait. Kota is one of the names I do remember (I am bad with names) - the gentleman who is a veteran who works on weekends, as well.  Always a pleasant experience when grabbing my morning latte.
The very best Starbucks, with the greatest staff. Has a drive through, also. Nice interior.

The best Starbucks ever! We have one literally right next to our house but we go out of our way to go here. The service is fast and the workers are always super friendly. If they weren't so nice I'd say they might be robots because they make everything so fast and the drink always turns out perfectly. They don't have friendly robots yet right? Even if they are robots its still my favorite Starbucks.

This store is consistently among the worst Starbucks I've been to.  Their service has gone down since my last review, and if it weren't so dang close to home, we'd never come back.  They have trouble making drinks correctly, are ridiculously slow, and service is never delivered with a smile.

If I could give 0 stars, I would. This Starbucks really needs to up its game with service. Employees seemed abrupt and unfriendly at the least. It literally took 20 minutes just to make a grande chai latte. We then ordered a hot expresso that turned out to be a COLD double shot of coffee. Staff never asked for our names and QUIETLY announced our orders. Luckily, we are from Seattle and will never be stepping foot on this property again!
Not very busy compared to most Starbucks. Kind of slow for the lack of business. Close to our hotel so that was nice
There has simply got to be a management problem at this place...I have never been to a place where the employees are consistently joyless seem to sort of care, but with not much enthusiasm.

Customer service wise this Starbucks is good. Fast drive-through, great customer service and clean restrooms, like almost every other Starbucks I've been to.  So why just two stars? The parking situation is horrible! The flow of traffic is really bad, in fact dangerous if you see how the patterns move. I would be very surprised if there haven't been any accidents or fender-benders here due to the design of the parking lot. Having said that, it's a block away from my office and convenient as heck, so I roll the dice to get my tall cup of Pikes...every single day.

Let me first start by saying that I just had the oddest most unpleasant experience at this location. I began my day with an iced cold beverage purchased at Starbucks and placed my belongings on a table and decided to use another so that I can "spread" my belongings and study. At first the atmosphere was welcoming without any interruption. A gentleman then approached me and asked to remove my table and my belongings because I am only assuming he needed the table to sit with his friend or significant other. Naturally (without hesitation), I took my belongings and squeezed all of my things onto one table which was fine because I figured the lobby became busy and the table was needed by others. I proceeded to look around and notice that the lobby was sprinkled with only 2-3 people waiting for beverages and several tables/seats available. The "extra" table was pulled to a separate location by what I soon found out was by management (or what seemed like was management due to his authoritative nature with fellow employees). I found this displeasing as I was interrupted and customer service/consideration was not taken into account. The table was not needed by others nor was it blocking an exit (in case you were wondering). How inconsiderate? I am so disturbed that I could not even continue studying and took my belongings and left the location. Sadly, I used to work at this very location and I know that things like this were not done to a customer (paying or not paying). Hmm... must be new management. 2 stars only because the baristas were exceptional.

By far the slowest Starbucks I have ever been to. Eve going inside the wait is longer. Not sure their staffing but they have staff that are doing nothing at times. Your drink will sit there and just melt. Go to the one on grand for better service.

Pretty rude, unnecessarily condescending and dismissive. I just attended a digital event in which the chief operating officer was speaking and she mentioned the welcoming culture this brand strives to create. That clearly missed this location as I experienced a team member serve someone who came in after me that they seemed to personally know and then walk away from me. When I called out "excuse me- can I pick up an order as well" what I got back was "what's your name- I don't know what you're standing here for..." ....as I stood in the mobile pickup area that I stood in, underneath an arrow that read "mobile orders", a couple weeks ago.

My drink is NEVER right. Ever. The drive-thru workers are inattentive and unprofessional. I ordered but never got a response from the person working the window so I drove around. The girl at the window never said a word, gave me my drink while talking to a co-worker, never turned to make eye contact or give me a total. I paid in cash. She never looked at it and closed the window. I could've given her $1 and she would've never noticed or cared. The only reason I even know what I owed is because I order it so frequently. I suggest going to the one on Bell Road!

They served me cold coffee.  As soon as it was handed over I could feel the chill.  I ordered coffee with cream.  Come on Starbucks, keep fresh and hot coffee ready!  You are a coffee company and if you can't serve a good hot cup of coffee you should be ashamed.  I handed back to the barista and she replaced it and said the person must have gotten it from a pot that was turned off.  WHY would that coffee be available to be served.  I'm a Starbucks fan and this was downright disappointing.  Do better!

Did an online order, waited inside for our order for about 10-15 mins to realize that new people that were walking in to order received their drinks before us. Then we realized our drinks were behind the counter top where we could not see them. when we walked up to the counter to tell the staff member that those were ours she was too busy talking to a friend in there, we had to grab another staff member who was making the drinks to give it to us. They saw us waiting for a while even when we became the only ones left in there.
Trash is overflowing. Cables need to be wiped. Bathrooms need to be cleaned. I think the morale is down across the board at Starbucks. Not looking good.

One of my favorite Starbucks in South Tampa! The staff is always extremely friendly. I usually order on the mobile app ahead of time even when I'm a minute down the road and they have my order ready to go when I walk in. The team they have right now is definitely able to keep up with the rush! The store is on the smaller side and they don't have as many prepared food/snack options as other places but the drinks are made with consistent quality!

This Starbucks is my go to location since it's right by the house. Consistency is key and this location always comes through. Warm and friendly greeting upon arrival, quick to get your order and have you on your way promptly. It can get a bit busy but the staff works hard to keep customers moving.
Staff is very friendly and super helpful. I ordered a carry out coffee service for 10. (I think it is called the traveler). My order was ready right on time and included everything I needed.

Good morning Yelpers.   So I am here to get my morning coffee. and the customer service I just have to help about.  The barista that works the drive thru is phenomenal. Her name is Mia.  Now when I say her customer service skills outweigh any others that I have encountered at this location I mean that sincerely.  Thank you Mia for your awesome service. it is because of people like you that I continue to come to this location.

To be honest, I love Starbucks, but this particular location I avoid. The employees are super nice, and know all of their customers, but it is always busy and even when it doesn't seem busy, the mobile orders make what should be a 5 minute wait a 15-20 minute wait. Most likely due to the proximity of the hospital and multiple businesses in the area, and the extremely convenient location for those staying in the neighborhood. If you have the time to spare, this is a cozy location, great for people watching and local flavor, I would not recommend stopping here if you are crunched for time.

I have been in this location a few times and never had any bad things to say until now. My mom sister and I were shopping in the area and decided to get drinks. We ordered the iced caramel macchiato w/ almond milk, caramel frap, and a strawberries and cream frap. When I tried the macchiato it did NOT taste like almond milk. I do not even know what they put in my drink since it did not taste like the usual one I always order- not sure if maybe the milk was expired or they made it differently but it tasted awful. I couldn't even drink it and I'm not the person to complain and ask for a refund but I could not even swallow it. When I went up to the counter the barista making the drinks seemed passive and annoyed (not a big deal because she was busy). Then I went to the cashier and told them about my incident- they did not seem very apologetic but rather passive. They did ask me if I wanted another drink made but I just did not like the treatment and was very disappointed by this location so I refused.

I have never done this but I am finally over the arrogant, non helpful attitudes at this location.  A handful of good people work there but this is directed mostly at the "upity" little guy with brown shoulder length curly hair working this morning.  I asked him a question and as always, he responds with an attitude of why are you bothering me, I don't know anything.  I asked another employee and the attitude was better,  but nothing.  Three customers in line helped me.  The Starbucks employees need to remember customers who buy from them every single day.  Remember they make coffee and there job is CUSTOMER service.

This morning, in the drive-thru, about 8:50, the twit had the balls to call me rude, and throw attitude because she couldn't seem to do HER job properly.   I ordered, and I quote, "A venti AND a grande, white chocolate mocha." She said, "Ok, that's a grande w.c.m. venti cup, see you up here." I guess I didn't pay attention, as I thought she was competent to do her job, but my husband asked of she got both or if she really said something along the lines of a smaller size coffee, in a bigger sized cup. I didn't know but I would ask when I got up to the window. Lol well, when we got there, I asked her if she got both and she said just the one. I told her it was two coffees, different sizes.  She looked at me and said with a shit attitude, "Oh, we only heard the one, it's not our fault", almost like I didn't speak english when I ordered and how dare I. There was even another twit hiding behind the window, saying how rude it was, when we were driving away. What a little puss you are girl   Lol some advice for you assholes, it's not rude to fix MY order, you messed up. Maybe you didn't like the fact that I had that look of "WTF" on my face, as I didn't think your job was hard, but I'm the one paying for them, so I have the RIGHT to get what I wanted, to make a correction if something is wrong and shouldn't have to deal with shit heads like you.   Maybe try instead, "Oh, I'm sorry,  I will fix that, I didn't hear you correctly", with a smile on your face because me, the customer, didn't make the mistake. If this was a local, smaller business, I don't think the owners or managers would think your customer service was up to par or approve, AT ALL. Grow up if you're going to be working with customers. You make the entire place look bad, not just yourselves!
Not a real SB, no rewards so I don't understand how they have a storefront in a downtown metro area? No wifi and they screwed up my drink, nice work! Won't be back.

My daughter and I stopped at this location on our way out of town, I'm so glad we did. It's a comfortable atmosphere, there's no overwhelming loud soud of hipster music and the employees aren't pushy. They got our order right and was extremely efficient. Now let's talk about the restroom .... Very clean and gorgeous skylights!

Been frequenting this amazing establishment for ten years. Over the years, there has been a lot of staffing  changes but I can honestly say the current AM crew is phenomenal! They are quick and attentive, always willing to correct any mistakes. Trista is a great leader and a lot of subpar service issued have been corrected under her supervision. The drive thru is often run by Ada and she is a bright spot in my day. Grey and Kenji are the best baristas we have ever had! Keep it up

Yesterday was first time ever going to Starbucks and today I've gone again , this company is really good and the staff is great too , this place is always busy and I know why , thanks Starbucks I'm committed now , will probably go every day now cause I'm fired up like an 8 day alarm clock , I'm going  try everything on the menu

I mean, I was in Reno.  I was in a casino.  There was a Starbucks.   I mean, could it have been any better? I was already pretty lucky there was an actual Starbucks INSIDE my hotel, so I wasn't really complaining.   The first day, I ordered my regular drink and it took the girl at the cashier about a good 10 minutes to take my order, and that didn't even include the wait time (which was about 10 mins). When I got my drink, it was scorching hot, like they decided to burn the milk when I asked for extra hot and through two cups and a sleeve it was terrible.   Next time, different group of bartistas were working, and the wait was quick and a lot friendlier. But same thing with my coffee, the milk was burnt and I didn't even ask for it extra hot this time. But it tasted weird, different, not worth what I paid.   So basically, it's a coffee shop inside a hotel that has the Starbucks logo slapped onto everything, but the service/ the drink/ the everything else isn't what you would expect.  Because it is in a hotel and tourist area, the prices were a little steeper than usually almost by a dollar and they do charge tax, so if you're using your SB card- which they allow- you're probably going to have an odd number remaining balance, which I'm somewhat OCD about.   They do accept your starbucks app and the giftcards, except they don't allow you to redeem your free drinks.   There's another one brand new opened at El Dorado.. I wonder what that's like.

I love starbucks but I will pass this location and do without my favorite latte!  Everytime I gave this place a chance I was disappointed.  The staff is miserable and always with a bad attitude.  Ive gotten the wrong drink multiple times and the wait, is well, beyond awful!!!  The service was awful and I rarely had a decent experience here. I will continue to do the drive by!!
Great experience this morning with the young gentleman at the counter.  The only person to ask how to spell my name. Thank you!! A great way to start my morning:)

No respect for handicapped people. The handicapped table has been used the entire time I've been in the cafe, over 1 hour, and no one cares. The first 40 min it was occupied by a Starbucks manager interviewing a candidate. Shame, shameful.

This is the best Starbucks I have ever been to. The energy and atmosphere was amazing. The staff is super friendly and knows everyone by name. This store should be recognized by the higher ups, for sure. The employees really seemed to love their job, and I think that's what makes it special.

Tuesday afternoon shift is popping every one is attractive   The drinks are always good and service at Starbucks is always the best.   This is a fairly new location so it's nice an clean and a cool place to do homework or just chill at

I've been waiting for more than 30 minutes on line for a coffee. If I didn't have this gift card, I would totally go across the street to Brew Ha Ha. The staff of 2 seems overwhelmed & kind of unhappy--no service with a smile. You would think a multi billion dollar organization like this would anticipate the busy periods & staff appropriately! Also, no recycle bins or containers. Shame on you Marsh Road Starbucks...I expected better.

Probably the worst Java Chip Frappe I've ever had, I am still currently sitting here and trying to sip it down, but like their coffee usually is, it tastes incredibly burnt, and it's over syruped.  But mostly, I can't get over this burnt coffee taste, and as I sit here writing this, the entire coffee shop has the "SOMETHING IS BURNING!" odor, and we are leaving soon. My husband and I ordered a Tall (12 oz. these size names, still are so ridiculous) Java Chip Frappuccino, a Venti (20 oz.) cold brew, an old fashion donut, an apple fritter donut, and a tiny oatmeal.  I've had a few bites and a few sips of my apple fritter & JCF, I am already struck with a nauseas wave & syrupy burnt taste in my mouth and my wallet is about $20 lighter.  I instantly regret coming here instead of Black Crow Coffee in St. Pete, but we needed something in the Tampa area quick.  Alas, we are still in search of a decent coffee place up in the Tampa Bay Area.   Laura served us and she was helpful, no notorious Starbuck 'tude, friendly service on this late Tuesday morning.  We wished we could have just paid after we ordered but she didn't tell us the total & take our card until after everything was made, warmed up, and served, but we were holding a lot of items and even though it's a small detail... we wanted to just pay after we ordered and sit down to wait for our names to be hollared out like normal.    We almost left due to lack of parking, there are barely a few people in here but the lot was completely full.  Still searching for a better place, but it's hard to come by in this area, so we settled for this Starbucks.  Meh, me thinks not for next time.
This is a great Starbucks location just for the loft upstairs. Great people watching opportunities, and in a charming older building.  Minus 1 star because my latte tasted a little burnt.

This place is the happiest place to be at 7:00. Friendly upbeat staff that welcome me by name every day. The coffee of courses great.  The place is clean and organized. All COVID-19 precautions are in place. I love starting my day at this Starbucks. It's hard to be a grumpy morning person after you visit this place.

This Starbucks has a very friendly staff and never mess up orders. Came here my whole time in NOLA and never had an issue. There is no parking Aaron all for this location and that is the only reason I it's getting  4 stars

So I went to the Starbucks today and I asked for a pink drink with zero strawberries Trenta they gave me a Trenta pink drink with extra strawberries in it then I went back to get a free remake they gave me a Venti pink drink with no strawberries Frick you Starbucks never go to this one go to Barclay Rt 70 DRIVE THRU!!!!!!!
I love this starbucks location'! The baristas are happy, friendly and work hard to get orders done efficiently and as fast as possible!

This is the best starbucks I've ever gone to. They go out of their way to be warm and friendly to everyone that comes in. Every order is handled quickly, efficiently, and with care. The store is always very clean, including the station for customers to get cream, sugar, and other supplies. Speaking of which, cream and milk is stored prepared in a fridge in the back ready to go for when one runs out. It is replaced with lightening speed. This is a busy starbucks, as it is off of a main route, but I believe people seek this store out over others because of the wonderful atmosphere. I pass 3 starbucks locations, but I only ever stop at this one.

I like this Starbucks a lot I mean there is actual parking here unlike a lot of other places. Plus Irvington rulesss not just because I work here but Irvington plus coffee equals greatness. The staff are really.good here to. The only problems I have are that it does get overly busy here in the inside so on a cold day there is no sitting. And my coffee does not always come out the best but everyone makes mistakes no big deal

Today I'm enjoying a Tomato Mozzarella Panini, which is my absolute favorite! My drink of choice is a tall Coffee Frappuccino!  I have to say that during this pandemic Statbucks has adjusted and has been my sunshine on cloudy days often!  Simple pleasures in life matter so my pleasure is amazing coffee options What's also nice is the customer service I receive.  As I always say, #SparklingMatters
Horrible service. Felt bad for the front line associate that was poorly trained. Sad...train wreck.

Today I was reminded yet again, why I stopped going to this Starbucks. Call me optimistic, but I thought I'd give it a second chance. The parking lot is extremely small and crowded even though they "added" some extra spots. The only parking that was available today was in the 15 minute parking spots. Some other patrons and I were asked to move our cars from these spots so that others could park there. I don't mind moving my car but there were no other places to go. And the Starbucks employee was somewhat rude about it. PLEASE do yourself a favor. If you're in Jenkintown and you really want a cup of Starbucks coffee, just visit the Barnes & Noble across the street. Save yourself the time and stress.

Just spent 20 minutes waiting in the drive-through and realized after I left that they gave me the wrong order. I called to get a credit, but was advised that this cannot be done without the credit card. I do not work in the area and I can't afford yet another 10 minutes to go back, find a parking spot, go inside, and settle up what is their mistake. Very disappointing and I will not come back to this location if ever I am in this area again.

Don't bother using the app to place your order, its faster staying in the drive-thru line. I've used the app multiple times and I am always waiting for my drinks. The app told me 2 to 6 minutes and I waited 15 minutes for my drinks. For the past three times I've ordered a bagel toasted with cream cheese the first two times they're out of bagels and the last time they had the bagel but no cream cheese or butter. What a waste of time! Completely frustrating and annoying.
A whole month after a storm and they're still opening and closing at ridiculous and inconvenient times. Very poor quality of service even when they are open.

If I'm going to go to a Starbucks (primarily for the lemon pound cake), this is one of my favorite locations in Tucson. Convenient parking, generous seating areas inside and out,  and friendly staff. The lines can get kind of long, but generally my drink (and lemon pound cake) are delivered quickly enough.

I frequent this location. And the Santa Barbara carrillo street location. The carrillo location gets it right everytime .And awesome workers that are happy your there.Im also near The Carpinteria location.Not because the nice workers or clean restrooms. But because of the convenience. Sorry,but this is the only thing going for this Starbucks. No one looks like they like their job and they turn their back when people walk in the door. Carol ,one of the workers ,is always rude and frankly gives out half empty cups of coffee. Also incorrectly done. Always acts as though you are a lot of trouble if you step foot in the store. And if you return the drink she only corrects the coffee half way. It's either cold,sugar syrup dripping down the side,or partially full. She will show you where the napkins are to clean it up yourself though. Some workers are slightly better.Its So sad because of the great location.

DO NOT patron this location as they  HATE the disabled as seen by the actions of this store who banned a customer for LIFE at ALL Tampa locations because  he stood up for the disable because non-disabled people were parking in the handicapped slots.  Boycott and go to Coffee Bean instead.   http://abcnews.go.com/US/starbucks-bans-man-life-asked-patrons-park-disability/story?id=32683177

Love this Starbucks, it's my neighborhood location. The manager Mya and all of the baristas ensure to learn the regulars by name and greet them very friendly. They also ensure every order is prepared properly and will always correct it if something is off.

This place is alright. It is just your standard Starbucks. It is a bit dirty but that is alright. Service is pretty quick and the seating is available enough. It is really nice to be here right now because it is warm while it is absolutely freezing outside. A homeless person just asked me for money while I was sitting here but I suppose that could happen at any Philly coffee shop. Happy to be here!
I don't like the borritos because mcdonalds  has better donuts

When the purchase is courtesy from others (gift card), then why not male your way to Starbucks. I really only purchase flavored iced coffees such as caramel and mocha and vanilla. Some times the iced coffees have been very flavorful and enjoyable to drink. Other times I have gotten iced coffees from here, it did not leave anything to be desired. Hoping for better flavored iced coffees.

"Cookie Monster," or the incredible drive through employee formally known as Ryan, made my Starbucks visit Sunday absolutely spectacular. He was funny, kind, quick and turned an otherwise average visit to Starbucks to above and beyond. I believe it's employees like him that make a difference in someone's day by spreading joy. THANK YOU RYAN - Coooooookies!
Really nice people.  Just wish they could get my order right.  I am a daily to double daily and my order is only right maybe half the time.  And it is the same order every time

Recently renovated.  The pros: more modern looking, there's more fridge space to offer more non-coffee items, the baristas are still the same ones pre-renovation.  There's outdoor seating now.  The cons: power outlets eliminated all except for 1-2, the new furniture seems to be intentionally less comfortable to keep people from hanging out for a long amount of time.   They still make the beverages in a quality way, but sadly, it is no longer the coffee shop that's designed to be a place for casual conversation.

This is the worst Starbucks in this area! The drinks taste nothing like the other Starbucks. The lines a very slow. I know how a triple shot should taste but they charge for triple and only use 1 or two. The drinks taste watered down so we try ristretto but I don't think they know how so that is horrible too. This is by far the worst Starbucks I've ever been to and I have traveled around the world. I've given them many chances. I even called corporate. This store can't be helped and needs new management or should just shut down. If you really want to know how to make coffee go to the Tampa (Gandy) location. They are friendly and know how to make Starbucks coffee. This store is no different than going to a gas station. The only difference is the gas station coffee is cheaper and taste better.

So glad they moved down the street away from the train station where it was a nightmare to park let alone get back onto Lancaster Avenue. I fequent this drive through at least 3 days a week and 99% of the time they are quick, kind and drinks are correct. I try to stay away from any business during peak hours but this is my exception to the rule. 2 thumbs up for easy access and convenience.

I always get the best service here. Today they even remembered my name with no prompting. Thank you Noel!  I have NEVER had this experience at either of the other two Starbucks I frequent on a regular basis. When I have a choice of Starbucks locations this is the one I choose!
Closes at 8 on the weekdays and 8:30 on weekends? Does not make sense. Staff is good otherwise.

Gal at the register didn't know how to reload a card. After two attempts she still caged my bank card for the total and the reload so I couldn't get points. Bummer! Sandwiches were still frozen in the middle and the coffee was the opposite and burned our throats. Worst was that we can eat inside restaurants locally and you can't drink coffee inside a coffee shop. Inconsistencies!

Horrible. Service is super slow. Went in on a Saturday morning and am still waiting on my drink currently after placing it 45 minutes ago. They need to pick it up! It'll be dinner before I get my coffee. If you want coffee for dinner, go to this Starbucks! Lost my service for sure.
Everyone who works in this starbucks is so amazing and friendly. They treat me like family!! Love coming here every morning.

This is a cute little stand alone starbucks (not tucked into a target store like so many)  nice decor staff is friendly....I do not drink starbucks coffee but I like that I can get a hot or iced black sweetened tea.  Found myself chinchillin here with my laptop for awhile one day and it was a nice place to be......

I looked to see if there was a Starbucks on the way to my friend's house, and this one came up as being on-my-route. I pulled into the parking lot and struggled to find a spot (there's a bunch of other stores in the same strip mall - like a Giant). I thought to myself that the parking situation was probably an indicator of how busy they were inside the store, so I put in a mobile order instead (to avoid the line).  By the time I walked out of my car and into the restaurant my order was already completed and ready for pick-up. I was beyond impressed with their speed and will definitely be stopping here every time I go visit my friend!

I practically live at this Starbucks for all of my work meetings. Today really blew me away though. Not only did they have a station set up to write post cards to our veterans in honor of Veteran's Day but Savannah who took my order was just such a pleasure. She was warm, nice and really turned my day around :)

#218  Been here lots of times and they have never got my drink wrong...except for today. It wasn't exactly what I ordered but pretty close and actually was very drinkable. I used to drink Starbucks almost everyday but the cost is now prohibitive. This is a good location no major issues.

I attempted to place an order for a traveler this morning and was treated by the employee who answered the phone like I was a moron for asking to order in advance; someone inconveniencing her with my stupidity. The employee indicated the only thing they could prepare in advance was travelers, and when I confirmed that was my order, she heaved a sigh and demanded to know how many, of what roast, and when I needed to pick it up. When I gave her the information, she scoffed at me for asking for a traveler in 10 minutes rather than 15. I'd have been happy to wait the extra five minutes if at some point in this interaction I'd been treated like a human worthy of breathing the same air as the employee who took the call. The same employee complained to me she was tasked with taking orders and making drinks simultaneously, which is apparently an excuse to chase away potential customers. I ended the transaction at this point, then redialed and asked to speak with a manager, hoping there might be an adult willing to offer some modicum of customer service. Instead, Jaime insisted I must be calling the incorrect location, then curtly asked for the details of my previous interaction, before simply telling me to have a better day and hanging up. 9/10 times I order coffee from local roasters and brewers. Shame on me for opting for the possible convenience of a chain this morning. My lesson is learned: I will continue to seek out businesses desirous of my patronage.

You might want to bus your outside a little more. Our time sitting here has been shared with a couple of kids who not only bought no drinks but also smoked cigarettes non-stop and openly drank booze from a half pint. Classy.

When in Tucson this is my go to Starbucks even though there's one closer to where I stay. This one is clean, has a really friendly team, and a drive through! Can't say that for all of the locations, unfortunately so this one is worth the extra time and couple of miles. I use mobile order and I'm on my way in a jiffy! Serious shout out to the entire team here and an extra high-five to Austin, who is a delight.

Watch this Starbucks when you order.  Three times in the past few months I have ordered a trenta drink and they have given me a venti.  I am sure that helps with their profit margin but not the best customer experience.  The first time I didn't notice.  The second time a friend picked it up for me so it was too late by the time I received it.  Today they handed it to me like I wouldn't notice.  When I pointed it out to the barista, she said they were out of trenta cups so she clearly knew the order wasn't what I had ordered.  I use the order ahead feature in the mobile app so even the label clearly states the the correct size.    As others have pointed out - management and customer service training seem to be in order for this location.

Loved this Starbucks, friendly, quick, efficient employees, and they asked me if it was ok to make a few adjustments to my acai berry tea when they ran out of a few ingredients.  Very very clean bathroom and store location in Philly, def stop in if you need some down time and are on your way to/from the Betsy Ross house.

Well they finally moved across the street to a little bit larger building. This one is a standalone building and it's nice, it's modern, but there still no comfy chairs inside. Same standard drinks and food.   However, I will say before I left, I asked for a glass of water and it wasn't triple filtered which shocked me. I had to throw the water out and I could tell the ice was good. Not sure what happened there. But if you're coming to study and have a coffee, it's new and luxurious. There's a patio; but the area is limited and a bit crowded if your looking for privacy. If you wanna sit at the counter, it's roomy and there are lots of seats. But if you want a nice comfortable chair to sit on and talk to your other guest... Move on down the road as there are no comfy chairs in this place.

I really like this Starbucks. I'm more of a PJs girl but this is where I come for my basic white girl iced chai tea latte with soy. As previous reviewers have said, there's not much indoor seating but plenty of COVERED outdoor seating. What's cool about this is that you can bring your puppy BFF with you! While Starbucks policy is that they do not allow pets, you can bring your dog inside to order before heading outside. This is a must when there isn't a drive thru!  The staff here is friendly and service is quick. Drinks are your standard Starbucks quality. Stop on by, drop a tip in the jar, and treat your doggo to a puppuchino on the patio!
Great quick service! Very friendly!   Ordered a caramel ribbon Frappuccino. So good!   They have a drive thru and plenty of parking.
Great place. They have very clean bathrooms and trust me I've seen quite a lot of bathrooms in my day!

Everytime I go to Starbucks I always get a venti dragon drink with no ice because I have extremely sensitive teeth . I always get my drink filled to the top with no ice at any other location I go to. I went to this location and the employee told me he couldn't fill my drink up all the way because I got no ice. I will never return to this location I paid $5.38 for a half filled drink.

I was staying at Harrah's for an extended weekend and was thrilled to learn that there is a Starbucks in the hotel, in the west tower, on the 2nd floor (near the elevators & hash house a go go)! I ordered coffee for myself and family members every morning we were there. It was always moderately busy, just like any other starbucks, but the baristas were friendly, quick, and made everything ordered spot on.  If you want to get total rewards credits for your purchase provide your TR players card prior to paying (I had zero issues with this). This is a wonderful convenience for people staying and playing in Harrah's or around. This is not a standard starbucks with seating and I wouldn't expect that from this type of property, real estate is a commodity, there are a few tables, but this isn't intended for a hangout spot, grab your coffee and go! Overall a great experience!

I was staying at a BNB DIRECTLY behind this Starbucks so Heyyyyyy easy walk to breakfast. The shop was clean, the service was nice and everyone was smiley for a Saturday late morning.   I ordered my usual: Bacon Gouda (YAY they weren't out!) & a venti very berry refresher. Sandwich was toasty, and the drink was delicious.   I have no complaints!

This place is AMAZING w Starbucks new mobile order option. They always have it ready in about five minutes. I give it 4 stars because As far as a study spot, I don't feel like this is a great place. There are not a lot of seating options and they are very close together. On occasion when trying to study here at had to leave because the music was a little too loud and I can hear it over my headphones.

I'm only a fan because they have the drive thru, but the drive thru was sooo slow!  And parking was limited so believe it or not, the drive thru was the better option.  Anyway, I still like it for its proximity to me and the fact that I don't have to walk in to get what I want.
Great coffee! Great customer service!! I Highly recommend going here for your next cup of coffee!!!

Great location and lots of seating- more than some other Starbucks locations. Very friendly staff and it connected to a bank next door. This allowed for even more seating and a conference table. Would highly recommend this location

I'm always welcomed when I walk in, and have had mostly pleasant experiences. Isaiah is by far the best barista here and why I'm giving 5 stars! I'll even make a point to order when I walk by and see he's working. He goes above and beyond to craft every beverage and is always super friendly. I wish every Starbucks had a barista like that!
Love the new crew here! The managers are so kind a long with the baristas. They always remember what my hubby & I order!

I happily live less than 2 miles from 2 Starbucks but this one is the closest. I am thinking, walk up here on a nice sunny day type of close because it is only about 2.5 blocks away.  The staff: They're always friendly and accomodating. They don't rush you.  You can always expect greetings when you come in and smiles on their faces.  They always get my orders right and they do it in an efficient manner.  The storefront: Clean and well-maintained.  There are several tables and comfortable seating inside. Some outdoor seating as well and a drive-thru.  You can get the usual drinks available at any other Starbucks here as well as the pastry/food options.  You can hang out and enjoy your beverage inside where you will find a few tables and comfortable chairs or take it to go.

This is a high volume store and the crew does a great job but I don't understand why the markup is so high. And going higher, apparently. Guess I will just have to start bringing my own coffee to work until the University gives out another raise. :/
The staff is always friendly, it's easy to get in and out of, and there's never too long of a line.  It's not the fanciest Starbucks you've ever been to, but they do a great job.

I, by no means, am a yelper. This will be my first review and if I'm being completely honest, as I intend to be, it took a lot out of me to even get on here and talk about my experience at this location.  I work in the area, and have to be in for my shift well before any other coffee shops are open. So out of sheer convenience, I frequent this specific Starbucks.  I have to say, as far as positive interactions with the staff go - they are few and far between.  This store, in my opinion, is pretty high volume. I've only ever visited once in a blue moon when there hasn't been a line of people, or a line hasn't accumulated behind me while I'm ordering.  It's rare that my drink is made correctly, even though I get the same drink from the same faces often enough. The consistency is never there.  The staff - awful. Working in customer service and coming from a customer service based employment background for almost 10 years, I am disgusted. The staff here is always miserable and rushes to get you in and out of the store. I understand getting flustered when the place is bumpin', but there's no reason to take it out on the customer.  I went in this morning to grab my coffee, the two men behind the counter seemed like they were having a hard time, one of whom I'm assuming is the manager. The manager in particular is downright terrible. He's always pissy, never greets me, comes over to the register saying "what can I get for you?" takes my order, and then shuffles me out of the way to get to the next person. I wait for my drink, say thank you, and he doesn't even make eye contact with me before he's back over at the register being just as cold to the next guest as he was to me.  As a manager, I would expect more and would think that this person would set a better example for his team. This is the person that is held accountable for the way his staff handles customer interactions, and if they aren't providing quality customer service, he is to blame.  If I could give less of a rating, I would. But there are some diamonds in the rough at this location that are always kind to me no matter how busy they are.  This store could definitely use some retraining in how to provide the best customer service experience to their guests.  If the horrible customer service wasn't paired with the inconsistencies in the drink orders, I would have never opened my trap. But I am so fed up with frequenting this location since I kind of have to, tipping every time I visit, and being met with such distain from about 90% of the employees here, including their manager who shouldn't be dropping the ball so much in front of his team.  There are better Starbucks locations. And I'll probably just go to the one closer to my house on the way to work from now on instead of spending my money here to be treated so poorly. Sucks.

Wow. Quite possibly the worst Starbucks experience. Came in with a couple friends, one of which lives down the road and usually avoids this terrible Starbucks. I asked the cashier if the "salted cream cold foam cold brew" could be made as nondairy. He said of course and gave me my milk options. I went with coconut milk. My friend then said she wanted the same thing but with soy milk. He then asked if she wanted it hot or cold. Idk how cold brew can be hot  he then said he thought she wanted a salted caramel mocha. We clarified what we wanted.   As we waited for our drinks, the barista sneezed in his hands and had a huge snot rocket. Gross. He then walked to the back, wiped his hands and came back out. Def wasn't back there long enough to wash them. He stood around behind the bar and a girl I assume was the shift lead motioned to him to wash his hands. So he rinsed them. Kept sniffling and touching his nose. Not who you want making your drinks, especially when you have a 7 week old, as my friend does. He should not have been working in food service. Point blank. If you are sick, do not work around food.   My friend asked the supervisor if someone else could make our drinks because of what we witnessed. As she was asking this, I see him blending heavy cream for our drinks, which of course, does not fall into the nondairy category. He hands us one of them and we don't even want to touch it. The supervisor sends him on break and comes in to remake our drinks. She asks if our drinks are the "salted caramel mochas", which clearly homeboy rang in wrong so we let her know what we actually want. She then says it's made with heavy cream so I point out that the cashier told me it could be made nondairy. He says "oh I thought you wanted salted caramel mochas". I asked if I could get something else since i don't want dairy and they say they'll try to make it nondairy. Which btw, totally possible but might not be as foamy.   After being in here 15-20 minutes for 3 drinks, to say i was frustrated was an understatement. There was no apology, no service recovery, nothing. Definitely need to retrain this staff, never seen such an incompetent bunch. The shift lead seemed to be the only one who knew what she was doing, understood food allergies, and cared about her job/customers.

Bf ordered a coffee frap and got an iced coffee instead---? It's quite a piss off when you go out of your way, and can't go back to complain because you're in a rush. So drove for nothing to get the wrong drink. Great.

I have probably visited 4 or 5 Starbucks' in the city, and this one is one of the better ones.  The staff has been extremely friendly and helpful every time I visit, and there is a nice upstairs seating area to relax and browse the internet while drinking your whatever.  On a side note, I picked up a box of their new instant iced coffee, and I think it is really good.  One box is probably enough for 10 or more small sized ice coffees, so the price isn't too bad either.

The staffs were very friendly. That's why I am giving them 2 stars rather than 1. I ordered grande caramel mocchiato and paid $5.45 for it. ($1.00 more than a regular Starbucks)The barista did not make my drink right so I had to add an extra shot. Usually, Starbucks offers for free to make it right but I have to pay 92 cents. So my coffee is almost $7.00. I have Starbucks gift card but cannot use it here. They were too pricey and didn't make a go I like that they are inside the hotel. Anyway, I am not going back to this place.

This is my favorite Starbucks in the entire city. Everyone is friendly and they offer outside seating WITH outlets and it is covered. I usually sit outside. There's also the cutest neighborhood black cat that would walk past. Everytime I went , there was a good amount of people there but it was not loud or hectic unlike some other Starbucks I have went to.

I used to come here all the time,love this location .The people are just so friendly and really fast with my order. Also,great interior space,always clean and great coffee. The best Starbucks in Santa Barbara.
The Brownsburg Starbucks is my favorite! Not only is the coffee and the food great, but the service is AMAZING! I always feel like I am being greeted by friends! Such a friendly crew!

I am a Starbucks fan, even though I usually purchase traditional coffee. There's many different locales better than this one. I went through the Drive-thru this AM, no picture on the screen which was odd. Requested a bagel, all out, fair enough, it happens, I took it as a sign to cut some bread out of my day. Apparently the barista or cashier was a mute! Not one word during the whole transaction, not even a "here you go", "ok" or anything. Just handed her my money, she handed me my drink, I was the only one in line. I said Thank You, to her and she looked like she wanted to tear me a new one. There's a much better one in Exton, super people, and the one in Edgemont makes me feel like part of the family. Not this place, is it the manager? The coffee was tasteless, and lousy, I made myself a K-cup later and reused the cup, had to be good for something. Definitely not the Starbucks norm. Devon, better, Edgemont, fantastic, Exton, supernice. Newtown Square and Broomall, very warm. Paoli...go somewhere else if you want a good start to your day.

Order a coffee and a bagel.  Coffee came out right away.  The bagel and food items not so much as fellow coffee drinkers were waiting around too. It's not a bad location.  I was annoyed to see another gal stocking shelves instead of helping her fellow worker at the counter who was in charge of taking orders and prepping the food. Oh she came, but only after she was done.  Yeah no, not in retail.  Get the crowds out.  Meh, Starbucks just isn't the same anymore.

I have been here six times now and all six times something is wrong. The top two issues are incredibly slow service and inconsistency with the way their drinks are made. I mean I've been to Starbucks all across the country. Big City Starbucks and small city Starbucks and this is the slowest one by far. I waited 27 minutes for an iced coffee! Safe to say...I will not be back.

Worst staff and horrible coffee.. they don't even have light fraps anymore- way to contribute to America obesity epidemic. Watch their stocks plummet - who wants to pay 8 bucks for a glass of fat and sugar - well maybe some but you lost me when u took away light versions.

Usually, this is my go-to Starbucks. They do always take a little bit to get your order ready, but USUALLY polite friendly people... Unlike most of the staff, there's ONE lady that's just PLAIN RUDE.. I stopped by at 8:07PM, drive thru.. (checked online and it said they were still open).. pulled up to the drive thru, "hello?" Nothing. "Hey are you guys open?" Nothing. "Helloooo?" NOTHING! Waited like a good 5min and no answer at all. I drive up to the window, (keep in mind they don't even open the window to answer my question), and ask the lady are you guys open? She literally looks at me never opens the window or comes over period. Just stood there and said, "Does it look like we're open??!" Making rude face expressions, never coming over, never opening the window, AND she kept acting like she couldn't hear me after that. IGNORING me... Plain nasty and RUDE.

This is one of my favorite locations in Tampa because of the convenience of having the Panera and FitLife Foods only a few steps away. The baristas here are very nice and already know me by name. Some I've noticrd have been there a few years already. One I know always has a cheerful attitude when I interact with. That's always a good sign for consistency and knowing that they must be happy there. I love how they are well stocked with a variety of sandwiches (sometimes depending on location they cam be hard to find). My order is also always correct when I come here (even though it helps that as a looooong time customer of Starbucks I know how to order). I love to come here for their delicious sandwiches for lunch and a drink on my way to work since its fast and convenient. Starbucks as a company has actually improved some with their food (its better than most fast food I should say).

I normally don't review large chains since people generally know what to expect - but this location seems super busy and it's worth warning people about the long delays - recently waited almost 30 mins from the time of my receipt to receive a plain old latte - which is 25 minutes too long.  Same Starbucks you'll find most of the time but if you're in a hurry you should probably keep going down the road.

I really gave this place a few shots before posting this review because Starbucks are busy, I get it. But I have had a few instances in the past week that I've never seen at another Starbucks in all of my years of buying their coffee.  Earlier this week, I put in a mobile order and arrived about 10 minutes later when the estimated time was 4-7 minutes. I got there and there was one man in line, and two girls behind the counter. The one was working on someone else's drink (maybe another mobile order I assumed). Turned out, it was the man who had only just ordered. I always understood mobile orders to be your place in line, but I didn't say anything. I waited about 8 minutes for my whole order (I got multiple drinks but the longest thing I waited on was food because one of the girls behind the counter was stocking lids very slowly instead of making anything). The next day, I put my mobile in earlier expecting the wait to be longer as it was a Monday. Put it through about 15 minutes early, still the whole order was not ready when I arrived! Took at least another 10 minutes for them to realize that they never finished one drink.  Then this morning, I put in my mobile order in early. It was a Saturday at like 630 am, so in my mind, it would be more than enough time. I arrived and stood around because the drink wasn't ready yet. Found it odd because as I was standing there the workers didn't say anything. Finally, I asked if my drink was waiting behind the counter or something. This is when someone finally informed me that the espresso machine had not been cleaned, so she couldn't actually make my drink as the machine wasn't working. I was trying to catch a train, had stood there for several minutes before I even said anything, and at the very least the employee could've reimbursed me on a gift card and left that out to let me know something wasn't right. I will not go back to this location. In all honesty, it's never busy, which I have to assume is because so many others before me have come to the same conclusion.

It's always amazing when I come here. Occasionally the baristas don't seem like they're taking their time so sometimes the drink doesn't taste right. I always get an iced caramel macchiato with 2 pumps of hazelnut. It's so yummy and they make it taste so sweet!! Drive through is quick and the workers are very nice and efficient. I recommend!

The new Iced Teas that they have here are deeeeeelish! Here I am sipping on a Venti Mango Black Tea Lemonade as I type this. The price is rather reasonable, and the tea is not too sweet. I am also a fan of the pretzel. Must get it warmed up for the added bonus. I have never been a fan of the pumpkin spice craze, but thumbs up to all that support it! Within walking distance, it's nice to have variety.
Extremely slow service along with not enough inventory to create the drinks you want.
Hey. Coming from the Virginia area we needed a Starbucks fix.  None for miles it seems until we found this one on the way to the Betsy Ross house. Phew.  Needed a double shot of espresso. .

This location has shorted me twice now when I ordered multiple espresso shots. On 2 separate occasions within the same month I ordered 4 espresso shots as a solo order. When I got home and measured it out, I was only given 2 shots.

They ran out of Sugar in the Raw...they're located across from an Acme. Why not go buy some? Would you tell people who use other types of sweetener alternatives (Stevia, Equal, etc) "sorry, we're out"???

I frequent this Starbucks to study. It is a larger Starbucks than most in Tampa area (I've been to 6+ recently to study); it has over 6 'reading' chairs (no table), 6+ two-seater tables with one side of booth seating and your standard long table with 8-9 seats. There is outdoor seating as well.  The staff is friendly and competent. I've had a few experiences at other Starbucks locations where, in a slow period, they're joking / messing around to the extent that it's disruptive to people trying to read or study. I've been to this location over a dozen times total and I've never had that experience here.  Five star location for sure, I'd highly recommend coming in to either grab a quick drink or stay a while.
This Starbucks location is very slow compared to all other locations I've been to. They need to have a little more urgency because their drive thru lines get insanely backed up.

Best inside in the area because it's roomy and has plenty of seating. I've gone there a few times to do work. However, even though the staff is very friendly, they are incredibly loud amongst themselves. I know it's not a library but man, they're super chatty and because the music is loud they talk very loudly. That's the only draw back IMO.
Excellent and friendly staff, fun morning atmosphere, great coffee! Always clean. Very accessible location, spacious seating, and not overcrowded.

Have had issues at this location before accusing me of stealing so I'm always weary about coming back. Thank you Vaughn for helping me today. You were awesome! I hope you're working  next time I'm in so I can have another great experience.

I have been to this Starbucks everytime I come into town and I have to say, it is one of the most cleanest and nicely ran establishments I ever visit. I stay in harrahs frequently on business and always get my morning cup of coffee here . The people are absolutely wonderful and their lobby is always clean clean clean! I also appreciate that they have a wide selection of drinks and pastries for everyone to choose from, especially at odd ends of the day! Overall I am always pleased and highly recommend this place is anyone is ever in harrahs and is looking for their morning or mid afternoon coffee and pastry!

Did a mobile order. Got there a few minutes after they were supposed to be ready. The drinks were, but we waited 12 minutes for our breakfast sandwiches before checking on them. The worker was rude and said "it will be over there when it's ready'. We waited 5 more minutes, then had to cancel because we had to leave. So...15-20 minutes after we got our drinks I told the girl to cancel. She actually looked up the order and said she "didn't get it". Umm...it was a mobile order and our drinks were there. Had to leave, no apology from staff, no breakfast. Paid for two sandwiches but didn't get them. Do not go here!!! I have never had a bad experience like this at Starbucks!
Great service during Covid! Drive though was super fast and easy. I ordered like normal, at the first stop. Then I paid and got my drink at the second stop. Girl was friendly.
Malika from this Starbucks is the best - always smiling, makes everything right, and knows how to cheer you up every single day.

I have been consistently coming to this Starbucks for about a year. During this year that I have been coming, there have been continuous moments of frustration where I have had an order that was wrong, incomplete, or in some way or form messed up. I love the food, the bacon egg bites hit the spot, and the drinks are lovely, however that's only when I get them, and get them whole. When I order I at times would get a drink and be given a completely different one, buy egg bites and get biscuits, or just not receive part of my order at all.  Today, as the psychopath I am, ordered a hot chocolate. The hot chocolate I received looked almost as if it had been drunk out of or in part dumped out. Nah. No.

I left my card one day , not even & one of the coworkers has the nerve to take it & not only buy food there using my card but seriously takes it to dunkin donuts wow like wow like I wouldn't notice . I had to leave a comment & I will be coming back in the morning I swear on god they will hear my mouth The one on 19 & embassy . Trash  so trash can't wait to go in tomorrow I'm not done

I want to brag on this location! They are always so kind and make or remake your order with caring hands! Joshua made my Christmas with his wonderful customer service and help! All the guys that were working understand their customers and make us feel welcome! I love it here!!!
i would've given u a zero but since i can't.... i'll give u a 1. waited for 25 mins in the drive thru. bullshit

Currently, this is my favorite coffee shop in the Mt Juliet/ Nashville area. I have had great customer service here and the best coffee/ drinks here.  A manager talked me into downloading the Starbucks App on my phone from another Starbucks location nearby. But, I use it most of the time for this Starbucks.  I recommend the Starbucks App. It saves time and you save money by getting some drinks for free.  Once you have the App, you can put money on it. Then, you can open the App, choose a location, order a drink and pay for it. Then, you go to that location's drive thru and tell them your name and that you have a mobile order and they give you your drink at window. It's that simple and it's that easy.  Experiences:  I have become a regular here so much that they know me by name. In fact, they even know me by my truck pulling up and before I even say one word, they say " Good morning Steve, we have your drink ready, you can pull on up". It is the simple truth: Now, that is customer service.
Consistently outstanding service and brief wait times, even during rush hour. Great staff, polite and friendly. I highly recommend this store!

Great service wonderful customer service I normally Get Hot chocolate and Banana Nut Bread Today I  Had the Double Chocolate Chip Cookie..warmed up of course. .it was mouth watering and delious.I want More..

I have never been to a starbucks where we cant use the restroom .. went in there at 7.45 with my children to use the restroom. . Wouldnt let us use it because we did not make a purchase. .. really?..so if I give you a penny will you let me use it. .. we are obviously not asking for handouts..and have been dumping money into your overpriced coffees... for years!! And you wont let me use the restroom????

The refreshers.... So so good and feels lite. I can break a tooth chomping on the ice to get to the dehydrated strawberries floating around in the drink. I also shake the empty cup viciously until every drop is gone. But I don't care. The drinks flavors are on point and it's a nice change of pace from a warm coffee.  Plus they'll always  give pup cups. Starbucks is wonderful.

Not sure why NAOMI is so sad, but this is a great location. The staff here is always friendly and consistent with their beverages. I love randomly stopping in here day or night and now that they will get it right without screwing up. The inside is always clean and the restrooms stay pretty clean too. This is my favorite Starbucks around.

These people have horrible service here they're very disrespectful of lot of profanity being used behind the counter top I thought that this was a family environment I was wrong every time I come here this like a new surprise now pissed off because I stood in line for like 15 minutes close to the time of closing when I get up to the countertop they give me nothing but attitude even the manager I express my feelings to them the manager threatened to call the cops I thought that that was upset I wasn't shouting I wasn't yelling I was just upset because I was standing in line that long before the time of cleansing and instead of dealing with me like an adult she throws a hissy fit and threatens me with the cops I am a highly decorated Marine who served his country and this is the way you treat me that's downright despicable this woman had purple hair she was they told me she was the manager how in the world does these people get these positions and they have such bad attitudes and bad customer service skills with people this is downright horrible please someone to step in and take charge of this matter before it spirals out of control this is my third time coming here and experience has something weird behind the counter top the customer service is its it's not what it used to be someone needs to step up and take charge thank you

Slowest Starbucks ever, though in fairness I was there about the time many folks who work in the CBD were coming in to pick up their mobile orders. More than a dozen patrons were waiting along with me,  and I had been there close to 15 minutes when one of the staff called out my name to say they were out of butter croissants, so would I like a chocolate croissant. No thank you. Also no orange juice. I always prefer a local coffee house anyway, but there was not one very close to our hotel.

While this is the closet Starbucks to me I can't actually visit them regularly because of the lack of consistency of the drinks and the staff. There are def some employees that are friendly and smile and the others just barely acknowledge your existence. The frappes are also sometimes good and sometimes completely watered down depending on the barista. I shouldn't have to dread seeing certain baristas and hope they make things properly.

Hands down one of the best Iced Carmel Macchiato I've gotten from Starbucks in a long time.  I normally go to a different location, but since that ones always so busy I went to the location on 1st and Wetmore. Safe to say I'll leave for work a few minutes earlier so I can go to this location from now on

With an entrance that shares the lobby of the Hilton Hotel, this is still a completely proper Starbucks with all the usual accoutrements including an independent wifi signal and Starbucks coffees and snacks.  Plenty of seating with tables, a sofa, comfy chairs arranged to face each other and lots of power outlets along the wall.  I just had a single espresso in order to justify the taking up of a table and sitting here for an hour while waiting for the library to open. But it's still a nice Starbucks!
We only have one kind of coffee right now and we're not gonna brew anything different, but there's another Starbucks 2 miles away.
Great service and as always a great product.  Its a busy location so don't be in a rush.

This Starbucks on big bend is honestly the worst one I have ever been to. The service is unbearably slow and I have never been to a Starbucks that basically made a hot sweet milk drink instead of a creme brûlée latte with two extra shots of espresso. Disgusting.

So we came in for a slice of chocolate loaf and a latte .... instead of sitting down to a delicious piece of chocolate-loaf...we get a hard stale piece of cake .... as I return to the counter I see the display piece of chocolate cake is missing .. and I ask the employee "did you take this out of the display case... and she replies " yes but I touched it to make sure it was ok.... of course I flip out .... the sign clearly says inside the case that those things are for display purposes only.   Those things can be in there for days at a time.   And she says to me  this one was not that old .... I respond well those sandwiches in there look like they have been in there for weeks so I'm sure they are bacteria ridden and could certainly cross contaminate ... I had my $3.16  returned but how absolutely GROSS  And then,  I see the other young lady eating in the coffee station ... literally hand to mouth... and then without washing her hands she starts making someone's coffee order.     :(

Grab and go was easy peasy. We popped in after finding a location that was nearby to the hotel we had stayed in and this one popped up. We were greeted right away and our orders were up very quickly. Drinks were made as ordered and staff was nice.     This one does not have a drive thru and the inside seating was closed, but they did have a limited amount of outside (uncovered) seating if you really wanted to hang out.    Overall: A  Easy in and out for a grab and go coffee, but no drive thru and lack of covered seating.
Invested with flies!! Beware if coming inside. Almost until workable conditions if you're looking to get some work done

Slowest Starbucks in Tucson. I ask beforehand in the drive thru if I can use two 25 star rewards on one drink (soy and extra shot) and she says no problem. I get to the window to pay and the girl is like "uh twi rewards? You only have one drink don't you? And says that it won't let her use 50 stars. Drink was fine but not worth the wait and price. Not sure if there is an extra shot even in here as it doesn't say on the cup... 1 star for bad customer service and poor speed of service.

This is a small Starbucks, but the seating is nice. If it isn't busy, it's a great place to park yourself for a while. I've only come once, during a less busy hour, so what I experienced was quite nice, with good service and clean facilities. It also has great lighting for both reading, a coffee date, or just meeting up with friends. I am looking forward to coming back here.

The staff is very attentive and friendly, however I am not a big fan of this location for a few reasons. For starters, I don't feel safe leaving my stuff for even a minute by itself. To add onto this, a gentleman came in and asked me if I had seen his stuff because he left bag and belongings (laptop etc.) for a few moments and when he came back it was stolen. Not a good sign! In addition, the people that come in are very roudy and unprofessional (screaming and cursing). Also they are always blaring music so it's impossible to get any work done here. A shame because I used to love doing work at the Starbucks on main but at this location it's almost impossible- Definitely don't think I'll be back to stay other than a quick mobile order pick up.

Fast service with polite staff. My order was correct especially because Extra carmel is the key and they did just that. This location sits nestled between a business building and cvs. There is not lots of room to drive with the surroundings businesses but you still are able to maneuver just be mindful of the traffic.

This starbucks is always super busy, day and night it seems.  The beverages here are hit or miss, depending on your barista.  The atmosphere is nice, they also have an outdoor fire place.  Parking is a nightmare though.   Today, I got a classic latte, half of the latte was foam, um I didn't ask for a cappuccino?  Lets hope for better next time...

I asked for non-caffeinated drink the barista was kind of snarky and looked over at the other Brista and they shared a small laugh and told me that all the drinks are caffeinated and then when I said that they were able to make me a drink yesterday that was non-caffeinated she said well no because everything is caffeinated and I showed her the photo of my drink on the phone and she said that's dragon fruit and it's caffeinated long story short she didn't want to help me at all she didn't want to take her time she didn't want to do anything for me so I ended up with the stupid strawberry lemonade, thanks. I really really do not like it when people treat me stupid I believe in being super super nice and I like it when my kindness is given back I do not like being made to feel stupid.

This has to be the worst coffee experience I've ever had. The employees are rude, and the coffee is downright disgusting. I have no idea how one can make a coffee so gross.
