I ordered a simple dark roast. It had a thick sludgy consistency. It was extremely bitter and sour, nothing like coffee. I rarely dump drinks, but this cup tasted so rancid that I had no other choice. I definitely would not recommend this business.

A lot of times I feel that we try to review one time places that we visit and there are places we visit regularly that don't get love.  I figured I'd give some love back to this Starbucks as I've been coming here on the regular since I moved to Buchanan Arts District.  I almost always order via the mobile and I can't say that I have ever had a problem with the speed it comes out.  I have less than a 5 minute drive and it's been rare that I've encountered having to wait for either a food or beverage item.  Throughout the recent snowstorms, they were on the money and I totally appreciated the store and staff and being able to make it in despite the conditions outside.
Love Starbucks!!! Wished their prices were lower, but other than that...I love it. The one on Magazine St/New Orleans is excellent!

By far one of the more people friendly and welcoming Starbucks I've been to. I'm not a consecutive customer but when I have some extra change or I can't stand another day of trying to choke down office kitchen coffee I'll make a stop in. What really stands out about this place other than the delicious coffee is the gentleman who manages the establishment unfortunately I haven't caught his name but I appreciate the fact he remembers how to spell my name (it's a bit off from how it's pronounced) especially since I'm not an every day customer. It's those little insignificant personal touches that go a long way and are truly appreciated.
Still a VERY slow Starbucks.  I have found that this location takes forever whether you go inside or through the drive through.

Not my favorite Starbucks. The service is ok and the food is the same. It's pretty quick service, but my sandwich wasn't very warm. It normally takes a while to make my drinks. This is a decent Starbucks for an airport.   The next time I'll probably just go to Chic-Fil-A. It was right across the food court.

Starbucks in my home town! The future is now!   Like Yelper Steve P, I too was very skeptical when I saw the Starbucks sign outside of the building that once was a mom and pop pancake house way back in the day. But I was pleasantly surprised by the large, inviting space when I walked through the doors. This would be a great spot to get some work done or have a casual meeting.   I ordered an Americano and asked if they had the egg white breakfast sandwich, since I didn't see it on display. The employee at the register looked through the refrigerator but, unfortunately, they didn't have my go-to. I opted instead for the bacon, gouda and egg sandwich which was tasty if not as light as the egg white option. My Americano was HOT - I couldn't drink it for a good twenty minutes.   Overall, glad to see this new location so close to home. No more driving to Delran for my Starbucks fix!

I expected poor service due to the reviews and it was quite the opposite. Pleasant, efficient, patient staff. I can count on one hand the number of times I've been to Starbucks so I don't know the coffee "lingo." The cashier was really patient with me as I changed my order and helped me order my coffee just the way I like it. I will absolutely return to this location in the future.

At this location you have to wait and ask for your mobile order. There's no specific employee that attends to the mobile orders so it takes forever. I would recommend that you have a specific employee to cater to mobile order pickups or allow customer to grab their own orders like other locations.

Leaving Nashville and heading north, we wanted to grab a coffee before we went too far and had to wait until we hit Bowling Green, KY. While a turn or two away from the highway, this Starbucks was easy to get to and get back on the highway from.   It's got a nice private parking lot but it shares a structure with a minute clinic which was a first for me.   The staff were nice and answered my questions about some new items easily. The menu and set up was pretty standard for Starbucks - minus the fact that the pick-up counter is all the way at the back by the bathrooms. You wait way at the back of the shop and then have to wade  through all the shop and the checkout queue which is kind of odd and could be really challenging for a wheelchair user, customers with kids, or someone trying to grab a seat, work, and grab their coffee when it's ready.   .

The drinks are always made correctly, even when substitutions are made. the staff is generally friendly, with the exception of a few discontent  personalities. I'm in here quite frequently, and have to say that the biggest downfall for this place is that the staff doesn't really seem to care that the line is out the door. The wait is generally obnoxious  and sometimes not even an option for me. I've been into MANY Starbucks over the years that are just as busy, if not much busier, and have not experienced such a wait...EVER. I don't know if perhaps the baristas get overwhelmed, or of the store is significantly understaffed, but this wait time is unreasonable. Many days I've taken the longer walk to the other end of the mall to get Dunkin Donuts, because the 15 min round trip walk is still faster than waiting for sometimes 30 mins or more.

Wow. Have never been treated more rude trying to buy a drink than I was in my last visit. The associate working at the register went beyond rude to being aggressively hostile. Not only did she lie about a drink no longer being available, she proceeded to berate another customer about an order she entered wrong.   She embarrassed him accusing him of not ordering what he wanted to the point he walked away with the wrong drink.  I understand it's difficult to work in retail these days, but no need to verbally abuse customers.

I have now ordered three different food items off of the mobile app. Part of using the mobile app is for convenience especially when I need to get to work. This is now the third time I have showed up and they do not have the food item I am selecting. How can you run out of bacon at eight in the morning. Absolutely ridiculous. The staff there is very nice and caring but they won't do a return. Very disappointed eps the prices I pay.
Drinks were good as expected from a Starbucks but our grande drinks were under filled substantially.. felt like we paid for a grande and got a tall

Hate to say it but all Starbucks are pretty much the same. This one gets an extra star because it's clean, the bathrooms are stocked and the baristas aren't rude. They aren't overly friendly, but I don't expect too much! They get the drinks right, they seem to be well trained and the app makes the purchase process go smoothly!   Be careful backing out of the parking spaces in the front lot!! There are bollards at the end of the spaces and I've seen quite a few people hit them backing out!

As cozy good as caffeine gets on this casino floor!  This Starbucks is tucked away in a corner and could get pretty busy. The young people who are working there are very personable and do a commendable job of keeping up with the long lines when they form. If you are staying at Harrah's or visiting and need caffeine, this is where you want to go for good coffee as it arguably is the best option you might have.

Went in this morning to get my coffee that was almost $10 (which I'm aware Of) handed the cashier a $50 as she handed it back and immediately said "I can't break that". Without even trying to see if a manager could check the office or help in any way. So I leave to try to go to the bank next door but that was closed because it is a holiday. So I go back to Starbucks to attempt to see if someone else can help me and end up getting the manager. Who WAS able to help me. I've worked in hospitality for the last 15 years now and there really seems to be no such thing as customer service anymore. If I'm paying almost $10 for a damn coffee why couldn't you take two extra seconds out of your hourly paid time to help me.
Nice and has large open space , plenty of seating at bar , inside and out.  Staff friendly and very knowledgable.
Very efficient location. Touron, who I'm guessing is the manager, was very friendly and so was the rest of the team. We had specific requests and they took them with ease.

Everything about this is standard in regards to quality of the product. The staff could be more pleasant but it really variates depending on the shift. All of that aside, the remodel stole the old comfort this Starbucks always had in its atmosphere. It has an uncomfortable feeling now and they removed a lot of seating so it's prone to getting more crowded and it won't be a great place to study or relax like before. But if you're just stopping by, it'll suffice.

If you want something from this Starbucks you need to go through the drive-through. The wait times are extremely long. You go inside hoping to get your drink and as you watch them serve every car in the drive-through before they get to your order it is frustrating. I've given this place about six tries now but it has consistent customer service. I think I'll take a pass.

Starbucks are a dime a dozen in this city and they're all basically the same.  Yeah, the coffee isn't the most AMAZING coffee in the world.  Yeah, the food is all pre-packaged.    What sets this one apart from all the others is the baristas. Since I work in the Empire building, I visit this location once a day (sometimes twice, sometimes three times...please stop judging me...)   All the people behind the counter know my name and know what I drink at what time of day.   I appreciate the fact that even though I'm one of hundreds in any given day, they still remember who I am.

So i usually never write reviews. But i absolutely love this Starbucks. Every morning i am greeted by Bob, who is the sweetest man ever. He is always happy and makes my morning! Line moves quickly and my order is always right!
Service was great as always . My fav ginger (Josh) wasn't there though.   Nothing like a True North first thing in the morning

I love coming here..but i had to say this everytime i come in there's the same barista offly rude! (The one with glasses) yes!!! I order a panini she asks if i want it warmed up i say  "Yes please" she opens the wrap and just leaves it next to the oven they have.. i sit and wait and see what she does shes wondering around goes to the back to do i don't know what..after ten minutes! I go up to the barista and ask her for my panini.. what happens!? She says  "Oh i forgot" i don't know why everytime i go in there she always does the same thing! Shes very rude!  Not a bit of good customer service!  I wasn't going to give two stars but if no one says nothing she's going to keep being rude to customers!!

Compared with other Starbucks locations, this one seemed fairly new. There were several comfy-looking chairs and the entire place was spotless.   The staff seemed a bit amateur. I asked about the ingredients in a pumpkin latte and got a bewildered look from a staffer, like I had just asked a question about quantum physics. After I explained ("You know, is there sweetened syrup? Just spices? What exactly gives the latte it's pumpkin flavor?"), she relaxed and ran through the ingredients list, which seemed too sugary for what I wanted (and there's no sugar-free version of the pumpkin latte). I went with a regular latte and the boyfriend ordered a mocha.   There were several staffers but the process wasn't as seamless as other locations. Still, this is one of the few coffee shops in the area that I could find on my Yelp app, so it did the trick.
The young people that work the drive through are delightful and I get good coffee and fine service and a friendly smile!
Staff was warm and welcoming.   They were out of cinnamon dolce so went with a vanilla late.  Same as any other Starbucks

This place is hit or miss. The lines can get extremely long and people belly up to the bar like they are going to miss their grande half caf soy gingerbread latte. I just had to have them make my drink twice because it was flavorless lukewarm water.   If the line is long I wouldn't even bother because you will wait forever for a drink they will probably make wrong.

If a Starbucks has a drive thru avoid it like the plague. It means that it is really busy they care less and it's just not a great experience.  This particular location was once a great spot to have a conversation with the Barista when it was at the old spot. Now it's just person in coffee out.  I also think the quality has gone down given the higher throughput. Try the Havertown or Newtown Square locations they are much better.

Starbucks is my favorite coffee place and this location is always popping! I usually attempt to mobile order just because the location is crammed into a small shopping plaza and there isn't a whole lot of room for drive thru traffic but it works! When I've mobile ordered it's always been ready quickly, when I go through the drive thru it's usually packed but moves fairly quickly and the employees take multi-tasking to a whole new level! I don't know how Starbucks finds such amazing employees for each location, and I mean that I have literally never went to a Starbucks with bad employees, but they knock it out of the park! My drinks are always made great and if I order good it's always made to perfection! Don't let the traffic scare you off! I promise you won't wait long!

10/28/21- Thursday in the night,  to who may concern:   I been coming to this Starbucks several time give them the opportunity to get better service but no result, the service on the following areas need to be corrected on the costumer service at the cashier is very important eye contact when your are talking to the clients (for example: I ask the Cashier (male) why I cannot have eye contact during my transactions he answer me very rough "I don't like it"  His hat was on his whole face. Also the order aren't been give Correctly to the client.   I hope this issues can be corrected for the best of the company.

I had an issue at this store this evening...poorly trained baristas. But my main issue is, If the service at this location is so good, why does store manager Devan Bratt feel the need to go online posing as a customer and posting bogus reviews? See this link:  http://stores-goods.com/Tennessee/Mt_Juliet/Starbucks_Providence_Commons

They are constantly out of product. The staff is slow and don't seem to understand that running out of the same thing every day by 9 am means that you should make more of it. They can't complete simple tasks like putting mobile order labels on the correct order - then acting incensed when questioned about whose food is whose.

probably the most unfriendliest starbucks staff ever. when I ordered a soy latte the first day I wondered about the extra charge (starbucks wont charge you extra for soy in europe where I come from). the answer was "because it is...". next day i steped into the store just at 5 PM asking for a coffee. the denied because they closed 10 seconds ago. horrible staff!!!

I'm not really a Starbucks fan but sometimes, it works. The employees at this location were all super friendly and engaging. You know when you have a bunch of questions about something and you can tell when the person behind the counter is losing patience? Not this crew. They were happy to answer the surprising array of questions I had about their iced teas.   Speaking of which, I ordered some type of berry concoction that was bright pink and fruity but very, very tart. Walk the extra block to Cafe Ole for their amazing raspberry iced tea if that's what you're jonesin' for (sorry, Starbucks). The only reason I didn't do that was because I had already been there just an hour earlier and didn't want to be that weirdo going back twice in an hour.

Best s'bucks on town!!!  Iced au lait to fight the heat.  Often has free pastries in the afternoon. Great crowd od respectful regulars.  New glasses for in house cold drinks. Me likie!  French press. Komodo Dragon blend is not so great for me...citrusy.  The French press coffee is phenomenal and not expensive!  Lots of parking, fun atmosphere, reasonably quiet. My current favorite s'bucks in Nola!
If your moving like I am right now then there's no place on earth to chill with the free WiFi then this Starbucks.   It's big, clean, nice, and awesome. Did I mention the free WiFi.

Conveniently located if you're walking around Haddonfield (if you're driving, good luck finding a spot). Staff is friendly and the store is clean and contemporary, sitting room with fireplace and stools by the windows to people watch. It seems like the school kids are always here at 3, but otherwise the line is short.

Closed at least 30 minutes before their scheduled time (10pm) with no note on the door or drive thru. People inside were just cleaning and taking things to a mobile storage unit in the back, giving each other "rides" on the dollies. Would have been nice to know they were closing early and have staff act more professional. I had a long drive I've ahead of me and needed a pick-me-up for the road...
Like the only Starbucks in the world that employees are maskless. Did mobile order gave me the wrong order. Employees are not engaging or friendly. Told me to get my own drink....ok
I'm a regular Starbucks drinker but this location disappointed. I don't know if they were having an off morning but the coffee tasted like nothing.
WHERE IS GRANT IN THE MORNINGS!!!! My friend and I LOVE HIM and he's never there when we go anymore:(:(:(:(:( Grant if you're reading this switch your schedules back to the mornings PLEASE

I have no problem for tipping for good service. However the big guy with the beard and knit hat always shakes or moves the tip cup once I am done paying. Now, I never tip at this starbucks simply out of spite. I mean come on bud, You are pulling a leaver on the coffee urn  filling up a cup and charging me 4 bucks, get the hell out of here with your tip cup.
Its a regular Starbucks, Nothin fancy. Right inside the casino next to the slot machines. Was never a fan of starbucks. I was here just because I couldn't find a decent coffee shop around.

It can be a hit or miss depending on the baristas. I always get my alternative plant based milk steamed because it makes it creamy and reminds me of regular milk. Some just feel like it's extra and won't do it at all. Never seen lazy fake nice service at any other Starbucks I've been to.  One time I feel like I was given regular milk because after drinking my stomach was upset. It is just convenient won't be going there anymore since I graduate in May!

Heading to my daughters softball game i wanted a cooling delicious frap!! So stopping in.. i seen one of my old co-workers who I used to work at Starbucks with. Years later!! He still works at Starbucks! So we were just chit chatting and catching up! It was nice and clean.  Everyone was pleasant and friendly and as always I will definitely return back Thank you Starbucks

No line in drive through and super sweet employees.  After my son's 2 1/2 year old wellness check, I told my friend we'd stop at Starbucks on our way to see her. This was on our way home, so it was a quick stop. Usually I go to the Starbucks on Racetrack, because it is a tad bit closer, but I'm starting to question my decision. That one always seems to have a line of cars (but it does move super quickly). At 10:40 a.m. on a Thursday, there were no cars at the drive through! The entire time I was there I didn't see another car. That's bananas to me. I'll have to come back and see if this holds true.   I was thrown for a loop when I tried to order a pumpkin cream cold brew and a chocolate chip cookie. They didn't have either. The pumpkin cream cold brew is seasonal, which makes me sad. The employee was seriously one of the most helpful people I've ever met. She kept asking me about my taste preferences and offering suggestions. With other drive throughs, the employee probably would have just given me time to look over the menu and make another decision, but she seemed invested in helping me choose something I would like.  I ended up getting chocolate covered madeleines (for my 2 1/2 year old) and an Irish cream cold brew for myself. Both were huge hits. The Irish cream cold brew was the perfect amount of sweetness for me while still packing a caffeine punch and my son ate all the chocolate off the madeleine (which is what I expected).   Since this Starbucks is so close to Costco and ALDI, I expect that normally it is more packed, but I'll do some field research and come back a couple more times. For Yelp, of course.

Probably still the worst Starbucks I've been too. They make you wait a 20min long line just to be told that they're out of what you want to get regardless of what time of day. Manager is rude and no help either.
Full drinks make happy customers!!! Stop cheating us with high prices and shorting our drinks.

Order a quad Expresso with white mocha and ice came and was given in a small tall cup with half of it with ice.  I ask them it usually comes in a venti cup fill with ice.  They insist it's all there but I know they're lying because they are to lazy because 4 shots n 4 pump of mocha already taken up 3/4 of a tall cup.  The cup I gotten was half fill with ice.

Dropped a couple stars because I've been waiting for 20-25 min. In line to pickup my Starbs and they should've posted by the Drive Through window that they are not allowing customers to pickup the order inside because I put my mobile order in, shopped at Aldi and tried to hop in to grab my drink but no luck - they're not allowing people inside for picking up their orders until May 23 FYI! I hopped back in line and it's awful - still waiting for a good while...I have to remind myself to never come here on the weekend!

This is it. Today was the day that I gave Starbucks one more chance and they failed me. I really never buy iced coffee anymore. David gave me a very nice coffee maker, so I can make iced coffee at home, but I was in a pinch today so I thought I would stop in here and grab a quick cup. Big mistake. Huge.   Time and time again they fill the entire cup with big chunks of ice and then put in the coffee. The result is a cup of coffee that I finish before the ice even melts. My grande was probably a quarter cup of actual coffee. Last time I asked them not to do this and they did it anyway. This time I totally forgot to mention it, but I feel as if it's common sense.   Just for the record: I don't blame the employees- I know they are only doing as instructed. I blame Starbucks HQ for being stingy with their coffee. As much as they are charging, they should be willing to be a little more accommodating.

Outside of airport Starbucks, this is the most inefficient Starbucks crew I have ever encountered.  We were stuck using this location for four days during spring break.  The crew is too busy talking to each other to focus on the morning rush.  Line out the door and only one (slow) register open.  While one crew member is greeting the customer, another interrupts him to ask for the pastry order (again) because he has already forgotten.  Never mind me, I'm just trying to order a coffee . . . the two baristas moved like sloths and (worst of all) prepared me a latte vice the cappuccino I paid nearly $5 for.  Only saving grace is the morning manager with the Starbucks tatoo on her ankle (now that's dedication)!  She needs to pull the rest of the crew up to her standards or replace them.

Maggie and autumn are the best..when they are not there the store doesn't run smoothly. They both are very warm and welcoming and know peoples name by heart. I luv coming in when they are working. Please hire more people like them.

Very clean with a great selection.  But for me, it is all about the people and the service.  The girls at the register are very unfriendly.  No smile and very unwilling to make pleasant morning conversation.  Most Starbucks employees are very friendly which is part of what makes it easier to spend so much on very mediocre over-priced coffee.  If it weren't for the fact that this place is near where I keep my yacht at the Harbour, I would not go back.

This is my favorite neighborhood Starbucks and the first time I've dined in since the pandemic.  Take note,management, this employee (picture posted) definitely needs customer service training.  He asked what I needed and when I started to point at the coffee sleeves and stirrers, he yelled at me, Don't touch that!!  (This is after he tossed the Stevia packet at me vs handing to me.).    I responded, you don't have to yell at me, I take directions very well."  He snarked back, We'll you wouldn't believe how many people don't." As if that makes his rude behavior and comments acceptable.   I get  and appreciate it that you're trying to practice social distancing but this man needs to get off the caffeine and practice good manners.

I really wish this location would get it together. We live close by and go often which is dumb on our part because nothing is ever right and the baristas have some serious issues with customer service. Don't bother ordering ahead on the app because that takes even longer.    Visit the one on Vets. They're super friendly and we've never had an issue there.

I love this Starbucks so much! I go every weekday morning and sometimes on my lunches as well. I always use the mobile ordering because it is so convenient. My drink/food is always perfectly prepared for me and ready as soon as I get there. The staff are all super friendly and they are super efficient. I used to wait in line before I got the app and they would just pump through the line. You don't have to wait 5+ minutes here just to put your order in and I love that. This is definitely my favourite one in the city!
Nice up to date location with plenty of shaded outdoor seating, long study table and comfy chairs. It was super packed but they kept the line moving and always friendly with a smile.

Let's face it we mostly do not visit Starbucks for the coffee but for the experience, the baristas are always with a smiling friendly face and super knowledgeable, they look me in the eyes and the customer service is  OUTSTANDING.  Speedy service, nice but not spacious on the inside - great outside seating area.   For the size of this location the cleanliness is always great.  Keep it up Baristas! :)

This Starbucks is my normal spot, and I love it. Warning- the drive thru line can get crazy long, so utilize the mobile order option on the app. The parking also is a mess, but the drinks are always right and the baristas are really friendly and efficient.

Welcomed me when I walked in. Parking is limited and right off of a busy street so don't park in convenient spots if you don't want your car to get scratched or bumped.   Bathroom is clean. Not a lot of seating. It's in a plaza so there is no drive-thru.   They have a lot of staff and seem to get the job done quick.   Popular location for plugging up for productivity, so get there quick!

Service is short - ordered a Venti Iced Caramel Macchiato through the app. & the barista interrupted me multiple times - I had basic questions...shouldn't they learn how to help customers with online orders since I'm sure it's majority of there business?  This Starbucks is just okay - service brings it to 3/5 (should be better IMO). This is a big difference between the customer service I get at this location vs. the one on Sheldon Road, which always goes above & beyond; it's a 4/5 @Sheldon as a result.  Also, parking is really tight, so I recommend the drive through! My previous experiences wasn't really the best (nothing special here), so I don't stop here often at all despite it being on my drive home - maybe I come here only once every 4 months out of desperation.
Great wifi and shared lobby with Hilton is a great idea. Lots of sittings and even for a small meeting.

This was once my regular Starbucks to go to. Every time I have had an issue, or things went especially well, the management has always taken me seriously. Now, when I do go in, they remember me and my regular order and ask how I've been doing. The neat thing here is that there is a line marking where the water came to from Hurricane Katrina, and the line is higher than I am tall. They have had the occasional rude barista, but just tell the management, and they will take care of everything. Parking in front can be tight, but there is parking in the rear. There is no drive thru. There is some outdoor seating. The inside is a bit tight with the seating.  Recently, the exterior has undergone a facelift. The paint has gone from a brownish-yellow to a grey-white. The exterior seating still has nice strings of lights that come on at dusk. The parking lot in front has been cleaned up and the lines repainted. It is nice that despite the fact that this is an established store, it is still cared for.  The restrooms are not gender neutral, but single stall, and I have seen women coming out of the mens room when the line has gotten long, and no issue was made from it.

Too slow! I've been coming here for a few months now. This is my neighborhood Starbucks. From the long lines daily at the drive thru, to the full parking lots out front. Inside a sea of people. While fully staffed. I'll still manage to stay in the store for almost 15mins. Insane. Not to mention the high prices and mistakes on drink orders.

Can never go wrong with a mid day wake up call. Everything here tastes great and definitely hits my sweet tooth. Their baked goods are always fresh and you can get them warmed up if you ask them. Sometimes they have bogo grande or up, who can resist a steal like this.

This is the cafe that I usually go. employees are super nice and remember most of their regular's name. They also have strong customer connection! I always enjoy talking with every single employee who works here. It never feels like they're talking to you because they have to; you actually feel close to them. Also for mobile orders there haven't been a single time that I've ordered and they didn't have it ready when I got there.  The cafe is always clean! and washrooms always have the needed supplies! I absolutely love this café!

I'm honestly not a huge fan of Starbucks or any chain coffee stops are the local hole in the walls always steal my heart, but this Starbucks location does deserve a pat on the back.  Their wifi is always on point. The customer service has always been kind to me and very quick in making drinks and/or heating up my sandwiches. They listen when you ask for special requests, like turning down the AC in a certain area in the cafe. The food fridge is always stocked to the brim with a wide variety of meal options for those (like me) always on the go and needing a caloric energy boost. And of course, the sweets are plentiful in the bakery case.  My favorite thing to get here is the Matcha Frappe with extra Matcha of the Cafe Vanilla blended which is like a vanilla bean frappaccino but with espresso added to it, and of course no sugars of whips added. I am also a fan of the panini sandwiches they press and the occasional birthday cake pop.  Need to get out of the house and get work done later in the day, and the other hole in the wall coffee shops are closed? Stop by this Starbucks and plug in - The experience will be nothing short of joyful and productive.
Worst Starbucks, worst staff. It's not clean, they're not nice, and they're not competent. Avoid at all costs. The reviews are awful for a reason!

Wow, I had to wait until the barista (if that's what you could even call her) finished her funny  story about being out of it, not sure if she was sick drunk or something else and falling down before arriving to work. After waiting I was informed they they had no decaf tea, in which it was so funny. I hate doing bad reviews but this one deserves it. Terrible service and a filthy store.

First Starbucks that didn't write Jade when I told them my name! I mean... they still spelled my name wrong, but at least it was some form of my name. They got my order right, and were very quick about it. I noticed they get a lot of mobile orders, and they are very good about dealing with those while simultaneously handling the orders in store. I got my coffee very quickly. There is plenty of seating, and the bathroom is clean. If you are looking for outlets, there are a few good spots to plug in. This particularly location is basically what I would expect from any Starbucks store.

I absolutely love this Starbucks .. so much so that I've become a bit of a regular. From the friendly staff to the fast service, I absolutely love coming in. Ashley and Pete are always there with my drink ready (often before I can even order!!) , a smile, and honestly get to know their customers.   Whatever the staff is being paid is not enough!!

I used to work right on 4th & Market and parked around this Starbucks, so obviously on my walk to work, I would randomly stop in for a morning latte. I LOVE this staff. I have been to many-a-Starbucks and they are top notch and my absolute favorite. They were always so friendly and though I'd only stop in once a week, they remembered me and always had a warm welcome. I seriously miss this group and every time I am in Old City, I make sure I stop in because I love dealing with them. Just thought I would let Yelpers know how wonderful they are!  P.S. - Like another guy wrote, be VERY careful with parking. The PPA will nab your car within 3 seconds. Also, peak hours, it can be a little tight in there.

Moving locations during the pandemic helped Starbucks only in the new location had a drive thru. It seemed right a way the old employees were gone along with the old building. From the time the new location opened they had production and quality problems. 6 months into the new year they still struggle with following orders and even caring when they get an order wrong. They know my order by now because it's so unique and my husband picks it up inside. Even when he reminds the person who is preparing my oatmeal, they ignore him and the online printed order.  And to those employees, there is a difference in taste between cow's milk and soy or almond milk.

A Starbucks on an island, in a way. You have plenty of businesses on one side of Big Bend Road, yet not the other. The benefit- Starbucks gets the attention a Dunkin or small coffee shop normally would.  Good customer service on the inside, yet this location is so popular that you will easily see 20-25 cars backed all the way out onto the road due to the drive-thru being overwhelmed. The solution? Park your car at the adjacent bank, walk over into the inside of the store, and pick up your order with almost no wait. Seriously, this location may have 8 baristas behind the bar, yet 6 of them are dedicated to make drinks for the drive-thru patrons.   Overall, not a bad location. Just be patient in getting your drink order if you like 20-25 minute drive-thru wait times.

Best Starbucks around. The lack of a drive-through keeps this place still feeling like an actual coffee shop. Baristas run like a well oiled machine, they make my order just a bit better than most Starbucks, and they even remember names and orders, despite being ALWAYS busy. An actual hospitality experience, something lost on many chains.

This has to be the worst Starbucks in Idaho.  I went through the drive thru on one of those rainy days, ordered my $7.00 coffee and left only to get down the road to take a sip of the wrong coffee.  I drove back to Starbucks, went through the drive thru again and explained that I had received the wrong coffee.  They apologized and said that they would make me another coffee correctly this time.  Well, my story should end here but it doesn't. They hand me the coffee and while I'm at the window I take a drink, it's wrong again!  At this time I just ask for my money back, after 5 minutes they finally give me a card with a $7.00 credit on it to use at any Starbucks.  I end up going to another Starbucks where they know how to make their product only to find that instead of a $7.00 card I was given one with the value of $4.00.  That is actually consumer fraud and I will never go back to that Starbucks again.

For the most part this location does a decent job in delivering good, temperature appropriate food/beverages. On many occasions they've forgotten to give napkins, condiments, and/or handed items out of the window one by one rather than putting everything into a bag. I know there's a pandemic going on and shortages on supply but let's think logically and give the customers what they need.

We love this Starbucks for a few reasons:  It is close to the blue route so we can get to it easily en route to and from work. The staff is always kind and welcoming. They are also most knowledgeable about their products' ingredients, and the ambiance is bright and the place is always clean! Keep up the great work, Starbucks!

Stopped in here during a conference we had in Nashville. We were staying at the Omni so this location is on the opposite side of the entrance from the hotel. It was a quick walk and our service was friendly and fast. You cannot use Starbucks rewards here though. Nice little seating areas too.

I was at Starbuck in Edgemont, Pennsylvania today February 19, 2017 at 2:15pm to get a drink. The young gentleman at cashier whom took my order is very pleasant. He did a great job. I was very happy with his customer services. On the other hand, the barista who made the drink whom was a young girl, she's very judgmental based on customer looks and their race. She looked at the customers and shake her head with a despised manners for no reasons. It was DISGUSTING. Shamed on you little girl and Starbuck need to get their rules straight when training their staffs. It is sad to say that this is an ongoing problems at multiple starbuck locations. STARBUCK, please train your staff right. An upscale business needs professional customer services. I love starbuck but time after time of disappointments, I gotta rethink about walk in to one. Sorry.

Came to Starbucks to grab a quick drink but no one seemed to be manning the drive through. I was throughly disappointed and did not get coffee. Then unfortunately, I got so tired from lack of coffee that I crashed my car. Thanks Starbucks.
They took so long to make my grande . I love Starbucks but this one is definitely not my favorite

It's too hit or miss. I'm not a big coffee person, so I only ever get the caramel macchiato when I need a pick me up. Sometimes it's good, sometimes it's nasty. It almost tastes burnt the last few times I got it. Recently, I brought it back because it was nasty and burnt and I heard the lady say under her breath "she gone drink half the cup then bring it back." I had only taken like 3 sips! And even if I would have drank more of it so what? It's nasty and I want it remade. But I still come here every so often I just hate that I have to have my fingers crossed like I hope it will be good this time.

Worst starbucks i ever been to. Service is dry and slow. And a few times my cup was half full. Like nothing but foam. Not the cleanness starbucks. Employees always look mad. The only reason i still go, is for free wifi. Hire friendlier employees and better trainers. No one ever smiles.
Slowest Starbucks Ever!! I've been to this location multiple times and it has never taken less than 20 minutes to get a drink.

I ran through the drive through on my lunch last week wanting something hot, and asked if they could mimic a drink for me. I asked the barista to make me a hot version of the new oat milk drink, and boy did she do a great job. I will be getting this from on now, and save the iced version for the summer!   The line was pretty bare and had my drink in a timely manner! Be sure to tip your baristas!
There are always 4 employees stuffed in the closet space that is the single order counter of this tiny Starbucks.  No wonder they are so impatient and rude.
Was super late for our train and they were able to give us the speedy service we needed (with a smile)! Solid Starbucks ;)   Thanks!

Today, I witnessed horrible behavior from the manager. Starbucks corporate needs to investigate and fire the manager at this location for the absolute horrendous behavior and attitude she has for her employees and customers. Your employees are not slaves. Treat them with dignity.

Coming from the Northeast, I can't believe there are so few Starbucks locations in Nola! I believe there's only two!  This one on Magazine has a great parking lot. Plus, there are so many plush comfy seats, tables for working, and they always make my drink just right.
All I can say is, I love my coffee!!! I use my coupons from my iPhone also I have the Starbucks app that gives me great deals and points

Terrible service. They are supposed to open for 5am, and for the 4th morning in a row we pulled in the drive thru between 5:05-5:15am, and the two-three women that are supposed to  working either didn't acknowledge us or told us they won't be serving until 5:30. Needless to say I won't be back and recommend the same anyone trying to go before 5:30am.

Last Monday I was having a crazy, rough day.. my phone was picked up by someone by accident and my whole life went blank.. You know how it is when you're cut off from the world, and you know no one's phone numbers by heart, LOL! But Brandyn, an exceptional human being at Starbucks, took my order and said, "It's on us."  I was so taken aback by this... it immediately calmed me and allowed me to sit down and get centered. Then another employee came over with a small brown bag with "I hope your day gets better" written on the outside, and a pink cake pop on the inside.  What an incredible personal touch! These guys ROCK!,  and they definitely made my day so much better.  Great job Starbucks.

This is such a reliable starbucks location. Tonight Byron was working at the starbucks window and he made our night! My sister and I were taking a break from caring for our Mom, and finally got to run out for some coffee. Byron was so kind and friendly that we were able to laugh and have a nice convo and just take break from the chaos!  We hope Byron gets a raise he's amazing! And the coffees were perfect yay!!
Awesome Starbucks. Drinks always taste good, stores always clean, and the baristas are friendly. Really awesome atmosphere too. It's a circular shape which I found cool. I'll be back!
They went down became so rude even to use the bathroom you cannot so they will excuse you from stupidity excuse this is pandemic what is there to do the service where is the service!!

This Starbucks is okay. It smells overwhelmingly of mold though. One of the best parts about Starbucks is when you walk in and the coffee aroma punches you in the face. Imagine my disgust when I breathed deep and got no such warm welcome. Blegh.  Anyway, the service was very fast and friendly. My flat white is not the best I've ever had. I think the espresso is pulling too much water, or maybe it's not hot enough. It's not bad enough to return and have them remake though.  Hubs' triple chocolate mocha frappuccino is freakin' delicious, but I don't know how you could mess that up other than using spoiled milk. It is just a really good drink (or calorie bomb, whichever).  Overall, not impressed, but it fulfilled its purpose. I hope they get that mold/mildew smell situated soon.

Usually not the biggest fan of corporate coffee but the people here are friendly and the baristas know how to make the coffee drinks properly. Plus, the Venti & Trenta sizes are extremely helpful to someone like me who needs insane amounts of caffeine to survive. If Green Line was more consistently good and less filled with uptight college educated new parents, or if Milk & Honey had nicer people, I would go to those. Amazing that we have so many choices in this neighborhood and the Starbucks is really the only place that always makes good coffee & treats it's customers with respect.
Best Starbucks crew in the area. Always friendly with a smile even when busy. Too bad they are temporarily closed for a few days. Go to the rt. 70 Barclay Farm SBs. There are nice there too.

Slowest  Starbucks I believe in Philly. They are in dire need of understanding time/order management and to understand if they have product in stock.  Service is slow and all over the place.  I would recommend doing a mobile order to save time.

absolute worst and most pathetic starbucks i've ever experienced. for context, i am 18 years old and have lived in bordentown for 15 years, and have gone to this specific starbucks for after-school treats since i was a child. last year my friend and i went to this starbucks at nighttime and she ordered a vanilla bean frappuccino. she was given a literal glass of unflavored ice. it was the texture of a frappuccino but there was no flavor. we tried giving it back and asking for a new drink but the manager insisted that we were wrong somehow and had to be persuaded to take it back and make her a new drink. months later, i ordered an iced coffee, only to discover burnt pieces of black plastic floating in it. one of the machines had malfunctioned. recently for some reason they have refused to open the front of their store and have been "drive-through only", which i think is absolutely ridiculous. they also are terribly slow because they have a shortage of workers, which i know because i asked a drive-through employee if i could apply online, to which she responded "oh yes, we are very short on workers!". i have applied twice, and have heard back zero times. now i work at chick-fil-a, a franchise which actually provided me with a punctual response to my application and offered me a job. i went to this specific starbucks again last week for a drink and a lunch and i was in the drive-through for 39 minutes (i was looking at the time). it took 39 minutes to go through the drive through because they have a severe worker shortage, refuse to accept applications for some reason, and also do not open the front of their store.

Here's a tip. I spend EASILY 100$ a week between my husband and I at Starbucks...Maybe have some approachable people at the drive through window where you are face to face with customers. Coming from someone who works in customer service, it was a horrible experience. The girl with the bright green hair was so rude. She asked me if I wanted a straw and I didn't hear her so she turns around and goes "I guess not." Hands me my drink and doesn't say anything. Lol get some new people at your location

Worst Starbucks for service that I have ever been in. A quiet Thursday afternoon with just a few customers and three employees behind the counter. Even accounting for all of them maybe having had a bad day, there was no excuse for the abysmal attitude and surly service. I asked for an unsweetened passion tea and confirmed that it was decaffeinated - which I was sure that it was since I have had it countless times before.I'm not even sure why I asked but knew that a massive headache would follow if I consumed caffeine. The young man informed me that ALL of their teas were caffeinated.I looked quizzically at him and asked if he was sure. He rolled his eyes and said he was sure. I ordered nothing and thanked him. My husband ordered  his drink and had it slid over to him by a woman who would clearly rather be doing anything else. She made no eye contact and did not respond to his thank you. I then looked up on my phone and confirmed that passion tea is indeed decaffeinated and went back to place my order. I did not gloat or act annoyed in any way but simply confirmed that I'd checked and my unfriendly employee then smirked, did not apologize for having made a mistake and punched in the order. Again I thanked him and his fellow barrister practically threw the drink at me and never made eye contact, turning away immediately. Rude and unfriendly- I will not go back!

Wanted to try out the new Starbucks within the Safeway in Vail. Have to say that the young man that was behind the counter was very nice. I believe his name was Zeke. There was a little line and when it was my turn he apologized for the wait. I asked him how long he worked here and he said about a month, I was impressed because he was working all by himself and still kept a smile on his face.

Fell in love with a barista after having a conversation about this cross country road trip I'm taking. Raises the stakes knowing I won't be back too philly for a long time. Felt the vibe, missed the opportunity. Thinking about her hours later, resorted to leaving a yelp review....have never stooped so low  Great experience 10/10 would do again  -a crisis having young man

Came in today (Friday morning)- this location is so very cute and cozy, I wish I had had the time to lounge and enjoy it! The employees here are awesome! They were pretty busy but so polite, and do not make you feel rushed, and very friendly. All drinks came out perfectly! Thank you and great job y'all!
Employees turn people away before posted closing time. As a former Starbucks partner, this goes against company values.
Drive thru is super convenient, but can be slow, especially during the weekday mornings. Seems understaffed at times, but so far havent had an issue with an order

Cashier with the dark pink and blonde hair has NEVER been friendly. As the first person you interact with in the morning....this should be unacceptable coming from someone working at Starbucks. Disappointed.

I stood at the counter for 5 min and every single person behind the counter just looked at me and walked away or flat out ignored me. When I finally got someone to acknowledge me, instead of helping me herself, she asked another girl to help me, who only acted that I was annoying her by trying to purchase coffee and breakfast. I won't be back to this location.
Crazy! So I wanted to order a coffee and buy one of their reusable cups. I asked if the reusable cup could be rinsed out before putting my coffee in it.  I got a BIG no! What? Crazy!

This Starbucks is in the worst location possible in traffic pattern. Despite placing my mobile order well in advance, typically it's not ready. Patience isn't an issue, I understand Covid has played hell on our workforce so I don't mind waiting. The issue is, when I place my order online, I pay for extras, an extra shot of expresso, etc. There are other custom selections to my drink order as that is how I enjoy it. However, when I arrive most days, they can't find my order, ask me if I just placed it or say it's just not in the system. Then when I get to the window and show my mobile receipt, the ask ok just a venti white mocha!? No, not just a venti white mocha because I already paid for these extras. It's near daily. My only other option is past my workplace and doesn't do mobile ordering.  The cost of your coffee is premium, the service should be too.
Do not go to this Harrison location. They give you drinks that are all ice and when you bring it to their attention  they just tell you...."everything is measured." I payed $5 for ice.

Horrible service. Walked in as the only customer with one order ahead of me. Stood there for 15 minutes while others got served first. Interaction from employees was less than friendly or understanding. Walked out with one drink instead of try two I ordered. At least I asked for a refund. Will NOT be back to this location.

I've never had this happen before but today I was not happy with my starbucks. I asked for a hot latte and received an iced latte, I bought a chicken wrap box......it was terrible!!! The wrap had barely any chicken it!! While the flavor was good, I had to eat a plain wrap with nothing in it BECAUSE I WAS STARVING and already back at my job. NOT HAPPY!!!! There has also been times when I have ordered my latte and got back to my job (which is in the same parking lot as my Starbucks!!) and when I sipped by drink I could feel how light it was, opened the lid and the cup was just over half full. Seriously!!!!!! So now I ask for light whip cream so they don't fill half of the cup with whip cream. Last time I checked....whipped cream was just a topping.
Drive thru is good, but frequently busy. The wait times are not long, staff in drive thru is nice. Coffee is good!

Yes, Starbucks are quite similar from one to the next but I wanted to drop a review for this one for two key reasons: 1.) The young gentleman operating the drive-thru orders during our visit and could have fooled me if he said he was an on-air radio talent. His positive energy and confidence poured through the speaker and brought an instant smile to our faces. 2.) The burnt butterscotch latte is delicious. Give it a shot while its on the menu.

I love that this location is so close to me.  I recently started delivering for Uber Eats as a side job.  This is one of my favorite spots to pick up deliveries from. They almost always have the order ready and if it's not ready, I never have to wait more than a few minutes. They package the items to perfection and write the customer's name on the bags, which helps me out if I'm picking up multiple orders.  The employees who work here are fabulous and always very welcoming.   I always look forward to stopping in!!!!

My only reason for 4 stars instead of 5 is because the parking lot is way too small and crowded for how popular this location is! I came to get my Fall dose of PSL and just as usual... I was not disappointed  The indoor seating is gorgeous and spacious as well.

With the choice between this location and the one inside westshore mall, this is definitely the better choice.  Plenty of seating inside but only two tables outside.  The staff was great and very pleasant.  Coffee was perfect as was the food.  I enjoyed the comfy seating by the window to chill at.

This Starbucks is not for you if you're looking to sit down and be on your laptop. It's small and efficient. I am fan of the mobile order and have yet to have a problem with it. This place works for me. Luck for you there's others wishing a block or two if you're looking for a spot to relax or do some work.

No no no no I was thrilled to see this place open near my home.  My first trip 7am Sunday morning and the bathrooms have lock codes on them. I asked the guy behind the counter why and was told so they can monitor who is using the bathroom. I hate this kind of snobbery so I won't be back.   I'm guessing this is an anti-homeless measure but there are no lock codes at the Grand and Lindell location or the S Kingshighway location and homeless people frequent those areas without over running the coffee shops. Locking the doors helps no one but it reveals something sad about the owners heart.  This is a brand new store so the owner isn't reacting to a historical situation. Perhaps they are acting on faulty assumptions about this part of town.  Maybe they are worried about losing business Whatever, doesnt matter. As said earlier, I won't go back. There are plenty of other Bucks in STL and there's a perfectly wonderful privately owned coffee place closer to my house anyway -Restituto (without locked bathrooms). There are two other local coffee shops in a 3 block radius and they don't have locked bathrooms or hordes of "undesirables" hanging around.  I love you Starbucks but this anti-human snobbery breaks my heart. What if a random homeless person happens by and really needs a bathroom break? I know what that is like because I'm one of the millions of Americans who has been in that situation.  I'm not going to spend my money in a place that makes me feel like I'm contributing to more human misery. If I patronize you when your employees are kind to me it will only remind me of your unkindness to others and it will ruin my coffee.

This place is awful. I ordered a drink and they didn't even start it until i asked 30 minutes later. 4 girls  came in 20 minutes after me and they got their drinks and food before me. I asked for no whip and they still gave me whip. I checked 3 times before i asked them abt my drink so don't call me a Karen. I've been here before and the food is very overpriced. And whenever i sit at the tables they're always so dirty and the bathroom is worse than a gas station bathroom. I stopped going a couple months ago because all their machines were broken and they're always out of materials. Do they not restock?  I recently came back and luckily they did have some materials but never most, whenever you check the app, half the items are out. This place is so bad, overpriced, messy and unorganized.

My least favorite Westbank Starbucks, but the closest one to me. Usually timely with app/in-store orders... that is IF you can order what you want because they're usually out of nearly half of the menu items - especially seasonal flavors/drinks! Ive started orders on the app MANY times only to be disappointed that the drink I want or even the syrup add-in I'd like is not available, although you can still select it. Never use the drive through - always long and slow. Order ahead through the app!! In general, their coffee is not nearly as strong or flavorful as that of local shops.
Depends on what time of the day you go the service is good. Jason at the drive thru window is the best! Always so kind. Also I LOVE that they have  real dog treats for my pup!

There was a bug in my drink. I don't know what to say, im completely disgusted. The lid was on the whole time and once I reached the end of my drink I noticed something dark floating up to the top and immediately noticed it was a big gnat/other bug. I dumped out most of the ice and managed to find it again at the bottom. Utterly disgusting. In addition, we ordered two green tea lemonades and they made one without lemonade and we had to go back to fix it.
Standing inside 40 min for one drink? Not worth it. They will always give your food cold and then give you attitude if you ask for it warmed up.

Retrain. Including manager!! Simple birthday redeem can't even process! And tried to blame me for it when I mentioned many times before I even ordered!! Didn't even bother being nice and didn't even get the correct beverage!!

This is the second time I have a wonderful interaction with the people in drive thru. The first time, I was whistling video game tunes and the barista liked it so much, I got an upgrade.  Today, I was repeating what my wife was ordering verbatim, including words like, "I guess, umm..". Then, a barista separate from the one taking my order came on the mic and said, "Bro, you guessing what your wife wants?  You gonna be in trouble."  That led to a wonderful yet playful conversation that made all of us chuckle, including my wife who was in the car with me. I do enjoy these kinds of interactions and they make my day in an otherwise gloomy year. To boot, the drinks were well made and the pastry I got was good.
A great afternoon stop, nice patio outside and beautiful inside seating.  The coffee is addictive !  ((( Great service too )))

I typically place a mobile order, walk in, receive and go at any Starbucks but I encourage it here. The staff is always friendly and prompt. When I leave, the drive thru is usually in the same spot though so order ahead and you don't have to wait.

Lady at the drive thru was real sweet. Girl on the mic wanted to argue what a black eye is. Just run a quick google search. It's coffee with shots of espresso, not sweetened coffee. I'm done with this Starbucks. Y'all are terrible.

My favorite Hot Starbucks Espresso Beverage is the Venti Carmel Macchiato!!  I admit I'm very picky about my order. But it's not like it's a .99 cent cup of coffee.    I was soo blown away tonight as the Barista made my Carmel Macchiato ABSOLUTELY PERFECTLY DELICIOUS and on point!!!  My hubby and I frequent this Starbucks Location drive thru whenever we can.   Hubby just orders the Venti Pike w/cream & 4 Splenda, he's not hard to please at all. If I wasn't with him, he'd settle for a cheap regular coffee from the Donut Place.   I, on the other hand, am not a fan of coffee. So, when we are out together, it's Starbucks only. Lol.   I order the Hot Venti Sugar Free Carmel Macchiato with 9 Pumps of the Sugar Free Vanilla Syrup, Extra Carmel Drizzle In the cup, 3 Splenda, 2% Frothed Milk, Just 1 shot of Espresso (not the 2 that comes with this) and with a straw to sip from.   Yes, I know it sounds like a lot but I am a diabetic and I also can't have a lot of caffeine so I have to be specific.   5 STARS from the Barista tonight, my Macchiato was Perfection!!  Thank You so Much!

Line was very long, but they were appropriately staffed and the line went quickly. Waiting for drinks was very minimal. Had to dock a star because they don't accept the Starbucks rewards. However, our Americanos were 50cents less than they are at any other Starbucks in California. Drinks were accurate. Plenty of seating.

Arrived @ 5:20 PM. Happy the wifi works in the parking lot on this cold day. Seats outside are not very socially distanced. All inside closed down. Understandable. Very friendly ladies behind the counter. Not sure if one is in training because two don't need to be standing around just chatting it up with each other ignoring customers. These two behind the counter just laughing it up, talking about up north and "some other girl the other day" and I can hear all these personal conversations over the music that I assume was playing. I was greeted by them so I'm acknowledged but they don't trace where I've been or how long I was there.   One speedy worker making ALL the drinks - no help and they may like it like that and then the 1 barista working drive thru. I stood in the store for almost 20minutes waiting on my order. I asked the ladies, who ignored me since the initial hello,  where my order was because I have been standing over there waiting. I was pacing after my order said ready but I watched the barista making ALL the drinks. I never received an apology just laughter- more like giggles and reply of "It got mixed up with drive thru, haha". I didn't find it funny that Over 10minutes after the app said my order was ready and no one asked me why I'm waiting around. Very disappointing. Most other Starbucks bend over backwards to make sure customers are happy. If one is in training then she is not learning the Starbucks way from her trainer. Luckily this is not my normal Starbucks because if so you just made we wanna walk into Sip85 instead of this because that experience was so stressful.   I wish management good luck because those two employees earn the giggle and gossip award but not hard working.   Most displeased

I'm not sure if I've seen a more well designed and aesthetically pleasing Starbucks than this one.   They have a beautiful patio outside with a privacy screen and the inside has ample space between the seating. One big table, a couch area, private areas, and a wall of small and medium tables. If I lived over here, this would definitely be a choice for doing work.   The staff were all in a great mood and very helpful with my sleepy self. I opted for the pumpkin crepe cold brew and it was ready within a couple minutes.   The area to order is a little tiny if a line does begin, but I think they move pretty quickly to mediate that problem.   Also my grande cold brew was $4.50 - what a steal for Starbucks since I usually pay between $5-7 at other locations.

I love this store. The staff is friendly and the atmosphere is great. Of course, I'm a big fan of their coffee, which is just as strong as I like it. My only (minor) complaint is that when I paid with cash, the barista put on gloves to take the cash, then took them off. That doesn't bother me, as we have to protect ourselves from Covid-19. However, when I asked for a stirrer for my coffee, she gave me one with her bare hands instead of allowing me to grab it myself. I didn't realize it until I left the store a few seconds later, as I was distracted. Protecting everyone from Covid-19 should go both ways. A stirrer that's going in someone's drink should never be touched with bare hands. Other than that, I've always loved this store and will continue to get my coffee there.

It's actually a Starbucks window in Harrahs casino no seating and always crowded with casino patrons. The prices are higher than a free standing store but if you need a coffee fix it's your only choice.

I just went through the drive-thru and it took forever.. No one inside either ordering I don't know why it took so long today and then she tried to tell me a mango dragon fruit was a creamy drink and she wanted to sit there and argue with me... To the little teenage whatever chick that was so rude and had an attitude... know your drinks honey because I sure do.

I don't frequent Starbucks much but I will be back to this location because of the awesome staff! Quick and friendly, and were so nice to recommend something to eat with my nitro brew since it can upset the stomach. Remember to tip your baristas! :)

Consistently a 20-30 minute wait for a mobile order AND the landscaping is unkept and outside dirty. Way to show pride in the neighborhood. I try to go to the Vets location even though it is far less convenient. They really need to get it together.
Pros:  Drive Thru  Cons:  Hurried service and over crowded.  Your typical Starbucks -not bad and not outstanding.

This Starbucks often goes unnoticed because it is tucked away in a shopping center with a lot of businesses, but the store is really nice inside and the service is always top notch.  If you have an early morning game at Golden Eagle or an early tee time at Redhawk, this is your spot!
Friendly team. Quick service. Music is loud and a bit wild. Loud dubstep. Team seems pleasant behind the counter. Smiles all around. Great job.
First full service Starbucks that I have experienced that does not honor rewards earned in Starbucks program such as a free coffee when it is shown on your account.
I don't know why I came back.  Told myself never again and here I am 20 mins into my order and no drinks... terrible.

Horrible experience   While I was ordering a drunk another female behind the counter interrupted my order and corrected me that Starbucks doesn't make skinny peppermint mochas anymore.   I found this intrusive and rude.  I thought I ordered a frappacibo peppermint mocha and actually received it over ice.  Yuk.  Additionally my child ordered a chocolate chip cookie not warmed.  She threw the cookie across the counter and said @dont forget the cookie".  I have never receive a cookie in a plastic wrap with the date on it. It has always been served in a paper bag.  Zero finesse or class at this place.  She was entirely unprofessional and diminished the experience one pays for and expects at a Starbucks. Get your cookie and coffee off the shelves because this was jus a bad experience.

It takes a lot for me to give a Starbucks a low score but this location is not my favorite. I ran into an issue where one of the drinks I ordered required "steamed lemonade" the barista was very rude when replying that "we don't steam lemonade" and when I asked why he simply asked the manager. The manager stated it was a "health code" violation and stated they couldn't make my drink. They didn't offer to sub the lemonade for anything or even to NOT steam the lemonade, they just moved on to the next customer. I contacted their customer service and they said there's no reason the lemonade couldn't be steam and apologized profusely for the situation. To this day I still avoid this location, it's worth it to drive further down the road.
This location is the worst. They are so slow that traffic gets backed up into the street. They never listen to what your order is to get it correct. Most days they are out of items too.

Standard Starbucks. The baristas are great!   Only problem with this location is they have never been willing to let me get a pour over or a French Press. So if you're looking to sit and enjoy something more than some Pike's Place, you may want to drive over to the Franklin Road location.

Can't even believe the horrible service we just had.  My daughter ordered a drink. No other customers in line ahead of us. As we stood for 6 minutes waiting all 4 girls stood there talking about stuff and not one of them made our drink. The one girl was alone making drinks for the drive thru alone while these girls talked and did nothing! .   One sat down then got up and finally made my daughters drink and it wasn't even made right we had to tell them it was wrong and have them fix it.  She even while talking and making her drink threw the empty container across the room to the trash can which it missed and fell to the ground.  Then she proceeded to throw the top from it across to the trash which missed too! Unreal.  Glad I wasn't a manager there seeing that.  Very unprofessional.

Visiting the area and came here to order the drink I always get with my friend and they made them completely watered down and wrong. Also talked shit right in front of my face and had horrible hospitality. If you want to spend your money and throw out what you bought right after then come here!
Very nice and accommodating...out of dark roast and gave me a special pour over at no extra cost

Great little Starbucks in St. Pete. The location has minimal seating and the parking lot is a bit small but it makes it a minimal sitting/loitering location which I prefer. Will definitely come back when I am in the area.

This is a hipsters paradise. And I am not referring to the clientele. Every time I have come, the male baristas have either well maintained beards (even well conditioned) or moustaches that they have spent a lot of time grooming and shaping into styles. They must spend a lot of their spare time grooming their faces. And their clothes? Perfectly ironed pants (even the jeans have the line from ironing) and their shirts are perfectly starched. Seriously. Its a good thing they spend so much time on their appearance, because they definitely don't spend any time at all focusing on the customer, or even what the customer JUST ordered and paid for. Or that they took any order from any customer at all. On the few times I have been, I have never seen an employee of color or female. All white males. I wonder what this says about the area, or the management of this location. Which I am shocked because this is in a diverse area.  The inside is clean, neat and well organized, though on the small side. If you are lucky, you might get something similar to what you ordered, if you ordered something like almond milk in your coffee, you will probably get regular milk in that drink. Be warned, strongly warned. But, you will quickly be given a beverage and your food order, even though it will most likely not be what you ordered at all. For example, on my most recent order, I ordered a croissant and an iced pumpkin spice latte, and they tried to give me a cake pop and a hot skinny mocha. I'm just sayin'.

It's Starbucks and this is NOLA which means there are lots of local coffee options but this location is great. Fast, friendly and drinks prepared properly, what more could a girl ask for...ohhh yeah she asks for establishments that are conscious of and follow COVID rules, which they do THANKS STARBUCKS

I ordered the green tea latte but with a cappuccino ratio of milk. Unfortunately, the cashier wrote, "latte, half foam" so I got a very heavy drink. I explained the miscommunication to the barista, she knew exactly what I wanted and remade it perfectly. 5 stars for the young lady!

This is a common place of study for me. While studying for home inspection state Licensing exam, I sent here every single day. The staff is nice, helpful, and friendly. Every time, no matter how crowded. My drinks comes quick and just how I ordered it. I've never had a problem at this Starbucks.

Was just in here the other day. One of the baristas spit in my double chocolate chip, then called me a nigger and threatened to call the police on me because I took to long to get from the entrance to the cashier. I'm not even black I'm just a dark Puerto Rican with curly hair. If it wasn't for her calling me a nigger and spitting in my drink it would have been a fine experience. 3 stars

A bunch of confused employees, over staffed yet slow and no one knows what's going on. Manager is rude and the food on display is moldy. Response to telling them said they're display doesn't reflect the provided food. Lmao come onnnn

Consistency and friendly service is the hallmark of any Starbucks. This location has lived up to this standard. My order is always ready at the time specified on the app and given to me with a smile (you can tell even when masked). Thanks Starbucks!

I've never wriiten a negative review on a business based on their political stance. But in the case of Starbucks, I feel most compelled to do so. My family and I have enjoyed your drinks and your assosiates are curteous and respectful. I can not agree with the foolish statements Made by CEO Howard Shultz regarding illegal immigration. Our nation has laws that are to be followed and respected. When an indivdual such as CEO Shultz advocates giving employment to individuals who disrespect our laws, I can no longer respect him Yes, Legal Immigrants are always, always welcome.It's who we are as a nation.Illegal immigrants are not.Instead of advocating to hire 10,000 illegal immigrants, maybe Starbucks should consider hiring 10,000 homeless veterans. Aren't our men and women in uniform worth at least that much?  A former happy customer, gone for life.  #RESISTthe Media
I was unaware that it is apparently bad manners to call and order a box of coffee the day you want it. The staff was unbelievably rude.
Just sipping on a Vanilla chai tea latte w/ soy, no H2O.... I know it's hot but my tastebuds were salivating for my favorite hot tea.  (Pause for a sip)   Yep, still as good as always....

This place is truly incredible. Background: this location is on my way to work so I frequent this place at least four times a week. The first time I went, I ordered my usual drink per my allergies and one sip told me they didn't pay attention to any of the customizations. But, I gave them the benefit of the doubt because mistakes happen.  Since then, I've started mobile ordering so it's ready when I get there and I don't have to deal with the morning rush. I have the timing down to a science. Normally things run smoothly enough, but they've never been fantastic. This morning I ordered a decaf black coffee and a sandwich. I got there ten minutes after placing the order, and the coffee wasn't on the stand. I waited a few minutes because there were six baristas running around, and I saw the drive thru line was a bit excessive. (Note there was never more than one person in line in the actual store.) After a few minutes I ask about my coffee and it doesn't register on anyone's faces. I show them the ticket on my sandwich bag that clearly says I ordered another item. They ask what the coffee was, I tell them, the woman in charge told another barista to make it.   This girl...has she never been awake in the morning? Does she know people have jobs? I don't know. She pulled the coffee filter out of the bag slower than my 90 year old grandma getting off a toilet. Then she went to the back and pilfered through some shelves. The woman in charge asked her if she made the drink yet and she whined, complained to her coworker about the harsh treatment, literally spun around a few times in the back, then came out and started pouring my drink. She overfilled the cup and had to pour some out into the sink, drenching the side of the cup. Then served the dripping cup up to me with a "thanks for waiting" without making eye contact.   Look, I understand the stereotypical White Woman thing so I try not to make a big deal about things like this, but I'm on my way to work when I go in there. I ordered my BLACK COFFEE at 7:37AM, and arrived at 7:48. I didn't get the drink in my hand until 8:01. And somehow, it's cold.   This location makes weaning off of my Starbucks addiction so much easier.
Went on Wed 11/4/15. Great service service as usual. Cashier in drive thru was friendly,  helpful and a delight! He had great red hair!

This place was disgusting. I'd rather drive to Rockville Road then to get something from here ever again. If I could give zero stars I would! Between given me the wrong drinks and old food with a bad attitudes, It's not worth it! My advice travel elsewhere.

The promise of Starbucks drive through is that they get your order right every time. Out of my 5-6 visits here so far my order gets messed up twice. This location is logistically great for me to and from work but I think I will go back to Quik Trip if I have to break for coffee.  Yesterday there was a mumbler at the drive through. I could barely hear him and he did not confirm anything I was saying to him. Of course the drink order is wrong, I asked for soy milk and got regular milk and when I asked him if it was soy milk, I was told I did not tell him that I wanted soy milk. Yes, he did offer to make me another drink and asked me to wait in front of the Starbucks instead of the drive through line. That's fair. And 10 minutes later I had to walk into the place to ask for my replacement drink. I give up on this place. There are so many other coffee shops in the area with better service. Sorry Ferguson Starbucks, you need to up your customer service game.

This is my favorite Starbucks in the New Orleans area. It is pleasantly located on Harrison Ave. in Lakeview, has a great outdoor seating area, and always has good service.   BOTTOM LINE: #1 Starbucks in NOLA!

Went in here for a quick pitstop Tuesday afternoon to re-energize my system with some go-go juice.  I ordered a Triple Grande soy latte.  This Starbucks has a Drive-Thru. While I was waiting, I noted to myself that it was weird to see Starbucks Barista's with headsets on. I pulled my pocket camera out and was about to snap off a shot of them with their headsets on and they yelled out "NO! You can't take pictures in here!" I inquired as to why? I sarcastically asked if my taking a picture of their Drive-Thru operation was going to reveal Top Secret Starbucks plans.  The manager came over and told me that they were more concerned with maintaining the privacy of their employees. I'll buy that argument - we all know what a bunch of stalker types all of you Yelpers are!  There was lots of inside seating here - and some outside seating as well. A number of folks were working away on laptops, presumably utilizing the T-mobile connection wireless internet connection.  I'll never come back here again since I was not allowed to take a picture! ;-)

One of the worst Starbucks in the area.... I tend to drive 12 miles away from this location because they don't have consistency. Besides of the nasty attitudes Starbucks needs to teach some of the people here how to make coffee.... it's hot or miss   I have never seen another coffee place temporarily close so much either....

I do not live in this area but frequent Starbucks stores at least 4-5 times a week so I have interacted with many different employees over the years and they have always been very positive. I can honestly say that I have never walked out of a Starbucks without placing an order until this time.
