I'm not sure that I can think of another time when I have been treated with such an attitude by someone. I asked a simple question and was responded to so rudely and the other employee standing there heard her and didn't seem to be phased. If you don't like people, get a desk job in a cubicle someplace. I won't be returning here.

I'm surprised that during a busy time there is only one cashier. The person before me had an item return and it took such a long time. No one said anything to me while I waited and waited. I come here a lot and just expected to be welcomed and not ignored.
This is my usual Starbucks.  The driver through is pretty fast and everyone is for the most part is super nice.

Stargetbucks...Lol, this is our nickname for this Starbucks which is located inside Target on Sierra Center Parkway. Great location and what better way to get a little pick me up when heading in to do a little shopping! Staff is usually very friendly and quick to get their orders out but can you please get rid of that large awkward order monitor that you have to look around to see me when I tell you my order? There has to be a better place to put that thing...   On a more personal note, I've been to a lot of Starbucks locations but this is the first Starbucks I have been to that does not ask for your name... I asked "don't you need my name..." and the cashiers response was "nope, your good" When there a lot of people standing around waiting for their drinks it would be nice to know who owns that order.   If the order is called out for a certain drink and someone else ordered the same size and drink as you... there is a moment of awkwardness when you both head up to get 'that' drink they called out...personal experience. Once you do get your order, there is plenty of seating which is also shared with Targets food area or you can just be on your way to complete your shopping!

Centrally located in the meadows mall, Starbucks is an oasis in the crowded shopping mall. With all there favorites, espresso, cappacino, latte, chat tea, frapachinno and more there seems to be a small line all day. The coffee is OK, not great, but the padded seats and free Wi-Fi make for a good people watching area and to get work done.

I got two large Venti Caramel Crunch Frappuccino's and as i was drinking it I realized i was tasting mostly whipped cream so i take a closer look and there was whipped cream filling my entire drink. I love Starbucks but I don't want to be just drinking Whipped cream i paid $14 for caramel flavored whipped cream. I would reccomend getting an iced drink which is just as good but not full of whipped cream

Perhaps it was just a bad day.   My drink order was wrong. I had to send it back. It happens. Never mind that I was seeing complicated frappucinos being served to people who came after me in line before I even got my wrong drink order. But that's one thing.   I guess you would think I ordered something off the wall.   My order... A large half decaf regular coffee.   You see, they make you wait until they make the complicated drinks for everyone else, and then they took care of my simple coffee. And when I asked if it was half decaf since they didn't state it, the barista admitted it wasn't and offered to make another, but not before they had to complete one more complicated latte that came after me.   Maybe it was just a bad day. But they reminded me why it's better to go to Dunkin Donuts or Wawa if you just want the brewed coffee.

It's okay. I asked for a drink that was a promo that just ended and was quickly dismissed. Then I asked for a normal drink. I asked for skim milk and she repeated the name of my drink. I don't think she was very interested in listening. I don't think this is a Starbucks I will come back to.
Nice small coffee shop. Kinda hidden. Some parking available free of charge. Drive up window available

As I sit watching while my drink is being made, I witnessed an employee restock supplies, warm up food, SMASH HER BARE HANDS DOWN THE GARBAGE and proceed to making drink orders. Major food violation for all to see. Starbucks needs to train employees to wash hands more regularly? This consumer will not be retuning to this one.

I have been by this Starbucks a few times. It's a quick right turn off of West Chester Pike. Unlike some other Starbucks, this one has some of the nitro drinks, which I really like. An employee of another Starbucks told me that the equipment for nitro drinks is expensive, and that's why a lot of places don't sell the beverages. It's not much of wait even with a crowd if you walk in, and the drive through was efficient when I used it. The inside wasn't the cleanest when I've been there, but I guess with high traffic, you can't keep things constantly spotless.
There are a number of nice Starbucks in the area, but the entire staff at the newly-remodeled Ridgemoor Starbucks is the best-bar(ista) none!!
Not to happy with this starbucks, don't go in with a big order they don't like it. And they can't print you receipt s 90% of the time cause they say their computer is broken.

Didn't think it would ever be necessary to write a review about an established and consistently good chain like Starbucks, but I have had 3 bad experiences here which is equal to 100% of my visits over the past year. I keep coming back because of the convenient location and me wanting to give them the benefit of the doubt, but this location needs to tighten up their operation. I have had wrong orders twice in a row and have been charged the wrong price for a drink when they were simple orders and in a non-busy time of day. I understand people make mistakes but the last time I went, the place was nearly empty and one worker swept in front of me while the other one was standing off aloof. It took a long time to even be acknowledged and for my order to be taken, and it was't even met with a smile or a greeting. Common sense and basic customer service go along way. Never thought these subsequent small issues would make a difference in my perception, but they really do. I will never return to this Starbucks again.

I think this is a great Starbucks location in Tampa. It's located on the corner of a busy intersection, at Kennedy and Westshore, making it equally accessible for you to leave and go take care of multiple things that you need to do in the area or, for you to come from various locations in the area to take a quick coffee break. Unlike Starbucks locations that are closer to downtown, this location is located in a plaza with ample parking, making it a more stress free option when you are on your coffee run. The inside of the store is spacious, with lots of table seating. I also really like the addition of high top table seating in the middle of the store. It makes for a more communal feel and it also allows slightly larger groups of people to congregate and meet up, always a plus for a coffee shop. The staff here are courteous and take care of your drink or food orders fast. And the bathrooms are quite clean, another plus considering the high volume of people that pass through the store. Will definitely drop by if I'm in the area again :)
Very LOUD and obnoxious employees. NOT a place to relax in peace, drink a cup of coffee and study, read, etc. Do not recommend.

The service was great. Employees are always friendly. The coffee tastes like coffee and made just as I ordered. The only problem is the design of the parking lot. You could easily get blocked into your parking spot for a minute or three longer than you'd like when the drive thru gets backed up. It happens very often. It has happened several times to me. Drive thru is the way to go if you decide on stopping for coffee.

There is not much to say about Starbucks.  Listen, when you are a Colombian, and you drink Colombian coffee everyday, and you have been harvesting coffee while being a child, a plain coffee in Starbucks won't surprise you much.  The consistency of the coffee is the same every Starbucks you go. I even become a petulant woman if I have to talk about coffee in Starbucks.  However, Starbucks have two items I really love and for which I return to it.  The chai latte, and the espresso shot.  Moreover, the workers are usually really nice people, super attentive and smiley.  They have something in common, their looks, they have tattoos, and piercings, and crazy glasses; they are happy people.

Super excited there is some sort of Starbucks near me.  This would be the closest I have yet to find to JBMDL, that is corporate.   This review only relates to the drive thru portion.    The employees were fun and super nice.  The coffee from the barista was just okay.  I think the outside of the building looks nice (it appears newer and well kept).  The fact that there is a drive thru is fantastic.  Starbucks is harder to find in this area as Dunkin Donuts trumps it (I'm a huge disliker of dunkin donuts).  I will come here whenever i'm seeking my Starbucks its in a good location to 295.

(IMPORTANT NOTE: Evidently I spent more than 2 hours in this location this morning, as I discovered when I went to my car. I discovered a $45 ticket from Diamond Parking on my windshield. I had no idea there were even time limits here. I left a message with North Town Mall administration to have the ticket reversed. My family shops a lot at this mall. If this is not taken care of satisfactorily, I won't be shopping there again.)  (UPDATE - Aug 13: Just talked to Larry, the manager of Starbucks, and he's going to look into it and call me back. No luck with North Town Mall Admin yet, as they don't ever seem to be in their office or return messages.)  (UPDATE - Aug 23 - I called Diamond Parking last week & they reversed the ticket & trusted that I was in Starbucks. They said next time they'd need proof, so keep your receipts! I always do.)  On a dreary, rainy day like today, I really appreciate the abundance of natural and nice artificial lighting. Looking outside at the pouring rain makes me want to stay dry and comfortable here.  This Starbucks is a good option for an informal meeting up to eight people. I haven't seen any tables this large in any other Starbucks.   The parking can be a pain at times because the newly remodelled Northtown Mall (well worth a visit) is quite busy, with a London Drugs, T&T asian supermarket, Bed Bath and Beyond and Indigo books as tenants.  Busyness - Mid-morning in this Starbucks is steady, but not overly busy. As one of the many Starbucks with a drive-thru, I'm sure that makes a difference.  Noisiness - It's on par with other Starbucks, if not a touch quieter. It's a bit bigger space than other Starbucks, so it seems to absorb more noise. The music isn't too bad either.  Ambience - This Starbucks is another one of my favourites. It's a bright space with lots of windows and pleasant artificial lighting. You don't have to worry about eyestrain here. It's a comfortable place and there are a couple of really nice paintings (prints?) too.  Power - Seems to be a good selection of outlets to plug into.  Free wifi - Available and works well. I think Starbucks made a good choice in offering free wifi.  Staff - What can I say? Starbucks does a pretty good job of hiring upbeat people. I don't know if I've ever had a complaint about their staff.  Seating - There's a good variety of seating here for groups, including a table that can seat eight (something I haven't seen at a Starbucks before). The majority of the seating is geared to groups of four actually. There are a couple of comfortable armchairs tucked away in the corner and there are three comfortable benches also.  In total there are 28 seats inside. There are also a couple of tables outside.  Security - I've found it to be pretty good here overall, but there are a few odd characters coming in every once in a while.

This is not the first time this has happened, online it states this stores closes at 8pm. I walked in tonight with my boyfriend at 5:59 to get tea and the employees say "we are actually closing" I understand business may be slow but this is the second time this has happened. What's the point of having a convenient Starbucks downstairs from my apartment if they are closing by the time I'm getting off work. It didn't help that I was getting tea because I wasn't feeling well and didn't want to have to go to the store. This store has a lot of work to do because not only are they closing early, I wanted to try these bantam bagels everyone is raving about. Not knowing how many come in an order I asked for two orders and a coffee, I get charged about $12 and I only get two single bagels. My friend later tells me that I got ripped off because I should have gotten 4 total. Not impressed Starbucks, not impressed at all.

They make drinks. But there's always new staff so they are constantly learning how to take orders and make drinks. There's usually a line/wait and usually the drinks come out different everytime. Go up the street to Roxborough and get a better drink with better service.

The poorest run Starbucks I've come across. Under staffed during the morning rush in a huge office building. 30+ minuets in line just to order. Cashiers running food orders. I've seen numerous orders getting screwed up. Def need s better management.

Kudos to the baristas on my last visit. Still squinting against the cosmic rays of COVID-19's corona, they've opened the doors, but only to the extent of the long arm of the law: one giant step from the entryway to the interior's antisocial barrier. Waiting for service at a nearby car dealership whose coffee maker was removed against fear of the virus sent me over the hill through invisible woods to grab a cuppa. The Buck's brew is more to my liking, anyway.  The barista's voice smiled from beneath the facemask as she retrieved my mobile order. The drive-through lane was (surprisingly) empty and no one allowed inside, so service was immediate. The green to-go stopper (so often out-of-stock in busier pasts) had been pre-inserted by a magical, plasticized hand. Condiments are also banned to public fingers, so you'll need to ask specifically for anything you'd normally manage on your own at the walkup counter. Yet, a cheery and successful visit was had by all.  Your mileage will vary. Like its sibling locations, service varies with each crew and visit. It's still a Starbucks cookie-cutter, chain store experience. But we must all do our part to keep the beans brewing. Mischief managed.

Stopped at this Starbucks on my way to a doctor appointment. I went inside, did not go through drive-thru so I cannot comment on that.   I was heartily welcomed as soon as I walked in. It's spacious, with lots of tables, a few couches, and comfy chairs.   I got my order within five minutes, it was correct and hot. I worked on my laptop on the table.  Bathroom was clean.   No complaints.

The dude working the register was rude and the service was terrible. They were slow and at least 3 people that ordered after me got their drinks first. When I finally did get my order it was the wrong drink, but at that point I just wanted to leave.
Would it kill this place to serve a better selection of soft drinks that are not coffee?

This Starbucks location is on my way to work, so this one is my go-to. I usually go through the drive-thru and I have yet to have a bad experience in the year and a half I've been ordering coffee here, which is either very lucky or rare for a coffee shop in my opinion! Although my order really doesn't vary much, I stick to lattes or flat whites and the occasional seasonal drink, so I guess I haven't really put them to the test.  Most of the baristas are friendly and fun, only one time have I ever had one that wasn't super friendly sounding but he seemed very new to working with customers. Almost like he hadn't tried working on the "sorta-fake but possibly actually friendly" voice that us customer service peeps perfect. Otherwise he was just very direct, which wasn't bad either.  Overall, this is a normal, good Starbucks experience at this location. It's usually a 25% chance that I'll also pay for the person behind me, hoping to start a chain reaction, so maybe you'll get lucky next time you visit this one.

I have ordered online multiple times form this Starbucks location. However, every other time when I arrive to pick up the order, they will tell me that the sandwich(es) that I've ordered are out of stock.  Any other Starbucks, when ordering online, will only let you order the sandwich if they have them in stock. This location needs to do better with updating their sandwich availability for online ordering - it is a consistent problem! A manager needs to figure this out and fix it.

The team that works at this location is nothing short of amazing. You made my day with your great service when I had a problem, no question asked. I couldn't ask for more. Try the pineapple infused back tea, zero calories and it tastes like you're on a tropical island. See you tomorrow guys.

Maybe the worst Starbucks there is around. They are slow, don't do the drinks in the order they receive them, and sometimes the order doesn't even come out and you have to go back to the desk to remind them or ask them what happened. All they have as an answer is "oh I'm sorry, what was it again?". I just don't get it.
The SLOWEST drive thru EVER 15 minutes in line  Not acceptable maybe more staff.  Need to speed it up

I am elated with the staff at this location!! I stopped in as they were locking the door and the young male barista willingly accommodated me with a Venti coffee on the house!!!! Thank you!! I now will head to Sarasota in java style!! #Starbucksrocks

I've grown increasingly frustrated with this Starbucks. I now go out of my way (driving ten minutes to another location instead of only 5 mins from my home to this location. They VERY rarely get my drink order right ( it will be very clear on the order sticker as to what it is) and I am more often than not requesting a remake. I am a daily Starbucks consumer and every time I have decided to give this location another chance, they have messed it up.
One of the bad apple in Starbucks family. Very dysfunctional store. Multiple mistakes taking orders and long wait time for not particularly many customers. A better training is necessary.

'We're out of everything! ' Seems to be the order of the day.  I was disappointed that they display the pepperoni  turnover in the display case but when I order it the product is discontinued!? Don't display product you don't sell.  Also all other fold over types are sold out. Finally-  It doesn't take 24 hours for a gift card to transfer to the Starbucks app, it happens right away.  I put an extra $10 on my app thinking that's what happened but when I refreshed my app the gift card had applied.
Barista Quinton is the light in my life   -1 star because they take  too long :'( waaaaaa

This is still my favorite location. Just recently the orders have been wrong alot, most recently in the last 2 days. Yesterday wasn't a big deal because it was just lemonade missing. Today is an issue because I've only had 1/4 of my drink and feel sick because the wrong milk was used. Mistakes happen but 2 days in a row and people have severe allergies, luckily I didn't drink it all and it will pass but please starbucks pay attention a little closer. (Plus we pay extra for these things)  1 Star-   Total failure of an experience based on this experience would not return. Would not recommend.  2 Star-  Below average on several levels, not a total failure. Wouldn't seek out to return or recommend.  3 Star-  Average experience, minimum expectations met. Nothing that says wow or that stands out. Would return if someone else wanted to come, not a regular go to.  4 Star-  Above average experience, will come here again and recommend to friends and family.  5 Star-  This will be a regular go to and will be highly recommended to people I know. Exceeds expectations on a consistent basis.
This was a typical Starbucks. Service was prompt. Staff was pleasant.

Ordered two drinks from the Starbucks in Grand Sierra Resort. One of them still had large chucks of ice that wasn't fully blended. The other was only 2/5 filled, and when we asked why, the worker just told us we could get in line again. Unacceptable because we always order the same drink at other Starbucks without any trouble or inconvenience.  Service here is lacking, as well as the coffee. Deserves better!

The employees at this Starbucks are friendly, nice and consistent with their service. People should expect it to be busy considering its location in Haddonfield. There is a good amount of seating inside and a few tables outside as well.   There is metered street parking and it's located on a corner of a four way intersection.   5/5

I hope people understand that this Starbucks is populated by human beings who have been working with the public throughout the pandemic--I hate to see negative reviews when they can't help that their staff is out sick or that they had to close early due to lack of supplies... anyway this is my go to Starbucks and I love the people here, always kind and polite even when I forget my order as soon as I walk up lol. You all are doing a great job.

Disappointed. 4 of 5 items in my order were incorrect. Two breakfast sandwiches had cheese when I specified no cheese. I ordered everything bagel bites but got plain. Usually Starbucks is great about these details but apparently not this one...

This Starbucks is a mile from our house. My wife will sometimes walk up when she wants to get in a bit of extra exercise. The store itself is pretty standard for a Starbucks. The service has been good and the store has been clean. It is in a relatively easy location, although the drive in is in a very awkward position. I would recommend ordering online, parking in the shopping center parking lot, and just walking in.

It's Starbucks. I've never understood high priced coffees but a large or "venti" blonde roast coffee is $2 same as any place else. This place has a great crew and a good comfortable environment to enjoy your coffee. I am a regular here and if I have one complaint it would be that it is on a busy street with a main bus route that leads to some oddballs coming through door but hey one persons oddball is another persons friend so what should I care?

Full disclosure: if there was a Yelp-variant solely for rating public restrooms, I'd be all over it. Alas, there isn't, so this review is mainly a shout out to the top notch men's restroom at this Starbucks.  S'bucks as a chain is typically pretty prime when it comes to restroom game, at least by my metrics. Always roomy single-stall lockables; none of that multi-stall nonsense. This downtown location takes that roominess to another level. The restroom is more comparable to a nightclub hallway -- strategically lit, sleek gray paneling lining the walls. The space between the door and the toilet is almost comically long... but there's a weird grandeur to it. You can sit on the porcelain throne at leisure, basking in the gratuitous space while the sounds of mainstream indie-folk filter through the air. Everything the (fluorescent) light touches is your kingdom. Jury's still out on the LED task-lit mirror -- at first glance, the bright light is mildly flattering but a lingering look really forces you to ask: "God, is my skin always this oily...?"  Overall, a quality location... for a Starbucks. Decent glimpses of downtown, especially if you take advantage of their street-side patio seating in warmer weather. For windy, snowy, or overcast days, there's plenty of space and seating indoors as well. Drive-thru available for those on the go. I'm not much of a coffee dude myself, but every time I've grabbed my usual (grande black tea, two pumps classic) from here, it's been up to par. Solid spot with bonus points for winning my lavatory love.

This Starbucks is excellent for evening study sessions it's usually pretty quiet and the baristas quickly learn your name and drink preferences. They are quite friendly even if you aren't a 'regular'. This starbucks has pleasant but not overwhelming volume level for their music.  I cannot comment on the service at morning rush times as I'm strictly an evening Starbucks customer. The wifi has yet to give me any problems. Over all good place to grab a cup of caffeine and crack open a book.  Parking can be a bit of a challenge as the lot has perhaps 10 spots in it and a drive thru lane between the parking spots and the store. Street parking is ample on North & South as well as Delmar.
The cashier was in no hurry and chided customers "don't come to Starbucks if you don't have patience". I would have left if I hadn't already waited over 10 minutes just for a regular coffee.

I frequent this location because it is so close to my work. Sometimes going more than once a day!   My drinks are always hot/refreshing and made just the way I like them. Sometimes I'm even upgraded to a larger size at no charge.   Since I've been going here I see the same faces on both sides of the counter! That's saying a lot for a coffee shop. I often wonder if some patrons ever leave!   After their recent facelift it has helped with flow of the queue that forms in the morning and lunchtime rush. Less in shop seating now from the looks of it, but I've never actually sat and enjoyed my drink.   The only thing negative I have to say is that they usually run out of sandwiches before lunch even starts! And there is one staff member who could benefit from smiling once in a while... But over all my experiences have been above average!

At Starbucks, you know what you're getting...Ironically, I'm not a coffee drinker, but frequent Bucks because if their internet and short business meetings. This Starbucks specifically gets three stars for two reasons: 1 the parking sucks; 2. It was disheveled and kinda dirty inside.  Eeeek.

There r no ppl waiting in line. And the server totally forgot I have a latte. And he didn't eventually make it with whole milk and light ice which I told him when I was at the counter. Poor service.  I like the chairs here better.
I've been here a couple times. Nothing good and nothing bad about this location. Just your typical Starbucks. Drive thru is a little hectic but never had a bad drink here.
This place is an embarrassment to Starbucks. Awful service and wait times for simple drinks, skipped orders and wrong orders. Go next door to the Wawa.

First off l, thank you for giving us a Starbucks. Secondly my girl Alex is amazing. She always greets you with her amazing smile. Today was the first time she waited on me (took my order). So I got to see her bubbly personality and her kindness. I told Alex how I wanted my drink and she let me know that there was already a drink on the menu like it so I was able to save (woot woot). She helped me understand the starred points system. But the best part is when I saw an organic Reese's that was dark chocolate (eww). She tried to explain it to convince me it was great. Then I told her next time and then she purchased it so that I could try it!!!! Made my day (it was THE BOMB btw). This Starbucks is amazing!

I love this Starbucks because the staff is amazing! The other patrons are usually super awesome as well! I work close by at Grin Dentistry and our staff orders from here daily. Anywhere from 10-14 drinks per day. It didn't take long at all for Jamie (a manager I believe) to learn my name. Or anyone else's name who happens to be getting our drinks that morning. I hand them a list of about a dozen (half of them with titles 10 words long,) and they get right on it with a smile! Usually, they put our names on the cups which makes it super easy to pass out back at the office. One lady in particular (shame on me for forgetting her name!) always makes the doctor's drink last because she knows doctor likes it extra hot. They also let me know as each tray is ready so I can load my car up as they go. Sometimes I wait until they are all done (especially in winter months) and have become pretty good at stacking 2-3 trays to carry out at once. The other patrons are always quick to open the door or grab a tray and help me out, even if I don't ask, and I think that speaks volumes for the atmosphere, staff, and clientele. It takes someone who truly loves their job (like Jamie and her staff) to always smile and be so friendly when they see me coming with that long list every morning. Thank you all so much! You can get a fancy coffee drink at a lot of places, but great customer service is priceless!

Alright so yes, this is your typical Starbucks. But I'm mainly writing this review to compliment Alicia(I probably butchered that) but to the girl closing Friday night! She is a gem and the barista who took care of both my friend and I. Even when It was past closing time, she gladly took my order. Yas girl.
Bad Stuff :  PARKING IS A NIGHTMARE. Weekends the line is out the door. for real!  Good Stuff:  Coffee, sandwiches and pastries, WiFi and a fairly big dining area.
Very friendly baristas but they need to clean there coffee shop!!! Customers were having to clean the tables themselves and the glass doors and windows need a much needed bath!!!!

First, there was someone drinking a coffee inside with a dog (not a service dog)....my biggest pet peeve (pun intended) at restaurants. People, use the outdoor seating if you really have to bring your dog. Also I asked the associate if they had a certain item. He told me no but in fact I did see it on the shelf after looking.
On the last two weekend visits. I have found the person at the register to be borderline rude and unfriendly. Guess I will be avoiding this location if I can.

This morning I decided to pick up an espresso at my favorite Starbucks on Speedway and Wilmot. Even though there were at least 6 cars ahead of mine, they managed to kindly take my order, get it right and with a smile. Thank you guys!

Starbucks is Starbucks... Unless it's this Starbucks. The friendliest, fastest Starbucks service around. If nothing else, go just to talk to Dan in the drive thru. His energy is so contagious, you may not even need any caffeine!

If there were a minus star I would give that!! The person behind the counter couldn't even look up to greet me. I could hardly hear a word she said and never once said thank you!!!  Than the girl who put my iced tea and sandwich on the counter mumbled to let me know my order was done and again grunted a thank you. Wow this Starbucks sucks. I've been to many and never had such an experience....blah

This location is in a great spot within the airport that is convenient to the Delta Gates but the service is terribly slow and the staff are very unfriendly. During a busy morning today they had only two staff working and a line that took me 36 minutes in order to even get to the cash register. They also rang up my order wrong three times and I had to correct them. This place is a total mess with so much potential.

This is my quick stop for my iced passion tea, I forgo the liquid cane and have it made with monk fruit stevia.  I have some dietary limitations and I can still enjoy this refreshing tea made to order.  The drive thru is usually pretty efficient and all the employees are pleasant.

The McMullen Booth Starbucks is my primetime chill out spots. It's one of the most spacious and least crowded that I've been to, which is why it is close to heaven for readers.  The interior is decorated in the rich, warm colors that Starbucks is known for, but unlike most of the cafes this one has two long, wooden tables for multiple patrons to sit and enjoy their java. This is perfect for groups, and personally my husband & I love coming here as a date night for some drinks and board games.  If you're looking for comfort, they also have leather chairs that are soft enough to sink into and stay for a while. When I'm not bringing the hubby for date night, you can find me with a kindle in one hand and a tall caramel macchiato in the other(which I order with nonfat milk to make it less than 200 calories. Yum!).  The onnnnnly gripe I have, is that sometimes the sounds of their appliances can be quite noisy, but who's ever heard of a quiet blender?   All in all, this is a wonderful place to come sip a tasty drink and kick back. The environment is calm & cool, the baristas are quirky & perky, and of course the drinks are always delish.

This Starbucks is huge! Unfortunately, a lot of the tables don't have outlets (hence the 4 stars). I haven't been here in a while, but it's nice to see that there is now a community table. The baristas are all nice and the place is clean.

For a Starbucks, it's as good as it gets. The location is obviously the best being on High Street, but what you wouldn't know is that the staff are always great! The manager/owner is always really friendly and funny. Generally, there's always a place to sit, which can be a problem at some locations. And the drinks are always made as ordered, again... can be a problem at other locations. Great location, great staff, and consistency.

This is the absolute worst Starbucks I have ever been to. The line is awful, the service is slow, and the employees are rude and inconsiderate. Many times in a row they've messed up my order and made me wait. I started ordering online and they still don't make it until I get there.

Not the worst, but they definitely need to work on some things.   This Starbucks is already located in a Target store, so I feel like it should lose points for that. I don't like having to wade through a sea of middle-aged, oblivious moms in yoga pants who would rather chit chat than let you get your coffee (this actually happened, I'm not stereotyping).   There was one woman working when I was here and she was being run ragged. She was hustling getting coffee, food and everything else that the MAMs were demanding of her. I seriously think she was thrilled when I just ordered a large iced coffee.  At other stores such a simple order comes out right away, but because there was only one person working, it took a while. Seriously Target, get that girl some help. The coffee was mediocre, as expected, and I only stopped here in the first place because someone gave me a gift card for Christmas because they obviously hate me or something.  Anyway, if you need coffee during your Target trip, then this is the spot. Otherwise, you should probably avoid.
Free ice water, it's got my vote.  Sure, I know that's a policy of all Starbucks but this is a valuable thing whilst traveling -- where bottles of water are often charged at Disneyland prices.
Had to repeat my order 3 times. The person at the window was rude. And then they got my order wrong!
Usually Starbucks is pretty good, but this one was awful. They messed up my order twice, and I never get anything complicated just a white mocha. I'm very disappointed

5 Star customer service. I walk in and everyone on the afternoon shift knows my tea. First time I have ever had this kind of service. This afternoon I walked in and Sally asked if I found kittens to adopt. As we talked she made my tea without asking what I wanted. That is great service. Paul, Mario, DeeDee and Margaret all provide fantastic service.I could make tea at home --- I would rather go to STARBUCKS several times a week. Thanks - Michelle AKA Venti Vanilla Rooibos

LOVE this new store! The staff are all well trained and ready to improve it even more. There are lots of comfortable chairs, free wiFi, and good music playing. Once construction is complete it will be very busy - so sneak in before this great location becomes overwhelmingly popular.

This place makes way better fraps than Bryn mawr. There is usually a line and only one person making drinks.  There is less seating than most Starbucks. I really love the seasonal fruitcake frappucino. This time when I ordered it the barista said "oh you actually like that?"  I told her it's my favorite and she said "the chunks of fruit are nasty". I rose my eyebrows and again said, "well I like it". When my order came out, again she said it would be better without the chunks of fruit. I find it terribly rude to ridicule a customer's order and unprofessional to bash one of the products you sell. It really bugged me.

This rootin tootin new Starbucks is a great place to stop, particularly for Causeway Commuters. More on that later........  Uness you were born on a different planet or raised by wolves, you know what Starbucks is all about. They're the originators of the overpriced coffee drink and have hooked millions of Americans on the fru fru mocha late hold up the drive thru line frapa drinko. But despite all that stuff, they are also incredible convenient and make getting an influx of caffeine pretty darn simple.  This shop is brand new, and located on the outskirts of Lakeside Mall. Getting in to the drive thru is simple. There is a large computer screen at the ordering area so verifying your order is correct is simple. Be aware that they have a camera and can see you when you order. Don't be picking your nose or they'll know.  Each time I visited this location I've used their drive thru. It moved pretty quickly and the employees were very friendly and helpful. Now for the pro tip for commuters:  If you drive the Causeway daily you'll know that the right lane of Causeway Blvd. moves more quickly than the center lane. Stay in the right lane, turn right at 17th street and get in the Starbucks line. When you leave Starbucks exit on the side closest to 17th Street and you'll avoid all of the traffic and have a tasty beverage. You can thank me later.

Looks renovated Quick service I wish I took a picture of my frappachino before I devoured it because it literally looked picture perfect. Not only that, but it was perfectly made.  Nice job Starbucks. Its rare to find a good one that actually cares about the quality of drinks being served.

The service was really nice. Very fast no wait at all hardly. I also had to purchase 13 5$ gift cards for a business event and the cashier made my experience so pleasant she was so kind! I appreciate this level of service.

I was initially at the Bread and Company next door and couldn't figure out what's what. So, I came into this Starbucks, instead.  Nice, small Starbucks. Probably only enough to seat about 20 inside; 9 outside. Kind of dark inside, but there are seats near the front windows. The wooden (made from reclaimed wood!) seats at the window are uncomfortable (no backs) and wobbly.  Pastry offering was meager, but as long as they have my cheese danish, I'm fine. I'd probably still go to the bigger one up the street if I wanted to find a place to sit comfortably.

Conveniently located just off 70 in case you need a fix in the middle of suburban hell.  I grew up just a few blocks away in Barclay Farm and it's interesting to watch as suburbia has eaten away at all the mom and pop shops and chain stores have popped up.  The parking is very tight here due to the amount of businesses in the area. Back up S  L  O  W  L  Y.  Don't forget to ask for a puppucino in case your beastie is with you.  Service was robotic and fine.
Fantastic barristas that can make anything you like with a cheerful smile.
Drive thru was super quick! Took me 5 minutes to get all the way from the road to the window! Kara was super friendly at the window asking me about my day!

Worst I have ever been too. I purchased at least one Starbucks a day at various locations. This is by far the weakest. Employees tend to lean on the rude side, service is very slow and forget doing a mobile order. They apparently loose them half the time. If the manager is reading this, which I doubt, maybe it's time to close the store again but this time loose the diversity training and focus on doing what you are in business to do. Provide service that matches a $4 cup of coffee. Last I checked McDonalds was across the street for $1.

This is a subpar Starbucks with a poor layout. Sure, the staff is friendly, but something seemed off about the interior. It is walking distance to Vanderbilt University, which is nice, however. There is outdoor seating for when it's nice outside.

My favorite Starbucks. What I like the most about this place is that they stay open till 12:00am! great for night owls like us! Also, the baristas are friendly and always treat like family. Sure, it does get pretty crowded at times and I would love to see some improvements in their tables and chairs, but besides that its a good place with hard working staff.

This Starbucks is awesome !   Personally, it's becoming a favorite!  I believe this is a newer location with a newish design, they even have cool plants, curtains, shades and a modern interior which is cozy and inviting.  The bathroom facilities are newish too.  What I personally like of this Starbucks vs other Starbucks in the area is they play music that's at a good volume, not too high and it's a smaller cafe so it doesn't get overly noisy like some busier cafes which can become a bit overwhelming.    I find that the baristas are friendly and welcoming, positive and in a good mood!    This is a nice cafe to relax and grab a quick bite.   Warm, friendly atmosphere.  I enjoy it!   Wish it was closer to me!

Such a great start to my day! I opt for the mobile order ahead at this location on my way to work. Between my drink and the lovely people, I am NEVER disappointed. I can get the drink at any location, but I haven't received the same smiles or "have a great day!" elsewhere.
Zero. really. Perhaps Starbucks was good ten years ago. But now, dirty, unfriendly and just another Corporation that forgot how they got started, us the customer made you.

I went to this location with my 6yr old daughter she wasn't feeling well that day and accidentally dropped her smoothie on the ground. She felt so bad and started to cry. The staff was amazing they gave her a new one and told her it was ok. They even gave her an extra cute little cookie.

eww.. Starbucks.  If you have any taste, you will not be caught dead in the green monster while in New Orleans.  This one is super tiny, and four blocks to Cafe Du Monde.  Go for it.  There's also coffee on the third floor of Virgin, two blocks away.

Classic Starbucks. Of course when I was there in the morning the cashier was pretty cranky but hey it's not the end of the world. Otherwise the service was pretty good and I enjoyed my stay here otherwise.
Nice guys here but very slow. Very slow. Can't even go here on a Friday morning because it usually takes so long.
My home Starbucks location. Always clean, efficient, and friendly. Great place to get my daily caffeine fix!

I have been to all over the country, and have never felt I needed to write a review for one unit now.   When my friend and I walked into the place they had a large display right in the doorway [blocking everything, so clearly they would like you to see it.] with their new refreshers in a can. When I picked one up to look at it, the cashier from a distance shouted to us "Those are gross. The ones we make are ok, but those ones are gross."   Ummmm...OK.  We approach the counter to place our orders and the girl walks away. We stand there for several minutes waiting for someone to ask us what we wanted to order.. but nothing.   THERE WERE 7 PEOPLE WORKING BEHIND THE COUNTER.  Once we did put in the order we ended up waiting15 minutes for it. There were no drinks they needed to make in front of ours. They were all standing around talking. The funny thing there was what appeared to be a trainer behind the counter teaching a new employee to do something.  The man that ordered behind us didnt get eye contact, asked questions that were answered with short snotty answers and was treated even worse.  We overheard conversations about employees and their medications, weekend plans, hair products, etc.   I am still shocked about the lack of proffesionalism and the dirtiness of the store, but what blows me away the most is that the drinks took 15 minutes to make. I live in Chicago and watch a team of 2 take care of a line of 30 people with no problems. These 7 people couldn't get an iced drink together. GET IT TOGETHER.

It's been awhile I debated on doing a review on Starbucks coffee shop close to where I live I been coming to this one for the past year since moving to the Valrico neighborhood area.  The service from the staff is a hit and miss depends on who you deal with as a biastra as some call themselves inside of the store or thru the drive thru.  Some problems with drink orders in the pass but the problems were fixed quickly by the staff like for instance wrong coffee was made or the Turkey & Swiss sandwich didn't taste good after they sold it to me so the manager gave me a refund for the issue.   Other than that above this coffee shop is great for coffee of your choice they do have WIFI, indoor and outdoor seating for the customer while sipping on your coffee either hot or cold or eating some delicious there.

I love this Starbucks! I come here to do homework frequently and the staff is incredibly friendly. They are very visibly hard working and notable at their job and get the orders done in a timely manner. The night crew are always nice and welcoming despite the later shifts.

4 stars for the location, cleanliness, and service-  One of my favorite Starbucks for sure. Yeh, not a lot of parking right next to the store but it is a standalone building in a shopping mall parking lot so there is parking available. The store is usually busy especially the drive through but the lines move fairly quickly. I've always had good service at this location and the restrooms are maintained well. Also, a rarity is the cleanliness of the self-service bar. It's not only clean but it's usually orderly as well. That's a difficult accomplishment with how busy this place can be so they obviously pay attention to the details. Typical Starbucks trinkets and sweets for purchase.

A genuine little piece of Seattle right in downtown Franklin -- What a beautiful addition to historic West Main St.! Now we're talking civilization. The only espresso shot in middle Tennessee poured in less than ten minutes! A WARNING however: This starbucks *is* a bit of a surprise. I mean, yes they do have the wonderful digital wall prints and tacky art-glass lampshades and cartoonish overstuffed furniture-- don't worry about that. But, there is a long hallway to get to the bathrooms in the back. TIP: If you just want to go to the bathroom and don't care for the "icky bitter coffee junk", go through the back door (adjacent to rear parking lot) and duck in unnoticed! I think this is a great place to stumble over from Mccreary's to and cover up your drunken stupor by doming a venti quad bullseye in one gulp, getting in your car and careening through the baby strollers as you make your getaway!
Never been inside but the drive thru staff are always pleasant and apologetic if they are busy. Great customer service for sure!

Have you ever gotten so busy and distracted through the course of your day that you forgot to drink any coffee, and then started to completely run out of gas in the late afternoon?  That happened to me today while I was shopping, and I managed to trek the fifty yards or so from Eddie Bauer to here without calling an ambulance ;-)  I give Starbucks points for having locations pretty much everywhere on the Planet, and this one turned out to be convenient in a pinch, but the coffee here (and at all the other links in the chain) is just AOK at best, and considering the prices, the value proposition is questionable at best.  Bottom line: If you need a decent cup of Joe, you can do worse than Starbucks, but you can do a heck of a lot better as well.

I definitely like the layout of this Starbucks.  It has a drive-thru.  You walk in and it looks like a long, wide hallway without space to spread out and the table means to do so.  The music selection is comforting and there is artwork on the walls.  The employees are super energetic, and there are a lot of them, so maybe bring headphones if you are concentrating extra hard.  Drink was okay - I think the liquid came to the halfway point of the cup which is sad.
This is my favorite  Starbucks the staff is very friendly and helpful.  That atmosphere is really comfy.
As always, this store goes above and beyond. They brewed a batch of decaf for our group. Good for Starbucks, good for us.

I love each and every Starbucks, and this one I can remember pretty well. The inside isn't as big as preferred, it's actually to small I would say. This could easily be a problem for some study groups or students. They did get my order right and every drink received from them tasted amazing I should add. I will be going back because this location is very close to my house, but I wish the store was bigger.
I live pretty much across the street from this location and have been here at least 40 times and have never had an incorrect order.  These 1 star reviews seem to be gruntled ex employees.

My friend ordered a flat white and it was ok, not as well prepared as she's had at other Starbucks.  I had a breakfast sandwich and friend tried it as well and lets say it mostly went in the trash.  To sum things up, the experience at this Starbucks could have been better if we were greeted with a smile and some enthusiasm, however young lady at counter couldn't do either as much as we were trying to be infectious with ours. Great customer service can a lot of times compensate for mistakes.  Hope this helps improve things at this location.

We come to this Starbucks once or twice a month. It's a matter of convienence vs preference. The service is so hit or miss, mostly miss. Today, my drink was made the wrong size and the barista huffed at me and was snarly about remaking it. I've found most of the baristas are lackluster about customer service and much more interested in chatting with each other or friends.  This location is frequently crowded with students studying or working.  The drive through tends to move fast and is usually pretty busy. The drinks and food is up to the normal Starbucks quality

The staff is extremely nice, but the drive-thru is always a MESS (just for coffee?) and the drinks themselves are a little overdone for me. (All I can taste is sugar). I like the new teas they have though!

This is your typical Starbucks location.  As noted by other reviewers, the parking here is terrible.  If you park and go inside you need to be very careful when leaving, as when you are backing out the cars waiting for the drive-through will be right behind you.  I do like the seating inside, as some spots have a nice view of Delmar and North & South while being out of the direct view of the rest of the room.

Cashier didn't seem to know what she was doing. I asked for a peppermint mocha frap and she kept asking if I wanted it with coffee or vanilla. I told her I want it with coffee and oatmilk. When I received the drink I noticed it was significantly lighter in color but thought it was because I asked for oat milk. After leaving I realized she gave me a vanilla frap . I obviously asked for a peppermint mocha. And said peppermint mocha repeatedly.

I had a few minutes before my flight was called to board. It was super cold and super early, which means I needed a good, strong, and hot beverage in my tummy. I walked the long, boring hallway to score a soy latte, which was practically my salvation for this morning.  The employee was super nice and took my order promptly. This location wasn't crowded at all, which was perfect for me since I'm a grouch prior to 11am. My hot coffee was ready in about 1 minute. I walked the long and boring hallway back to my terminal with the holy grail in my hand.   The soy latte was perfect. Hot but not scalding hot, thankfully. I was able to get my caffeine infusion before the flight was called to board. I'm pretty sure the other passengers were quite jealous of my tonic. It may have something to do with my yummy noises I made as I made quick work of my tasty treat.
My First time NOT having a Great Cup of Coffee at Starbucks !  I ordered the Chai Latte and it was Wonderful...  (((( Try The Chai ))))

I generally go to Starbucks to by COFFEE beans. Normally I purchase other interesting items they have on display. Yesterday I went to Starbucks to get coffee as usual. They had none on the shelf. The person with whom I was speaking asked manager if they could make me a couple of bags. I don't know what was said but it amounted to. "We're to busy" well that's the last time I'll go there. Come to find out I can get it for the same price at Amazon. And, save time and gas. Plus I'm not tempted to buy something for which I did not go shopping.

As a general rule, I try not to write reviews for chains, but i make exceptions on occasion.  This store seems to be investing in the community and is trying to find a balance of being a drive-thru and a destination.  It's definitely an anchor for Irvington.  Overall it's a pretty comfortable place to chill and enjoy free wi-fi.  The demands of being a drive-thru store makes it so the baristas are not as friendly as I've seen in other stores.  I wish Lazy Daze (or another indy coffee store) would get it's $#!+ together to be a real competitor to sbux in the area, but from what i've seen, that's not the case at this time.  Coffee: standard starbucks Food: yes Vibe: standard starbucks WiFi: Yes.  free. Plugs: Yes.

This location is poorly operated and the service is always slow! Yes, I understand it takes longer to make some of the specialty drinks but the service here is very slow! This morning there was a long line and a woman complaining that her mobile drink was not ready for pick up! Again, forget the sensitivity training and train your employees how to actually provide service at a reasonable pace!!!!   The 3 star rating is for the decent size parking lot and decent size indoor/outdoor seating! Other than that not much else I can say!!

This Starbucks has one key feature that puts it on my regular itinerary. It's near where I sleep and therefore can provide an early morning double espresso when I'm too lazy to crank out some coffee on my own.   Honestly, I've been to so many Starbucks that I can't really tell them apart anymore. Why is there not a Yelp badge for this amazing accomplishment Hello!? Yelp! I want a badge now.   Ok. Maybe this is the caffeine talking.

This location has a drive-thru...but if you use a reuseable cup for a fancy drink, they still use a disposable cup until you get to the window. Go inside instead if you want to not waste.  The staff seemed a bit distant and not too customer friendly. In fact, the barista seemed a bit impatient with me while I was deciding how to make my however-you-want-it Frappuccino.   Overall, nothing too noteworthy, but does the job.

Not sure what is going on lately, but since the new manager and new weekend staff it is a hot mess here!!!  SOOOO slow and uninterested ... Night and say difference from the excellence if Pat, Mel and the weekday staff....hate coming on on weekends :(

It's worth mentioning that the employees at this Starbucks location are all fantastic. Management has really established a great team of individuals and they all function like a well oiled-machine, getting drinks out quickly while still making customers feel welcome. The drive-thru is often way too long (the only negative)... so I'd recommend going inside and ordering. You'll get your drink faster and the folks inside are great.

This location  is sure busy!  I'm here on a holiday Monday and for the last two hours all the seats indoors have been utilized as well as the patio.    I LOVE the fact that this location has a huge patio with like 6 tables!  The staff are competent, the drinks taste like your typical Starbucks, so that's all good.    Though, they lost a star as I cannot find plug ins for laptops.Thank goodness my laptop has a long battery life - I'll def come here again to work.

When I first started coming here coffee was always made right and consistent. So far my coffee order has been messed up twice. I asked for a grande iced hazelnut latte with an extra shot. I was charged for it but when I received the coffee there was no extra shot. The cashier never wrote it on the cup. When I asked if there was an extra shot in the drink the barista told me, "no its not written on the cup" with annoyance and I understand you're busy, but I just wanted what I paid for. She proceeded to fix it and instead of remaking it she took my cup and poured the extra shot in an already completely filled cup. So when I closed the lid most of that extra shot spilled out. The second time was today. I went through the drive through and ordered the same drink. When I received my drink it was definitely clear looking and when I tasted it I knew it was made with skim milk, which I did not ask for. I was in a rush and didn't have time to ask for a remake. The barista who made the drink obviously wasn't paying attention to how the coffee was made because no where on my cup does it state I asked for skim milk. It sucks because I pay for good coffee. Lately it's been hit or miss.

This is the Starbucks I go to most often.  It's usually very busy, and the space is L-shaped and narrow.  t's often hard to get a place to sit down inside, and there's plenty outside, if that works for you.  It usually doesn't for me.  Why am I giving this Starbucks four stars?  Good service and good location.  I often go to Trader Joe's to shop afterwards.  It's often on the way to or from home, and I came here for trenta-sized Passion iced teas to counteract dehydration during cancer treatment.

Like every other Starbucks around the area same seating inside and outside, drive thru ordering and same with inside. They offer food to order and same with drinks including free WIFI! The customer service from the staff could use some work but other than that it was a great experience.

My favorite Starbucks. Friendly baristas. My drinks are always well made and staff makes an attempt to know you're name and make friendly conversation. Have experienced the "pay it forward" drive thru train a couple of times here which leads me to believe we have some kind customers and neighbors in this area. I love that even when the line looks crazy it never takes long.
Pretty much the same Starbucks as all others.  Consistent coffee drinks.  Very nice that there is one right there in the hospital.
Chapters gets me here, coffee is nasty and bland, stick to the cold drinks.  -Yelpku #1

I was embarrassed when I spent 12 dollars, gave him a 50 because that was all I had and he took my stuff back. He asked if I had anything smaller and I said "no." I apologized and started to leave and he said, "I will do it this once but won't do it again." I explained to him that I forgot my debit card and had Christmas money in my purse. I still gave him a tip because  thats the  type of person I am. He kept going on and on about me giving him a 50 and they don't take large bills due to security reason. I offered to leave because he was making me feel bad, but he ignored me. Then he handed me my stuff and turned and left. I told him to have a good day and he ignored me. I am writing this sitting in my car outside this location right now because I'm that upset. I have never been treated rudely at a starbucks and will never visit this one again. He could have simply said he won't do it or he could have been much nicer about it. Management should teach their team to treat others as they want to be treated!

Unfortunate that this was one of the worst times I've had Starbucks. Both drinks we ordered were supposed to have caramel and mine, a caramel macchiato, tasted so plain. There wasn't even a hint of caramel flavor. My friend's americano with caramel syrup tasted heavily of just sugar added to a crummy coffee. Low key made me wish I had just gotten the gas station coffee instead.   The baristas were nice but the drinks were not. Wish I would've tried the drinks before I left :-/
Starbucks  - Please Lord Jesus train your employees some customer service!!!

The baristas at the drive thru are so nice and courteous.  Alexander is especially nice and interacts with the customers. We are not just a voice on the intercom. And the coffee always come out as requested.

I yelped the hours as me and my girls were looking for a late night snack and it said 10:00 pm. I got to the drive through at 9:53 and was told that they closed at 9:30 pm for the night! How disappointing that we were interested in driving a ways to get there by 10:00 pm and they HAPPILY said they were closed REPEATEDLY

Absolute worst service ever for a Starbucks.  Ordered drinks and cake pops and waited in the drive thru for 15 minutes before even paying. Lady working drive thru window was very short and rude with us.

Typical Starbucks here... busy time, and still friendly and efficient.   I'm sure you all know the pricing... same as every Starbucks.   Exactly as we wxpected... which is good... nothing at all to complain about. but nothing to really cheer about either.
I was really excited to see Starbucks come to Hermitage.  My only gripe is that it's just a bit small inside - just a few tables.  All in all, though, your average Starbucks - close & convenient.

Abysmal service. Will never go here again. They are inside the Sheraton Hotel and apparently feel that is all they need to get a steady stream of customers. There are a lot of other coffee shops in the area.

Small location located in a small Webster Groves shopping plaza. Surprisingly parking is a breeze and the café is easily accessible. There's minimum seating inside and outside on the sidewalk patio. Plenty to explore within walking distance in case you'd like to park & stroll.   Staff is very pleasant.  ~Christy
Loving the feel of this Starbucks!!!! Always clean and a great "group" table if you want to come with friends or study groups....
Quick 24hr caffeine stop if you need a break from the war on I4 between Tampa and Orlando.

I visited this Starbucks on 9-3-2013 and don't think I would recommend it to anyone else.  The tables were dirty. The windows hadn't been cleaned and there were dead flies on the window sill.  Not a good first impression.
Don't wait in the rediculous line at this store,  order on the mobile app and you'll be in and out, just about as fast as drive thru. SB coffee is good every time though.

Best breakfast sandwiches in the world! I know there are a million Starbucks in Indianapolis but I always go to this one just for one reason , Rowan. Rowan is the manager at this location . I had an issue and he immediately took care of it . Amazing customer service ! Also super fast drive thru!

A one star rating is too generous!   This should be a -5 to truly capture the negative experience. The quality of staff has gone down hill significantly over the past few years.   On a recent visit, I politely asked for a straw since there were no straws in the bin.  The staff person didn't make eye contact and didn't say a word.  He went to the back, got a bag of straws and literally threw it across the counter with an indifferent glance.   I don't need to pay $5 a drink to be treated this way.

Starbucks is overall a great spot because you can not only grab coffee but you can order Frapp, hot chocolates, lemonade, food items plus utilize Wifi!!!! Plus if you have a Starbucks card you can obtain points for each of your purchased items.

I keep trying the drive-through but so far I have only ever received an incorrect drink order (it has been 5+ times now). I am not sure why this is I have never had a consistent problem at any starbucks and they don't' even offer to correct it either.  That being said, this is just one of those starbucks that don't know how to handle the rush and if its not too busy and you go inside they are friendly and nice and get your drink right...just stay away from the drive-through! I keep thinking it might get better and try every once in awhile but it has literally been a bad experience every time.

Let me first say that I have not yet been to a Starbucks that wasn't super busy but this one is ridiculous.   However,  it is to be expected because of it's close proximity to the Westgate Brandon Mall, the AMC movie theater and the I-75 exit.  When they first opened they were extremely slow getting orders out, especially for the drive thru.   I thought I might run out of gas while waiting.  They have gotten a little better with time but still too slow.   Keep in mind the location if you plan to go to this one.   You WILL wait.
This location is a convenient alternative to the more crowded one a little further down, sans drive-thru.

My drive experience was super fast which I like but fast isn't always good cause I was given the wrong drink plus was overcharged. Very disappointing experience for my first time here. By the time I got on the vet and took my first sip it was too late to turn back around after paying for toll. Oh well my lost right!

I work at the hospital across the street and this is my go to for morning coffee! The employees are all SUPER friendly and take the time to make each interaction personable! My coffee always hits the spot and gets me through my long days!

Stopped in at 11:30a. Not busy. Quick service by pleasant staff. This is a small store with strictly table & chair seating (no sitting area) serving the reliably consistent high quality Starbucks products. As I'm watching my diet, I had The very good Cold Brew iced coffee and low fat turkey and egg white sandwich.  I was very happy with both.

The Century Park Starbucks is a nice stop if you take public transit and need to kill time in between trips however keep an eye on the time. This Starbucks is located near the Century Park Bus Depot and LRT Station. I would choose this Starbucks over the Snack Bar that is located inside the Bus depot.   I have visited this location a lot and I have to say the staff are friendly The Baristas excel at their drink making. The staff are always polite and usually every employee will say hello.  If you ever need a drink suggestion they usually have good ideas however, I usually stick to my "Usual" choice of beverages. They have yet to get it made wrong. They usually have a good selection of their baked goods no matter what time of the day which is nice.   The drive thru is always quick and easy. The inside is the typical Starbucks decor and merchandise but that's not a bad thing. The downside to this location is the parking. There are only a few spots to park, There are nearby lots but sometimes walking isn't ideal so that's why I tend to stick to the Drive thru. I shop at the nearby Sobeys so I'll end up stopping by as a treat.
STARBUCKS!!! caramel bruleé is delicious! Too bad its not on their regular menu.
Love this location! I make sure to come here every morning when I'm in town. Lex is an awesome barista!!
What can I say I am a sucker for a good latte. Today it was on ice. This is a new Starbucks. Staff were friendly.

Gross. Every trashcan was overflowing both outside the restaurant and inside the restaurant. Bathrooms included. Saw several workers walk by and even walk by by with empty trash cans (returning them to the dumpster area). I guess they have just given up?

I came to this Starbucks this morning and was trying to find the cheapest coffee I could so I could go to work 30 minutes later. The lady behind the counter saw I was kinda off and asked if she could make me a drink of her own. I agreed, 2 shots of espresso 2 pumps of vanilla an 1 pump of hezelnut and I filled the rest of the cup with creme. I wish I remembered her name,, she made me the best coffee ever.

This is the second time I have been to this Starbucks and ordered a Vanilla Cream Cold Brew which tastes like it has no vanilla at all. This is always my go to drink in the morning and have never had issues when ordering it anywhere else.

Gotta love Starbucks!! Unfortunately, this particular location is always so busy that every order takes an eternity to get!! And when you need your coffee fix the last thing you want to have to do is wait. When we came through the drive-thru with a fairly simple order of a couple of coffees and a protein pack, it took a good 15 minutes minimum from the time we got in the Drive-Thru line to the time that we got our coffees.  It won't make me stop using Starbucks considering they have the best coffee EVER, but it might deter me from stopping at this particular location again in the future.

I visit this Starbucks often. Too often. It's a problem, actually. But I digress. Nine times out of ten I'm happy with my drink but I do agree with what someone else said about getting a different result depending on who's working. Some don't understand that cream and sugar does not mean the coffee should be pure white. For this reason, I've started ordering "light cream" to be safe. The parking situation is horrendous and very poorly planned considering the location but it usually doesn't take more than a few minutes for a spot to open up (which, by the way, is always the fastest way to go as the drive thru is always backed up). The employees do a great job of getting people in and out which is very necessary given the terrible design of the place. I keep coming back again and again so no complaints here.

I stopped by at this Starbucks location while on the go to work. They are stationed right next to the Kroger in Belle Meade making it hard to miss, great parking right out front.  I like to be adventurous in my Starbucks order and went with the nitro cold brew with sweet cream...it wasn't necessarily my favorite. It wasn't hot or cold, just a weird in between, and didn't have much flavor either. The egg bites were also just okay, but didn't taste very fresh.  It was fairly clean, and there were plenty of members on staff to get speedy service. There were plenty of tables and seats available making it great to get work done or just hang out at.

This used to be my favorite location , but it seems like management may have changed. The employees don't seem to have that same personable feel like those in the past. I miss seeing the same people I did for years that actually knew me would actually ask how I am and we would laugh and chat while waiting for my drink. Now it's pretty much in and out. I know no one nor do I think the people there really will take time to get to know me. Otherwise , it's clean and in a good location.

I been here a few times in the past ordering coffee and sandwiches when I am in the area. Always busy here during the mid lunch/afternoon drive times during the drive thru and at times inside as well. Keep in mind parking places are hide to find so people do tend to park over at Mcdonalds to walk over to Starbucks.  I like the Vinte Mochafrappachino and my mother likes the White Chocolate Mocha hot. I wish they still had the Turkey & Swiss sandwich but they quit carrying it awhile back don't know why it was delicious!  If you decide to come to this location try come early cause this location for Starbucks really does get busy in the parking lot/drive thru causing it and some what inside as well.

Not too long of a wait and the baristas were friendly for the most part. My hazelnut frappucino was perfect, added a shot of espresso which they didn't let die and put the right amount of torani pumps in it. They serve great drinks here.

My happy place. Parking is terrible & it is hard to get a comfy chair, but every customer is treated like royalty. It is crowded often throughout the day, but you will always get a perfect drink. If you aren't 100% satisfied, they'll make you another. Justin is a fabulous barista! He's never too busy to greet you by name & to make suggestions when you want a change.  Please don't come to Yardley's Starbucks! Enough people already & if you do, don't you dare park in the handicapped spot of you don't have a tag. There are only 5 spots & many of us will call the police to move you!  I should mention the regulars all look out for each other. It's an office & second home!
The slowest service ever. I've lived close to this starbucks for a few years and always regret going. Never changed, always slow.

I'm usually don't use this Starbucks for my coffee needs, but I was in the area and couldn't wait. I definitely love that they have their patio with many tables and chairs. As I walked in the person at register greeted me, and asked if he could be of any help.  I wasn't sure so I let people behind me go first.  (Always hard to decide if I want to indulge in a Frappuccino or a plain ice coffee).  I was food and just ordered my plain coffee with 2 pumps of sugar free syrup.  The were super busy as I saw their order pickup tray full, but amazingly my drink came out super quick.  They did have two baristas working the back making drinks, one refilling the things and the back, and then the other person at register.  Love, love love their efficiency, many time other Starbucks just take forever to make a simple drink.  I with this Starbucks was closer to where I live!!

I am a fan of this Starbucks location. Friendly staff, quick service, and good merch options in the storefront. My order is always delivered correctly and with a smile. There are spots in the inside to sit and they also have a nice prop area with umbrellas.   I don't think a Starbucks without a drive thru can really earn the 5 stars (sorry!), but that's my only negative about this location.
The line here is way too long and the way the line is lined up is not efficient at all. Just asking for grumpy customers..
Don't let the long line fool you in the morning. These cats are fast! Seriously, a line of cars that wraps around the building takes less than 5 minutes to clear. Very impressive.

This Starbucks is located inside of the Sheraton New Orleans. If your in town for a convention its a great spot to grab coffee and a snack. The downside is they are very slow at making drinks and the line usually has at least 15 people in it. I am not exaggerating.  If your not staying at the hotel I would suggest you go down the street to PJ's Coffee for a quick morning pickup. I've been going to this Starbucks for three years because I've attend a conference there all three years. This Starbucks is here strictly for convenience.

Silly, sassy and irish? Are you sure you're at starbucks? Yes, this place is interesting because of the staff. I get a giggle every time I'm in. There is a man who works the drive through who wears a kilt and asks "how can we caffeinate you today"?  I do enjoy the high tops to chill and almost always have to park in the back behind the building next door because it's always hopping. It's busy but you can usually find a place to sit. I've skyped here and had meetings as well as just relaxed.   I even once overheard someone complain about their order and the wait staff took care of them immediately making sure they felt taken care of.   This is my favorite starbucks in the area though I almost always miss it because it's blocked by trees and hard to see. Oops!

i know it's something small but the smallest things are what makes everyone's drinks their own. i also just think it's ridiculous the lack of effort this was. how hard is it to just give people the extra stuff they want???

The baristas here are awesome. Shout out to Danielle for remembering I splurge on Fridays and get a PSL, lol. Probably the nicest staff, the store is always clean and the drive thru moves so quickly! Love this location!!
Great spot in the heart of whyte to enjoy a coffee. Great outside patio as well for people watching

Great location. Staff very friendly. I wish I could remember the name of the young man who took my order. He had red hair and was outgoing and had a nice friendly smile. Lauren prepared my drink and it was yummy! I hate seeing the reviews complaining about the clientele, It was a beautiful day and I sat outside with a homeless man. Instead of complaining sit down and engage.
This is a super normal Starbucks. Nothing really pro or con except he outdoor seating is quite nice.
Super busy on Sunday morning, but with the usual efficient Starbucks quality of service, it wasn't a terrific problem. Getting in and out of the parking lot, however, leaves a lot to be desired.

Teams cans all overflowing ... Six workers... I was the only one in line. Told them about the trash and 10 minutes later, with no additional customers... Trash still overflowing. One trash can was directly in the view of the counter staff. Skip this place, find another coffee shop.
Staff is extra friendly and helpful. Two grades of decaf is also extra nice. Newer, clean location in the shopping center. Don't drive fast you go right past it.

The staff at this Starbucks is very unfriendly and unprofessional. They made my drink wrong and would not offer to remake it. They discuss difficult customers loudly in front of the store. Would not recommend.

I went through the drivethru and was service by Ross! So I can only speak on my drivethru experience. I was promptly greeted by the server on the other end. He was polite, well spoken and greeted me with energy and a genuine friendliness. He repeated my order back to me. Upon pulling up to the window he greeted me again with a smile in person. My food was ready and I was given napkins without asking. After my transaction was complete, he wished me well and hoped I had a pleasant day. You don't find great genuine customer service very often, so I want to give kudos where it's deserved! Especially since I'm a former Starbucks employee ...I'm a little more critical.
I ordered a coffee and a scone to go, after 20 minutes I still did not have my order. Needed to cancel. This is probably the worst Starbucks I've ever been in.

I work down the street from this Starbucks and come here somewhat often. It's always pleasant, fast enough, and they make the coffee exactly how I ask :) I'm actually writing this review though because I want to specifically sing the praises of Ali, who made my day last week!!! It had been a really rough and stressful week and sleep was lacking. I drove by here to get an afternoon coffee so I could make It through the rest of my to do list. After I ordered my coffee and was waiting in line, I looked into my purse only to discover that I had left my wallet at home! I thought I just might lose it considering the week I had had, this was just the cherry on top of things! I knew when I got up to the from they would probably roll their eyes and be frustrated that they had made my coffee and now I could not pay for it. When I got up to the window I told Ali that unfortunately I would have to come back because I had left my wallet behind. She was so sweet about the whole thing! She told me that I could go ahead and take the coffee, and I could just get them next time. I feel like this is so rare in big chain places these days, and there was no reason whatsoever that she had to do that, she just simply went out of her way to do something nice and she honestly made my day! Starbucks is lucky to have Ali :)

This store has been slipping for a long time but I feel like I need to write a review with my terrible experience today. I spent literally not exaggerating 20 minutes in the drive-through waiting to get to the window. She took my credit card but did not tell me the total and handed the card back to me with no receipt.
