I know this is the modern age and a lot of people pay with their phones but I don't and I expect to be told the total and be given a receipt which they did not do. I finally had to ask for the total. When I drove away I realized that the drink was made incorrectly. Am I going to go back and get another one? No, I don't want to wait in line again.  The store needs some serious up training and some help in the organization.

So I'm doing the KETO thang! And what other way to try to stick to a meal plan Than buy trying different sugar free drinks! I've tried it several locations and this location was one of the worst at making it look like diet crap! They did not mix the heavy cream and it made it look like spoiled milk was added to my drink! I asked the barista and she brushed me off by saying that's what you ordered.   But did you have to make it look so nasty? Next visit they got it right!
This is the first time I have ever posted a negative review, but this is the worst...Starbucks....ever. Extremely slow service, rude and incompetent employees.
Terrible service - SLOW!!!  Unless you're retired or on vacation, not a good idea.  Not up to Starbucks standards.

Now, I'm going to seem like a hypocrite in this review if you take a look at my Starbucks review for the location in Creve Couer. My issues with the Creve Couer location had to do with not having a larger food selection and not having a drive thru...this U City location has both and I'm still only giving it 3 stars.   While you can get a wide variety of breakfast and lunch foods at this location, the convenience of the drive-thru is negated by the slowness of the service. This Starbucks location is super near to my apartment so I've visited a lot at all times of the day and different days of the week...and it's consistently slow. The staff is friendly (as usual) but I've happened upon what seem to be an inordinate number of "newbies" at this location. That is, a lot of the employees at this location seem to be unfamiliar with their products.   If you wanted to go inside, the parking lot is very small and the location (at the intersection of North & South and Delmar) makes for some problematic driving. When the drive-thru line is packed, that entire intersection becomes a hazard...seriously.   Still, when I need reliably consistent coffee and I want to get it without parking and going in somewhere, I usually go here.

Perhaps I'm lucky because I've never had a subpar experience at this Starbucks location inside Target.    Compared to Starbucks everywhere else, this one always has the shortest line (at least whenever I show up). It's conveniently located at the grocery entrance.   My beverage orders are always made to perfection. The staff keeps the short lines moving, and orders are ready in a timely manner.   Always a great way to start a shopping spree!

I still have some Starbucks gift cards in my account from Christmas so chose to come work here for a few hours this morning. Tons of table and workspace. By very busy inside the store on a Saturday morning. Nice and clean and everything you'd expect on the menu at Starbucks.

Every time I travel, I make sure to make a pit stop here. They always have a smile on their face and thank God I've never had to wait long for my drink. This is of course your typical Starbucks so it has everything all the other ones do. This one is clean and their bathrooms are clean too. Get your joe here!

This location is hard to get to and when you are inside, it is small, packed and very loud.  I was half way finished with my heated sandwich when my coffee finally arrived.  If you can avoid this Starbucks and go to one of the others around here you will be better off.  Not to mention that when I sat at the dirty counter and asked for it to be cleaned, yes she wipped the counter but it was so wet that I had to ask for a towel to dry it which I had to do myself.  Go to the one on Roger Rd , better service and clean.

Waited over 10 minutes for a grande White Mocha! And then two people that came after me got their drinks first! The guy making the drinks saw me there and didn't even bother to ask what I was waiting for. The cashier that rang me up finally asked what I was waiting for. Worst experience with Starbucks!
Very rude to me and my friends when asked for water. Told us we suck and then ignored our order when people who ordered after us were getting their coffees. Will not go back

They were out of so many things. We had to change our order and then we had to change it once more when we drove up to the next window because they discovered they were out of that as well.....but at least the woman was nice about the situation and apologized. I wouldn't hesitate to return here since this was my first time.

While the drive-thru can be slow (generally the people placing orders), the staff is awesome! Very friendly & helpful & generally quick with an order. Seating indoors & out is limited, but still enough most of the time. Can get a bit noisy because of limited space, but not too bad.
Always busy, and usually pretty quick service. The parking lot is too small, but that's not their fault. Thank goodness for drive-through!

Holy Macanoli!!  This is easily the busiest Starbucks I've ever seen. It's small and the line is backed up to the door.  The drive thru is 10 cars deep.  There are 8 baristas with well differentiated task priorities who fill orders remarkably efficiently.  Though there are easily 6-8 customers awaiting orders at the pick up counter for in store and on line orders, it is not a long wait.  I'm impressed. Quality is the standard I've come to expect from the Starbucks brand. Well done!

Staying in the hotel. Decided to come down and grab a cup of coffee and they closed early. Today I came down for breakfast and they don't have oatmeal "today". I'm not surprised at the overall rating of this location.
Great service and a great atmosphere to study. This is a busy location but they are quick and get you through the line fast!

Arrived this morning around 7:45 am and was able to walk right up to the counter to order. The place was packed! Lots of people waiting for their specialty drinks. But ordering a plain coffee is super easy and quick! Parking was easy to find as they have a lot out front. Lots of baristas working behind the counter and they clearly have specific people working on food orders too! This Starbucks knows how to handle a crowd!

The place is fantastic! I went here two days in a row while at a course. It is super busy and everyone packs into the Starbucks. But let me tell you... The staff are never phased by the big line and by the time you get to the front there as chipper and happy as ever. I couldn't think of a place more busy and so full with that pleasent of staff. If only Walmart was this good of an experience I might actually go to Walmart.  As for my coffee, tea desert. It's the exact same as every Starbucks I go to. Nothing over the top special but the service is fantastic.

This is cool.  Great windows open on the world around.  This is a very down in the city sort of place to get your coffee.  And not only do they have all the standard Starbucks stuff.  But they have the clover here as well.  I've not seen it anywhere else in Reno.  There are five different coffees available on this special brew process.  Don't know much about it...but I guess it's like a french press and a vacuum put together.  My coffee was rich and dark.   Just like I like my..... ANYWAY...  The parking here seems a bit bothersome but there are still a decent number of spots.  And lastly...they have glass mugs!  I usually skip starbucks for the other places in town because I'm not a fan of coffee in plastic.  Cool they have the mugs!  I will be trying a cap the next time I'm in!

I love Starbucks. I'm loyal to them, and have been going there for years. I have a gold card, and am quite addicted!  I'm pretty picky when it comes to location. I've determined that this one is my favorite! There are two newer people who always screw up my order, but it's rectified, and I leave with a great drink. I usually get the same drink, and this location makes it the best. Sometimes I go in the morning before class, and everyone is so friendly! I'll be honest, I'm not a tipper, but the baristas in the morning win me over every time!

Everyone was so rude here. I work at a Starbucks and if I acted the way they did I would have been fired a long time ago! It was only 9:30 am and no one was in there...it was like we were bothering them by being there.

By far the best Starbucks in the area.  Great staff, clean store, consistently accurate with orders.  Truly a team willing to go the extra mile for their customers.  My mom means the world to me and they treat her like royalty, so when someone asks which starbucks to go to (this or the other on Germantown Pike across from Shoprite) my answer is always this one.
Love this place! I come here to study all the time and the batista makes the drinks great. Im closer to the horsham branch but they're awful at what they do.... I drive a little more to come here
Bacon, egg, and cheese, grande Carmel frapp, and a cake pop... No better way to start your morning. And since I'm a regular they always have more order down and ready to go!

This store is one of the best in Santa Barbara! I stopped in on the way to work, thinking that I'd be late anyway. I almost forgot about my terrible morning by the time I left. I got my mocha fast and the employees were very friendly and worked like a pit crew. Will definitely be back!

The baristas at this starbucks are so awesome!!! People complain about the wait time but really people do you think it's always their fault? It's our choice to go through a drive through when the line is down the street don't be surprised when you wait for 10 minutes.
应该是所有住delmar的Wash U的留学生必去的地方了，国际连锁，大多东西都是标准化，即使看不懂菜单也能猜到，早餐的鸡蛋火腿汉堡不错。
Best baristas around! They remember you and your usual. Seating is a bit crowded (especially if the Chapters isn't open) and if you sit by the windows in winter keep your sweater handy!
I love this Starbucks! The employees are really nice, and my drinks always come out quick and correct! The same crew works pretty much every day and they are very friendly!

Not sure why so many bad reviews.  I've been 3 times in the last 3 days and don't have a single complaint. Seen pretty consistent to me. The hot mocha is my favorite and I drink it daily now. I tried the slow roasted ham breakfast croissant and I was pleasantly surprised.  It was very good for a coffee shop. I've enjoyed the service and even with long lines I've gotten in and out of the drive thru quickly. I do remember telling the person to give me a minute and she said hey no worries I'm here til noon. Lol made me feel comfortable with looking for a second.

I had the best experience during my week long stay during Mardi Gras. The man in charge was so thoughtful, and patient while training a very new employee.  I found out on my last day that he was Krew Zulu. Can't wait to return.

This place always seems packed probably because it's the only one near University street on campus. I ordered my drink ahead on the app and it was not ready at the predicted time. All the seats were full so I had to take my drink to go.

This is my favorite of all the St. Louis and Chicago Starbs that I've ever been to! I work and live fairly close by so I come here several times a week - sometimes just to stop by for a quick drink and sometimes for hours-long major study sessions.  The staff here is AWESOME! Super fast and very friendly. Can't ask for anything more. Several times I've stayed there studying right up until they close at 9, and they're still totally friendly and not at all "please leave." Love it.
Amy was completely rude to me and the lady in front of me. If I didnt live so close I wouldnt come back. Other ladies were nice except Amy.

I usually go to the Starbucks in Montecito, but stopped here to avoid traffic on Coast Village Rd one afternoon.  I know they don't serve bold after noon, so I asked them to "pour it over".  Unfortunately, this took 20 minutes and was not really hot when they handed it to me.  Strike 1.  The barista informed me they don't sell the double choc. chip brownies at this location because they don't sell well.  I guess that means that I am not the typical Carpinteria customer... so I went to the donut shop across the street and bought my brownie.    I would avoid this location...  the Montecito Starbucks is by far better and the help is much more helpful with hot coffee.
The drinks are good but 15 minutes in the drive through? No thanks. They are SLOW. REALLY SLOW.

Hi !! I absolutely love this Starbucks! I used to grab a latte from here when I was working in Regency square earlier.... The staff is prompt and cordial and I would give five stars to this place ..our favorite coffee house..

This is not the go to place if your in a rush. This Starbucks is a bit snail speed slow. If I didn't work so close I probably wouldn't be quite the frequent flyer in here. The staff is really friendly & they do a good job keeping everything clean. There are tables if you need somewhere to sit and enjoy your coffee for awhile. They do a good job keeping everything looking good. My one major gripe is when you order a breakfast sandwich & a coffee. You are basically waiting for your coffee then your basically walking back to the cashier area for your sandwich which really just disrupts the flow especially when things are busy. Everyone is kinda staring at you like "What are you doing?" when you walk back over and get your sandwich & I have heard people mutter "How inconvenient."  Other then that this place is A-Ok.

This is the only Starbucks with a drive-thru that is legitimately on my way to work and in close proximity of Highway Farty. I have stopped here several times over the last few weeks to feed my new obsession with the fall Chile Mocha Latte. Let's not even talk about how good these things are. I used to boycott this Starbucks because it took over the Grand Del Taco, one of the most enjoyable memories of my misspent mind altered late night adolescence. The Chile Mocha Latte lured me here. It's not my fault!
I was blown away by how friendly the Team was. The store is super clean. Tommy. Adorable. And amazing customer service.  The Barista Alexandra reminded me of the good ole days. Awesome.

Tried to make sure they had the iced tea I wanted before I ordered it.   They said they did.  Charged me.  I waited and waited.  They announce they are out of it.  Offered me something else, but I don't like the alternative.  I asked for my money back and they did.  I also unfortunately paid a 26% tip on my app for the before getting the drink.  Mentioned that.   The gal with out even giving a darn that I didn't get ANYTHING from them except a wait, announces "can't give the tips back".  So note to self do not tip them assuming you'll get something before it actually comes unless you like tipping to wait

Vastly improved.  The staff has turned over and this is a much better Starbucks than it used to be.  I've been going regularly again and my order is always correct, the service is lightning fast and the employees are pleasant.

I love this Starbucks they have the best baristas ever at this location. I'm a regular here my mom works nights and am a college student so she'll get coffee for me in the morning and I'll do the same at night time we always go through the drivethru. 2 of our favorite baristas I want to give a shoutout/shine spotlight on 1) Carrie- she's there almost every morning nicest most cheerful person especially for being there so early 2) Andrea- fastest, perfectionist that always makes the drinks spot on and makes sure yur coffee is wiped off and lids on correctly which is always a plus All the baristas are amazing these 2 are just constants that we see and talk to on a regular bases that know us by name

This Starbucks is the where it's at Starbucks. It is smack dab in the middle of downtown St. Pete and has competition from three pretty close ones, but that doesn't seem to be a problem, for business is always booming.
Redeemed my Starbucks rewards here, got a chicken wrap box & a large strawberry açaí refresher. Delicious!

I don't go to the Starbucks very often but stopped by a couple times this week when i was in the area. I was able to order and move through the line pretty quickly during the morning rush around 7:30am. I ordered my favorite drink an iced caramel brulee latte and was devastated that they were out of that flavor! They offered me a toasted white chocolate mocha which i don't like white chocolate but i appreciated the suggestion. They had pumpkin which i settled on which was made well. I would definitely come back to this location when I'm in the area and it's a plus that they share a building with Chipotle if you're around during lunch.

I visited this location today. It was not really busy. For the 1st time in all my experiences I received my order and I did not have the labels on the cups, very small issues. But bigger issue is both my drinks were made wrong. My pink drink was just coconut milk and floating strawberries. My Carmel ribbon was on about 2 inches of drink. The rest was Whip cream. I tried to call 7 times . Of course the 1 time I didn't get a recipient is the time my order was wrong. Next time I will taste before I leave. I am sure this is not the normal.

Business was slow (due to the time of night) so service was pretty fast. Tastes delicious. No complaints. It was pretty late so I feel the barista's quiet/short demeanor is due to her being tired or something. Either way, super excited to get my caffeine fix at like...2100-2130.

Was really impressed with the cleanliness of the restaurant. The staff was nice however the bread choices were limited.the staff said they come already made which means processed food.so the one I wanted they were out and the bread I wanted only came on that one sandwich.so I settled on the vegi.sandwich which was quite delious.the bread was hard.

This is the slowest Starbucks I've ever seen.  The line in the drive through is often backed up almost to the interstate and I can't tell you how many times they have messed up my order.  Unfortunately it's the only Starbucks in Plant City so I have no choice but to suck it up and wait wait wait! I used to visit 3-4 times a week but now I barely make it twice a month.  If it weren't for the gift cards I receive I probably would never go!!

Today I ordered a venti Java chip frap with no chocolate sauce, add a shot, and caramel brûlée pieces. I also ordered an ultimate caramel frap. I get to the window and pay and get handed my ultimate caramel frap. Which has regular whipped cream and not the caramel one. Then my java chip one has no java chips, no whipped cream. The woman at the window is a nightmare, she asks is there something wrong? Rudely. I tell her I asked for whipped cream and there wasn't any and there's no java pieces but it's fine. She says "we are out of caramel pieces but you should know that". I tell her there are caramel pieces on it but it's fine. She says okay have a good day. Excuse me but shouldn't you be asking to fix it? Or apologize? Or idk don't say "you should know that"... when did I become a condescending  Starbucks employee.
I ordered my drink and they didn't make it correctly. Instead of remaking my drink in the size I ordered they gave me a smaller size.

Consistently the slowest Starbucks around. A simple coffee order will take at least 15 minutes, even when there are only a few people in line. Don't come in unless you have 30 minutes to stand in line. Otherwise, service is average.

Worst morning staff around 630am the guy in the morning is awful I won't be going back this is. Never gets my drink correct - can  he even smile? I don't think so! I refuse to stop at this location!!!!

Worst morning service ever. Rude! My drink is never correct and I have to end up going inside to get the correct one I paid for. The drive through line takes foreverrr. Won't be back I'll drive to Carmel to get my fix now

Drive thru lines were slow both days we went. Customer service was lacking & needs major improvement. Asked us how to make a drink on their menu... then proceeded to make it incorrectly anyways. Not impressed. Worst Starbucks I've ever visited.

The furniture looks like it came out of an old college dorm.  Not uncomfortable, worn out, uninviting.  Environment is cold and warehouse like.  WiFi doesn't work.  I ask Starbucks person and they say it doesn't work for them either.  Have you tried fixing it?  I ended up going to one on 96th street.  Much better.
Nice big and beautiful!!! The drive thru is sometimes a little hard to get to but no worries!! I love the set up, inside and out

Went through the drive-through asked for a Trenta unsweetened very little ice green tea with two Splenda. And what do I receive... A Trenta with extra ice green tea sweetened with sweeten low! Really!!! So then I went inside and asked the lady if they have Splenda and she said they do and they had to remake my whole drink

I ordered a cold brew w/ salted cold cream and instead received my cold brew with sweet cream. This has happened once before and chalked it up as just a mistake, but this is the second time! I suggest you always check your drink for accuracy before you leave the drive thru.

This has to be the slooooooowest experience ever. In the time I ordered and waited for my coffee my boyfriend manage to fill up his 35gallon tank of gas next door. I was able to take a trip to the ladies room and still I waited. And there was barely anyone in the store. Be advised to just go next door and get your coffee at Wawa's.

First and last time I'll go to this location. I opted to go in instead of going through the drive thru. It took 16 min to get my coffee and there was only one person in front of me. They clearly prioritize drive thru and mobile orders!

The location is outstanding and the staff is excellent. It is without question the best Starbucks location I have visited. Granted, I am not much of a coffee drinker and have only been to 4 or 5 locations in my entire life, but this store is at the top of the "World Class Customer Service" list.  I am known to have a plain bagel with cream cheese and a BTL (Arnold Palmer). The one time they made my drink wrong (because I was chatting up the young man and distracted him), they offered to remake my drink without hesitation. In fact, he caught his mistake as he was handing me the cup. I took the drink he offered and told him to not worry about it (I felt a little responsible, I think he was new and I was distracting him). They remembered the next time I came in and comped me my drink.  I know you have a lot of choices when buying coffee in Tucson, but I promise you will not be disappointed if you choose to visit this location. Not on product, customer service, or their commitment to our community.
Was told, "Can't do decaf through drive-thru." Out of pastries we asked for. Not the first time we've come here and they were out of stuff...both early morning and afternoon.

I went there today to use a card/coupon we received in the mail to visit "the newest downtown Starbucks location." The card says they are open until 6 pm on Sunday; however, when I went shortly after 5 pm they were closed. We then went on the Starbucks and Hilton websites to verify their hours and both also said 6 pm. Very disappointed and it made my decision on which Starbucks to go to a lot easier.

I definitely like this particular Starbucks better than most, thanks to its spacious seating and available outlets. At other places, you have to sell your first born child to get a seat with some electrical juice, but this location has a very LARGE table in the center area with outlets built in to the underside of the table....allowing anyone sitting there to be plugged in! There are also a couple more around the perimeter, but I always look forward to spreading out my study materials at the table.  The staff has always been super friendly and they make great new drink suggestions!

Ordered my latte with no foam and it had about 2 inches of foam. I had to get to work so I couldn't ask for it to be redone and had to just use napkins to scoop it all out. Definitely wasted money :/ I was a barista for ten years and always made sure to customize drinks for people because that's why the go to places like Starbucks

I am finally sitting down to write a review for this starbucks.  Sadly, it is by far the worst run starbucks I have been too.  First, I would like to start off by saying that literally the ONLY reason I go to this starbucks/continue to go is because there is a drive-thru.  and to be honest, their wait time in drive-thru is horrendous (you can even watch the counter at the window when you do step inside.)    Issues I have with this location are as follows: wrong food given undercooked food given long wait times overcooked food wrong drink order (had this numerous times) cold brew often off the menu (consistently for the last 4 sundays in a row I have gone.--someone is seriously messing up on saturday.) lack of organization/awareness of employee to employee and employee to customer.  I am not sure how long this post should be, because I have been going here for months and every time I think, "ugh I can't believe I spent my money at that location, AGAIN."  I have kids so it's hard because I don't want to have to get out of the car all the time (I know, first world problem) but if the area had ANY other coffee place with a drive-thru I would be going there instead.

I have always been astonished at how friendly Starbucks employees are.  No matter what time of day, what I order, they are always chipper and energetic.  I thought that these traits were required to work at Starbucks (unless you are in NYC, of course).  I came here at about 5:30 am the other day.  This is not my usual Starbucks but I was in that area and needed coffee.  The employees that I encountered seemed to be angry that I would dare to stop by for coffee and a bagel.  In fact, only when the gentleman gave me my coffee and I didn't drive away did he remember that I had ordered a bagel.  He then shouted to someone in the main-area that he needed a bagel, thus making my wait way longer than if he had put in the order when I ordered it.  I got no napkins with my bagel.  Perhaps I just came in on the wrong morning.

I come here 5 days a week, they have my order down. Everyone is usually pretty friendly, Farro is by far my favorite one there though. He's always friendly and he makes my drinks the best. Going there makes my mornings.

It's your standard Starbucks so the drinks are well-made, the baristas are nice and everything is very clean. I give 3 stars because the location is horrible. It's right across the boulevard from the mall, so technically it's a prime location but it get's way too busy and the lines don't move fast enough. The drive-thru gets especially bad both in the mornings and afternoon. The layout of the parking lot doesn't help the situation at all. It's very easy to block someone's car in while waiting in line. I would advise going inside instead of the drive-thru, and don't park anywhere near the drive-thru or you might get stuck when you try to leave.

Home of the coffeebots. Went here 30 days straight and ordered the same coffee each time (dopio for here).  Much of the time the cashier didn't realize there are porcelain cups for this order, so I ended up saying that each time.....over and over each day, or the barista would see the order tab, but put the coffee in a paper cup.  When I pointed it out, she simply poured the coffee into the porcelain cup leaving the crema behind. I've been to other places where they recognize me after a week or so, but here the bots are tuned to their headsets and even though they appear to look right at you, they don't see you.

One thing great about this Starbucks is that it opens at 5a. The staff are very nice and fast. I just tried the new Horchata Almond Milk Frappuccino. It was pretty tasty! My fave is still the Mocha Frappuccino.

Writing a review on Starbucks in general seems a bit useless- we all know it and have our own opinions of it.  This location specifically stands out because it has transformed a landmark building from a useless eyesore into a gorgeous, functioning gem.  A gem which provides tasty caffeine!!!   I am sad to say that my first experience here was not a good one.  I got a ham, egg and cheese breakfast sandwich.  The sandwich was still frozen on the inside.  I realize they don't have a kitchen there, but it was an unwelcome reminder that its basically the same thing as a microwave breakfast sandwich from the grocery store.  Ew.  I'll still go there for my caffeine fix.  A lot.

Terrible baristas. This place was a dumpy little corner off the street with a weird area feel. I wouldn't go in here again if I were dying for caffeine. I just can't believe that Starbucks hires workers like this. Boy have their standards fallen.
Nice Starbucks in a nice little plaza. Great staff, and fast! As a Starbucks should be!

I always have the best experience when coming to this location. I usually use the drive thru and the girls who take your order are just the nicest ever. They are always so sweet and friendly and cheerful and unpretentious. Service is usually fast and quality of food and drink is always on point. I highly recommend this location.

I so badly want to give this location a good review but I have visited too many times and been disappointed-have to say that this Starbucks has such a high turnover and maybe this accounts for their inability to make a consistently good drink, specifically their lattes. I really think it's the lack of training. Almost every other location makes their drinks consistently, but at this one, they either let the espresso shots sit too long ( causing a burnt taste) or fill it overflowing with milk (which dilutes the taste) or serves it lukewarm. They are also 5x slower than any of the other locations in town. Whatever it is they are doing, more times than not, I walk away frustrated and vowing never to go there again.

I think this is pretty sad honestly this has happened the last 3 times and it finally got to the point where I felt the need to post it on here (it won't let me attach the drink where it isn't even close to full, so I uploaded it to the photos)
Ordered Cool Lime refresher, and got almost like water. It only has a hint of lime flavor and full of ice and water. Don't go this location.

I have never had such rude service at a Starbucks before. Quite shocking for the area of Nashville. I walked in used the restroom and went to the counter where I was the only person in line and waited while being ignore for at least 4 minutes while the lady carried on a conversation with her co worker all while Counting her tips. Once she finally finished and announced her grand total of tips she finally recognized that I was standing there patiently waiting to order. She was not very friendly nor apologetic for the wait.   I order my drinks and my brothers (all the same drinks) and once received walked outside and took a sip of my drink and recognized that it didn't taste like it normally does. So I walked back into the store where the lady who made the drink basically told me that it's not normally a sweet drink and do I want her to add sweetener? My brother also at this time tried his drink and verified his wasn't sweet either and came in behind me as I'm speaking to the lady. The other lady jumps into the conversation and basically says that his normal Starbucks is not making his drink following the recipe. So they are not normally sweet. However they can add vanilla sweetener to our drinks if we would like. Instead of arguing with them we accepted the vanilla sweetener and went on our way.   I felt this duo are just lazy and argumentative and did not want to remake the drinks properly.

I met up with a friend for coffee on about a week ago and hadn't been to Starbucks in a while, so we met here. It was busy, but not too loud inside. There was no line up, which was nice and we got our coffees in a timely fashion. There were a lot of tables and places to sit to enjoy your afternoon. The latte I had was delicious and very well made. I didn't add anything different or extra into it, and sometimes that can make it very strong tasting, but this one was different. I was very happy with it. After about an hour of sitting chatting, one of the baristas came around offering to try some of their banana bread. She was very polite and made sure she didn't really interrupt anyone if they were talking. The banana bread was very moist and very flavorful. I would definitely hit up this location again, especially in the summer/fall when it's nice out as the patio looks very nice as well.
On a recent visit smelled strongly of chemicals. Frappucinos were watery. Pastries were ok.

This is one if not one of the best Starbucks that I go to. They know you by name if you are a regular and just overall friendly staff. They take the time to make sure that your order is right and are overall satisfied. They are prompt and precise and can tell that they have fun and enjoy what they do!

Went there for a check of the view and coffee as we live 2.5 miles south. Good service , not exemplary but exceptional scenery. No problems so far and lacks some of the sometimes over the line wandering folks at rhe Kietze store.  Will be back.
Was here for Scentsy convention . A plus service. Bought a pastry and it was moldy! Yuck! But The were mortified and Made it better.. Always Called me by name

Stupid me..   Why did I go to this location...  It's always given me problems...  Even the wife said, "I thought you were going to the other one..."  Our breakfast plans fell through, we needed coffee and we were headed home.  We decided to use up a starbucks gift card and we ordered coffee and sandwiches.  Impressively, the sandwiches were done in seconds and we had them before we even paid.  Then we waited for over seven minutes and the sandwiches were cold by the time we ate them...   We got our drinks and went home to eat.  While drinking our drinks in the car, we noticed both of our drinks were wrong.  The cashier wrote JCF, for Java Chip Frappe on our drinks, but the person making them thought the J was a V I think...  Both of our drinks lacked Java, but were completely vanilla beaned up...    So why didn't we go back to fix the error?  Well, this location and parking lot is the absolute worst.  Once we were driving down Germantown, we would need to make an illegal u-turn, then go to the mall to turn around, just to get back here.  With the traffic already building, it would add twenty minutes to our trip.  Forget it...    As a side note, I did complain online to Starbucks and they gave me $15 back.  We spent slightly over $20, but I'm satisfied with that resolution.  We won't spend that $15 at this location.  Karma once again for not listening to myself or you other yelpers...

If this were your first experience of the Starbucks franchise, you would walk away neither overly impressed nor depressed.  For those of us who've easily been in dozens of them, the feeling is about the same. In our particular case this week, it was a necessary evil -- we were trying to locate yet another tourist attraction (this sounds like complaining, but trust me, we were having a fabulous time) and the iPhone and its handy GPS were nearly dead, so we ducked into Starbucks for a drink and a good long battery charge.   I'd never had a Starbucks smoothie before and I'll never have one again. It was just not like a 'real' smoothie -- no fruit chunks, no bold fruit flavors, nothin' interesting, and a strong preservative taste to seal the deal. But chances are I'd have said the same thing if I'd gone to any other Starbucks nationwide, so don't take that the wrong way.   The girls behind the counter were LOUD and goofy, but at least they were having fun at work. Plenty of parking out front.

A small Starbucks inside the Sheraton Hotel. It's the closest Starbucky near where we were staying on Bourbon St.  It has a limited amt of tables. The register person was very nice.  All I wanted was a Starbucks mug so can't comment on how they make their coffee.

Never had poor customer service from any other Starbucks. One of the employees who took the order was very impatient. And whoever made the drinks did not know how too. Went back to redo the order and they argued that they were right and that the person who made them was a manager. Horrible!

Very green apron! Nothing to complain about here.  Partners moved fast and were always friendly despite the sour faced rude customers who ordered in-between text messages and ongoing phone conversations.   For those who find their store too small and the their service too slow, trying getting off the phone and look the person behind the counter in the eye when ordering and stop treating them like a barely tolerable app on your phone.

Used to be a great location to come anytime to study - service was great and atmosphere was enjoyable. Not sure what happened because on what you think would be an easy Friday morning at the coffee shop, there is no music in the store, the employees' conversation from behind the counter fills the whole place instead, and they're mopping at lunchtime! Time for a change. #makestarbucksgreatagain

Surprisingly when I walked in there was a couple in front of me ordering. There is normally a long line when I come in or go through the drive-thru.   Today when I went to order the barista was not friendly at all. I usually have a great experience when I go to starbucks. This woman didn't say anything until I ordered a croissant and she asked warmed? Then she asked for my name.  This was the worse experience I have ever had. They are usually very friendly. Even the drive through talks to me more.   I will continue coming to this starbucks due to the fact that there is a drive through and makes it convenient.

Overall, friendly people and of course my drink was great. I would have given them a 4 star rating, however the drink/dining area was dirty. The tables needed to be wiped off and the floor swept.  It wasn't as clean as some of the other Starbucks, but the friendly people made the visit worthwhile.

Ugh. This Starbucks is a mess in a lot of ways. The parking lot was so poorly designed, it already creates a plethora of issues for this franchise. The workers work at their own pace. They don't care what the customers think, they just work on their own clock. They are accurate with the order, but how hard is it to take a coffee order? Not hard. If you're brand loyal it doesn't matter but if you just like a good cup go to the Buddy's Brew down the street. Always a zoo here.

This place provides the standard Starbucks high quality product served efficiently by pleasant barista staff.  I love the comfy outdoor couches (weatherproof plastic of course). That said, I find the inside small and cramped with only high top seating except for a single lower table. Not welcoming atmosphere since the remodel necessitated by the closing of the bank branch with whom they shared a large lobby with confer station areas and work/conference tables.  I go there on nice days and for drive through. Hate the interior.

When you have to work all day and then come here and visit sick family members, you sometimes need a pick me up. My mom was in the hospital for 5 weeks and this Starbucks was located inside. I was working 45-50 hour weeks, then needed to come here everyday and there were many days I needed something to wake me up some. This Starbuck is the average Starbucks, nothing different than any other one. Although they did not ever get my order wrong. It's just such a tight spot to order and pick up your order. There is something about tight facilities, and parking lots that Starbucks just seems to have to have.

Literally the slowest Starbucks I have ever been to in my entire life AND THEY WEREN'T EVEN BUSY!!! There were four freaking baristas working, two people ahead of me in line (one didn't order a drink, just a slice of lemon pound cake) and it still took 20 full minutes to get my tall iced coffee which literally requires no fancy mixing. You just pour the coffee from the pitcher and some cream and viola! I almost asked if I could make it may damn self at one point. So frustrating, every time I come here it's slow. I have to remind myself to not come to this one.
I still and will always say Starbucks is overpriced and over rated, but the coffee is still good.  The people that work here care about their jobs and are super nice.

I am always blown away when people I barely know remember me. Our cashier not only remembered me, but asked me if I still had partner numbers from the very short time she knew me as a barista. We chatted a little while as she rang up my order for a green tea lemonade and when the barista on bar handed me my drink, it was a green tea latte (the cup markings are super close). I actually would've rather had a green tea latte -- I just didn't want to pay for it. The tea lemonades are cheaper. Both baristas apologized over and over and offered to remake it, but insisted it was fine. More than fine, as I said earlier. They personified what it means to be a Starbucks partner. Sure, they messed up, but they took the appropriate steps to correct it and I left a very happy customer :)

This starbucks sucks butt I'll give it to them because they are very friendly.   They are constantly messing up my drink order BUTT they offer to fix it with no problem.  They are also pretty slow... Don't ever go in when they have a long line.
Coffee is good as expected. One problem though the drive thru is atrocious! Every time I come I sit in this god forsaken line for 30 mins. I think I have a problem.

Continues to worsen.  Extremely slow anytime of day.  I've stopped coming to Starbucks because the standards have dropped on quality and service.  If they had any competition in this part of town, this place would close down.

This is one of the easier locations to get to except during morning hours. Go after 1030am. The drinks still do not come out consistent. I came here for 3 days straight and ordered the same green tea frap.. all tasted different.
Awesome service. I always am greeted with friendly individuals. Watch out for the parking lot which can be a cluster-f*** in the morning.
Ok service , really nice layout. Only one creamer out and it was not labeled. At the end of the day it's a coffee shop and served it's purpose.
On line says it is open until 11p. All signs are lighted. Pull up at 10:08 and closed. . Disappointing at least turn odd Drive thru sign and menu board and update in-line hours.

This is generally a great location.  I stop in whenever I'm in the area and its right down the street from my salon.    My drink is always perfect and the customer service here is great.  The employees are really friendly and relatively speedy for a Starbucks.   The only downside to this place is that there is only street parking (or a short walk from the city lot) and you'll have to resist rolling your eyes at some of the snooty customers (but that's just Newtown).

Drive thru line can get really long, but they move pretty fast.   Surprisingly easy to pull in and out of the parking lot (if you're making a right on Grand, that is).   Drinks are inconsistent - why isn't me venti latte filled up all the way? why do sometimes I get that little green spill locker and sometimes I don't?   Haven't been inside (yet) but the pictures I've seen look pretty cool.

Spacious relaxation area to have coffee. The baristas immediately greeted me and helped me figure out exactly what I wanted. I found it very cute that they had turkeys available to write what you are thankful for :) the coffee was brewed and ready fast!

I absolutely lovee this Starbucks location because of the super friendly staff here. I love it even more now that its renovated and has more seats! This location is always quiet enough to start some work or just sit and chat with friends.      It's also in a great location since there's a froyo place, pizza restaurant and whole foods right across the street!

One week ago at 9 pm one of the employees was trying to let people leave by cleaning the floor with the soup next to their tables, and on the entrance, I was the only one inside when 2 others came, they were not able to enter because she left the soup on the floor, she saw them and did nothing, then I left and these two left as well.. Out of all starbaucks I've been to this is the worst.
Right downtown! Cute location: inside & outdoor seating. Again, it's Starbucks....quality & consistency of product were right on. Baristas were friendly & this was a clean location!

I'm the type of customer whose drink is unique (I know, I know) and memorized by a couple of the veteran baristas. "The usual?" they ask when I stroll in or roll up to the drive-thru speaker. I love my veteran baristas at this Starbucks. I even went on a date with one once! (Whaaaaat?)  And I've been coming to this particular location for the better part of two years, so I don't really feel bad about the rating I'm bestowing upon it today.  It seems that they've recently hired a slew of new employees who a) have no experience working with coffee and b) fail to listen to your drink order in the drive-thru line, resulting in having to repeat it 3-4 times.   So what happens in this instance? Well, it seems this Starbucks is keen on training said employees during their rush: when people need to get the f%#@ to work. So the long (LONG) line that runs all the way into the traffic on Girls School Road (literally) remains at a standstill for a solid 20 minutes.   A solid 20 minutes of waiting, followed by a new barista that either gets your order wrong repeatedly or just pauses, followed by ".... Um... What...?" in the most monotone I-don't-care voice I've ever heard.   Look, I understand new employees need to be trained on making drinks and serving food as well as taking orders in the store and in the drive-thru line. But maybe rethink your plan of action and have the new guys MAKE the drinks during rush and TAKE the orders during the slower hours.  Until they get situated, I'll unfortunately be taking my business to the Starbucks further west in Avon. Sad face.

Perhaps the SLOWEST Starbucks in the country. Regardless of the hour of the day or the amount of people in the store, I've never left without a wait. Regardless of the specific barista. It must be something about the management and the workflow. Avid unless you have time to kill
This Mother's Day Sunday crew struggled to keep up. Our drinks came out 5 minutes apart from each other. Only one person making drinks with 7 people working!
Slowest Starbucks I've ever been to. Consistently, every time, S. L. O. W.  Food and drinks are good when you eventually get them.

Have been here a number of times, and not overly impressed. The staff usually seems to not know much about their own products and my drinks get messed up often. I would rather go to a local place instead!
Typical Starbucks. They get my order wrong 20% of the time and they leave caramel on the cup 80% of the time. I don't understand why they don't give you a napkin.

Typical Starbucks, big and roomy, always a place to sit... plenty of parking too.  However, they rarely get my drink correct.  And it seems to be happening more and more often, and it's making me not want to go there anymore.
Great location! The workers are always nice and friendly. There's never a mistake with my order. This location always seems to be busy but there is never much of a wait.
Not happy at all they gave me warm and not hot coffee.  Tf. Too pricey to screw that up.
Iced coffee with sugar free vanilla syrup and a heathly dallop of heavy whippng cream.  win.

Excellent staff; they keep things moving. The clientele can be a little snobby, but whatever. I like how friendly the staff is. I am usually here killing time before doctors appointments, so the location is perfect for me.

I love this Starbucks. It's the most spacious one I've been to. I never have to worry about finding a place to sit if I chose to stay and drink my coffee. The service is friendly and fast, and my drink is always how I want it. I highly recommend the pumpkin spice latte, the iced cinnamon almond milk macchiato, or a Frappuccino. I hope they bring back the mocha cookie crumble and s'mores frapp back next summer!

This is my usual Starbucks and have been coming here for almost 4 years. I'm not a coffee drinker so I appreciate the baristas keeping me updated with non - coffee drinks. I LOVE these three in particular: vanilla chai latte, green tea frappucino & valencia orange cooler. When I occasionally try a coffee blend they make sure that it's not too strong for me. Based on how they take care of me I'm sure they treat other customers just as good.
Was not the service I'm used to getting. If all other stores had personalities like the one I encountered at this one Starbucks would go out of business
Great customer service on every visit.  Everything is always delicious.  Ive only used the drive thru at this location, but they are courteous and accurate.

I am not one for chain coffee places, but as far as Starbucks goes, this is one of the best. It has an intimate atmosphere and is located in a very artsy shopping block. Unfortunately, it is almost always too busy and even in the heart of a weekday afternoon it can be difficult to find a seat.

I'm going to review this specific one first, because a majority of people know of Starbucks.  So, this one is quit busy before and after work, also for lunch.  There are NO couches, so don't come here to lounge.  It's also a bit of a weirdo magnet.  I was in their one day and this guy came in was waving his hands around in the air pretending he was deaf, but NOT signing anything coherent.  They tried to help him and he just stood at the counter, they finally asked him to leave and he wouldn't.  Then another guy was standing outside spraying himself and the air with windex, while an overweight white guy enters in African garb and a 1980's portable tape deck.  So, come here for the entertainment, come here for a coffee, but don't expect to lounge around like at other Sbux.  What makes Starbucks worthwhile? 1. Consistency and 2. Service.  I'm not picky about my coffee, so I could really go anywhere as long as it's close.  I spent most of my college years (avoiding study time) in a coffee shop before I even heard of Starbucks.  But, what i love is that I know with virtual certainty that if I walk into a Starbucks anywhere in the world, i can get the same drink and they will remake it if I don't like it without hesitation.  Now, we've had our run-ins with Sbux employees, (Willow Grove, New Hope...I'm looking at you.)  But, entering an unknown local place, is hit or miss by a large margin.  Did the guy order enough milk for the day?  Are they using a crappy mix?  Do they even want you there?  You never know, but when you find the place you like it's yours forever (or until Starbucks moves in down the street.)  Starbucks, is definitely expensive.  So, don't feel bad about sending your drink back...but don't be a jerk about it.  We're all staring at you.  TIP: GET A STARBUCKS CARD!  FREE:  2 hours of wifi a day, free beverage with bean purchase and free 'select' syrups and milk.

Yet another Starbucks. However as similar as they all are, they are all different!  This one is busy!  Fast!  Friendly! And yes clean!    The menu board is rather lean! Must be a lot of regulars here.

I was in there with a friend and he has frequented there several times.  When they call the customers name to pick up their order I could barely hear the name called and I have good ears. They only say it once and if your talking to someone you might miss it and your drink will either get hot or cold.  My friend ordered a cold coffee drink that he usually orders. This time they got the orders wrong 3  times. Almost making us late for church. They don't know what customer service is. I won't go back there again.

Dan is the Man at this drive thru.  Actually, most of the staff that service the window are friendly, personable, attentive, accurate and prompt, but Dan adds entertainment to the list.  Good coffee and other drinks.  Not liking the new items offered through la Boulange or however, you spell it.  Most of the pastries need to be heated to taste good, as they are refrigerated or stale tasting otherwise.  On the plus side, they seem to have more offerings than in the past as far as pastry variety.  Quality of food is no where near Panera, but tops on beverages.   You will encounter long lines at windows on rush hour.  Parking lot is tough and not driver friendly.  Use caution.
I wish I could give this place 0 stars.    Terrible staff. Beyond rude.  Long wait times.
Trash was overflowing and my frap took forever to get. My husband ordered a double espresso shot and they served it in a large cup that you'd get a large coffee in.

Every time I go to this Starbucks I am disappointed. There was probably about 5 baristas behind the bar and not one greeted me when I came in or acknowledged me when I got to the register. I basically had to wait until the girl on the front till saw me and acknowledged me. They leave far too much room in my iced coffee when I asked for no room at all. I've asked in the past to have it topped off and got an attitude in return, I wouldn't have to ask at all if it was made properly to begin with.

I feel like I should update this review because lately I have noticed the service seems to be better. The baristas seem happier and the overall cleanliness has improved as well.   I still think this is a really busy location so not great for a meet up. And the seating here is awkward and uncomfortable... but the improved service makes me more comfortable for popping in and out when I need my caffeine fix.
Great place employee that serves me allways make feel welcome.  I really like going here I hope they keep up the good experience for customers. Yes I recommend this Starbucks to my friends.

The drive thru associate was rude and condescending. After I told him I couldn't speak louder because the car in front of me was in the way he snarkingly told me to calm down and "informed" me that the cars would move eventually, as if I were an idiot. This is not how a Starbucks should treat their customers. I would expect such attitude from a Burger King drive thru associate, not a Starbucks team member.

This location is ok..the staff has completely changed! The old crew was great, very nice and personable!! It's a very small location with a few tables and a bar with stools by the window. They do have two outside tables as well. We have been going here for years and have met some very nice people! If it wasn't for knowing other customers here it's possible I would stop going! One last note...two restrooms and no code or key needed...but buy something anyway-IT'S A BUSINESS!!!!!
Love that a Starbucks has come close to where I live. I would study here however the music is a bit too loud.. Please do a music check.

Only 2 stars for this one because 1) It was New Years Day and they only had three baristas working. For hungover people, a little quicker caffeination is never a bad thing. 2) The drive through customers were being served much quicker than the folks in store. 3) The woman in line ahead of me was ordering about 8 drinks and then decided to argue with the cashier because he didn't reload the proper balance on her Sbux card. That wouldn't have given them less stars in itself -- but I literally waited TO ORDER 14 mins. At least could have started my drink while resolving the issue?

All employees I gave the 4 star to r gone :( this Starbucks is left with incompetent staff. They made my drink hot when I asked for iced ... They slip in whole milk when u ask for skim ..no more friendly hellos or remembering your name :(  Now it's just coffee .

I am giving four star primarily based on my PREVIOUS visits. Just went through drive through and the lady there was NOT pleasant at all. :( She seemed very unhappy with being there and when asked if everything was okay (because the wait was longer than normal) quote for quote she replied "well there's 85 of you and only one of me so..." sorry but I didn't say it rudely nor did I need attitude back. Very unhappy with this experience, but on a NORMAL basis the people are VERY pleasant and happy! Hence I gave four stars! :)
Service was quick. No hand soap in the bathroom! Tables were dirty. Lots of staff, but no one was attending to the minor details.

As far as I'm concerned this is the best Starbucks on my commute. I pass three and a fourth is a touch past my office. I used to go with my mood and chose whatever one looked the least busy but at 6:30 am none of them were terribly busy. This particular one has made one mistake in approximately 200 visits. That's a darn fine record. This one has a drive through and some comfy chairs and a sofa as well as tons of tables of varying size so there's ample seating. Service is very good as is the quality. I spend $4.61 five days a week and almost everyone knows what I'm ordering, my name and no room. So why not 5 stars? The parking lot is a nightmare. If you end up parking just as it gets busy good luck getting out. It's set up in a way that the drive thru when busy can literally prevent every single parked car from getting out. I guess this used to be a drive thru restaurant so they were dealt a bad hand to begin with. Oh I just wanted to address the speed issue that seems to ne a recurrence in reviews. Generally I think they do fine at this location. I do see one particular "crew" that when they work are significantly slower than other "crews" but it looks to me to be due to not working their strengths not because they are doing anything wrong. Btw love Ana and Sue. They are always cheerful.
I love this location. Rachel and Pioquinto are my favorite baristas. They are terrific!
Nice people work here, always a smile . They run a busy shop fast and efficient . Coffee tastes same as in all other star bucks so I guess it's the employees that make this one special .

This starbucks is great, the people here are very friendly and the location is a nice place to just sit back and watch the traffic. My friends and I love coming here for our bike meet ups and catch up while watching nice cars drive by.

We come to this Starbucks at least once every two weeks if not every week.  It is conveniently located in between our house in Orlando and my parent's house in Tampa.  This is by far the slowest Starbucks we have ever been to... Consistently.  Service is poor, you would be lucky if you get much more conversation than 'what can we get for you'.  We thought that this time we would go inside to speed things up and get a better experience but that didn't change anything.
Could not fulfill online order for a cup of coffee in 15 minutes. App indicated 3 to 6 minutes. Tried calling store to contact manager later, no one would answer and no voice mail.

This Starbucks is terrible. Mobile order took thirty minutes!  And they were not apologetic at all - actually very rude when I queried about my drink. The three workers clearly were not trained well because they had to re-do several drinks while I waited for mine. What's crazy is this Starbucks was packed! This location could use a complete overhaul with its workers.

I like the location and the layout of this store. Right in the heart of Downtown, a block off the main strip of the bar scene, opposite from Jannus Live (RIP Jannus Landing). The decor inside is very modern with some soft leather chairs along the far wall. The staff is very friendly despite the fact that you know they have to deal with a lot of drunks and the downtown crazies. You'll have to pay to park most days (all days?) but you have to do that anywhere downtown. I'd go here more often if not for the parking problem. But it's still a great Starbucks.
The location is convenient and in the morning the outside area is nicely shaded. Service is okay.

They are very slow. They show no urgency when they are busy. Walked in to get our drinks on a Sunday evening, it's not outrageously busy but they are at least 5 groups here with us...should be taking 5 mins. This Starbucks takes at least 20 mins for drinks every time. It's just inconvenient & not a good experience.

So from the last time i reviewed this location years ago they have improved and are a million times better now. My drink of choice is a Venti Americano with heavy creamer and vanilla. If this is done right its one of the smoothest drinks you could imagine, drinks like water practically but you still get 4 shots of espresso. If done wrong it taste like instead of using espresso they used coal from an old fire. Its very hard to get right. That all being said, my past few years coming here they've done a flawless job of perfecting that drink for me, most of the people working started to remember me and my order also which is huge for me. Now granted were all just human, occasionally during extremely busy times it may not be totally up to par, but everyone working has always been more than happy and willing to fix the order. This is one of my favorite locations in the city without a doubt.

Don't normally do reviews on Starbucks due to the monopoly they have on the coffee market , I try to support the little guys. I did the drive through and my barista Dan was so sweet he saved me .. Wait for it ! A 20 cents .. Eyes rolling I am sure .. It is not the 20 cents that is the point.. It's the fact that he had exceptional customer service and he took it upon himself to be proactive and offer ways to save a few cents .. I like that brain cells were being used and not just another drone like barista at your service .. Way to go Starbucks on Kennedy .. You have a star

Probably the worst Starbucks I've ever been to and I've been to a lot. Drinks never quite right and staff who seem to not understand that when you pay almost $4 for a drink - you want it to taste in the ballpark of what you get at other local Starbucks. Avoid..
9am on a Sunday morning & its busy AF here!!! 15 cars in line for the drive-thru & 17 people in line at the register. Cuz-ray-zee!

Not a fan of starbucks coffee but it's a good place to do some work . The staff is so nice and you can also seat outside,there is a couch and chairs. I always get some iced coffee and spend a nice time working (good music too).

I love Starbucks and will probably never stop, but this location is making it really hard. Mornings are busy, but the staff barely acknowledges customers, and about a quarter of the time they get the app order wrong - usually because they put too little ice or too much milk when we specify extra ice and only a splash of milk, for example. They also seem annoyed during the rush when I bother to ask the baristas to please hand me my app order, which I can't grab myself. They don't know who I am, even though I'm in there at least 4 days a week. One good thing though is that the app order is always ready in a timely fashion, even if it is not always exactly the way we ordered it.

This place was crazy busy. Steady flow of people coming through. Thankfully they kept up pretty well & we received our drinks  without waiting too long. This particular Starbucks is the smallest one I have ever been into. It is also set up strangely, but maybe that's because of its location & tininess. Ha! As soon as you enter you are standing at the order counter. Then you move around the counter in a line, finally to end up on the other side. There you will find some seating if you choose to stay. There are also a few tables outside. We left & enjoyed our drinks at the monument.

Great Starbucks, always very friendly and great customer service. 5 stars for the new Starbucks! A little small inside and it's always packed, but that's a sign that it's a great Starbucks! Will be back for more!

It's tough for me to give any Starbucks higher than a 3 star review (my hometown being the exception), so consider this a positive review overall.  The service is good, though I found it very slow to get my drink. Sometimes the place is packed beyond belief, which stinks, but others it's empty.  The space is good - lots of seating - and they're friendly and pass out samples often.  Worth a visit if you're nearby looking for some Starbucks. But if it were up to me, I'd go to a local shop instead.

This Starbucks is by far my favorite. I can only write a review about their drive thru.... Always speedy, they never make mistakes, and the location is ideal if you're on your way to the mall or work. It's my hidden coffee drive thru!!!!!   Also some of the most talented baristas around. They only Starbucks location I can say "make me something amazing" and they DO!!! Thank you for helping me make it through so many 12 hr shifts!!!

This Starbucks does the trick. Fast, friendly service, ample seating available. I've never been here during "rush hour" but I can imagine it gets to be pretty bananas as per the great location.   One drawback... The bathroom. Yuck. Needs some attention.
My favorite Starbucks In the area now. They are super friendly and quick. I also like the setup and ambiance of the store. I will have to go more often.

The WORST Starbucks I've been to! Customer service is obviously not a priority at this location. I ordered the tuxedo mocha and they claimed not to have it until next week but on the app and everywhere on the website said it was offered this week. The person behind me ordered the same exact thing and was able to get it...um what? Was it because I was brown or because the cashier just didn't like me? I was so confused and upset about this encounter. Never have I ever received such poor quality of service. I can understand the long lines and the wait time but I definitely cannot understand the quality of customer service I was given!

It's a Starbucks in a really neat building! I love how Starbucks has re-claimed this space and made it usable again. The major downsides are that it's very busy and the drive through is incredibly slow. Major upside is that this building is a two-fer and you can get Chipotle next door.

Just as clean and friendly as any Starbucks I've ever been to. Apparently they are having issues with their suppliers and therefore are not making sandwiches or wraps (aside from breakfast ones) for the foreseeable future...hmmm??
Great experience. New Starbucks opened late in 2013. Nice Tables and chairs. Great baristas. I've not had any issue. At times especially mornings can be crowded as expected 5-10mins wait.

Don't get me wrong super friendly service, but almost every single time I come here they manage to mess up my order whether that be making a hot drink iced or an iced drink hot, or not heating up bakery items. You're better off driving to Brookhaven. I wish I could give a better review because the staff is so nice and the location is cute and convenient, but for the $$ your order should be right the first time

This location has the same menu as most.   This is a very small store.   They had plenty of employees and they were fine as far as doing their jobs however I never saw a smile.   The reason I am giving them 2 stars is the floor was dirty, the "line" floor where they were working was filthy and the cream/sugar area was dirty with overflowing trash.   Must be in need of a new manager. If it were my store some heads would roll.   It's Christmas time, would a smile be too much to ask for?

This location is never crowded when I go in the morning (before 7am).  They have parking spots right outside the entrance on meridian.  They have messed up my coffee a few times.  I would say I hate to have foam in my coffee.  For some reason I ask for no foam and I still get it.  A lot of it.  I will not stop at this location again.  I've given them enough chances.  Not worth my time when other locations can get it right.

The plastic smiles and "third place experience" facade quickly crumbles on a 97 degree day when someone asks for water. Waiting in line I watched a man attempt to get a glass of ice water for a homeless woman outside. The employees were demeaning and patronizing, one telling the man they had no ice as another scooped ice into a coffee. I realize it is a business, but you are also part of a community, refusing to provide a cup of ice water to a good samaritan to give to a  homeless person (who did not come inside) on a dangerously hot day is nothing short of mean.
This is a large Starbucks with plenty of good seating. Good neighborhood with good restaurants for a lunchtime break.

this Starbucks has great employees especially trevor, Jonathan, and patti but I'm not sure if she still works there. these people are great, they are easy to talk to and they are super friendly and they get your order right! I defiantly recommend going to this Starbucks for there great service and hard working employees. they deserve a raise!

As a caffeine-addicted adult that desires a moderately quiet space to work, I would have to give high props to this Starbucks location!   Both times I have visited the site, I have been greeted with smiles and an error-free order. The ambience is well suited for working in terms of noise and there is plenty of seating, including a bar-style row of chairs by a window.  The only downside is that parking in front of the shop is a bit limited but there is plenty of parking in the immediate area.   If you are looking for a good study spot with A+ service and a quiet ambience, check this location out!
I go to this Starbucks a lot because it opens really early, has great tea, has friendly, competent staff, and a lively clientele. Food is ok.
Slowest Starbucks I've ever been to--- drive thru is never a speedy process but this location is always packed.   Enjoy their happy hour deals!
Omg I love the coffee here.  Now I can see why Starbucks is so popular.  Only one coffee in my lifetime has been better than Starbucks, and that was Scot Coffee out of Allentown....

I'm not going to review the coffee because we all know what Starbucks coffee tastes like.  This review is more for the overall vibe of this location. There are a few other local coffee shops in the area, but if you want to use your rewards or just really need a frap, this location is friendly and always full of locals. The patio is dog friendly and makes for good people watching.

Starbucks sucks. It's so awkward and the quality of beans they use are poor. I know it a coffee shop but everyone is so quiet and observing you can feel the snobbery in the air. It's like pigs at the trough. Id rather go to some family owned place.
Slowest. Drive-thru. Ever. The manager needs to get with the program and train the employees better. No reason there should be a ten minute wait with three cars in line each only getting one drink.

Standing in a Starbucks right now. Barista had one person with a two drink order in front of  me. Turns out to me time consuming drinks. I'm waiting for an iced coffee. No other customers are in line. Three employees are standing around talking, not realizing the one of them could make the simple iced coffee for me so I could be on my way. Instead they continue to yap away, totally unaware of anything around them. Waited 5 minutes for simple unsweetened iced coffee. Congrats on the 1 star ladies. U earned it

I don't think there's a Starbucks in existence that has actually figured out how to have a layout that's good in terms of seating.  But there are definitely better ones in the immediate area better than this one.  Very limited seating.  If you like to hang out at a Starbucks and get work done or socialize, this is not the place for that.  Which is a shame, because the location is so good.  Staff is nice.  Fare is standard Starbucks quality.  But there's a absolutely terrible woman who has been there every single time I've gone the past year or two who takes up the corner, never actually buys anything except a cup of water for her tea, talks loudly, and shoots dirty glances to every customer who sits down as if they're taking up her space.  She's either a homeless person who always goes there or the owner.  Either way, they really need to start locking that down.

I usually come here on a coffee run for my family while they are at work, so I usually have a large order. I have never had any trouble with my order. It has always been right. I've only had 'burnt' coffee once and that wasn't even with the big orders. Everyone is super friendly. I love this Starbucks over the others in the area. They're nice, they get my order right, and they get it to me quickly.

All I have to say is yum!! Starbucks is delicious. Their coffee is warm,tasty, and how coffee should be. Their passion tea lemonade is sweet and very good. I also enjoy their pumpkin and banana walnut slices of bread. The location is great.  I'm fond of Starbucks.

Coming in here just now reminded me why I stopped coming to this location ...... lack of customer service that in some cases as today almost borders on rudeness .....  I should have taken my card back and driven 2 miles down the road to another location ......  Hopefully I will not give in to "I hope it's better this time" when shopping up here.  What separates one Starbucks from another is the customer service ..... not the drinks .......

This is my favourite Starbucks in town. It's located on Whyte inside Chapters book store which makes it more interesting. I can sit there and spend hours working or reading a book or browse the Internet without being disturbed. There are a good number of tables and staff are usually friendly. My complaint would be about not being served in mugs and plates, and small choice of baked goodies.

Clean and very friendly service.  Plus, they have a cute room with numerous tables, chairs, and sofas.  A great place to grab a cup of coffee (or the like) and catch up.  Parking can be a hassle but there is the metered lot in Union Square.

I rarely write reviews.  However, this is the worst service ever.    Very disappointed They had 8 people behind the counter and could not manage a simple order. Seriously waited 15 minutes for an iced coffee as folks behind me in line got fancy iced drinks first.

I've always been a home brew coffee kinda girl, so it's a rarity when I venture into a Starbucks. However, my boyfriend is a huge fan so we decided to go on Saturday.   After staring at the menu completely overwhelmed for what seemed like an eternity, I decided to try the Mocha Light Frappuccino. It was the most delicious thing I've ever drank. It was a cold, silky combination of mocha and chocolate. A Grande only has 130 calories too.   In my eyes, that's worth raving over!
Nice remodel. Staff was friendly. Even delivered our food to the table. That is, when they're not real busy! And it's always pretty busy.
Stopped in and waited 10 minutes for the drink to be made, a single person making drinks, really?!

Ah Starbucks. Well once again brand recognition is in full force, looking at the outside I could have been anywhere (Madrid? Penang? Sydney? - nope I was in St. Albert) and the inside has the same cozy, casual feel to it. The store front faces west which is great for our area because that means they get the afternoon/evening sun.  The service is good - not great as sometimes you have to repeat your order or the staff feel the drive-thru takes priority. The food and beverages are exactly what is expected from a Starbucks (that's what you get from a standardized chain) but I find this fact comforting because I know what I order will taste the same no matter where I am in the world.   I like that there are multiple plug-in locations - on the walls and underneath the bench seat. This is great if you do need to work for a long period of time because then you don't have to fight other people for the limited power.  There are 5 Starbucks locations in St. Albert and this is by far my favorite - for some reason, even for how "out of the box" the store is I really like the feel of this one - it is less busy then others, the staff are always welcoming, and the parking lot is very user friendly. I love to meet up after dinner for coffee with friends throughout the year here.

The location is key to the success of this coffee shop. Not far from Washington's Crossing, Bristol, Morrisville and such. The layout is nice with lounge chairs and a high top bar in the front. Tables around the side in the back along with an small outdoor deck. The staff is knowledgeable and are efficient with their sense of urgency to the customer. Free wifi (as with most Starbucks). I visit a lot of coffeehouses in the greater Philadelphia area and this is one of my staples. So yeah I like it because it's solid not necessarily because it's a Starbucks.

The other Starbucks in Hermitage. I was there Saturday morning while my car was being worked on. This one doesn't seem to be as busy as the other Starbucks in Hermitage. I do wish they had a larger patio

When my husband and I moved to the area two years ago, we were ecstatic to have a Starbucks with a drive-thru minutes from our house, but in the last six months or so, it's gone completely down hill - and the experience is the same inside and out.   First of all, there's a wait, 75-80% of the time.  They're just slow.  Unless you do a mobile order- so by all means, do a mobile order, but that does defeat the purpose of the drive-thru.  My lattes are always "light" -- meaning the cup isn't full (not even with foam) and there's a ton of foam in the portion that does have something in it.  And recently I've had 2 lattes that had an awful burnt aftertaste.  Once I even returned to have my drink re-made - I waited a good 20-30 minutes to be acknowledged and I feel like they were agitated about it.    Another time I received the completely wrong drink through the drive-thru, so we had to turn around and I had to go back in to get it fixed.    It's really frustrating - the location is nice (the patio is a perk) and the drive-thru is convenient, but if I can't get the correct drink and when I do I'm paying for 2/3 of a cup, what's the point?   Make the extra effort and go into the location in the Target- same parking lot.  Or the Giant on Henderson Road - their team there is AMAZING.  All I can say is this Starbucks needs to get back on their game.

Pretty average Starbucks. I asked for an unsweetened, no ice, lemonade-iced tea. I received a full of sugar, iced tea. Not the end of the world but as the only person in line, I was hoping for a bit more attention to detail.   The pastries were stale on a Sunday evening... If they're not fresh don't put them out!

The staff is consistently quick, friendly, and funny at this Starbucks location, whether you are in the drive thru or dining inside.  They are great at making recommendations and accommodating you. This is my favorite local Starbucks to hit up.  My favorite drink to spam: Caramel Macchiato

I go super early in am (0540) for my tall white choc mocha & they make it perfect & on schedule!
