Plus the girl makes sure not to over fill so there's no spillage or leaking which is a must when making these beverages, we don't want to wear em-I've been to others that are slower then molasses & half my drink spills out from how crapy the lid is put on or over filling! I'm satisfied so far!
I am not a fan of the coffee.i usually get latte ir capaccino..i am a fan of the bakery items..just personal preference.they also have a small gift area.

I really like this location. I usually meet up here on Wednesday mornings before work for some discipleship time. The employees are always friendly even when it's 6:30am, and I like that there are a couple of tables outside to sit out and get some fresh air.

This is a great Starbucks location right off of I-4.  It is open 24 hours and has a quick drive thru.  You can't miss the location as there is a giant Starbucks sign viewable as you approach the exit.  I have had no problems here with the quality of the coffee or speed of service as both have been great.  My only complaint would be with the friendliness of the staff serving the drinks.  Some cheerfulness and politeness goes a long way.  The last time I came in, it seemed more like they didn't really even want to be there.
Not enough electrical outlets.  They need to install places to plug in for those who need power for devices.  It's 2015 SB peeps---one or two outlets right next to each other just don't cut it.

I'm not a Starbucks fan, but sometimes it just can't be avoided. Had to be on this side of town and was meeting an ex-coworker for coffee to catch up, so Starbucks was convenient. Its not a very comfortable Starbucks as the seating is kind of on top of one another, but the shop was clean.  The service was polite and quick.  The coffee was just meh. I've become addicted to the Cafe con leches in Miami. Ask one of the "baristas" (said in my most sarcastic tone) at Starbucks for a cafe con leche and you'll get a blank stare. I ordered a Caffe Latte as that is the closest they have, but still just meh.

Always clean and friendly. On occasion their soy milk isn't great; it's almost like they forget to shake it. But, overall, it's a great place. Newly renovated and plenty of indoor seating; there are a couple of tables and chairs outside too.

If I was to leave a review before today I would have been 5 starts, but today is the day Starbucks lost my business for a very long time or for good. I frequent this Starbucks at least 3-4 times per week. With the virus scare they have shut down the store to drive up only. My medium duty wrecker can not fit in drive through. Today I was refused service bc I was standing in the drive through, which is funny bc last Thursday the staff told me I was always welcomed to walk up to the window. Not very good service to someone who is essential to people and i am not refusing road services to anyone
The drive thru is ridiculously slow here. 4 cars in line and over 20 minutes on an afternoon-not the busiest of times. They also don't have the nitro cold brews at this location.
Love the whole staff there.They greet you with your name  after couple of your orders.They learn your customized drinks and get them ready when they see you walk into the door.They are just amazing.

A full-fledged Starbucks INSIDE a JW Marriott resort?!  Couldn't ask for a better combo!  It was a nice treat to get a cold frap on a hot AZ summer day.  There is nothing else for miles and miles from this resort as it is in the hills above Tucson, but well worth the drive and the bonus was this Starbucks.  You don't have to be a guest here either to enjoy it and prices were the same as any other Starbucks.
Super nice employees and customer service but this is the slowest Starbucks I've ever been too! I've been to busy Starbucks in the am and I get it but they've never been as slow as this one by far
Used to be a customer here 4 days a week, but I walk in dripping sweat when it's boiling out without a wallet and ask for a cup of tap water and am told that I can't have any. Thanks a lot.

I really like this Starbucks, it might just be my favorite Starbucks!  I say that because all of the employees are very friendly and sweet.  I came in dripping wet from the rain and one of the baristas offered me a trash bag so I wouldn't get more wet, how sweet!   This Starbucks is conveniently located to my gym and work so I'll plan to come here again.  They definitely give service with a smile, which goes a long ways these days!  I think I'll choose to come here more often!

Very portly managed. Was waiting in line since 3:45 via drive thru and the sign says they closed at 4:00. I was about to place the order by the time it was my turn and it was 4:06 and the barista said "sorry we close at 4:00". There were about 8-10 cars after me on line before 4 and no one from Starbucks with a sign to cut off the line at 4. When I asked the barista that we have been waiting before 4 she said they didn't care. Very rude and poorly managed. She sure angered a lot of people me included who waited 25 minutes.

Let's start with the good! The staff are really friendly, their bakery items (I had a browny) were really good, I didn't have to wait too long considering how busy it was, and got the correct order (although with way too much cinnamon). On the down side I have a few issues that will result in me never returning to this location. I get the logic of being busy and cutting little corners to get customers through the door, but throwing dirty used steamed milk jugs and whatever else that needs cleaning across your clean work space into the sink is gross. Re-steaming milk can get people sick. Not cleaning down the steamed milk spout and pushing through your lines can cause some bad cross contamination and get people sick. When pouring milk into a steamer jug and managing to pour it through your hands somehow you need to learn how to properly pour milk. If your work station is more milk then stainless steel, take the second and wipe it down. When lifting your steamer jug all the different milks are dropping into whatever is sitting there. If for whatever reason I couldn't have lactose I would be one sick person. All this being said, I didn't mention anything to the staff because the barista looked a bit overwhelmed. So I won't go back knowing that if the visible corners are cut who knows what else there is that I didn't notice.  But again, the staff were super nice. :)

I dig this Starbucks. They have huge tables and some are communal tables that you share with others so if you are anti social, don't sit. Baristas are quick and super friendly. I think I saw on the door that they are open until midnight each wk--unheard of!

Stopped in today for a fix before a work meeting nearby.  Service was efficient. Staff couldn't have been nicer.  I was told to have a nice day three times by three different associates in the 5 minutes I was there.  Keep up the great work!

Free standing Starbucks. The parking lot you pull out of is like on a hill, so be careful not to scrape the bottom of your car when you pull out. Basically like any other Starbucks. No problems, and I think they now have free wi-fi. You can also sit outside if you'd like.

Extremely disappointed every time I go to this Starbucks. Because of its prime location it always busy, but the drive thru is terrible. Monotone, unfriendly service and my coffee rarely is enjoyable. I have a simple request, yet depending on who's working... It's always different.

1 Star for this location only because I love Starbucks! I have been to this store several times...after yesterday I will park my car and buy from the Starbucks in nearby Frys, or drive to another location! I'm puzzled as to how a Starbucks store can get their service so WRONG!!   A few times, I have had excellent service via the drive through usually around 6am. Up until yesterday, I thought the worst store was in the Las Vegas airport. That being said, in the past 3 weeks I have experienced terrible service here on 4 separate visits. Isn't anyone training these people???   Yesterday I ordered my coffee from the mobile app (at 2:36pm), when I drove up to the store (a good 15-20minutes later) the drive through was packed but the parking lot was nearly empty, so I parked and walked in. Two or three people were sitting at tables, and I was followed in by an older couple. I walked up to the counter with the sign, 'pick up mobile orders here'. I saw the guy was completing a drink, and I saw my little red cup ready on the counter behind him. I asked him if he could grab my mobile order, not looking up he replied, 'yes'. Even though he walked past my drink several times in the next several minutes to take drinks to the drive-up window, my coffee sat there. Over and over he walked past it to take an order to the guy at the window, then back past it again (this time with two empty hands) to start filling another order.   I continued to look in the direction of the other guy behind the counter (by the till) who seemed to look my way a few times. The couple, who came in after me, had already ordered and paid, but were now standing at the counter chatting with the guy.  Finally another woman came in, I thought this was a good sign, assuming he would acknowledge her and the other couple would see someone else was waiting behind them. Nope, he just continued to chat with the first couple while the other woman was now waiting. At this point, the guy filling the drinks was done with the order for the older couple. He motioned that he had their drinks, grabbed a cup and began to fill another order!!  Let me say again, I did not stand there 'quietly', I asked a second time for my mobile order, I knew the guy had heard me and he still would not make eye contact. I paced, stood on my tippy toes, looked at my watch several times (it was now 3:25pm). I considered walking out, the whole thing was bizarre, but I had paid for the stupid drink and I wasn't going to leave without it.   Finally, after the 2nd woman paid, I walked over to the other guy.  He didn't look at me or acknowledge me in any way. I asked him loudly if he could grab my mobile order for me??? He said, 'oh you have a mobile order, you need to go to that counter (pointing to where I had been standing, pacing, etc!! Really buddy...you can't tell me you did not see me there!! ). I explained I had and refrained from commenting on his snarky remark. So he FINALLY handed me my coffee.   One of the other bad experiences here was about 2 wks earlier. On that day there were literally 5 employees behind the counter. I walked up to the girl filling drinks and asked for my mobile order. She said, 'yes, just a minute', she was not only filling orders but also attending to the cars. She glanced repeatedly at the 4 other employees huddled behind the counter, as if hoping they would help (she was the only one working). I felt sorry for her so I waited assuming she would grab my coffee on one of her trips, nope.   She continued to fill other mobile orders, (no other customers were at the counter) while glancing several times at the other employees. They were deep in some kind of conversation never looking at her or me.   Finally, one broke free from the pack, so I walked over to him, he did not acknowledge me, I asked him if he could grab my mobile order. He was rather rude and then I realized he was the SAME Snarky GUY who gave me my order two weeks prior!!!   I just feel stupid for waiting so long especially after several other bad experiences at this store.

Workers are always friendly, drinks are always exactly what I ordered! This location is close to my work so I stop by often and throughout the day. Today, they asked how to spell my name and for me that's a win! Highly recommend this Starbucks location!

Great atmosphere, and great on a rainy day. Employees were friendly. Tables were clean. The employees didn't ask for my name for the order. When it was ready it was just sitting there and I was oblivious to it. But not a big deal since it wasn't busy. I would hope on a busy day they would pay more attention to this important detail. They had free samples of their pastries so that was welcoming.

Slowest most rudest Starbucks in the brandon area. I ordered some tea and it took 15 minutes for them to make it and there were only 4 people in front of me and no only that but when it was my turn to order the lady just stood there and stared at me for a good 15 seconds and then i said hi and then I proceed with my order. Is it hard to greet your customers? I also bought a Valentines day cup and i asked how much it was and she was like "theres a sign. Right. There." Ive applied here 7 times and still winder how this place hires slow rude people. Never coming here again.

I asked for a sample cup of their Veranda. It was yet to be brewed so I didn't want to bother her to brew some just for a taste of it. So I asked for a Pike instead. She complied, not smiling. Maybe a half hour or so later I again asked for a sample of Veranda. Again, it had yet to be brewed. So I asked for another sample of Pike. She hesitated for maybe a half-second then complied with a sigh, again not smiling. The music was great!

I am so sad right now writing this review. As an old Starbucks employee and now a very true patron it feel like this particular Starbucks has started slacking.  I am hoping it is just a phase they are going through.  Anyways I work nights and I am usually at Starbucks by 10 and they close 11 to get my two ginormous wonderful Trenta iced coffees only for the past week or two they have come out looking like lattes (toooo much milk and not enough coffee!!!) and another night they were out of iced coffee and subbed it out for iced Americano (which is good but so much caffeine it made me sick bc I sucked it down like I do iced coffee).  So two days ago when I was here I asked if they had enough iced coffee because I know it was close to closing time and by close I mean an hour till... I was going to get tea instead to avoid paying for milk instead of coffee (I don't like milk). Needless to say one of the baristas was nice and one was very rude and tried to make me feel like I don't know what I am talking about but I get the same drink multiple times a day.  Phew that is a lot, but I hope their night service gets back to what it use to be!! Pleasant!
The Starbucks is about to lose my daily sometimes twice daily business. Mobile order placed at 11:15 not completed until 1127 too much chatter between employees. They are having a great time!

This is "My Starbucks".  Consistently good cappuccinos.  I ask the barista to make them "dry", and they are excellent.  I also get my coffee here and have it ground for my espresso machine.  The staff manages to keep up with the customers. Easy and fast to get a drink and be on my way. The store is very small, has tables outside.  A well kept secret.  There's parking both in front and in the back.  Clean restrooms.

It's 7 p.m. in January. It's less than fifty degrees outside. The sun is down. Everyone in the shop is wearing a jacket or a sweater. Many people are hugging themselves and shivering visibly.  Have any of the employees noticed and turned down the air conditioner? Fuck, no.

This is my favorite location! The manager, Emily, is clearly running the store as though she owns it. Her employees represent how she hires. They are all fabulous. They all treat me with kindness every time they see me. Some of them have my drink started as they see me walk in.  To name a few, Terri, kyana, Ashely, mike, Joan, and a few others that I know well by face, but not sure of their names. They are all wonderful. I'm super impressed with how well the store is run, but also how they all clearly work so well together that it reflects in the overall happy, warm feeling you get when you go in.

A pretty solid Starbucks. The mobile orders are always quick for me, usually about a 6-8 minute wait. The baristas are nice and the space is open, although I haven't spent time inside in awhile. My favorites are their Nitro Cold Brews, and that seems to be a pretty popular drink, because sometimes they run out of it. The only reason I gave it 4 stars is due to the location... the parking lot here is really cramped, and I always get anxious getting in and out of it. Otherwise, I love the Batham Bagel bites, mini scones, and of course the coffee - especially those Nitro Cold Brews as well as the Flat Whites.

I come here most mornings to get my bacon and Gouda sandwich and an iced coffee. This morning I stopped intending to get the same order and the barista told me that the "ovens were down". Okay, fine. So I just got my coffee. On my 40 minute drive to work, I pick my iced coffee up from my cup holder and it spills ALL over my car because she put the wrong size lid on my venti coffee. Not having a good morning and now I can't even drink my damn coffee.
Don't stop in this drivethru if you're in a hurry! They take forever and mess up orders.
Average Starbucks on south street. Staff was friendly and it has a decent amount of room to sit! I got the smores frap. Deadly but delicious!!

So this is the second time that I have come to this Starbucks and both times they were like snails. I waited in the drive threw for 30 minutes and then when I came inside and waited 30 minutes for a cup of coffee while there was a total of 4 people working. They were talking more then  do anything anything. Will not be coming back!

I met a friend here once a week and the service is always quick but not very personal.  there are so many people here is hard to find a place to sit. Not a good place to work but a great location and fast service.

Dumb butch gave me the limp wrist when I handed him my change cause my broke ass did not have bills.  I specifically counted and divided my change and verbally told him so it would be a time saver but no of course I'm trying to steal smh  I asked for no ice and these bitches in the back ground laughed.

This place is busy even on a Sunday afternoon.  The staff is amazing at cranking out drinks like a vending machine.  Props to you.  I've never seen a starbucks cranking out drinks like that fast.   The only thing I wasn't very happy with is that the barista made drinks for their friends ahead of the line as a favor.  I think that's cool and all on a normal day.   Well not when the store is super busy and I was in a hurry to get out of that place.    I got a drink only because I was driving by and needed to stop by for bathroom break.   As a courtesy to the store, I bought a drink that I didn't need.   That extra few minutes wait was not cool.

Coffee is okay. Drive thru service however is sub par. I ordered 2 iced coffees but was charged for 3. The second iced coffee I was handed was covered with coffee on the outside which ended up on my tan seats. Very sloppy.

Terrible, worst Starbucks experience I have had.   So I waited in line for 10 minutes, there was one person in front of me and it wasn't busy. There were literally three barista's working on this one persons complicated order. The "customer" was disrespectful and obnoxious and obviously had no self-awareness or regard for anyone else. After I finally was able to place my order, and iced coffee, I was very irritated but knew I would get my drink immediately, because it's an iced coffee. If you don't know, and iced coffee takes no preparation, it's from the pitcher into the cup.   NOTE: The customer in front of me had the three baristas make *three* of her drinks over. smh  Now commence level two of terrible Starbucks experience. Now I am waiting for my drink for the next 15 minutes. It's a drink that takes ZERO preparation and NO TIME to make. As I receive my drink, the "customer" in front of me gathers her drinks and walks out the door. I ask how in the world this debacle happened, and if the store manager is around. The barista snidely informed me that "customer" in front of me WAS the store manager and that she is now gone.   At this point there are 16 people in line and now about 12 people waiting for drinks. At NO point did the manager see/perceive this is a problem. Now there are multiple customers, "real customers", complaining about the terrible service. I can understand if the store was busy, I can understand if new baristas are being trained, but I can't understand the store manager allowing this kind of bottleneck and they way she carried herself.   This is a case of terrible leadership, speaks a lot to the general performance of the store I am sure. I really hope that some regional manager is paying attention. There is health, money, and local reputation on the line.   One extra note, the store manager spoke very harshly and lacked any respect or professional presence. Even if she wasn't "on the clock", this is tragic to the health and culture of this local cafe.

Has a pleasant ambience, a great place to come talk with a friend or do homework. The variety of drinks offered is impressive, there is always something to get for everyone. Drinks are made quick and always taste delicious. Definitely worth your time to stop in and grab a coffee.

I have a love/ hate relationship with this Starbucks. However, I keep coming back because it is really the only coffee place by my job on the way. They are efficient and fifty percent of the time I can appreciate that. However, if they mess up my order and push me through the drive-thru before I can notice, I have issues. It's not like anyone on the way to work would have time to wait in line and have time to get the drink fixed. I have been over charged for larger drink sizes than I had received (I'm not a regular Starbucks person so I don't have my order price memorized). By the time I pull myself out of my half-awake stupor to try my drink and look at the receipt I am long past the drive thru and in the dreaded abyss of work. However, they usually do not run out of things and they don't try to obsessively upsell you on mediocre pastries. So there's that I guess.

Okay, so I bitch and complain when it's deserved - so I figure it's only fair to give kudos and warm fuzzies when it's earned. So here's a warm fuzzy for the folks at the Starbucks Kiosk in the Meadowood Mall. *throws warm fuzzies their way*   This one goes out to the tall, skinny guy with glasses (who I'm pretty sure has a boyfriend rather than a girlfriend). You rock, dudeski. You're a hot chocolate Super Hero! I'm sure you thought I was crazy when I was explaining that something funky was going down inside my hot chocolate (that was made at a different Starbucks).   But there was no fear in you, no sir! You yanked off that lid and you took a big ol' whiff and even though your face indicated a serious problem with my drink- you did what not many others would do- you bravely, and without hesitation, took a swig of a strangers drink. You risked your health for me! For all you knew I could've had some strand of Herpaghonasyphilaids -and you just ingested it without batting an eye. That's SO gangster.    So Kudos to you, Mr. Funky Hot Cocoa Drinker Guy!  Starbucks should give you a gold star for great customer service! Your sensitive palate detected the rancid mocha mix in my drink and then you went even further to remake my drink at no charge to me- even though it was another Starbucks' mistake.
Prime location and pretty fast service.

Thankful for this starbucks inside the hospital where i work! Very convenient and service is fast considering that they cater to every medical professionals, hospital staff, visitors, patients and students who are in need of starbucks. The baristas are also very warm and welcoming. The only thing I'm disappointed about is that they don't have the tomato and mozarella panini that i love and usualy order at any starbucks. Otherwise, starbucks saves my coffee craving! After all, at the start of every work shift I'd so want to skip, the smell of good coffee is all I need to get through it!
I wish there was a way to give a negative star. How is it possible to be stuck in a drive thru for 40 Minutes and still haven't received your coffee? DO NOT COME HERE !
Drive thru is busy but understandably so as its off the highway.  Had to wait a bit for them to take my order but it moved fairly good after that.  People were friendly.

I am not sure if it is due to the fact that there are so few coffee shops in the area (next closest one to my knowledge is the small Tim Horton's in the Esso station) but this Starbucks location always seems to be busy regardless of what time of day that I go.  The food and drink quality is pretty consistent with other locations that I have visited. The overall quality solely at this location also seems quite consistent regardless of the time of day I go at. They are always well staffed behind the counter and the only real issue I sometimes notice is that the cashiers will ring in quite a few drinks but rarely come over and help the person working that end of the bar once they have dispensed with the lineup.

This is a relatively new location in the area. Getting in and out can be tricky due to construction in the area. There is nice outdoor seating. The interior still smells new. The baristas seem nice, but the last time I came, one lady was instructing a new person on how to make a drink, evidently he has been making them incorrectly and she was getting frustrated. I can understand, and it was probably a good thing that it was a slow Sunday afternoon, and not in the middle of a rush.  My order has never been made wrong, and I like the nice sleek lines of the counters, tables and chairs here. The drive thru line is clearly marked and separated, so there is no confusion about if you are in the line or not, and no way to cut in, which is nice.  I am sure the kinks are still being worked out, it just doesn't seem like they are 100% yet. I am sure they will improve with time. I am so sure that being a barista is not the easiest job in the world, and pretty thankless.

Dear Starbucks,   Thank you for living on the corner of 9th and South street. You are dependable and comfortable and you boost morale around this part of town. Most importantly you make up for all of the god-awful small coffee houses in the area that I always get suckered into trying. I salute you for being consistently decent. Never change. But seriously, stop changing your menu and make pumpkin flavor a year round thing. Alright, peppermint can join the year round bandwagon too.   Love always,  -a devote non-coffee drink drinker.

The employees here treated us so well. We ordered our drinks and the staff noticed my five year old had a birthday shirt on. She asked her how old she was. Then when we were waiting for our drinks one of the baristas gave our whole family free cake pops. They even upgraded our kids drinks to Venti's. Super sweet staff! Other Starbucks could learn from them.

I've stopped in here a few times recently. It is such a busy location. The first time I came in and ordered--big mistake. I was there for nearly 25 minutes waiting in line and then waiting for my drink to be made. The next time, I ordered ahead on the Starbucks app.  This saved a lot of time, as it only took 10 minutes and I ran other errands as they made my drink. I'll definitely do that from now on.
Writing this review leisurely as I sit in drive thru going on 15 min now. Way too long to wait for a 5$ coffee.  Ok. Update. 25 min. Order was wrong. Yikes. At least they gave me a free drink coupon.

I like this location. The staff was friendly. They had a fair amount of seating and the place wasn't crowded so it was easy to find a seat- that being said I went mid day during the week. They don't have any fancier seating like some starbucks but I don't mind that. The bathrooms weren't great but they weren't bad either- better than quite a few Starbucks I've been to.

This is hands down my favorite Starbucks in town. I work in the mall and come here at least 3-4 times a week. The staff is all super friendly , they remember my name and my drink order . While they can sometimes get super busy, the staff is great at moving quickly and keeping the line going. Wish there was a little more space inside, but the awesome staff makes up for any size issues.

Not impressed with this location.   3pm on a weekday, busy city corner, drive thru line out of the parking lot,  so I walked inside.   Big mistake.  The cars that arrived after I ordered, received their orders before I did.  Next time, I'll drive the additional distance to get proper service elsewhere.

When has Starbucks ever done me wrong?  Well, today.    This isn't "my" Starbucks.  I frequent others but was on the West side of town dropping my daughter for a night with her grandma and she requested a vanilla bean frapp.   We drove thru and I must admit the server/speaker was very helpful, upbeat, and funny.  And thank the sweet lord they had the horizon chocolate milk for the little one or we might have had an issue.  SO, we get our chocolate milk box in a timely fashion but sat waiting for the vanilla frapp for an eternity!  When passed to us I hand it to said daughter and like any smart person she eats the whipped cream off the top first as we're pulling out of the lot.  As we're driving she says..ewww this tastes awful.  She said it tasted like drinking just iced milk.    So, my issue is just with this locale.  Long wait, and a drink an eight year  old turned her nose up at.
Slowest Starbucks I've ever been to. Read the rest of the reviews this is not only once it's all the time.

Service always good, meets almost all Starbuck's standards - except about NO smoking.  Every time I come here there are smokers outside.  The cigar smokers are the worst.  I've asked store employees but they too don't want to be the smoke police.

The people are great and the coffee is even better. Very friendly people. My favorite is the iced chai tea latte with soy and vanilla and how these guys make it never seems to fail me. Pretty awesome.
Loved how there's a drive thru, but my tall white chocolate mocha with non fat milk, no whip cream was not good.  Does EVERYTHING taste different in FL?  Sigh.
Consistent Starbucks food and great staff. A great place to have coffee and watch the neighborhood regulars. Come here every morning when I am in Philadelphia.
Literally the slowest Service I have ever experienced. 20 mins in the drive thru behind 3 other cars is absurd!
Drive thru is the slowest in the land. Drink is usually right.  The location is really great, with great access to the highway. Don't forget to join Starbucks Rewards for your free drink!
Quick drive through always a great coffee or lattes! Only thing I see is that they need to keep their outside seating clean!!!

The staff at this location is loud and annoying. They are always so busy talking to each other instead of paying attention to what the customer orders. The staff at Ina and Oracle is the exact opposite. They are polite, quiet, and always get the orders right.
Service has gotten much better at this Starbucks so I'm taking my 2 star review to 4 star

Beautiful setting, Wonderful coffee, and most importantly beautiful people. This Starbucks has been my go to place for almost a year now, and I absolutely love it. The staff is wonderful and loves to start small conversations with you and the coffee is well prepared. The atmosphere could be better and they could use a few more chairs but overall it's a wonderful place.
It is Starbucks. Coffee is just okay but we don't have many other options in town for a cup of caffeine. Five stars for this location because the workers are always super friendly.

This is my favorite Sbux in town.  They are always friendly, and the drinks are well-made.  The parking lot can be a little treacherous when they're busy...today I parked in front of Snarf's (they weren't open yet) and walked across the drive through line.  Saved myself 20 minutes by getting out of the car and walking my happy a$$ into the building instead - just sit in your cars, people, it makes it that much faster for me.  Also, I love that the building reminds me of a spaceship and has easy access to the highway.

Just horrible. Longest drive through I have EVER been through. Ordered two drinks, pulled up to the window and was handed one drink, then sat at the window for 12 minutes waiting on the second drink which was just a hot chocolate. No apology for the wait either. Absolutely unacceptable. And I saw three or four baristas inside so there's really no excuse. Pure incompetence. Will not be back.

It has only been a few months, but I have noticed a drastic improvement on the speed of service here.  I have continued to use the drive thru and noticed that the wait times have decreased and I haven't had an order messed up in a while.  I don't go inside so I can't rate on that.  I can say the service has improved.

I'm a very frequent Starbucks customer (gold card member:) and I'm on a road trip across America and have stopped in at least 15 Starbucks and this location wins the award for the #1 most grumpy employee. The guy working at the counter was very nice (the only reason I gave a second star was because he was so nice) we bought food, drinks and a mug ($30+ bill) and everything was great until we realized we forgot to ask for an ice water. Daniella could not be bothered. We asked nicely but we were obviously a huge burden on her. First my boyfriend asked and she didn't even look up, so he was going to give up but then I went back to ask and she had a huge frown on her face and I was obviously a huge inconvenience to her. If there had been a crowd of guests and a line I wouldn't have even asked, but there were literally three customers in the store. I waited while she made a few drinks, and then she angrily hands me a tall cup (usually they give a give a grande or even Venti) with no lid, no straw, nothing, with a big frown on her face. I've never once been made to feel like that at a Starbucks until today so I felt the need to share.
Gross. A simple cup of coffee was watered down and burnt. Hands down the worst cup of coffee I've ever had! They had a nice staff but they lacked any knowledge of coffee. Won't be back anytime soon
Very busy.  Good food and coffee as usual.   If you don't mind fighting your way through the drunk homeless sitting out front begging for your change, then it might be worth it.
The worst Starbucks ever! I have no choice but to come to this one, but how have these people not gotten it together! Awful!
This coffee shop was our favorite, however quality of beverage preparation continues to decline. Even with the batista promise.
Using up a gift card for an afternoon caffeine treat. I haven't been to this location in forever. Remodeled and very nice. Tons of space with lots of workspace tables and comfy seats for visiting.

I come here daily and I have to say that Alisia is hands down the best barista in town! I ask for different varieties of coffee and every single time she makes it with perfection, always so delicious I just want more and more! Awesome job Alisia!!

We decided to stop in after an afternoon of shopping at the Westfield Brandon Mall. It was a good decision. We wanted to try their new blonde espresso. I think it's a great idea to have two different types of espresso. Most people who complain about the burnt bean taste should really like this smoother version. There is definitely no bitterness. It's actually quite smooth. I had the blonde latte. I thought it was very milky & creamy. I love milk, so I was happy with it. I noticed that you don't need to add as much sugar to the drink. My mother liked her shot of blonde espresso, but she felt that she needed more than one shot. It was gone in one gulp.   The location is very busy. I give them a pass when it comes to cleanliness. I do think that they may need more staff because the floors and tables needed attention. The garbage bins were overflowing as well. If anything, they just need an extra person to maintain that part of the business. Other than that, the service was great. We were quite happy overall.

We ordered several items, they all came, except one drink. A family of about 8 ordered after us, they alllll got their orders before my missing drink. Waited probably 15 mins total, as orders passed. I finally asked the girl, who SAW me standing there (I even said "just waiting on my drink" when a guy asked if I was in line and she looked right at me, didn't bother to look into it), and she said "ummmm what size was it?" (Does it matter?) Then she looks all over the counter top, whispers to another barista, then comes to me and goes "it'll be ready in like a minute"... I said "has it even been started? We're going to be late for our appointment if I wait." And she goes "she's starting it next" so I was like ok well, I gotta go. Not worth the $4 coffee, and she said "ok bye."  It's one thing to miss an order, people are human. Don't BS me about it though when I'm WATCHING you guys not even making the drink.

I come here once in a while because it's close to home. Service is usually friendly. I've been in a couple times since the Holiday Drinks have been out and they have been out if the skinny mocha syrup each time. This is not something that is random.  I have found that they do not always have their products in stock. This can be frustrating when you are expecting a certain specialty coffee.  Parking is usually limited because of the other businesses around. Drive through is usually pretty easy to get through.  It is always warm and friendly when we go in.

Great staff, nice facility, but the parking lot is small, and it fills up quickly. Add drive-thru traffic on top of that, and it can be difficult to navigate, especially before the first cup of coffee in the morning.
Average. Coffee was bitter. Great raspberry lemonade.

This is my local Starbucks and I must give them props because I've been going to this one for years as it was moved recently to a closer place by my work! It is very convenient I will say the parking lot is a war zone and I get scared for my life by all the drivers who ignore stop signs here! Other than that there is no better Starbucks location in the Montgomeryville area! I literally will go out of my way to go to this one as opposed to many others in the are because I know my coffee will be perfect here as I've had bad experiences at other Starbucks in this area! Please look no further for the best Starbucks cup of coffee in the area!

Starbucks is Starbucks. I prefer the locally owned brews, however in a rush you can't beat the perfect same consistent coffee from Starbucks. Their 2am--2pm treat receipt is a great deal right now. (make a purchase before 2pm then show your receipt after 2pm same day to get any cold grande drink for $2)... And besides the coffee, I am really liking that Berry Hibiscus tea. I can't get enough of that this summer!

The staff is usually friendly & helpful - I had the unfortunate situation to have to deal with a woman named Yonnie - extremely rude & unprofessional!  Stay clear of her unless you want your day ruined.
Friendly staff, fast service and love the layout! All the greatest things you want from a coffee house. Especially if it's Starbucks!

Being located the the Brandon mall doesn't detract from this location at all.  It's your average, run-of-the-mill Starbucks.  During any of my visits, I haven't experienced the slow service others mention.  I guess I've just gotten lucky, or maybe I've just been bumped to the front of the line due to my dashing good looks & charming personality.  I like to think it's the latter of the two.   This location can get busy, due to the location, but as I mentioned earlier, they do good with herding people through.  Drinks are made with precision, just like I'd expect from any other Starbucks location.    Overall, the prices are high, but it's a pain to make a Java Chip at home, so I'll keep paying them.

Worst service at starbucks ever..the heritage starbucks never surprises me with just how slow they are capable being. 5 people behind the counter all  screwing doing nothing. Took 20 minutes with only 2 people on front of us.
Nothing super special about this place. Had some good tunes going. But typical starbucks. Great location with outdoor seating.
Very slow Starbucks. Was not very organized and did not really know how to deal with the large crowd.

This location isn't bad. Usually the specialty drinks are made well. I've never had a bad drink from this location.  The drive thru gets ridiculous. Usually long and slow. I would avoid it unless you happen to get there when there's no line. Everyone seems friendly here, I've honestly never dealt with a rude barista at this store. The location is convenient as much as it is a pain. Getting out of Regency, unless making a right onto 60 from the side road next to the plaza, is so frustrating.  Parking is ample, no issues there. All in all, this is a good Starbucks. I'm a Starbucks addict with a Gold card since 2007 and a daily trio or 2 everyday of the week so my Starbucks review credibility is good lol Enjoy!
I think I've said this before....but.....crack open a vein and just inject it directly :-)

Invariably the spottiest Starbucks I have ever been too.  I have had good experiences and bad here but in general with the volume of people, crush of vehicles in an already congested area and mostly disinterested crew this place is officially off the list for me.  Last visit was just plain awful...cashier neglected to give barista the ticket for my beverage so it was never made (and it wasn't even that busy).  Took me about two patrons (my mistake) to realize something was amiss. Was in a hurry and just had them credit my card...no other recompense offered.  But Saturday AM was fine so go figure.  During peak hours do yourself a favor and don't stop here.  As soon as burn thru my remaining gift cards I am moving on from Starbucks.  Another cost increase introduced today also...no thanks.  Plenty of other options available.
This Starbucks is way way slow..... I was disappointed in how long I had to wait. The service however was good - the person I came into contact with was pleasant.
I usually don't like Starbucks, but this one has fat, friendly service and a big outdoor patio with comfortable furniture.

The barista on register this weekend needs lots of training!!!!!! She didn't know how to redeem my birthday reward, which was so annoying.  I just received it at 12am and she just kept saying can't use it until your bday. The SBUX app on my phone, says it's available and it expires on the 28th. She got everyone's order wrong. Her manager came behind the counter eating with food in his hand. He was talking with a mouth full of food and started scolding her for taking food from the back because it was for the next business day. Overall customer service sucked so BAD. The manager on duty made it worse. I'll just wait till my weekday crew is back in effect:/
I just ordered a venti size coffee with no room for cream... it only half full! What the heck, If there is no quality control at all here just shut this location down already...

This is a small Starbucks located in a shopping center. The staff were friendly and welcoming, also very fast and professional. I decided to go to this Starbucks because it as the closest in my travels and I was in dire need lol!   I ordered my favorite, the Carmel Macchiato. The barista had pre-made espresso. However, my drink was delicious, and not watered down as I expected since the espresso was pre-made.   If you're in the area and in need of Starbucks this place is cool to go to! It's cute, small and near other stores/restaurants! It's right next door to Qdoba!!!

Tonight's theme: Social Sunday! I know this isn't a library, but this isn't a bar either. The crew was so loud and disruptive and not because they were calling out orders or anything work related. While they were well outnumbered by patrons, they managed to generate so much more noise than everyone else combined. My friend and I retreated to the old national side for a chat/work sesh which didn't help much. Prior to closing time, one of the workers walked in stating 'just so you know we close in ten minutes' laughed then turned around and walked out. Bizarre to say the least. My friend even commented she wanted to write a letter to mgmt. Think she was still ticked about how long it took to get her hot chocolate and the lack of pastry assortment tonight.
Quaint, quiet, good coffee but no cushy seating and some scattered trash on the floor..  My tall Red Eye is super good though! :)

I travel a lot, and am very happy to be a loyal Starbucks customer nation wide, but I have to RAVE about this location in particular.  This is my daily Starbucks stop (when I'm in town, of course)  Amanda is an amazing manager and everyone's smiling faces makes our morning!  From Patrick, Emily, Chelsea, and Diane we are sure to have great service! These folks are well trained, and aim to make customers happy. There is no wonder this location has so many regulars.  You feel like apart of the family when you leave!

I understand we are in Newtown and you're supposed to expect being a snot but if I'm standing in front of someone don't ask the person behind me if they need help and then walk away to do something else

If you like hanging out with people who seem to have no where to go, haven't had showers, hog up the tables all day, then this is an excellent Starbucks.   The staff is nice and friendly.   Wonder why they have pass codes on the restrooms.    Hmm.   Well that doesn't seem to help too much.

Was excited to use a gift card I had before so headed to class. Asked what was in the green tea latte, was told WATER & matcha powder. So I proceeded to order the drink and oatmeal. I got to my car and took a sip and felt a fuzziness in my mouth. It smelt odd. I went inside and asked if there was dairy...to my SURPRISE there was 2% milk in it! I am allergic to dairy and I just want to make sure there is an understanding of diet and food intolerances. It's very important to customers. I have worked at fast food and understand it can be frustrating but you will loose customers and word travels fast. I just want workers to respect the customers requests.

Extremely friendly! Me and my wife and her dog are moving from Seattle to Nashville and spent the night near the Starbucks last night. Before hitting the road we stopped here for coffee and breakfast. The two ladies that took our order and gave us our food we're both exceptionally friendly.  The food and drinks we're both fresh and the appropriate temperatures. They also offered us a "pup cup"  Me and my wife both had the double smoked bacon cheddar sandwich. I got some large guava juice thing and my wife got a Frappuccino.

What can you say - it is a Starbucks! The staff is what makes this place so good. They are the best. Always clean and coffee is fresh. Pastries aren't bad for frozen but I wish they had more "non-sweets" available to eat. Coffee is average but their specialty drinks are darn near perfect (try the white chocolate latte). Much better run than the one at SIUE. PS - if you go in the summer, take a jacket if you plan on staying. Brrrrr!

I hate to say it because I've been to this location quite a bit over the last two years... It really declined about a year ago to maybe six months. They've improved a little since then, but not enough to make the rating average.  You have about a 50/50 shot of your drink being made correctly. Today I got whole milk instead of soy and unfortunately I have lactose issues. That's not the first time that's happened. Sometimes you order something cold and it comes back hot. This is definitely a location where you need to stress the details and make sure you mentioned everything you want. They've been out of stock a lot in the last year. I think it's due to COVID and the fact that they're a popular location.  The worst it's ever gotten is when I have to take the drinks from this location to Harding because they're just bad. There was one chai latte I swear was straight up just water and soy milk.   Not my favorite location, but I think with the right team they have a lot of potential. Just wish I went in with certainty that I'd get the right thing every time (or almost, mistakes happen).
Cute Starbucks location right in prime location. This location is very small but the customers were friendly to share the large community table. Staff was friendly and coffee was good as usual!

I'm on Weight Watchers so I don't waste points on overly sweet espresso drinks too often. But thankfully, Starbucks has a pretty big skinny menu. I worked in a SB before and there is a formula for every drink. Skinny/Light drinks are made a particular way to lower fat and calories. That being said this location almost always has to remake my drink because they just don't HEAR me when I ask for a skinny/light drink. Which makes it take a lot longer. The staff usually seem distracted, frazzled or impatient. I only bother with this store now if I have a gift card, or I just order tea which is pretty hard to screw up

Can't stand Starbucks. The coffee, I mean the actual coffee taste burnt and is acidic. Yes great service but so crowded and dirty often I don't like going in. To be honest If I can't find a nice pour over at a local place or make it at home I'd rather and often do drink coffee at circle K. Kinda love it and for $2 it's a better deal.  Quit saying you like coffee and going to Starbuck. You shouldn't be able Get diabetes from your coffee drink.  Nuff sed.

Possibly the worst experience I've had at a Starbucks drive thru. There was only one car in front of us, it still took 7 minutes to get to the window. The cashier instead of giving us our drink when we arrived at the window, decided to start taking an order for a car that had pulled up behind us instead, so my  drink just SAT there for a minute. You should take care of your customers whoses  drink has already been ready for almost 10 minutes before taking a new order.
The staff here are impatient and rude. Not really a welcoming Starbucks. I'll try another location next time or a different coffee shop.

I liked to stop by this Starbucks when I was doing some shopping. Some of my favorite Starbucks concoctions:  - nonfat extra foamy misto with sugar free vanilla - americano with caramel drizzle (the caramel sauce that usually goes on frappuccinos... so rich and gooey!) - coffee light frappuccino - green tea frappuccino  A good place to meet up with friends too... yum! I would definitely hit up the local coffee shops as well but have nothing against the ever-reliable Starbucks!
The device is awesome and food impeccable definitely would go here for a quick snack or treat for the kids. Mocha frapp is the bomb

I really hope Yelp doesn't kick me off for reviewing Starbucks. But, to be fair, it's the LOCATION that I am reviewing, and not just Starbucks in general. Let's be clear.  I just love these guys. They are always super duper friendly and I've never had a bad experience. I usually rock the drive-thru (please don't kick me off, Yelp), but the few times I have been inside has always been pleasant. And not just the "Thanks for coming!' pleasant, I'm talking light banter pleasant. I love light banter. Especially when it's done right. And now that I'm pregnant and have a laundry list of a single coffee order, they never seem to judge me. It's ok, I'm judging myself for the grande iced decaf nonfat vanilla latte.  The only reason for the 4 instead of 5 stars is that sometimes their service is a tad on the slow side. But even this is ok with me. Once, when I thought I was having my first bad experience there because the dude was taking forever, it was actually him remaking my drink because he couldn't be sure he'd used decaf. And to top it off, he just gave me the "screwed up" drink in case I knew someone at work that would want it.   Damn I love these guys at the COLLINSVILLE Starbucks.

Starbucks on South Street. They're a standard Starbucks and the people-watching is good. They've got a couple tables outside and Whole Foods is across the street. Never had any complaints about this place, but I don't get complicated drinks for the most part. Coffee or the salted caramel hot chocolate. Mmmm.

I think it is because we spend so much money for coffee these days (it's amusing to think about the times in the 1920's when you could buy a cuppa joe for 5 cents!) that places like Starbucks, Peets, Blue Bottle and Handlebar are judged on another level: a higher level than most places you can buy an espresso drink or a coffee. This makes sense and this rings true. My experience with this La Cumbre 'bucks has only been positive. From the manager to the Baristas and everyone in between the service and interaction between the employees and myself has been pleasant, fast, committed. There are a number of bad reviews about this place yet i have never received bad service here and i have been here maybe 100 times over the last several years. I have to be honest and say something that i have been wanting to say here on Yelp for a long time:  you must take control of the service you get in a place in which you are paying good money; particularly when the people behind the counter are young people who are in the work force for the first time. I say all of this to counteract, even in a small way the bad reviews of this Starbucks in my adopted city of Santa Barbara. If i have an issue with a 6 dollar cappuccino, i speak up and they will make it over. "They" can like it, they can not like it. Not my issue. I am the customer. I am the one paying. Typing this here it does sound a little harsh but in reality, is it really? I am polite yet firm.  In any event this Starbucks unit is one of my faves along with that in La Canada, Studio City and the drive thru in south Glendale. The people here are, once in a while mesmerized by the beauty of SB, the beachiness, the surfer-like paradise and the loveliness of the land  but a little firmness in how you order and how you react to bad service goes a long way. I mean paying $2-5 plus for coffee is a serious thing.

Best Starbucks customer service ! This is the first time I come to this location and the stuff members are awesome ! We went through the drive-through and the girl on the speaker understood my fiancé accent , he usually always struggle , also the lady at the window was very nice and caring! I really liked coming here !

This is my go-to Starbucks when I have a lot of work to get done and need a good environment to work in.   Compared to a couple other Fishers Starbucks locations this one is a little smaller in square feet but seating is more ample in my opinion, I've yet to have problems finding a spot to set up shop.   The staff is always super friendly and take the time to notice regulars. I have never had a negative service experience at this location personally nor have seen anything negative with other patrons, even considering I've spent multiple days working in front of the counter for 12+ hours. I'm impressed.  Coffee is coffee, Starbucks quality coffee across the board.   Pro-Tip: Bring an extra layer if you get cold easily. During the day and especially as the night goes on, it can get pretty chilly inside (which is nice on hot days).

A little better than your basic run of the mill Starbucks as there is good parking out front and a large and comfortable second floor lounge with natural light.  Perfect for lounge lizarding, so be ye frog or toad you can raise your body temp with a nice latte and some sunlight on a chilly morning.

Don't go to this Starbucks. It sucks. The decor is nice but it's always super packed and the baristas do a very crappy job with your drink. Not only that but they don't have some of their advertised drinks in stock -_- I ordered a grande caramel macchiato and I opened the lid because it felt lighter than usual and yes it was not a "grande" probably just a tall in a  grande cup...to top it off it looked really gross. Go across to the street to beyond bread or anywhere else

Not going to go harsh on this particular Starbucks & I'll give them the benefit of the doubt. I came in around 1:00 p.m. and it seemed like there was only one poor employee doing it all. She managed to move the line along fairly well but I felt bad for her, since she looked so harried.   Starbucks is clearly able to keep enough people around slamming out over-priced coffee drinks, where this poor soul should not be doing it all. I visited the bathroom, where you need a code and they had run out of t.p. which I brought to the attention of the lonely barista.  To Starbuck's. credit, there was paper in the bathroom later after I came back after my visit to the Salt Cave.  At this 800 State Street Starbucks, there was a big Christmas tree put up, which I stood by admiring. It was a nice holiday touch. Also, I had never seen a two-story caffeine-peddler store, whereby you can go upstairs to rest in peace and look down upon (literally) the other patrons.  I would just advise that the store is staffed properly so that one person behind the counter doesn't have to do it all. Later, I did see two other employees and felt better about things.  4 YELP STARS.
Very friendly very fast.Great customer service,

This location is probably my favorite location so far. For being in the heart of Downtown St. Pete, it is never overly-crowded, which I definitely appreciate, seeing as I'm usually needing to sit down and do some work. I'm not sure if it's because of my purple hair (it probably is), but the staff seems to remember me, which just gives this place a home-y kind of feel.  As for the menu, it's standard. Just like every other Starbucks.  The reason I go to this Starbucks instead of any of the other locations in St. Pete is because it is clean and a friendly environment.

This Starbucks was quite clean and pretty. There's a gorgeous landscape painting by the restroom and plenty of Starbucks objects to buy on the shelves. Not a lot of sandwiches or wraps to buy though. There's other Starbucks with bigger displays featuring more foods on their menu and display cases. The waitresses attitudes were a bit rushed and they could have heated my family's coffee a bit more. My chai tea was hotter than their coffee. The chai tea was delicious though! I loved how I could add chocolate, cinnamon, nutmeg and vanilla to my drink myself! The little bar had those powdered ingredients ready to add. The people in there seemed a bit older than the average clientele at Starbuckses. Overall it was pretty good!
This location deserves 5 stars, but it is always so busy that it takes 15 minutes to get your order. The baristas are always fantastic and on point so if you can spare the time, treat yo self!

I'm not a big Starbucks coffee fan but frequent this location for mommy coffee dates. They have a nice big center table that's wide enough the people across from you don't bother you at all. Their pastries are fresh and we enjoy them. Staff is friendly and can move a line very quickly. Also there's normally no trouble finding parking.
Great little place tucked away. If you are in the Trenton area don't bother going to any other Starbucks this is the one.
Convenient location and efficient service despite how busy they are. My most-visited Starbucks, by far.

This location is very hit or miss with their service and quality.     I have had them not get my order correct a coupe of times and the person taking orders/at the window acting like they wanted to be anywhere else but there and the drive-thru line takes forever to get through!    However, when you catch them on a good day, they are awesome! I was only in Tucson for 7 days (and literally went to this location every single day because my family members live off Cortaro Farms), but on the day that Rebekah (and a guy whose nametag I wasn't able to see) worked, not only did I get my sconed warmed up (like I asked), I got a correct order and total and smile at the window.  So this place can be a crapshoot, but it was the closest location to me during my stay and proximity to a caffeine fix takes priority!   Footnote: They were just renovated!

Horrible, I was told what I said, which is not at all what I said, then get to the window to be belittled by Theodore. I've gone here everyday for the past 3 weeks and have ordered the same drink, and was never treated or talked down to like I was today. Never again. Goodbye Starbucks

The only reason I'm giving this Starbucks a 2 is because the drive thru people are amazing and they never make my drink wrong at drive thru. BUT I have went inside a total of 4 times and without fail EVERY TIME they have made my drink wrong and forgotten to put vanilla syrup in the Americano.  Thank heaven for mobile ordering and the fact that now I just get coffee from other locations instead of this one.

25 min wait for 1 latte with no special instructions. Just right off the menu. Way too long and no apology for the wait. I love me some Starbucks but man it's not worth that wait... it's supposed to be fast food.

I'd like to add to the poor customer service reviews at this location.   Four people behind the counter - I couldn't get acknowledged as even existing let alone as someone who wanted to spend money and leave a tip.

Went two days this week, once inside, once through the drive through. Both times, equally slow. I'm trying to get my gold star before the new ridiculous policy takes place-this location is on my way to work but it looks like I need to search for a new one. I don't have 15 extra mins to waste in a line or a drive through.
Love my frappuccinos... Great fast service very friendly through the drive thru.. Love being a gold member... Get free stuff...

Great Starbucks. Nice people, and they prepare the drinks well. A lot of Starbucks have stuff that's kind of watered down, fortunately that isn't the case here. The building itself is pretty clean too. They're also the only place that spells my name right.

Their selection is great but I hate that this is my local Starbucks. They are slow as dirt and Alicia is nasty! I've witnessed arguments between staff at the register. I've been given incorrect drinks I've even been told that The way I know a certain drink is made is wrong (because we water down milk in an iced drink). I won't come here unless I have to. Last resort. Il drive out of my way to yardley or even Hamilton if the ones in target and the mall aren't open yet!
Great service and perfect coffee, and always very friendly. The only down is that they never ask for a name when the take order, so most of the time other people take my drink when mines ready.

This is one of the slowest sbuxs I have ever been to. They frequently mess my order and never repeat the drink order back to you. I go out of my way to go to the sbux at 71st and Binford because they are amazingly quick and so efficient.

Not my favorite location, lines are always too long in the mornings and they take forever to take your order then to fill it. Barista's constant talking to each other about non-Starbucks related orders are annoying and could be partly responsible for their lack of attention to details. The drive thru Starbucks up the street is even worse so I guess this is the best city line has to offer.

Ordered an earl gray latte.   It was made correctly and tasted good.   The reason for the 3 stars was the service.  I stopped in mid-afternoon, not what I would consider a busy time.   Service was very slow.  In their defense the person in front of me was more interested in their phone rather than placing their order.  I kind of gave them a pass in this one but during order pickup the employees were more interesting in talking to their friends that had stopped in rather than fixing customers orders.   This was a weekday and during normal working hours, customers might have something to do rather than wait for employees to talk to their friends.   This is not the level of service excellence I expect from a world class company like Starbuck's.

I love coffee. And Starbucks is always a good choice.  Something I really love is in the summer the occasionally have  Dog treats for your companion. And it's what makes this location a high favorite.

Pros:  - Baristas are super awesome and very helpful when it comes to drink choice!  - Very quick service.  - Clean.  - Stable internet.  Cons:  - Limited parking space.  Notes:  - Be careful not go up the hill to fast so you do not damage your car.  - Be careful parking, great deal of traffic in that small little area.

gross. early in the afternoon today, I went in here for a drink. watching the barista make my drink... he stuck his fingers in his mouth then went to finish making my drink. I stopped him and told him I wanted him to wash his hands and for someone else to make my drink over again. I regret that I didn't obtain his name or demand my money back. I'm feeling disgusted now thinking about how many other times he's dug around in his mouth with his fingers and touched equipment, food, and anything else for that matter. truly horrified by this and will not be returning to this location
So small inside. The staff are very indifferent,  we are in plant city people, no room for snobbery!

Love this Starbucks location!  I work and live near this one and they always do their best to keep it CLEAN (including tables, chairs, etc) and the decor is really relaxing.  Even when they closed for repairs/redecoration, they opened up a tent location right outside so that you could still order whatever you wanted from their typical menu!  The staff has been there for a while and knows me as a regular.  They don't burn the soy and I love getting my lattes from there.  The food is well-cooked/heated.  The place is usually packed but I can always find a seat either inside or outside.  There are always Starbucks-related products there and the shelves are straightened and products are stacked in a way that makes you want to approach them.  All price signs are visible.

Drove thru for some tasties.  I think they forgot to put syrup in my macchiatto.  :(  Still she managed to get our complicated order right and they were quite Efficent.  It is Starbucks what is not to love?

I am giving this Starbucks location one star for their lack of humanity. Here is what happened:  As I am waiting for my drink at Starbuck's on this cold Winter's night, in walks an old man who needs to use the restroom. I sense his need was urgent. And being that it was late virtually all of the downtown establishments were closed. So the counter gal tells this kindly looking old man that if he buys something that she can give him the key to the restroom.  I am aghast and speechless at this incredulous lack of humanity (obviously a store policy that the gal felt compelled to enforce). The man quickly says that his companion will buy something. His companion, a young man, possibly his grandson, begins thumbing through his wallet for money. Just then the counter gal tells me that my credit card did not go through. I come around the counter again to pay and I see the young man looking through his wallet still. I thought he did not have money so I told the gal that they are with me and to let the man use the bathroom. Then the young man says, "No, it is ok. I would like to pay for your order." He hands me a gift card. My order was $10.10! He goes on to say, "it's worth it as long as he gets to use the bathroom." I was incredibly touched by this young man's compassion, so concerned for his companion that he would pay without hesitation $10 to Starbucks so that old man could use the facility. Then he wished me a Merry Christmas. This young man blessed two people that night with his compassion: His grandfather and me.   As for Starbucks, you need to change your store policy. I will never shop at this location again.

I'm in this Starbucks a lot and I've really got to hand it to the staff here, they're ALWAYS pleasant. They work really hard and I've never seen any of them mess up an order.   In regards to some of the other reviews: If you see a homeless person in a cafe and your first thought is "They don't belong here!" maybe it's time to check your privilege.  A muffin is like $2 and I bet that "homeless" "unpaying customer" is probably hungry/thirsty.   Tip: If it's over crowded, there's a beautiful library four blocks away (on North Church) with lots of seating and a quiet teen-free environment, perfect for reading and studying.
Even worse the second time around. Thought I'd try again since it's on my commute. Watery drinks. Horribly long wait.
Great service! Kinda my first time by myself and the lady was patient!! I was nervous but went well and my order was amazing! Will be going again soon

The staff here is very friendly and efficient.  Love the outdoor seating in the loop.  Sounds odd, but their decaf coffee is best tasting (pike).  I order a Pike half decaf and it is consistent tasting every time while other stores are not.

Smallish inside and no wifi available.   The drive thru is PAINSTAKINGLY slow. I've been through it a handful of times, and am writing this review as I sit in the line. It's been ten minutes and I've moved forward three car lengths. This seems unacceptable, especially at 4:30pm on a Wednesday.

I have reviewed this Starbucks before, and for this exact same reason. Why is this location just completely unable to remain open for a fixed schedule? For the past 2 weeks they have either been closed or running on a reduced schedule. There is NO EXCUSE for this. Someone on your team got COVID? Not an excuse, you are a fucking business. Your team is doing training? Not an excuse, do it outside of business hours. There is nothing more important to a BUSINESS than making MONEY and this reduced schedule/not being open is bullshit. This location just needs to be closed down. There is a Starbucks the next exit down with better service anyways. Please Starbucks, get a hold on this location. Get it under control. Poor leadership and business decisions being made here.

This is a good Starbucks, super clean and very bright. People are almost always friendly. The drive-through line tends to move along slowly, same as the internet. If there are more than 3 people inside using the internet, don't expect to get online. Otherwise, grab a comfy chair and read a book or meet a friend.

It's a Starbucks. It's in the loop. The door is in a weird place on Lealand - so that's kind of awkward - but whatever. Staff is nice.   If you can't get street parking in front, there's a huge lot in the back.

This is the best coffee shop in Nashville. The crew here knows what they are doing; they'll take good care of you. They are fast, courteous, and service-oriented. They make good coffee, always fresh, always hot.   It is worth your while to make your way over here for the best coffee in town. It shares parking with several shops, so just grab a spot in the garage behind the building, and come in the back door.

This location is very hit or miss! You'll either drink the best coffee you've had in your life or drink a cup of bitter funk! The only way you can really guarantee your coffee will be drinkable is if you order something that's loaded with sugar (which is gross anyway). Other than that, this location isn't the most consistent!

It makes me so happy that there's a Starbucks in this location.  Super convenient to a neighborhood that's largely interested in organic food and granola-type coffee shops.  Thank you, U Sciences, for bringing my beloved big-business espresso to the 'hood.

Its an average Starbucks location.  I think the best quality is the amount of outlets along the glass wall.  Maybe I'm spoiled but the staff at the location closer to my house is really friendly - the peeps here not so much.  Not that they are rude or anything, just not friendly.  The wifi kicks me out about every 15 minutes which is annoying.  They've recently rearranged the seating area to allow for more space but it feels pretty cramped now.  They do have a large outdoor patio area which I think is awesome weather permitting!!

This is my Starbucks home, and I've never had a bad experience. If there's ever an issue, they happily take care of it. (Like with anywhere, sometimes things happen.) For instance, they were out of my preferred pastry recently so I opted for another, and they not only refunded my money on the ones they ran out of, they gave me my second choice for free! I'm not sure where the barista went that automatically knew my order when I walked in, but I'm always treated well and with friendly service. I can always count on this location!
Longest wait time ever. Took almost 30 mins to get 2 iced coffees. Line doesn't move at all. VERY SLOW. Avoid drive-thru

This Starbucks always gets the simplest orders wrong. I order a venti iced caramel mocha with no whip cream. It came with whip cream and tasted like milk. This is not the first time they have messed up my order. There is another Starbucks on US 301 that is much better.

Yet another Starbucks and just as consistent as all the others.  This one has two things going for it that I like.  1. Starbucks is known for their incredibly tight parking lots.  This one is better than most. 2. They share this parking lot with a Phillie Pretzel Factory.  I prefer a pretzel instead of the overpriced, insane calories of the Starbucks bakery case.

I have to defend my neighborhood Starbucks. I frequent this location (as seen by my somewhat embarrassing 21 check ins here...) and I have yet to have a negative experience. For the first few weeks they were open, I would come upon no one in the drive thru and I would feel like a VIP, zipping through quickly. Even now, since the word has gotten out that there is a drive thru Starbucks in Midtown/Downtown, the wait in the drive thru is minimal.  The baristas are friendly and knowledgable and they serve my coffee and food as expected. I especially like the friendly girl with the braces. She is rad.  So, there, Starbucks Downtown, you have been rightfully defended (my work here is done).

This was the first time I've ever come to this Starbucks, but I will be sure not to come back. The poor barista working was left all by herself while her partner was in the back doing who knows what. While they weren't incredibly busy, the woman before me had placed a rather large order. The barista then called her partner out from the back for some help. He asked what drinks he could get started on for me and my friend. I placed my order for an iced green tea latte and he gave me the wrong size. There is quite a size difference between a grande and a venti but the price difference is small so I'd much rather have the venti and get the bang for my buck (considering more than half of it is full of ice anyway).  Then he hands me the cup which is basically milk that he threw the matcha powder on top. He did not mix it at all and handed it to me. The girl barista quickly pulled the drink back and said "Umm this needs to be mixed" but by the time she said that be had already run  to the back room.  He definitely was not a trainee, I would have had patience for that. He was just in a rush, depite the fact that there were no customers in line behind me. I honestly would have rather waited a few more minutes to have the woman barista serve me than to have this guy. He made me feel as if I was wasting his time and an inconvenience to him.  Overall I'm a bit disappointed because the store itself is nice and the woman barista was nice, but the other barista really set a bad first impression for me.

Understaffed and extremely slow. Don't come here if you're in a rush.. This is the worst Starbucks I have ever been to. The lines are always long and slow to get through and the baristas show no sympathy when you do eventually get to the front. I only go here when I'm in no rush and extremely desperate for a coffee

Now Downtown Reno, only has one Starbucks and off south Virgina street and when you exit you can only go one way, what a pain in the but!  That being said, I like hot coffee and they are best of all the Starbucks when it comes to hot coffee!  Doesn't matter what time of day the coffee is always hot and the people are on top of their jobs!

In March 2014 I rated This Location a 4 due to no food only Drinks today I go in sane thing as I Left I asked a blonde headed woman when was the best time to get a sandwich her response was we are very busy here, i asked the same question she said it's best to get here before 12 noon I said wouldn't it be better to order more sandwiches her response was No its supply and Demand..I said well I'm asking for a sandwich she said come before noon. I laughed and said if I'm ever here at that hour I'll stop back thru..

After getting only a few hours of sleep, I definitely needed coffee before my early morning shift at work. I ordered 3 drinks: a Venti passion tea lemonade for a coworker, and a Venti hot caramel macchiato and a quad shot with a splash of cream for myself. Having previously worked at a Starbucks, I understand the feeling of having to open and deal with grumpy people coming in to get coffee, so upon receiving my drinks, I left a $5 tip and thanked them greatly for working so early and being so cheerful. After only a 5 minute drive to work, I found my quad shots were almost ice cold, and my HOT caramel macchiato only seemed like slightly warm milk and there was no taste of espresso or caramel in it.

Was very dissatisfied I stood at the register for at least five minutes without even getting said anything to nobody asked me how I was nobody told me that they would be with me soon I pretty much just stood there while everybody just kept walking past me. And then when I finally was able to place my order my friend I just went in and placed her order she was outside watching my dog so I try to ask about her order while I was waiting on mine to be made and they wanted to act like everything was an inconvenience the girl that was making the drinks at the bar I do not know her name but she was a little heavyset and she did not have any eye contact with me her customer service skills or should I say the lack there of that was absolutely none. I'm completely shocked because I do you coming to the Starbucks a lot but this Starbucks has me thinking that I will start going down to the Southport location and set of coming back to this location because they don't know what the word customer services! If I'm paying 5 to 6 bucks for a drink I better be gettinggood customer service when I'm inside paying for the stuff that I am choosing to buy from the Starbucks store! If you don't appreciate your customers and how can you appreciate your job.

I don't make it to Starbucks often, but when I do it's usually this location.  Every time the staff is very friendly and helpful.  A few weeks ago my friend and I went in here in the evening.  I asked the cashier to recommend a drink that had little or no milk, and she quickly made a suggestion.  Trusting her, I ordered what she suggested, and it was very tasty!  Now....I just wish I remembered what it was called.  ;)

Worst Starbucks ever, staff ignored me when I had a problem with my order. In fact worse looked at me at the register and turned their back on me and continued to talk. All I wanted was to buy the banana nut bread I had asked for and they did not add to my order.
