The worst Starbucks, our drinks are never right from here and the place is always a disaster. It's in a great location so I wish it was good.

I don't hit up this location often, but I mobile ordered dinner. I needed to pick up something quick so I decided to try a chicken sandwich and get some tea since it was cooler outside.   I know this is a busy location, so I mobile ordered. It was ready when I got there, which was great. The sandwich was hot and the tea was double cupped so it would not be too hot on my hands.  I had a chicken pesto sandwich and honestly, it wasn't that good. This is the second hot lunch/dinner sandwich that I have tried and I would not get either again. I usually just come to Starbucks to get coffee but occasionally I need to get something to eat. When it comes to food, I will be sticking to the breakfast sandwich that I like, and the bakery items.  The tea was perfectly brewed.

So slow. I have been here a few times over the years and every time I remember why I try to avoid the exit 19 Starbucks. They could care less about customer service. If you get in the drive through and there's only a couple of cars, don't be fooled. It will take 20 minutes. Today, I finally got to the window, I paid, and then she disappeared- almost forever. I could see my drink sitting on the counter. I just stared at it for an eternity. Pure torture.

I have a love/hate relationship with this Starbucks....   Hate: I get anixety in the drive thru... the line is crazy and unorganized and I hate getting so mad when people dont follow the proper "drive thru" etiquette. It makes me want to ram into people sometimes which I really dont think is a good way to start off the morning.   Love: I mean... its Starbucks and its a drive thru... need I say more? Plus... the morning road rage really wakes me up and the long lines does give me a chance to check my email/groupon/yelp/facebook.. yes the line is that ridiculous.   I do go here every morning so I guess it can't be that awful.. sometimes when the line is crazy they do give drinks for free.. which is nice.   Need a new drink? Try .. iced coffee.. non fat milk.. sugar free carmel... its good, low fat and cheap (if you consider $3 every morning cheap for coffee).

I think I'm being generous with the stars. Yesterday I went to this Starbucks and got a caramel frap and I tasted the first two sips and after that it was like straight up ice. Wayyyyyy too much ice that I had to throw away my 'ICED drink'. I was not a fan and I am a fan of Starbucks. This sucks because the service was really good so I didn't taste my drink before I drove off or I would have asked them to definitely make it over. Honestly the worst drink I have had from Starbucks.

My friend is a healthcare worker who serves trays to 50+ patients in a COVID infested hospital, putting herself at risk every day, while being even more at risk due to her asthma. I was very upset to hear she went to this Starbucks and they told her the free coffee for health care workers is only For nurses doctors etc, when she is making a sacrifice as well. This was very ignorant. She had to explain to them why she was a healthcare worker when they are the ones offering it?? She deserves better after the risk she takes everyday.
Terrible, latte was not made correctly went back asked for another and hooray it was made correctly. No I am sorry just a dirty look. And they weren't even busy. Never again at this Starbucks !

SERVICE Rocks!!  Stopped at this Starbucks location after checking into the Hampton Inn just a few blocks own 34th Street and my wife wanted an Iced Soy Latte.   Despite being close to 4 pm the store was crowded but the line moved very quickly. The gentleman working the counter was super friendly and helpful, they did not have any dark roast brewed and I ordered a Pike but he virtually insisted he would make any pour over I wanted. I ordered a Sumatra and he had it ready for me very fast while keeping the rest of the line moving.   Very clean, well organized and comfortable store and SUPER clean including the rest rooms.   If every Starbucks was like this I would be a regular!

Ew. Inside was decent, but the outside area smelled like urine and there were multiple people walking around barefoot or sleeping in the chairs. I sat down without realizing that I was actually sitting in a chair that stank of  dried pee. Never going back there.

I'm a dyed in the wool Starbucks devotee and I love this one. Many seating options (I love the upholstered chairs). This is a deep store so not all seating is chilled whenever the door opens on a wintry day. Staff members are pleasant and efficient with a sense of humor (when I can't see what's right in front of my face).
This Starbucks is amazing. Is always friendly and kind I highly recommend coming to visit location they will take good care of you
Been here often for coffee to go and in house. Service has always been great and order has always been correct. Yes it gets extremely busy but I've always seen the staff do their best
How do you review Starbucks? This one is the same as all the others, except that it shares space with a bank. The drive-thru is busy, but it moves pretty fast.

They refused to give me a cup of hot water today even though I have gotten hot water here many times before. They said it is company policy because they some customers get burned by it. I asked how that was different than giving someone hot tea and they had no answer for me. Very disappointed. Usually Starbucks employees are very reasonable but they wouldn't budge.

Good service...inside moves faster than the drive-thru in the AM. They make some of the better Chai Tea Lattes. But my reason for three stars is that they are consistently out of the Petit Vanilla Bean scones by 8 am! I can understand being out around late morning, but 8 am? Stock more, people!

I really don't like this Starbucks. They take forever, when the one just up the street on 82nd doesn't have a problem getting my coffee out in less than a minute. I just don't think it should take 15 minutes to get my coffee.
This place has the A/C on freezer cold. Bring a sweater. Everything else seems normal.

Dana is so awesome! This Starbucks has the best customer service! Made our drinks perfect! Cute little location in down town saint Pete, walking distance from the peir, defiantly stop in and get a cold drink while walking around!

One of the best things that this Starbucks has going for it is its drive through and friendly service. It's fast and efficient. Depending on how crowded it gets, the line can get ridiculous because of how the traffic can flow into the strip mall that this Starbucks is located in, which can be a pain to get into if you happen to be on the opposite side of the street and trying to cross traffic.  If you chose to go inside, parking can be tight, especially during the the morning. If there is no parking in front, you can park behind the store itself and walk a little. The inside has a few tables to sit, but space is at a premium, especially when crowded. The line for orders can sometime merge together.   When the weather is nice, there is a small patio with a few tables and chairs that allow you to watch Pendleton Pike traffic while enjoying your caffeine.

I'm a huge SB fan and this is one of my go to locations because it's within walking distance of work BUT it's oh so small with very limited seating and has an even smaller merch selection.  So if you're downtown and looking for a coffee while on foot, this may be the just right SB for you.  But if you're looking for a location to linger at, enjoy on a date, or work from your laptop, this one isn't ideal for longer stays.   As an aside, this one is connected to the LRT system and you can access it from underground if that floats your boat and you want to avoid mountains of snow or ice :-)  All my drinks have been made correctly here over the years but I do long for a larger location close to my work that's got a more inviting feel to linger longer than just picking up my drink and heading out the door.

A Starbucks with a drive-thru gets an automatic four stars from me, and then I start deducting from there. I can see that this Starbucks would get really busy because it's in a prime location, but my visit was quick and efficient, and I visited during a workday in the morning.    Getting to the drive-thru can be a little confusing. For best results, enter from Girls School Road, not 10th Street!

Do not go to this location. They up charge everything and the prices are not consistent with regular Starbucks stores. Also the staff there does not cater to the customers needs, and one person in particular is extremely rude to customers and will give you attitude if you disagree with the price inconsistency. As a loyal, daily Starbucks customer I am extremely disappointed. It goes against the Sbux motto of good customer service. I've now changed my schedule to go to Kahwa every morning instead.

This is a great Starbucks location! Different seating options (sofa, tables, bar stools), Clover brewing for signature "Starbucks Reserve" coffee from around the world, free wifi and a drive-thru. On top of that, nearby eating options make this the perfect place to sit down and have a drink after your meal (if you don't want to eat at Starbucks, that is). Definitely recommended. Oh, and since they brew Starbucks Reserve, they also have reserve coffee bags for purchase.
Never have I been to this Starbucks and been served in an adequate amount of time. Either there is not enough Starbucks to service this area or this one in particular is severely understaffed.
Too much staff none of whom understand the correct names for routine Starbucks items. Just say no.
Consistently received drinks that don't taste right or that are not made the way I order them. Always disappointed in this place.
Nice location on a Sunday morning. Friendly helpful staff and spacious layout. A full range of pastries snd sandwiches are available. Ample parking too.

Nice little location by the Eller house. Staff seemed a bit new and explained my drink selection (French vanilla latte) is usually three shots of flavoring, and asked me how I wanted to split the hazelnut and vanilla shots. Which I thought was weird, but what do I know.  The latte was lacking, which is why I knocked the one star off. I found a table and used wifi for work and making work calls and it was easy.

Nice drive-thru which was great when staying in the area on vacation with our dog. Also, looks like this location has nice outdoor seating, but we didn't get to use it since we were on the run to see family each time we stopped in here.
Decently sized location Above average Service (line moved quick) Drinks made properly Condiment section a little messy
Relatively roomy Starbucks with a fairly decent parking. Nice to see in the neighborhood!

Starbucks is about at typical and predictable as it gets.  This location is nice, clean, pretty spacious, the drive thru is always speedy, the staff is always happy to serve you (even at 6:45 am- I don't know how they do it).  My drink almost always tastes the same- which is nice when a tall skinny vanilla latte is a staple in my diet during days when I have more tasks than time.    If you're in the Brentwood/ Rock Hill area, this is the Starbucks I'd reccommend.  The one on Brentwood Blvd is kind of a logisitcal nightmare in terms of getting in/out and finding a place to park.    Also, DO NOT order the new Vanilla Spice Latte.  It tastes like your grandmother's perfume.  It's bad... really bad.
Excellent baristas! A lot better service than usual. Very knowledgeable of their products and very helpful. Thanks guys!
This is my favorite Starbucks in Indy!   The baristas are always so happy and pleasant, and typically produce stellar drinks.
Great Starbucks. Quick and friendly service. Clean bathrooms.

Usually pretty fast and I have never had any problems here. 4 stars for a Starbucks doesn't ever make much sense, but this is the most convenient drive through when I find myself driving a lot during the work day. That means I end up coming through this drive through pretty regularly. With that regularity, you would expect at least one issue. Never ever!   McDonalds next door is constantly slow, but these workers must actually be drinking that coffee they are brewing, and even if the McDonald's workers are, that stuff just is not of the same quality, I don't are what the commercials say. The staff is fast and friendly.   Whether it is a White Chocolate Mocha Frappe with extra shots of espresso or just a regular coffee with cream and some espresso shots, it will do the trick and it never takes long.
Despite the fact that this place is insanely busy the staff is cool and makes a good cup

Starbucks is always amazing! My only complaint is how busy this location is because it is the only drive thru in the area. Sometimes the line is all the way in the street in the mornings and I have waited as long as 20-25 minutes to go through a drive thru. However, that is not the workers fault! They go as fast as they can.

For a Starbucks, this location is one of the best. I've been frequenting this location for a couple years now, and they are quite consistent with making the drinks good. The baristas are all very friendly, there's a fair amount of seating, and the place is kept clean. It's really my favourite Starbucks on the south side!
This location saddens me, it's always gloomy and drab. They really need an update! And brighter colors on the wall. They are by the beach after all.

A busy Starbucks location with a small seating area. I like this location as it's large south facing windows make it warmer than many of the overly air conditioned stores. Sunny south facing patio often has nice flower pots. Standard Starbucks fare with efficient staff.
This is a wonderful Starbucks. Only one with a drive thru anywhere nearby. The employees are second to none. Especially Dan. He's the MAN! Only problem is it's slower than most.

One of the lady was wonderful! Seem like she says "you can make it anyway you want"...something like that phrase, customizing the drinks. I was having a disappointing day, but she brighten it back up. =D . One person can change a bumming day to a great day.
Literally the slowest Starbucks I've ever been too. Both times I've been here. Don't go through this drive thru unless you have 30 minutes to spare.

Although I find the adjoining space next to old national bank a bit peculiar, it definitely helps to open up the space and provides more seating options. Just seems like a security risk having a bank wide open and exposed like that but that's just the "what if" analyst in me. Nice spot to get some work done and it wasn't as noisy as other locations I've been to.   But what gives about the limited hours the blonde roast is available? Is this a morning coffee only? Menu is a bit misleading I must say :/ Kinda like Jimmy Johns still listing sprouts on the Turkey Tom 'which. False advertising but I digress. Caffeine + wifi= productivity!
Love this Starbucks! I often stop by here on my way to work, they are always fast, friendly, and my drinks are always perfect and delicious!
Service today was horrible.  The weekend staff at this store have consistently been horrible.  My coffee order was correct but did not taste correct. .  They were very light on the mocha.

Five star for quick accurate service.  The cashier handed my croissant to another employee who heated it for me, so it was ready when I was done paying.   A third employee made my tasty specialty coffee- yummy after 4 hours of traffic from SoCal.   Quick, accurate coffee making!

Terrible location!! I go yesterday after opening and order the Pike Place, their most popular coffee. I'm told they don't have any brewed. Okay, I get another coffee that's strong. I go today at 4:58 am, they open at 5:00 am. I expect to wait at the door for 2 minutes as SB usually opens the doors within seconds of the opening time. A woman comes to the door and tells me that they open at 5:00 but they don't have any coffee brewed, so it will be about 5 more minutes. She had no intention of letting me in. I commented that perhaps they should have started the coffee 3 minutes earlier, a time that will allow them to sell at the opening time. I leave and go to a SB 5 minutes away. The SB that I went to had the Pike Place ready, at 5:04 am!!! This SB is 1 block from my hotel. I will not be back. It's a new SB, so I suggest that someone in management tell them what "open at 5:00" truly means. It doesn't mean that the lights are on and the door is open, it means open and ready to do business. Obviously I'm cranky if I don't get my coffee first thing in the morning. BTW, I have never left a negative review for SB, as I love SB coffee.

I needed a late afternoon fix and slipped into this store because it is the closest to friends with whom I hoped to connect for dinner. There was an unbelievable downpour and it seemed prudent to get off the road and "just chill!"  This Starbucks is like hundreds I've been to all over the country -- cozy, modern, busy and predictably, friendly.  I didn't experience anything above and beyond and I didn't observe anything Starbucks wouldn't be proud of... What I saw was exactly what Starbucks set out to create:  a comfortable refuge from the rest of the world where you can get a good cuppa Joe, maybe something to nosh, and either get stuff done or "just chill" with (or without) friends.

The baristas are bunch beauties. Drinks are always made correctly and make me feel as good as some cocaine on a Monday morning. Sounds depressing? Actually it's the opposite. Me and a couple boys last week did a full send and nailed some broads. Woke up Monday morning did the white and it made me feel like I was born in a new light.

If you can get a parking spot, this is a pretty decent Starbucks. The one at Kroger isn't that great and the Green Hills Starbucks is a zoo 24/7. The line moves relatively quickly and my drinks seem to come out great.

This place is the worst Starbucks I have ever been To. The manager Scott work behind the counter like a he knows what he is doing but for all the moving around. The coffee maker has no drinks on it and he is not getting anything done. I have waited several time for up to twenty to twenty five minutes for a single drink. At least once a week my ices coffee is hot. It just not a good place .
I love this Starbucks! I don't like Starbucks in general but the people at this one are always so nice! I don't mind coming back to this one every time cause everyone's so nice here.

This particular Starbucks is on the ball every day and I visit coffee shops on the regular. The lady in there by the name of Heather is a machine. She even remembered my order from my last visit. Now that's what I call customer service. All around I'm going to be making this my regular stop in the mornings.

This is a very good Starbucks. The parking is not the best in the world but it is located right next to the interstate and Walmart. The staff here is always extremely friendly and the drinks are consistent with all other Starbucks locations.

Worst customer experience was at this Starbucks. Cashier didn't key in the exact order of food I place. Instead she just said 'the price is the same' I guess it doesn't matter to your inventory?   My colleague ordered a cup of cappuccino. No joke. It was entire cup of foam. Understood that cappuccino is supposed to have half cup of foam. The employee said 'cappuccino is all foam'. Seriously?  Employees here have bad attitude. Terrible experience at this Starbucks while on my first trip to New Orleans.

Terrible, terrible service. I waited 20 mins in line at the drive through to arrive at the window to realize they had not captured my order. Matthew, a Starbucks employee, was extremely rude and told me he could not take my order at the window and that i would need to go inside. I explained I had an infant in the back seat and that is the reason why I didn't go inside in the first place. He finally agreed to take my order at the window but did it in the snottiness way possible. Starbucks typically has a friendly staff so I was super disappointed to run into such an unpleasant person at the edwardsville location.

I really like this Starbucks location.  It's quite small compared to others, but I find that this makes it feel cozier.  It's not usually too busy, either, so there's always a seat to be found, and I've never experienced a long wait.  The employees are super friendly and helpful, and they've always made my drinks exactly how I've ordered them.  The condiment bar is always stocked up nicely and is very clean, which is a huge plus in my book.  This is an overall great Starbucks!

This Starbucks is convenient and like most of the other establishments delivers a similar experience.  I find it kind of funny that it's right next door to a Dunkin Donuts, but maybe that's a benefit for those of us who don't want to pay $6 for a scone made from organic power berries harvested from the heart of the jungles of expensivecoffeebeanland.

This Starbucks is located in Regency Center, and most of the time, entry and exit is smooth.  There is ample parking, but if you just wanted to hit the drive through, this location is pretty quick.   Other than that, it is your average expensive high calorie coffee joint.  So far, it's kept pretty clean, and the service is friendly.    My favorite is the Java Chip.  I get it every time I go to Starbucks, morning, noon, or night.  Yeah, it's a milkshake.  Don't judge me.

Wow, today service was at a new low. I walked in and no one looked at me. I literally stood by the cash register for several minutes and no acknowledgement. Finally when the female (I will be nice and not say her name) took my order, she repeated it back to me wrong as I could tell she wasn't listening. I corrected her and then she proceeded to order the wrong drink!  When I got it I looked at all 3 employees standing behind the counter and said this is the wrong drink AND then I told them that it is frustrating coming to this Starbucks because the service is always bad.  They just looked at me and one said, we will make new a new drink. That was it. No apology, no free drink, nothing.   I gotta believe that the only reason this Starbucks is still open is the location; certainly not the service. I'm done with this Starbucks and will go to other locations that care about me.  The Starbucks on Broadway and Kolb is amazing!!

Very disorganized and incapable of getting basic brewed coffee served in under 10 minutes. Stay away if you actually want to get on the move in the morning. Other locations will take care of you better

I'm confused as to why this Starbucks doesn't have a complete 5 star rating!  For starters, there's actually a parking lot...in PHILLY! Mind you, it is shared with a few other stores and a TDBank, but dont worry, they wont tow your car for parking in their lot for 5 min. to run into the Bucks for a morning latte.  Secondly, this location received a complete reno, not so long ago.  The difference is great, and the flow of traffic to the register is much better.  The real reason I love this Starbucks however is the people.  The associates are so nice, know you by name, and can spell out what YOU want before you can tell them, simply because they remember.  Because of these reasons, I will go out of my way to stop there, even though there is a Starbucks 3 minutes from where I work. :)

As far as coffee goes it stands by its name and brand image. May be expensive but worth every penny. My favorite part is the extreme consistency they provide you. I can order a drink a 100 times from them and it will taste exactly the same 100 times straight. Whereas I can order same drink 100 times from Dunkin donuts and it will taste different 95 times with 15 of those times I need to force it down just so my 2.50$ doesn't go to waste.   If you are looking for quality and consistency then pull out your wallet and head to Starbucks

I like Starbucks...I am not a die hard fanatic for coffee with a lot of calories, and tend to prefer what I make at home, however I do receive gift cards quite frequently and I love it because it's a treat for which I don't normally indulge.  This location is pretty typical of what you'd expect from a Starbucks. It's bright and clean and  there is ample seating inside and outside, and a drive through, and they all seem to be pretty busy throughout the day. On my particular visit, there was quite a long line but it also very well staffed, so we did not have to wait very long to place or receive our order. This is a really great place to wait if you happen to be visiting the Tesla station. It's literally right next to it, and for heaven's sake you don't want to try to leave this plaza and come back...in the time it takes to fully charge, you would spend the majority of your time trying to get out and get back in. Sit back, relax, and enjoy people watching.

The baristas are very nice, great environment and drinks are pretty good. However they don't seem to know how to make a lot of the drinks on the app. The other thing is they turn the AC wayyyy up and you end up freezing sitting in there longer than 5min.

This Starbucks located in Clayton is pretty, neat, and clean every time I come in. The parking isn't always great with just one block on Wydown and busy Hanley but I can usually find a spot. It seems to be very popular with bicyclists on the weekends there is usually a crowd outside. The door handles state that this is a Starbucks Reserve which it seems like they have the capability to do some specialty brews. I generally order ahead after the gym and always get my drink made on time and correctly. A Starbucks is a Starbucks but as far as locations go this is one of my favorites in town.

So I don't drink coffee, if I did, however, I probably wouldn't drink it at Starbucks due to their preference of burning their beans....  That aside, this is my favorite Starbucks location in the St. Louis area... considering the fact that I used to live in Illinois, work in Brentwood, have a doctor off of Manchester (and one on W. Lockwood), and live in the city... that says a lot.   First: My two fave baristas there are Teresa and Erin, hands down. Friendly, they know my drink, know my name, and sometimes I even get smiley faces on my cup.  Second: It's a larger Starbucks, so it doesn't feel claustrophic like the one in the mall does AND the one on Manchester does.   Third: There is definite consistency in my drink. Granted I always get a Caramel Apple Spice... but this is the only location that I have experienced my drink tasting the exact same every time.  Fourth: They're nice even when they're busy. I once had a barista at the Brentwood Blvd location get really mean regarding the order that I ordered my drink -- at the time I was drinking Mocha White Mochas with a shot of espresso... and apparently I said it in the wrong order and made her life more difficult as a consequence (or so she told me). That's when I started drinking Caramel Apple Spices. Never looked back (fall in a cup).
Cleanest Starbucks I have ever been to. Staff is always friendly and are hard workers. I come here at least 3 times a week to work and have never been dissatisfied.
If you are looking for a quick drive thru---keep driving. Slowest Starbucks drive thru in the area.
Not my favorite Starbucks. It's cool inside and a good place to study but its always too packed, takes too long and the staff kinda sucks.

This is the fastest drive thru in America! It blows my mind. There can be a line of cars wrapped around the building at 8am and I have never waited longer than 5 min in the line. It's magical and everyone is always friendly. Nothing better than a delicious, well-made coffee, breakfast to-go and a friendly face telling you to have a fantastic day when you are on your way to work. Great place!
My favorite Starbucks in town. Near my Casa and is well staffed.  Even with a line they are quick and friendly.

Any Starbucks is usually great, as it's my cafe of choice.  This is the one I usually go to, as it's closest to my house.  I've always had decent service, and the drinks and food are fresh and taste great.  This cafe is clean and enjoyable for meeting friends at.

We were in Yardley and went to this Starbucks numerous times. I had their very large decaf latte (with only two shots) and it was made perfectly as you would always expect from Starbucks. AND they had current New York Times and Wall Street Journals for purchase (not often available unless you are in a kind of "class" area/place. Great views and ok seats,

Never seen a 24hr. Starbucks before. Great stop on the way back home from a beach weekend in Clearwater. While our drinks were being made David was very friendly and chit chatted with us. We appreciated his humor.

Disrespectful employees. Yelled and threaten to call the cops on my fiancee because all she was asking if the drink that the barista has made was hers. She asked 3 times and everyone of the employees ignored her. The barista gave her an attitude once he gave her his attention. My fiancee tried to speak with a manager but none of the employees wanted to cooperate to direct her to one. One of the employees claimed that he was the "manager" and refused to give her the District Manager information. A scene has already started in front of the many customers that have been or was being served. He ended up giving her the "manager" number which my fiancee called and when she asked who she was speaking to, they hung up on her. The managers number ended up being the store phone number. Nothing was resolved.  Never go here unless you're looking to fight with the disrespectful employees.

I have to agree with a previous review that this is, by far, the slowest Starbucks on the planet.  The staff is always friendly enough but when there is no line and there are only 2 people ahead of me, my Pumpkin Spice Latte should not take 10+ min from paying for it, to actually enjoying it.  Their slowness became a running joke between myself and my sister who used to be a barista.  I would text her as soon as placed my order, and then again when I had my drink in hand so she could witness (via text) how insanely slow they are.  Because it is close to work, I previously visited this location 2-3 times each week.  Not anymore!  My last visit was about 6 weeks ago.  The barista was having a conversation with another mall employee about how much she hated her job (inappropriate, perhaps?).  After 12 minutes, I asked about my coffee.  She had given it to the girl she was chatting with by mistake.  She apologized and said she would make me another drink.  But, I had to wait until she finished the drinks for the 3 customers who were waiting when we realized the mistake.  Don't even get me started about the huge difference between the quality of Starbucks in Nashville compared to Portland and Seattle (where I'm from).  Vastly different and an embarrassment to the company.  It's no wonder why my friends who are locals are not big Starbucks fans.

As a location, this Starbucks is difficult to say the least.  The line for the drive thru is frequently wrapped all the way around the store and maybe two or three cars waiting in line on 4th street as well. This is particularly the case in the morning when their can easily be 15  cars waiting.  The drive through line also makes it very difficult to park in the lot so you can't app order and pick up easily as well.    There is overflow parking behind the gas station next to it.  Inside, this is an amazingly comfortable coffee shop.  It is clean, wide open, and very accessible. Despite the volume of customers, they never seem to run out of what I'm looking for.  Customer service has always been great here for me as well!    Starbucks coffee will certainly scratch the itch but it's nothing to write home about.  It's good but it doesn't blow you away.

This place sucks! Not only could i not get a cappuccino because they didn't have enough espresso OR employees, but they didn't even have a building! It literally doesn't even exist!  You're better off going somewhere that is real, like the library or the DMV. At least THEY have coffe, sometimes it was even made that day.   Besides, Saxbys is a better cafe than Saxbys

This Starbucks drive thru is notoriously slow!  The only reason we ever go to this one is because it's close to our house.  Aren't they supposed to ask if you want your receipt?  We just stopped and she didn't ask if we wanted it and they got the order wrong.  We asked for a venti and got a grande.  Won't be going back to this one.  We'll drive out of our way to go to another one.

Our local Starbucks and a great location due to it's precise location, if that makes sense. This store is in the La Cumbre Plaza just off State Street. Not much seating inside, but do they need it? This is Santa Barbara. Weather is almost always perfect. The abundance of seating outside is what I like about this store. It's plentiful and peaceful.   Staff is super friendly and they'll get your orders right. I recently saw a woman drop her entire venti latte over one of the tall tables and it spilled all over the stools around it. A staff member immediately stopped what they were doing to help her clean it up and even made her a fresh one. Good friendly service if you ask me.  It can get decently crowded early in the mornings on the weekends, but this is why Starbucks has their mobile ordering system. If you visit a Starbucks even once a month and don't have the app, you're missing out. I usually order 3-5 minutes before I arrive and my coffee is always waiting for me when I get there. No line waiting at all.
This place is so filthy. I get it, it's a chain coffee shop. BUT, The bathrooms are always out of order or just nasty and gross, and the service is just the slowest.

This Starbucks is always busy and yet happens to be extremely understaffed each visit I've been. Wait times easily reach 15 minutes and that's on a good day.  The staff here has always been fairly unfriendly, which is unlike most of the stores I've been to. I do enjoy the Causeway store more, but this location's Drive-Thru is convenient.

This is pretty much as "good as it gets" for a Fourbucks. There is a drive through - but the walk-in service is even better with a spacious dining area - and super clean (brand new) facilities.  Excellent service and a full staff during the AM rush.  Plenty of parking.  Yay!!!

I just drove through the new edwardsville location! They are always so friendly and attentive so i continue to go back. Today i ordered 14 cake pops for the staff at my job and they went above and beyond to hook me up. My coworkers were ecstatic about the surprise and I will continue to go to this location only. Thank you so much for the awesome service and positive Sunday vibes!

It is very unfortunately if you have a bad bladder and have to use the restroom there is no where to use the restroom here in the city because most of the restaurants like this are not allowing the general public to use the restrooms

While Starbucks isn't the greatest coffee in the world, I have coke to be really attached to this location. The mid-day crew has come to know my order when I come in. I spend hours in here reading my bible and doing sermon prep.   I  the venti iced coffee, heavy cream and 3 stevia.
Ordered 2 coffees.  1 a pour over decaf Left after waiting 30 minutes Actually no stars but that's  not an option!

I have come here multiple times. I always order a milk alternative in my drink and pay the extra money (I am severely lactose intolerant) this is my 5th time at this Starbucks that I have received whole milk and ended up extremely sick. All day long. So if you have any dietary restrictions I'd keep a close eye on your barista here.

By far the worst Starbucks I've ever been to!! Ask for a Caramel Frappuccino & was given a black coffee. Never ever have I done a review so yeah it was absolutely horrible, by the time I realized my order was wrong it was too late. I understand there was a crowd but that is no excuse to totally screw up an order. Also my order was left out for someone else to grab because no one ever called my name!! They will never get my business again all customers should be treated with the same respect!
I've stopped at this Starbucks numerous times and I have to say it is the slowest Starbucks I have ever been to. Thankfully they do get your drinks correctly.

Showed up at 8:30 AM. Having barely been open a few hours the store looked like it had been through a holiday rush - disgusting bathrooms, overflowing trash cans, a sticky dirty floor. Truly embarrassing if I was the owner. It's as though the previous night's crew just closed up shop and the morning crew came in and also did absolutely nothing. Small things go a long way - clean your store.

Love the location and the design of this place. But ... The service is the worst that I've ever experienced at a Starbucks.  One friend shares on Facebook the following: "The female morning manager is an idiot, and rude on top of it all. I'll drive to the CWE to avoid this place. Glad I'm not alone in thinking this place is a cluster."  The place is gaining a bad reputation with people who would be very loyal to the store because of the history of the locatin. Unfortunately, the treatment of customers by the staff is chasing people away.  Starbucks needs to seriously get a staff attitude adjustment in place or it will probably only get worse.
New location is open - avoid it. Inside is dirty.  The service is pissy.  And the drinks made taste awful.
Worst Starbucks from Tampa to Orlando. 30 min wait in the drive through every time. No apologies. It's just how they function and don't ever strive to fix it.

I used to love going to this location...when there was a very nice barista (never caught her name) but I haven't seen her in awhile. Since her departure, Kim is always there and she is horrible. She's always chatting away with the other baristas, not helping customers in line or making drinks for the waiting customers. She is rude and her drinks are inconsistent. This last trip, I overheard her telling the other barista that she won't be getting the vaccine and will get a fake vaccination card. I stopped going to that location even when I'm shopping at Albertsons, I will go out of my way to the other locations.

The location of this Starbucks is everything. Walking in the heart of Downtown Indianapolis and enjoying a nice warm Starbucks cup of coffee is ideal. That's why this one is SO packed all of the time I'm convinced.   Don't be turned away when it's busy. They move quickly but they are just limited on space. I just patiently wait my turn and get the heck out of there. Not a great place to sit and relax.

The 4th location that I frequent regularly. I have been coming into this store for 4 years. Working in a extremely high volume store the partners at this location are consistently on it as a team. I have seen them move a packed (and I truly mean packed) store full of customers in and out with top customer service skills and teamwork. I have also seen them in slow times when they work together to make sure that the store is in top shape for the next shift coming in. They get hundreds of regular customers in and they remember the very way each likes to have their coffee made - even if it is a month or two since that customer has been in they still remember. I could choose to go to the other location around the corner from them, but... No. I prefer to stick with the one that has the best bar none.

Love this place, it's close to my house, and the staff here are energetic and amazing! I've been coming here as a regular now after my morning class to knock out 3-4 hours of homework! Can't complain since you get free wi-fi too! The customers are pretty friendly as well, I'll greet them and they greet back without a weird look! I can even leave my table to use the restroom or go out side to talk on the phone with my stuff all on the table and feel safe about everytime. So far no one has tried stealing or messing with my stuff so I'm happy for that! The coffee here taste great and haven't had a bad order yet. You can catch me here every Tuesdays and Thursdays for this Spring Semester!
Best customer service of any Starbucks I have ever been to! Smiley and friendly! Dana is such a fun employee, making us excellent drinks and loving life while doing so!

I didn't realize that the stores at the mall don't open until noon on Sundays, so I got there before the stores opened. Luckily, Starbucks opens earlier, and I stopped by to get a coffee while I waited for the stores to open. Just a Starbucks, but it was big enough and had seating. What else do you need in a Starbucks?

I've been here 2 days back to back and yes there's always a wait. One day they managed the line really well having one of the baristas come out and take orders of ppl waiting and writing the orders on drink sleeve. I really appreciate this at any and all Starbucks they do this at! It REALLY helps! Good job ;) today the line was about 10 mins but no one took the orders in advance...   All baristas are friendly and nice.   Can't comment on the mixed drinks much I'm a dark roast drip all the way. Kids enjoyed their fraps though.
very efficient and speedy Starbucks. even when the line is long, you won't have to wait too long for your order. also, the staff is very friendly

So, I'm ranking this Starbucks as a 3 compared to others in Tampa.  Why so low?  The parking here is a nightmare as is getting in and out of the actual parking lot.  They are seriously out of two of my favorite things....vanilla roobois tea and rice krispy treats (or rice dream bars as I think Starbucks calls them) at least 75% of the time.  I've asked them multiple times why they are consistently out of these items and no one seems to have a great answer.  The bathroom is normally pretty dirty too.    Giving them the benefit of the doubt though this is a very high volume Starbucks and their staff have always been warm, attentive and pleasant.  They are efficient as well for the volume of people they push through on a daily basis.

The staff here is really nice and the Starbucks is clean and overall convenient. However since Starbucks has come out with cold brew as the new product , I have been dying to try it. This is the third time I have gone to this location to order it and they do not have it. This is my neighborhood Starbucks so it's pretty disappointing that they are advertising a new drink that they don't carry. I had to leave to go to another one further away just so I could try the new product.

I'm not normally up in this area so it was probably my first and last time coming here. No complaints about this establishment. It was clean, had quick service, and my coffee and scone were delightful. Just about what you'd expect out of any Starbucks.

My investigation leads me to believe this is the store now known as 639 W Ina, not 7151 N Oracle.  I've hit them up several times through the drive-thru and service is average. Not the typical uber-nice Starbucks customer service experience but still pleasant.  I was mistaken in thinking this location opened at 4:30AM like the one at Ina/Thornydale. I had to be at work super early and came to the drive-thru at this location and stopped at the speaker box but could only hear what sounded like someone wiping down the mic or something. After a couple of  "Hello? Hello?"s without response I pulled up to the window. I was noticed within about 30 seconds and the woman went about unlocking the window and opening it. I asked if they were open and she told me no, they open at 5AM. I said, "Oh, OK, I thought you opened at 4:30 like Ina/Thornydale and Grant/Swan." She then said, no, 5AM and pulled the window shut.   As I put my car in drive and glanced at the clock in my car I saw that it was 4:58am. Really? She couldn't have offered to serve me 2 minutes before opening? I realize she didn't have to but c'mon...this is Starbucks, and they're known for great customer service.   I drove away disappointed and hit my regular store at Grant/Swan and headed in to work.

Tiffany is my favorite Barista. She has a smile on every Sunday Morning. She remembers my family and asks about them when they are not with me.  The rest of the staff has a group dynamic that makes you want to see them as much as you want your morning coffee.  They have created community in location that is frequented by people passing through on the highway.

I love love love this Starbucks. The baristas are very friendly! They make my drink very quickly too and it is perfect every time. They were having trouble with their cash register today but they were still very nice and calm. They took my order and I was able to pay with my Starbucks app that they can scan. We found a solution and I was able to get my coffee.

Starbucks is just OK.   They never make anything sweet enough for me so I always have to get extra flavors but they are coming up on their food menu which is always helpful.  Just wish they weren't so pricey.  Good quick drive thru though.
Pretty much standard for Starbucks. Smaller format with about six 4-person tables and 2 small bar height counters. Nothing bad, but nothing fancy about this location.

I went to Starbucks to purchase 1 frappe and decided to just added the points to my reward card. Nothing too hard. The employee ended up loading money to my rewards card without my permission and charging me for 2 frappes. I can't believe they would do that. If I go inside to fix the problem I know that they'll give me the same "it'll be 4-5 business days till the money is back on your original card" and then have to deal with the fact that it's not there. I'm not going to deal with that.

I used to work about 30 feet from this Starbucks and it has been hard to be far from it now. I never had to wait in a long line and my food and drinks were always done right. To make things even better they have the best and nicest baristas, shoutouts to Christina and Jermey for always going the extra step to make customers feel appreciated and welcome. Miss this Starbucks location!!

Service is ok, but it's crowded with people who buy one coffee then decide to homestead taking up all the seats. Doesn't your college have study areas? Instead of sitting to enjoy my coffee for ten minutes, I'm in my car. Thanks Millennials!

I used to come to this Starbucks almost weekly on Friday nights after a dinner out.  It had been a while and stopped in here with a friend to get a coffee.   The parking lot appeared full but there were only two people ahead if us.  By the time we picked up our drinks, there were set least 10 people on line.   It took a while to get our drinks even though there were six people working the counter.   Everyone was polite and there was quite a buzz among baristas.  Coffee was as expected, good, but there is a reason they call Starbucks "four bucks."  There has been some redecorating with a modernistic feel.    The big, comfy couch is gone and the have put in a divider in the seating area to make the space seem less cavernous.  There is also outside seating and they pipe the music to the outside as well.  An OK experience.

First of all, I am not one to complain. I am a loyal Starbucks customer but would not recommend this site with current front of store staffing. In the 15 minutes I was in the store I observed that most food orders were either forgotten to be processed or resulted in lengthy delays. I witnessed multiple customers leave because of how slow the ordering process was and the lack of customer service.  In my little time at the store this morning I saw three drive-thru customers come back in to get their order corrected.As I heard one drive-thru customer say when they pulled away from the window-Huge Fail at Starbucks today.

This store is consistently slow and dirty. The employees DO NOT care about customers and seem either lost, disinterested or both.   The ONLY reason I come here is that it is convenient.  Maybe they know they have a captive audience and take customers for granted.   How does management allow this to continue?

The worst Starbucks ever! After sitting in the drive-through for 30 minutes to finally get two drinks, my cappuccino was made incorrectly. I then handed it back to have it re-made and when it was made again, I was handed a drink that was only 3/4 of the way full and still not made correctly. I had to go inside, write my own drink cup, wait another 10 minutes and finally got my drink. Thankfully it was made right this time and the girl on bar was nice and knew how to make a drink. I am appalled at how bad of an experience I had today and will never return to this location.

Love Starbucks but this location is pretty slow...customer service is nice & pleasant & they still make ur Starbucks coffee favs delicious but could use some extra people to deliver beverages a little timelier!  this location is also open 24 hrs so great for anytime enjoyment plus it's right off I4 so it's pretty convenient for most peeps on the go...hopefully, if u get lucky u won't have to wait as long for ur drinkable delight!

The drive through at this location is AWFUL. There are two entrances to the drive through, which means that every morning an entire line of waiting cars gets skipped as someone pulls into the other entrance and gets in line ahead of them.  I am giving this Starbucks only one star purely for the drive through design. Everything else at this location (drinks, food, employees, etc) has been great.

Coffee is a lot harder to find than daqueries in this part of town, so Starbucks would have been fine for a fix, this is on the far side of the Marriot lobby, went in at 1:30 in the afternoon and they were already closed, WTF?
Though long overdue, the recent renovation makes this a great coffee shop with a variety of indoor seating options to satisfy all. Also excellent outdoor area.  As always, the coffee is the best.
Fast and friendly service.  The drink is on par from other Starbucks.  So far, no indoor nor outdoor seating.  Just drive thru or to-go orders.

The local Starbucks where everybody knows your name. Well, at least they do when you come here almost everyday!  My favourite Starbucks location, I've been coming here since the days of University and crazy exam schedules. The baristas are friendly, the drinks are consistent, there is lots of parking, and the bathrooms are always clean.  A word to the wise, avoid the frappucino rush. What is the frapp rush you ask? The 3:15ish time on the weekdays when junior and senior high students alike congregate en mass from the transit station and all order frappucinos. If you are here during frapp rush, be prepared to wait and rediscover your inner 15 year old self!

You have a very rude (continuously) female barista who must hate her job, or hate life in general in the morning hours before 9am on the weekdays. I've experienced this and have seen her be rude to others as well. She either needs a cup of coffee or a reality check that she's making other people's mornings not so pleasant. Makes me want to go to another coffee shop.

Not sure why but I can't get this location to show up for mobile ordering ever, even though the website says it's available.  Employees are always friendly.  Would use this location more if mobile ordering started working.

Since this place is a chain, I think I'll spare the description of the food/drinks.  The location isn't necessarily the most convenient.  The door is placed in an awkward position on the side on a smaller street perpendicular to Delmar.  It's a little annoying to get into the establishment and find parking.  However, there is a huge lot behind the loop that's free.  So, if you don't mind the walk,  it is the easiest and most convenient place to park.  The reason I gave this place two stars is the service.  I've been here a couple of times on off hours. It seems that the staff is not that attentive.  (Maybe I've just been unlucky both times, because this never seems to happen to my friends that frequent this place).  Both times, I have had to wait several minutes before someone would take my order (even when there isn't a line) and there are other baristas that seem to be free.

Stopped in to add money to my card & place an order.  Staff seemed new & could not get my order correct.  When paying, was advised that my card was locked?!?!  Shift supervisor couldn't help, very frustrating.  Had to call Corporate the following day.  They could clearly see what the store had done.....they voided out my addition to my card.  Corporate fixed the issue, however, I was advised that this clearly should have been performed at the store.  Please TRAIN your staff correctly.

Came here when one of my high schools students did not want to go into Wet Seal... so we came here and talked about his college options etc.... The night crew was really nice and they chatted with their regulars while cleaning up for the night... no complaints here

Standard Starbucks experience. This one located inside the Marriott, so be prepared for long lines at peak times, especially during conventions. They do a pretty good job getting people through the line quickly with a small staff, though.
The coffee is your Starbucks selection and quality. There's a lot of seating at this location. It's clean.

Great location, clean, friendly staff, and a really nice outdoor seating area. This is one of my favorite Starbucks around. It's newer than most, so it has a modern feel with the wood fence outside, fresh coat of beige paint, and comfortable inside seats. They know how to personalize the drinks you order, so no need to walk inside to get your drink remade. Yay! Enjoy! Xo
Friendly service and nice seating area. Staff was nice even after I accidentally broke a mug! Sometimes lines were long in the morning.
Staff is friendly and quick to fill orders. Ambience is nice and there seems to be plenty of space to work in the main area, attached room, or outside. There's a drive thru, too.

Wanted a quick Starbucks stop for coffee and treats and this location was great!! Fresh coffee, yummy bakery items, and very prompt service!! Definitely recommend if you're driving through or in the area!

What can I say?  It's Starbucks.  They do have some of the more friendly Starbucks employees I've encountered at this location.  I think because of its proximity to 465 and Carmel, there is ALWAYS a line at the drive-thru.  You will get much faster service and be much less irritated just parking and going inside for your coffee.  Trust me, you don't want to be stuck behind the Carmel soccer mom ordering her Venti 1 pump caramel, 1 pump white mocha, 2 scoops vanilla bean powder, extra ice frappuchino with 2 shots poured over the top (apagotto style) with caramel drizzle under and on top of the whipped cream, double cupped.

I agree with Stacy L.  I've been staying at the Marriott for a conference since Sunday, and every day I go in, I feel like I get nothing but bad news.  They don't honor the Gold card free refills, soy, or syrups.  They're usually out of something every day...almonds when I want just a light snack - "oh sorry, we ran out."  Very small selection of sandwiches, and haven't seen a protein plate at all in this place.  Staff usually acts like they don't give a damned.  They close super early...what Starbucks in the US do you know that closes at 5pm? Do yourself a favor and bring your business to the Sheraton across the Street, or one of the smaller, independent coffee shops nearby like Rue de La Course (too many in NOLA to miss) who actually care about their customers and selling a good product, rather than passing the time doing as little as possible until they get off work to go party down the street.

If I could give it 0 stars I would. The employee at the drive through window was extremely unfriendly. Not at all a good experience. I won't be returning to this location. I'll buy my $5+ coffees somewhere else where customers are treated kindly.
Easily accessible and the standard fare of Starbucks that you'd normally expect.  No complaints! I parked on the street in the yellow zone while my cohort ran inside for the goods!

I love coming to this Starbucks location.. Westpark Village is a very pet friendly neighborhood, which is a plus because not many businesses are pet friendly anymore. The staff is very friendly, accommodating and they work as a team to get all customers in and out as quickly as possible.
I don't always drink coffee, but when I do -- I drink Dunkin' Donuts. It's much better than Starbuck's, which tastes terrible.

Quick access, good drinks, a few indoor seats, and consistently good drinks!  I like this location for it's convenience because in the mornings it's a little slower than the location down the street. While it doesn't have a large indoor area to sit down it does have a few seats where there are a couple of regulars that offer a kind a greeting. If you feel like chatting, it is never a challenge to find someone to talk with. Usually, I need to get some grad school work done (or catch up on yelp reviews) and I hide out behind my computer. The seating outside the store is plentiful.  The baristas are always helpful and it seems that they could use a couple of extra hands, but they are good at what they do and always kind.
Pretty good Starbucks. Nice patio. 2 wishes: open till 11pm in summer & wipe off outdoor tables occasionally. I am a regular here. They just upgrade their furniture and havea lot mores seating.

What sets a Starbucks location apart from another?  The peeps behind the green apron...THATS WHAT!!  This location rocks !!  Quick and friendly exemplify this downtown Reno location. We enjoyed our daily jump start here during our recent ski trip. Visit this location to enjoy FIVE STAR service if you DARE!!

I've been to this Starbucks location couple of times and the experience of getting coffee here always been the same, HORRIBLE! Minus the dirty counters and grumpy baristas who visibly hate their job including the coffee they made you they also will make you wait for it. The tipping point for me was when I ordered a simple drink (Tall Pike) went to get Half & Half at the dirty counter but found it to be empty, skim milk - empty, whole milk - empty, I asked barista if I could get more Half & Half, she looked at me and said sure! then ignored me for good 10 minutes until I left, other baristas also ignored me while being busy hating on their job. I went to the near by gas station to get me Half & Half for the coffee I purchased at the Starbucks. (Anyone else see anything wrong with this?)  So If you still decide to come here, get in line for slow crevice and free humiliation.   Next time I'll come here, but who am I kidding I will never come here again.

Great little neighborhood Starbucks. Our favorite. Not as busy. More likely to be soccer moms or dads after workouts than the corporate rush. Love it! Saturday mornings are best. Drinks well made and baristas know what they're doing!

I've rarely had bad experiences at Starbucks, as I've been a barista and know how difficult it is to make people's drinks perfectly, and be accommodating. So I really don't expect much.. But today when I was with my mom I had a really strange experience. Let me start by also saying that my mom visits Starbucks EVERY DAY and spends a ridiculous amount of money there. I don't know how she does it. If there was an ultra golden gold membership, she would have it. Every other Starbucks in the area knows her order and is never short of super accommodating. But today we went to this one on the way to Philly instead.   We proceed to order at the drive thru, and my mom orders her usual: 4 shots of iced espresso with half and half.   Upon receiving our drinks my mom asked the barista if she could have another small splash of half & half in her drink, because the color was a little darker than she likes. This is not that strange of a request. She likes a lot of half and half and I have heard her ask for this a million times. Sometimes the baristas are generous enough to give her a full cup on the side. (You can even go inside and walk away with the whole pitcher of cream at the service bar, and they never bat an eye). But either way I have never seen her have a problem. Today, however, the barista working the morning shift looked at us like we had 5 heads. She said she would have to charge us for a latte if she added a splash more of half and half. A SPLASH!!!! Okay now, even though my mom likes her half and half, I can assure you there wasn't nearly enough of it in the drink to consider it a LATTE. She insisted that she had to charge us for a latte even after we mentioned that she only wanted a splash more, and that she'd never been charged for a latte before for asking the same. They even said that we should come inside and add more at the service bar ourselves, but they couldn't do it for us. UM. We went to the DRIVE THRU for a reason???   I don't really think these baristas know what they are doing. Super rude and unaccommodating. Also, what happened to the "Don't like your drink? Our Baristas would be happy to remake it for you" motto? More like "Don't like your drink? Too bad, fix it yourself or pay for a different drink" at this Starbucks. Won't be returning to this Starbucks unless they deal with their staff.

fancy new building can't atone for rude self entitled help. Quality is poor and drinks are not made to order.  The staff here needs to either be retrained or purged.  Go to the Flourtown location better staff and much better quality.

HORRIBLE customer service & to boot $10+ they gave us 2 coffee and milk when we paid for specially lattes....Not impressed.  I've been a "Starbuckets" drinker for years and always looked forward to the Holiday flavors.  Was in a giving mood & decided to treat a co-worker after lunch.  OK so she has a complex coffee order (I zoned out somewhere in the cinnamon, triple, no foam, whip...)  The gal in drive-through had to ask a few times herself to get it right....no biggie.  Get to the window and waiting for order I could sense hostility....the gal makes a comment about "head-set not always working" co worker said it's OK you got it right, insert awkward pause, then "you seemed ungrateful"  HUH?!?   I was FLABBERGASTED thought WE were the customer and your goal is to get my order that I paid for.  I was floored. After a "manager" gets snarky with us and rolls her eyes we pull away at this point scared to drink coffees and realize they just gave us coffee and milk not the drinks ordered.   NOT coming again to this location and suggest unless you are into S&M don't go there yourself.  Thank goodness the folks at corporate are decent and understanding.

Great location, friendly baristas, always receive correct order. Parking can be a challenge because the lot is small and usually full. Drive thru is my favorite way to go. Convenient, efficient, and if you order ahead you can zip through the drive thru as well. A win-win!

The good: Nice location. Outdoor seating is way cool.  Near the Clearview Mall. Friendly Baristas.  They have stock, when others don't.  Near a gang of eateries. Love the bar style window seating.    The not so good: I find this location to be a little more stuffy than others, stuffy as the patron wise. I don't care for the drive thru, it's space deficient & a little hard to navigate. Parking is very limited.  Easily missed when driving, seems to be just stuck in a 'tiny, oh this will do' space.  But I'm glad they had my Eggnog Latte on tonight... tis the season.

Every time I visit this location I end up waiting anywhere from 10-15 minutes to get my coffee in the drive through. Given the fact that this is the only Starbucks near CWE that has a drive through I continue to visit it. The employees are nice but the service moves at snails pace.

This is the slowest Starbucks ever. Everytime it never fails. Do they train someone here everyday?? They are never short staffed so I can't put my finger on it. The people are always nice but geez it's always low. Guarantee when you pull up the first thing is , " welcome to Starbucks please wait for a moment and I'll be right with you.  So annoyinnnggggggg

Just a regular Starbucks. Pretty good service and a weird trendy atmosphere. You can watch them make your order which kind of sucks because I see them using Wal-Mart milk and generic ingredients which beats the purpose of getting expensive coffee!
Have used them a couple times for coffee "travelers" to go. Always ready when I come to pick it up. Friendly staff.  didn't get 5 stars because i agree location is difficult to access.
This the best Starbucks in the area! The staff is kind and funny. They always make a great drink and a bad day better. The staff is beyond great!!!! This is my favorite by far!

This place is a disaster.  SLOW is the only way to describe the service.  Not staffed properly or with well trained individuals.  Then to add to problems the parking lot and drive thru are the size of a shoe box.
Love this location! The baristas are always up beat and friendly, knowledgeable and faat. They updated the seating and added. Ore tables but took away the comfy arm chairs to relax in.

Don't go here often, but stopped in recently two consecutive mornings. The staff's too laid back - seems to be a South Street culture of "easy does it". They don't know how to move a long line (or a short one for that matter) like most Starbucks.   The 2nd visit one person in line (me) at 8:40am. There's three employees behind counter two working on drinks. The third, a manager standing 3 feet away, sees me waiting and then looks away - she's too busy and concerned with doing the inventory with a hand held electronic device. Apparently customers in line are less important than completing inventory.   From now on it's Chapter House cafe just down the block.
Poor customer service, long wait, and not very clean.... Next time we'll make the extra drive to find another location.

I don't usually review big chains since everyone is familiar with it already, but I really liked the barista here so I needed to share.  I believe his name was Alex. My LivingSocial Starbucks eGiftcard was expiring, so I HAD to use it. I don't really know why I even bought it since I'm not a coffee drinker. I'm just a sucker for deals. Anyway, I was taking my time deciding, but he didn't mind. He offered his help, so I explained my thought process. "I don't want coffee, so I'm thinking a creamer, but I don't know what flavor. Which ones taste better as creamers?" He was ready with a list to rattle off, along with his own opinions on them. Made my choice easier.  My gift card only had $2 left on it. My boyfriend's gift card also only had $2 on it. So when we were about to throw his away, Alex gave us a tip to save it. If we register it online, add $5, Starbucks gives us another $5 for free. Sweet! (Again, I'm a sucker for deals.)
Over priced and not friendly at all. Go here for free wifi and comfy places to seat? I think not!

Starbucks stores are slowly getting the attitude that they're invincible. Like "you need us." And no apologies. Yes, there are exceptions. But I can lo grade more stores than hi these days.  It's pretty foolish, as corporate empires take decades to build, and weeks to crumble, it seems.  I think of what some knowledgeable person told me in Seattle. "We like to think of SBUX as the McDonald's of coffee - mediocre product available everywhere." So to me, SBUX boils down to ambience and friendly service. And while they can precisely engineer the ambience of each store, the customer service is always the wild card in every case.  This store, like so many others, is sadly one of the majority. Inconsistent cashier service, long times to make drinks, and indifference toward the sale are hallmarks. Yes you have a few nice people behind the bar. But for the majority back there, it's the tired and true SBUX mantra: You Need Us.

I understand that this location is in a high traffic area but this morning I waited almost 25 minutes for a MOBILE ORDER. The staff was visibly stressed out but failed to let customers, who for the most part were running late, that the water pressure either was low or just not working.   A couple that ordered (via the app) grabbed their drink maybe 5 minutes before I did and a lady that had a drink being prepared at the same time as me just told them to give her her order and she'd add the water herself when she made it to work.
Great location but limited parking. Lots of drive-thru traffic. One block west of Hanley on Delmar.

The worst and slowest Starbucks on the planet.  I have gone to Starbucks all over the world and there isn't one more poorly set up or worse service.  They need to tear it down and rebuild it because you can't even get in or out!

Came here for my usual iced venti half-caf Americano with three pumps of sugarfree vanilla and room for cream.   There was like -2 parking spaces so I had to put my hazards on and be "that person" to get my drink since I sure as hell wasn't driving to find a spot for a 5 minute coffee run.  Cute place, deck looks out on the river which is nice.

Huge shout out to Batista TOM left my wallet at home, & thru didn't have tap & pay here for me to pay with my phone. So his kindness and generous heart bought me lunch. There's still kindness in the world. I feel so grateful and inspired now.

Spent $4.91 on steamed milk with caramel sauce. Not a single shot of espresso anywhere, definitely not what I consider a caramel latte. I came in around mid-afternoon when it wasn't very busy either. Very amateur mistake from an expensive coffee franchise.

If I'm paying $6 for a specialty drink, I expect them to fill it to the top. I go to this Starbucks all the time and sometimes they are great and other times they struggle. I normally complain but I didn't have time today. It's ridiculous. Give the customer what they are paying for.
We LOVE THIS STARBUCKS!*!  Everyone is friendly and very very fast at concocting delicious drinks! :)  Try the vanilla bean creme ! YUM!

You would think that with this being a new location and with 5 people working there, and no drive thru line, and no one ahead of me .... that I'd have a great experience / wrong. I already wrote to Starbucks because not only did my order get made wrong once , but twice. The girl ringing me up had a problem ringing me up so she asked another coworker for help which I guess might have been the problem but when the first words that come out of your mouth for an order are "iced" you expect to see an iced drink , not a hot drink. Well they made me a hot drink. And then I told them that I ordered it iced they remade it - although looking at me like it was my fault , perplexed that there could have been such a huge mistake made on any of their parts - they remade it  But it was still wrong. Since I had groceries in the car I just left and wrote to Starbucks. When you spend $6 on a coffee you want to get your order correct. And in case you're thinking I probably ordered something outrageous, I didn't. I ordered a premade drink off of their menu.  This place is pretty- but it wasn't even clean and although it is large almost all the seating was full. It also was a tad dark. When I'm in the area I'll definitely skip out. That's what happens when you judge a book by its shiny new cover I guess!
Jake is easily the best barista Ive ever seen. It was so busy,  but Jake maintained a great attitude and worked very hard.  He deserves recognition by management

This Starbucks is fine.  It is, however, small, and it gets very busy at certain times of day, with a lot of people picking up coffee on their way onto the blue route or the turnpike.  During rushes, the employees crank through orders more quickly than at a lot of other Starbucks, but you can still have a long wait in cramped environs.  Also, the parking lot is small and getting out of it can get complicated.  The product is the Starbucks usual and the employees are good, but it's not the fastest or easiest place to stop at busy times and the seating is limited so it's not the best Starbucks if you're looking for a place to have a conversation or to sit back and sip your coffee.

Worst Starbucks I have ever been to. One worker on a Saturday morning, stuff all over the place, out of caramel and other items, nothing in the cases. Looks like the remnants of a hurricane hit this store. Will never come here again as I don't have time for this nonsense. Going on 15 minutes waiting for my drinks now.
The already over priced Mocha quickly turned $160 cup of Mocha, when the lid was closed on the paper seams of the cup. Realized it dripped all over my pants and shirt when it was too late.
Not my favorite Starbucks! The staff is always subpar and my coffee is never up to standard. The parking sucks, it's only for the people that stay in the hotel, or walking off the street..
Love me some Starbucks, but the line and parking is very busy. But nothing will stop me from getting a Grande Raspberry, White Mocha Cappuccino :-)

Starbucks is a Starbucks. And this one is really not much different. I do have an interesting front patio though. Still the top is not enclosed, instead it's just rafters. So it provide some shade which doesn't help much in the Arizona summer. Also, this is the third time I've been here and they continue to get my order wrong. I had to go back inside to get it changed out. What's more, is they have seating inside suited more for students. Lots of tables; not soft easy chairs. Oh well, at least the people who work here are nice!

This is definitely the slowest Starbucks I've ever been to.  There were 4 people behind the counter and it still took about 15 min to get a drink.  2 people were handling the drive-through, 1 was cleaning and the person that took my order at the counter disappeared for about ten minutes, then came back and went on a 10 min break.  I got to watch many drive-through drinks go out and finally after a solid 15 minutes I received mine. I wish 222 Artisan was closer I would never step foot in this place.

Horrible experience. Coffee grounds in a Pike and no syrup for a basic favorite. It was mid-afternoon, really?! The barista was in a league of her own. Blonde female with glasses, pony tail and headscarf who huffed, puffed & rolled her eyes when asked anything. Slid drinks to customers with the obligatory thank you. She was not only rude to customers, but to her colleagues. Never going back to this one. You shouldn't either. Walk a few blocks to 5th Street or across the street to Whole Foods.

The Irvington Starbucks is my favorite Starbucks by far!!!  I come in just about everyday and the place is clean and they have amazing staff!!!  I come in so much they know my name and they always engage me in conversation.  It's a good work environment and the music is always good here!

The employees at this Starbucks know their customers really well. The drinks are good and the service was friendly. This is Starbucks really small and narrow so don't plan on going there if you want work done because noise echoes a lot.

They have the Clover machine here! Baristas are very friendly. I had a nice conversation with Andy while he was brewing my Nicaragua Reserve coffee. Learned so much more about the Clover itself as well as the coffee I was drinking.  Clean interior, attractive design, GREAT Starbucks!
This was the closest Starbucks to where I am staying. Service was fast and efficient. There was plenty of seating. This location is at the Oxford Park Centre.

OK, we all know what you get at Bux, so it's really no different.  What makes a great Starbucks is the staff...it really is.  I used to work there, so I know what to look for in my "third place".  I moved to the Carmel area last year and recently switched daycares, which takes me past this wonderful drive thru location.  1st, score for the drive thru!  2nd, score for the fabulous staff!  I would say I do like their morning staff A LOT more than their night staff.  I come in OFTEN...I'd say - 4-5 days a week and in the a.m. I go through DT.  I have officially become a "regular" as today, the woman who works the window recognized my voice and immediately starting repeating my order.  She oftentimes will also give me a warm chocolate chip cookie if I ask really nicely.  I am a little disappointed in the night staff as they just that aren't into me.  That's OK...I guess.  Maybe it's b/c I am showing 24 weeks PG and they are judging my caffeine intake...I don't know.  This is my "go to" for nighttime meetings for Beach Bum as well.  There is typically a lot of tables available and everything is always clean! Score for the night shift.    Atmosphere and staff, people....this Starbucks has it.  I consider this my new third place!    While writing this, I am enjoying the perfect 1/2 decaf, grande, non-fat, no whip three pump white mocha stirred w/a straw. They nail it every time!

After Spin class, nine of us went to Starbuck's first ever Evening Hours Wine night. The weather was perfect; we enjoyed sitting outside . Everyone ordered wine. The service was a little slow, maybe because this was the first day to serve wine? The staff was friendly and helpful. A pleasant lady brought our drinks to us and asked if we needed anything else. Later she came back and cleared our dirty dishes.  The wine is served in glasses. Nice. And much to our surprise, a snack of seasoned pumpkin seeds was included. Everyone seemed pleased about that.  The wine was a good quality, mine was delicious. I heard several comments about how good it was.  However, everyone seemed to agree that the pour was extremely small. And for the price-  seemed a little chintzy.  Starbuck's, please reconsider the pour size and make it comparable to the wine quality and the price. Do that and I will come back with a Five Star Rating! We were all excited to know you are now having wine night.
It is probably the worst Starbucks service in the city. Coffee is fine if they get it right. There are much better coffee places near by.
Yowza! Knowledgeable and chicly cool. Best baristas we've found at this point.  They need to be with the constant coffee chuggers churning through the doors. Get your 'Bucks on here.

This Starbucks, like most, is uber busy between the hours of 7AM and 8AM. But the staff is cheery, the facility is clean and the chairs are comfy. Safe yourself sometime and go in, rather than waiting for the drive up; the line insides seems to be speedier than the line outside.

Starbucks is Starbucks. Pretty much all the same, except for the inventory and the employees.  Every morning they were "out" of something I wanted. And they were never particularly apologetic about that. And they all seemed super apathetic.  Three stars because caffeine when you're jetlagged and at a boring conference is ALWAYS a minimum of three stars. But certainly no more.
I've noticed a few improvements at this location recently. The staff is obviously working hard and making improvements.
I need some coffee and some internet.  The store had music so loud it was painful.  I packed up and left after I could not get employees attention to turn down the noise.
Always a table open.  Great Wi-Fi signal.  More cushy chair and sofa options than any other Starbucks in the area.
