So, I'm driving on Sunday afternoon heading from Tampa to Orlando, looking forward to a good jolt of Starbucks coffee to keep me awake. After waiting about a half an hour looking for a Starbucks sign, I see one for this location.  I pulled into the lot, and it was pretty busy. I figured I could live with that, knowing that my local Starbucks moves pretty fast through the line. I was sorely mistaken. When I walked in, probably 15 people in line and virtually no one sitting down. After standing there for 10 minutes and not seeing any progress. Myself and a few others bailed. The person in front of me said they seemed more focused on serving the people in the window than people in the building.  I walked over to McDonald's and got a couple of apple pies, coffee and was on my way. The people in front to me still had not been served.  This is not typical of my experience with Starbucks.  Caveat emptor!

I do love the fact that it is in the lobby of my hotel, but I give it five stars for more than that.  The staff here was exceptionally friendly and when they made a mistake they went overboard to make good on it.  I went to the free standing location 1 block away and it was not the same customer service.  Great experience here!

This is probably the worst Starbucks I've ever been to.  It's a shame that this is the one I now live closest to, because I frequent Starbucks.  But some of the employees here are so rude that it makes me never want to come back.  I have been coming to this Starbucks fairly regularly for over a year now.  Orders are frequently forgotten/botched, drinks have been poorly made on more than one occasion (I'm talking the same old crappy ice in an iced coffee refill or very little ice in a venti latte, to the point where you take a swig of warm latte), and some employees are downright rude.  If I had experienced this poor service one or two or three or four times over the course of a year then fine; that can happen anywhere.  But I'm talking cross your fingers...you don't know what you're going to get when you come here.  You get a bad experience about 30% of your visits here.  It's important to note that this rudeness does not apply to every employee at this location, however.  I have experience the entire staff there and a handful are nice, cheerful people who do not give the customer any attitude.  Many, though, are the exact opposite.  Unacceptable.

Love this Starbucks. It has a great view of the river and bridge from the porch and serves the same great coffee you'd expect in any Starbucks. Just make sure you know that around 7:30 am it's typically very crowded from people going to work or students getting their morning coffee. Also, going to this Starbucks around 3-3:30 on a weekday is dangerous as it is just about a 5 minute walk from a middle school and a high school, so often times you get crowds of kids coming in that take quite a bit of time to order and are very loud. If you avoid these times, though, this place is great.
An interestingly shaped Starbucks location in a strip mall. There is good amounts of seating here, but service is slow.

I've been using starbucks as a second office for years. I just moved my family to a nearby location and so I've begun frequenting this starbucks. Food and drinks are good. The place is clean. But the staff seem really stressed out or something. The lady at the front counter was just acting weird and saying strange things, and she needed me to repeat myself a few times.  The other staff didn't seem aware of the fact I stood at the counter for two minutes hoping to get a packet of honey. Again, it's 7:00 AM on a Saturday, I wouldn't blame them for being exhausted. I'm sure I'll add more stars once the staff gets friendlier and more present.

Love this location. It's our tradition to stop before church on Sunday's. I've seen this place super busy and the crew works quickly and efficiently. My drink has always been great. The staff is friendly. The decor is my favorite in Reno.

Its hard to believe that a company this big allows it's employees to dawdle and move as slowly as they do. I work in the service industry, I know first hand that what they do is not hard, and should not take long at all. I waited 8 minutes for my frap with nobody in front of me. Insane. I love Starbucks but this locations lack of speed and general nonchalant attitude makes me fear being late for work every time I go in.

This is one of my "go-to" spots!  In addition to stopping here on my way to work, I spend a lot of time, setting up camp, and doing work here. The staff is very cool and really know their regulars, and, most importantly, the place is always super clean.  Just really dig the vibe of this Starbucks. The only thing I wish this place had was a Clover machine, but from what I understand, that's a corporate decision.  But, yea, overall I give this place 2 thumbs up....oh wait...we're doing stars....overall, I give it 5 stars!

Slow af. You will wait at least 15 minutes for no reason. It's too early for this type of frustration. Tf and they are fully staffed. One on register, one at window, 2 making drinks and one just walking around like he's trying to find his way.  Fucking ridiculous. First and last time here.

In reviewing what it takes to get 5 stars I have decided to remove one star. My previous reviews stand and please read them. Four stars is still very good and I have enjoyed most of my visits here. It just doesn't live up to what I consider is 5 stars now, which is paying attention to the details of the business and getting almost all of them right.

I went to buy a gift card. No one said hello. The music was insanely load though, so maybe they said it lightly. Either way obviously they didn't care. I picked out my gift card. Brought it up front. The girl just stared at me. So I told her I needed to put $10 on this gift card. She then looked at me like I was an idiot and asked if I needed to preload more money. I told her no, I needed to get this gift card (the one I handed her) for $10. To which she replied in a very condescending tone you want to buy a gift card with a gift card. I said no again and said I needed to get this card with $10 on it. To which she came back with a where did you get this card. I told her at the rack in the front and pointed to it. She then just rang it up and said $10. Overall my experiences with this Starbucks are lackluster. The employees are unnecessarily rude and blasé. I 100% would not recommend visiting this location. It's a beautiful Starbucks in a pippin' location with the RUDEST staff ever.

I skipped my Brentwood Starbucks in favor of trying out this location. The Brentwood location is very slow and the parking lot is like playing chicken which is getting old everyday. This Rock Hill location seems to be pretty new and looks really clean. I was able to order and get through the line much faster than what I've been used to down the street. The employees were friendly and my order was made correctly. They even apologized for my wait which wasn't even half as long as I would have waited in Brentwood with no apology. This seems to be worth the extra couple minutes drive I will have to come back and check it out during the morning rush soon.

Love the feel of this Starbucks. Nice layout with ample room for reading, studying, meeting, etc. There is kind of a community area with comfy chairs as well as a big round table in the center conducive to larger groups.  The aesthetic is a little more industrial, which is kind of cool. And finally, a convenient Starbucks location in midtown/downtown/SLU! So nice.  And yes, there is a drive thru.
Visiting from way out of town, wonderfully amazing customer service both times I was at the location.

This Starbucks has some of the friendliest Baristas in town. I visited right before they were about to close with a group of friends and there was no rush whatsoever. In fact, they were very lively and were playing a game with their manager to see if they could get a customer to do the Zena Warrior call. Well, luckily they picked the right people because my friends and I are always up for a good challenge. In return for participating, they gave me and my friends all a free drink. It was the most fun I've personally experienced within a Starbucks. Definitely made my night!!

Stuff is awesome the service is fast and amazing. This Starbucks is always busy but you never wait to long.  Starbucks coffee is a little over priced but once in a while its good. Love the holiday flavors. Pumpkin spice one of the best of all times.

An average of 3 stars after 2 visits.  So convenient that they have a drive thru and the line moved rather quickly.  Great that there are two exits to either go east/west on route 38.  We sat inside second time around and it was empty on a Saturday morning.  It was clean except for spills on the sugar/milk table (didn't know what to call it, sorry).  Minus 2 stars because they did not have cold brew and i did not really care for the baristas being petty towards one another.  They were lacking professionalism.  I don't really care to hear them bicker about who get's to go on break first.  This chatter is unnecessary to hear 1st thing in the morning sans caffeine.  Please don't make your patrons feel uncomfortable.

Drive-thru line is always wrapped around the building and moves at a snails pace. Decided to do a mobile order instead and just go inside. Found out it is so slow because there are only 3 people working (1 at the register...making drinks and food too, 1 making drinks in the back for the drive-thru primarily, and 1 running the drive-thru window...while also making drinks and food). Seems really disorganized and understaffed. The woman asked what I was waiting for, and I told her a mobile order, so she fumbled through the receipts and went and made it...and then brought it out saying it was "hiding" in the back...when it was obvious she just made it.

Super convenient location and great staff. Everyone was extremely friendly, efficient and helpful. There was a short line when we arrived but everyone's help very quickly and our coffee and food was ready in no time. Lots of parking and easy to get to from the freeway.

Rating a Starbucks is not easy breezy. There is a Starbucks scale, and there is a NOLA java scale. I will combine the two into one.   I would prefer other local coffee places to Starbucks (three stars on NOLA java scale), but out of all the Starbucks(es?) in the area, this is my number one favorite (5 stars on Starbucks scale)! I do love their hot sandwiches, especially on the go for Bfast, as well as their coffee frapps. The service is always quick and pleasant. There is some parking behind the building (wohoo!).  I average the two to make it four stars. And they all lived caffeinatedly ever after!

No two Starbucks are alike! Well... they're close but this location definitely stands out. There are two locations within walking distance of my work and I would choose this one anytime.  Why? For one, the staff are always upbeat and friendly. They're always joking around with each other and it seems like a fun work environment. Also, while the lines can get long, they always seem to go quickly. Sometimes I order ahead on my cell phone and my drink is always ready to go when I get there. The drinks here are always made well and the few times they've run out of something I wanted, the staff always have a similar back-up to suggest.   The seating inside is cozy and comfortable and they have a nice outdoor seating area for those hot summer days. I think I'm going to head over there now for an afternoon pick-me-up!

Starbucks is Starbucks.  I always go there when I am out of the country. I am addicted to their frappuccino with espresso shot.  This place has really nice staff!  Free wifi always at Starbucks! Accepts AMEX.

I've been to many Starbucks and this location never disappoints. Employees are kind, patient and willing to recommend a good drink when you're to tired to decide. Today I ordered my drink and when I got to the window I discovered I lost my ATM card. Ericka looked at me with a smile and said "It looks like you you're having a stressful day don't worry about it." A small act of kindness but it meant so much. This is why this is my Starbucks!

I came through the the drive thru and got a Carmel frap with extra caramel and a bbq pork sandwich. My drink didn't have hardly any caramel on it and the sandwich I had to throw away because it was so hard I couldn't even bite into it! Very upset that I had to go without lunch.

This Starbucks really needs help no matter what time of the day I come here I end up waiting ten minutes or more for a drink the only time they were fast was when a couple people were standing with a clipboard watching the person in the window... hopefully they get the help they need one day

This location is doing great during covid with limited staff!! Sure wait times can be slow during rush hour but they are working really hard.  But I have to rate 3 stars. I LOVE working on my computer at this location, but they haven't done ANYTHING about their insane fly problem for the past two months!!  it's obvious there's a problem, but they haven't done anything about it...  There are a good 20 or so flies hanging out on the benches by the windows, and have been for weeks now.  They're all over our computers, drinks, and food.  Get some fly traps, PLEASE!!!

Starbucks is Starbucks. The drinks, the food, the atmosphere is the same as every other Starbucks. The difference with this one is the staff. They are wonderful. They are always friendly and helpful and they always seem to go out of their way to help me. I really enjoy it here.

This Starbucks is usually top notch. I'm not sure if it was just a bad day or if they don't have the hang of drivethru service yet. We had to give our order three times to three separate people, and the order was still wrong. My chai was so sweet I couldn't drink it, it was only filled up part of the way, and it lacked the three shots of espresso that I asked for three times. If I go back here anytime soon I am not using the drivethru because it was chaos.

This is how my ultra caramel frappuccino looked like. Before ordered in Marana outlet Starbucks,it had layers ,like on photo of promotion ,and was good sweet .Here ,in speedway was just blended not sweet drink (sweet just on top , from middle to buttom was without taste cause it hadn't  this "ultra " caramel) .

They are now a ghost town as the shopping center is being renovated.  Parking is horrific, if the entrance is even open.  Combined with my experiences here I've pretty much decided even though it's convenient and I like the drinks I'm not going back.

I have been to over 100 different Starbucks all over the Midwest and South and this is the only one that consistently gets my order wrong.. I,get an iced white chocolate  mocha with caramel drizzle, not a hard drink to make, and yet they never get it right.. It is either way too sweet like they added extra sugar or it is soo bitter you can't drink it.. Just wish this store was as good as all the other ones I go to in my travels.

This is a great location. The baristas learn your name and your drink. Good conversation and great beverage! I had a s'mores frappucino made with espresso shots and it was delicious. The restrooms are very clean, as well as the entire location.
I always have to wait in line for at least ten minutes or more. The same goes for the drive thur Not very convenient when I'm in a rush!
Worse customer service, never going back. If you don't like your job then leave , if you are having a bad day then leave.

What makes this Starbucks standout is its speed. There is nothing like waiting out the door, this is a tiny Starbucks, and getting you drink in under 5 minute. This has to be hands down one of the most efficient Starbucks I've ever been to.  Another pro is that this place is open late throughout the week and is open an additional hour longer  on Friday and Saturday, which comes in handy if you are waiting for the garage to empty out due to a event at Hilbert Theater.  If you are looking to hangout, relax, and sip your latte, this probably isn't the most comfortable places to do it at. The inside is a tight squeeze and can get extremely noisy. They do offer bar seating along the wall, a few tables in the back, and some horribly placed seats at the counter where the drink pick up is.   If the weather is nicer, they have a few tables outside that allow for prime people watching on the Circle, or better yet, just enjoy your drink while sitting on the Circle's steps.

This Starbucks consistently closes 15 minutes before closing time. We drove 20 minutes out of our way to get to this one before they closed, and we got there 15 minutes before closing. We show up to the lights turned off, employees standing inside, and they wouldn't answer us in the drive thru. I've worked at Starbucks before and know that this is a big no-no and is basically stealing from the company. Next time I'll order on mobile so they can't avoid it. So frustrating!!
Very crowded Starbucks with long lines and long wait times for orders. Best thing about this location? LOTS of seating both indoors and outdoors. This one's not bad if you're not in a hurry.

I come here almost daily and enjoy my very consistent iced chai tea latte!  With that ring said, most-to-all the staff is very friendly.  The only negative thing I can say about this specific location is the dining area is almost always dirty and the trash is always full to the point things are sticking out.  I can only blame this on small amount of staff on.

I come here just about every morning before work and get my "grande pikes in a venti" cup. The staff and manager are very friendly. They refer to me by name. I love the atmosphere. Food is great. ( of course coffee is too). I've been to a few Starbucks in the area and they seem to be dirty every time I'm there and just not friendly at all. But this location is always clean, including the bathrooms. If you want a place to  relax with your friends or to study then I highly recommend this place. Its the best in St Louis in my opinion.
This is the best Starbucks I have been to yet! It was fast, friendly, and great customer service. My drink was made correctly, looked beautiful, and tasted amazing!!

It's Starbucks so by now unless you live under a rock you should know what you're going to get. So with that said, the x factor is the employees, the restaurant space itself and the cleanliness of the restaurant.   The employees at this location have always been kind in my multiple visits here. Today I received a coffee and the barista asked if I could wait for her to pour a new batch because the coffee I received was on the stale side. That goes a long way because just the other week down the street at another location, I did receive a stale Starbucks coffee and it's pretty disappointing. That's your pick me up at work that you've been craving for the last two hours and then you finally get it and realize you should have just had the regular stuff in your work's cafeteria. So I'm appreciative that this staff goes the extra mile and acknowledges your presence when you enter.   As far as the space goes, it's not a large space but this location is never crazy busy so I've never seen a seating issue here so it appears to be just right. And cleanliness has also never been an issue here, there's never packets of sugar thrown around the condiments counter or trashed tables.  Overall, it's a Starbucks location that has good employees, always seems to have seats available and is clean. Definitely recommend if you like Starbucks.

I stopped in at what was  the original Delaware County Starbucks.  It has been updated a few times since it first opened.  They stuck with only tables on the last two renovations.   I hadn't been here in a while but stopped in while waiting for a friend to finish their appointment a few doors down.   It was a Friday evening and I was a bit disappointed.    There were several people working the counter and I got good service and good coffee.   Why then was I unhappy, you are thinking?   As I am waiting, cruising the Internet, I am in the company of 6 or so pubescent tweeners who are talking drugs and private parts to the extent that in any other location, this would be sexual harassment.   Thank goodness it only lasted 15 minutes, but I was surprised that the staff just ignored it and let it go on.   After they left, I was inundated by two more sets of teenagers.  Not a place for adult drinkers like the Havertown location....

Don't go through the drive-through! We go through the drive-thru frequently as it's so close to the house, but it looks like it's time to find another location. The store has serious problems preparing hot food and keeping things in stock. We've had multiple occasions in which hot sandwiches were not cooked. I don't mean they weren't hot enough - I mean we've had more than one sandwich still frozen in the middle! Of course, we've been a few miles down the road when we found these issues and didn't have time to go back to the store.  They also have trouble keeping supplies stocked. Twice we've had them tell us they are out of Splenda. How does a coffee shop run out of sweetener?   Finally, this week, the store did not have decaf coffee brewed. I've run into this at other locations and they served me a decaf Americano for the price of the decaf coffee. Not this location - I even asked them about it and I was told that isn't standard. Again, how does a coffee shop not have coffee AND, if you don't, why would you not substitute another item at the same price?
While they make a decent coffee like most others, I wouldn't recommend coming here unless you want to hear some of the rudest starbucks employees complain about their lives
If you have EXTRA time to wait in drive thru this is the place to go . Waited in line for OVER 20 MIN. OLIVIA WAS RUDE
Always have friendly and fast service! The drive thru is convenient during the cold months when you don't want to leave your car.
30 minute wait in drive thru (5 cars were originally ahead of us) ....ordered two white mocha s. ...both were terrible.  Worst Starbucks I've ever visited.

So apparently I've just haven't learned my lesson with these guys. By far the worst Starbucks I've been to! So tonight me and my husband went out for a late dinner and afterwards we wanted a drink from Starbucks so I looked on my app and saw that one was the only close by that was open. So we took the chance and went and sure enough failed us. I got a venti pink drink and my pink drink was light pink that was it! It looked so plain!!!! Why?!? Because they didn't add any strawberries! There was so evidence of any strawberries! I'm not complaining about the price because hey i know I pay a lot for a drink but hey I love Starbucks so whatever! It's yummy! But anyways if I'm paying that much I should be getting what I paid for! What it's suppose to be! My husband got a white chocolate mocha hot and he said it tasted bland. No flavor! And on top of it I placed the order on the app so we don't have to wait too long. My husband gets his drink and makes him wait another 10 minutes for the pink drink! If I want Starbucks I'll just go somewhere else and drive a little further out of my way to get some I guess! Ugh so annoying! The worst Starbucks ever!!!!!!

I've experienced better at this Starbucks! this is my go-to Starbucks until now.. I went in yesterday and they were out of decaf coffee. No big deal, they offered a decaf americano instead, which i declined because I was in a rush to work. I go in today, and luckily they had some brewed decaf coffee. BUT, they only had enough to give me half a cup! c'mon now people.....

Easily the worst Starbucks I have ever been to.  They consistently run out of items even when you get there by 6:00 AM, especially the bistro boxes.   Usually the excuse is that their truck delivery didn't get there when it was supposed to.  And they are extremely inconsistent when it comes to drink quality.  Just this morning they tried to give me a strawberry acai refresher WITHOUT any actual berries in it!  I've given this location numerous chances thinking that maybe there was a new manager or something, but after roughly 8 months, it's clear that this is simply a terrible Starbucks.  I'll be going elsewhere from now on.

The service at this Starbucks is below poor, waited 15 minutes for my latte and never got an apology for it taking so long. No customer service AT ALL! Very disappointed for a Starbucks! In addition everyone working behind the counter had major attitudes, not one staff member smiled, nor acknowledged any of the customers. I thought when you gave your name it was to be called so you know when your order is ready. It was more calling out the drinks instead. 3 people ordered the same thing and we were left wondering whose was who. Management need to retrain it's staff at this location.
Drinks are fine, and the employees are nice, but the outside area is consistently dirty--trash surrounding the sitting areas. Kinda gross unfortunately.

This place never gets my drink right...and I mean NEVER.  Really sucks bc this is the closest starbucks to me.  I order at the drive through and it never fails...order is wrong. To top it off there's only one girl in there who is super nice.  Like, unusually kind. Everyone else is annoyed or checked out.  I have no idea what's going on with this location, this kind of service (or lack thereof) is new to me.

I have to admit the Google WiFi is a major attraction because it works perfectly with my Moto X WiFi phone -- so much better than the AT&T systems. BUT the service is excellent and consistent and friendly to boot. Currently my favorite one in town.

It's not that this is a bad Starbucks.  It's just that I believe the only reason it survives is because it tries to lure people away from Chapterhouse Coffee which is right down the street, is cheaper, has better food and  much better coffee, and is more spacious.

This Starbucks is like most Starbucks, except for Allen. Allen is the nicest barista I've ever come across. The entire staff is so nice and they all genuinely like their job. I'd go out of my way just to come to this particular Starbucks again.

This Starbucks was decent.  Our barista was ridiculously friendly.  I mean, she almost made me uncomfortable with her enthusiasm.  But it was better than someone who you can tell hates their job and doesn't want to serve you.  The place was empty but our drinks still took longer than they should have, which was upsetting because it was really cold out and we wanted to relax with a hot drink.  We grabbed a couple of the big comfy chairs but the table was covered with coffee and tea stains and there were 6 (count them, 6) empty coffee cups there.  The pace was not clean, let's put it that way.  The only reason they are getting 2 stars over 1 star is because their caramel apple cider is to die for!  It is the most delicious drink on the planet, of that I'm sure.  it's like dessert in a cup.  A little piece of Heaven.  But other than the drink itself, not a good experience.

My least favorite Starbucks not only in Reno but in the planet! I've been to Starbucks all over the world and the Keystone Starbucks is by far the worst one of them all. They forget something every time. Whether it be creamer, ice water, or even my sandwich. Go out of your way to another Starbucks and stay away from this one.
Your basic Starbucks, nothing note worthy. It was an efficient experience and they managed to keep the line moving pretty quickly.

This Starbucks is not a great location if you come with someone else (or more).  There are only about 4 tables inside and they are all pretty small.  There are a couple tables outside, but in the summer, who wants to sit outside!  There are only a couple of big chairs that you can lounge at in the front.  However, even though there are not many tables, I have never had a problem getting one (although a couple times I snagged the last one).  There is even a bar that you can sit at and watch the baristas make the coffee (which I find a little creepy, but would be great if you're friends with the baristas and feel like sitting and chatting).    The baristas are really friendly.  No matter who is working here, I always hear them chatting and laughing.  They are pretty quick too.  Since this is a small Starbucks there never seems to be a long line.    What is kind of funny is that parking here is the easiest, since it shares a lot with Publix and a few other stores.  We need to switch this parking lot with some of the busier Starbucks locations!  There is no drive through at this Starbucks.

If asked, "How do I take my coffee"... I reply "Seriously. Very Seriously!".  Clearly I have had enough coffee today because I am on fire with these reviews! This was my first time visiting this location (only came through the drive thru) but I am overall very pleased. The baristas were quick, and pleasant at the window. My drink order was also correct ... VICTORY! I was in chipper enough spirits, between their quick speed and my holiday tunes, that I even had to pay it forward in true Christmas spirit!
Seems like it's pretty new and has a huge lounge area and outside seating.  It's connected with the Hilton.
Just ordered online, paid for it get to drive through wait to get to window and only then get informed they're out of it. Totally defeats the reason for ordering online. Big waste of time.

Drive-thru moves quickly and the staff is friendly.  I tried their sausage/egg/cheese/english muffin breakfast sandwich recently and it was delicious.  In fact, it was one of the best breakfast sandwiches I've ever had, period.  Very surprising.

Starbucks is the McDonald's of coffee shops, right? So, I should probably temper my expectations.  That said, the whole experience is corporate, lame and I can't wait for it to be over one I'm there.    From the other customers who would push you out of the way to get their Joe 30 seconds sooner, to the barista that mumbles the drink orders as they come up, the need to go there is a useful reminder to never let yourself go to sleep without coffee in the house for the next morning.
The staff is their best asset. Their service is fast and when I had an unsatisfactory drink one time, they quickly resolved it, no problem and with a smile.
Staff and product are fine. But this is a tight cafe.  If you're going for a take out its fine. But this is a tough place to meet.

Great Starbucks location. They are always very fast and very friendly. The only thing is is that the parking lot is kind of small and at the busy times it can be hard to get into park. Iced hazelnut latte hits the spot every time!

If I could give zero stars I absolutely would. The Starbucks was supposed to open  at 10- I got there at 9:55 on my way to Hershey Park and was told "oh it'll be at least a few more minutes." Cool it's still not 10 so that makes sense. I wait another five min, then another ten. It's 10:15 and there's now a line forming and the guy working there keeps looking up and continues to bring in carts and set up. At this point I'm confused why he's so busy and doing all this work and unable to start taking orders. Maybe someone called out sick? But instead of an explanation he just kept looking up and seeing how many people were still waiting as if he wanted people to just give up and leave. By 10:20 he ended up coming to the front and pulling down the metal cover to show that it STILL wasn't open. No explanation, no update on whether they're actually going to open or not. I've never had this type of Starbucks experience and was pretty shocked since typically Starbucks is very efficient, friendly and reliable.

The line can be REALLY long in the drive through.. going inside seems to be punishment for not waiting in the drive through! I tried that once and they filled 5 drive-through orders for every body inside it seemed! Drinks are always yummy though!

The worst customer service ever. Waited while people who were behind me, got their drinks. Saw 5 orders made that were wrong and had to be redone. One three times!   Starbucks area manager - you had best get over here and do some training.  Unorganized.. unmotivated. Unhappy..  Went back the next day in case it was a one off.. this time they had no blonde espresso .. no internet.. no app pay.. no credit card pay.. and no bags for the mug we bought...  Seriously.. unbelievable.. the worst Starbucks ever..

Tried the new Strawberry tea infusion with 2 pumps of syrup not 7, as per the recipe. Wonderfully refreshing and ice cold. Here's hoping they keep it on the venue beyond summer. Friendly barista today and clean interior of the store. A five star day for them.     Got my order correct today. Inside clean and a personable barista.    I have no idea how the Starbucks could possibly be rated as high as it is. Today I was here and asked for a mocha frappe P with caramel. The barista asked me if I wanted the caramel in it or on top. I replied I wanted the caramel sauce on top. I received my frappucinno

This is a great Starbucks.  The staff is wonderful.  Plus its less than a mile from our house!  This is just to give another shout out to the wonderful staff at this Starbucks. You are so nice and helpful and appreciated!

Today, 1/16/18, around 5:15pm, my friend and I arrived after placing a mobile order to find the Starbucks with a measly paper taped to the door saying they are closed due to staff shortage. Lights still on, mobile ordering still available, and 3 employees inside. What staff shortage? They refused to open the locked door as we were trying  explain that we already PAID $30+ for a mobile order. This left a bad taste in my mouth after many visits with poor service at this location. Go to another Starbucks

Standard Starbucks but in a great building  - the flying saucer building! 3 Stars is a stretch considering after 2+ months of nearly daily visits at the same time, they still can not remember my name or drink. Guess I am asking too much.

This is the friendliest Starbucks I've been too. My visits to other locations were good, but I feel like this location goes above and beyond. The staff is friendly and upbeat. Lines move fast.   I drove through the other day and ordered a breakfast sandwich and coffee. The woman working the drive through read back my order at the window and realized she had made a mistake. I wasn't even bothered by it. I told her whatever you had rung up was fine, I'm not picky. She changed my order and gave me a 4 dollar credit to my next purchase! I thought that was so nice of her to do that. I didn't even care about it but she went out of her way to provide good customer service.   One million stars!

I have a confession to make. I like Starbucks. I mean, I do prefer unique, independently owned cafés. Still, if I see the Starbucks logo on the horizon, I usually "need" coffee in a classically conditioned response. The thing I like about them: you can typically count on a consistent drink. It tastes the same from coast to coast.   However, I stopped at this location for a quick caffeine boost before taking my kiddo to play. The service was just not friendly. The cashier's eyebrow was lifted because my sister took an extra minute to decide on a replacement drink when her initial request was not available. The cashier was very impolite. There were probably 6-8 other employees working. They were also unfriendly...and slow. Our drinks took longer than usual despite the lack of any other customers. Unfortunately, when they were completed, they were not made correctly.   We debated whether or not to return to the counter, and decided that having paid for a "specialty drink," it was worth a replacement or refund. Long story a little less-long, they were extremely rude about that too. I'll avoid this location in the future.

who doesn't love starbucks??? this location is not the best but it gets the job done. don't complain about drive thru, the whole purpose of drive through is for convenience of not getting ur ass up out of the car and getting it yourself, so for a place that makes ur drink right on the spot why would you not expect to wait??? The baristas in here are totally nice. Not too big inside but that does not stop the laptop crews from coming inside, makes you think who does not have wifi at home...

Typical Starbucks. We had vanilla cream frappe, cold chai tea and sweet cream cold brew. Barista was nice not overly friendly busy talking with other barista. Tables were clean. May stop by if back by in the mall

I came here between yet two more doctor's appointments. It was a day full of appointments, and I  hadn't eaten much of anything. I had an hour to kill. There were no pre-made sandwiches available. When I asked for a breakfast sandwiches- nothing- they were hit hard at breakfast. There wasn't even a fruit plate.

This is your typical Starbucks at the service areas on I76. If the line is short You will be in and out, But long line do not bother waiting as they are extremely slow in general. I ordered a trenta green tea lemonade with 5 pumps of cane sugar and no ice. The staff where very pleasant and friendly. There was no line so I was in and out in no time at all. The drink was made perfectly and tasted great.
I like this Starbucks because it's generally less busy than the others in the area. I normally don't have a problem getting a table and the wi-fi usually works.
This may be the worlds slowest Starbucks.... 7 baristas on the floor and I've been waiting 15 minutes for an iced tea...
New location is now open - avoid it.  It's dirty inside.  Service is downright pissy.  And their drinks aren't made right.

I first tried this Starbucks because of how cool it looked. Bad idea.   Every day, I order my Starbucks oatmeal without it being re-packaged in the wasteful paper bag. On my first trip to the Spaceship, I ordered as usual. When my order -- bagged -- was delivered to me by an irritated-looking woman, I pulled the oatmeal and fruit out and said "I don't need this bag. Will you reuse it?" She snatched the bag from my hand, scowled at me, held the bag up in front of my face and crumpled it into a ball. I said "Wait! I'll recycle that!", but she smirked and threw it into the trash as if to say "screw you, asshat." The overwrought drama and hatred in her reaction freaked me out -- what kind of employee acts like that to a customer??  That's when I saw the title "Manager" on her name badge.   I've been back to the Spaceship when the lines at the awesome, friendly Starbucks (in the CWE or at 55 and Loughborough) are too long. But the Spaceship team is always sour and surly, disorganized (too many people behind the counter, but no one doing anything), and the orders nearly always fubar in some way.  This is certainly the coolest looking Starbucks in St. Louis. But it seems to be managed (during morning hours, at least) very, very poorly.

Went in tonight during finals week. The store wasn't busy and the place was very clean. Every employee there was incredibly kind and my drink (even though it was a little complicated / custom) was made perfectly! 10/10 would recommend

I'm lazy, so I love a Starbucks drive thru. I have a Starbucks in Westchase on Linebaugh maybe only 3 min away, and another on Racetrack road. However I have to walk into the one on Linebaugh  and the one on racetrack isn't convenient during school traffic and there's always a long drive thru line. So, I go out of my way to this location on Anderson. It's never busy. They make my geen tea lemonade sand toffee nut mochas correctly. The drive thru at this location isn't extra tight or hard to get to from the street. This is the easiest starbucks I go to.
My order is always been correct employees are always courteous it simply makes my day or morning even if I go through the drive-through

Poorly managed. Always has a huge line and never has 2 people on register. If you don't mind waiting 10-15min for a cup of coffee enjoy. But a lot of the problem lies with the lack of options available at the Sheraton.

Stopped here at our usual spot for coffee. My wife and I got a couple seasonal lattes, pecan praline and Carmel brûlée , both coffees are standard and consistent as Starbucks usually is, however the drive thru guy was, saying less than friendly would be a step above his attitude.  I don't know how you can have such a stick up your a** that early in the morning, but I did my best to be friendly and got nothing in return.  Cheer up drive thru guy, your life ain't that bad, your still breathing another day

This Starbucks is one my favorites. It is really spacious with high ceilings and a nice aesthetic. I have always experienced great service and the snack selection is diverse. There are plenty of electrical outlets and I have yet to see it packed. There are a variety of places to sit, high tables, lower tables and lounge chairs. There is also a pleasant outside seating area. This starbucks has a drive through as well. The lighting is comfortable and balanced. Overall it is one of the better locations with a really nice environment.

I love this starbucks. The staff is friendly and always has a smile on their face. The usual for me is the white chocolate mocha hot with an extra pump of syrup so that I don't have to add sugar and a slice of banana bread but today I tried the mango dragon fruit tea and omg was it amazing and refreshing I will definitely be trying it again

I'm not really a Starbucks person, but this is a great location.  There is lots of seating, the staff is so nice and knows you, and they make it seem like it's not a mega chain.  As with all Starbucks, the drinks are great, and it is a little $.  It was /the/ after-school hangout in junior high - how lame - but brings back good memories.
Hate getting in and out of here in the morning! Drink correct about 80% of the time.

I've been to this location about a dozen times. My husband and I enjoy going in on a rainy day and reading for a bit. The inside it pretty big and they have outdoor seating but it's usually pretty crowded. Unfortunately the last 2/3 visits have been negative. We stopped on a Sunday morning before church and went in because the drive-thru line was super long. I ordered the same thing for both myself and my husband. It took 15 minutes for our drinks to come out. Not kidding. I requested the drinks have sugar-free syrup because of dietary restrictions. We rushed out once the drinks were made and didn't try them until we got to our car and were out of the parking lot. One of the drinks was SF and the other wasn't. We had to throw the one with regular syrup away and couldn't go back because our drinks took so long. The woman who made them was clearly flustered because they were super busy but she made them back-to-back. That mistake shouldn't have been made.  Fast forward to this morning at 6:50 am. No one was in the drive-thru. I ordered my drink (the same one that was messed up previously) and gave the woman my money. A man made my drink and set it on the counter and then went to the back, I think. The woman also disappeared. Literally. FOUR MINUTES PASSED and my drink was sitting there. She came back to take someone else's order in the drive-thru and then proceeded to get a cup and start making their drink. I kindly said "excuse me, I have somewhere I need to be. Can you please give me my drink?" She didn't say anything and just handed it to be. I was in shock. I'm not kidding when I say it was four whole minutes. If I could've reached in the window and grabbed the drink myself I would have.  I tell this long story because both of these poor visits involved the same woman. A little bit older with short blonde hair. She can be very nice but tends to be a little out of it at times. I hate to call someone specific out but in both accounts she was the one to blame. I still plan on visiting this location but hope that this particular employee gets the hang of her job.

I honestly don't know how long these baristas have been working here let alone being an actual certified barista but it's just amazing how much they don't know about the drinks. I came in this weekend and got an iced matcha green tea latte with light ice and no foam and when I was handed my drink I was given a white drink with the matcha powder stuck at the  bottom and I asked to have my drink shaken and he told me that I had to ask for it to be shaken because it doesn't come that way. I told him that I was a Starbucks barista and that they come shaken so that the powder is mixed into the drink. Long story short he again handed me a drink that was 95% milk because he just dumped the previous drink into the shaker and so then it was just over shaken milk with extra foam and extra ice. Overall extremely disappointed with the service and the quality of my drinks I bought. And this isn't the first time I've had terrible service here. No more second chances for this store.
The outdoor summer seating just boosted this rating up all the way! Great comfy spot to chill and enjoy their coffee! My go to Starbucks in the area!
Great staff all around in the evenings. Very patient with the toughest customers. Thank you for an enjoyable evening.

Every time I have been in this Starbucks there's no more than a few people in front of me and at least one table free.  I'm sure it's a busy location but I've been fortunate it hasn't been crazy busy.  There can be a few others by the milk area but it clears up quickly.  I usually get the Refresh tea for $2 and like the mint taste.  The staff are friendly and efficient.  The last time I came, most people were seating on the patio so there was available indoor seating.  The locations makes this one of my favourite Starbucks.  It's close to fun shops to check out and great to stop in for a mid shopping drink.  There are benches outside if it's too busy inside.  Parking lot can get busy but there's residential streets that you can likely find a spot and enjoy a little walk.

This used to be our favorite location. But lately it's just not being kept up. Trash is always full, tables dirty and the floor needs sweeping.   Still one of the best staffed. Needs better management.
Great location, good staff  We ran the gauntlet in NO to get to this starbucks which was under a mile from our hotel near the airport. Large location, friendly staff and they got our drinks right!

This is my normal Starbucks and it's usually pretty good. It isn't the best one in the world but far from the worst. They baristas are efficient and my order is almost always right. This one stays super busy but I'm usually a satisfied customer. Friendlier customer service would make this a 5 star for me.
I just a iced Carmel coffee. Delicious refreshing , made perfect. Starbucks is the best.

The staff in this Starbucks is always nice and friendly, except one blond girl who seems angry with customers and colleagues. She doesn't smile, no eye contact and moves through the crowd to empty trash as an icebreaker :) There were several customers, waiting for her to finish and she looked at them so sternly that everyone froze on the spot. I hope it's not a usual attitude just a few bad days.  I tried hot latte 2 times and it wasn't good, so they should work on that. Other than that I really like they open from 5/5:30 and that location is convenient.

Staff is typically helpful and friendly. Occasionally they are lacking at the condiment/creamer station. It's frequent that I have to request creamer or stir sticks. This morning no one could locate the stir sticks and it wasn't all that busy today either.  This is fairly typical, but it is an indicator that they're busy.   It's my favorite/preferred convenient stop for Starbucks.  Regardless, I rarely see a familiar face. It may be the random times I go in or a high turnover; no big deal.  They serve me my coffee and it makes me happy.   I don't buy anything other than fresh brewed.

As far as Starbuck's go, this one is definitely middle of the road.  They do have some outside seating which is nice but inside is nothing to write home about.  Standard run of the mill furniture with a lot of small tables.  Lots and lots of white.  Definitely could use a bit more color and decoration to liven it up a bit.  Staff are friendly and attentive but the lines drag a bit in the morning.  Not a place you can typically get a regular coffee at say 8 AM and be out the door in under 2-3 minutes.  Looking at least at a 5-10 minute wait.      It greats crowded on the weekends too and finding a seat inside is pretty tough by Noon.  Lots of students studying and to a lesser degree people working.  What is nice is that you have a bit more space than other Starbucks which is good if you need to spread out a bit to work.       It's convenient for me because it is right around the corner but parking can be a bit of a drag at this location too.  If you need a Starbuck's fix or want to get here early enough to grab a seat on the weekend, this location works but if I wanted to enjoy a coffee I would probably walk up to one of the places in downtown Clayton instead.

I have been coming here for over a decade.   Small with the usual expectations.  A couple of outside tables are available.  WiFi is free but you have to ask what network. Four bucks for a grande cappuccino..

So far, so good. It's now open as of September 1st. The configuration is kinda weird- seating areas are long and narrow, so plenty (or all?) seats near a view outside to Market or New Jersey Streets. Always amazes me how many of Gess they can pack into such a small geography! One more spot for the die-hands to get their PSL. No drive-thru. That would be what I'd like to see in a downtown location, though I guess traffic would be an immediate issue.

These baristas are always talking and ignoring customers. I can see once in awhile but it never fails to be ignored like we are not here and there isn't 20 people waiting to get to work on time. I really need to find a new coffee spot

This is the most inefficient operation on the planet.  Last Sunday around 9, I drove up to this location, and as usual, it was completely backed up.  I parked my car next door at the dry cleaners to avoid having to wait in that line, which experience told me would take forever.  I entered the facility and there was no line.  I placed my order for 2 croissants, 2 blueberry muffins, and 6 vanilla scones, and because I was in a hurry, I told them to skip heating anything up.  All of the items were in the bakery display.  I was then directed to wait to receive my order because there were 15 orders ahead of me in the DRIVE THRU.  Nothing in my order had to be prepared; just tossed in a bag.  There were at least 5 people behind the counter working, but I was told "she is working as fast as she can".  So not only is this food overpriced (and frankly, most of it available at your local market), but the service is terrible.  When I told them I was not going to wait for them to service the entire drive thru customers first, and to either give me the food or a refund, the manager refunded my money and thanked me!  So, I drove to McDonalds on Olive where I placed my order, paid my money, and was out in less time that I spent at this Starbucks with nothing to show for it.

I can't believe I like this place!  It's against everything I stand for in coffee. Its known roast is black to the point of being charred.  Its beans are a week old or more, it's overpriced, and it's so sterile.  This location is no different.  But the people here are so nice.  The barista said that they could make me a Pike's Place brew with a pour-over method, which means a fresher cup than the standard brew urns.  It cost no extra, and it's fun to watch them.  Pike's Place is far better than their standard roast, since it's not nearly as bitter.  After I got my drink, I saw that they offered a blond roast, promising a not as dark a bean.  I approached the manager, who was extremely friendly, and surprisingly honest with some of his views.  Once I had to leave, he gave me a sample package of the blond roast to brew on my own.  I left this Starbucks happier than I had ever before.  If you're on the West Side and you're one of those poor saps who LIKES Starbucks, this is undoubtedly the best one in the area. I am so pleased with my experience.

I stop here a few times a week and every time I always say I shoulda done dunkin'.  This has to be the slowest drive through Starbucks has.  I've waited over 20 minutes multiple times for my drink.  On the days I go, I have to build an extra 15-20 minutes into my commute to make up for their slowness.  Thanks Starbucks

I'm not a frequent Starbucks customer, but I do enjoy their coffee and treats on special occasions.   I had an extremely positive experience just yesterday at the Starbucks drive thru in Zionsville.   The coffee was great (I ordered a frappuccino) and the barista was warm without being too over the top with it.   And patient as I navigated the menu.   I don't really like to feel rushed when I'm ordering and like to at least appear that I know what I'm doing.    All in all, an excellent experience.   Others in this world are hopefully taking notes as to what Starbucks is doing right.

You almost never see this location without a line in the drive-thru or a full cafe! This is THE place in the IRV where folks gather for business meetings, after-school study sessions, sewing circles and book clubs. You'll find a cornucopia of people here!  Wait times here are sometimes longer than other locations - sometimes suspiciously long when, after what seems like ten years, I only see one beverage handed over. And occasionally, a group of rowdy teenagers will interrupt gossip time with your best girlfriend. But outside of that, there's little here to frown upon.   The place is cute and well-kept, and the baristas are friendly enough. Obviously the drinks and treats don't stand out from any other generic Starbucks.   Certainly there are other locations I could travel to that would lessen my wait time, but it's really the convenience to my home that keeps me coming back time and time again.

A couple years ago I had a barista create something for me at my request of hot sweet & no caffeine. A steamed lemonade with passion fruit tea, not sure what the actual name is, but soo good when I'm not feeling coffee. I've had some Starbucks locations have no idea what I'm talking about when I try to order this, and some who are like oh yeah, no worries. I was pleasantly surprised that this location knew exactly what I was talking about. It was exactly as I remember sweet, tart and nice and hot.

Whoever was running the register today told me the wrong amount of espresso shots for my drink.  I only found out later when I asked the person making it.  You may want to be cautious if you are limiting your caffeine for heath reasons. You have basically one job at Starbucks: Know how to make the coffee.  If you can't do that consistently, you should probably seek employment elsewhere.

Let me preface this by stating that I rarely reivew things.  I have only good things to say about this stabucks, I frequent many starbucks and this location is by far my favorite.  I am a very consitent person and like to go in when i know my favorite barista will be there.  The service is wonderful and they really do make an effort to get to know you and to be friendly.  A special shout out to Kevin the barista for being such a positive person and always brightening my day!  I am always happier when I leave this location than when I come in.  They have nice tables that have excellent light for studying and it is always very clean and welcoming.  Additionally there are rarely lines inside of the store, but the drive through is usually busy because of the proximate location to the highway. Best, Reagan

This is one of those locations that you can see yourself sitting there for hours on end, studying or reading. I like the glass windows and the ambience. It's got just enough busy-ness from Jasper Ave, while also retaining the quaintness of the residential street behind.  Staff is friendly and food/coffee comes out quickly. It's a Starbucks so you know what to expect. The best part about this location is being able to people watch! They have a steady stream of people coming in and out throughout the day.   I should come to this location more often... it is lovely.

I had above average service in this Starbucks. A pour over and a latte. They were busy too.  The latte was wrong but they fixed it all before the pour over was done. Drinks were all nice and hot. Yum!  We will be back
Nicest Starbucks in SB. Lots of space, beautiful decor and sight seeing. Beautiful people wall-to-wall

The location of this Starbucks is not the greatest. You'd think with it being right off I-10 it would be but it's very difficult to get out, especially if you have to make a right hand turn onto Ina.  The staff is very friendly, though, and that makes up for the traffic problems.

We all know Starbucks sucks in general. The coffee is cheap but overpriced.   Starbucks has 50¢ refills that eventually become free if you use a gift card that you keep reloading. Cafes with coffee that's actually good don't do this, so I like it here if you want to drink 4 coffees in one sitting. Especially cold brew ends up being the cheapest per ounce.  The restrooms have codes, but they give it to random hobos who don't even buy anything?  Around 3-4pm during the school year (September-May), a bunch of twelve year old kids come in for free water and disturb customers. One even dumped out the water and filled it with the milk (that's for your coffee). The workers won't do anything about it.  At least 5 times, hobos came in and asked customers who were sitting for change. The workers wouldn't do anything about this either (even though you're not supposed to do this inside a business...).  but this location has the best window seats in the city for people watching, especially when it's raining or snowing.
Love this Starbucks! It's close and convenient for me, there is never a line and the employees are always fast and nice, too!

I usually don't leave bad reviews but today was definitely the last straw. This place is such a hit or miss. Sometimes it's good and sometimes it's like they make drinks with their eyes closed. How is it that I get a caramel frapp and I get no coffee in it. Maybe a dash of coffee was put in it? Starbucks is a coffee joint right? It tasted like nothing but creamer and water. Like seriously? How can you mess that up? I didn't even complicate the order like some ppl tend to do. I just ordered something right off the menu and you mess it up. Maybe I just need to find another Starbucks to go to.

When you place your order online and people that have ordered after you IN LINE get their order first, something isn't working. I ordered online and 5 people, who ordered after me, got their drinks before I did. Apparently, the barista never checked for the online order. WTF?! Hey Starbucks! Don't push online ordering unless your people are trained to check for the orders. Again!!! WTF?!

I have to agree that this Starbucks is slower than most, but the staff are friendly and they make your drink right, which I appreciate! The ambiance in here is a bit lacking due to the location- there are often shady folks lingering near the front during the day and often loud kids coming through once the bus lets out, making it not the ideal place to study or relax. It seems that the staff have to change the key code on the bathroom daily because of this to keep its use down to only paying customers, but it's still hard for them to keep up with stuff going on around a blind corner from the barista area. The station with milk, straws, etc is usually missing some things and the trash is often overflowing, but I can't say enough that I think the staff here works hard to keep it nice-- I just think they've been dealt some tricky cards.

I was able to get my vanilla-bull$@&t thing in an exceedingly expedited manner, so what more can a highly caffeinated woman ask for in a Starbucks? Considering this location sees lots of traffic (it's a drive-thru Starbucks at the busy 96th Street and Meridian Street intersection), I was impressed with the efficiency of the staff, and that they got my order correct.

Stopped by for coffee for the road.  The teenage cashier tried to convince us to buy a bag of the tribute coffee so he could get some free food.  Then he told us that if he sold a million bags of it, he would get a BMW.  Slightly entertaining, yet off putting.  And the bathroom didn't have any toilet paper, only toilet seat covers, which is certainly becoming a trend.  Just not one I'm a fan of.

Love, love, LOVE my Starbucks - it's not secret.  This location offers up all the standard Starbucks fare but it's a tad smaller than most and the parking is limited :-(  You can only get into it from the St. A Trail or Bellerose Drive on one side of each road because it's in a high traffic area.  Consequently, it can take a little extra time compared to some of the more readily accessible locations.  Be sure to download the Starbucks App to your smart phone in order to earn stars for every purchase (Buy 12 get 1 free) and offers and discounts that are offered exclusively to the App holders such as an extra hour on Frappy 50% off days, B1G1 free from time to time, or the more frequently offered $1 off an espresso beverage.    You can also put money on your app, link it to a gift card, or register for auto load with a credit card so that you can conveniently pay on your phone!  One tip if you're trying to get to Gold member status or earn more stars, request to pay for your purchases separately so that you can earn a reward star for each transaction.  Another thing is remember you can customize the drinks anyway you like.  For example, you can get a Frappuccino made your way - it's doesn't have to be made with the sugary base.  You can ask for it to be made with decaf espresso or made with dark roast coffee, milk of your choice, ice and a sugar free syrup if you want to go sugar free.   You can google all kinds of Starbucks off shelf or secret menu ideas/recipes for your next visit!  And if you're doing a LCHF diet, remember that you can always ask for your lattes or fraps to be made with half and half, heavy cream or whipping cream!  If you order any of their drinks as a "Breve" they will make it with half and half rather than milk.    The point is, customize the drinks to what YOU want!

This used to be my favorite Starbucks location. However recently I have been extremely disappointed. I use the mobile order to get in and get out and skip the line. Within the last week I have placed 2 orders and when I arrive not only is my order not ready( I have no problem waiting a minutes) but it hasn't even been started. Disappointed Customer
Very convenient downtown Moorestown location, sometimes long line due to rich "Escalade" moms buying all their kids lattes and macchiatos.
Quick efficient, it was far enough away from the convention center to get a latte fast!

I wish this location was cozier and more inviting. There are no soft chairs or areas to sit and visit. It is loud, cold and hard. This atmosphere just makes me want to get my coffee and leave, rather than catch up with a friend or do  some work. There used to be a areas with big leather chairs, but they've been replaced by a counter that has people with their backs to the room. Disappointing.

Nice little Starbucks with local architectural flavor. not the best location if you're driving, and there's no parking on the street. A few blocks away is a brand-new Starbucks with a parking lot. Service was fast and friendly. Bathroom was clean.

I've only been to this location a couple times and both instances were quite pleasant. The staff seems friendly enough, however one of the ladies taking my order seemed like she was a bit frantic or something. Maybe she was just wanting to close up for the night. Other than that lady I find the parking is in a little complex that is tough to navigate when it's as packed as it was. It's more or less exactly what I expect from a Starbucks. I have nothing bad to say about the drinks though. They're delicious.
This is a solid Starbucks, they are slow compared to other Starbucks, especially any of the busy ones in NYC or Philly, but always friendly.

I rarely give bad reviews. I always wonder is it the day...was it a specific employee but no...it's not.   I have been in drive through and sat outside a few times now and find myself going elsewhere for a few reasons.  The drive through lines here are terrible even with 4 cars. I have been through many many...lines and gone through faster with a better service attitude that this one.   We went in and had slow mediocre experience inside as well. There was one time a girl said she was there working from another SBX helped us and she was wonderful. Truly.   Again, 3 o clock...4 people ahead and I was in the line for 20 minutes plus. The service via intercom was less than expected...and slow on top of it.   Consistently this has been my usual experience here. Sad. I prefer to drive further on 60 for better service and atmosphere. It is a busy location but I have yet to enjoy my experience here.   Sad.
Really charming location. Love the outdoor seating area and people watching in West Park Village.  My drinks have been as expected. Mainly the outdoor seating and area is the draw for me.

Very nice people.   My espresso was a generous 3 shot beauty.    Just what the cardiologist ordered to jump start my afternoon "groggies."    Nice large common table to sit at and ponder all   Will be marking this one as a Reg.
Thank you thank you thank you! To manager of the Starbucks for donating to my classroom for preschoolers with autism! They donated so many straws for a sensory activity and we are so appreciative!

Went to this Starbucks today, we got a grande Caramel Macchiatto and a tall Skinny Vanilla latte in the drive thru. When we got home we found out our coffees not full, they didnt fill it up to the rim, there was only about 3/4 of coffee with the foam in them. We were disappointed.

This morning I decided to pick up an espresso at my favorite Starbucks on Speedway and Wilmot. Even though there were at least 6 cars ahead of mine, they managed to kindly take my order, get it right and with a smile. Thank you   Update, I just stopped by on the way home to grab an iced tea and return a wireless cup/speaker. I wanted to buy a cup on display, but they had run out. I tried to write a thank you note to their adopted soldier deployed in Afghanistan but there weren't any more cards and none to purchase. Then I waited over 20 minutes and watched people come in after me and get taken care of, so I walked up and they asked what I'd ordered, so i guess my order was forgotten. Kind of a fail today, I'm glad I wasn't on my way to work. At least my Pineapple Black Tea tasted good.

I work in the shopping center and I am glad to have this specific Starbucks in the area. The staff is always helpful. And wait time is minimal. I recently started using the mobile order and by the time I walk over there my drink is ready to go.

Cannot believe this location is at a great location and the baristas not so much.  Thought I was in a DD, simple ice tea was all wrong. Got a grande instead of a venti, extra sweet, instead of what I asked, and light ice, when asked for extra ice.

Slowest Starbucks ever. I downloaded the yelp app and left this review in the time it took to place my order and get it, 2 cars back from the box. This isn't the first time. Very nice staff, and great new location. Couches outside are a nice touch. Please...pick up the pace!

I have never had a problem here! Hands down my favorite Starbucks. The staff is the absolute best! Great customer service, clean shop and always get your drinks right. Saturday mornings are always super busy but the staff always seems to zoom through the line and maintain their great job!

I swing through the drive through on my way into the office, and they have drive through service down pat! Even when there is a long line of cars the flows through the adjacent parking lot I am through the line and on my way in no time flat.  Everyone that has assisted me at this locations has been super friendly.

Just opened Thursday, feb 11, 2016. It's already constantly packed solid. Being next to Villanova university, I'm sure they knew business would be good for them. My biggest gripe is there is no drive thru but aside from that, I'm very happy how centrally located it is. It's close to my house and near lots of things but also there is ample parking. I've never seen so many parking spaces around a Starbucks before. Getting in to Starbucks is easy enough.  But this is a very fast-paced Starbucks and not relaxing. It's crammed tight with college students, local middle school and high school kids and people on their laptops.  The seating isn't comfortable.  Mostly tables and no comfy chairs. Also, there is no noise control. It's very loud inside. This Isn't the best Starbucks I've been to but I think it fits the area and it's good for what it is. I will be back for sure.

The baristas here are great and it's definitely a great spot for people-watching on South Street if you are able to grab a window table/seat.  It's always busy but the service is very friendly and I have yet to receive an incorrect order.  Plus, they also have the Evolution Sweet Greens juice, which I haven't been able to find elsewhere.

Starbucks at Starbucks yes they have comfy lounge chairs or barstools with high top tables that you can sit enjoy your coffee and enjoy the free Wi-Fi. This particular location is usually pretty packed. Most of the students from Kaiser University and HCC end up studying here so we can ours are not recommended for this particular location because it will be fun. There's an outdoor seating area where you can come and sit with your dogs and it is right in front of the movie theater.
The staff is SO warm and friendly at this location. You can tell they like their jobs.

Mmmmm... Venti, triple expresso, vanilla bean late... Skinny of course :-) No one does a late like Starbucks.  But for what they charge, they should be good.  A tiny point of contention though... Their drivethrough may be great for a Prius... But not a truck.  Get out and walk in.  The bonus is the smell of a coffee house will greet you as you walk through the doors and everything will be fine from then on.
No real complaints about this location as far as the drinks or service. It's just always incredibly packed. You're lucky to find a seat when you go in. Best to get your order to go!

Last Tuesday morning, I went through the drive-thru to grab something before work. I ordered a vanilla bean cold brew and pumpkin scone. Drink was made perfectly and the woman at the window was friendly. The car line was long, but moved quickly despite that; it was about a 15-20 minute wait. I would come back again.
Fastest drive thru ever!!  Don't be afraid of the long line, it goes fast!  So thankful to have this quick Starbucks so close to home and work!

If there was a score of 0, I would give it! 25+ minutes for a simple Hot Chocolate. Store was over staffed with 6 people behind the counter - not focused on helping customers, but hugging one another, playing on their phones, running around the store and going to the back of the store while ignoring customers.  A lady who claimed she was the manager was oblivious when she finally came over to start making drinks to help , now making 7 people behind the register.  She proceeded to scold me , that she was unaware of what her employees had been doing or not doing because she just got on the floor.  In the meantime she had been less than 5 ft away putting up boxes!  When I told her I had been waiting, she got annoyed with me, sarcastic, and never apologized or accepted responsibility for her and teams' attitude.  If this is the best this store can do, then just close down, because your careless employees will ruin you.

Inside is noisy and the drive thru is perpetually packed. But the staff is super friendly and they do what they can to manage such a busy location. If you need a place to hang out and chill maybe read a book, you may want to choose another business. If you want some tasty coffee drinks and maybe don;t want to get out of the car, this place is for you.  Just a quick tip: Don't park in front where the drive thru entrance is. Even if the drive thru is deserted, it fills up in 0.3 seconds and you'll be waiting 10 minutes for the car in line behind you to move so you can back out and leave. If the next guy in line lets you back out...

This 'bucks sits between me and work so I'm there a lot.  It's good, the people are friendly and they always get my order right.  As  the other reviewers mention the parking lot is small.  It's not difficult getting in and out if you're using the drive through but if you're parking during peak times it could get interesting. Even though it's not the biggest in the area the do have bistro boxes.  (not all of them do) So if you're looking for a little food with your coffee this is the place.
Same great service. The re-model is excellent! Still one of my favorite Starbucks in the country.

Once again, I'm here to give this Starbucks a bad rating. I frequent this location as I live across the street, but today, i heard the manager come over to a customer and tell him he had to leave if he didn't buy anything. I get it, but also, having worked at Starbucks, I know Starbucks is supposed to be a "second place" and by kicking someone out.. that doesn't exactly fulfill that "second place" saying. I'll add that there were about 4 other customers in the store, so in no way was this man taking up space as there were plenty of open tables. Ridiculous.
Life saver! Absolutely love the staff here, and the coffee is always amazing. If you want a good laugh with great coffee, I strongly suggest coming here!

Good cappuccino. Terrible environment. I was enjoying my cappuccino and working on a research paper when a loud customer began a dialog with the Starbucks employee (Joshua). The conversation turned to Joshua's co-workers and supervisor. Joshua began bad mouthing fellow staffers and his boss. It began with Joshua talking about a co-worker who continually clocks out ten minutes early. It went downhill from there. I had to listen to all the nonsense for approximately 20 minutes. It was terrible. Joshua was loud and the customer was even louder. Ugh!  UPDATE: Nobody reached out to me, after my review was submitted several weeks ago, with either an apology. I can only surmise staff matters aren't important to this business.

Usually, I never have any problems coming to this location. However, this morning my husband and I went in for a mobile order I placed through the app. We got there and told them at the intercom that it was a pickup order with my name. We pulled ahead and the lady told us we the total. I thought that when I reloaded my app with money that it didn't go through, so my husband went ahead and gave her his card. Then she hands us a sandwich and we realize it's not our order. We tell the lady and she says she'll have to get her manager to refund us. The manager comes to the window and says we should have told her it was a pickup order. We told the guy at the intercom. So there was no communication on their part. They also didn't repeat the order before taking our card. So I assumed my money just didn't go through to the app. The manager also says that the first lady at the window didn't have a headset on, again trying to excuse their bad communication. Drive through should always have headset, and if not, they should at least have some communication with whoever else is working drive through. They voided my payment. I appreciate getting it fixed but I did not appreciate the attitude and the blame for their bad communication.   This was at about 6:30 in the morning on a Tuesday.

I spilled my coffee, luckily next to the manager, and she was more than accommodating. And, even though it was completely my fault, she got me another cup on the house. It's the little things that make a place great and she seems to have a grasp on that. Thanks again!!
Super friendly service, some of the staff even knows my name by now.  :)  Also, they're great at offering suggestions for when I want to change up my usual order and try something new.

This is my favorite location! I love Adam, Ana Lee, Jasmine, and several others...I'm really bad with names and have a hard time remembering all. But pretty much all of them know me by name lol :) They are all awesome! Drinks are always on point, no matter how crazy mine get!! They are real and are not fake. And handle rude people really well for what I've observed!
Great place open late (11pm) on a weeknight, I think even later on the weekend.  Needed a traveler late (family pack) and they were very gracious to get it ready for me right at closing time.
best starbucks i think i've ever had. the staff is always super nice and accommodating and i LOVE how precise they always are

What is different about this Starbucks is that it's situated next to a lake. There's outdoor seating, and it's got a nice view.  However, the parking is terrible. There are only four or five spaces on a tiny stretch of asphalt next to the shop, and the parking is terrible if you enter the lot from the "wrong" direction. Exiting is also a nightmare.

As stated before, I love Starbucks! This location is located in the French Quarter so it was very busy most times I went. I visited there about three times during my stay. The first time, I ordered my favorite caramel macchiato which was sweet like I like it; however they forgot to give me my cookie so after waiting in an extremely long line, I had to wait even longer. L On my second trip, I only ordered my favorite drink which was made to perfection again, but the barista was extremely slow so where as the line was not very long at all, I still waited FOREVER! On my third and final trip, the cashier forgot to give my drink order to the barista. I received my breakfast sandwich (sausage, egg and cheese, which was mediocre) in a timely manner, I had to bring my forgotten drink to the attention of the barista, who provided my drink (perfect againJ) very quickly. So there were a lot of pros and cons with my experiences here; however it was conveniently located and I would probably visit again if in the area.

Wonderful Starbucks location. The parking lot can be very congested due to the slow moving drive thru and it is frustrating that people stop traffic to wait to get in but that is because people are not willing to just walk inside to pick up their order... the outdoor patio at this location is what truly makes this Starbucks different and.  Eternal then most. Drink quality is consistent and staff are kind.

Wow, this is not a great Starbucks!  It's location is what it is (forget it if you're heading west on Germantown Pike -- this is for the eastbounders only).  The bathroom seemed old and kind of dirty, which is  unusual for a Starbucks and the store seemed a little crummy as well.  It wasn't terrible -- they made my drink just fine, but it just wasn't as nice as their stores usually are.
