I guess I go to Starbucks with a certain expectation of their standard and this store does not seem to meet it.

I absolutely love this Starbuck's location!  I work in this building and I have to say the crew here led by Zeb is amazing.  They are located right in the hustle and bustle of downtown Indy on the Circle so it is constantly busy yet the line is always flowing.  They are friendly, generous, attentive and fast.  They remember our orders and our names and they always have a great upbeat attitude.  When asked, the team enthusiastically gives spot on suggestions as well as clarifications when you don't quite know or understand how to ask for what you want.  I watched a biography of the history of Starbuck's this weekend on CNBC and feel that Howard Schultz would be proud of this location, they definitely embody the Starbuck's philosophy and culture.  I get a Grande Pike everyday and it really is just a better cup of coffee.  The outdoor seating is nice too in the summer, great people watching as there is always something going on on the Circle.

such great service! such good looking and fun, funny employees! the service was excellent, they love to make conversation. i had an employee talk to me on his lunch break and it was so nice to hear his story. i'd recommend this starbucks as a passerby or as a local!

This is by far the best Starbucks in town, I come here to study and it's pretty quiet. There's plenty of seating and is pretty large for a Starbucks which is really nice. They have the happiest, nicest employees i've ever met at a Starbucks. It's always very clean as well. I always love coming here and it's becoming a regular spot for me.

Great service, great atmosphere and great coffee! Parking is limited ans seating inside can get cramped, but it is still one of my favorite locations.  It's a little like Cheers, where everyone knows your name!  Why don't people who go often use the Starbucks cards, don't people realize that after so many drinks you get a free one!  If you are getting a drink anyway, put it on a card and register it and get the reward.  It just makes sense!
Never sat in there and had my coffee before,but, I must say this is one of the cleanest and quietest locations I've been to. Really nice and efficient staff, as well.

The workers at this starbucks are so friendly, whether its 5 am or 11am. I like coming here to change up my regular study spots. There are a lot of tables where you will see people doing work. It gets quite crowded on weekends.  Note- they will be closed all next week for a renovation! They did not know what it would look like but they said they think there will be more room for standing in line !

It's Starbucks - it should be the same around the world. However, this is the most sluggishly slow staff I think I've ever seen! The lady at the till took my order wrong and after giving me my total had to correct it based on the wrong size. The lady in front of me is waiting for her drink to be remade as she ordered - decaf - and I still stand hoping  the four baristas will get their act together so that I can get on with my Saturday! I'll drive to another location next time.

My second time here to the Starbucks location not much to say about this location it has the same like other Starbuck locations such as  WIFI, Coffee, cups, indoor/outdoor seating with umbrellas.  The service is great when not busy and when in busy expect wait times in the drive thru in mid afternoon time due to being next to the shopping centers, mall and restaurants.  Never had any type of my order when ordering for myself or a family member.
The staff are highest quality.  If anything is not to your liking, they will make it over.  They care enough to do that!  They care who you are and what your orders are.  And they can converse!!!

I am a student currently living around the CBD and have walked to several coffee shops around the area. I cannot sing enough praises about this Starbucks. This is hands-down the best coffee shop in the city. Almost never too crowded and a good chance you have a table or couch to sit on and enjoy your coffee while having a meeting or getting some studying done.   Some of the most pleasant baristas you will ever meet. Check it out!

Parking is pretty bad here so unfortunately lose a star. And if you are on Germantown PK its really only accessible if you are heading EAST. And the place could use a good dusting and window cleaning. Staff is professional and very nice. The coffee was hot and good and the cinnamon roll was light, airy and tasty.

I really dislike this Starbucks location. They never put whipped cream on my drinks when I always specifically say I want whipped.   The drinks taste the same as every other location, besides the missing whip.   Their drive thru is also terribly designed. If there are more that 3 cars in line you have to wait in the road or middle of the lot and hope no one cuts in front of you.
Starbucks + Drive thru = HEAVEN!!!! Staff is great, friendly and knowledgeable.

Nice sized Starbucks inside. Drive thru is never super packed and they've always gotten my order correct in the drive thru. Tonight I placed a mobile order and they almost forgot an item but they quickly corrected it and apologized. Baristas are friendly and helpful though.

I have been here maybe 3 or 4 times and every single time i am there they leave the back door open for hours and it is freezing for all the people sitting in the chairs. I have asked them to close the door and the girl gave me a nasty response saying they are getting a delivery which i understand but does the door have to stay open ALL NIGHT FOR HOURS???  This happens every time i go in there.They do not deserve your business and most of the people that work there are rude. Go to the one in Glen Mills or maybe not at all!
Regular manager is due back soon and new items will be added to the menu tomorrow.  Question:  which aroma do you prefer...coffee or BBQ?  I'm so glad a few favorite baristas are still here!

I enjoy Starbucks and this location. It's right in the middle of town and you can sit outside and enjoy your coffee and the weather. My only complaint is the long wait and it's a bit dirty. The tables and chairs always have some kind of spill marks and crumbs and the bathrooms are just filthy. I'm sure if they fixed those things up it would be much more appealing. Getting my grande white chocolate mocha now!

I don't know what the hell happened since the move to this location 2 months ago, but the staff that was reasonably friendly and efficient at the old location has apparently been replaced by a staff that's inattentive, surly & completely incompetent (Yes, they're mostly the same people, it seems, just very different attitude). Yapping/yelling/carrying on with one another about personal life stuff while ignoring people in line waiting to place their orders. Snapping at customers (including myself) for no discernable reason, sometimes just for asking for things like creamer. And I've personally seen them mocking someone waiting for their drive-thru order. Totally unprofessional. Plus, the place is brand-new and already looks like a dump -- the 4-5 times I've been in here, at varying times of they day, there has always been trash all over the floors/tables and overflowing trash cans, and none of the employees seem to care. I'll never go back to this location again, and I hope a Starbucks regional management person reads this and looks into this for themselves to rectify it. Don't just take my word for it. This is a REALLY bad look for a new location.

This is the closest starbucks to my house. Not bad most of the time, standard. They drive me crazy cause I request light ice on cold drinks as they fill the cup and you end up with about a third of a cup of fluid. If I don't get light ice the 4 dollar drink is gone by the time I get home. Ordered light ice today and they filled the cup. I think I'll just skip this starbucks aND stop at one where I'm not paying for ice instead of a beverage.
Very slow, the drinks are always wrong and it looks like it's our fault when we say it's not what we ordered.

I was starving and needed coffee desperately when I stopped by this location. It was a cold day outside so needless to say all the indoor seating was taken. The barista was very friendly and was quick about getting my coffee started. My spinach wrap was a different story. It seemed to take awhile and when they said it was ready, it was a breakfast sandwich. Apparently another customer had taken my wrap. The barista apologized and started another wrap for me. They served it as soon as it was ready, which meant it was super HOT. The taste was good though. Not my favorite Starbucks location, but not bad.
Maybe one day they will learn how to properly prepare the oatmeal. Or, maybe I should just stop coming to this one.

Lowering my review to 1 star. After being to countless Starbucks all over the country the Newton location is now consistently the slowest, by far. I think they must have management problems because after years of seeing familiar, hustling staff it's now new faces every time. No sense of urgency and the floor and tables were filthy. Yuck. I'm done.

This is the greatest Starbucks of all time. The people that work there are super friendly and super attractive. The atmosphere is relaxed and awesome and the drinks they make are to perfection. If you chose to go to any other Starbucks then you are just wrong.
This location is always so kind & polite, even when it's extremely busy & there's a little bit of a wait they are so delightful.

I should have driven to South County.   I want this store to succeed. It's in the town I live in. Unfortunately it seems like I might be the only one. There are 15 cars in the drive thru so I walked in,  to see 20 people waiting. I promised to bring drinks home otherwise I would have bailed. I keep hearing the word REMAKE at the end of the orders being called out. Theres an issue that needs to be figured out. Multiple mobile orders seem to be dropped. People waiting are noticeably displeased. I repeat I want this location to succeed, but they will have to do it without my future orders. (The pics attached show stock that has been sitting at the counter where the orders are placed for atleast 10 minutes and theres something(coffee-i hope) spilled all over the credit card terminal. )

I am a regular at this Starbucks because it happens to be on my way to and from work every day.  There are many reasons why I continue to come back other then the fact of convenience, those being;  The barista's are always so friendly and polite; every time I've gone, they're smiling and casually making polite chatter with the customers.  The service is generally very prompt even during the busy morning hours.  When they do fall behind, they always apologize and sometimes depending on how backed up they are and how long you have been waiting, they give you your drink on the house as an apology.  This Starbucks has either drop in or drive thru so it's fairly convenient during the winter season when you don't really feel like getting out into the cold to drop in.  They also serve a variety of different delectable treats and delicious foods.  I've only had one "bad service" with this location and that was that I ordered a venti, paid for a venti and was given a grande. I contacted them and advised the mistake and was apologized to profusely and mailed coupons.  I appreciated that they took notice of their mistake & promptly fixed it.

I had the weirdest experience here yesterday. I've been coming to this Starbucks for a few years now, it's convenient, the staff is friendly (I love Hunter!he's so sweet!) and my drink is always made right. I usually use the drive thru but yesterday I walked into the store to have a coffee date with my mom. So I order a frapp for myself and a pike place coffee with milk for my mom. The girl at the register served my mom's coffee with no milk and she served her espresso roast. So I take it back and request the coffee be changed to pike and with milk. She serves the pike and then tells me there's half n half at the bar. And I'm like "I would like milk please" and she again tells me there's half n half at the bar. So now I'm just staring at her with a confused face because I'm not asking for half n half lol I was asking for milk. Finally she rolls her eyes, puts milk in the coffee and rudely slides it to me. It was so weird! Is there some sort of "no milk in coffee" policy at Starbucks that I wasn't aware about? I don't understand why she gave me such a hard time about milk in coffee.
Great staff! Very helpful with questions and very friendly! The store could use some attention though as far as straightening and cleanliness goes. Wifi does not work on their patio.

I know Starbucks is known for their coffee, but I love love love thier Spinach Feta Wrap!!!  The coffee is pretty consistant from location to location, and I've noticed the staff is always super friendly.    The Edwardsville location is no different.  Good coffee.  Good food.  Friendly staff.  = AWESOME.

Horrible!!! Waited over 10 minutes for a decaf coffee at 7:00 at night! Don't most people drink decaf at night? Got half a cup and I couldn't wait anymore. Never offered a free cup. Rip off!!! I'm so mad. Why can't Starbucks understand that some people can't have caffeine. Should have went to Wawa for the $1 coffee. Would have gotten it 10 minutes quicker and wouldn't have waited 10 minutes for half a venti THAT I PAID FOR!!
This Starbucks is very consistent. Food is on point, their chai tea latte is properly mixed, and staff was friendly. All you can ask from a place of business!

Does not accept Starbucks gift cards. Had my gift card in hand on the counter the entire time while ordering. Only came to Starbucks to use a gift card and ended up having to use cash. Manager was actually the one to take my order.

Can every Starbucks be like this one? The staff here is amazing! So kind and they always wants the customer experience to be great. And...guess what??? Even when other area Starbucks stores never seem to have things in stock that I want...basic things...this location is always well supplied.  It's spacious and clean.  Mainly I like it here the most as the people who work here really care.  This location should be the one all others strive to be like. The customer experience is stellar. Bravo! Best Starbucks on the planet!

Always fast, with friendly service! This little neighborhood Starbucks doesn't ever seem to have a time when they aren't busy, and that's a great thing! And they have a very extensive staff favorites list, which is always fun to try!

Ok here we go....  1. I am from NYC. Starbucks is on every corner there. I have basically spent so much money on Starbucks I might as well hold a stock in it. 2. I live and breathe Starbucks. 3. I cant live anywhere where there is NO starbucks. 4. Metairie had one  and so wala! I was happy...... '  HOWEVER, I must say this one in particular kinda sucks. I do not  know whether or not they just are not good at customer service, or dont know what customer service is but I have not had many pleasant experiences here. I unfortunately still frequent it because its the only one around here.   example noted: I get my free drink monthly from my gold starbucks card and I happily order my Toffee Nut latte... me standing there for a good 10 minutes waiting for my drink as the "barista" chuckles and laughs with fellow co workers as she makes drinks..one, two , three, four people get their drinks and me.. still standing... waiting.. no one noticing. not the chuckling barista. THEN... when no one else is there finally!!! finally!! she asks what am I waiting for???   reality check starbucks barista girl. in NYC this would not have happened. second the drink was sub par and third I should have gotten a voucher for another free drink considering the amount of time and lack of acknowledgment you gave me.  Like I said, if there was another one across the street, I would ignore this one. but unfortunately this is probably the only starbucks around that I know of. Unless there is one magically hiding around close to metairie. ......

Came on a late Saturday morning.  Not crowded, right before lunch time.  People behind the counter were much more interested in their very involved (and loud) conversations than actually serving the customers.  Very long wait time for my drink, and then, when I finally got it, it was wrong.  Baristas here are clearly not paying too much attention to what they're getting paid to do.  Several other customers before me had gone to sit down to wait for their drinks because it took SO long.  This poor service is unusual for a Starbucks, in my experience.

We will start with the good, this particular Starbucks is located just off of I-4 and is open 24 hours a day.  After that though, it kind of heads downhill.  When we stopped in, it was late afternoon, and nobody else was in there.  For whatever reason, it still took way too long to make a few simple drinks.  Another reason for the low rating, there is no place to really hang out here, as there is with just about every Starbucks.  No chairs or couches, the place is purely built for you to go in and out, and that is all.  Overall, just not the Starbucks experience I am used to getting.
The workers here were super nice and let us try samples and everything, definitely recommend
Big fan. This place was busy. They were fast, so the wait went fast. The employees were friendly. And as busy as they were, the was very clean.
My go to starbucks! I really like it here, the workers are so kind and always do a great job with my drink!

Super slow! One would think as it is an airport Starbucks, efficiency would be the key word, but  no way. They are slow, employees are chatting to each other, and it can take 15-20 minutes to get coffee. Of course there might not be any cream by the time you get that far...use at your own risk of delay!!!

Don't bother ordering a mobile order. They will make you wait an unreasonable amount of time and then won't bother to serve you at the door. Waited 5 minutes for them to get a straw for my drink and then wouldn't even look in my direction. I would drive an additional 5 minutes to either of the other Starbucks than stop at this one.

I realize the Carmel Apple Spice is probably the least exotic drink on your menu...but if you can't be bothered to make it correctly...then stop selling it...today I paid $3.54 for a cup of warm Apple juice..No...whip cream No...carmel sauce....Come on guys...Really????

I really like the layout of this Starbucks.  There is a variety of seating choices (long wooden benched tables, small armchairs, and standard tables, with outdoor seating as well), with plenty of electric sockets for laptop use.  And being that this Starbucks isn't the biggest, I think they use the space nicely.  My drinks have always been made just fine here, and the baristas are friendly.  Parking is pretty easy, so what more could you ask for?  A great location to stop at after a morning workout at Spectrum!

What can you say about Starbucks. They have their quality down, their coffee down, and their pastries too down! ;-) So I hold the staff's feet to the fire. These people rocked it. They were friendly, polite, and extremely helpful!  I was tongue tied in ordering my "Skinny Venti Vanilla Latte! After we all laughed, I was schooled on the right way to order it! Their restrooms are always clean. That's huge to Gigi, the Mega-Princess! :-P The local bulletin board was filled with resources. I have to say this is one Big Corp Cuppa I enjoy! And this particular store rocks!

I feel like I need to write this because this is a recurring problem at this location that is absolutely ridiculous and frustrating.  This morning I placed a mobile order for a Venti iced tea at around 522. I got to the location at 5:30 that means there was eight minutes to make an iced tea. When I got there my order was not done. I continue to wait another eight minutes for my drink. Meanwhile there were three people working there. Two people inside and the one on drive-through. When I got there she asked me my name and I told her she told me she was working on it while she continue to do the drive-through and I continued to wait. The girl at the front did not do anything. She was talking to another employee instead of helping the girl on drive-through. My issue with this is that there's a sign that says if you wanna skip the line order on the mobile app. If I wanted to sit and wait another eight minutes for my drink then I would've simply gone to the drive-through or just order it when I got there. This is not the first time that this is happened at this location. Every time I order on the app I'm waiting for my beverage. This is absolutely insane. Why advertise skipping the line when you are constantly waiting for everyone else that is already there. This is simply poor customer service. And to top it all off my drink was not done properly. This is also an issue at this location because sometimes when I get my drink there is a ton of dragon fruit and sometimes there's about two pieces. Inconsistency is a major problem here. This location is most convenient on my way to work however I will be driving a different route just to avoid this happening again.
Great just like any Starbucks, just loses a star because if the amount of beggars out in the street in front of it and that come inside and harass the people

I'm a little hesitant to post this review, but recently I've been having a lot of meetings at Starbucks. I prefer Raging Sage, but Starbucks seems to be convenient for most people. So yeah, I give four stars for convenience. And they make my Americano right! I don't have to coach the barista on the fact that I don't want water in my iced American. Surprisingly, they don't even add water to an iced Americano, so they earn points again for convenience! There's really nothing on the Starbucks menu that I ever crave, so it's just nice to know that when I do have a meeting scheduled at Starbucks, that I don't have to make a pitstop at Raging Sage before or after.

This location is HORRIBLE!! You wait in line forever inside and at the drive through!  The drive through is set up so if there is more than 3 people in line you are blocking parked cars. The staff can be so obnoxious and rude! Forget about ordering a cold drink  by the time you get to the window the ice is melted! If you can, avoid this location!

ugh.  how many starbucks does one town need?  answer: zero.  avoid this corporate piss palace and support your local mom & pop coffee shop.  try Reds or Muddy Waters  if i could give negative stars i would most definitely do it.

I just love starbucks and this one rocks!  There is one female manager who is always on top of things and very personable.  The staff are always very nice, they remember your orders and are good w/ my boys.

Hey it's a Starbucks. Hard to tell them apart except for the staff. They do a rotation between Newtown and Heacock road. Starbucks embodies the ultimate McDonaldlization of society. Regardless of your staff you can always for the most part get what you want and it taste fairly similar. I.e. friers on the right levers on the left. So what I'm saying for an organization that is not franchised, who really runs them. Typically it's going to be your students and possibly mid level managers. Customer service is paramount. I think the idea for many customers is they like to build relationships and see that familiar face at each location consistently of the staff.  I've noticed that both customers and employees seem to be discouraged when they are store hopping. It feels good when you walk in welcomed by your first name and they know what your order is. I think that a Starbucks cared they would stop the movement and allow those relationships to form. Their sales would increase their ability to upsell would increase as well. It's all about relationships folks. I give three stars not because of the staff because of the executive management that does not value this type of relationship. I believe training is the underlining cause. Which probably breeds inefficiencies if someone does not want to move as the incentive is to excel to run the store. But this is only for the short term as Starbucks has a high attrition rate as this is only a stepping stone.
I go here pretty regularly because location is convenient. But I find the quality of service here to be consistently poor compared to other Starbucks.

This store is the slowest. I have worked in the food industry for 4 years so I understand being backed up. But every single time I come here it is backed up. They are never consistent with drinks either. I've had to repeat my mobile order and wait over 20 minutes for my drink.

This Starbucks is always terrible. Me and my girlfriend just moved to Franklin two months ago, ever since then, we have had nothing but bad experiences at this location. They are always out of an ingredient needed to make a drink, or they are just not carrying it completely. This location also makes drinks wrong constantly, and give us wrong orders as well. We now drive to the location off of cool springs blvd just to not have to deal with this Starbucks anymore.
They now have a secret code to use the restrooms which are locked. You must punch in on a keyboard. Extremely annoying. Take your coffee needs elsewhere.

To be clear this is the stand alone Starbucks on Rt 38.  It's new, has a drive thru and a decent little sitting area outside.  It's open early so I stopped twice after walking at Cooper River. Bad service.  Very unconcerned about a customer being in the store.  The first time I thought I just got the staff on a bad day.  Tried again and it was even worse.  Four staff, nobody even facing the customer area. Just standing around, two with their backs to me.  Nice big store with nice early hours but who needs to get that kind of attitude early in the morning?
Slowest Starbucks Ever.... three years I've been coming here and they never disappoint... guaranteed time to get a latte ... 8 minutes. I will say the people who work there are nice but come on

I have stopped here on several occasions with each and every visit ending on a poor experience. Out of all the SB stores in Tampa this has to be on the bottom of the list. I have been given luke warm coffee on one occasion and coffee without sweetener on my last visit. I only complain due to the fact that as we all know first thing in the morning when your in that morning wake up funk all you crave is a great cup of SB wake me up. It's a total bubble buster to leave the drive thru with your expected cup of happiness and end up tasting some blah tasting cold java....I don't believe I will be giving this SB a third attempt..Sad to say I will be avoiding this location and opt for one I have had better luck at...
Large dining area, quick service, friendly staff at this location. If you like Starbucks, this is a good one.

This is one of my favourite places to meet friends or colleagues for a coffee/tea when the weather is nice.  The outdoor patio is great with lots of tables and chairs that aren't crammed close together that you can hear every conversation around you.  It's in a great are too.  If weather doesn't permit to sit outside, there isn't a lot of seating inside and it can get cramped.  Some of the table and chairs are close together and most are for tables of 2.  It also can get busy during coffee break time and I have been where the line up is to the door.  The waiting area for drinks can get crowded too.  Will keep coming back when the weather is nice to enjoy the patio!

This rating has nothing to do with the drink. It is all about the service.  It was horrible. Since when does Starbucks give bad service? I felt unwelcomed when I entered the store. I wasnt greeted. It felt like they wished people weren't there.  They were busy complianing about another guest while I was placing my order. That is not something I wanted to listen to at the counter.  When I was handed my drink, I was not handed a straw. I walked over to self-service counter to get one and there was not venti straws. So when I went back to counter to ask for one, she huffed about me interupting her rant. Really??? If you focus on customer service and speed drink prep, customers would be happier. Maybe this is why I go to the other store on 96th street. They are way more friendly.
A bunch of rude college kids rushing your orders. They often get our order wrong. We stopped going there.  I'm not supporting any business that won't respect paying customers .

I literally drive out of the way DAILY to visit my friends at this Starbucks.  You won't find a better staff that is dedicated to bringing you the best hand crafted master pieces with such sweet personalities!  Now that they have been given a bigger space with a drive thru they have continued to maintain that one of a kind customer relationship as well as keeping up with the increase in demand.  Definitely stop and see them.

On my way to work just back from class I thought I'd stop at Starbucks and I am so glad I did. I had to reload my card with a credit card which I thought you know all of this going to take a little while it  was so quick so easy Sophia was amazing she was in the drive thru window. It's so refreshing to start your day off right. Thanks again Sophia

Great location and friendly baristas! Perfect reason to stop in for coffee and read. I'm always happy with my cup of joe here!    The pastries are usually limited, not sure if it's because people have a major sweet tooth in the area or they are under stocked. Seating is a bit sparse but they have a few tables outside for a sunny day.

Waited in line 20minutes to hear that everything I was interested in eating was sold out.  I asked why, her response was that there was a convention at the hotel.  Really, I would think that that was a common occurrence and that maybe Starbucks management should take such things into consideration when ordering for the week.  Missed sales means less profits!!!  But,they did have coffee and it was delicious!

Unfortunately this is the closest Starbucks by my house. I should probably start making my own drinks at home and save myself the headache. They had a sign in the front that said to try their store favorite drink (it was a sign on the black board with chalk) -- anyways it was a matcha frapp with brown sugar and green whip....so I  decided to order it thinking "Oooo this might be delicious"...yeah, well they didn't even know what I was ordering & didn't know how to make it...I received some over sweetened drink with white whip... and some brown powdery sprinkles on the whip...so I  asked why the whip isn't green and they said they don't do that (so why advertise it?) and then I  asked what the brown stuff was on top of the whip...they said brown sugar garnish.....it was cinnamon  this drink was SO sweet that my teeth literally hurt (and I love sweets)... did I mention this Starbucks made me cry before because I ordered a venti iced tea and they gave me the wrong size/wrong item and when I made a comment about it I  was apparently inconveniencing the barista... this place is not cool.

The service at this Starbucks is always just okay. I frequently go between this store and the Starbucks on Pantano and Speedway as well as Kolb and Broadway. The customer service at the other stores is always far superior.  I come here because it is convenient and very close to Trader Joes!

I am a daily Starbucks junkie. Gotta have it. I usually only go to this location when I am too lazy to get out of my car. This location has messed up every latte I order. Never fails. Line is sometimes long-sometimes slow. When I came to this location on a regular, they never recognized my voice to my drink and name like the Big Bend location does. Not usually friendly-maybe because it's so close to UT?
I love this Starbucks. The people that work here are always nice and fast. It's a bit small on the inside but the drive through is usually quick.
Pretty big with outdoor tables. Convenient location at a mall complex with a drive thru window. Free wifi is nice. AC is blowing. Floors and tables are dirty and trash needs taking out.
i had to wait 20 minutes for my caramel macchiato. 20 minutes! no thank you. i really didn't enjoy coming to this location

Wah!  The local coffee house I used to visit when I was in this area went under!  Bah!  Well, a man has gots to have his mocha, if you know what I mean.  Hmm.  OK, that sounds dubious at best.  Anyway, I went to the local Starbucks instead after discovering the local independent was gone.  Service was actually friendlier than the last time I visited a Starbucks.  But the coffee was not up to even Starbucks normal standards.  Espresso in my mocha was off...did they change their espresso roast since my last visit to this chain?  Or was it just burnt or something?  Is Starbucks losing consistency?

Iced coffee was old/had a really weird taste. The manager tasted it after I brought mine back and agreed and made me an iced americano instead! So there was a mess up but they were so awesome about it!

We come to this starbucks whenever we are in the area. Great, fast, friendly service each time we've been in. I always get the caramel Machiato, or the white chocolate mocha and my other half always gets his norm the americano.

Road trip Coffee.   The Caramel Ribbon Frappuccino one of us got  looks like a dessert. It has Marshmallow whip cream added, wow.  I got my usual Grande Caramel Macchiato. I added a pump of White Chocolate Mocha, so good, just not hot enough.   Cute little Starbucks and the people are very friendly. 5 stars for that.

This location has a couple of very sweet employees that always try to be friendly and upbeat. It's always a pleasure to see them!  However, I come here atleast 5 times a week and I never get the drink in under 20 minutes.  I've had to leave because after 20 minutes I am officially late for the next appointment and I just don't get the drink I paid for. The best way to get a drink is to order when you are 20 minutes away so you don't have to wait more than 5 minutes in the store. The owner of this store should be embarrassed about these numbers. But I've been coming for a year and it's getting slower!

Stopped in to get coffee for my long ride back home as I'm from out of state, and was very happy with the customer service from the staff! The young lady (wow that makes me sound old, lol) who took my order was very pleasant! The store is beautiful & if I had the time, I wud have loved to stay awhile & enjoy the atmosphere!! And of course my coffee was simply to die for! Thanks for giving me a really nice moment in my day!!

This is my LAST VISIT to this Starbucks. I met a friend this past Friday afternoon. I walked in and no one was in line so I was excited to be able to order my right away. WRONG!!  The two people behind the counter were busy arranging the food and counting money. The talk slim girl at the cash register didn't even look up. I stood there for several minutes without acknowledgement. I walked out and told my friend what happened and so she chose not to order anything. We sat outside at the tables and drank our own water. We used to meet here 1-2 times per week. We changed our location and will now go to the Starbucks on Speedway and Wilmot. This is a double bonus location as Trader Joe's is in the same complex.

Lovely to look at, but impossible to love.   The setting alongside Lake Afton is as pretty as a postcard, but the 3 car parking lot is ridiculously inadequate. It makes a difficult traffic location that much worse, forcing people to focus on parking rather than driving.  Double and illegal parking happen every day, creating a dangerous condition. Amazing that the Yardley government permitted Star$$$ to do this.    The coffee is the same as everywhere else, which to me is poor to average for the basics, but decent if you fall prey to the insidious upsell pressure to go latte or capp in order to be able to imbibe the burnt tasting brew.   The facility is pleasant once inside, with windows facing Main Street or the lake. It is also a front row seat to the near miss accidents that routinely happen there.
Coffee is ok, staff is great, but it is consistently cold in here. Turn the heat on, please! :(

This starbucks is at a busy intersection and the parking lot always seems to be full or almost full when I come here. Despite the limited parking, I really like this one. It's a starbucks so you're basically going to get a standard as far as food and beverages go, but I have to say I had a really great customer service experience there yesterday. The girl taking my order was incredibly friendly and very cheerful. The girl making my drink even started a brief conversation with me while she was making my drink. They always ask for your name when they're busy so they can keep track of drinks, but they weren't busy and asked for mine anyway and then called me by it through the experience.   I hit this place on my way to baltimore sometimes, so although it's a mile and a half off of 95, I don't mind the extra time it takes to get here if I get friendly service

Worst Starbucks experience I've had. I was the only person in line and wasn't acknowledged for quite a while. Till person was sub par, and made me feel weird. Bar person had a headset on the entire time - which I would understand if she was talking to drive through customers - but I could tell quickly that she was talking to her coworker through it (the till girl). She made no contact with me. I've worked for SBux before and this was the worst experience I've had.

It really catches me off guard when I swing by this location with about 30 other people in line most of the time, yet somehow the staff here is ALWAYS as nice and courteous as they can be. This is probably my favorite Starbucks around.

Starbucks is, of course, something I love to indulge myself with and by indulge I mean purchase on a semi-regular basis, but who's counting? My go-to drink is always the same and, like McDonalds, Starbucks does what it does well and with uniform predictability. My grande non-fat iced dirty chai always tastes exactly the same. Same goes for muffins, breakfast sandwiches, and of course the occasional cake pop.  So, why three stars? Everyone else said it - parking lot and drive-thru are about as cramped as can be. It can get messy when you are parked in the lot and can't get out b/c the drive-thru is backed up - or when you want to leave the window and there's someone trying to turn onto Kennedy from Dakota... nightmare! My advice, backtrack to platt or cleveland. Anyway, other than that, the location is fine and the staff is friendly! I can't say i'd really want to sit out there at night, unfortunately, though, so that sort of takes away from the coffee house charm.  Quiet atmosphere inside, mostly people popping in for quick orders or set up with laptops. Frequent business meetings, which makes sense, since it's in a good central location.
Slowest starbucks I've ever been to. Waited in the drive thru line well over 10 minutes! Ridiculous!
Love the fact that they have a drive thru service. Saves me time and fills me caffeine addiction before going to work.

This is my all-time favorite Starbucks location! I have been a loyal Starbucks Gold Card member for some years. There have been a lot of Good, even some great locations... but this one by far is my favorite. It is very convenient for me because it is on my way to work, but there are times I have had to go to other locations for different reasons. Every time I do, I am disappointed because of the HIGH BAR this location has set. The manager is exceptionally helpful, friendly and professional. The staff is AMAZING. Friendly, upbeat, proficient. I have never had problems with my order. They're smiles make my day! KUDOS! I'm a fan!  ~Annie Kidd

We were staying at a nearby hotel, mobile ordered, and picked up our food at the hand off plane! Our order was not quite ready for us but almost! There was not much of a connection at the handoff but our food and beverages were great! We will return on our trip!

Great location right inside the GSR and the staff is always usually very friendly, but during times of the year especially holiday events the line service is way to slow. They need to figure out how to bring in some extra help, cashiers and overall people movers...the last thing you want to do is wait 15-20 mins to place your coffee order. You need coffee not to be grumpy and making people wait for coffee makes them grumpier.

I travel through out the country and visit a Starbucks daily. I truly believe in the product and experience. This location needs assistance. Customer service is lacking and productivity is almost non existent.   3/10/16 if zero stars was an option that's what today's visit would get. This store needs some supervision and to be attentive.   Starbucks fan for life just need to find a new store off the expressway.

It gets really busy here both inside and drive-thru.  The parking lot layout is designed poorly and can be a hazard for those using the drive-thru.  It's also hard to get out of the plaza during certain times if you are trying to get back on Brandon Blvd.  The place needs a little tidying up.  There are lots of crumbs, napkins, and spills on the tables and floors.  Trash cans are overfilled and lots of flies hovering inside.  Great location but the inside of the store needs a some attention.
Seriously the most friendly  starbucks drive through I've ever been to. Tasy breakfast and a decent cup of Joe all packaged by a really awesome worker

My last review was made out of frustration with the staff that week. But now that my regular baristas are back, I'm back in my happy place. The service from the regular staff is incredible- very happy and they get to know the regulars

Took a little coffee break coming from Hearts Castle back to L.A. This place was busy but the staff was very friendly. Coffee  was delish as usual. Bathroom is clean. And it's easy access from the shopping center where it's located.

Since it's opening a few months ago, I have been to the Drive-Thru at this location twice. Both times, I was helped by Rebecca, who was simply a delight! She was so kind, upbeat, and had a great attitude! The first day I visited, I was in a crappy mood and her energy turned my whole day around. Wonderful customer service like hers doesn't happen every day, which is why I was so thankful to see her a second time. Nothing beats getting your coffee with a friendly smile!

Short and sweet review - This location offers your typically Starbucks faire. I marked this one down because the customer service is hit or miss with mostly misses. There are 4 Starbucks locations in my area and this is one I visit the least. Not the friendliest baristas I've encountered. Don't feel the same welcoming environment I feel when I walk into other locations.
Its a starbucks,  they are all about the same but this one seem to have the world smallest parking lot and drive tho, people are always trying to get in and out and its just a mess out their.

As far as Starbucks goes, this one is pretty awesome. Service is comparable to other starbucks around the city (which does mean it's slower than tims) and everyone is friendly and welcoming. It's nothing that stands out, but it's cozy and it's probably one of my favourite Starbucks in the city. Their drive through always makes my day too if I ever go through early in the morning!

Don't even know where to start! They don't put names on orders, how do you know whose bagel is whose! So shit show at the counter needless to say people trying to guess who was whose! Then my drink was wrong and when asked to remake it they asked if I even knew if it was my drink or not.... well had my name been on it I would be positive but for now I'm assuming since no one else came to grab it! So when they made it right the employee mocked me while the other employee gave me my drink!!! WILL NEVER EVER RETURN!!!

Very friendly staff!! Comfortable atmosphere for a Starbucks. I don't care for Starbucks, but I like that Starbucks. I had a caramel frappuccino and the foam was so sweet. Terra the cashier took her time with me. I had a gift card that needed to be scanned. She took her time and made sure everything went through correctly, my final bill was .23. Stop by for a refreshing coffee drink after riding the street car.

This location has to be one of the sloppiest of them all.   This morning, the bathroom was not only filthy, the toilet was backed up and almost overflowing!  The floors were dirty, and the half & half dispenser was totally out, and no one seemed to care or designated to look after these "housekeeping" issues - I guess it's because they were too busy making coffee drinks, but appearances and cleanliness is important, no matter how busy you are!  There is always a wait, and it was prime time (between 9 and 10 a.m.).  PJ's is looking better and better - I won't buy anything here, as a matter of principal - I walk over with a friend, but don't patronize the place.

We literally just came from this Starbucks and I want to throw up! Not only did they run out of ingredients for my favorite holiday drink but they freakin gave me a cover with someone's lipstick on it! I'm soooo grossed out!!!!!

This Starbucks is always busy and packed, but mostly because it's the size of a large closet. There are only three tables in the whole joint, and I'm sure that's on purpose. With the number of people shuffling in and out, it's tough to be productive. Fortunately they have more tables outside. So if the weather is nice, you can enjoy some great sites of Indianapolis' downtown circle.
Low key Starbucks on Oracle. The usual friendly staff and plenty of comfy chairs.

This Starbucks is probably one of the best I've been to! The store itself is clean, the majority of the time. A couple times I've come in and some of the tables were dirty, but who knows, the people could have left a minute before I walked in. It's usually not too packed, but then again, I try to avoid it early mornings. When the drive thru is busy, more often then not they've either apologized immensely for the wait, or even put my drinks on the house. The employees are a generally happy group. I understand people have their bad days, so I usually blow off bad attitudes, since it happens so rarely. I tried applying here once, around November, and went in to talk to the manager about it. She seemed pretty rude, but I haven't seen much of her since that day, and the employees rarely act that way. Over all a good Starbucks. I'll continue being a regular there!
You will sit in the drive thru for at least half an hour between 12-5pm. If you don't, you got lucky. They are slow and still get a simple order wrong.

Literally just left this Starbucks drive thru. I drove through yesterday morning and was specifically looking for the raspberry loaf to have with my chai tea latte, unfortunately they were out. I was craving Starbucks again today so I went back to this same Starbucks cause they make all the drinks THE BEST. Anywho, drove through, asked for the raspberry loaf again and they didn't have any again which was no biggie but I was craving one so I got the next best thing which was their classic coffee cake. Drove up to pay and get my items and the lady who helped me at the window remembered me from yesterday and apologized for not having the raspberry loaf, shortly after our encounter the store manager came to the window to apologize and inform me they'll make sure to order more raspberry loafs to be safe and because of the inconvenience (which it wasn't at all) they comp'd my classic coffee cake. True class, professionalism, and customer service at its best. BEST STARBUCKS in town...on all levels!!

Very slow service inside. It seems that they pay more attention to the drive-through customers. Coffee was okay but they needed to restock the milk.  Spacious interior but I didn't care for the loud music playing.  Outdoor seating but noisy as next to busy road.   Too bad no one was smart enough to put the outdoor seating to the side or back Where it would be quieter.

Service is always speedy and accurate! I'm a creature of habit so I always get a soy white chocolate mocha latte. Lately I've been a fan of the pepperoni turnover.   Grabs seat outdoors kraut in the cafe if you have some time.
We ordered a caramel coffee and they made it perfect.  Nothing special, just a run of the mill Starbucks.
Polite folks, but politeness doesn't stock the bathroom with toilet paper.  You have a job people!  Would you freakin' do it?
When I get a refresher it's always way under filled and the music inside was cranking. Sounded like a club in there. Very hard to study.

I'm conflicted on this review.  It is obvious by my 90+ check-in's here that I am a fan of Starbucks.  However, the customer service at this location seems to be lacking.  There was a time when they were a lot better but in the last few months it has been declining.  Let me start by the presentation of my drink.  My paper coffee cup shouldn't look like it was dipped in the coffee.  There shouldn't be coffee around the rim of the lid either.  Second, they have a hard time getting my order right lately.  I order the same damn thing every time; a tall with 4 splenda and light ice and a grande with 5 splenda.  My issue is they always forget the light ice.  Light ice doesn't mean one ice cube in my drink.  I order light ice because I want to immediately drink my coffee and not have to worry about 3rd degree burns.  If my coffee has light ice it shouldn't burn my mouth and make me use profanity.  Other than that, it's a Starbucks.
They just opened and the service is excellent! They are VERY pleasant, my order was perfect and I normally have a complicated order. This is deff one of my new favorite locations

Second time visiting was a vast improvement from the first.  The first time I waited FOREVER for my coffee...this location was extremely busy during both of my visits where I went inside to order.  The line moved quickly and the barista was very polite.  We had a bit of a longer than anticipated wait for our coffees but I think that is do to them focusing on the drive thru line.  I feel they could be a bit more organized and the place could be a bit tidier but my coffee was delicious and the patio outside relaxing.  I hope they keep up the improvements as I'd like to keep coming back.
Great starbucks location. Very friendly service the few times I've been here. Love the drive thru bonus.

I kind of loathe reviews of chains, but I feel obligated to make a comment seeing as I "checked in" at this particular location. Darn you Yelp iPhone app!  I pretty much feel "a-ok" about all Starbucks regardless of location. Their beverages don't send me into a transcendent state, but they do warm me up and make me feel like I'm giving myself a little treat. It's especially nice when I remember that I have money on my little key fob thing and can rack up those little stars towards nifty rewards. Who doesn't like a freebie drink on their birthday?  The staff here was friendly. They made my drink correctly. That pretty much sums it up.

Recently I've seen an improvement in service. I also have noticed several new employees and so am happy that management must be listening to feedback. If the service levels continue to be positive I will update my rating. Keep up the customer focus......it matters!
Recently opened! This place is new and has plenty of windows for sipping your coffee and wandering off. Staff looked a little busy when I arrived but thats just the new normal with mobile orders

A community a Starbucks as you'll probably get.  If you catch them at the right time you might even walk away with a free sample.  That said they do get busy and you can tell the staff gets a bit overloaded.  Try to come by at off hours and it will improve the experience.
waited in line at the drive-thru for 30 minutes at 2:30 PM. No option to leave because of the curb on both sides. Absolutely ridiculous. Will not return.

Part of the AWESOME crew at the Starbucks in Havertown, Pa (On West Chester Pike).... I say "part" because Greg and a few of the girls are missing from the photo.  I've probably been to 100 Starbucks (haha maybe more!) in various states/cities/towns, and this is by far one of the better ones. The best in every way - The service, management, consistency, atmosphere, seating, the space and the overall crew. My favorite Starbucks! Grazie guys for your wonderful service and personalities! You're the BEST and an asset to the Starbucks Company!!! @starbucks @jakeskalish...

Located on Whyte Avenue along what some have dubbed "Coffee Corner" this place is packed, overflowing, loud, and too busy. I generally head to the independent Block 1912 across the street rather than a chain place if I can help it.

I have had bad service with one of the employees, he has no customer service skills whatsoever. I was  the only one speaking, all he did was tell me the amount I owed. The only reason I'm giving it 2 stars is because it's Starbucks and the smoothie was ok.

This starbucks is pretty reliable in terms of service and drink quality. The wait in line can take a while but they make the drinks very quickly. I got a caramel macchiato and they made it perfectly - not too sweet, with the right amount of caramel, foam & coffee.
Typical Starbucks the staff is friendly and the bathroom code is easy to remember which is more than I can say for some of the other Starbucks I've been too.

I'm the only person here and they are taking care of drive thru.   There are three people working and not one of them can handle the people that are walking through the door.   When I asked if my drink was ready the answer was I'm sorry the drive-through is busy  Hi I'm not in the drive-through  The only person not making drinks is taking out the garbage  Worst Starbucks ever

I've been waiting 10 mins for one caramel marchiato and a frapachino not to mention the guest that c a me before me didn't even get their drinks I got mine first.  Horrible. The drive thru was wrapped around the store

I'm a diabetic and before I ran my daughter there I researched online my drink options. Satisfied with my choice, I ordered the drink I found online only to be told this location didn't have it. ( some sugar free vanilla spice option). However, the barista said she could make something very similar that would meet my sugar free requirements. Delicious! Thanks to her, I am a sugar free vanilla latte with 3 pumps chai addict. I've gone to other Starbucks and placed my order and have had comments made by the employees on how that must be yummy. Kudos to her!
The place looks nice for my first time there. But I had my coffee made by a very rude barista. I am discouraged from coming back to this well invested branch but poor choice of staff.

I don't usually have coffee at Starbucks but when i do, I make sure it's Reserve beans on a Clover machine. Stay thirsty, my friend!  (apparently this is the only Starbucks in the state with a Clover machine. Google it and try it, you will be addicted. Columbia beans are great and a good value)

Best Starbucks in the city!!!  They are prompt, courteous and friendly.  Because Ive been going there for years, they know my order.  All the employees are great.  FRAN IS THE BEST!!!  She is a gem!!  Kelly, Adrian Aya'naziah and Seth are wonderful!!  Always upbeat and glad to see you!  This Starbucks is like the CHEERS of Philly..............where everybody knows your name and drink!!   Cheers!!

this place is one of the newer Starbucks in the area so facility wise, it is big and clean. I love how they have the cold brew and nitro cold brew ready to go every day; other places I've been to runs out by the afternoon. the staff is ok; haven't had too much interaction with them but they seem nice enough.  I like the big circular table in which is standing height (for an average human being height). is it great to get some work done while you are standing; put's a lot less stress on the back.  the bathroom is pretty clean at all times; kinda surprised for a new facility like this they do not have an automatic flush. parking is great; they have a huge lot where you can park.  I think one of the biggest pluses to this location is that it is going to be part of a strip mall. they'll have new things coming in so if you need to take a break and walk around, you're going to have that option. there is also a CVS down 0.1 miles down so you can walk and get some snacks and such if you like.   overall, good spot, good facility

The customer service needs to be revivewed.  The cashier seemed very impatient or he just didnt want to be there.  Then when my friend mentioned about here Americana was not up to the top of the cup, I tried to laugh it off, but the three girls were laughing and rolling their eyes.   I felt very disresepcted and it WAS not like the other Starbucks I enjoy.  Never will return.

WTF are you you looking to me for?  It's just a Starbucks FCS. WRONG!  This is NOT just ANY Starbucks. You've got the best staff in the chain right here.  You'll sometimes hear the service is slow.  That's  because every Sum 'B  in Pinellas County starts lining up at the window at 4:50 AM for coffee.  There's near accidents and angry exchanges every morning as caffeine addicts jostle in and out of Fourth Street cueing up for their first fix of the day.  Nearby there exists a half dozen other very good places to get a cup of coffee and even better donuts.  But those other places don't make you feel as good about being up that early and starting your day.  When these folks meet you at the window it's with a smile like my Daddy used to get on pay day when he brought Momma home his check. They're still giving the best service.

Starbucks is my pick up of the day and by far has the best dark roast when compared to most coffee shops. However, beware of going to this location around 5 pm. I placed an order at 532 for cold brew black venti, paid and revived my order at 550.

I've come into this location a couple of times and the service had been fine.  This morning was a bit unexpected.   I'm a Gold Card carrying member and spent $10 on two drinks this morning.  While I finished my first one listening to music, I approached the counter to get another.  The nice kid attempted to get it as a refill, but when he asked the manager how to ring it up, I overheard her say "Absolutely not".  No biggie, I didn't mind paying full price (although $5 for cold foam seems a bit excessive) What disturbed me was after I received my drink, I asked for additional cold foam as it was lacking a bit.  When I asked the girl at the bar, she snootily said "you could have asked for additional foam when you ordered it".    What happened to them making it right if it's none done right the first time?   I'm sadly disappointed in this location and will likely find another location to frequent.   Poor customer service ladies.... just poor form!!

Some of the employees are nice. Some are in too much of a hurry to get back to whatever else they're doing. Drinks have been messed up.. have had drinks shoved out the drive-thru window with a quick "have a good day," and the door closed on my face before I could even ASK for a straw.  All-in-all, not impressed.   I've come back to lower my rating. This location is awful. All of my drinks taste terrible, like they let the espresso go bad before it makes it into my drink (burnt flavor). The staff is REALLY lackluster. I've spoken with Mike Rose (district? manager) twice and no results have come from the discussions he and I have had. "Oh, yeah! I'll talk to my crew and make sure they make sure to give you a straw. I'll talk to them about not speaking to the customers in a rude tone of voice." Well, the lady with the blonde hair and glasses still snips as customers.   I've decided to go out of my way and walk into the Warminster Starbucks. Fabulous workers and the drinks are always on point.

I've given this Starbucks at least 10 different attempts and the majority of my visits have been horrendous. The few good baristas cannot make up for the too many poor ones with bad attitudes and bad work ethic.   The only positive to this location is their drive thru. Be warned! They cater to the drive thru more so than their visiting customers. If you're in a rush, your best bet is their drive thru or else you'll be waiting twice as long inside the store
This location has greatly improved over the past few months. They always make my order right and the line moves much faster. I appreciate management taking action and improving the service.
Slow AF but the barista made my very berry hibiscus really well. I've had them made before where my drink was a shade of pink. The one made by the guy at this location had a nice strong flavor.

I've always had a bad experience at this location. Last time there was some weird leak in my Frap cup and by the time I got to Sephora I noticed the trail of coffee behind me. The staff is always rude, and very busy too.   It's also not a location that you can sit and chill at. It's a revolving door and reading isn't possible, too many distractions.
Poor service, terrible line control. 4 people behind the counter yet only one making drinks, with lines out the door. Last Carmel macchiato outta this one

This Starbucks sits deep inside the Marriott. So what you get is a plush Starbuck's experience. There is a separate room for the plush chairs.  This is one of the best smelling Starbuck's. The same fragrance smell that fills the Marriott fills the air in this Starbuck's. The result is that you feel like your breathing in potpori.   The prices are steeper than your usual Starbucks outside of downtown New Orleans. However, the atmosphere is so relaxing that I felt like getting in my daily coffee here was worth the steeper price.
Nice location. Friendly and knowledgeable staff. I ordered a Venti Mocha Frapp with two shots!

I like this Starbucks because it stays open until 10:30 pm on weekend nights.  It's probably one of the few places in DelCo to get a late night cup of Joe and have it not be a diner or an all night Dunkin Donuts.  Staff is nice and pleasant.

I love new locations and I am extremely satisfied with this location.  Love the decor, service and all the amenities (drive thru, outdoor seating, can walk to whole foods which is a minute away).  One of the baristas welcomed me by name on my 3rd regular visit; it shows the baristas emotional intelligence.

I grew up down the street from this Starbucks and come into town to visit my parents often. Usually people are friendly at this location but the past 5 days we've been going in the people give us attitude and are nice to the regulars and sometimes get them their drinks before us, then don't call our orders when they're ready. I hope their lives get happier!
Most  ridiculously long drive-thru wait. Not even sure how it was possible for it to be that long. There was no escaping the line after 15 minutes lapsed, and they didn't care at the window.

Normal starbucks just located in roswell, new mexico.  Maybe area 51?  Not too far from Slu's campus it is under the UFO looking thing.  I keep my eyes peeled for Will Smith and Tommy Lee Jones to stop a bug from taking off with the galaxy.  They have a drive thru.  I would have expected some kind of delivery system, but maybe it costs too much to fly that saucer?  Some people need their fix at starbucks every day.  I don't know if I'd trust little green men with my brain chemistry or risk prolonged contact with the unknown.  Proceed... with... caution!
Manager and staff are awesome. Same good stuff as every Starbucks. Best staff I've seen

The staff at this Starbucks is exceptional. I have had nothing but positive interactions with these hardworking folks.  Of course, it's Starbucks- my grande iced coffee is always the same, but I visit this location because I get my beverage quickly and I'm greeted with a friendly hello and smile each time I'm there.  I know this location is a tough one to work at, as I've seen the employees have to deal with some issues here and there. I give them a lot of credit and I want to thank them for their hard work!

MY home Starbucks. The baristas know my name. And I can sit at the bar and not feel like a scourge. They accomodate my requests whenever I ask with nary an attitude to be tossed. I love them. As far as a corporate entity goes, this one has the feel of a neighborhood joint, and that's the story of how Starbucks finally won me over.

This is my home-base Starbucks and it's because it's friggin awesome. They have a large sitting area with its own 'private' community room near the back so if you use Starbucks as a glorified study lounge (like me), you'll always find a place to sit. Also, there are a ton of power plugs so it's great to plug a computer in and work.  They also have the Starbucks Clover machine there which lets you get really great coffee instead of typical drip coffee. I personally am a bigger tea person so I don't try the Clover machine a lot, but I know a lot of people who rave about it.  Most of all, I find the employees at this Starbucks to be super nice. They're all really friendly and they do really care about making sure your drink is just right. And when you're paying a premium price for a drink, service is a big deal.  Between the location, baristas, and just Starbucks in general -- the Sicklerville location gets five stars from me.

They never cook the bacon on the bacon Gouda sandwich! I always ask for them to cook the bacon separately from the sandwich, because bacon can not cook with the bread on top of it... that should be common sense. They need a new system to cook those sandwiches or stop selling food with uncooked rubbery bacon. So I tell them to cook it separately, they overcooked the sandwich so much I almost broke my tooth, couldn't even bite into the bread it was so hard. I'm so pissed. This happens everytime I try to get a sandwich from any location.

Deck overlooking Lake Afton makes this a 4 star coffee shop, especially when tapping into the wifi, working remotely and enjoying a latte and a cake pop. Great friendly service and plenty of seating make this a staple for lazy days of coffee consumption and paper reading. Not a lot of parking...repeat: not a lot of parking!
This is one of my favorite Starbucks. They drive thru is always fast. They can handle large orders quickly. Make sure you use your Starbucks card to earn points towards FREE drinks.

This review is not for the quality of the product but for the customer service, or in their case- lack thereof. This Starbucks location continually has crappy customer service, and from the way it looks- it's all coming from one cranky bitch.   I went in one morning to get a breakfast sandwich (Ham & Cheese Artisan sandwich with no egg)...and noticed that another type of sandwich had bacon on it. Mm, bacon...right? So I asked if bacon could be put onto my sandwich. The answer: No. Let me be clear, it wasn't the "no" that was the problem, per se. It was the snarky attitude of the woman behind the counter. It wasn't 'no sorry' or 'you'd have to purchase both sandwiches'..it was "No" with the implication and attitude that the answer is no because she'd rather go stick her chest out and flirt with that married man over there than dick around with something for me.   I prodded as to WHY it couldn't be done, and got the *sigh* and even an *eyeroll* from the woman behind the counter. How STUPID of me for not knowing that the sandwiches comes pre-packaged, right? *slaps forehead* I totally forgot I worked for Starbucks last week! I pointed out that if egg could be taken OFF of a sandwich, then surely something could be put ON a sandwich. I also pointed out that I had no problem paying for the other sandwich so that the ingredients could be combined into one. Apparently a ham & bacon merger was imposssible for her. When, let's be clear - every other Starbucks is capable of doing what she is telling me she cannot do.   I am the customer. I did not ask you for anything for free. If you have bacon in the building and I ask for bacon on something- then do your frickin' job and give me the damn bacon! If I ask for a Bacon Frappacino I expect you to start blending. I have no clue what this woman's issue was...maybe she needs to get laid because her bitchery was off the charts. So I grabbed my drink and told the manager at the time (who is now retired) that I would not be back.  And I meant it. Until I went back. UGH.   I got in a situation where it was either stop there for something, or starve til morning. Blah Blah blah I go in at 6:20pm and order my sandwich. Miss Stick-Up-Her-Butt was there and couldn't make my food because she'd turned the oven off. Um, WHY? Why did you turn off the oven 40 minutes before you're supposed to close? Simple. She wanted to leave and instead of serving customers she just turns off everything and turns people away. Lame.  .  .

Apparently the make all their expresso drink iced unless you specify HOT. So this results in a further delay when u get your drink OR no drink at all if you figure it out when your husband gets home with it and assumed you ordered it that way. Tried to call and discuss this with a manager but their phone constantly rings busy which means they took it off the hook. Real professional!! I'm so over the Sub par coffee and awful customer service. LaColombe is only 20 mins away and so worth it!!

Every time I've gone to this Starbucks, my Frappuccinos have all been liquidy and not icey at all. It's been disappointing and a waste of money. We only have gone there more than once due to convenience in location, but it's not worth it anymore. Maybe Dunkin' will be a better option.

Love this store but Some times you have to wait in the road just to get into the start of the drive thru line. Guess thats any star bucks now days. Parking is usually terrible if you plan on going inside also. But customer service is usually pretty great and the drinks are good also. I've even got my order for free one time since he said I waited to long in the line. Will definitely continue to stop by.

I know that it's just a starbucks so my expectations are not crazy high or anything, but other reviewers are correct when they say it is slow service here. Although the coffee is fine, the baristas always seem to be in a bad mood to me, and they are ALWAYS out of food items like cake pops and sandwiches. The only reason I keep coming is how convenient the location is to my work.   Today I ordered a Gouda sandwich and it was falling apart in the bag which irritated me, but I have no idea if that was the fault of the barista. However, I also got espresso con panna and the cup was 90% whipped cream so I couldn't even get to my coffee. I had to open the top and scrape half of the whipped cream out. I guess to be fair, was a generous portion for a small upcharge.   Something else I would say about the location is it is especially uncomfortable for hanging out in. All there are are hard wooden tables, no soft couches, no space for more than 4 people. The lighting is really weird; it's hard to describe but basically one side of the place is blindingly bright and the other is dingy.

This Starbucks was alright compared to many others I've been to. Their wasn't much of a line, and my order was taken quickly. The service was a tad slow but I was in no rush. The specialty drink I got was good; however, I believe it comes with a chocolate drizzle on top and the barista did not put this on top of the whipped cream. I was a little disappointed, but the experience was good overall.
Best Starbucks around! Staff is very friendly. I've only ever done the drive thru so I can't comment about inside. Even if the line looks really long it moves super fast!

Very impressed.  I met a friend here and when we got up to the counter, I ordered just a blonde roast with soy.  Well, they were out of blonde and instead of me having to make a different choice, they made me a pour over blonde.  Delicious!  Since I had to wait, they even gave me a coupon to get my next drink at any Starbucks on them.  Great customer service!

Great store with awesome baristas!! Had a hard time figuring out a drink for a co-worker and the lovely lady ( sorry, forgot her name ) took the time to help me and explain the difference drinks they had available that matched my description. Drinks came out very fast 5 min wait? And it was pretty heavy traffic ( around 8am). Will definitely return!!!
Really great Bucks! We are there usually every Saturday morning and they are always cheerful and fast! This location has become our favorite in Nashville.

Not much to say about it, it's your typical Starbucks however with more mess ups than usual. Go there almost every morning and I'd say about 3 times a week we have problems with order mess ups and just them forgetting our order in general. That's bound to happen anywhere you go but when it comes to be an occurring thing can be pretty frustrating. They usually have a loaded staff too and considering most people have to go to work and other events, would be nice to get some better service.

What a pleasant way to start the day! The staff this morning was beyond pleasant. It is honestly the best way to start your morning when someone sings your name as they place your order, smile and say have a great day, AND they spell your name right on the cup! Thanks ladies!

I have a Starbucks gift card in my possession. I went to use the gift card this morning at this location in the drive thru. I ordered a cup of tea and an order of egg bites. When I reached the window I handed the woman my card. She said she couldn't scan it and do I have it on the phone app? No, I don't. I have it in my hand. She said she couldn't use it so I drove away without paying or getting my order because I was so infuriated she wouldn't enter the card manually. When I got to work annoyed and hangry without breakfast I pulled out my phone and entered the card into the app and the full balance was on the card. Clearly this isn't an issue of she couldn't do it. It's an issue of she wouldn't do it. Will never be back to this location. 7/25/18 - 9:45am.
Ordered online but the workers were super unhelpful and basically ignored me. Supremely rude staff. Looked at me like I was an alien for ordering an extra drink after passing past drive thru.
It's fine if you want a run of the mill starbucks item. If you want an amazing coffee look down the street for Volo.

I feel like there are nine million Starbucks in Philadelphia but this one is my favorite. This is good because its really close to my place.   The staff is really nice and pretty quick with the drink order. The best part is the big table they have in the corner and lots of power outlets! For a full time student like me, its nice to have a place in the hood to get out and study in.   The hours are kind of a drag. They close up by 9 pm most nights so if you're planning on going or staying late, call ahead to find out their schedule.

This is a clean and comfortable Starbucks location with adequate indoor seating and a decent amount of tree-shaded outdoor space. The staff, while friendly was mildly inattentive. The barista seemed a little more interested in conversing with her male co-worker that going ahead and finishing my half-done beverage. It's not like the delay hurt the flavor of my drink in any way, but it seemed mildly disrespectful of my time.

Came by this morning on May 14 around 5:30 AM, and the doors are all locked the gal looks at me and tells me drive-through only. I told her I only have a big box truck and can't make it through the drive-through. She took my order which I ordered a coffee and a Danish and she brought it to the front door for me since I could not go through the drive-through. Great customer service at a time where things are hard for everyone

When it comes to coffee shops and stands, I'm usually not much of a Starbucks type of person. It's nothing personal. The coffee is decent, but I just prefer to support local coffee shops.  On a recent mall outing, I was in the mood for some iced coffee and my choices were a long line at Cafe du Monde or no line at Starbucks. I guess I'm an easy mark: no line wins over line every time, no matter what. This isn't a full on Starbucks shop, although they offer everything you would find at a regular Starbucks. It's pretty much a coffee stand in the middle of the mall. The man working was really friendly and the iced coffee I got was good and decently priced. I'm sure an iced coffee with 2% milk was the most "normal" order he had heard all day.  The cool thing about this stand is there are plenty of sofas and chairs, in case you want to lounge around and enjoy your coffee and take a break from the rest of the mall madness.
This Starbucks is straight. Drive thru service sucks, but if you're coming in to study or chat with friends the baristas at the register are a lot nicer than the drive thru folks.

This is probably the worst service I've ever had at Starbucks.  No hello, thank you, or anything.  Just a blank look at me when I was waiting to order.  The baristas behind the scenes are busy making drinks for those in the store and also the drive through which was a steady stream on a weekday afternoon.  We waited for our drinks and there were other drinks but you could barely hear them say the name, if they even did at all.  My cup was sticky but not a big deal as I got a napkin to wipe it.  I paid $5 for a drink that was half filled with ice.  I'll have to remember that next time I want to cold drink to ask for little ice.  Only stopped in as we were in the complex to run errands!

Coming through the drive thru I began to have a very nice conversation with the barista that was working the drive thru we were making jokes and just enjoying the conversation, I jokingly said don't burn my coffee and she immediately became defensive and an awkward silence began. And when my coffee finally arrived....it was burnt

One of my favorite Starbucks.  Very roomy inside with lost of space to meet.  The staff is super friendly and the drive through is my choice for fast in and out.  The parking here is tricky and in short supply

Great location, right next to Rumba.  We came here to have a drink while waiting for Rumba to open.  As I stood in line, noticed a fly inside the cake cabinet next to the counter.  It was going to town, taking turns landing on all the cakes.  I  mentioned this to the cashier, who opened and closed the door to the cabinet several times.  He did not reach in to try to get the fly out, and the fly ended up untouched and unbothered.  The cashier walked away and started mopping the floor.  I picked up my receipt from the printer as the buffoon cashier forgot to give it to me.  However, my drink was good and satisfyingly cold.
friendly staff & clean store. Love the drive thru because it keeps the traffic moving when we want to hand around. They also have a nice outdoor patio area.

The usual as to coffee. Small selection of pastries, etc. And small sitting area.  Biggest issue I have at this location is the staff, which seems to change all the time.  The current employees do not seem to have much enthusiasm for what they do.
