Excellent baristas! A lot better service than usual. Very knowledgeable of their products and very helpful. Thanks guys!

We are there most every day of the week. The attentive staff sees us walking in and brings our drinks to us.  This shop is also great for being great citizens of the community and support many causes.
This is by far the best Starbucks in the region. The staff is friendly, quick and accurate. The outdoor seating and dog friendly atmosphere makes it fun.
Sweet staff! Long lines but it's constantly moving. Food and coffee always yum. Good location by the freeway.

I know it seems silly to write a review for a Starbucks, but my experience today was pretty terrible. Ordered a coffee frap, asked for half the usual amount of sweetener. Girl at the counter charges me for two pumps of classic. I ask why I am being charged extra for something that is already in the drink. She says the drink doesn't have any sweetener in it. I say yes it does, I know because they are always too sweet for me, which is why I ask for half the amount. She proceeds to argue with me, so I say forget I mentioned it, just get me the coffee frap. While I was waiting for my drink I looked up the ingredient list on the Starbucks website. Guess what? There's sweetener in it! I totally understand customers can be wrong, but in this case I was absolutely right. And even if I wasn't, there is not reason to be disrespectful to anyone. This chick has such a bad attitude it's insane. On top of that, the store is disgusting. Bottles all over the counter, overflowing trash, food on the ground. If you need a reminder of why you should go to your local coffee spot and not a chain, stop here.

I have been to this location several times and it was always good until today. Arriving around 1:30 I use the restroom and no soap. I wait for my daughter and this place is downright dirty. Tables all uncleaned and stuff all over the floor. So dirty I went outside. There were easily 5 staff members doing mainly drive thru with no in-house lines but no one was cleaning up. I stayed for a very long time visiting and drinking coffee. Not till 3:30 were floors swept by incoming next shift staff member. Bad impression.
Cashier issued the wrong receipt. I went back through the line and asked for the correct one. On this 2nd time, she did not say a word. At least an apology! Coffee and everything else ok.

So, I've finally ventured into this South Street during the weekend. Nice staff.  Just wish I could figure out where the trash was and that there was a little more seating. It was a little cramped once it got to be later in the day as I did my work.
Awesome service Christina is amazing Remembered my drink from last time I was in today, in fact remembered it so much that she reminded me I left something out! Loooovvvveeee great service like that!
WHOA - stopped by on the way through. This place has a deck that looks over the water. Very cool!

Standard Starbucks. The people are pretty nice. Once, someone in front of me in the Drivethru line bought $5 of my purchase with the idea that you pass it along, which I did. They said it can go for a dozen or so customers in a row sometimes.

Love the friendly people working here, and the fact that they always know what I want when they see me pull into the drive-thru.  Unfortunately, knowing what I want, and actually giving me the correct drink for 3 out of 5 days this week makes this lady not a happy camper.  Not the first time this has happened, not even the third time this has happened.  It's a weekly thing.  The manager is understanding when I call and complain, and yes, they always offer to make up for it.  And stupid me, I always go back to this one store, because it's close.  However, am going to try another store that's not quiet as convenient to see if they can actually prepare my drink correct for 5 days in a row.  That's $25.15 for the last 5 days, and I have only been able to enjoy $10 worth of it.  May never go back again, sorry.
is one of the fast drive trhru starbucks so far

This Starbucks is always busy. Expect to wait for a seat. The store is like any other Starbucks but with a few things that make it stand out a bit from the rest:  1. They hang artwork from local artist. The artwork is nice and it is for sale upon request. I like this.  2. The staff is a bit more friendly than other Starbucks I've been to. I think they might genuinely care about their jobs... Or, maybe they're just nice people.   This is a good rating from me... for Starbucks.

For the hustle and bustle of this Starbucks, the workers seem to work really well under pressure. One worker went out of their way to start a conversation with me, ask me about my day and inquire to make sure my drink was satisfactory. I simply wish there was more seating so I could spend more time there. In addition, it's a little distracting with all the Handlebar Indy and Pickled Peddler riders trailing through using the bathroom constantly! Overall, a pretty good experience.
Very quick service.   I walked inside.   I am having the chicken and artichoke sandwich warmed up.  I am drinking passionfruit lemonade tea.  Very clean.

I have to change my rating unfortunately. There's always an issue at this location lately. We ordered a mobile order the other day, and it took over 20 minutes. This time they're down 1 espresso machine. But of course! If it's not the espresso machine, the oven is broken, they're out of all teas, they're out of espresso all together... The list goes on and on with the mesa location. I miss the staff from last year!!

It's a Starbucks, so as far as Starbucks go, they are top-notch. Witty banter between coworkers entertain us customers as we wait for our beverage. Even so, the wait was short n' sweet.   I enjoyed sipping my drink at the window high-top and people watch. Lucas Oil Stadium is a stones throw away... if there was a wind, a strong wind. But seriously, I can see it from my high-top table.   Last note: I like my chai like I like my men - stong, smooth, and keeps me up. And my friends, they make a strong chai here. :o)
Come here almost everyday on the way to work! I love the quick service and fresh coffee/food. The very best part of this trip is they call me by your name everytime you walk in the door!
Friendly staff and inside seating is comfortable.  The coffee is on par as the others.  Saw a few students there hang out or work on their homework.  Decent size, not too big, not too small.

Hey, it's a Starbucks.  However, I felt compelled to review this particular location mainly because of the staff.  I have never had a poor experience here--never a wrong order, never a long wait, never a Rudie McRudester at the register.  Plus they've got a pretty good selection of pastries and lunch-type items which are my go-to when I'm short on time at work.  Also, I love Starbucks in general for the fact that I can order something "skinny", and no one laughs in my face.

Went here after dinner tonight with my sister and a friend. We all got some very yummy treats. I had the chocolate chip cookie which was quite delicious. My sister had the lemon cake. She loved it. And my friend had the banana bread. he said it was really good and he gave me a bite, it was pretty awesome.  I had a vanilla latte one of my favorite drinks there. My sister and friend had the Salted Carmel hot chocolates. They loved them.  This place is pretty nicely decorated and quite packed. It's obviously very happening little spot. The wait staff was very friendly and when I wasn't very thrilled with my first drink they made me a new one. The customer service is always quite good at Starbucks. I have been to this place a few times in the past and would definitely go there again.

B4 work, this Starbucks does brisk drive-through business on the outside, with some customers stepping into the attractive-though-smallish dining area on the inside. As my friend Bill C. notes, customer service is exceptional for a coffee place. The staff person working the counter was visiting with a regular as I arrived (but quickly took care of me), and was introducing herself by name to a newcomer who followed. Bill C. didn't seem too thrilled with the baked goods, but my apple-raisin muffin was just fine. The standard Pike Place coffee was very good, too.  Perhaps it's not as hip as E'ville Main Street, but out here at the Crossing there's better parking.

I went inside this time and there was no line at 9am on a Saturday which was amazing! Imagibr my surprise when I order exactly the same coffee that I get in Chicagoland and it's 50 cents more here. Again I'm here to help with the hangover from staying at my parents house while visiting, so I can't argue. Just wondering what is more expensive here....

I went here on Thursday and I wanted to get the Oprah Cinnamon Chai Tea Latte but they said they didn't make those but they make Chai Tea Lattes and then she asked me if I wanted a tall one for free and she said if I like it I should come back in and buy another one. It was delicious and the worker was so sweet for doing that for me.

This starbucks is deceptively bland... Until you step inside.  Its centerpiece is a large wood table (I couldn't tell if it was oak or particleboard), with strategically placed power strips underneath, so that each person at the table could potentially plug in his/her laptop.   I actually got a decent amount of studying done there...that is, until the teenie boppers showed up at 8. (a busload showed up at once, got some frozen frapuccinos, and commenced  talking & giggling uncontrollably with each other)  Shouldn't you be out at a party?
The whole point of using a personal cup is so that I'm not generating waste by getting a disposable cup.  You are Starbucks, figure out a better way to measure!!!!

Close friendly and convenient! They have a drive through and the staff is SUPER friendly and make the visit delightful each time. Coffee is consistently great and they have BREAKFAST!!!  Just wish it was a bit bigger due to the traffic when it gets busy, but it's a small complaint.

Nothing bland or regular about this Starbucks.  Starbucks drinks and treats are Starbucks, but the people that work there make it a pleasure to start your day or stop by to refuel.   A great place to have a quick meeting for work or check in with friends.

Today is double star day!! That means much needed coffee. Luckily this Starbucks location was on near by, its my favorite location for respectful employees who get my order correct on the first try and speedy service!

It's a STARBUCKS. I'm pretty sure everyone who reads these reviews already knows what to expect... but I'll write one anyways! :)  I always find this location to be extremely convenient and busy - right in the heart of touristy South Street. (If you do come here - make sure you walk one block up to see the Magic Gardens!)   The coffee's hot, the sweets are scrumptious, and the baristas have always been super friendly in my experiences. I like to bring my own coffee mug for refills, too -- you usually wind up with a much larger cuppa'joe than ordering a disposable tall, grande, or vente.

I would stop by this location more often but the parking lot design makes it frustrating. The drive thru line blocks the parking lot. The entrances are poorly designed where cars block each other in both directions. If the flow was improved they would probably ove a lot more cars through.   My other issue with this location is the consistancy of the drinks. I usually get a vanilla latte and I often wonder if I got the wrong drink.
Baristas have major attitudes and are super condescending. Slower than any Starbucks I've been too so far. Least favorite Starbucks, but the paintings outside by the drive through are awesome.

This one's in the Gloucester Township shopping center on the corner,  nice tables outside but you sit by the drive-through if the cars are backed up . They make a pretty good Cortado, which of course is not on the menu but it's basically a latte equal parts espresso and warm milk with no foam.
Great location if you're looking to grab-and-go, but not to linger as seating is limited.  The drive-thru can sometimes be a pain, so I normally go inside to place my order.

Great downtown location.  The crew is always friendly and efficient, even when very busy.  I do wish there was more seating, but it works.    The location has a drive thru and outdoor seating, so when it's not too hot or too cold you can sit outside.    I've had coffee here a number of times and it's always hot, fresh and correct.  A nice break during the work day.
Great way to start the day! Smiling baristas and delicious coffee!! Such a nice place to meet up with friends and enjoy a good cup of coffee!

This Starbucks has a lot going for it. It's super-clean, and service is fast & friendly. There's plenty of seating, incl a few outside tables on the "deck" overlooking the pond. Very nice. I know some have complained about the limited on-site parking here, but there's an easy solution, unless you are in a BIG hurry. If driving, as you pass the Starbucks and it's small parking lot on your right, go one more block, and make a left onto Penn Valley Drive. There is ample free parking on the street there and you're only a block away! If you can't walk a block to get your coffee, well hey, maybe you shouldn't be having that Vente Double Mocha Praline Snickers Bon Bon Double Whip Frappaccino, eh?

This Starbucks is located inside of Publix, which is in the same plaza as another Starbucks. Competition between the two Starbucks has been created since the opening of the one inside of Publix.   They offer everything as any other Starbucks, but with a shorter line and a shorter wait.

Typical Starbucks I guess but I must have confused the cashier with my mind bending request to transform 15 pennies into a dime and a nickel so I could use the meter. She took this baffling question with much confusion as she made her way up the mental Everest. Dumped my changed in the drawer and said have a great day. Oh jeez.

Terrible! I've stopped here for the past 8+ years and the lattes are not prepared correctly and the wait is long! Tonight zero cars in drive thru, not many inside. I waited at least 6 mins and the latte average.

Nice Starbucks, good atmosphere and not as cramped up as a lot of Starbucks I've been to. The deal breaker here was the restrooms. They were FILTHY! Dust build up on everything in there was a dead giveaway that they are not cleaned regularly, thick brown urine buildup on back of toilet.  Nasty sink. The best way to judge how well employees keep a restaurant is by the bathroom. If they can't wipe down a toilet, how well do you think they are going to clean those machines they have to fully breakdown? Those bathrooms haven't had a thorough scrubbing in at LEAST a week. Buildup as thick as what was under the toilet seat takes a while to accumulate.
FYI: Last time I was here, it was only open until11pm...although that still puts it as one of the few Starbucks that stays open past 9pm or 10pm.

This is a good Starbucks! They just opened a few months ago and I am so happy they have finally opened!   This location is located inside of the Acme in the shopping center (where the Chick-fil-a is). Since it is inside a grocery store, it doesn' have everything that a stand alone Starbucks has (some of the food items and such) but they have most and what is there is done well.  The staff here is really friendly and personable! They make it a pleasant experience coming to get my coffee. And they are pretty quick as well

STILL the slowest Starbucks on the planet.  They got my drink order wrong and then told me it was because of a new person (which is what they told me last time too)  maybe hire a few more advanced baristas?

4*/5* not bec of staff (they're 5*). The loss of star is for corporate's heavy hand in creating waste twice. 1) They don't recycle. They don't even try. Big company like SB throwing cups away by the 100's daily, per store. Do the math on that. Imagine a Costco dumping out a truckload of cups into the landfills. 2) Food is discarded per corporate BS. The expiration date on a wrapped sandwich is today, the sandwich is unsold FOR FULL VALUE, then its trashed. No 1/2 price last hour. No "buy $3+ coffee and get free sandwich". No "receipt lottery" 1 hour before close (name on receipt, goes into hat, pull the winner) No donation to shelter or company. They can't even wrap it in a separate trash bag and leave it next to trash pile.  Compared to Coffee Bean in California that announced, 15 mins to close, that the following sandwiches have today's expiration date and anyone who wishes to take them may do so. They were good sandwiches. Not $6 ones but $3.50 ones.  Starbucks is so corporate that they waste stuff left and right. The staff is usually as wooden as Pinocchio- this location was different (dude with full beard knows his stuff and is chiller than a morgue corpse)  Recycle. Donate/raffle/secondarily discard the day old food.  SB is the Walmart of coffee. Too big for their own good.

Seriously these Starbucks employees must be on speed, or they just have the best organization system ever. This location can be a pain to actually drive to seeing as how they have been doing construction on Meridian for about 3 years now, but once you are there the drive thru line moves like a breeze, especially in the early morning work rush. There has been a time or two in the afternoons where the line can back up, but on those times Starbucks recognized the delay and covered the cost of my drink! This is also a prime location to catch the "Pay it Forward" train where the car in front of you paid for your drink so you pay for the car behind you. The patio also has a good mix of tables and cushioned chairs so you will be sure to find whatever type of seat your behind is beckoning.

It's usually pretty good here, good service & coffee. This is my Sat. morning hang-out, and the last 3 Saturdays have been less than paar, I'm sorry to say. They have a new barista who needed reminding of what I ordered, didn't greet me, no "thank you," and barely said anything at all. I found her potentially rude.

Oh how I usually love everything about Starbucks but last night this stop was a little off! I ordered 3 drinks both all of which they either got the drink wrong or the size.. They even asked what I ordered again and I told them and they didn't fix it! I gave up at that point! The coffee was delicious as always and I have stopped at this buck before and it was great. But last night something was a little off! Also the bathroom could have used some attention, but I'm happy they had a co-ed bathroom with baby changing station..  I will give them another chance but kinda a bummer.
Nothing warms you on a cold dreary day like a vanilla latte. Love this Starbucks!  Always busy but always great!

Right on Main Street in New Hope.  Nice outside seating, although your view is of the intersection.  Quick service with a smile and the typical Starbucks quality.  No parking at the store since it on Main Street so don't think you are going to just pull into the parking lot and run in.

Located just inside the Tar'gét store. This Starbucks is just what you need when looking for a caffeine fix during a toiletry reload day. It has plenty of seating which overlooks apparel, trinkets, the front entrance doors and cart hub. My barista Alicia was super nice and crafted my drink to perfection.   Starbucks Reward cards are accepted, pastries on deck, lots of ceramic mugs, travel tumblers, bags of coffee and more.  ~Christy

Was this place always this bad...and I never noticed it?   I have no choice but to come to this Starbucks since it is 5 minutes from work...which SUCKS. Basically, all the old workers are no longer there anymore and I consistently see new faces every other week...which is not good. I understand that it can be overwhelming when it gets busy but it does not give the workers an excuse to be throwing everything around...I just wanted an ice coffee...that's all and it took forever for me to get it even placing the order 10 minutes ahead. It is super disappointing because I love Starbucks and more and more this place is getting worst. I guess there is a reason why i always see new faces here and I feel like the management needs to retrain the workers here because they always seemed angry or overwhelmed.  I cannot wait for the DD's construction is over so I can start going there instead.
The staff here is always friendly, I use the mobile app and they always have my drink ready and already know who I am and have it up on the bar for me.

I have been to this Starbucks several times since it has opened and am really pleased by the friendly staff and the coffee. I wouldn't recommend always going into the drive-thru since it's packed often with cars.   Therefore, I will share a small secret with you....   behind the Starbucks building is more parking (that's where I go). You can park your car, walk inside, order and be out faster than those waiting in the drive-thru. I have saved so much time doing this. I'm serious! I get my Iced White Chocolate Mocha (and a sugar treat) far faster than those waiting in line in their car. It saves me serious time.  I think the staff have a good multi-tasking system, but I have never had to wait at the counter in-store to have my order taken and it only took the barista less than two minutes to have my drink ready for me. Each Starbucks is different, I have been to other locations where it was ok waiting in the drive-thru.   Plus, there is very comfy seating outdoors (like a super large couch). My bum doesn't always like sitting in hard metal chairs so the outdoor couch is a nice change.

I am a huge fan of Starbucks! It's definitely helpful to have a cup of coffee or tea to start the morning off but the staff at this location are really rude.   I've been very patient with them but its not great to deal with impolite people (especially staff workers) when you are looking for a pleasurable day.

I agree the service is slow as the drive through seems really busy. But the staff are friendly and apologized both days I visited for the delay.   It has a very large interior seating area, decent outside seating with umbrellas, and two club chair seating areas.   Lots of parking and the noise level is average.   The bathrooms were clean when I visited

Perhaps im spoiled coming from Seattle where Starbucks are almost always excellent, but this place was consistently awful.After about 2 months of orders being botched, and blank stares when it was pointed out to the staff, we found a good Starbucks on Broadway. My favorite here was being told "we ran out of bagels.thats why we didnt bring it out to you". Oh i see.I was just expected to pay for it:.No apology.  AVOID THIS PLACE .

Hooray for a Starbucks with a drive-thru! This one is located right next to a gas station, right off the interstate, so it's convenient. The drive-thru line moved very quickly, and my order was correct. What more can you ask for?!

I think hard before giving any business less than 4 stars during Covid-19 even chains. I certainly do not like to complain during these tough times. Again, I don't want to give any less than 4 stars unless the food/drink isn't good and it's pricey.   I was so happy I can still get my veins pumped with coffee. I fell in love with a Caramel Macchiato that included .5 pump Caramel & light ice. The Caramel syrup is a nice touch in my opinion. The drink also includes vanilla syrup, too.   When I went through the driveway, the person taking my order was patient as I deliberated between a coffee or a venti tea. I went with coffee because I needed the caffeine boost. When I went to pay, it was a seamless process. I use the app. To rack up points to redeem for the future. They also give away a FREE drink during your Bday, so I redeemed mine today. A couple days ago, a patron paid for my drink at a different Starbs location, so THANK YOU!   The Barista was wearing a mask to protect herself and others at this time - very commendable! If you dine inside, stay 6 ft. apart.  Thank you so much, Starbs, for always being there for me during these really, really hard times. I looove you!   XOXO,  Joy X.

I always forget this starbucks is there.  There is always seating when you go at the right time of the day.  I get my hair done at circa 79 so it's a great location to grab something sweet before getting beautified!

The customer service at this location is subpar. They don't reinforce the mask policy and they run out of a lot of ingredients and don't offer any substitutions for a drink you might order because of their own inventory issues. I have finally had it with this location. Save yourself the time and go to the other location about 2 miles up the road for a better experience.
Typical 5bucks with nice outdoor seating (only if it didn't smell like crap and if it it weren't hella hot outside).
It's a Starbucks by name not quality, all the food prices in the case are covered so you don't know how much they are charging for the food

This place is a really great location for my work commute and I look to make appointments in this area. It is right off highway 69. It is a bit out of the way to get to the location, but not bad. Wifi is reliable. There was no line to order and my drink was made quick. One con that I think is directly tied to the location is that it was packed. Had to wait for a spot to sit and I was asked about my table when I was going to use restroom and again as I was leaving.

The service was fine it was the lack of smiles or eye  contact that causes me to give it only three stars.   My only order was a chai Tea latte and a slice of pound cake. The product itself was fine and tasty.

Not bad for a Starbucks, the inside is always clean and the baristas are friendly. The drive thru on the side of the building is a bit precarious to navigate, however, especially if you drive a larger vehicle. There are plenty of scrape marks on the high curb to remind you of all the cars before you who did not heed the severeness of the rather sharp turn (probably close to 90 degrees!).

I don't know why, but years of not liking Starbucks and now I'm hooked. Still not a coffee fan but I love their black shaken iced tea. There is something about it that is SO yummy! I get a venti and it lasts me til lunch at work. It's a little caffeine and hydration. This location's parking lot sucks because it gets congested by the drive thru line. You can park in the lot of the shopping center and walk without having to worry about the line. The line goes REALLY quickly so I usually use it. I am there early if I go, around 7am, so lines aren't as long as closer to 8. I don't get food there often, but their vanilla bean scones are really good and the only thing I've tried.   Staff are friendly and quick. I go often! Just wish it was a little less $$.

I wanted to leave an excellent review of the wonderful service I received from the employee Jody. (Sorry if I spell your name wrong!) I've been to many Starbucks but he put in more effort than any sales associate I've made contact with at other Starbucks. He made the experience fun and enjoyable. He asked if it was okay to surprise me with a drink, and I ended up loving it! It was an iced soy milk Chestnut Praline. I loved the surprise. This is something that makes people regulars. Thanks again, I'll be back!

Having been here quite a few times, I suppose it's time for a review.   I can't say enough good things about the staff. I witnessed the longest line known to man this morning and the staff handled it cheerfully. There were no signs of stress, just the notion that they work as a well-oiled machine.   I never had an order come out wrong, but I did knock off a star for the parking lot. It's pretty rough, especially in the rush hour where everyone is trying to get their caffeine fix.

I go here all the time as it is right near my work. Christina, Seth, Dante. . . the entire staff is awesome and more than happily indulge my indecisiveness when I want to try something different.   Yay!

I love Starbucks and one with a drive thru. There were 4 cars in front of me and they went by very quickly. Less than 5 minutes in the drive thru and the employee was super nice. I order the egg white bites and they were delicious. Whoever though to use the label to help hold down the spoon is a genius. I don't like having lots of loose things in my hands and that was innovative to me which I appreciate. Thank you!

Staff was very kind and respectful. The only thing is, that the drink I got I barley got any liquid. Thats my fault I guess.. Either way this place is pretty good and I can tell they care for their costumers that come through to get something delicious.  Honestly I would come here again thank you!!

My sister and I are frequent Starbucks customers. This locations customer service was beyond mediocre. We walked in and none of the store associates greeted us or asked us if we needed assistance. We waited about 5 minutes then asked if someone could help. Starbucks should be a company that trains all associates on high caliber customer service.
Grumpy employees, poorly made coffee, got a cookie for my son and for some reason it was frozen so it was gross and not edible. Dunkin is cheaper and a better experience.

Wow...this is my first experience with an unfriendly Starbucks. I was given the wrong order going through drive thru. I went in to get it corrected. I explained the problem and was told that it was rung up incorrectly and was given no apology. They gave me my corrected drink and I was told to go stand in line to get my refund if I wanted to. She took my receipt, and as the last person went through, I was told by "D" that she would get to my refund in a moment. After 3 times of saying that to me, I said that I had waited moments, and she asked what my problem was. I was finally given the difference owed to me.  Starbucks is usually the top in customer service. This location fails with me.
Expensive pricing, mediocre attitude of staff. Not on par with Sheraton standards, yet it sits in the lobby.

I was a regular here... Maybe 150 venti non-fat latte's in the last year and frequent tips.  In recent weeks, service has been horrible.  This morning, I was the only person waiting for service and waited for ten minutes until I finally walked out.  There were 8 baristas, some doing nothing and some chatting with each other, near the check out and ovens.  I walked outside and only two cars were waiting for service.  Longer term baristas are great, but clearly something has changed as things back up when even a little busy.  Shame.  I will try Kahwa.

What's Joe Pesci's line from Lethal Weapon, "you get ****ed at the drive-thru?!" Needed a lift to make it home on my daily commute between St Pete and Orlando, so I stopped at the exit 19 Starbucks and ordered a chai tea latte.  Two exits west on I4 I went for my first sip and it was coffee!! I hate coffee! Joe Pesci was right, I wasn't turning around, so they got me.
Slow and slightly rude. I usually go out of my way to go to the other nearby locations. This is one of the slowest Starbucks I've been to.

Short version: This Starbucks is terrible. You're better off finding a different location.   Long version: I never thought I'd have to write a negative review on a simple iced tea order, but here it is.   I sat thru the drive-thru and ordered a black iced tea with no water or sweetener. Got the drink, drove off and realized I got an Arnold Palmer. Yeah it's annoying, but mistakes happen.   I drove back, got in line and told the person up front I received the wrong drink in the drive thru. Instead of just making me a new one, they decided to get the gal who originally made my drink to come help me. I realize now that it was at this time that I entered THE TWILIGHT ZONE.   She remembered me from the drive they and asked why I came back. I let her know I that I just wanted a black iced tea and not a tea mixed with lemonade. She seemed annoyed with my request and then started grabbing a bunch of different items and asked I wanted lemon in the tea again??? No, black tea only please.  I was patient with her through this whole process, but this barista seemed pretty frustrated with me for not being an Arnold Palmer fan. She was slamming stuff around. When she finally served up my drink (which really as simple as pouring pre-made tea on ice) she mumbled: "Have a nice day yesterday" and quickly started to walk off. I quickly launched back with "Excuse me? Did you really wish me a good day YESTERDAY?   Really, I was more curious as to how what she said could be construed as an insult, but alas she pretended like she never said it. Whatever, I had my tea finally. I took a big victory sip and tasted the leftover chai spice from the dirty container they obviously reused for the black tea.   I knew there was no hope in getting it right at that point, and just fled the battlefield uncaffeinated, but fully inspired to write this long post.   Basis for my rating: I think taking 4 stars off for screwing up my order, having a garbage attitude and not using clean containers is fair.

This has got to be the best Starbucks. Let me start by saying that Amanda and Kyle make my experience feel like no other. When I walk in I'm not only greated with a smile but they know me by name and what I drink, it reminds me so much like the tv show "Cheers" when I walk in there. The service is top notch.  Take notice of these employees, they are what makes me drive past other starrbucks locations, because there is a closer a Starbucks but I choose to go here for their hospitality. They don't have a drIve-thru, but when the employees treat you like this, you wouldn't want one.
I love this Starbucks.  They always get my order right, and they remember me when I come in.  Great place to hang out too if you need to catch up on some business or socialize.

Another pocket Starbucks, similar in size to Church street Starbucks downtown. But here they offer acres of free parking. The greatest mix of local yuppies and visitors this side of Cool Springs comes here for caffeine energy to drop loads of loot shopping here at the most upscale mall in the great state of Tennessee. If the limited seating is full consider getting your coffee at the big Panera Bread, also inside the Green Hills Mall.

Had a bad experience at this Starbucks. Made a mobile order from the app and chose the location. It took my money. We get to the front only to find out that this location does not except mobile orders. It sent it to the next Starbucks.  The barista said to call the other location and cancel my order and get a refund.   After ringing a very long time with no answer I hung up and called back.  More waiting but  when the gentleman answered he said he couldn't cancel my order.   They have no control over mobile orders.  Now I have to take my time to call the corporate office and try to get my money credited back.   They should not have locations listed on the app that don't accept mobile orders!!! What a mixed up way of doing business!!  Actually not this locations fault I guess , but they knew it was an issue and has been and haven't corrected it !!

Worst Starbucks ever. I waited for almost 10 minutes after ordering on drive thru. Employee did not even apologize for the delay and then completely forgot about offering me napkins, sugar and a straw. I needed to ask her for it.

When we take the motorcycle out for an early Sunday morning tour of the Tucson Mountains, we love to end the trip here for breakfast so we can sit out by the bike and enjoy.  The staff, as at all Starbuck's, are friendly and the food is quick and good.  Thumbs up!

This Starbucks is in a cute little part of Webster Groves next to Gazebo Park, where events and farmers markets are held in summer. It's just like any other Starbucks but this is right next to my work so I go there all the time, usually around 2:30-3pm when I hit the afternoon slump. The staff are friendly and always prompt with my order, and they rarely mess it up.  My go-to drinks are the white chocolate mocha with non-fat milk and no whipped cream, as well as the java chip frappacino, skinny and without whipped cream.  The clientele at this location are a mixture of business people, hippies, young teens (Nerinx Hall, an all-girls high school is just around the corner), university students (Webster University is within walking distance), artists, entrepreneurs, and stay-at-home moms. You really get a mixed bag here.  I typically don't sit and stay to drink my lattes, but when I have in the past, the furniture is comfy, with a variety of seating choices for individuals or small groups, and the place is kept clean and orderly. It doesn't wow my pants off, but it maintains the high standard of Starbucks that I expect. A solid standby.

It's difficult to be fair when discussing Starbucks!  The name is so polorizing.  True coffee lovers, people who really know and love coffee, who believe, correctly or not that Italy is the only country in the world where you can't get a bad cup.  Who only buy their beans at LeColombe or have them Fed-Xed from David Schermer in Seattle, who have a definite ratio in their heads of what % robusto and what % arabica makes the best brew. These people hate Starbucks.  They vilify their "baristas", they rail against their penchant for over roasting, their fascination for stupid names, their insane list of combinations and their have-a-nice-day-robotic-Stepford staff.    The rest of us are thankful that Starbucks, regardless of how they did it, raised the bar on coffee excellence in this country.  No one can deny that before them, 98% of the coffee served in America was swill.  Now, as a direct result of the marketing juggernaut they have become, even WaWa serves coffee that is better than good restaurants served less than 15 years ago.  OK, they still have to work on getting rid of the flavors, but it's a valid point.  For those of us in Philly, we don't have to go to a Starbucks so long as we can get to, Anthony's on Ninth Street, Le Colombe on Rittenhouse Square and Manayunk, Cafe Fulya on Second Street, The Last Drop on Pine, Ants Pants on South, Philadelphia Java Company on Fourth and on Second, Fitzwater Cafe on Seventh & Fitz, Saxby's in Haverford and a few others that I know when I drive by, and certainly Ray's on Ninth for if there is cleaner coffee making apparatus in the city, I'd like to see it,  But in a pinch, with my daily schedule sometime denying me access to these quality coffee houses, I get a short one at the local SB fully understanding that the good houses got pinched into action by that audacious company out of Seattle.  Love them or hate them, we, the coffee drinkers have benefited by them.

This Starbucks (about a block and a half away from another Starbucks--one of the many charms of living in a metropolis) is offers consistently  great customer service and a friendly, attractive staff.  And who doesn't appreciate a hot barista (though most don't stick around long).    An added bonus of this Starbucks?  It's the only one in the city located in a super-luxury condo building.  Personally, I like to sit in a comfy chair, sitting on my Egg Nog latte and pretending that I live upstairs.

After the unpleasant visit to the Starbucks on Whyte Ave, I felt the need to head to a spot that I know delivers.  Imagine my surprise when I discovered that this one wasn't listed on Yelp.  So, in addition to a coffee, I get to write a first review.  Yay me.  Location wise, nothing spectacular here.  It's in the middle of a retail park, so not a lot of foot traffic to watch and make fun of.  Yes, I'm one of those people.  It is, however, clean, the staff are friendly and competent, and they have pictures of some of the local police squads on the walls.  Any place that shows such obvious support for law enforcement gets my business.  It's also close to Best Buy, so once I get my techno-geek fix, I can also get my caffeine fix.  One stop shopping for addictions.  Can't go wrong there.
I love this Starbucks...efficient drive thru, great service, friendly people, delicious coffee what more can I say? fabulous!
This new crew although very friendly and trying hard, are a little disorganized and frequently out of products which doesn't make sense since there is a distribution center 45 minutes away
Great location. Friendly people. They remember me and my family. Even what drinks we like. That's awesome.
I went into this Starbucks recently and was impressed by the friendly staff and quick service! Definitely my favorite Starbucks location in town!

EXCELLENT service! Whenever I come in- I'm greeted my name! Delicious beverages- always made perfectly. AND, the beverages are always made really fast- everyone there sees my work t-shirt and understands that I've got to get back, and I've never even had to say a word! So considerate of them! I am never given a hard time for not knowing what the cup sizes are called- or the fact that I can't pronounce all the beverage names! Your refreshers are delicious, and the bagel I once had was so yummy! Perfect texture, with REAL butter that was in its prime! GO STARBUCKS ON CENTRAL! I hope you keep prospering amidst this pandemic.
Worker named Jeremy was very rude. Worst Starbucks experience I've ever had. Confused as to how someone can act that way at their place of work at get away with it?! Come on Starbucks.

Ehh, this starbucks is ok. I came here mid afternoon on a Saturday and the place was packed. Waited in line for at least 5 minutes to order and then another 5 for my drink. Luckily, my drink was prepared well and correctly. The service was average, nothing to complain about but nothing spectacular. The barista making the drinks was disheveled in appearance and had liquid all over the front of his apron. Granted, I get that spills happen but his apron was gross, he should have changed to another one if he was going to continue making drinks. Overall, average experience. This place seems to have pretty good reviews so perhaps I caught it at a bad time.

This Starbucks sucks. The customer service is horrible, they all act as if they don't want to be at work, and seem unhappy there. The first time I went, I ordered a small Carmel macchiato with an extra shot. When I received my drink it was a bit watery. I let that one slide and didn't think much of it. However, second time around I ordered the same exact thing but a medium instead. It tasted horrible. Again, it was so watery and had no flavor. I was so disappointed because I love Starbucks. I will not be returning here again. Save your money and go to a different Starbucks.

Sometimes you need a little bit of generic basic bitch things in your life. This place is one of those places for me. My favorite Starbucks in the city. Everyone is friendly ( usuallyI only go in the mornings, the late staff looks miserable at times but I understand).

This location does quite a good job. I have gone inside as well as through the drive thru. The drive through can take a bit of time when it's busy, but if there is a line up and you're short on time, park and go inside.  Service is always fast inside, but parking can be an issue every now and again due to limited spots.  I usually get myself a soy vanilla latte, sometimes I get an iced one. They are usually great but every now and again I get one with burned espresso- meaning the shot has been out over 30 seconds before being put into the syrup or dumped into steamed milk/soy/almond etc. If you go back and tell them, they will of course make you another.  The interior is that of all Starbucks, modern, clean and has a good amount of seating.  The staff is always friendly and helpful, they keep their goodies well stocked and aren't usually out of too much.  This is a good location.  Enjoy.

I'm just confused on if this was the standard way to make this drink? Every matcha latte I've gotten has always been mixed? This is seriously milk with powder setting on top. I understand if you're busy and I'm sure there is a drink set up that is company standard across the board but this was really disappointing seeing and having another customer complain about her drink not being right either. Maybe I've just been spoiled with other baristas who don't even know me and they mix my ingredients together??

The next time I think it'll be fun to watch a scary movie, I'll think back to last night when I stayed awake until 5am, anxiously waiting to either be dragged out of my bed by evil paranormal forces, or fall into a possessed state and kill my dog, my neighbors, and ultimately myself. Finally, when fatigue overwhelmed fear, I slept, but woke up only an hour later to the sound of my dog's muffled dream-barks.   Thankfully, I knew holy humans with warm, reviving beverages were a mere 2 blocks away. I ventured out into the crisp morning air and despite the darkness at dawn, I thought it better to be mugged than remain captive to my racing imagination.   One cup of coffee later, I returned to my doomsday domicile, curled up on the couch and thumbed through my roommate's coffee table literature: a photography book aptly titled, "Women Before 10am." Better dreams next time. Avoid "Paranormal Activity;" it will haunt you!

Old favorite spot to get coffee. Debey has been rude to me on 3 occasions, making me never want to go back. Once she ignored me as I waited for her to never look back at me after handing me a half full cup of coffee in the size I ordered. Another time I asked for a drink to be split, never was a problem in the last 2 years for this, but she acted suddenly angry I asked and gave me a small hot cup and no lid for an iced drink. I waited for a lid  but she ignored me then also. I am tired of feeling this rude person's passive aggression. I wonder how many other have fallen victim to this. She should not work with the public
Great atmosphere at this location. Unfortunately, my chocolate croissant was soggy. But I recommend the bacon Gouda breakfast sandwich! Mobile ordering is great.
Ever wait in cue at drive-thru for twelve minutes...and you're the only car?  Why would they need iced tea in 87° weather?  At RUSH HOUR??!?!     You just never know!

This is my least favorite location in Tucson and I've been to them all! The staff here is unprofessional and slow... Currently I've been standing here for FIFTEEN minutes waiting on a breakfast sandwich. That's not counting the ten I waited for my six drinks. They keep asking if my order is complete and I keep telling them I need my food! Some of the employees are hustling and a few of the staff are clearly trying to hold this place together.   I would strongly recommend going to the Grantand Swan location where everything runs smoothly, they are professional, and are able to get drink and food orders out in a timely manner.

Really love this Starbucks! The employees here are super nice and I have always had really quick service. I also love the fact that they have a drive thru (YAAAY laziness!!). All around a solid starbucks and a staple for my caffeine fix
Have been going here for a decade but staff keeps changing and not for the better. No longer a friendly place  have moved on to other coffee shops

Just the same as any starbucks. Local one in downtown.  Often hard to find seating as this place is packed...especially outside when it is good weather.  Nice, friendly service and often fairly fast.  A nice place to stop for coffee when downtown.
Still...this location is one of the most unfriendly around.  I also didn't receive what I ordered.  I will not be coming back until they get their $h*t together.
Ten minutes in drive thru.  Second car in line.  Eight minutes were AFTER I paid.  WTF!

I just waited 10 minutes for my mobile order to be completed. This is after placing my order 10 minutes prior to walking in the door. It took 20 minutes to make my drinks, from the time that I placed the order to the time that I walked out of the Starbucks.  Let me reiterate, it was a mobile order. When I arrived there was no one in line, the baristas were making two drinks. There were two baristas and a manager. The manager finally asked what I was waiting for and I told him that I was waiting for a mobile order. He took the mobile order from the printer and put the stickers on 2 cups and told the baristas that they needed to be made right away. The baristas were making drinks, the manager was wiping off the counter. The cups sat there and the manager continued to fiddle with other stuff behind the counter. Here's my question. Why wouldn't the manager just step in and make the mobile order. BTW the mobile order consisted of two iced teas. Not brain science. And the barista finally picked  up one of the ice tea cups, looked at the sticker and began to make the ice tea. Whatever he did, he did incorrectly, because he then threw that drink into the sink and began again. He finished that drink and then picked up the other cup. There was no immediacy in any of this. If this is the worst thing that happens to me today, I am happy, however the reason for placing a mobile order is to have the drinks ready when you arrive. If the Starbucks have been swamped with people, I would understand. If the manager had stepped in to help, I would be a little more forgiving. But all three of the employees seemed to be inept at accomplishing the goal of getting to teas onto the counter. And I am writing this review, because this is not the first time that this has happened. If any corporate Starbucks people are listening, it would be great if there were an alarm of some sort, a bell, a beep, buzz, that would allow the employees at the Starbucks to know that there is a mobile order waiting. Just a thought.

I have been to this Starbucks about five times. Each time I've been here it has been an extremely frustrating experience.  I tend to go through the drive-thru because I am a graduate student and I am always in a hurry to get to my next place. However, whenever I am in the drive-thru there is always an excessively long line and the drinks always take a long time to make. Obviously, the person who frequents the drive-thru tends to be in a hurry. But, instead of making my drink quickly and efficiently it has been made incorrectly about ninety percent of the time and takes about ten times longer than it should. The first time it happened, I thought it's just the drink, it's semi-complicated, I get it. The next time I chose something as basic as a Iced Caramel Macchiato. It looked like the correct item so I was thrilled, but it tasted awful and still took ten minutes to receive it.  I know how tough working in the service industry is, I work it in, but when I timidly asked them if they could remake my drink or brought attention to the fact it wasn't what I ordered they failed to take responsibility for the matter and made me feel horrible for bringing attention to it. Customer service should be high on the priority list for a well-known coffee place, but instead it was more than lacking.  I vowed this morning that I will no longer be frequenting to this location. Save your money and go to the Central West End location, they always have a good attitude and they are coffee connoisseurs.

First time I came here, I had just dropped my cat off for surgery. Went to get some coffee for the nerves. An older gentlemen who worked there could tell I was out of it and asked. Very friendly staff and great service. Love this Starbucks and I frequent it regularly. :)

This Starbucks is truly amazing! The staff is extremely welcoming and kind. If a mistake is made, they are very quick to fix it and more than willing to help. They always have a great attitude and have smiles on their faces that you can tell are there, even through the masks. The dining area is clean and organized and quiet. The drive through is quick and easy to manage, even in the mornings when the line is wrapped around the parking lot! I can't recommend this store enough.

Im happy to find this Starbucks as an alternative to finding parking to go into a Clayton location. I went through the drive thru in the middle of the afternoon with only one car in front of me. It was a little slow but not terribly bad. I got a Venti iced matcha latte my go to afternoon drink. When I got it it wasn't filled to the top and had too much ice. This happens sometimes with the shaken teas but it's nice when the baristas are aware of it and make sure the drink is full for the $5+ you pay for it. Overall it wasn't bad I'd definitely use it instead of Clayton.

Your typical Starbucks! Neither bad nor great. Glad it's here though. I always need a coffee on the drive back to Nashville! Convenient location. And, even though people forget, just because they have a drive thru doesn't mean you have to use it! They still have an inside (which is a lot faster)! I'm feeling (and sounding) snarky today, so I'm going to end this one here.

Okay, so I get that I'm a Washingtonian, and I'm probably a coffee snob. Being that I live in the Starbucks capital of the US, I find it annoying when I go to a Starbucks and they do something extra stupid. Like allow some spoiled rotten teenager tell me they don't brew any "new" (wait for it) COFFEE after 5pm. Uh... really? To my confusion, he responds by standing there, like I'm the dodo head, and he's following corporate policy or something. Frustrating!

This is a cute location as it's right across from Monument Circle, very kind staff and quickly prepared my order as well. I love walking to this location in the morning, getting a cup of coffee in continuing to walk the rest of the city. I highly suggest stopping at this location if you are visiting in town!

Decent Starbucks. Very small parking lot, maybe 5-6 spots. Behind it is a huge Lake & in the winter when it's frozen, it turns into an ice rink. Kids and adults alike, play hockey and twirl around from morning till sunset. They all pile into Starbucks to warm up after, so it's always busy during the winter. There's about 10-12 tables/chairs down one side of the store. There are four leather club chairs by the front window which are a coveted spot. Across the front there's a counter top with some stools. Two big, clean bathrooms in the back, with baby changing stations. The place was clean. My particular barista couldn't have been nicer! There's also additional seating out back, overlooking the scenic lake for the warmer months. The only thing missing is a fireplace. The Newtown location has one, which makes it my favorite location. But, they closed today 1/7/18 for 3 weeks of renovations!! Ahh!! Now, I have to get my caffeine fix someplace else till they reopen. All in all, this location is nice, clean & mobile service is quick.

The barista at this @Starbucks location who's name is Steven was very nice and patient with me when I was attempting to use my 'Birthday Rewards' via the Starbucks app on my iPhone.  I wasn't really familiar with the app and Steven helped to familiarize me with it in addition to explaining to me the benefits of the rewards program.  Gold star for Steven!
This place seriously takes forever. I've been to Starbucks all over the USA and this one is the slowest! They take 15-20 mins in the drive thru when there's only two cars! Workers are nice
Made the trek because it said they were open for another hour online but they were already locked up and mopping when we got there

This is truly the slowest Starbucks that I have ever been to in my life. Every time that I go there, which is has been a total of about four times I spend no less than 15 to 20 minutes at the location just to get my drink to go. On one instance I pulled up and saw that the drive-through line was too long so I decided to go inside to place my order. I was able to order my drink and then proceeded to wait for my order to be made. I spent 15 minutes waiting for an iced chai latte. I had to get back in line and asked the employee where my order was just to find out that they forgot my order. After that it took about five more minutes for me to get my drink. I went to my car and tasted my beverage and of course it wasn't made properly. I did not feel like going back inside and I noticed the drive-through was significantly shorter so I decided to hop in line. Once more it took me 15 minutes just to get to the intercom. At the intercom the employee gave me a hard time about not liking my beverage (I typically get the same things Sinai know how it's supposed to taste). Once I got my new drink I tasted it and again it wasn't made well. I did not feel like going through the process to get them to remake it again so I just left. I'm not sure what the issue is but every time I go it takes so long in my order is never correct.I would also like to add that It is in a terrible location. The drive-through line cuts in a parking lot that has one or two other stores in the same little strip. So if the line gets too long and let's say you're trying to go to another place you'll have to wait until the line moves so you'll be able to back up your car to leave it's actually ridiculous.I love to get a drink whenever I go to Elmwood to shop but I've made it a point to either not get anything or to go to another location just so I won't have to go to this one.

[465]  This busy corner Starbucks always prepares to order.  It's hard to get in /out due to the super busy parking lot.   No drive thru.   They really need double the size lot.    We go when it's not busy ..  I'll definitely be back.

One of the most efficient Starbucks I have been to. Not to mention the baristas are amazing, and really seem to love making conversation with their customers, which makes us feel more important! If Ryann or Logan is working, they will 110% make your day.

I frequently visit this Starbucks location and the establishment is always clean, shelves orderly, and the staff is extremely nice.   Today when I went into Starbucks with my husband, the staff went out of their way to make sure everything was great. My husband ordered his usual and I ordered a water and a white mocha.   He received his coffee and my water  pretty quickly and we sat down waiting for my mocha. We waited for a few minutes and when he went to check on the status, an employee had already made it and placed it on the counter and someone picked it up.   Realizing this, they apologized (totally wasn't their fault) and quickly made me another. I really appreciated the fact that they immediately made me another drink and apologized- even though the apology wasn't necessary.

I have been to this location several times over the last few years. I have had excellent service and poor unfriendly sevice. It depends on who is working as with all place.   My most recent experience as usual there is a long line in the drive through, but it moves fairly quickly. Once I placed my simple order of a venti White Mocha, it was just a few mintues before I was at the window. My order was ready, hot, and handed to me. I loaded more money to my gold card, signed the receipt and was on my way.   The window attendent was friendly and quick. I normally go to the drive-thru location on 96th because it is less crowded, but it is usually more traffic issues to get their.
The place is dirty and small. Too bad for the most important plaza in Indianapolis. The cashier was ill-mannered. She didn't say a word to me at all.
This place has the best staff of ANY Starbucks anywhere in the world (and I've visited plenty!) Kudos to Janet and the rest of the AM crew - she might be the world's friendliest barista!

Has a good sized sitting area to sit and relax. Management could have thought about increasing the area in front of the counter when they last renovated the place. It can get really tight when a line starts to form. I noticed that the baristas are not that quick in preparing orders. So expect a longer wait compared to other Starbucks.

My opinion might be slightly biased because waaaaayyyyy back in the day I worked at this location. Although, the fact that I am now a customer should speak to the quality of this store. Overall this store is clean and fairly efficient. Sometimes they can be uber sloooww in the mornings, which is why I marked them down a star.  On more than one occasion I had to wait quite a bit of time. Fortunately, I went in today around 7a and everything was easy breezy. Most of the baristas are fairly friendly and are always ready with a drink recommendation. One thing I like about this store is that they value their customers and make an effort to learn something about you especially if you are a regular. If your in the area, you should definitely stop by!

Conveniently located for some people. Contains its regular crowd and occasional monkeys. That's right,  I saw some lady with a monkey, just looking around and exploring. So much for "No more monkey business".  Employees are skillful and nice, unless you come in 5 min before closing, then they just might be skillful.

I visited this particular Starbucks on my way home one Sunday evening. I walked up and proceeded to order a drink (one of 3) because i was placing the orders on behalf of people that were not present. So naturally i had questions. The barista that fixed my drinks had an immediate attitude towards me. I asked her if the drink i was ordering was a 'hot' beverage or a 'cold' beverage, and she responded with a ( terrible condescending tone) it's 'blended.' I was very confused and asked her again, 'is it a hot drink or a cold drink.' Her response and tone did NOT change. I ordered the drinks, paid and sat down while i waited for my drinks. Alexis stood by her register when she should've been making my drinks, because ultimately that's what i paid for, correct? Shortly after that, two customers walked in and i know based on their interactions that she knew them. She proceeded to have side conversations while i waited for my drinks. Finally, when she 'got around to doing her job' she decided to make one of their drinks first ( which need i remind you they walked in after) while i waited. I was irritated. She sloppily, and angrily made all three drinks all while having a conversation about something totally unrelated. On my way home i decided to call the store. Alexis picked up and well i asked to speak to the manager. And guess what? She said 'well, im the manager. How can i help you?' Long story short, i told her my thoughts, she hung up on me and i rang corporate. Her only defense was that she said, 'well, the reason i made you wait was because that's how i was trained.' Take away her title and give that job to someone more appreciative, deserving, less condescending and more geared towards customer service. Pathetic customer service! I hope she gets reprimanded!

Normally I don't have a problem with this location but today the young lady on the window delayed the line by talking and petting a dog! If you talk that's ok but when you handle food, especially my food, don't touch. To add to the problem she called another worker over and she petted the dog too! I don't know what happened after that because I got out of the line. I hope they washed their hands but I doubt it.
Great location, but the lines are long and staff are less than friendly. My drink tasted different than normal.... Not entirely sure why. Otherwise, just a standard Starbucks experience.

This Starbucks blows.  2 stars because it has a drive-thru.  They mess up my order constantly and it isn't a complicated order.  It's 4 shots of espresso, ice and water.  The Starbucks on Westshore and Boy Scout is the gold standard of drive-thru Starbucks.  If you are crossing the bridge to Tampa anyway on the way to work or something, go to that one instead if you can.    I also heard a bunch of homophobic slurs coming out of the drive-thru window one day during what I can only assume was a joke by the staff.  I thought that was pretty unprofessional and could have been very insulting to someone.

Typically drive thru and although the line may appear long at peak hours, the line actually goes pretty fast.   The employees at this Starbucks have messed up my order multiple times, but they always attempt to adjust the drinks to make them right.

If you're looking for one of the nicer Starbucks in town, this is the place to go.  The location is perfect if you're a South Countian - right smack in the middle of a busy area, hospital, food establishment and grocery shopping.  The staff is super nice but I think that's just the company in general as I can honestly say I have never gone to Starbucks and not had a nice employee.  The company has started to add more items to their food offerings.  I have yet to try them but I cannot say no to their pastries as it is.  Something you may not know - if you buy the Starbucks coffee bags to make at home, if you keep the bag and take it to one of the stores, they will give you a free cup of coffee when you turn it in.  Very rewarding!
Amazing staff, great view in the outside seating area. I love the people and pet watching here, it's like my second home when visiting my parents in the area :)

This once was my favorite Starbucks location, but recently they've fallen behind on keeping the place clean - there always seems to be overflowing trash, or wrappers/crumbs on all the tables and floors. On a more positive note - There is a lot of seating, including a long table.  And the baristas are usually very friendly. And the coffee - Let's be honest - Starbucks Coffee, is Starbucks Coffee - that's why we like it. It is standardized; no matter where you go, it's the same brand and pretty much the same product. I am your typical Grande Caramel Macchiato extra hot no-foam latte girl, and this place never fails to give me that inspiring jolt of caffeine infused inspiration.

Pros: new starbucks location, new chairs and tables, and now with drive thru! Ughs: Longgg wait times, single worker making drinks, slowest Wifi ever (tested), flys flying around.  So this is the new starbucks that moved across the parking lot. It went from a corner unit with one restroom and moved into a duplex with two restrooms. you would think the upgrade would help, but I feel like its still the same old environment in the store, in terms of speed.  I order simple drinks like a shaken sweet tea or a coffee. Usually at other SB stores since its so fast it can be done immediately. But here, nooo, they put you in a queue with the rest of the cups and that queue apparently takes second priority to drive thru customers. So even when there is less than a handful of customers that ordered, my drink will take 10 minutes. To dispense coffee. or shake some pre-mixed tea. what. the. hell.   Also, did they move the flies over from the old location? There are fruit flies that used to buzz around the tables, indicative of not keeping the environment sanitary. I figured with the new store it would not be a further issue. But now the flies are back. I killed two. I'm sure more will be back.  Finally, what is the deal with the Wifi. I thought this was google starbucks wifi. Or whatever that means. Before the speed was decent that I could watch at least a youtube video. But lately that has stutterred and buffered to a crawl. So I did a speedtest.net at the location, and lo behold, the downspeed bandwidth turned out to be 0.76Mbps. WTH? I'm yelping right now from the starbucks down the street and the download speed here is 8.06Mbps here.   So, yeah, I think I'd rather go to this starbucks that I'm at right now, or drive real far to palm harbor. because this trinity one is blech.

Super convenient location being right outside of TSA security check-in.  Line backs up sometimes especially if you're waiting for an espresso coffee drink and they're also making frappuccinos.  The fastest way in and out is to get a regular drip coffee since the cashier can get that for you quickly.

S L O W   S L O W   S L O W service & not friendly at all. No one cracked a smile, no one said "thank you" & Starbuck's won't be getting my business from this day forward. Too bad coz it's so close to home- good thing P.J.'s is right down the street .
This place can be either a hit or a miss depending who makes the drink and whether or not the person is new. I do like the friendliness of the staff and I always leave good tips for their hard work.

Disorganized, the slowest service I've ever seen a Starbucks have, and they run out of supplies EVERY time I have ever been there (even ICE). I tried so many times but I won't be going back to this one.
The lady Emmma was rude and unprofessional. Starbucks shouldn't have people like that working there. Ruined a whole business. It's sad.

Poor coffee, wrong cake, half hour in line at drive in.  4pm Saturday afternoon.  On our way to the beach from Plant city we thought a simple coffee stop was just what would make the drive nicer.  When we pulled up to the window 25 minutes later our cup of regular coffee and tall cappuccino were not even ready.   They were making a frozen drink for the car behind us and left it on the drive in counter while we waiting over 5 more minutes for our simple order.    Once you enter the drive in you cannot escape therefore if you have to wait 30 minutes you are stuck..no way out.     Finally on the road and in the bag was pumpkin bread instead of banana.   We didn't want that.    We will never ever go there again.  Word to the wise, grab a wawa cup of joe next door instead.

I am a fan of Starbucks due to the fact that I like to know what I'm getting and usually you get the same thing at each store.  This store on the other hand is different than most, while the coffee is the same the staff leave something out.......like kindness! I could say maybe they are just having a bad day, but it seems that any day that ends in y is bad for them!!  Go for the coffee not the relationships, b/c this isn't your local  friendly shop!
This is my favorite Starbucks in the area. They are usually pretty quick, friendly and haven't messed up and order yet!

I don't know why this happens but when it's only the male employees working they mess up Orders, forget orders and don't call out name.  I'm on a time limit. Last time I waited 10 minutes I walk up to employee area and said if you can't make my drink I have to leave I'll take refund. The guy said oh you didnt get your drink. He said to other guy where's his drink. He said I am didn't get order. What the Hell they are 3 feet away from each other and using ear pieces.  I'm done with this location.  I will say when the females employees work no issues.  Management need to be updated on this and  changed if they can not train correctly. Maybe giving money back on this issue. How will the store ever learn.

Even when this place appears to be busy via drive thru, they still manage to get drinks out fast and accurately! I've gone a few times the past two weeks and haven't been disappointed yet. I have never been inside, so can't comment about that.

Inside of cafe is so messy, not clean at all,, My coffee, americano,, tasted horrible. Which is hard to make it terrible ,, it is just black coffee after all.  And my partner ordered caramel macchiato,, it was not any better. Horrible as well.  Waste of money on coffee
Great Baristas, good coffee, large seating areas with laptop plug ins and plenty of cool people to chat with. Gotta love this Starbucks.
Nice location near me,  Free Wifi and good place to meet someone for a coffee or do some work.

This Starbucks always have fresh coffee.  The staff is very pleasant and I have made this location my go to place.  I love the convenience of the drive thru. And the cleanliness of the outside and inside.  It's located in a popular location which makes it easier to get to where your going.
Weelcome In  James said So Friendly & Upbeat Clean Love the Design of the store I'll be coming here.often  Summer Out door seating

Awesome service, other than our drink longer than we expected (order mix up), and the barista was so apologetic and tried to make it up to us with a gift card for a beverage... It was nice and thoughtful.

This is the best Starbucks closest to me, unfortunately. My drinks are always consistent but when I pull up to the drive thru, which is quite often to pick up my mobile order, i cringe when the older gentlemen answers the drive thru order. Today I receive my third lecture from him on how to properly order my drinks on the mobile. Today's lecture was longer than normal because apparently there was a situation with order prior. So I had to YET AGAIN wait for my coffee and be lectured by this man! I thought I left my father in Ohio, clearly not!!   NOTE TO ALL: If you don't properly order your mobile order, be prepared to be lectured! Dad works here!!
Consistently excellent service, and the espresso has bite. Best SBUX in Tucson.
Half an hour line with half a dozen cars.  This Starbucks is the slowest and worst I've ever been to.
It's another one with a fitting exterior. It is the place we all know and appreciate for it's uniformity and selections. It's not perfect, but it wants to be.

Prime location on the way to/from work or accessing highway 40 at Jefferson.  I appreciate the alternative to the busy DelTaco location at Grand and 40.   Interior is a bright, welcoming, open floor plan with plenty of seating once we get back to dining, working, meeting, chillaxing at cafes again. The restrooms are upscale chic as a public restroom can be with the soap/water flow/dryer in a single sink location.  This really cuts down on paper towel waste  it I like to have a little tissue to open the door after washing my hands personally.   Service was prompt and pleasant (I was the only one brave enough to walk inside I guess) the drive thru was popping.  My coffee was made just as I'd requested and I was on my merry way.   Oh, plenty of outdoor patio seating and parking available.   ~Christy

Bonus points to this Starbucks for being located inside of my hotel (New Orleans Marriott).  I was able to visit each day to get my daily Iced Green Tea craving satisfied.  The cool thing about this location is that the barista told me I could bring my cup back for 60 cent refills!  Score!  I was ready to take complete advantage of this until housekeeping tossed my cup out while I was out of the room.  Grrrr!
Very lovely staff. Very kind and hardworking. The girl with brown curly hair and the guy in the hat and long beard answered my questions about becoming a barista! Love y'all!

Never write reviews but frustrated. Spent a half hour in line and the coffee was cold and not even made right. Service seriously declining here in last few weeks. . Definitely a big change last few times I've been in. Going to find a new one to pick up from going forward after this has happened multiple times lately. Stinks cause it's my local for work and home.

I go through this Starbucks on occasion when I'm Driving through Brentwood. Every single time they mess up my order today they didn't give us the food we ordered and to Re ring me up and pay more .. and the latte tastes like water - this happens absurd single time the lattes are completely watered down and service is Shit and slow - never again

I always appreciate good customer service, and this location does not disappoint! The staff knows its regulars well and knows how to take of them. There have been times in the drive thru where we'll here, "just pull up to the window - we know what you want!" which makes it super convenient when you're heading back to work!   A staff is only as good as their management team, and management has done a great job training their teammates to do the best they can. Everyone is so friendly even during a rush! And we rarely have to wait very long for mobile order or in line order.  I would say that our drinks are made to our liking 95% of the time. Sometimes there are discrepancies but if we bring them to their attention, they're quick to fix.   Of course, you've got inside-outside seating with a drive thru, with the entirety of the cafe always pretty clean. Definitely one of my favorite locations.
Staff was unfriendly, the drinks were terrible. I spent 5$ on something I couldn't even drink because it was so bad.

I read the reviews about how slow the service was before I went to this Starbucks, and I did not expect it to be so slow for my visit. It wasn't very crowded at all when I entered, and all I ordered was a nitro cold brew coffee. That's something that takes literally 5 seconds to make. There were only two other people in front of me, and it took me 10 minutes to get my coffee. To top it off, the baristas were taking their precious time. If you're in a rush, don't pass through this Starbucks.

They just opened last year and every time we pass by they are alway super super busy. The line is always at least 5 people. But they actually move pretty fast because they have enough people working so that's good. If you like Starbucks then make sure stopping by when you are shopping.

This Starbucks is really good, I have to say it could be 5 stars. However, it will never be 5 stars because the parking lot has only 5 spaces and one of them is handicapped. Which is extremely odd because the inside seating area is massive? The staff of very friendly and the location is awesome! Small historic town vibe going on here. It is very relaxing with small outside seating next to the lake/dam that runs through town.

Great service at this location.   It's the usual fare but the menu is better detailed and the service polite, pleasant, and not perfunctory.   It has a drive through and on this Sunday afternoon, the line was six or seven deep.   There are plant of outside tables but you are on busy Route 38 so I'm not sure how relaxing that would be.

I just can't say enough wonderful things about this Starbucks location and the incredible staff!!! So many times I pull up to the Drive Thru and all I have to say is Good Morning and I am almost immediately recognized ..greeted by name and my usual order appears on the screen!  When I walk into the establishment I feel like Norm on Cheers... because 3-5 people shout out my name when I walk in! I feel special there to say the least!!!!! Even when they are swamped they move quickly and are so Friendly and professional!!!! LOVE MY PEEPS AT SPEEDWAY STARBUCKS!!!!!!!
Fast and easy. Always gets the order correct and staff is friendly and knowledgable.
The drive thru service is so so so slow it's comical. Something needs to be done to make it more efficient

They mess up my coffee every single time. They don't know how to listen or follow directions! At least the coffee/espresso is not burnt tasting though. I go to it only because I work across the street, and it's convenient. Not anymore
Not a great example of how a starbucks operates. Ordered my coffee and had a burnt taste to it and threw it out. Tasted as though the grind needs to be adjusted.

This is my favorite location by far! There is almost never a wait or a line inside. The drive through is another story.. But come on people save the planet and get out of your car for 5 mins!  The employees at this location are on top of it! One time I was here I had an issue with a rocky table and a lid that was not on very well and it ended in my toddler being covered in my coffee. Much to her dismay in her brand new outfit..
