She freaked out I freaked out making sure she was ok (the coffee was hot) she was fine and much more concerned about her new mini mouse shirt than herself. I took her in the restroom as 3 different employees came and asked if she was ok.. By the time I got her washed off in the bathroom and back to the lobby they had cleaned up the mess, to include my cell phone. They had remade my drink and set it on the table with another cake pop for my daughter. They went above and beyond to make one hell of a morning as efficient and good as possible. I did not say anything to them about how anything happened just went in mom mode to fix the issue with out complaint as moms do. I was not going to complain, I was actually going to buy another coffee. But I didn't have to. I love that this location is so close to work. I make sure to visit this location over any other.

This is a nice big Starbucks with lots of seating as well as a few kiosks with Starbucks drinkware and gift idea items. The staff are very friendly. I was afraid there'd be a long wait in the morning, but it was fairly quick and I was off on my way.

It's a Starbucks, which is just like every other one on the planet, but this one has a drive-thru, which is nice....if you don't want to add stuff to your drink.  But, the place has parking, room to sit, it's clean, so it's what you'd expect.  Good enough.

This Starbucks is not for people who like a friendly staff. They were not rude but they were not like the type of staff I am used to having at my CA Starbucks. So if you don't mind that, then this is a good Starbucks.

I still remember the anxiety of my first time at a coffee shop, and no, it wasn't a Starbucks. But, it was a looooong time ago, and back when Starbucks was not a household name yet (don't ask don't tell). BUT, I was in highschool and was living in Seattle and learning that coffee drinking was more than, well, "coffee" :) and drinking; it was espresso, cappachee...what? and music and hanging out and books, and yada yada yada. Back to the purpose of my story, even the newbie trying to order a large (lol, large, haha) Mocha drink thingie (not that that would ever happen these days, I think babies come out drinking Starbucks and Redbull. I digress) will be treated with the utmost of patience and friendliness at this location. They've always been that way with me when, even I, (I used to work at one) forget how to speak Starbuxian (I made that up, catchy right?) or just plain have a brain fart and can't choose WHICH delicious diet no-no to indulge in. Although, they're sandwiches are a good choice for a meal and allow you  to stay in your diet plan should you choose, and the cake-pops are just enough of a sweet-fix (and look like one bite, but believe me it's just right) to tame your sweet tooth. And, if the end result is not perfect drink-wise, here's the word, PERFECT (there's your insider knowledge folks), they gotta make it again.  But, the Barista's here have always asked me without prompting if my drink was right, with smiles galore. And I've left happy and highly caffeinated.  So, although I'm still prone to favor those Seattle-type grunge artsy coffee houses of my youth I have sold-out and become a Starbucks fan, this location gets 5 stars (never a line and always friendly). Oh, another thing, if you order the delicious pastries they will heat them and make them uber delish. It truly does take them up a notch. You know, like you might want to eat it in private kind of good....teehee. Try the lemon cake, never had it before here and now it's my fav.
I ordered three drinks. They were all made wrong the first time. The second time two were made wrong. The third time, they were still made wrong and I gave up trying.
Friendly staff but understaffed , dirty , small , bathroom dirty ....  look for a different location !

I've visited a few times and the workers are always friendly. The barista spent a little extra time making my zebra mocha mixed and presentable. The tables are always clean and the location is convenient and a perfect place for a pick-me-up for a day of shopping.

My first experience here at this newly opened store. I love Starbucks and stop in many different ones. This is a new and industrial look. It is wide open inside but only has a few cushioned chairs whereas other have a decent amount. But, the customer service is top notch and that goes a long way! Stop in here if you have a chance.

Rude baristas gave this place poor review. They messed up on my order and gave me a cold drink when I told them I never said cold. Therefore she gave me an attitude and said, "you told me cold. Can't believe this." She wont admit that its their fault. I asked to speak to the manager and shes also rude and just slid across free coffee pass without saying a word. That's not what I expected. I don't know where she heard that but all of them needs to get trained on customer service. I'm not paying high price coffee for rudeness.

I have one experience with this location- a drive-thru for a green tea latte. But it was all good! They got us through the line fast, and my green tea was hot, yummy, and perfect, and I got it all on a $5 gift card (with about 25 cents to spare!).

The line at this Starbucks is always horrendously long and today, after waiting in line for 15 min, they're out of cold brew and the strawberry refresher that I ordered is basically water. Not to mention the lid had drops of coffee on it. Anyone who has ever ordered the strawberry refresher can look at this and tell it's just watered down. It's disappointing when you wait forever, pay almost $5 for a drink, and this is what it looks like.

the FOURTH time i have tried a hot breakfast sandwich from here and the middle of it was cold and icy. honestly disgusting and i tried to give them a chance but literally going out of my way to post this. nothing irks me more than cold eggs. get it together!!!!!!!! tired of wasting my money.

My sister frequented this Starbucks location more than I did while we were living in Tucson, but here's what *I* have to say:  It's clean, the employees were friendly (except one who laughed at my Visa Card picture, but maybe I deserved it--I had my picture on my credit card), the deserts they had the last time I went in were delicious (okay, I didn't try everything, but the chocolate cupcake was good), and they made my Caramel Light Frappacino perfectly (because I like my drinks light and my sweets packed full of awfulness and sugar--I'm full of contradictions like that). When my sister went, she said they had her and her ex-boyfriend's order down to a "T," which I thought was kind of cool.   Otherwise, it's a decent, typical Starbucks. Nothing awful, nothing exceptionally noteworthy...yet, here I am...making a note.   Ha.

Customer service was very very good ! Jatoria helped me. What a wonderful person. One that will brighten up a day when a person's day isn't going so well. She is very contagious. Her wonderful spirit is catchy and she made my speciality drink for the first time and it was perfect (venti starbucks double shot on ice w/cold brew whip cream)  Thank You Jactoria ( I hope I spelled your name correctly

This is a worse Starbuck's I have been to, nationwide. I ordered mobile order and my estimated time for 3 drinks to be ready was 7 minutes, I ended up waiting for 30 minutes and when I asked associate about it, he was rude and replied, we are busy, don't you see it. That being said, stay away. I waited total of 40 minutes for my mobile order. That is unacceptable

Excellent service ! Such nice and friendly staff. This is the actual way my drink is supposed to come . If you take a look at my last Starbucks review my cup was half empty while this being the same order and it is completely full !
Nice atmosphere. Always busy. Free wifi.   Good place to meet up with friends. Always super hot  inside.

i never stop here because it's always so busy but i did today and my order was completely wrong i requested a venti ribbon crunch frapp with extra crunch and a warm banana nut bread. what i got was a burnt banana nut bread and a grande ribbon crunch with no crunch! i will never be back my order wasn't hard at all very simple and when i told the worker that i wanted a venti all she said was "oohhh i'm sorry!" come on now.  don't waste your time going here for sure, all you'll get it wasted time and wrong order

The customer service needs a little work and the place is not very clean, because when I sat on the table it was dirty and o should not have to say something in front of all the customers after having a cashier with little manners.
Friendly, clean, drinks as expected. Love love that they're open late. A bit more room on the inside and outside to sit and chat or to get work done.

Relatively spacious caffeine station, complete with drive-thru and outdoor seating which is perfect for the heat and humidity we have in the area ;-)  They are helpful about using the iPhone app to pay from your car which is a plus, since some places will not accept it if you can't physically reach their barcode reader.

Please please For the love of God get Faster I was stuck the Day before yesterday in the drive-thru line for 29 Minutes!!!! At 11 am!!! Then last night at 530 another 25 min wait in line again! If it wasn't for my Love of your coffee I would Never Return!!! I'm a regular 6 days a week come on get it Together!!!

Waited for 30 mins for our drinks inside, saw others bypass us when they were behind us in line. Note, if you want a drink in a reasonable amount of time, apparently you shouldn't come on a Saturday or go inside.
I love the way this works. The design os more like the Christmas one it's better than the Tampa version of Starbucks and it's not that bar in the inside. What i like the most is the parking spot.

Baristas have major attitudes as are super condescending. Slower than any Starbucks I've been too so far. Least favorite Starbucks, but the paintings outside by the drive through are awesome.   I've come back to this Starbucks on 3 different occasions since my last review because I'm always in the area and each time my drink order has been wrong. I do not take it back anymore if it's wrong because it seems like I'm an inconvenience. This store's convenient location is entirely what  keeps me coming back.
It's Starbucks, and I'm not a hater.  But this place was understaffed and kind of dirty.

I used the drive thru to get a cup of coffee and a breakfast sandwich.  It took quite a while to get through the line of 4-5 cars.  When I got my order i found out that the sandwich was the wrong one.  Good thing is, it was still a tasty sandwich.
Pour over was good until I got a mouthful of grounds at the end and was driving, gross and couldn't spit it out

After waiting in the drive thru for a really long time, we finally got our coffees. Mine felt really light so I opened it and it wasn't even 3/4 full (and that's including the whipped cream). I had to park in their not so great lot and go in to get a replacement. The rep remade it and gave me a $4 courtesy card, but that drink never should have gone out the door. Also, the chalkboard sign outside said they would be closing early for Thanksgiving - it is early March.

BY FAR THE WORST STARBUCKS IN TOWN TO STUDY AT! ***  ***That is obviously a false statement- I'm hoping people who don't really read these see it and it keeps them from studying here and taking up my tables :)   This is actually my favorite study spot in all of Philadelphia... and I am NOT a starbucks person.   Reasons: 1. Free wifi 2. WITH PLENTY OF OUTLETS 3. Nice staff 4. Only tables made for two people- so it prevents annoying crowds that talk a lot (yet somehow they still miss the handicap sign on the front table) 5. I don't get phone service here... which you may think is initially a problem... until you realize how productive you are!  6. It's steps away from public transit, yet tucked away enough to keep it from being swarmed by the 9-5'ers 7.  I'm not sure if it's because it's located next to Fox and Hound or that it is close to the gay district... but it would be a lie if I said I didn't enjoy the man candy that walks by between 4-6pm every weekday :)  8.  Also, it seems to be people who actually study... i'm sure the two person seats and unlimited triple white chocolate mocas don't hurt that any tho (not that I order those...) 9.  They don't care if you bring outside food in... as they realize no real person could sit there for 12 hours straight and eat the crap they have
No matter how many times I go here, Emily always has a smile on her face when she serves customers. I know the job is busy and tough at times, but it never seems to phase her.
Tried a new drink today... chi tea latte.... Tasty, iced and will go for it again! Drive thru was easy with good prompt service! I like the cup to sip my drink and not have a separate straw! Thanks!
Awesome location - the service was excellent, super friendly employees, everyone seemed genuinely happy. A ton of space to get work done, very clean. Strongly recommend!

It's Starbucks - what do you expect?  The GF and I end up here a fair amount as they stay open late - 11 PM most nights, 12:30 on weekends - so she can study, while I write Yelp reviews, read a bit, catch up on email, knock out a bit of code, etc.    As with most Starbucks, the tables are small, and more conducive to sitting and chatting with a friend, rather than working on laptops, though there are outlets every 18" or so along the south windows.  The table layout is such that there are two lines - one against the south windows, the other running parallel to it.  If you're sitting at one of the 5 inner tables, you'll need to stretch your cord across if you need an outlet, though it works fine.  Tables are usually about half occupied with people studying and using laptops.  There's a patio space as well, though as we're usually working on our laptops, we rarely sit out there.  Staff is friendly enough - I got my large dark roast free tonight since they had just started another batch of drip and they felt bad about my having to wait a few minutes.
This is my favorite Starbucks in Reno! Great staff. Very friendly. Just an overall great business!
I love going to this Starbucks because once I go in Hillary already know what I want and she such a happy person makes me feel so welcome..! She should get a raise
Very Poor experience at this location. No Menu, staff unfamiliar with tea options. All items i requested were unavailable at 11am on Monday such as teas and croissants.

This is my favorite Starbucks the staff always puts a smile on face they know exactly what I want when I walk in the door. Keep in mind this is probably the most busy Starbucks in the STL their always hustling in bustling with joy. The entire staff deserves a big pat on the back. And a raise!

This Acme in the Village of Newtown Shopping Center recently underwent a makeover to bring it into the 21 Century.  The two main improvements were the new beer aisle and Starbucks, both very near the right side entrance to the supermarket, just beyond the bakery.    These in house coffee shops are a version of the outlets found in airports around the world. A decent work area for the crew, with smaller units for pastries and light fare, and very limited displays for their branded coffee and accessories.   The mermaid has been here about six months and they are efficient in their operation.  One early evening this week it was well staffed with a friendly crew ready to serve.  The barista kept her poise as she mixed my Chai Latte. Never missed a beat in our repartee. The others were cleaning up in anticipation of closing within the hour.    Perhaps, because they see the same customers routinely, but this shop was far better in its customer service than the equivalent business in an airport.  They show that the opportunity to build relationships enhances the overall experience.  No doubt, for both parties.   Even this Starbucks-phobe found this captive distribution point enjoyable.  It even made their drinks taste better somehow.   Good job!

I love the fact that this Starbucks is close by my school and they have a drive thru but this is the second time I've received the wrong order! If you are in a hurry I would not advise you to go here early in the morning looking for great service. Overall the drink was tasty but the sandwich I was given was completely horrible!!!!

Newer Starbucks location, friendly staff, enough parking, and neat setup inside. There's lots of space inside and I liked how there were different types of seating instead of identical chairs and tables all lined up. The decor is also new and interesting compared to other locations I've been to. This Starbucks has nitro brews which can't be found at all locations. Only complaint was that all the tables seemed to be dirty when we arrived, and it wasn't busy.
Highly disappointed it's 38 outside and this guy is telling me they don't make medicine balls and people have colds and it's one of the coldest days in history.Wow I'm not coming back here .

Please build a bigger Starbucks this one obviously cannot accommodate all the people in the drive-through are in the line in the building it is not adequate place to get your coffee.  Very disappointed at this location, customer service, and accommodation for customer, will never be back
Never a long line, friendly and quick service! Comparing this to the location in Macy's makes it look like a 5 star spot.
Line was extremely long!!! They are consistently out of salt topping for salted caramel mocha lattes! 1st world problem! Polite workers and drinks are tasty!

Probably one of my least favorite Starbucks location mainly because of its constant chaotic state housed in the smallest of buildings.   The drive thru lines are usually long and ridiculous and if you decide to go inside to save time you might run into yet another line with a not so quick return time for your drink order.   Not much planning went into the parking lot or size of this Starbucks when it was placed in Edwardsville. They are in need of a new location.   My suggestion download the app, pay ahead, park (good luck) and go inside and pick up your order.   As far as parking goes: I would recommend not parking near the entrance, as the drive thru like may prohibit a quick and speedy entrance/exit. If the cars in the drive thru line are not blocking the pass lane, then you can drive around the building and find parking away from the constant flow of coffee lovers.

This is the Starbuck's I come to study at the most. I've been here so many times, this review is long overdue.   I love his big it is, sometimes it's difficult to find a larger Starbucks. But this one is great! It has large tables and there is always one available. I especially love their couch seating, super comfortable to sit in for a while when I have hours of studying.   Minor flaw is that the staff here can be hit or miss. I have my favorites, but there are a couple that could be rude. I say this because they are consistently rude. Other than that great location!

I am downgrading this store because for whatever reason the management cannot figure how to keep it clean during busy times. The response "we just got slammed" is no excuse. I like most of the staff, and they work hard and are friendly, but the store HAS TO BE KEPT CLEAN.

While I may not be the biggest fan of Starbucks - you know, it's the whole "giant chain" thing. I just prefer to support local! Also, I don't love their cappuccino, the espresso just tastes bitter. Of course you can't taste it in a big, fancy, 900 calorie frappuccino... Another reason why I don't like Starbucks, it's yet another American chain that is geared to getting people f-a-t! But enough of what I don't like about SatrB's and on to what I really like about this location: I just love the location! High Street is so quant, full of adorable shops and free parking. Then there is the patio situated under the gigantic sprawling trees of the east Glenora address - shaded and breezy in the summer, gorgeous in the fall with a plethora of falling leaves. In the winter you can sit in the big windows and watch the snow fall as you work. They also have 4 stools up at the counter which is a cool spot to park yourself and your laptop. So, if I'm going to be at a Starbucks, this is definitely the one!
Barista Molly has a lot of attitude  for someone working in the hospitality industry.  While chummy with select customers, she is cold, impersonal and hostile to others.  GET RID OF MOLLY!

The best Starbucks around! They are miraculously quick in the mornings in drive-thru even though the lines can get very long. The staff is so nice, and they seem to really care about the customers. I go here at least two times a week, and I have never been disappointed.

I had a great experience at this location! They were super busy, but they still worked diligently and were friendly. Ashleigh allowed me to make 3 orders since I had a promo to get 100 extra stars ;) She patiently and thoroughly took all my orders. We waited only a few minutes to get all 3 drinks.   The place is clean and there is plenty of seating and room for people to wait for their orders. I love the convenience of the location and hours!!

Busy location for driving through during weekday mornings. Not sure how it is during the weekends, but assume that it's just as busy if not more so. The interior is very big with lots of seating. They have yet to mess my coffee order up and the people at drive thru are always pleasant, smiling and friendly with me.
Love this new location. Various seating arrangements and the Baristas are always friendly.  My Tevana Chai tea in the bags is wonderful. Thanks Joel!

If you would stop talking over me then you could hear what I'm saying. The fact that I have to repeat the order 5 times (triple non fat caramel machiotto seems pretty simple but ok) and then you get an attitude with me bc I have to tell you not iced EACH TIME then possibly you shouldn't work the drive through bleach blonde with bad skin and a horrible attitude. This Starbucks is always the worst
Why is it that every Starbucks you go to the people that work there are so nice! Lucky for me and anyone else in traffic this one is right off I4.

Nice location with a flurry of activity due to the college, seminary, high school, and local business. The place is busy but I've always found a seat to park my java and laptop and the staff is nice and not boorish.  John, the store manager, is a cool dude and He'll converse with folks.   This place draws some real characters like a resident "Bob Dylan" and the yoga trainer. There is a park across the way making for awesome people watching.   This store gets a little cold at times due to the location of the doors but it's acceptable.

Honestly they were out of everything we tried to order except for a white chocolate mocha... this is a busy location, but it's right by my house.  The guys working here are super awesome and were as accommodating as possible... in the end I just feel bad that they weren't set up for a busy Sunday by upper management.  Nevermind... I wrote this post as I was eating my second spinach feta wrap.  The first one was cold in the center so I sent it back. The second one came back the same way.  The first couple of bites are scorching, then by the time I get to the middle, it's freezing.   The heating methods they are using here are horrible.

Great staff, very efficient!!!  Everyone has a great attitude and welding smile, no matter how early it is. I love this Starbucks. I go to this location and only this one. The manager and assistant managers are very active with there staff and get involved in the customers.
Pssssht STAH-BUCKS kid, don't get betta than this, wicked good coffee, wicked good people, especially on a crappy New England day in Nash.

We stopped by here quickly before a dinner reservation, he needed coffee for the ride home and I wanted to try the new Crystal Ball frappuccino.  It was Easter but the employees were very friendly. I asked for mine with soy milk and I noticed the employee making it with regular, so I asked for soy again and she asked me if I needed it in a new cup or not (ie, was I very lactose-intolerant or not). As for the drink, it was just okay ... the peach whipped cream was very good though.  Very tiny location with only one bathroom, otherwise this would get 5 stars from me because of the customer service.

It's a sad day when a Starbucks has bad customer service. Her name is Jo and you'd think with a name like that she'd understand mix ups between Erin and Aaron but she wanted to blame me for handing me my sausage egg sandwich and nitro cold brew. The receipt was a mobile order and I am Aaron, then after her coworkers asked her she tried to blame me EVEN MAKING ME GO TO THE TRASH FOR THE RECEIPT. Yes, I had to go into the trash to get my receipt. Never again Jo, I pray management sees this and other poor reviews and decides to do something about it.
I have always had a good experience here. It's a cozy place to sit and work and the deck by the creek is a plus. Always had good service over the years.
Yay!  Finally! The old Del Taco building put to good use!! Great space. Lots of seating and tables for reading, studying...  And there is a drive thru if you're in a hurry!
I have always enjoyed this spot. The staff are nice and the place is clean. Plenty of places to sit and the music is pleasent. I would recommend this place to anyone.
Very extremely rude staff! The female at the drive through acted like she hated her job and took it out on others! Won't go back to this one!

This location sucks!  They couldn't break a $100 bill and refused my transaction because I didn't have have a debit card or credit card for my transaction.  Who wants to charge a $7 transaction on a credit card!   I've never been to an establishment that refused cash because "I don't have access to  my twenties, so I can't provide change"  I will not be revisiting, I'm sure if I offered a large tip  there wouldn't have been an issue with me using a big bill.

Each time I visit this Starbucks, I am disappointed. Most Starbucks locations are SUPER AWESOME, but this location consistently keeps a staff who are snarky, aloof, and disrespectful. Step it up kids!

Love Starbucks but this one would drive me to the local diner. Personnel are not trained properly. I waited for service this morning while 3 "baristas" were searching for something.   I ordered a tall Veranda and he asked what's that even though it was posted as one of the 3 coffees being served. Went to the food court instead. Other Starbucks at the mall is very good
This Starbucks is super cozy. All the employees are very friendly and it is a cute little spot for coffee

No bathroom use allowed! Customer beware.  After buying $25 of items, my 4 yr girl was told to go next door to a competing store by the Starbucks Supervisor.  The Supervisor randomly shutdown the bathroom for no reason other than high traffic.  The place was packed with 30+ patrons, but the staff shutoff a clean bathroom.  Don't have accessibility concerns, medical problems, or a parent of children and expect basic human support.
The service at this place is extremely rude and employees make nasty comments about customers in front of them.

This Starbucks is the  average Starbucks. Lots of area for seating, indoor and outdoor. Staff is friendly and somewhat attentive. I ordered a tea, and had the cash in my hand. The barista decided to leave me hanging and made my tea first. I wish he would have taken my cash. The board that shows what Starbucks offers is a bit disorganized. I felt confused as to know what to order.also, staff does not know their product. My friend asked for a Vanilla Steamer, but the barista at the counter had no idea what that was. If it wasn't for the manager my friend would not have gotten what she wanted because the staff does not know their product!!!!!!!
I mean come on it's Starbucks and Starbucks is amazing !!! This is got to be the best place to grab a bite to eat or the main purpose COFFEEE !! Forever a customer

I agree with the consensus that this Starbucks doesn't live up to the others in the city. The baristas there just seem so overwhelmed and confused, the poor things. I much prefer the one at 12th and Walnut, where you can find the best barista in the city, Denise!

Went to this Starbucks Friday night to relax and the staff was very unprofessional. All were talking about things that were inappropriate. I saw each of the three staff members that were working, eating while they were preparing drinks!  Some were even talking to each other with a mouthful of food. I didn't see any of them wash their hands after they snacked on rice crispy treats, prior to preparing the next drink. Rather gross. There was no background music to drown out their conversations and you could hear them making fun of how the drive through customers were placing their orders. Customers like me pay top dollar at Starbucks, not only for the drinks, but for the ambiance and I have to say that I was sorely disappointed tonight.
My lattes always taste perfectly delicious here. I've been visiting this Starbucks since 2010. The girls are nice the drinks are always prepared just right.

i really like the cotton candy frappachino. it is amazing! must have. its a non-menu item, but just ask for it and they should know what you mean. the food is okay, but the drinks are awesome. just play it safe and just get the drinks. what i love about starbucks is that the baristas are so friendly and they sometimes make small talk while you wait for your drink. the reason while the last star is not there because it is sometimes a little bit dusty and when it is really crowded the drinks take long. i recommend this coffee shop to everyone who likes coffee (i dont drink coffee).
Coffee good but I make better expresso. Their cappuccino is excellent but I find everything there to be kinda expensive.

Oof, this was a hot mess experience. I would typically describe my frequent visits to this Terminal A Starbucks location as "meh" but this particular visit especially unfortunate. They were busy as usual and it seemed that two of the three employees were bringing their A game while the third, Amber, was a weak link. Oh man, where to begin?  Despite the crazy busy line of travelers, she was texting on her phone (germs galore) then proceeded to plug her phone in to charge with the cord wrapped around the nozzle of the whipped cream bottle and phone on top of everything... Note that she was the individual creating everyone's drinks.   She then wiped her nose on hand while making preparing a drink. Seriously, this was a health department nightmare.   She then talked to another airport employee about how much each of them were paid and stopped working (while the store was super busy...) to ask these tacky questions openly.   I don't want to read too far into it but I could feel a sense of frustration from her teammates. Here's to improvements and additional training...
I arrived 35 minutes prior to their closing time and they were...closed. Bummer. I made a special trip for beans in the morning.

I like this Starbucks, except that it is a little less accessible from the north bound lane of 309.  That's not there fault, but it's a definite inconvenience and deterrent some of the time when I contemplate stopping for a cup of joe.  Nice staff.  Good seating.  Nice atmosphere.
Ollie/Olay (not sure if that's the spelling, sorry!) at the window was so kind and friendly to us and provided above & beyond service! Made for a great experience, many thanks to them!

It's a typical Starbucks. You should know what to expect. I will say that this location is probably the best in downtown Indy, so when looking for your 'bucks fix when here, head to this location. Only downside is lack of seating in the winter. I love sitting outside during the summer though - people watching on the Circle is always interesting.

** Low Ratings?? They Are Reviews 1+ Yr Old. One is 4 Yrs Old. Why Did I Ck?? I'm Not Driving. Behind the Wheel? I would PEEK QUICKLY/SAFE @ THE HINTS. Sorry, Now their hiding behind "The Reviews:("  I have Zero complaints about this StarB location. Convenient next to I-4 W. Exit 19. It's spotless clean. Seems like it's had a nice Reno. Modern, lots of seating w/WiFi charging access. Configured into different sections. Even, for the non-techies. Wanting to relax for a bit. Or, come in w/a colleague or bestie. Seating for business meet/greets.  Most important. "Bean Talk" from placing an order w/Armando at the Register. Great people skills.  The Baristas are polite. Fast efficient service. I ordered the A Grande Mocha Soy Frappuccino. Needed a chocolate Jolt.  I must say. ##WOW!! If Chocolate is an aphrodisiac or wake up your senses.  I feel like dancing.  Yes, I do miss the Hints On Yelp. Though :-( "Yelp. Pls, Bring your Hints back.*** NO OTHER W.SITE HAS THE HINTS.*** We're feeling lost out here.  Lots Road Warriors.

Hit and miss here at times.  At once a knowledgeable server can help you.  At another, they can seem mystified.  Formulaic drinks and syrups are ok.  I do like the twists that can be added to each drink. I am mystified that I can get a green tea chai latte at all other locations ever visited but here.  What's up with that? Maybe I'll call ahead next time.  Great job in moving the bulky sales shelf from the middle of the seating area.  Feels more bright and inviting.
every friday is decaf mocha latte day. 2 extra pumps of mocha and make it grande please.

The service here is consistent and friendly. The crew here really hustles, which is important to me. They care about you and they care about your order. The free wifi makes it a good place to get some work done if you need a break from the office.

These ladies and gentlemen drive for absolute customer service. They work hard And pay close attention to special drink orders. If you had trouble here in the past try again now. It will be worth your time.

Incredibly rude and out of the few times I've been there my order is almost always made wrong. They gave me a smoked butterscotch frappe instead of a caramel which was fine. But upon asking for it to be remade they looked at me like I was speaking in a different language. Then the drink that they remade for me tasted even worse than before. And that was my last time EVER visiting this location.

So slow!  They only had one person working the register and one making drinks. At any given time there were at least 20 people in line. Not only were they super slow, they gave terrible customer service. The staff at this location really need to be retrained.

I was on the road from San Francisco to Los Angeles and stopped by this establishment. The reception was non existent for me, but the white guy behind me got the "welcome sir". I waited in line like any good citizen. Then came another person,a white girl this time and she also got the "Hi and welcome" and the server asked her almost instantaneous if she had made up her mind. Of course she did and she got to order while she stood behind me!!! I was totally ignored. I mean..whats up with that?  If you have an explanation i would really like to hear it. I was well mannered, well dressed...khakis and shirt. I hope to god that me being asian had absolutely nothing to do with it!! I finally got my order and i didnt even get thanked for leaving a 2 dollar tip while my purchase was only 8 bucks! I mean really?

This place is ALWAYS dirty! You would think being in the heart of DOWNTOWN Indianapolis it wouldn't be but IT IS!  I only stopped in this location because it was 1000 degrees outside and I love their cold Strawberry Green Tea. Well,  along with the nasty store the employees had NASTY attitudes. A woman asked for a cup of ice  after she finished her drink and you thought she asked for a million dollars!!! The barrister loudly proclaimed that "It's hot outside and we don't have ice to spare!" What,  they don't have a ice MAKER? Is the ice bought in from City Ice?  And as for my STRAWBERRY GREEN TEA, I may as well have paid  $4.00 for WATER!  The flavor and color of my drink neither  tasted or looked anything like green tea, let alone STRAWBERRY GREEN Tea!
Really nice place.  I like this one because you can always find a quiet corner for a meeting.  Everything else is pretty much a starbucks.
I love all of the drinks and usually go everyday! The employees are always very nice and friendly. They are also fast when it comes to completing orders
Nice Starbucks. Service was fast and friendly.nice dining area but small. Service was fast and friendly. Convenient parking in the strip mall.

Let's be honest - Starbucks Coffee, is Starbucks Coffee - that's why we like it. It is standardized; no matter where you go, it's the same brand and pretty much the same product. I am your typical Grande Caramel Macchiato extra hot no-foam latte girl, and this place never fails to give me that inspiring jolt of caffeine infused inspiration. What makes this place stand out to me in comparison to the other Starbucks in the area: The staff. I have spent a considerable amount of time studying here, during which I have witnessed the kindness of the staff on so many occasions. They truly are great, and manage to get their work done without being jerks...even when the place is so busy there is a line out the door... Brava. :) Keep smiling! :)

It was kind of a surprise to eye a Starbucks in a shopping complex just off one of the seedier strips in the city, but a very welcome one, as my need for a warm wake-up drink was high after hours of sifting through dusty thrift-shop merchandise.  I ordered a cappuccino, which was the usual tasty treat, and a fruit and nut bar, something Starbucks has been carrying recently as a healthier option. They're basically a granola bar, lightly sweet, and what really makes these stand out is the addition of dried apricots and cherries. Yum!  Maybe it was just the time of day, or the fact that coffee shops in this area are few and far between, but this Starbucks appears to get quite a bit of business.

Every Friday since thats my late day at work  I always stop at Starbucks and they have lots of selection for breakfast and for desserts, Most of the times you can get a breakfast sandwich ( like a bacon egg and chesse muffin) or their glazed doughnuts, or even their Iced Lemon Cake :) or their Marble Cake :) They even have fruit cups and salads as well. Since its winter time i would normally get the Signature Hot Chocolate or their Hot Green or Hot Passion Tea, very very good! The funny part is i never get coffee from Starbucks. I stick to my Tea's or Coco's. Spring and Summer time i would get the Passion Shaken Ice tea ( with 9 pumps of sweetner) or the Blended  Lemonnade. They have some seats to sit and where you can also bring your laptop and sit and enjoy your drink

I'm happily working my way through the gift cards my students' parents gave me at the end of the year. Part of me wants to dislike Starbucks and only frequent local places, but honestly I was excited for the excuse to have a frappaccino (sp?). I don't actually like coffee unless it's in dessert form, but I assume that most real coffee drinkers already have an opinion on Starbucks.   Starbucks all over have gone slightly down hill in the baked goods department, but you can still get something decent.   Although the place is fairly small I had no problem finding one of the comfy chairs for reading.  The staff is friendly and seems to know the regulars.

I never hopped on the Starbucks bus, I've always been a Dunkin Doughnuts or Wawa kind of girl. There are two in the KOP Mall but this review is for the one near Bloomingdale's.   I received a gift card so I ventured over early in the morning. I know these Starbucks get pretty crowded.  The staff that I encountered were very friendly and adept. No complaints here on service.  My companion ordered a coffee I didn't understand and I guess this is why I've never fit in here. I need coffee every day of my life but I need it in the most simplistic terms possible. I need it to be large, not grande and I need it with cream and sugar not foams or God forbid my portrait drawn in it. I skipped the coffee altogether and decided to get one of their breakfast sandwiches. It was made with a very flaky, grainy kind of biscuit that I personally did not enjoy but the  inside ingredients were tasty in a previously frozen and reheated kind of way.

My girls love Starbucks Every time we pass a Starbucks they want to stop. Yesterday we were celebrating my daughter's birthday weekend, passed Starbucks and had to stop in the drive up to get their favorites. Well, to our surprise and disappointment. The cookie and cake pop was delicious as usual, but the 3 drinks were not good at all. They lacked flavor, half the drink was liquid, and the rest taste like slushy ice, with lumpy deflated whipped cream. There was only 1 car at the window, which left pretty quick. We were next, but waited at the window for a very long time. It appeared the barista was making several orders while we waited. Now there is a line around the building ‍‍So so disappointed. A total waste of my $22, then they kept my bank card and ruined my daughter's birthday shopping spree because we had to leave the mall to go back to get my bank card in order to shop. I don't recommend 2705 Veterans Blvd.

Really terrible. Came in with my mother and friend before the SATs for a little pick-me-up. 3/4 drinks were messed up. Mine in particular was a grande cool lime refresher. It didn't taste anything like the refresher I had been drinking for the past two years, it was extremely bitter. I asked a barista about it, and she responded by pouring 1/2 of the drink out and filling the remainder of the cup with water. Wow. I'm pretty sure Starbucks policy is to just make a new one for you if they mess up. Regardless, it still tasted horribly sour after this. It's a shame that the service is so bad, the interior itself it quite pleasant. Won't be coming back after the Wayne location re-opens. Save your time and go there instead.

I don't like this location staff. They don't know how to make coffee. I ordered ice coffee more than 3 times. Always, coffee is overflowing. I dropped coffee in my shirts. And then, one of staff who is working drive thur very very UNKIND. Even though this Starbucks is very close to from my place, I'm Not going there. Never Ever !!

Came here with family. No criticism, but this is an ordinary Starbucks. The service is good and the product was of good quality. Nothing spectacular; again, not a criticism. Patrons were moved through the line at a respectable rate.   I will mention that the clerk was patient with a customer who had trouble selecting tea.   I would return.

As odd as the configuration of this Starbucks' drive-through is (nestled into a parking lot for a high rise building housing multiple businesses) it seems to always have a good sized line of people waiting. Probably because it's one of the only drivethru Starbucks in the downtown/midtown area currently. But no matter how long the line, the staff always seems to get through it quickly, efficiently, and professionally. They always seem happy to help and greet you with a smile no matter what. I think that's really what sets this Starbucks apart from the handful of others scattered around the Reno area. My order has always been correct too, which definitely makes it stand out to me. I definitely think for the circumstances this location is placed, it does a great job. I just feel bad for anyone trying to park in the surrounding lot during one of their morning rushes!

Same drink I order at every other Starbucks but taste and looks disgusting recommend trying a different Starbucks. I'm vegan and it looks full of something white floating in my drink almond milk dose not do that. Looks maybe they added whipped cream and instead of starting a new drink they just mixed it in

So glad to have this Starbucks right here in my neighborhood! Everyone that works here has always been friendly and eager to get your order right. Service is always fast and efficient, but if not completely accurate, they work hard to fix your order ASAP.  The best Starbucks I've ever been to! They're always busy but always friendly.

I can see why this Starbucks has a higher rating than some of the others. It's quieter and therefore you get a lot more customer service from the baristas. how are drinks were made perfectly. That's not always the case. There's a nice little patio area and the inside is a little more cozy then some of the other locations. Also, it's a lot cleaner since they're not so hectic. If you're a drive-by girl guy, they have that too. It's pretty easy to access it off of Oracle, and be right back on the road. I like to hang out if I'm going to enjoy some coffee. Give it a try, you might just like it!  I wanted to stick with my great review I had given of the 4 points, but when I saw the two dead cockroaches in the bathroom I felt sad. However... The people who work here are so nice and friendly and I actually saw them cleaning! To me that makes this location even better. I just wish they would go in the bathroom to see if it needs cleaning and get rid of the two bugs. Maybe they haven't seen them quite yet LOL. I still like this location because it's quiet and more cozy and some others.

This location is close to my house so I tend to visit a few times a week. Staff is always nice and my drink is always amazing. But...they need a second register!! It seems when I visit that it is always busy and a line forms immediately. They need to keep the place a little cleaner as well. Dusty and sticky on the chairs and tables (I take mine to go but I see things while waiting in line).

I love this Starbucks!  I stop by at least five days a week on my way into work.  Yes the drive thru does get a little backed up from time to time, but the customer service is worth the wait!  This location has some of the best baristas in town!

I cannot function properly without my daily dose of coffee. This is my go-to spot every morning as the location is very convenient, immaculately clean, & qualitatively quick. The workers here are incredibly friendly and the baristas I've interacted with have all been just as courteous. They've even memorized my usual order, that's going above and beyond customer service. Great place with a nice working atmosphere.

This is my favourite Starbucks in the entire city. Clara is the most wonderful barista I've ever encountered. She always suggests things that might make my drink better. All the other girls have a big smile on their face even when it's 7 am. Even when I order with my phone while I'm basically pulling up to the door, my drink is ready by the time I walk in. It's always clean and they always have the feature reusable cup in stock.

I come here often to get work done and occasionally a strawberry frappuccino. It's always clean and inviting. I give this location 4 stars because of an employee who made my experience more frustrating than necessary. Starbucks recently changed the strawberry frappuccino recipe and name to "serious strawberry frappuccino" but I had been used to ordering it as a "strawberry frappuccino" for years. When I ordered it, the barista said "We don't have that anymore." and left it at that. I was very confused as I had just ordered it a week prior which I mentioned. She then replied, "we have the serious strawberry frappuccino..." How annoying is that...  I'm quite sure she knew what I meant.
Please rope off the back of your parking lot so that your patrons don't get their cars towed by Grand View Apartments.
Welcome to Starbucks.  Come for the coffee stay for the TB.  I get the third space concept...but, homeless dude sleeping in your business... No thanks

This is by far the best Starbucks in the Nashville area - Cordell is always upbeat, the staff really go out of their way to make you happy. Drinks are hot, and the only annoying thing is that someone needs to put cones up between the two handicapped spots in front so idiots stop parking there!!!  Nice seating in the back, comfortable for laptops or reading. No pressure to move on. And yes, a really great mix of types.

Much like it's counterparts in the Silver Legacy and El Dorado, this Starbucks sells the exact same drinks you would get at any stand-alone location but inflates the price due to its, "convenience", of being in your hotel. I get it, but at the same time, Starbucks is already overly inflated so the addition of this kinda adds to the sting.  Unlike its counterparts, however, I've always found the customer service at this one to be particularly good and in line with most of the stand-alone locations. The other two casino locations I mentioned above are hit and miss. This one always gets the service and quality just right.

The staff is usually quite friendly and they make their drinks well. However, it is always - without fail - super dirty. Every time I come in here, the tables have trash and crumbs or spilled drinks on them leaving them sticky.   Today, two baristas let drink orders pile up while they were chatting with a friend about his boy troubles. Pretty unprofessional. :/

There are no plugs at this Starbucks. I came here while visiting Reno with the intent of spending 2 hours working before hopping in the car to work some more on the drive home (while my husband drove of course).   While looking for somewhere to plug my laptop in so I'd have a full charge for the drive (and because my laptop doesn't hold a charge very long), one of the employees let me know they don't have plugs anymore because people would post up too long. There truly must be a better solution to this, like, a time limit on the plugs or something along those lines.  I work remotely so I spend a LOT of time and money (much more than I probably should) in their stores, and I really feel it should be safe to assume reliable WiFi and plugs will be present.   Booooo!

I visit this store whenever I have an need to be in the area of Fairview and Cole.  While the intersection itself is (and always has been) a nightmare, Starbucks is always there to meet the needs of those seeking a java jolt, a tasty snack or some tea. I never feel rushed ordering and the baristas are always fun to talk to.  Now if only Starbucks would open more drive thru locations in Boise!

Still need much faster drive-thru service but they always make my Ice coffee taste Amazing.. I stop in 1 time a day and it's Always Delicious!!! They have 2 gentlemen that are always at the drive-thru both are Delightful and always Smiling

Now this is a fantastic Starbucks.  Fast, friendly service with employees who remember your name when you walk in!  Pretty impressive.  They are super fast with the line, which can get long in the morning.    Lots of outdoor tables to talk about the local news of the day with the downtown loiter squad.  They also have the "Clover" system if you want a uniquely brewed coffee.  Try it out if you are curious - you can ask for any coffee to be made in the Clover.  These are common in big cities but DTSP has one!    Even though it's "just Starbucks," the staff here goes above and beyond the call of duty to get you your much needed caffeine fix.  Also, they shut people down from using the bathroom if you don't buy something.  I personally like this philosophy because the general Starbucks public bathroom policy makes most of their bathrooms end up being where bums shave and do other various activities, some more hygienic than others.

I don't understand why they use online ordering for!!! I ordered 15 min before I arrived, 2-workers, no line! They haven't even started my one drink!  I guess this what happens when u have poor management!

I can't believe how downhill this company has gone! To spend for 5 to 6 dollars for coffee and it taste like crap the quality is terrible customer service has gone downhill they rush everything go here every day and it's not worth the money anymore

clean but MOBILE APP ORDERED, ALTHOUGH CONFIRMED DID NOT GO THROUGH. At least on their end. So caution when using mobile order. They were good about it and it was nice there  Otherwise your typical Starbucks!!  !

Just a quick stop through the drive thru for a passion fruit ice tea before heading out. The drive-thru line is usually manageable and they are always quick and friendly with service. Great location right off 60 and across from the mall.

I have driven past the location numerous times and didn't even realize it was there.  I went in because I just didn't know what I wanted. I had a great experience staff rocked and the location was clean, lots of people inside working away on their computers :) It was great. Coffee delicious and a yummy breakfast bacon and egg sandwich .. happy tummy!
Long wait usually but the staff is super friendly and helpful everytime.  Worth the wait!

They charged me and then skipped my order and eventually somebody asked me why I was standing there and somebody else said oh sorry. And then it was too late and I gave up.  But it all worked out because I went to Denny's and I had my food in front of me in less time then I waited at the Starbucks for my espresso.

Agh. Meh. Mmmph. And other shoulder shrugging sounds.   A.) it's your standard Starbucks as far as menu, decor, etc. go B.) The free wifi is less than weak. Double "meh" C.) I get that the suburbanites have few other coffee shop options, but holy shiza this place is jiggity jammed.  D.) friendly service E.) It gets "the job" done

The staff here is amazing and the service is awesome! However, the physical space needs a major facelift. It feels dark and empty and nothing like a normal, cozy starbucks. It's clean and well kept up but doesn't have any charm.

Staff is erroneous. I have never waited so long for a drink at Starbucks, even when it has been packed. To top it off, I recieved my drink nearly 5 minutes before my friend, and he ordered before I did. Drink was shamefull and goes hand in hand with the ill-founded workforce. Everyone who works there looks like they want to be anywhere else, but starbucks.

Nice place to study. It gets noisy later on in the day and especially when kids get out of school so bring your headphones!  The staff is very friendly and fast. They also have a water refill station near the bathrooms.  Only downside is nearby parking is metered so definitely bring those quarters!

Pros:  barista was friendly and the place was very clean. Internet worked well and was fast.  My drink I ordered (I always get the chai tea latte) was delicious as always.  Cons: someone else accidentally took my drinks so I ended up having to wait extra while they made me a new one. Not necessarily Starbucks fault but still a little frustrating.  Also they did have a small wait in line to order (about 5 minutes) which wasn't horrible.

Excellent location for a relaxing Saturday morning trip to the coffee house. The ambiance is enjoyable, the barristas are knowledgable, and the location is just right for walking around Downtown St. Pete.

The absolute worst experience I have ever had at a Starbucks!!! I've never experienced such rude workers in my life. I'm still in disbelief at what was said to me at the drive-up window. Will never go back.

These people messed the up easiest drink and were rude about it. I ordered a tall iced green tea with no sweetener. I received a cup filled above the brim with ice and then filled in with tea. I pointed out that there was practically no tea in the cup, they said it's fine and moved on. I guess I just paid $3 for a cup of ice with a splash of green tea. I'f I lived in Tucson, I wouldn't come here again

Lovely drive-thru location with friendly service. Situated in the same plaza as Club Fitness which makes it super convenient for grabbing my PreWorkout morning Joe.  There's plenty of seating both indoor & outside patio. Merchandise for purchase includes the typical overpriced Starbucks mugs, tumblers, coffee beans, brew systems and more. Cold case packed with grab & go lunch-a-bles, pastries, yogurt, fruit & so on.   ~Christy

I wish they had more seating. This place has a great warm & cozy vibe. I'm pretty certain if they had comfy seats it would be hard to get people (me!) to leave. I'm guessing it caters to the millions of people who work here in the Comcast Building (which totally makes sense) vs the occasional passerby who wants to sit & relax for a while.   Otherwise, it's a Starbucks. Just like any of their other places as far as drinks & food goes.

Not much to talk about during pandemic. Because the place become take out only. No dine experience now. But they do keep everything one direction. They keep the entrance in one direction and social distancing.   I have the dragon fruit mango lemonade. The drink is refreshing. They do get the drink correctly. Slightly need to wait. There was a lot of drive thru customers. My wife have the mocha frappuccino. It has a lot of chocolate in it. Rich chocolate flavor.

It's my 2nd time visiting this location, not crowded like the many other locations I've visited. Nice and pretty spacious. Compared to the other locations, this place is bigger. The staff is very nice and welcoming. I was even allowed to come back later to pay for my drink because all I had with me was a hundo, very considerate. The this I don't really like its that its located at the corner and not Much parking space, but it's ok, the more u walk the healthier u are ^_^. I ordered a venti green tea frappe and it was great! I even asked the manager, whom took my order, if she could make sure that the green tea powder was throughly blended. Two other locations I went to, I requested that, but it was not throughly blended. I can see there were still green tea powder at the bottom of my drink. But this place, the manger was very nice. She even offered to blend my drink twice to make sure there there were no green powder left. Thanks a lot for he bit of extra effort. My drink tastes great!!

I come here every now and then to get coffee (obviously) if I'm in the area. I've never had any problems with service. It's pretty roomy inside with the classic Starbucks cozy feel. They have a small area outdoors to enjoy your drinks. I like that they're open later. My drinks have always been consistent.

since the app said in store was available, i ordered our coffee/food via mobile order so i could just walk in and walk out.   instead, the doors are all locked, no signs indicating why and the drive through line is wrapped around the building and almost spilling out on to dale mabry.   if the store took responsibility to be honest with it's customers and remove the 'in store' from the app your drive thru line wouldn't be ridiculous and people wouldn't rate so poorly.
Ok. Service. Not a corporate location, so they don't accept Starbucks coupons. Not cool. :-(

I was in the store earlier today & things were busier than usual. A couple customers were being extremely rude to the employees, but they still kept smiles on their faces. Ashley & Andy were especially helpful, despite the stressful situation. My coffee was good, as usual.

So, I wanted to change my review from last time. I came back here to study but I realized that some of the tables were very wobbly and not in great conditions. Furthermore, the bathrooms (both of them) were very disgusting. As well, when I got my Pumpkin Spice Latte, there was already a lot of splatter on my cup, and the rim looked like someone drank out of it (though it is more reasonable to believe that the latte had overflowed and left its mark). However, I highly recommend the Pumpkin Spice Latte - very sweet and warm. Service was okay.

I love this Starbucks. If nothing else, go here just to look at the decor. It's so cool! The look and feel is totally New Orleans.  The bathroom had such amazing light fixtures.  The staff was friendly and the line moved quickly. The location gets busy so be prepared for some sort of wait- it's in a high traffic area.  They had a good playlist kicking too which definitely helps. If I were local this would be the only location I shop at.

I've ordered from this Starbucks a couple times during COVID through UberEATS. They make the order super fast and always get it right. Great customer service (I had to call once to inquire about something).   I also love Starbuck's company policy of allowing anyone to use the store and the bathroom. That's an amazing contribution to the public and I am thankful they made that policy a few years back.

This place is BUSY! Every time I drive by, no matter the time of day, it's busy. But, the staff is friendly and quick. The peach green tea lemonade is the perfect treat on a warm summer day. And you can never go wrong with any of their bakery items. I am quite partial to their scones! Yum!

There was no one in there when I came in, just walked in an ordered a simple grande iced black coffee. I waited 15 minutes. They were only filling mobile orders. I get that they may have precedence because they want you to use the app, but I was the only customer in the store. Why put me behind 6 mobile orders? This was the longest time I have ever waited at a Starbucks ever. Usually they all have great customer service, this was a surprise. Disappointed.

This is the best Starbucks in the area! They are so friendly and take effort to learn the names of regular customers.  They are also SO fast with mobile orders compared to other SB. The other day I went to pick up a coffee and saw a water on the mobile order cart. I asked about it because I've never seen water on the mobile order app, and the barista said you can't but she knows this customer always wants a water with her coffee so she puts one out with her mobile order coffee now. That's the kind of impressive customer service the employees have here! :) I've been going here for years and even though it's different employees over the years, the friendly customer service stays the same.

Meh...it's a Starbucks. What can I say. It's close to home but I've only been once and honestly I would rather drive 5 miles down Campbell and get some seriously bomb coffee.  I swore off corporate coffee a couple of years ago and every now and then I end up having to come for one reason or another. I ordered a Dirty Chai and it was horrible. Tasteless honestly. I could barely taste the Chai and there was no spice at all. It was probably the blandest tasting Dirty Chai I've ever had.  That's really all I have. No love loss here Starbuck's

Just posting an FYI, you can use your Starbucks card BUT you can't use your rewards here. I've never experienced that so thought I'd share the FYI. They also don't have the yogurt parfaits, salads or sandwiches here. They do have the pastries and breakfast sandwiches.

I consider this my Starbucks, simply because it's so close to my apartment and I go there frequently. Located just off of 75th and shadeland in the strip mall with caplingars.  Everyone is very friendly! You always feel welcome when you walk in. Even if it's crazy busy and coffee is being churned out at a high speed. Drinks are always great! Never have an issue with those, and they will even make things that aren't on the menu, I love London fog tea lattes and the fact that I can still have them makes me very happy. The selection of food products always impresses, they have all the snacks, and baked goods. Plus if you want to get coffee swag on top of your coffee, this store is for you! The selection of cups is always abundant I think I have purchased 5 separate mugs as well as ground coffee to make at home! This is my forever Starbucks!

the guy working (trevor) was so incredibly nice and friendly. It's so refreshing to have great service beyond just a transaction. we joked around a bit how the computers were down because I know that can be a very stressful situation, especially for a crazy business like Starbucks. I just wanted to give Trevor a little spotlight for being a great employee (and an overall nice guy)

They will no longer offer the hidden menu. To the end they won't blend my biscotti into my drink.... Allergens are an issue I suppose but I feel that if you have mixers for nuts and one without you can avoid this issue. Just seems like the staff is trying to not work hard. Rare poor customer service for Starbucks.
The staff are very friendly here and they move people through the line quickly. Unfortunately the parking is awful in the evenings, but before 8am there's plenty of parking right outside the store.

Hours say open at 5:30am on Yelp. It's 6:36 and the door is locked with one person inside getting ready. Traveling on the interstate so just wasted about 20 minutes of travel time. Review is for hours only. Didn't get to sample wares.

Every single time I've been to this Starbucks, I've had an amazing experience. The staff aren't just nice, they seek to make my day. And they do it every single time. They have to have amazing leadership to pull this off every time. It's the way customer service used to be.

I'm a regular at this location because of the Ina construction but now that you can cross the freeway I suggest going to the location in front of Target. By far the SLOWEST service ever. Every time I go it's 20-30 minute wait no matter if they're busy or not. The drive thru is the absolute worst. Went inside to see if the wait was better and ended up standing there for 25 minutes. What a joke it is to have 5 people working the line but having 1-2 drinks leaving the counter every 7-10 minutes because employees are too busy talking amongst each other.  Seriously, drive to a different location. It's worth the extra drive.

We came here tonight for 1/2 price Frapps. The guy who took our order seemed nice & knowledgeable. However, the poor guy with Red hair looked like a deer caught in the head lights. Don't know if he was new, but nobody was helping him at all & it took him about 10 mins to make one frapp.  If I were in a hurry & needed to get to work I would've been livid! But that was very unprofessional of his co-workers to leave him hangin!

its my local Starbucks :) Love it because its pretty close to the house. I go in for my passion iced tea, unsweetened :)  Friendly staff, its clean :) Its a small one, but I think most of them are. Nice place. I like it. Its a typical Starbucks, but this staff is friendlier than most :)

This is the Starbucks that took over the well-known Flying Saucer building on Grand, near the SLU campus. It used to be a gas station, then a Del Taco. They were going to demolish it and then Starbucks came in.   You'd think the Starbucks would be huge but it's only half of the building. There are 2 community tables, some counter space, some random chairs around the sides, and only 2 small private tables.   Lots of light from the huge windows all around the building. Full of students.

I love this Starbucks!! The 24hrs is enough to make them better than all other bucks. I come here almost ever day. The drive through is always on bang though. They lose a star for the slow part but it's fine with me because I never just walk in. I have never had a problem. One employee even gave me her markout Try 3 pumps of pumpkin spice in a hit chocolate!!

Iwas In the restaurant business for many years, I love this location Britney and her amazing staff does such a great job I always feel like I'm at home when I go to this store I have to name a special thanks to Eli Brittany, Angelica, Gabi, Shana, Shannon ,Mitch and Shelby just to name a few of the awesome staff there...

Okay I'm shocked!!!! It is the Busiest time of the year & Starbucks have 2 employees working!!! Tomorrow is Christmas Eve Day!! I really feel bad for the young man making all the drinks!!! The young lady is at the register and trying to help and keep customers happy. I figure STARBUCKS is either cheap or no one else would work. Just makes NO sense!!!

The  barista   seemed bothered to take my order, didn't ask me my name, which was Olay, because they were not  busy. I was standing waiting for my drink and she put a drink up on a higher Stand, didn't make eye contact with me so I  didn't think it was my drink, and then went back to making another drink that I naturally thought was mine , she made that drink and then with a big smile called for "Justin "and said  your drink ready!  Then looked at me and with her eyes pointed at my drink like "there's yours".,but she never said a word.  I will say that she was  RUDE RUDE, RUDE!  Drink tasted fine.

Very busy today! We had cold brew with salted Carmel cold foam and cold brew with sugar. Loved the salted Carmel foam drink! Cold brew with regular sugar is grainy, ask for syrup. The accidentally made ours twice so we ended up with two for ones. Good day.

Pretty much your typical Starbucks. Nice outdoor seating area. They get my order right and are quick.  It can get quite busy when the school kids all storm in the store at once.  If the weather is good, venture down to Wydown Park just a block east on Wydown. Its usually very quiet and has a lot of nice benches.

Vivian ist meine Lieblingsmitarbeiterin! Sie ist sehr süß und glücklich :)   Meine Schwester und ich fahren durch der Drive-Thru und wir fragen nach ihr. Vivian hat ein schöne Stimme und immer macht sie under unsere Bestellung korrekt!   Unser Hund liebt Schlagsahne und wann wir zu Hause zurückgehen, isst er ein bissen Schlagsahne hahaha!   Vivian weißt es, also sie uns mehr Schlagsahne geben. Seh nett!  Ich empfehle das Starbucks und fragen Sie sich nach Vivian!!   Tschüss xoxo

Great Starbucks! The outdoor seating is an awesome place for some good people watching. After reading other reviews, I found out that this place is open 24 hours. Knowing this, I'm sure I will be visiting at 1-2am someday in the near future.

The Asian barista made a great iced double shot so we ordered another. But the young (teen?) barista lady ruined the next one. Strongly suggest baristas taste the drinks they make, it will give an idea how good or bad you make them. Great place to read and has extended room for customers!

I've never had one Starbucks screw up so many drink orders multiple times! In the drive thru no less. People choose the drive thru in order to save time. Having to turn around and go back inside completely takes away from the experience. You've been warned.
I love it here. They have made a few mistakes on my drink but they do anything with in their means to make it up. The staffs awesome.
Amazing service!!!! Had exactly one dollar to buy a reusable cup and they used tip money to pay tax for me!! Very quick and very friendly!!

I'm usually not one to review Starbucks since they're all so similar, but this one stood out to me. The cashier taking my order was really knowledgeable and helpful with my questions. I ended up ordering the iced guava white tea lemonade, unsweet. The barista who made my drink, Parker, was super nice and personable. She struck up a conversation about the drink and enthusiastically gave me recommendations for other drinks I might like. The drink itself was really good- I'd definitely recommend getting it unsweet. I'd definitely come to this location again, for the staff alone!

The service was awesome and fast! Everyone was nice and greeted us.  My card wasn't working and they made sure I was still taken care. When you need coffee the last thing you need is delay. And they were awesome!!

Panic attack in a parking lot.  I feel like this review is long overdue.  Every single time I leave the Brentwood Starbucks I feel like I've been through a battle.  Not only is the parking lot a nightmare, making it difficult for the people going in as well as the drive thru, but the service is unbelievably slow, they get orders wrong all of the time, and the staff either doesn't want to wait on you, or wants to talk to you all day.  There are problems here.  Most people don't have 15+ minutes to dedicate to waiting for coffee!  I hate writing negative reviews but being that this is the Starbucks that is literally 1 minute from my house, I've been coming to this location for years and It's only getting worse, this review is justified.  Lately I've realized it actually takes less time for me to head to the Rock Hill or Webster Starbucks than to wait on Brentwood.  The other locations have me in and out within 5 minutes no matter how long the line (which is awesome!)  Brentwood needs to take a cue from their fellow stores and step it up a notch.

I've been a hardcore Starbucks coffee drinker for years now. Wanted to take the time to acknowledge and thank the staff of this particular location because they are all super friendly and work as a team so great. I've been to many Starbucks where you may have a select few top employees but whoever the manager is of this location deserves a raise. He picks a diverse staff that all work great with each other and create a very enjoyable atmosphere for their guests. Thank you - keep it up :)

As far as corporate coffee houses go, this my be my favorite in the city. Its a great location to meet my clientele and it just has a nice vibe to it that doesn't exist at other city or county locations. You know you are getting a good coffee and this location is spread out enough....more than most places.

Not a bad Starbucks location. The inside is nice and clean, but the outside (mostly the drive thru area) aren't too great. There isn't enough room in the parking lot for the often long drive thru line. Overall though I must admit...I'm getting really sick of Starbucks over priced drinks....when most of them are sugar/milk/ice rather than actual coffee.

I use to love this location, it was convenient for me,  until today!! OFFICIALLY NEVER COMING BACK. I went through drive thru and had a worker named Ana. I had to turn around to go in (which was my problem), and when I came I explained what happened. The two, Ana and a coworker came off very rude. The girl made my drink, and wrong, which I didn't drink after even buying it because I did not trust it. The other girl rung me up etc. and I payed. I still didn't get my change. Ana bring me my bread and drinks and leave it all the way on the other side of the counter and she says here you go(expecting her to hand it over because that's what a normal worker would do) so I wait. She says here you go again and I just grabbed it because I am done at this point. I then grab a straw for my drinks, and I'm getting looks from them. And I walk out the door and hear a very rude voice saying Have a Nice Night... of course from Ana. And then I hear my change being put into the tip jar, which THEY DID NOT DESERVE, even the little amount. I was shocked that she didn't remind me of my change... I would honestly like to see surveillance to be notified that my drink was safe... i am very disappointed and have no plans in making a trip back here, I would rather drive further.

With so many starbucks in this area it is easy to avoid this one. Staff is not necessarily rude, but they definitely aren't nice. I felt like them making me a drink was an inconvenience to them. I'll go down the road to the smaller Starbucks where even when it's super busy, my drink is a priority and my presence is acknowledged in a positive way.

I love this location. I grab my caramel macchiato and head to my favorite spot, the long couch in the back, the only drawback is there isn't always a table available when needed. Hey it's kinda expected because of location though. The staff has always been friendly and the bathrooms pretty clean. Quick Fyi: When trying to sit and study at times there music is pretty loud so if you need/prefer quiet this may not be your spot.

Based solely on mobile orders: I used to frequent the 5th street location for my mobile orders but I started to get a super short pour on my drinks so I abandoned them and headed to Liberty.  So far so good! My order is usually waiting for me when I walk in and if it's not it's always just a moment before it's ready. Drinks and food are up to the Starbucks standard I expect and the staff is friendly too. Words of advice - if you park in the spots parallel to the drive-thru and it's rainy you will step down into a good couple inches of water! Good thing I was wearing my rain boots that day.  Generally, I avoid the lot and park in the spots on the street anyway, much less headache.

Alright, where to start....  I love Starbucks. I usually visit them in a daily basis to procure my morning coffee and on rare occasions food. However, their corporate model to prioritize mobile orders over in-store orders is atrocious. Why? Because putting mobile orders first, for individuals not physically in store create a bad customer perception. There is nothing worse than sitting around watching mobile orders stack for people who have not made it to the store yet, while people like me who are currently in the store are forced to wait. I hope that this model eventually changes.

After visiting the Starbucks at Ina near Oracle, I'm happy to say this is definitely our favorite.   It's clean, my arms don't stick to the tables, I don't have to lay down a placemat of napkins and the garbage is never overflowing. Most of the staff are great!  Some of the newer baristas need to get a rhythm going when taking care of customers.
Our fav. weekly "hang out" place to wait for an hour when my daughters have piano lesson! Nice store, well kept & nice staff! We'll be back next week!:))
This new Starbucks location is very spacious and welcoming with plenty of indoor seating as well as a small outdoor seating area. The staff is friendly, as usual.

Gotta tell you I hated starbucks before coming to the US.. I loveeeeeee this coffee and their breakfast sandwiches I'm telling you their chef is brilliant.  Now go in here in the morning my hubby is here every day  - Jeff has learned so many customers names - the man is just a cutie - All the employees here seem to love to be here and I actually get in a good mood knowing I am grabbing a coffee here...   The staff makes it!!!! Thank you for caring

Typical Starbucks except the need for a code to use the restrooms. Had a cafe americano with cream. Barista put in significantly insufficient cream. Think they put in a literal drop!. Cake poo was fresh and delicious. Busy place.

This is one of the nicer Starbucks in the area...right now they are just drive thru because of COVID which is understandable and I appreciate them taking precautions. They have great customer service skills and I have always been satisfied with my drinks which is not the case for some of the other Starbucks I visit. Before the virus I used to sit in the cafe and get some work done however there wasn't much work getting done because one of the baristas named josh (I think) is quit the distraction...but anyway all in all it's a great stop.
I wish starbucks was a smoke free establishment! I love sitting outside on the patio in the evening to study but it gets ruined with cigarette smoke and i usually end up leaving at this point. :(

This Starbucks is on my way to work, and I frequent it a few times a month.  I just get coffee, "Venti Bold", and don't do the fancy drinks.  Unlike most locations, this one is an odd nexus of incompetence: 1. About half of the time I visit they are out of bold coffee.
