You would think they would learn to always have enough on hand, but the incompetence prevents it.  They prefer to take my money, then tell me I'll have to wait for them to make some more. Lesson: when patronizing this store don't pay until your coffee is in-hand.  Wawa is three blocks away, and keeps their urns full. 2. Front counter service is the least of their worries. You know how most Starbucks locations are energetic and the counter person is eager to trade caffeine for money?  Not this one  They actually move slowly, respond slowly, and speak slowly.  It can take a while before someone notices a person waiting at the counter. 3. I've never seen the General Manager on-site.  I think that explains a lot about this location.

Standard Starbucks, but I like frequenting this one because there seems to be outside seating when I need it.  Drinks are good and I have no problems here.   Drive thru can get out of control at times and make the parking lot more difficult to navigate.

We thought the service was great. The lady who took our order was new. However considering how busy they were I thought she did excellent under pressure while maintaining a really friendly demeanor! Even though I had a complicated drink order, my drink came out correct and delicious. It took about five minutes for our drinks to be ready, but like I said, they were busy, and Carla did a phenomenal job juggling all those orders.

It's Starbucks ok? I frequent this one since it's close to where I'm staying until my apartment is open so I get my Cafe Americano here! Grande, to be clear. No frills and no extra anything any it! (I believe the fact that I don't order 95 things to go in it freaks out the blue dyed chick in the window every time) Makes me smile though!   Restrooms are typically clean! I subtract one star here because with gender neutral you get the worst of both worlds, men smell worse and contrary to popular opinion, women trash the bathroom worse! So if they checked it once an hour instead of once a shift this would probably be one of the better Starbucks in Boise!  Lastly, service is pretty fast! I know, I know, my drink is plain of course it's fast! But no the people in front of me are getting theirs quickly and accurately so no complaints! Go get your java on folks!

Hey do you want the worst Starbucks experience ever?! Then definitely go into the Starbucks at the Doubletree hotel in downtown Nashville. There were 4 employees and about 6 people in line. Not only was it the slowest Starbucks but it was also the least friendly. Hey gals, you're in a customer service role, guess what that means you're basically an actor. So when I asked "Hey how are you this morning?", before ordering my drink...it's you're job to pretend like your day is going well-not roll your eyes and ask what I want.   Also how many baristas does it take to make a grande toffee nut latte? It takes 3. One to make the drink, one to play therapist as she slowly makes the drink, and one to quietly say the order when it's done....so quietly the other customer and I had to make sure we were getting the correct drink. I completely understand talking to your co-workers but when there's a line of drinks waiting to be made and food being ordered, leave the personal woes on the back burning and do your j
Super friendly service with a smile. I cannot remember the gentlemen's name who took my order, but he was super nice and made my day.
Great location with comfortable outdoor seating right off the interstate. A recommended stop if traveling I-74. Stop here before you drive into the Indy metro area if you are heading east.

Please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please please clean your dirty bathrooms!!!!

I come to the Starbucks at least five times a week, I sit inside for about an hour and a half and get probably 2 to 3 refills on my iced tea. I have never had a problem with attitudes or my drink wrong at all, this is my go to Starbucks in Tucson. I do have to say that lately the homeless situation at the Starbucks is not good but it's never really affected me. Also, you cannot hold it against them, it's not like they like to give out water or let them use their bathroom either. Maybe they could get locks on their bathrooms like some other locations in Tucson have?? Sarah Is one of the best employees and shift leads that they have. Always professional quick and extremely hard-working, I have seen her have to deal with countless problems and am always impressed.  Thanks for always giving me the caffeine fix i need!

SUPER SLOW SERVICE regardless whether it's 6:15 am on a Tuesday morning or 10 am on a Saturday morning. 15 minutes in the drive thru is completely unacceptable! I come here because it's right by my house but it's SO inconvenient because they're always SO slow!

I go here a lot because it is so close to my house and right next to Trader Joe's which I frequent but every time it is so crowded - with a line around the building for the drive thru and a line inside as well. The parking situation is terrible. You basically can't get to their parking because of the long drive thru lines so I park in the main parking lot but you have to walk across the drive thru line to get into the store (they've now put fencing up so you have to walk around). At least half the time I am in there they either completely forget to make my drink or make me a frappuchino instead of a latte (seriously why has this happened on more than one occasion?? Latte sounds nothing like frappuchino). *Sigh* but i'll still keep coming here because #imlazy and a #coffeeaddict. Positive notes? The coffee is good when I get it, sometimes I get a free drink out of them messing up my order because otherwise they just throw it away and they offer my pup a free puppuccino usually!
Great customer service on every visit.  The employees ate always so chipper.  They make my visit thru the drive-thru great.

Have had good experiences and just okay experiences here. Most of the time it just depends on who is working cashier that day. Generally, they get the job done but for some reason my dragon drink never comes out right here in comparison to the Starbucks in IV--the coconut milk is always separated and I definitely recommend saying light ice. I also wish they carried blonde roast and that you can mobile order/use rewards. I understand they're not corporate but why no blonde roast?

I came in to this Starbucks and their mobile ordering had gone down, so there were a handful of angry patrons. The staff here handled the rude crew with grace and patience, and kicked into high gear to get everyone taken care of. Not every patron was grateful for their hard work, but I hope the staff knows that it didn't go unnoticed. It's almost like people get crabby without their coffee! :)
New location. Service was quick and friendly. Food and coffee were as expected from Starbucks

I go here all the time and there is literally NEVER the same employees... very confusing... not sure if it's a training store or what. Sadly my cloud macchiato was completely wrong today ...  Even more sad I expected it because my orders have been messed up in the past. Every employee I've come in contact with is super nice though.

Mixed feelings. This opened up as a replacement for the Starbucks that closed across the street. Huge, new and clean as a whistle.   The sad part is the drive-through addition. The old Starbucks was a neighborhood gathering place. People knew each other. The outside area was a prime place for families. This one is a bit sterile. The drive through location is an affirmation that the beautiful town of Media is joining suburban anonymity.

So I bragged to my hubby about the caramel brûlée latte and finally convinced him to try it. I have had these  at different locations from NJ to NY without any issues. So we tried this one this morning and brought them home to enjoy with breakfast. To my dismay they did not add any caramel syrup nor the brulee topping. What a disappointment as I had my heart all set on that delicious cup. I gave them 3 stars because the young lady at the drive through window was very sweet, so service they had down packed, but consistency in making the order,  not so good. So disappointed as this is the closest location to me. Will I have to travel further to make sure my order is made as it is supposed to be? In their defense, they did offer to remake them but I would have to make the drive again and was not really feeling like doing that on my day off as I already have a full schedule.

I've been coming here for about 4 years pretty regularly during my time as a Penn employee and student.  I always noticed that I would get my best service in the morning, during the normal hospital/school/work rush (730 am - 9 am) and at night service was slow as they must put the new people on at that time to train (because who is rushing for that 6pm cup of joe).  I went away for the summer and now I have returned to school to find that this Starbucks has apparently put their night shift on during the rush hour. Two days in a row I stood waiting at the counter as someone forgot my very, very simple order of a tall pike with a shot of espresso.  Literally I am standing there, starring at the workers as they hustle to make everyone else's mocha-choca bullshit and here I am just waiting for my drip coffee.  Both times I asked what was up after about 7 minutes and they are like "Oh, what did you want again?" WHAT?!  And before you ask, yes, they apologized and as a consolation prize, I got one extra half of a multigrain bagel... after waiting 20 minutes.   WHAT!?! x 2.  I have never ever experienced anything like this at a Starbucks and especially not in a high traffic Starbucks.  I will literally never come back to this location and I will steer people away.   I will also post this review on your Facebook, once I find it.

Horrible service.  The manager needs to be fired.  She was abusive and berating with her staff both Saturday and Sunday mornings.  On Saturday, I stated that it was very hot.  Her answer should have been, "let me adjust the temperature."  Instead, she said, "I know it is crazy."  Her employees tried to help but she was not managing properly.  Almost passive-aggressive with her criticism.  I suggest coffee bean up the block instead.
Certain baristas consistently place my coffee on the counter edge nearest to them and furthest from me. I'm barely five feet tall; I'd like to not have to climb across to retrieve my caffeine.
We waited for half an hour and every single part of our order was messed up. Literally every drink.

Stopped at this Starbucks on our road trip to Jacksonsville.   This is one of my favorite Starbucks. I used to stop here every week on my way towards Daytona for a work project. I like my coffee hot and they always make my grande,  non fat, caramel macchiato. no foam, extra hot latte perfectly.

This is one of the best Starbucks I've been to in a long time. The food comes out hot and fresh and the drinks are made by expert baristas. The speed is good for the area they're located in and it's amazing!!!!

Just across from the City Centre Airport (ECCA) and the Air Museum you'll find this comfortable Starbucks. It's not too far from downtown if you were looking for a meeting or workplace with loads of free parking. I would come back to this one.  Busyness - pretty steady traffic coming in. It's a busy road with a grocery store nearby.  Noisiness - about the usual for a Starbucks. The blenders are noisy. It's summer. I've noticed there aren't many soft surfaces to absorb noise in most of the Starbucks I've ever gone to.  Ambience - It's comfortable and bright, and the decor is typical Starbucks. It seems to be popular with other laptop users. The air conditioning is just right. Blondie is playing in the background today.  Power - Not too bad. There are outlets for laptop users, but some of the middle tables don't have outlets.  Free wifi - Yes, of course, and it works well.  Staff - Lots of staff here and they're friendly.  Seating - There are 16 seats inside with two comfortable armchairs. The tables are a little small for much more than a laptop and a coffee, but there's one large table that works really well for laying out a bit of work. It also has a small patio with a couple of tables and an umbrella next to the drive-thru.  Security - As there's a lot of traffic, it would be wise to watch your laptop and belongings. I know this area borders on an part of Edmonton that's not the safest.

Sloooooow!!!! Went in when the drive through line was 20 cars deep (yes I counted).....so I went inside. I was immediately helped and then waited over 30 minutes for a grande cappuccino!! Apparently the drive through gets priority and then online orders. Don't make the mistake of going inside. Won't be at this location again. The male worker at the steamer didn't make it any better with his death glares at all the customers waiting for their drink.

This Starbucks is conveniently located in a mall near my house; I went there this morning to get some 'steps' in on my Fitbit.  The guys working there are really friendly and my Venti, Non-Fat Cappuccino came up as ordered and right away. They didn't even laugh when we realized the gift card I had only had seventeen cents left on it.  I had their Reduced Fat Turkey Bacon Cage-Free Egg White Breakfast Sandwich with low-fat Vermont cheddar on an organic whole wheat English muffin. I'm really not that yuppy, I got it because it was about 210 calories.  The sandwich came up pretty quickly and in general, it was pretty good, tasty and hot... it's just that they ( like lots of other places) don't cook the bacon!   Honest, I cook turkey bacon at home all the time. You can get it crispy!​  Good friendly service, healthier sandwich option and pretty good value...

I've been into this Starbucks once before, but only briefly. I'm impressed by the space as it has a high ceiling, lots of windows and decent sized tables. It's not a residential area, so it seems like most of the patrons drive here. It's a well laid out, comfortable place.  Busyness - For the middle of the afternoon it's fairly steady and a bit of an older crowd. I guess there must be no schools nearby.  Noisiness - Not bad. About average for a steady Starbucks. The drive thru keeps them busy evidently. The music is definitely set to background level. I like that.  Ambience - Typical for Starbucks with modern, coffee-shop decor.   Power - Power is available to pretty much all the seats, except the center ones. Wouldn't have far to stretch your power cord though.  Free wifi - Available and works well. No connection problems. A touch slow though.  Staff - It's adequately staffed with the usual, friendly staff you expect at Starbucks.  Seating - There are 17 seats inside and a patio with about 10 seats. There are two comfortable armchairs at a table and the square cafe tables are big enough you could probably fit two laptops with mice. Five of the chairs are at a bar, and I've never been a fan of them. In all the Starbucks locations I've been to, I'm not sure I've ever seen anyone actually use them.  Security - Seems fine. Not a bad area of the city, but you know the drill.

The people that work here are nice. They are friendly and smile frequently. But 80% of the time they get my order wrong!!! I do not use the drive thru either. I always walk in. I think that some of them are stressed out from the volume of customers. Relax dudes, it's only coffee!! I don't mind a little wait! I won't return here. Been here a lot and It's not worth it anymore. Pantano/Speedway is a good one as well as Broadway/Craycroft.  Bummer.

What the hell, Starbucks?   Lately, I've begun going to Second Cup more often. Not because I like their coffee, but because they're quieter. I used to like this Starbucks a lot. No more.   I went in here yesterday. I just wanted to sit and relax and write. I usually have my earphones with me, but had forgotten to throw them in my bag.   Lately, it seems like every Starbucks I go in has one barista who has to be SUPER SOCIAL, the loudest, most obnoxious person in the entire joint. Yelling to be heard over the coffee machine? They're not that loud. Everyone in the entire place DOES NOT NEED TO YOUR HEAR YOUR EVERY WORD. Customers do not need to appreciate all conversations between baristas!   It's going to be a LONG time before I come back. And that's sad. I used to like this place a lot.

This is a great Starbucks location. The staff is always great and friendly. It's also a great workspace all day long with nice couches. Just be careful as it's a popular location during rush hour: The drive through can backup onto 4th Street and the parking lot fills up fast. Luckily there is an overflow parking lot next door.

This is the slooooowest Starbucks I've ever seen anywhere. You could leave this one and go to the Mt Carmel location and get your order faster. There should be a contest for worst Starbucks in the country.
Working online!  Great place to work!! This Starbucks has alot  To choose from coffee drinks snacks..great layout With different seating areas clean restroom...friendly enough staff!

Super slow!!! But super friendly. There were only two cars in front of me and they didn't have a massive order or anything but it still took 15 mins to get my drink. That's a bit excessive for a drive thru. Sure, I could have mobile order (which I usually do) but this location said they were out of what I wanted on mobile app but really they were not out. My frap was melted and my tea looked like it was sitting around for awhile. I'm sure they made the drinks right away when I ordered. They just took a long time through the drive thru. Anyways, I'm sure I'll be back to this location if I'm in the area.

The workers have always been friendly to me. No problems with any orders. Pretty quiet outside usually, away from traffic. A few times people have been really loud outside and start yelling and that can get annoying. Some people need to realize that they are in a public place even if it is outside on the patio, it is not a park. There are not many parking spots but I have always found a spot. Some people do not like that there is no drive through but I don't mind that.

The bathrooms were absolutely disgusting. One had no toilet paper whatsoever and the other one had an explosion of trash on the floor. When I informed the worker at the counter, all I received was a heavy eye roll and an "okay."

This is a really nice Starbucks. Get service, always good coffee. The only reason I gave it a four star is because of its location and drive through, it is slow moving and I've had to wait in the parking lot not the drive through lane until It starts moving. But over all it is a very good Starbucks.
This place rocks! Great friendly staff who are efficient and fluent in coffee drinks. A+

At first glance, and being an out-of-towner, I couldn't tell that this location had a drive-thru. It's situated behind the location in what looked like the Bank parking lot. It was very busy and the line slow, but the excellent service and my drink (recommendation of the cashier) being so good made up for that.

So I've come here on several occasions and they always seem to be "out" of something. So this time I'm here and I go to order a cloud macchiato and the girl ringing me up says they're out of the cloud powder that they make the drink with. So I said to her, "it seems like you guys are always out of something." So then she proceeds to tell me that all the Starbucks in Brandon are out. I knew that was absolute BS the minute she said it bc how would she know that? Did you call all the stores to find that out? No. I didn't appreciate being lied to, especially since I went to the Starbucks in the mall minutes later and they made it for me. Sad.
Please redesign this location drive thur parking is awful.  Also order a cappuccino and it barely full, when I specified fat free milk & sugar free syrup.  So what happened to three quarters or so??

Computer isn't able to properly process payments through the app. Don't ever have problems using my Starbucks app but came here two days in a row and suddenly I'm having those issues. Don't have problems at Elmwood location or at a Covington location in which I frequent OFTEN. Won't be back.

This sleek modern architectural design of a Starbucks building, invites you to pull on in.  And if the Starbucks itself doesn't pull you in, then the connecting Chipotle may!   Yes, I'm a sucker for some Starbucks and Chipotle.  I don't think I've ever consumed the two in one sitting, but I'm open to the idea now.  Neighboring the Saint Louis University campus area, this Starbucks can become very busy at certain hours of the day.  Students flocking in for their caffeine fix and plopping down with their Mac Books.  That being said, I wouldn't count on every visit being a breeze.  You may end up waiting a little longer than had you choose a different location.    There's not a vast amount of parking available for the Starbucks in particular.  In fact, there's barely any.  You may need to end up parking in one of the neighboring lots, and turn an eye to the signs informing you not to park there.  Hey, sometimes the rules need to be broken in the name of a latte.  Luckily, there is also the drive thru option available at this location, which nicely wraps around the building.  *Drink of the moment: Iced Soy 2 Pump Sugar Free Cinnamon Dolce Latte.  Is that a mouth full, or what?  Yeah, a mouth full of deliciousness!

It was my first time in this location and It has been a very long day  The gentleman on the drive through was amazing he had great attitude great customer service he cheered me up and told me I was doing a great job  Who ever you are keep it up ! You're making people's lives better

11/24/19 - just wanted to give Christina the thumbs up. Very sweet and hard worker. Made sure everyone was taken care of while cleaning up the area.   Old My experience today was less than wonderful. A young lady took my order then got her food and drink and went on brake. I had to ask another barista for my drink. Then I asked the first one where my sandwich was. She pointed to the barista at the register and said she'll take care of it. The other barista had no clue as to what I ordered. This has never happened here. I quite disappointed.   3/01/19 - Eddie's last day. He has been a plus plus for this location. He will be missed immensely. ;  2/12/19 - So disappointed to hear that our wonderful asst manager Eddie The Arizona Pavilions location will be leaving us.   1/11/19 - VERY disappointed that they didn't have my favorite sandwich today, chicken and artichoke  11/20/18 - So disappointed today. Not one sandwich in the cooler at 11:15 am on   Once again we are here. Our favorite place. Love our Baristas. Jessica, Treigh, Caleigh, Alex and Taylor. If I've forgotten anyone please forgive me. So many great people work at this location. :) And of co

The Starbucks was very neat and clean. The outdoor seating is nice, I don't like sitting inside all the time because I get cold easily. More than I can say on others. I had great service, I ordered a strawberry refresher and it was really good, just enough strawberry!

Love this particular Starbucks it is much bigger than the other stores in the valley and it is right next to axiom nice to come her in the morning after a workout. Service is always awesome and there are plenty of funny and interesting people! Saul is the nicest guy! He's the Mann!
Love Starbucks!  Must say that the some of the Baristas at the location near Loft need to learn their menu & pay attention!

It's Starbucks so I can't say much. I enjoy a latte now and again, but of course, it's pricey. This particular location can get really congested. Staff is okay; they don't quite have the friendliness of most Starbucks baristas I've encountered. I wouldn't recommend coming during peak times, or if you're in a hurry.

Love this Starbucks, love these baristas. Jacob is THE BEST - friendly, fun, remembers drinks and names - he brightens the day. Carrington remembered us from a previous Starbucks and is really good to us. It's our favorite Starbucks!

New Starbucks on the block. As it's pretty new it's still pretty clean and quick moving. Good amount of staff working today, quick service, clean counters etc. They have cold brew on tap if that's your thing.  There's a good amount of tables, but lots of people in and out so might not be the best place for you if you're looking for a quiet space to work.   Overall, pretty good as far as Starbucks goes.
I have never had a bad SKINNY CARAMEL MACCHIATO (the only thing I order haha) here. The service is always super friendly and quick! Love this place.

Ok, went back to see if maybe my initial 1 star review was a bit harsh. Nope it wasn't. Extremely long wait, staff is moving but nothing seems to be getting done. Lots of frustrated customers in the store. District manager has let this store go down the drain. Sad. You can always go up the street to Mama Java's for local business, but you will wait there too.

Ordered a strawberry funnel cake Frappuccino. The barista wiped some errant whipped cream off the bubble top with a dirty rag and handed me the drink. She then proceeded to wipe the counter in front of me with the same dirty, wet rag.  Gross.  Emailed Starbucks and was credited $10. Greeeeat.  There's a Starbucks a couple of block down,  across the street that doesn't contaminate your drinks and let me use points.

Worst Starbucks I've ever been to. Took 20 min to get an iced coffee and a cold brew in the store. Then, half and half was empty so I asked for a refill and it took another 20 minutes for that. In the mean time, I asked for some half and half and they wouldn't even give it to me from the counter; "we have someone on it"... uh ok so I have to wait a total of 40 min for an iced coffee with half and half? Yeah ok Starbucks, no thanks.

Look it's a good starbucks, but you know.. it's just Starbucks. Chapterhouse is 1/2 a block down 9th.. COME ON!  Tip: buy yourself a starbucks giftcard and register it online to get 2 hours of free continuous internet at starbucks (have to use your card once a month).

I'm not a frequent visitor to Starbucks but I don't mind this location when I'm in the mood for a drink.  I came with my coupon for a free drink to celebrate my birthday.  You can sign up online and they will mail you a card for a free drink with a 2 week window to use it.  It was a sunny day and I was craving a sweet drink.  I was glad to see no line up (this location seems to be a lot quieter than the one 2 blocks west) and I asked the barista what drink would be like a frozen hot chocolate.  I didn't catch the name of the drink but it sounded good so I ordered it with soy milk.  The only thing was, he should have asked what size I wanted instead of giving me the grande.  I probably wouldn't have been able to finish a venti though as it was a filling drink.  The barista who made my drink was friendly.  I enjoyed my drink as it wasn't too sweet but it had little pieces of what seems like chocolate bits.  I found it was very filling as well and I wasn't hungry for food after.  I wish I got the name of the drink, but I'm sure next time I can ask the same question and likely get the same drink.
Every time I go there and get serious strawberry they always have someone new and they always mess it up they need to just close down
Drinks were made really well, super convenient to stop for gas and grab a coffee. Mocha Frapp & Vanilla Latte on point!

This location is closed. Starbucks opened up a new building with a drive through across the street and slightly west neat the Racetrack at 7141 E Fletcher Ave. I hope they do well there. However, I can't imagine getting in and out during the evening commute will be too fun.   Steve is great!  They have Nitro too!

Not my regular Starbucks but I stop by every couple of months, I've noticed within the last year it seems to have taken a downhill turn. The couple of staff I recognize are always friendly and polite, but they constantly seem short staffed and thus it really slows down service compared to other locations. There's ample seating but it isn't my choice location.
This Starbucks location makes my coffee right every time, which not a lot of locations can say! The staff is also very friendly and efficient :)
If you hate your life at Starbucks you shouldn't work at Starbucks. 2 girls behind the counter were sharing a brain. Flies and garbage drinks.

hanging out in a coffee house/cafe to do some work while sipping a drink and possibly a snack?  Having to feed a meter the entire time is not the best plan.  This place you have to find a metered spot and then feed it.  Meters are expensive here.  The store is not as warm and inviting as many other of these and it's not as nice looking inside either.

Worst one in Tucson, went once had horrible experience, tried again a year later, still went bad, asked for a no water extra ice green tea no sweetener in a venti cup.....instead I got a black tea in a grande that was overly sweet.... Don't hire kids, there stupid....

Always wonderful. I recommend the skinny or regular mocha. Kids love the frappaccino. Using the app makes service and payment so much faster.   I am not sure how I ever lived without it. Service is consistent with the exception of one location at Tesson Ferry and Gravois in south St. Louis. The coffee was not hot and took forever to get a plain coffee.
Standard Starbucks pretty new looking. They are slammed, long line in drive-thru but not much they can do about that. You can always go inside
Sad  all the dead plants on the patio. No more couches on the patio. Doesn't look like too many people frequent this location at least on a Saturday evening.

We walk in and the four employees in here are all working the drive thru and standing around looking for something to else do then we walk up and order one of us heads to the restroom (you need a code 51111) and the floor is filthy the paper towels are gone and/or on the floor with with four something to do don't you think they could replace the paper towels, so I tell one of the baristas and she said "ok I'll let them know" as if they have a janitorial staff!   After another guy uses the restroom and comes out with dripping hands in front of everyone and asks them for towels they finally replace them but don't do anything else to clean up in the nasty restroom.  And this is when brand new places bathrooms go down hill fast!. The restaurant area was also never tidying up during our visit.  7 at night, not busy, no one swept, pushed in chairs.  4 employees, so sad!

There's a 50/50 chance that you will end up with the wrong order going through the drive thru. Take it from someone who goes to Starbucks almost daily, this location is horrible. The drive thru takes FOREVER and then they rush you through just handing you a random drink that's not yours. I've kept track, and they are currently sitting at a 50% accuracy. They under fill every drink and someone's they taste horrible. I avoid this location and only go if I'm shopping near by. Save yourself some trouble and just go to McDonald's down the parking lot and get cheaper coffee that's made correctly. Corporate needs to be on this location NOW. They are starting to lose valuable customers because of the customer service and wait times.

I have literally been coming to Starbucks my entire life and I have never had such amazing service as I have had today. Jessica is so sweet and attentive, she set my morning up for success. Everyone else was super nice as well!

We stopped in this Starbucks on New Years Day on our way thru Fishers.  This place was hoppin'!  The crew inside were extremely friendly and very warm and welcoming to us.  We ordered two Zebra Coffees, a cheese Danish and a coffee cake.  Both of the pastries were warmed without us having to ask (bonus). The coffee was prepared correctly and tasted delicious on this chilly morning.  The only thing we disliked about this location was the parking lot, it is quite small and not easy to navigate with the drive thru -as you can imagine - people in the drive thru line are afraid you are going to steal a spot from them, so they block you trying to park or turn left to find additional parking.  Overall nice experience!

Using the drive through is ALWAYS a delight. At this particular location they seem to "get it" and always get my order correct. In a world full of ups and downs this is a constant- yes. In honesty I have never stepped foot "inside" as I am usually in a hurry. But as far as accuracy in the drive through-- it is great. Thank you team.
Great staff and excellent service.  Fast, organized and clean.  The staff remembers your name and greets you when you come in.  Thumbs up!

1/11/19 - VERY disappointed that they didn't have my favorite sandwich today, chicken and artichoke.   11/20/18 - So disappointed today. Not one sandwich in the cooler at 11:15 am on   Once again we are here. Our favorite place. Love our Baristas. Jessica, Treigh, Caleigh, Alex and Taylor. If I've forgotten anyone please forgive me. So many great people work at this location. :) And of co

Served caffeine when I ordered a tall, decaf capp - in the early evening. Did not sleep!!! Starbucks - do one thing and do it well. Serve coffee. Plus, the milk and sugar stations were overflowing with disgusting trash during this visit and a previous visit earlier in the week. This Starbucks is not good.

If you want to be chastised by Eve the barista about your clothing, go here.  I was having a tough day and stopped in to get a latte pick me up.  Instead what I got were questions from the barista about how I was dressed and it made me feel dumb.  I was embarrassed and it was really uncalled for.  Starbucks May need to Work on their customer service training.  I will never go back to this location again.

This Starbucks has personality. I went in yesterday and one of the employees was singing and everyone else working was super happy and very engaging with each customer. Last week, I went through the drive thru and after they said they were out of the Oprah Chai, I ordered a regular one. When I got to the window, they told me it was their treat that day. I like free coffee obviously, but more than that I like employees who are happy and take the time to notice ways they can take care of their customers. I've always had great, speedy service and my order has always been correct at this location. Only downside is they keep it FREEZING inside. Bring a jacket. Thanks!

Not only did we have to wait in line for an extended amount of time but we were only allowed to order grande size drinks and they were out of the drink we wanted and out of all 3 food choices we wanted. On top of that they charge almost 50% more for their items than regular Starbucks and don't offer use of the rewards program...so for 4 drinks only grande size and 3 petite scones and 2 sandwiches and 1 order of egg bites it was $48....I have never spent over $37 at a regular Starbucks and that was with Venti sized drinks...lastly the gentleman who took our order messed up my drink order and didn't seem like he really knew what he was doing. Very disappointed won't be going back to this Starbucks again!

I will say this, the staff here really does care about their customers. If they get something wrong.. the staff is more than happy for fix the problem for you. The bathrooms could be better attend to but I can understand they get busy and it's not always easy to get over there to clean them.

Okay well I found like a million of these ball like things in my drink....I'm absolutely disgusted. By the time I discovered it I was already at work and couldn't do anything to get my drink fixed. Just gross!!!
Nitro cold brew. Enough said. Glad this Starbucks had it and let us try a sample of it. Friendly staff. Glad to have stopped by.

They mumble names here, and served my drink to someone else. Their solution was to have that person give me my drink... I would expect more given the high level of service from the Starbucks where I live, but the employees were too busy talking to each other behind the counter to notice the customers trying to ask questions at the counter.

Happy to add a star from my previous review. Ordering ahead on the Starbucks mobile app for pick-up in store is super convenient. I've enjoyed their almond milk honey flat white over the past couple months and always enjoy indulging in a holiday drink. This location's drive thru is usually pretty long but it goes pretty fast. Seating inside is currently closed due to covid but you can still go in and pick up your drink if you ordered ahead.

Possibly one of the friendliest overall staffs of any Starbuck's I've visited! They make you feel like family if you are a regular and know many people by name. They are always smiling; no matter how busy. Manager is a friendly fellow named Aaron and is always ready to listen to any comments or questions. They've never gotten an order wrong. Lastly, I love the utilitarian vibe here!  Note: I didn't take off any stars for parking but like the other Edwardsville location, it can be tricky leaving. Remember that you can't get out by going around to the driver-thru entrance. You can get out by going back to entrance or through the hotel parking lot.

Corporate office needs to evaluate this store! Slow service throw drive thru. Don't get orders rite the first time, I have been coming to this location nearly 6 years and the last year or so has been with soo many problems occur

They have never messed up an order, but they need to update their hours!!! They are not open until 11 pm on Sundays... I pulled up to the drive-thru at 10:00 pm and they told me that they were closed for the night! I was kind of sad after I drove all the way out to Edwardsville just for Starbucks.

This is the best Starbucks I've ever been to, and I've been to every one in pinellas county. I have been coming here almost daily since they've opened and I love every person that works here. They make the experience even more than just getting coffee. They call me by name, they know what I like, they all are so sweet and fun to be around... I've never had a bad experience here - ever! They are very efficient yet mindful and they always make my day!! Thank you, All!!!

Consistently dirty but close to where I volunteer so it's where me and my wife would come for a quick coffee.   Then one day a very rude man stared down my wife (I wasn't there at the moment otherwise you'd be seeing a news story not a yelp review) and when she asked from across the room "do you need something?" He just nods his head and mumbles "uh huh".  No break of eye contact and no remorse.   So as she gets up to leave he literally assaults her by grabbing her arm and she has to yank it away and ask him to quit touching her.   When I heard about it I came back to see if the man was still there and he was not.   Needless to say I'm just about fed up with these types of folk who frequent these establishments.   I don't place all blame on Starbucks as how could they know this would happen but I do feel like there could be some better people management of who loiters there.   After hearing about the new policies going into affect it makes me wonder if this won't get much much worse.   And if I even tried to be specific or upset I'm sure I'd be labeled as the bad guy. So who knows. I guess just have to take "buyer beware" to consideration.

I am a huge starbucks customer and I have to say that this is my all-time favorite Starbucks location. I've gone to this Starbucks location for probably four years now and have never been disappointed or had any issues with the service. All of the employees are extremely kind and welcoming, the space is always very clean and well-kept, there is plenty of space, & the service is superb. Joey, who I am assuming is a manager, does an amazing job of ensuring that the customers are taken care of and leave satisfied with their visit. When I do come to this specific Starbucks location I am usually there for multiple hours doing work and I find myself choosing to come to this specific location over any others, even if it is 15 minutes away while a Starbucks is right next-door to me, just because I enjoy this location that much more. I can definitely say that they are doing a wonderful job, and that I feel extremely welcomed and valued as a customer of this location. Keep up the great service guys!!
Been going their for years until the other day,gave me a toasted bagel with mold Very disgusted
Clean shop.  Orders taken quickly and correctly.  Coffee arrived hot and fast and perfectly made.
dis is rlly gr8 fam da ppl noce the air is noce and cold and dem drinks are too. you can ask if you want soy or coconut in your drink.  the tea is free refils if yu gawt dat green stateus

I love Starbucks cold brew, but definitely into the pumpkin cold brew right now. I'm going to be honest and say that if there were a local coffee shop near this Starbucks (which is by my work) I would definitely go to the local shop because I'm 100% supporting small business. However, Starbucks is a lot closer to my work and just convenient. This one is fairly small so it does get kind of loud unless you can find seating outside

My first review of a Starbucks.  As a shareholder and frequent traveler, I use SB as my default coffee place.  Mostly for the consistent product and ease of access.    Anyway, I was excited to move to Tucson and have a Starbucks within a mile of my house.   I have been frequenting this particular SB 5 days a week for three months.  I have to say,  I absolutely stopped going.    Here's why...........  1.  I can wait at the front counter like an idiot for 30 seconds to a minute with no one greeting me, looking at me, or helping me.  Doesn't sound like much,  but seems like an eternity when you have 5 staff just walking past you.  2.  After three months, over 180 visits,   same order every time.   Every Time.       They act like they've never seen me before.   "Do you want cream with that?"   Yes.   Like the other hundred plus times. FYI: Grande Pike with lil room.  Not too tuff.  3.  They have dropped off my coffee at the counter,  not looking at me or saying anything and just keep walking by.  No ty, have a nice day, here you go.....   wow.  So... yesterday I go to Ina and Orange Grove SB.  Walk into the front door.  "HI Guys!  What can I get you?".  Day two.....  "Welcome Back!"  Found my new Starbucks.  It's worth the extra .5 mile walk.    I do miss the nice porch and the guy that drives a Harley and listens to his headphones every morning.

Extremely friendly staff. This is only my second time here, and the guy taking my order remembered me and my obsession with Peppermint Mocha.  Orders come up quickly. Nice sized space with plenty of tables for dining or working. Outdoor space and clean facilities.

You can't ask for better service- the staff is dedicated to making sure your drink is perfectly made and given with a kind smile to boot! I love anytime I go into a business and the staff is happy and playful- and this Starbucks obviously has a great team and good leadership because they were enjoying their work- something rare today! Then there was the order- Very Berry Hibiscus Tea for me, my daughter opted for a coffee creation only she could come up with and they obliged her writing her own order on her cup- yes she is that type and Connor got his 4 pump only latte that every other Starbucks makes with 6 even when he specifies 4. Happy customers for sure!

42 people in line and 3 employees. I'm not complaining if it's a $2 coffee.  But if I'm paying $7 for my venti iced nonfat upside down caramel macchiato, I expect to have a half decent customer experience.  Little help here?

Great location   Atmosphere: very spacious and open. Comfortable seating  I ordered my usual half passion half black iced tea unsweetened. It was perfect.   They have plastic sippy cups now. I think it's great that they are cutting back on using plastic straws.

This location is awesome. The staff is consistently friendly and the best part is this place has a bunch of comfortable places to sit, and tons of outlets.   There's almost always a shortage of outlets at any cafe but this spot has you covered.  Also I just like the way Starbucks tastes. I can get a cold brew for $3.19 here where at the Well down the street they're $5.  I understand they are some kind of nonprofit but for a small cold brew that just feels too high. We're not at an airport or movie theatre sheesh.
Love the coffee but they are painfully slow. Ten minutes to get a drink once I've ordered and paid? Unacceptable. This is the slowest location I've ever encountered.

WHY go to starbuck's when you can support local coffee shops? This place literally doesn't need to be here, and the workers are clearly NOT from the neighborhood nor do they live here.   Here for supporting workers but this is isn't the hot take.
Third day in a row, ordered mobile 10 min ahead and- 18 min and still waiting!   Never had this happen at a Starbucks!
The lady who doesn't brush her hair was being so rude and did not smile , was not polite. Made a nasty face because of my ethnic name. Rude. Definitely will not return.

Always happy with the service I receive at this facility.  And the location is perfect for me, my only complaint is that they don't have enough cushion styled seats so that I can go there and edit for hours. Barista Sierra is so sweet she almost always gets my order correct and that's just the best customer service I could ever receive!

I went in here this morning and I was already running late. There were 7 people in line in front of me so I felt pretty nervous. Ahh, I needed my coffee so I sucked it up and waited in line.  The line moved quickly and soon I ordered my favourite beverage: a skinny vanilla latte (and a tea latte for my boyfriend).  As the barista was finishing up the drinks in front of mine, the woman in front of me spilled her drink everywhere and it held up the line. The barista was really friendly about it, told her not to apologize over and over again and she quickly cleaned it up and continued on with her drinks.  I really liked this location: It had the speediness of downtown cafes but the friendliness of a neighbourhood coffee shop!
A solid example of a Starbucks. You get what you usually do at a Bucks. No surprises.  They keep it clean and kind, and the fireplace in the corner is cozy warm. Parking is plentiful.
18 minutes for an iced coffee? I was the only customer waiting for a long while?  And then they screwed that up too?    Come on, folks.  Wake up!

The only thing I like about this place lately is the food. Can't drink coffee and I used to love their Chai tea until they changed it to Orpha!!! It's awful!!!  The staff really needs to be on top of cleaning tables and emptying the trash cans. They're consistently overflowing. So sad. This used to be a beautiful location.

With the Fall season in full effect, cheers to welcoming back the Caramel Apple Spice cider! This beverage with a warm pastry of any kind is so clutch. Basically a caramel apple in a cup! My first sip was like walking through an apple  orchard with warm caramel syrup in hand to drizzle on each Granny Smith Apple I bite lol.   If you like apples, dont let this season pass by without trying one!

They recently updated this Starbucks location. It looks brighter, more open and modern. I don't feel like it added any more seating space, but now there are lounge chairs and some booth spaces as well as a longer table to accommodate individual people working. The two-person tables are round and a bit small if you're both wanting to work on laptops or have books to spread out so keep that in mind. I do like the look of the space and they did fix some of those nasty potholes so the parking lot's improved since my last review.
The coffee is always consistent. I love the pikes blend coffee. It's always clean inside and the staff is always pleasant. My only complaint is sometimes the wait is to long
This location is constantly understaffed .. wait time is absolutely awful ..morning , day of night ..

This is a great Starbucks location. It's right on West End, perfect for being checking out the Parthenon, heading downtown, or going to Vanderbilt. There's 3 other Starbucks location right down the road, so if you miss this one you aren't far away from another one. Their drive thru line is always fairly long, but they get through it pretty fast! It has a large indoor and outdoor space, great for studying, working and meeting with friends. The staff there are all very friendly, and helpful if you are unsure of what you'd like.   See you soon Starbucks!

Unfortunately the worst customer service...drive thru closed, no breakfast sandwiches ready (all frozen) even though they were displayed in the case but no sign mentioning they weren't available till 9am.  Btw, I waited in the long line a second time for this breakfast which I couldn't get!  Keep in mind there were 6-7 employees walking around.  On top of all that, the hostess was unpleasant (like she hated being there) and the coffee was bitter (which is hard to do with a PSL).  The line was exceptionally long due to the drive thru being closed, which remained closed for at least an hour.  Errrrr not a nice way to start the day! not worthy of even 1 star today

Normally, I have no complaints about Starbs, even when they mess up a drink occasionally. However, today drew the line. We went through drive through and everything seemed fine. Had to repeat the order, but that's fine. The issues is when we were paying and getting our drink. They gave me my coworkers first and the label said it didn't have SG syrup she requested. When the girl came to the window again with my drink. I asked for the drink to be remade since it didn't have the correct syrup. My arm was extended for longer than needed before the girl took the cup with a huff to be remade. She handed my drink to me and it didn't say almond milk on the label. I asked if it had it in the drink. She rudely, 'No, because you didn't ask for it!'. I looked back at her and told her I DID ask for it. I have a dairy allergy. I extended my arm again with the cup and asked for it to be remade with almond milk. She took a long time to take it the cup. When she came back, she had both orders correctly. This seems to be an on going issue for this Starbucks, making their drinks incorrectly. Every time my friend and I come here, we always have to have them remade. I don't plan to return to this location for a while just due to how rude the cashier was. Customer service is everything and this girl is clearly lacking in that department.
Every drink is perfection. I love the drive thru. It is quick. I have always had great servers at this location. They will even add extra toppers on the frapps if you ask. :)

The partner that just got off work ordered five drinks thus causing a already slow team to slow down even further. They first made my hot latte that I ordered cold. I placed my mobile order at 4:28 and I have been waiting til now for my mobile order (the time is 5:08.) They need to get a grip.

When to this Starbucks after having lunch at Pumpernick's restaurant.  There was no line. It was happy hour. My daughter and i were able to get a drink for the price of 1.  Great deal! My drink was made as a requested. I will continue to go to this Starbucks  when I have a craving for Starbucks

This place have kicked up there game recently. The lines are moving much faster now! I'm basically in here everyday now. The workers are amazing. They all finally got my name right! Yay!!!   So many drinks that I love but can't get all the time bc of the crazy calories: Salted Caramel Frap & Mocha- Delicious. I love the salitness and sweetness Double Chocolate frap with the choc whipped cream-YUM White Choc Mocha-Mmm  hahaha...I could prob list the whole menu, but I won't.  Oh, the breakfast sandwiches and pastries are wonderful as well. Give those a try!

Came on Saturday at around 830AM waited in Drive-thru about 20 minutes, I was late for work my iced coffee was ALL Ice I'm very Disappointed!!!!!! Get Faster waiting that long and Still having drink made Wrong Is crap!!!
Customer service was great , regular coffee with 6 splenda , ice , half and half was perfect cup of JAVA for me !!!
The store is typically short staffed and out of the basics like milk, napkins and a smile or thank you.  The only positive I have to post is the location.
I avoided this location for a solid 6 months then tried again.  It has certainly improved and is very much in-line with the Starbucks locations.

When a friend wants to meet the best choice for me is Starbucks The seating is very limited so I was happy to snag an open table!  My drink of choice is a Sugar-Free Vanilla Frappuccino During the pandemic I can appreciate the measures Starbucks has taken to ensure the environment is sterilized, safe, and efficient w/amazing customer service!  Coffee makes me happy

Alright, here's the deal. It doesn't take a brain surgeon to realize I prefer local coffee over corporate coffee. I think it tastes better, I like the look and feel of local shops, and the warm and fuzzy feeling you get when supporting a local biz owner trumps all the cake pops in the world (although damn, those cake pops are good, SBux.) That being said, sometimes I have to make a choice based on where I am and how much time I have. Occasionally (not often) those factors land me at the Wydown Starbucks.  It's not a bad place to be for a few different reasons, #1 being the space. This Starbucks is huge and there are many seating options, including couches, tall tables, big tables for groups, and also seats ideal for people working alone. There's also a cool little setup of tables outside which I can appreciate on a nice day. Secondly, the employees are so nice and efficient. When I throw my order at them of a doubletallicedsoysugarfree vanilla latte, they don't even flinch and it comes out quick and just how I like it. Obviously, also, the free & reliable wi-fi doesn't hurt.   The trouble happens in the 3:00 hour. Whatever schools are nearby let out sometime around then, and it's as if someone opens the doors to the schools and says "release the hounds!" Students from middle school aged to high school aged come barging through the doors of Starbucks, likely looking to consume more sugar 'cause they probably haven't had enough already, and it's literally the loudest place on earth for about an hour. Girls crowding over each others phones yelling "OMG! I CANNOT BELIEVE HE SAID THAT!" Boys poking girls in the side causing the girls to shriek at scarily high levels. It's bad. It's funny to look around at everyone in there working because they literally can't believe their eyes (and ears.)  If you've gotta go corporate, this Starbucks is a fine choice. Just go in the morning or early afternoon, and if you choose to go around 3... well, don't say I didn't warn you.
The service was great and I received my drink quickly. The iced coffee I ordered tasted like how I hoped it would :)
Gave attitude and was short and snappy! Poor customer service. Better off going to other coffee shops in the airport!

A far better experience than my last visit here a few years ago, the service was excellent and they delivered a great product thankfully...   It's no mystery that Starbucks is by far my favorite stop for coffee and other selections that are spontaneous dictated by my cravings at any given moment. I have visited literally hundreds of Starbucks' location since they've come into existence and although they all seem to be pretty consistence with the quality of the products they offer; the service, quality of the product, wait-time, cleanliness and accessibility of the location are the five points I will rate all of them on going forward - so to make the point I've recently incorporated a rating system based on this short list to calculate the rating of the locations I visit. I feel all these criterions are controllable by the corporation, the franchise owner or store manager.  Service - 5 Stars Quality - 5 Stars Wait Time - 4 Stars Cleanliness - 4 Stars Accessibility - 3 Stars  Rating 4.2 Stars

This Starbucks does not know how to make the drinks. I order a double chocolate chip frap and it was watered down and was barely chocolaty at all. Horrible plus bad service when no one was inside or in the drive thru.

Update the damn app!  Hate it when I decide what I want and order it.  Then when we get there that item us out of stock!  So exasperating.  Started coming to this Starbucks because the other store didn't update their app either.  Would change if there was another store in the area.  Grrrr, just update your app when something is no longer available.  Please!  Otherwise what is the point of having a mobile app to order ahead?  Blah!!
Best Starbucks experience! Fabulous service start to finish. Delightful. Wish I could remember the gentleman's name but nay. Carry on!

I love the free wifi, lounge chairs and outside seating area! It feels like a real coffee house. The part that needs work is to have an electrical outlet by each table or in the table/on the floor - this is my mini-office and I can be here all day - java it all up - yet need better electricity outlets! Love the coffee, service but wish the price was more reasonable.
Dirty place, counters are filthy, baristas are not attentive, all tables needed to be clean

Awful experience here. I grabbed one of the pre-made sandwiches and realized it was past expiration, I told the cashier before I paid for it that I wanted to switch it for a fresher one and he made a big deal about walking 3 feet away to grab a new one. Then when I asked if he would heat it up for me he rolled his eyes at me. Meanwhile I ordered a green tea, and as it was less than 30 degrees out I didn't think to specify that I wanted a hot tea. I received an iced one and the girl who handed it to me kindly fixed the error. But as I sat and ate my lunch the guy working the register openly (and loudly) bitched to another coworker about me specifically. He complained that customers are so LAZY because it's OUR job to notice that the wrong style cup was being used for our drink order......I didn't acknowledge him, just left. And won't be returning.

Very slow service. I write this review as I've already been sitting in the drive-thru for 15 minutes. Also, they seem to always be out of Trenta cups. What's up with that? This seems to be the slowest, most disorganized Starbucks I've ever visited. I happen by here about once a week, and it is consistently poor.

When you have a broken machine customers who aren't having a drink made from that machine shouldn't be told it will be a wait. An iced drink should take a matter of seconds to make. Maybe having a plan to organize your workers for a situation like this would be more efficient.

I visit this Starbucks all the time because it's right across the street from my office. Because I visit here so often, the baristas know my name, why  I really like! :)  Other than that, it's your regular Starbucks store. There is no drive through. It is kept pretty clean too.  There several schools near by, so at 3pm, this place can get pretty crowded with students and that gets annoying.  The baristas have fun personalities too if you get to talking to them!

The new coed bathrooms are disgusting. I don't know why Starbucks did this but they're gonna lose what little business I am still giving them because of it. The bathrooms were never very clean but now they're truly disgusting.

Now I know why Starbucks does not mix the Frappuccinos all the way! It's so the drinks can look pretty waiting for customers to pick up their orders! My favorites are the matcha green tea and Chai Fraps!
You just can't go wrong with a cup of hot coffee and a bowl of oatmeal in the morning.

Not the best Starbucks.  They seem rushed even when there is not many people.  As usual this time of year at all Starbucks they are out of the ingredients for the special holiday drinks, but they do not make a note on the board or on the drive thru, so you have to go through the order and "sorry out" a couple of times to get an order in.  And unlike other Starbucks they do not make an attempt to offer suggestions, cut the price, or substitute a similar ingredient, they just leave you hanging with minimal apologies.  There is indoor and outdoor seating that his facing a busy street.  The location is convenient to some shopping, ie Trader Joes and Bookmans, but not a great location to just take it easy.  If you come here it is only, obviously, to get your chain coffee drink and to move on.  If you have a minute or two drive a couple miles east to the Speedway and Pantano store or to Broadway and Kolb for the recently remodeled store.

Terrible parking and terrible customer service from one of the workers here. She's super rude and didn't even greet anyone coming up to her. Just assumed what size I wanted my drink (which was wrong).

I came here on a Friday afternoon when no one else was in the drive thru. I ordered a matcha latte which was good but it's pretty hard to mess those up. The girl had to ask me to repeat my order since she was laughing with another girl over the drive thru while I was trying to order. I was a little annoyed but they got my drink out fast and got me on my way.

Worst Starbucks ever.  The drive thru is a consistant 10 - 15 minute wait.  I have tried going inside and it is just as bad.  I usually go to Garden State Park where the service is excellent, but on occaision go here because it is closer to work.  Everytime I am reminded why I don't go there regularly.  Once again, my order today after waiting 10 minutes was WRONG.  The best part is that the girl servicing the window said, "The woman in front of you ordered the same thing.  I let her pick which cup she wanted."  Maybe she should have read the cups, because we didn't order the same thing. She ordered Chai Tea and I ordered Chai Tea Latte.  Since I have learned to check my order before pulling away I am the lucky one.  The woman who grabbed her cup and drove off got a nice surprise. HORRIBLE!!!!!
Loved that they offered me a "Pup cup" for my dog when we went through the drive-up.  It made his day!

I've been to several Starbucks locations since recently moving to the area, & hands down, I'm giving this one an automatic 5 stars simply due to being the speediest, fastest, quickest - however you want to put it - Starbucks drive-thru that I've EVER been to. There was a long line to the road even, & I was in & out in 5 minutes from start to finish, including drive-thru time!!
This store just required my daughter in law to buy something in order for my three yr old grandson to use the restroom. WTH

I stopped in here to get a Macchiato and to have a little time to myself to read. It was pretty busy inside and a new person was still in training. After waiting a few minutes and everyone behind me already gone another young woman noticed me  waiting so she checked on the person making my drink, since  he seemed to be having a little trouble  she happily took the macchiato he was trying to make and dumped it and started over again showing him how it was done. I'm so happy she did start over again to make sure my drink was just as I  had ordered and expected it to be. ( to keep it vegan you just hold the caramel drizzle and use soy milk)

This is hands down the worst Starbucks I've ever been to. The workers are miserable and were loudly complaining about my order because it "takes so long to make." It's an iced green tea! Usually Starbucks baristas are happy and excited to do their job so I am shocked every time I come here hoping it'll be different. I guess the manager hasn't learned to hire the right employees.

I cannot stand that this is the closest starbucks to me - they hear my order correctly, i can see they do on the stickers they print out. But they never follow the instructions on it - simple things, like, "iced" or "hot", "nitro lid" and "no straw," or "sugar free." Slow down y'all

I arrived and the drive thru was long, so I decided to go inside.  I waited for 45 minutes for one black grande coffee!!!!  As I got bored, I counted the cars in the drive thru.  50 + cars were served before your inside customers.  This is unacceptable.  I am highly disappointed in my experience.
I use the mobile app to order and have to wait at least ten minutes for my order. Every. Time. In addition, the parking lot is a nightmare.

Decided to go through the drive thru and get breakfast on my way to work, took a bite of my ham and egg sandwich and the egg/cheese were frozen while everything else was warm. :/ Ended up going to work hungry. Human Bean is 1000x better.

Wouldn't recommend this place.  The cashiers are hit and miss.  It is more the overall cleanliness of the place.  The pastry case looked disgusting and there were flies on the register and around the store and it just didn't seem clean (715am visit).  I know sometimes a fly or two gets in in the summertime but there was 1 just sitting on each register and 2 flying around where the coffee is being prepared.  Gross.

So... I ordered a coffee and two pastries. No chocolate shakers by the mixing area. I tried to get the attention of a barista who was talking about her dental work to get a chocolate shaker, but she seemed too busy explaining her dental work to notice my tries to get her attention. Another barista saw me trying to get her attention and told me they've been out of chocolate for awhile. "Really? Then what do you use for the coffee drinks?", I thought to myself. It was around 7am on a Saturday, so not busy at all. The area where customers mix their coffee was pretty dirty with sugar and stuff. I don't know the coffee industry but shouldn't someone be on cleaning duty when it is slow? Oh, and the pastries were still wrapped in the plastic they came in. SMH...

In the middle and late 1980s, this particular location was a Café Paradiso. I was more of a tea drinker than a coffee drinker then, but every once in awhile, I went to Café Paradiso for their snacks, baked goods and an occasional cup or two of coffee. It was a nice place and I liked it a lot. Then, one day when I was working and going to classes at a nearby university, Café Paradiso was gone; it was converted to a Starbucks. And it's still a Starbucks. My Café Paradiso was gone! Quel horreur!   So now, it has continued to be a Starbucks ever since then, and it's not too much different than all the other Starbucks locations. Some have outside seating, and others don't have outside seating, this one has outside seating. The interior must have been remodeled because it's dark like everything is mahogany wood or made to look like that and depressing inside. I'm not sure what they were trying to accomplish with that, but for me, it doesn't work. It doesn't feel inviting and I didn't want to stay there very long.   They offer typical Starbucks stuff. They were limited on the Starbucks merchandise like cups and mugs. They had food offerings like made up sandwiches and what seemed like limited baked goods.   Parking is a problem now because the formerly free spaces along Wydown are not free anymore. they have an electronic pay station where you have to pay for the parking now. I don't know why they changed it, but it is now really inconvenient to park there now. Those electronic pay stations are a major pain in the ass because they take coins or cards, and you have to put in your plate number in addition to paying with either the card or coins. I hate them. Around the corner are the old fashioned parking meters which I prefer over that damn pay station. So just beware of that and you will get a ticket if you don't pay.  I won't be back anytime soon.

We stopped by the Channelside District Starbucks today on Kennedy, places our simple order (just ice teas) and they served 8 customers who care after us their orders (more complicated drinks). We asked to be refunded, no one apologize or cared & we left. Very disappointed! Willing to spend not easily earned money & completely disregarded! Wish I could give no stars!

SLOWEST STARBUCKS EVER!  I popped in for a gift card, decided on a coffee to go as well.  Time stamp on receipt was 2:17p; did not coffee in hand until 2:24p.  The inside service was completely in the weeds; drive-thru a priority for service (average time seemed to be 3+ minutes).  I recommend drive-thru if you're in a hurry.  Knew I should have gone to the one in Target...waiting sucks.

I ordered the egg sous vide and got a piece of black chunk at the bottom of the egg when I bit into the egg it crunched hard in my teeth . And tasted really bad I don't understand how you can get something like that with egg sous vide. And why do I always have to ask for a napkin? Is this Starbucks way of saving money?

I've been bad. I've been visiting this Starbucks more frequently that I should be, but it's all in the name of good health. Scout's honour.  One, it's exactly 4.0 km from my place, so husband and I have been taking more walks here in summertime. It's a sunny route and it's good exercise, and so this Starbucks has become our "halfway done our walk" treat.  I also go to Spa Lady in the same parking lot, so sometimes I grab a post-workout frap on my way home. Whaaat, I deserve it.  At the end of the day, a Starbucks is a Starbucks is a Starbucks. The same drinks, the same treats, the same amount of seating, etc. I just really like the location.

No drive thru here, but that's okay, what this location lacks for in size and convenience is surely made up for with the amazing staff. I came in on a Sunday morning to get my caffeine crave; and this location didn't miss a beat as it relates to service and the quality to come to expect from Starbuckies. This is truly your friendly neighborhood Starbucks.  Note: Parking is a bit of a a tight squeeze and there is plenty of customer seating outside.   Cheers,

Hellooooo new, conveniently placed Starbucks. Fellow SLUdents and I have been waiting for you for quite some time.     First perk: they kept the Del Taco building, I just love it, it looks like a spaceship.   Second perk: There is a drive-thru and they made it accessible to the shoulder going on to 64 so you don't have to wait at the stoplights just to get back out.  Third perk: The interior. So cool. The big "Saint Louis" light up logo, the all recycled wood surfaces and industrial metals with the Starbucks Reserve section is sharp. The seating is great: some high top tables, and  tables facing the all open window view. In a wrap up: it looks spiffy.  And lastly the fourth perk: The service was stellar today. I know it is only the second day but every worker was smiling, working hard, and not complaining. Even when it was jam packed (and it definitely was) this morning at 9 AM they were bustling about but not snapping at each other while they navigated the Starbucks station.     I like the atmosphere, the staff, the accessibility and, oh yea, the pumpkin spiced latte. I'm a happy camper.

I've driven past this Starbucks many times and thought it looked like an interesting place. I've never stopped, but today I found myself downtown and thought I should stop in and have a look.  Overall I'd say it's a pretty nice Starbucks, but it suffers from one fatal flaw as a work location (unless you live nearby) and that is the pay parking. I didn't spend a lot of time driving around looking for free parking, because I don't think it exists in this area. $3.00 an hour.   It's great if you live or work nearby and have the parking taken care of or are using transit to get here. Otherwise, if you're driving , forget it, unless have a meeting set up here. Work for a few hours and it's going to cost you, not to mention the inconvenience of having to go plug the meter. It's at the bottom of an apartment building, so I'm sure the residents know this location well.  Busyness - There are a lot of apartments around here so I expect this place is pretty busy in the morning. I wouldn't mind a Starbucks near my house.   Noisiness - For being pretty steady, it hasn't been too bad for a Friday afternoon. It's winter now, so I don't know what kind of crazy person would order an iced drink to get those blenders going. But, it is Canada.  Ambience - This isn't your usual boxy Starbucks. It clips off the bottom corner of the building so it has an unusual layout. The windows seem to line about half of the walls, so it's quite bright inside, with high ceilings.   Power - I didn't really see many outlets at the tables, so you may have a problem unless your laptop is miserly with the power.  Free wifi - Available and works well.  Staff - Friendly and efficient, as usual.  Seating - There are 21 seats here, with one large table to work at. If you're into relaxing in an easy chair, there are four of them tucked away in the corner and there's an electrical outlet there.  Perfect place for a power meeting or power nap. The rest of the tables are the square type with two chairs each. I like them much better than the useless round cafe style tables.  Security - It's a nice neighbourhood, but if you've read my other reviews you'll know I'm just paranoid about that kind of stuff.

This particular Starbucks is on the ball every day and I visit coffee shops on the regular. The lady in there by the name of Heather is a machine. She even remembered my order from my last visit. Now that's what I call customer service. All around I'm going to be making this my regular stop in the mornings.   Still all of the ladies there are very chipper this early in the morning and my drink is mad perfect. Well done.

Great service at this location. I'm not particular with my orders, but all of the baristas are friendly and quick and offer great suggestions for someone who rarely drinks coffee. Parking can be a struggle during busy times, but there's a good amount of seating. One really long table (seats about 8-9 comfortably) and then a cozy little spot near the front of the store with couches. A few other smaller tables that seat about 4. Good spot for groups or one-on-one meetings.

Visited this location for the first time and really appreciated the assistance from the manager Abby. She was really helpful with providing information on their new one step coffee brewer. Will definitely recommend this location to others.

Clean store. My drinks are always correct and delicious. However, the service is very slow. When doing a mobile order it typically says 4-9 minutes but I end up waiting 5-10 minutes for my drink AFTER arriving. They need more staff but drinks are on point.

This particular location has the absolute WORST customer service in the industry! From the consistently slow drive thru, incorrectly made beverages and unbelievably rude service this location has it all.

Tends to get crowder but I need my daily fix. I like this location because of the. CLose proximity to the turnpike and route 1. Also has outdoor seating and better parking than the starbucks that's closer to my office.

I love that it is connected with Einstein Brothers Bagels.  It is the nearest one to where I stay when I am in Tucson and having a drive thru is an awesome perk.    The only down side to this Starbucks is also a plus...The Location. It is great that is so close to where I stay but in the morning getting out of there can be crazy with the work traffic.  However that has never stopped me from stopping and getting my morning cuppa.

As usual we got our fast and very friendly service.   Loved the contactless service they are providing along with their reliable regular customer service.  They have no touch card scans and no touch on your cup.  Masks and gloves were used also.  Way to protect customers Starbucks!
This location has consistently performed below expectations. They not only are the slowest, but often do not produce the drink ordered.

My star rating is low because this is the smallest and busiest Starbucks I've ever been to. At any time you would expect to find a working person get coffee you can expect the line is out the parking lot,  across the street, and backed up to the light. I can't imagine how much business they are losing simply because of that. If they opened up another location in the area that would greatly help. I'm also not sure if they changed their to go cups but mine are always leaking! I have coffee stains all over my car :( Fix the location problem, fix your cups and I'll be a happy camper

I come to the drive-through of this Starbucks several times a week, and it has to be the One of the busiest and one of the fastest Starbucks drive-throughs in the Tampa area, if not all of Florida. They are always busy in the morning, but the wait is never long and the service is always friendly!  Part of me doesn't want to write this review because I don't want is to get any busier here in the mornings.

super slow there were 3 cars ahead of me and it took 20 minutes to get my order. that is super slow service for any drive thru. to top it off, the "all day menu" ran out of many many items. really really disappointed.

This customer service will turn any smile upside down!  The staff is very smug and unfriendly and I leave felling horrible about not only my experience but spending my hard earned money in exchange for an attitude, behind the bar!  Wow!  Unbelievable.  NEVER going back.  My time is valuable and I rather share it with people who are positive and friendly!  And Grateful!

This used to be my favorite Starbucks. It was relatively close to my house, I liked the town it is located in, I loved the staff and I loved sitting outside on Lake Afton. Alas, on my last visit, the life had been sucked out of the place. We couldn't use the loo. The staff was uninspired. And we couldn't even sit outside... Thankfully, I have a better SB in my own town. Good bye Yardley.
Do not go here on Saturday morning!! Slow, slower, slowest. 25 minutes for a cup of coffee is abominable.

Friendly staff. Convenient location... Often there is a bit of a waitMy only real compliant is they run out of Cold Brew 60% of the time... Why not plan and make more cold brew? I feel so petty complaining when there are way bigger problems in the world today... this just seems like an easy fix.   Update- unfortunately I spend a lot of time waiting for orders or drinks here. I usually get plain coffee, cold brew, or green tea but somehow there is usually at least a 10 minute wait... the staff is super nice and it's close to home so I keep coming back but after todays 12 minute wait with 1 person ahead of me ...
