I think it is probably faster to drive to a nearby Starbucks or coffee shop. Since it's been a consistent issue I'll go somewhere else and try again in a few months. Maybe something will change :-)

I used to love her...but I had to kill her...    I just tweeted: CRAP! GOT DRINK WRONG - AGAIN! Ordered 130degree...didn't take a sip for 15 minutes & STILL BURNED MY MOUTH!!! Now I'm YELPING!....  C'mon y'all! You guys know who I am!  Grande decaf 2pump mocha, steamed at 130!!!!  Do you know you only steam it at the proper temperature 65% of the time now???  I used to go to Starbucks on Fowler but they only got it right 30% of time. You guys used to be great! What happened? :(  Oh, and BTW, it's D-I-L-A-N! LOL ;)  P.S. Nichelle, miss you! :)

Everyyyyyy sinngggllleeeeee timmmeeeee I go they mess something up to the point where they mention it at the drive thru "I know this happens often" uh no every time update your mobile app so orders are current so you don't get pissed off people and have to refund the entire order then provide a courtesy " next ones on us" gift card

Slowest Starbucks I have ever been to.  It took them about 5-6 minutes to take my order, even though there was no line.  After I ordered, I waited for a while to get my drink (they said the drink sticker didn't print).  Not very friendly either.  Barely made conversation and didn't acknowledge my drink until I specifically asked for it (again which would be okay if it was crowded, but it was me and one other guy in there).  I don't know if my standards for Starbucks are too high, but I am used to having much better customer service than this place.  I will say my drink turned out okay so I gave them 2 stars for that!
I love the fact that I can get a Starbucks and shop at this Giant.   My experiences have been generally positive.
this is so much different than all the other starbucks i've ever been to. oh my god, have you been here. you totally have to check it out. it's so great.
friends of mine call it 'fourbucks'  i tried to order like a regular cup of coffee, and was confused. i can't believe i can buy a cd of the music they are playing if i wanted to.

Sad beyond sad service  The people are nice but have not been trained properly.  In order to get your order right, expect 3 remakes and losing 30 mins of your life   Mama's Java- please extend your hours and save us from misery.  Seattle - Please save these poor folks...pitiful

Isn't the point of mobile order to get your order in line for preparation?  Twice this week I placed my order 10 min prior to arrival. The app said would be ready in 6-8 minutes both days.  As I entered I noted who was in the regular line.  Both days people who placed their orders after I arrived (about 10 min after I placed my mobile order) were getting their beverages and mine hadn't even been made.  Yesterday my order was ready 20 min after originally placed and today it was 17 minutes.  Very frustrated.  I get it is a busy location....but both days it looked like they were picking and choosing what drinks to make...not necessarily in order placed.  And to top it off today they were out of vanilla syrup at 7:30am...poor planning.

Just went there and staff was extremely sweet. My family and I had Joy at the drive thru window. They actually covered our drinks, thanking us for being patient. Funny thing is, their service was EXTREMELY fast and no patience was needed. Thank you to Joy and the rest of their amazing staff for such a sweet gesture and clearly being so caring to their customers. We love you!

I'm a fan of Starbucks. I am a huge fan of the new feature in the Starbucks app for ordering ahead. You just order, go to the location, and get yours off the counter. Efficient.  Here in #Nashville, a couple of local #Starbucks spots have AWFUL lot designs. Starbucks (West End) and Starbucks (Dominican). West End has a massive and dangerous drive-thru line sitting in the road all morning. Dominican has not nearly enough parking, creating a free-for-all need to invent solutions on the fly. Sometimes, this results in a cranky customer yelling at another, like I was yelled at this morning. Surely causing your customers to bicker was not part of the brand promise, Starcrack. This lot also has a dead-end into the oncoming drive-thru traffic, complicating the discovery of the lack of adequate space even more. (Starbucks Smyrna is also guilty of this one.)  I realize the previous Councilwoman from my area of town is credited with securing the Starbucks (and neighboring Lexus) deal, but clearly the planning was poor. Kinda what you'd expect, given her reputation. I was actually going to take this issue on if I had run for Council, especially the West End part.  So the question is, what are you guys gonna do about it??

This Starbucks seems to stay pretty busy. Lots of traffic but they seem on top of things. I have a certain way that I like my coffee doing Keto. I've only had one barista to make it where it tastes different and not in a good way. Everyone else that's ever made it tastes the same. If that barista is making coffees and I happen to go in there I will just wait to get my coffee.

I ordered a Nitro Cold Brew with sweet cream. The first attempt came back as an iced coffee. I returned that and it was replaced with a poor facsimile of the advertised product. I have ordered this drink at least 10 times in the past from other Starbucks and it is always properly prepared and delicious. Please do some training to ensure product delivery that justifies charging $5+ for a coffee drink.

Had to update my review because the service was so good today. Not sure the barista's name, but he is always so kind to me when I come in! Always greets me! I wanted a chocolate croissant, but noticed all the pastries were out of the display and sitting out on the counter so I asked if there were others and he took a new one out of package for me. I ordered my drink wrong and didn't realize until I got it and he was so nice about having it remade. I ordered an iced green with no water and meant to do classic syrup instead of cane sugar (since they changed it). My tea tastes perfect.

It's true this Starbucks is truly a night mare when it comes to parking and going thru the line. However good news as they are building a new one out by the high school and SIU WHICH SHOULD DRAMATICALLY REDUCE THE CONGESTION PROBLEM. on the other hand I am more than pleased with the baristas. In particular the lady who normally works the day shift. She's on the ball, in fact remembers my reg order. The best thing you can do is order ahead thru they app. It's always ready and I cut her run thru the drive thru or run in and pick it up. F

This Starbucks is so clean, friendly and has a very nice vibe to it. From the moment I walked in until the moment I left. I love that they have outdoor seating and different kinds of chairs inside to sit. I also love the big drive thru sign -u can't miss that! Love the high ceilings and the newspapers on the tables for you to read. It makes you feel as if you belong.

Last time I go to this particular Starbucks.  Woman who helped me took my order and just left the cashier.  I stood there for a good few minutes and had to ask the neighboring cashier what happened.    Cappuccino barely had any liquid - majority foam.  Go to a different Starbucks.

Worst Starbucks ever! Do not visit. I wouldn't of even given them a star if Yelp didn't make me. I'm here on a Sunday morning when they should be at their best and their baristas are nothing but rude. I ordered 2 drinks both of which have had to be remade at least twice. The barista at the bar thinks she can speak rudely to the customers as well. Make sure not to speak loudly, otherwise you will get yelled at. If you can skip and move onto the next Stabucks I would.

This was my first time visiting this location and I already wish I lived close by to it. We came at 6:30am and they have been non stop busy since we got here (its now 7:30). They keep the line going fast and have a great routine going on behind the counter and get the drinks out fast. I'm impressed and again, really wish this was the Starbucks in my neighbrhood or even my town. Deborah is very kind and makes a point to make conversation with customers while working very obviously hard behind the counter. Andrew is also nice as we sit at the counter he makes sure to make conversation with us and others while working. James took our orders and was conversational, kind, and made a point to get our names and call us by our names. This is a great starbucks location with great workers!
It's bad enough that Starbucks has a one person only bathroom, but this one takes the cake for being the worst! They have ONE bathroom ONLY now! This is so inconvenient when you have to go!
We had never been here and randomly stopped by on a road trip. Go through the drive thru just to see Bob's smiling face. You won't regret it, he's the man!

Went here tonight for coffee for myself and my boyfriend... horrible experience. We came through  the drive thru. The lid was not put on my boyfriend's late properly, he took a sip and it poured all over him and my car. Went back to inform the store/Batista's and was treated poorly. Was told it was not there responsibility for coffee spilled by the customer. I explained the lid was not put on probably and very upset about what happened. He said the were closed and I had to leave. I asked to have a replacement made which I did get plus a refund. I am just appalled by the way I was treated with disrespect by this employee. What's ironic is the person in front of me in the store when we returned was in there because his coffee was made wrong and had to come back also. Obviously there is some room for improvement here and need for customer service training. Will not be back

The app says you can place an order to pick up except that you can't and by the time I got my drink through the drive thru it was cold and so was the sandwich I order I typically like Starbucks but this was ridiculous

In the drive thru today, this Starbucks had two jars outside the window. One said "yay Trump" and one said "Nay Trump". Both had several dollar bills in them. I asked the barista if this was something that was done every Inauguration Day or just this one. She replied that they did it during the election because everyone had a strong opinion. First of all, where is that money going? Leave it to Starbucks to make money on a divided country right now. Where is the "glad Obama is gone" jar? I find it totally offensive that a company that charges me $5.00 for a large iced tea now wants to make money on the divisions in this country that they profess they want to see unite. I'm guessing the CEO of Starbucks only wants a unified country if his candidate is president. I have gone to this Starbucks a few times despite their terribly slow service and have overlooked the many times they have gotten my order wrong, but after the Trump tip jars I saw today, I'm finished. I will not be going to this Starbucks, or any other one for that matter, again. They choose to bring their political opinions into their business, then I will choose another business.
I have just visited this Starbucks location once and have had the java chip frappe.   The consistency was great. Will go back again

This has got to be the dirtiest Starbucks in Tucson. The floor was full of dirt and pieces of what seemed to be some white debris almost all over the floor. People were stepping on this and carrying it to other places.   Every single available table needed cleaning and there were a lot of hangers-on concentrating in their laptop and doing maybe homework. Some of these had nothing on their tables that were sold by SB.   What seemed to be homeless folks just walked in and out to just to walk around. Gladly the restrooms were locked, but any employee could have given them the numbers to get in. Restrooms needed some minor cleaning.  My grande coffee was really a short with foam taking 75% of the cup, the weight of the cup gave it away. Should have complained but I just wanted to get out of there.   We enjoy SB from time to time and I must say that except for most tables always needing cleaning and folks just using their laptops, this on was a rarity. Thankfully!

Most people don't like change and there is a feeling of security by knowing what to expect before you even buy something. This is why this Starbucks is so popular. You have all these great little coffee shops all over this city and yet Starbucks still gets people in here. My only guess is that most of their costumers just moved into town and that they are afraid of trying someplace new or local. They are comfortable paying $8 for a coffee that they have had back home in NYC or LA for $11.   No one, costumers nor staff, seem happy to be there. No one looks each other in the eye nor attempt to make small talk. Maybe they are pissed about having to pay $8 for a coffee. You won't get this type of experience at local coffee shops.

I had a great experience with Amy this morning. I messed up with payment and she went out of her way to make sure I got some coffee in me. Excellent customer service, wonderful experience. Thank you again, Amy! You made my morning!

This isn't my first choice for Starbucks in the area, but it's up there on the list! They seem to stay pretty busy, but any time I've gone in to work for awhile, I've been able to find a seat somewhere - though it's been close once or twice.   Sometimes the drive thru is slow. Sometimes my order's not quite right when I get it, though it's more a matter of how they make it rather than receiving the wrong item. The parking lot is currently hard to navigate between the pot holes and tight lot space. But despite these few "issues", I enjoy this Starbucks location and keep coming back again and again. The friendly baristas and chill work space make the occasional hiccups worth it.

Starbucks is by far my favorite place for coffee! We have lived in Boise for the past 3 months now and this location is the closest. I honestly have never been to a worse Starbucks. The staff isn't that friendly and the drinks are made terribly. The first time we went I just thought maybe it was an off day but no. We went twice after that and drinks still aren't made properly. I'm not sure why this location has 4 stars but it's definitely not worthy. If you are in this area and think, "Oh great a Starbucks!" keep driving until you find a different one.

This is one of the better and more consistent Starbucks around town. The main issue here is the lack of parking. If you cannot find a spot, I suggest being brave and parking in the lot to the North on the South edge near Starbucks. The inside tends to run on the chilly side, so you might bring a jacket or coat with you. I like to work here because there is a long bench of bar stool type high seats along the windows that have power outlets under them. Facing the windows while working can help avoid distractions and conversations going on. There are also a few tables great for groups. Be wary that the drive thru is often wrapped around the building in the mornings.

I'll be completely honest. I'm not a Starbucks fan or even a coffee drinker so my perspective may be slightly skewed towards the unfavorable but I will say that even if I was a huge coffee drinker and fan of Starbucks, this particular location seemed especially sad to me. And here's why.  Take out what you'd normally get at a Starbucks: coffee and your name spelled wrong on the coffee cup by the hipster working behind the register, and what you're left with is the atmosphere and just how the store feels when you're there.  Every time I go to this store to pick something up for my friends, it felt dead. I mean there were people there but it just didn't feel alive. The soft music playing in the background was more depressing than not, the tables and chairs weren't inviting and felt unwanted, even though it wasn't sunny outside, they'd have their curtains drawn halfway which made it even darker inside than it should be. Also every time I go there and there're people sitting at the tables, they'd be speaking softly to each other in a somber mood. I don't know. I just don't like the vibe at this particular spot. I used to work at Starbucks way back in my college days and it was an exciting spot with streams of people, loud music, bright lights, etc. It was inviting and upbeat. This place, I feel like every time I go here I'm intruding in on some funeral.  Alas, this is the closest Starbucks from my home and my friends leech off of me so because I'm so nice (maybe not in this review) I'll probably keep coming here for their drinks.  Parking: Shared lot Price: What you'd normally pay at a Starbucks Wait: None

Drive-thru here is really frustrating. First, as soon as you pull up before you can even begin to order they open with a hard-sell "what can we warm up for you to eat", or "can we get you some egg bites". I find this really obnoxious and overkill. Just ask me what I would like and let me take the lead please. Once you do order it's usually slow and half the time wrong. Today the till went down and when I got to the window the barista actually asked me to GIVE HER MY DEBIT CARD so that she could go and tap it in another location without me! When I refused, she then told me to go and park out front and come inside to pay. I handed back my drink and said no thanks, not what a drive-thru is for. Terrible customer service.

If you go through my Yelp reviews, you will come to the conclusion that I spend lots of time in coffeeshops, especially Starbucks locations. You would be correct.  As a medical student/physician I am, quite literally, a lifelong learner. Starbucks fits the bill for a place that offers both a conducive place to study as well as consistently strong enough coffee throughout its locations.  In terms of places to get work done, this is one of my favorites in the area. It's located in the heart of the Tyrone area so if you need to run nearly any errand before or after spending some time at a coffeeshop, it is extremely convenient. Parking is not an issue either.  Secondly, the openness of the cafe really helps let in lots of natural light which helps me focus. There are also lots of other people here getting work done so it feels like a productive atmosphere. There's a big table too with tons of outlets.  Thirdly, the baristas here are all extremely kind and quick! This is a very busy location and I've never seen them lose their cool. They make working under pressure look like a breeze.  Overall, this is probably my favorite Starbucks in Pinellas County.
Super standard Starbucks but good staff and clean area. Clayton is super nice to walk around too!
The service is great. The amount of homeless inside and out is really a blight on an otherwise great monument circle experience.

I could never give any more stars to a Starbucks. Their coffee is good and all but I hate the rude hipsters and snobs who flock and take over. This particular one has a back entrance away from the beards finger banging their Crapple laptops so you can order and then bolt.  *edit* Karen is hands down the best employee there and deserves a raise, everyone else gives sub par service and drinks.

Even though they never manage to spell my name right, and there is always a long line, I really like this Starbucks.  They are not all the same.  Here, I have never had bad service.  Everyone is so nice and they always have a filled case of yummy treats.  I'll keep going no matter what they right on the cup :)

As Starbucks go, this place is solidly below par. This assessment is based on below average ease of entry and parking (although the latter has improved with a neighboring business moving out) and more importantly, the staff here is just less friendly than other stores.    As an aside, it's definitely on the smaller side seating wise, and does not really have any quiet nooks for working, studying, etc if that is important to you.  Bottom line:  it's not horrible, but I try to hit other stores when possible

Drive thru at 545pm.... My order a hot coffee with whole milk and 1 sugar, I asked for half decaf coffee. So the girl said we don't have decaf coffee, we can do a pour over which takes 5 min or espresso for the decaf part. We asked if we would be charged for the espresso because I'm usually not and she says no. Then says ok it's $4.65 so them questioned her why it's $2 more, she said for your latte. I didn't order a latte, she said don't worry I'm not charging you for the latte pull forward. I explained what I wanted and she didn't seem to understand. A hot coffee made with half decaf and half regular with whole milk and 1 sugar. She had a huge black eye, which made me feel bad, maybe she has a concussion or got hit, who knows but if she has a medical condition, maybe they could've had someone else working the drive thru. She then asked for help and I was starting to cave and just get a regular coffee and decided to just go elsewhere. I can't understand how when we ordered, she prompted correctly like she knew what to do until we questioned the price. Isn't starbucks known for complicated made to order drinks? Not sure why this one was. The dunkin donuts 2 minutes away offered to brew decaf for me, so Dunkin got my business this time

Place is overrun with aggressive homeless ppl. Management needs to run them off. Have compassion for homeless, but don't want to be aggressively panhandled while I'm trying to eat. I'm a gold card carrying Starbucks hard core customer whose Been to many locations across the country and the staff at this location Is really rude. Second visit here they were all talking in front of me, turned their backs to me when I approached the counter and kept taking, and then all walked away and did not take my order. Finally one of them who was taking came back and helped me. Guy who got my food and drink was rude. Won't come back here if in the area.
Pretty disappointed Starbucks. I ordered a Grande Chai Tea Latte this morning & when I opened it up, a quarter of it was empty. Well, that sucks. Next time can I pay a quarter less of the price?

By far the worse Starbucks I have visited, this says a lot as I travel for work, I assumed being just off the interstate that I would be in and out rather than waiting just over 20 mins for a cold brew with a minimum crowd. Needs organization or some management to keep things streamed lined. Hard to expect much from plan city though.
SIDNEY is absolutely RUDE!  she was the barista on the 16th of february!  im surprised sbux has a staff member who probably hates her job and shows it to customers!

During my travel, this location has been the closest branch to my stay. They have been so accommodating as I am a healthcare worker and I have gone at least three times per week over the past few weeks.Their hours have been cut short 10-6:30pm due to the crisis, but I am grateful that they're still open so I at least come on my days off. Thank you for volunteers coming from other branches just to stay open for this community!

I don't come here often but I had a gift card from last Christmas and happy that I did because I was able to try a delicious drink that I think could become a favorite of mine.   During a Sunday drive my husband and I decided to stop at this location for some coffee. Soo the drive through line was super long but to our surprise we got through it really quick. My husband had the Carmel frappe and I had the dragon drink. I honestly didn't know what to expect from the drink but being that I didn't have to actually pay for it I said what the heck !!!! LOL   The Dragon drink it light, refreshing and not overwhelming at all. I expected the coconut milk to be a little stronger. The flavors combined really well and Yes, I would order this drink again. My only hesitation would be getting it from a different location because as we all know different locations make the drinks different and they never really taste the same.   Our customer service experience gets 5 stars as well as the overall experience
FYI they are currently closing at 4pm. Business hours have been a moving target, from 9pm to 7pm, then 6, then 4. Hours have not been updated online.

This Starbucks is normally on point but a couple of days ago I went to get a drink and waited in the line of the drive through for 25 minutes before I got up to the window to get my order. I was so frustrated that I didn't realize that my order was wrong before I pulled off. I tried to go back so that they could fix it but they had already closed 10 minutes early. Online it says they close at 1030pm but the door says that they close at 1000pm.
I really like this location . The staff is very friendly . There are two floors which is pretty cool so there is always room to sit down .

Recommend placing order on phone app as expected long queues (although fast moving). The staff has always been nice and 1 of the times I had such a lovely server he explained me in detail difference between different types of shots, ingredients, everything. He was super nice and patient. P.S. there was no long queue that day so could buy that extra time knowing more about the coffees. Some of my favs are - Chai Latte, White Chocolate Mocha, Creme Brulee (Seasonal) Frappuccino and Tomato Mozzarella Panini
I don't think I have to explain this one... my faith in my favorite coffee store is really going downhill recently.

This place is okay, but what Starbuck's has no venti-sized straws. I literally have to go to Jamba Juice to get a fucking large straw. Just be warned, if you buy a Trenta or Venti or any large sized drink, they will leave you hanging. Better work on the customer service.

This is a subpar Starbucks which likely only survives on the business of Marriott guests. I waited 15 minutes for my steamed milk which I had to request twice after the baristas forgot it. My pastry was stale. The bathrooms are a bit hidden. Go outside to the hall, turn left passing the guest gift shop, and continue down the hallway.

This Starbucks has the most RUDE employees.  They need a class in how to talk to people! My friends and I walked in and forgot our mask in the car not realizing it..."do you have your mask"? We said "oh were sorry,  we can go get them", response,  "do you know where you are"? How darn rude!! She said it with a terrible attitude.  Followed with "you can leave now"! Wow!! Do they want service or not. We were going to get them and those words were unnecessary. We got our mask as we wanted coffee and had been traveling.  Attitudes continued and its the last time we will EVER stop at this location again as we go past here on our road trips frequently.
Could never understand all the fuss over Starbucks.  Without all the stuff they add to their coffee it tastes like sheep dip.  If you want real coffee go somewhere else.

Wonderful Starbucks that uses the old gas station turned del taco space wisely.  They have a great selection of coffees here including the new rare/exquisite line.  I was able to buy a bag of Jamaica Blue Mountain ($30 for 1/2lb) and was thoroughly impressed by how bold and inviting the beans were.    The layout of the location is unique as well.  Less outlets so there aren't too many stragglers.  There are a couple larger center tables that multiple students can sit at, but I believe they are meant for more of a social gathering type atmosphere than a study atmosphere.   This Starbucks also features the Clover brewing system which is a technologically advanced system that seeks to brew coffee at the perfect temperature for the perfect amount of time.  I tried it (it costs about 50 cents more), but I did not taste an immediate difference.  Maybe I should have bought a normal coffee to do a taste test?

4.14.2020 There is a notice on the entrance that the drive thru is open BUT if you place a mobile order, it will be placed in the usual spot near the counter. Ensure you make eye contact with a barista letting them know you placed a mobile order and they will bring it to you. I caught them right as they were about to lock the door and the employee brought my egg bites to me.

One of the better Starbucks in my opinion. They actually keep their outdoor tables clean. (The Starbucks on Hazel Dell has disgusting tables). And the baristas are cool too.  For some reason they have begun locking one of their only two doors before dark. This is a main door and not on an alley so there is no security reason. From the patio to the open door is almost on the other side of the building.  I don't get it. It's 2018 folks, make it convenient for your customers!

Well, we thought we would try this location again. And, once again, tables looked dirty and were sticky, garbage was full and something spilled on the floor (which I pointed out). Took at least 15 minutes to take care of it!  However, it was nice to use a "real" knife to cut my sandwich. Don't think we'll be back here at this location.

Love this Starbucks. The service is quick, the coffee is consistent. The outdoor patio is a great place to sit, and there's lots of room inside as well. Other than that it's a pretty standard Starbucks.

Yes, I'm actually reviewing a Starbucks. If you drink coffee, you likely know already whether you like the taste of their coffee or not, and the coffee here is no different than any other Starbucks. The staff, however, is OUTSTANDING! I'm here way too much, so much so that I know there are a lot of other "regulars" who are here way too much as well! The staff know me and my usual coffee buddies and if any of us come in solo, they'll ask about the others. They'll also ask me about my kids, even down to knowing what my kids wore for Halloween! Needless to say, they remember everyone's regular coffee order as well. When staff members have moved on, I have seriously missed seeing them in the morning. The missing star is only by fault of the Starbucks Corporation - lousy planning with the parking. If you come in the morning during a rush, you may be stymied trying to find a spot and the bank next door is very clear about not wanting you to park in their spaces.
I literally use to come to this place every morning and I'm stopping. Rude workers and rude people go here. They open late constantly.

This is a new Starbucks in Nj on route 38. It's right. Txt door to PJs and across the street from the Morristown Mall. It's very clean and the staff are pretty awesome. I strolled in around 9:30 on a Saturday evening after going to a banquet.  I wasn't sure what I needed but I ended up with my go to drink a big passion tea. They close at 10pm in Saturday evenings and has already began cleaning. The bathrooms were clean and I would recommend this location.

What a great way to start a day than a good cup of coffee and a friendly staff that greets you by name.  Just when the emails start rolling in I just twist my cup around and enjoy the hand written "Have an Amazing Day!" Thanks for starting my day with a smile.

I've never been to such a slow Starbucks. Corporate needs to come back in to retrain staff on how to churn through the customers without making each order a successful minute ordeal.  One star; because it's in the hotel there's not even the benefit of relatively clean bathrooms.

This Starbucks always, Always has the nicest staff! Even the store manager always greets me when I come inside. They're also so friendly. Most of the time the line goes by so quickly, especially compared to other Starbucks. I am normally not a fan of pumpkin spice, but I tried their pumpkin spice cold foam. Guys, this is amazing! Thank you so much!

If you like there Pike Place Coffee then this is the place to Come because , it seems, that's all they ever have on Tap. They have plenty of Pour Over Coffee. That's like you make coffee in the woods , TrainTrack, style of Coffee. Pour hot  water over Coffee Grounds and that's your cup of coffee. It takes about 15 minutes for them to pour the hot water over the grounds because they are waiting on people in between pours. For the money they Charge i should have a choice of several flavors . They also have plenty of Rock Hard, Low chairs and tables to sit at. You get a Free Backache just for sitting there drinking there Pike Place. Have to see a chiropractor after every cup there. Not my place to injoy a cup of coffee
I love this spot  The employees are wonderful  Sweet professional  They never mess up my complicated order The manager is so friendly  I think she is top notch You can't go wrong going to this store

Most of the baristas are nice but there is one guy I've run into that is kinda sassy and I cannot tell if it's personality or if it's a displeasurement of being there. I was given a snarky comment when my ordered drink was made hot instead of iced. Which weirdly, the ice was given to me in less than two minutes but the hot took ten? My main issue is this location is really slowing down. Waiting in line for several minutes, 20 to 30? I might as well shut off my car to save gas and the planet. I'm going to start going to the other location on 135 or even the in store ones.
They never disappoint. I had a bad experience at the Algiers location, but this location made up for it.

As we've come to expected with Starbucks there are no issues with their coffee or other items.   But this place was pretty dirty. The floors needed to be swept and scrubbed in a bad way, and the restrooms cleaned. When we went the men's room was broken, and only one restroom was open everyone had to share.

Never coming back to this location ever again. A mobile order for a white chocolate mocha was ordered at 8:37a and at 9:15a I had yet to see my drink appear. I asked the young man nicely about the status and I was told we are busy and we will get to you. I patiently waited HOWEVER I had to address the young man again after I noticed folks who walked in after me got their drinks before me. I thought the purpose of the mobile order was for my drink to be ready when I showed up maybe I am wrong. I didn't appreciate the lack of customer service experienced and the young man acted like I was being an unreasonable and impatient customer. After waiting over an hour for a mocha I knew it wasn't me. When mg drink was ready they still didn't bother to let me know my order was ready. In the words of Trillville "Neva Eva, Eva Eva" will I go to this location.

I am at this Starbucks ALL THE TIME. I work just north of downtown (between downtown and Germantown. This Starbucks is the nearest coffee shop I can walk to (or a coffee shop inside the farmer's market, but I prefer not to do that). It's in a hidden little area within the Doubletree Hotel. Walk down the diagonal sidewalk thing and you'll find it. The lines can get very long (a lot of state employees frequent this location in large groups), but they're pretty fast most of the time. They also do a happy hour (between 2 and 4, I think), when it's buy one get one free on drinks. It's just a Starbucks, but it's gotten me through many an exhausting day!

This Starbucks is my go-to since it is nearest to my house and they always seem to make my coffee right. My order is simple: a basic White Chocolate Mocha. Today I went through the drive-thru and ordered just that. When I received my cup of "coffee", upon taking a sip, I realized it was literally just hot water and milk. When I walked inside a explained to the barista that it tasted like hot water, she laughed and said that "they were just playing with me" and that "the pump was broken". So, they KNOWINGLY gave me a steaming cup of water out the drive-thru window, fully expecting me to drink it. I'm quite disappointed, this is not the Starbucks customer service I am used to.

Everyone that works here is very friendly. I love coming in in the morning to get my morning coffee and sometimes a nice pastry item. I spent a lot of time here at night to working on my homework and everyone, no matter what shift, is always so friendly and eager to be helpful. This Starbucks is always clean. It is a little small but there are plenty of places to plug in your laptop if you need to.

This morning I enter this Starbucks and the young lady wearing big frame glasses on duty was extremely rude and had poor customer service!!! Please train your employees to be more customer service friendly !!!

This is a super convenient location for downtown commuters who park at Market District garage. It's way to easy to do a mobile order in the Starbucks app and pop into this location on my way to my office on the Circle.   The baristas are all friendly and make my winter mornings a little bit brighter with their cheerful greetings.

I don't know why I go here when in the area.  There is always something wrong with my drink!  My double shot today was more milk than coffee, and way too sweet.  The visit before that, my grande double shot  was all ice with a third cup espresso.  I was so livid I took a photo and sent it to corporate headquarters.  They responded with a credit on my account.  The staff is marginal and always talking among themselves. Today was my last visit to thus store.  Best store in area is Kirkwood on Kirkwood Road....always amazing consistent drinks, and the staff is beyond friendly.
This particular location is the busiest yet this morning the service was swift, refreshing, and on point!  I'm picky about my cappuccino and it was made perfect

Stopped by the other day 'cuz I was craving a caramel frap..an old high school fave. I'm not a huge coffee person but I do love the fraps here!! As I'm pregnant snd don't typically drink coffee, I asked how much caffeine was in the drink. The first barista didn't know but was honest and friendly and kindly directed me to a second who was able to tell me. I appreciated the honesty of the first and the knowledge of the second. My frap was great and definitely satisfied my craving!

I havent done it in a WHILE Due to this awful recession id rather buy bags , booze and more booze, However working until 1 and not falling asleep until 1:45 and having to be up again at 6 is not something I can do @ my feeble old age. I needed a boost and red bull is more of an after 9am pick me-up .  Im thinking and thinking and thinking THE BUXX  pops into my head like a lighting bolt from GOD on this magical ASHE WED ( oh its a sign)   Tall NONFAT extra shot latte w/ 2 splendas has given me the power to crunch these #'s until 4 today   GOD bless those baristas @ the starbucks on marsh and silverside!!!!

First try at the blonde espresso and I can say I liked it.  It has a sweeter and smoother taste to it, caffeine wise I cant really tell if it has a stronger kick.  As for the rest it was normal but I like that starbucks is trying out new things with there coffee even when they have 800 different things you can get and then customize on top of that (not complaining).  As always using mobile order has made picking up coffee on the go so easy and probably the best feature.

This is a cool place that a lot of locals hang out at at all hours of the day.  im in noway a starbucks coffee drinker (hot choc and chi tea fan) but i do like to go down there on a chilly night and get hot choc and read.  H/O they are doing away with being open 24 hours (me thinks for now).  I found out through a friend I see up there on week nights i go.  The other night i tried to find a sign by the front door alerting people of the change but no sign!  I hope mgt at least tells the homeless old men that sleep in the store on a nightly basis of the changes!

I recently returned to town after a trip and when I went in to order my drink, the barista made it a point to welcome me back and ask me how I'd been since she hasn't seen me.  I know how busy this Starbucks can get, so the fact they really get to know and remember their customers is impressive!  Kudos to their customer service.
This place is a homeless shelter, just happy my local starbucks is not like this or I would never go again.
Long wait usually but the staff is super friendly and helpful everytime.  Worth the wait!  Love my starbucks!!!  Even the at home verdions.

SOOOOOOO slow. I thought the couple times I had come in they were just short staffed or busy but no, they are consistently slow. If you're ever in a rush don't go to this location, and if you do expect to wait 15-20 minutes for your drink.
It is exactly what you expect from Starbucks and for a franchise that is about as good as it gets.  Sweet constancy.

This morning I'm surprised I didn't  have a pleasant experience! When I approached the drive thru window I said good morning! Hannah (looked at name tag) moved her headset and said what?  I replied, I was simply saying good morning.  She said oh, and turned her head!  There were no pleasantries at all!  She never said have a great day, just handed me my purchases.  I drove here in the rain because it's my favorite location.  So disappointed......
I love this Starbucks...I always order my coffees with coconut milk and they do such a good job! Not to mention the staff is amazing!

My review is in response for 2 issues. First issue was my drink. I ordered a nitro cold brew with sweet cream inside. When I received the drink, it was missing the sweet cream. Ok no big deal, I can just take it back up to the counter and they can add it. When I did this, there was no apology. It was almost as if it was my fault for bring it back up there. If you make a mistake, own up to it and apologize. Had I been in the drive thru and this happened, I would have been pissed because if the way the drive thru and parking lot are designed. This is my second and biggest complaint about this location. The parking lot has only 1 entrance/exit for the parking lot and the drive thru line was blocking it both when I arrived and an hour later when I left. I had to sit and wait for the line to move before I could get in and out of the parking lot. Because of this, I will not visit this location again, unless it's for the drive thru. On a positive note, the lobby has a generous amount of tables and seating.

First off I would like to say that I (we) visit Starbucks quite often but today was  terrible. Today is St. Patricks Day, and we are a Business,  so we decided to place an order, as we do on all Holidays. Needless to say our employee is still there... 1 1/2 hours later. They lost our order. He kept telling them he needs to get back to work. We have a full Restaurant and full Lobby. I called them and they said " We will get to this order as soon as we can" . I told him he needed to get back to work...Just get our money back but they said they could not do it. I have heard from several other people that this location has went downhill but I usually don't listen to people since I have never had a problem, until now. I just wanted to let you all know that this has upset alot of people. I usually don't write reviews but today I was very upset.

This Starbucks location is hit or miss. Mostly miss. Since it's in my neighborhood I visit often, which makes me kind of depressed to think about now that I'm sitting down to write this review.  They recently re-did the parking lot, which seems to have made room for an extra space or two. I know it's not their fault, they don't have much to work with. But when I have to park in the handicapped spot because all of the other spots are taken (even the make shift spots that hang almost into the road), I get dirty looks from other customers. Give me a break people!  As for the coffee, it's your standard Starbucks brew. I don't usually purchase drip coffee, I'm more of a latte gal.  I'd have to say only 1 or 2 regular baristas are friendly. The rest act like there's someplace else they'd rather be, and that's to put it nicely. One of them is super talkative and it takes forever for her to get through a line of customers because she wont shut up. I wouldn't mind any of this so much if they made my latte correctly at the end of it all.  But they usually do not. And you know what? I'm tired of asking for it to be remade because it makes me feel like a snob. And i'm not a snob. So I just bought a french press. And I'm looking into purchasing my own espresso machine. C'est la vie.   It's a shame that there's not a nice independent coffee place in Jenkintown that has a chill vibe and plenty of seating. I guess there's not market for it outside the city.

Overrated as usual, Starbucks. Service the same as all over the nation. Nothing special. NEW COVID19 mobile ordering on door for pick-up is NOT on monument side, but other door. Dumb walk up pick-up system. Ordered ahead on mobile app and still had to wait. WTF?!

Probably the most unfriendly baristas I've ever encountered at any Starbucks in the country. No joke. Glad I will likely not be in this area again any time soon or I would probably be annoyed on a regular basis.

I understand people may have bad days. In the service industry you deal with people that have no reason, that are rude to you and don't care about any other circumstances that you are facing.  The answer to that is  not to treat others the way others have treated you. With that said, whether you care or not, I buy at least 2 coffees per day from your location. When I pay $6 for a coffee, trust me I know what my order looks and tastes like. I don't ask for a free drink, only what I ordered and paid for.

I was going to preface this review with a defense of Starbucks, but changed my mind and decided to not get into the politics and simply stick to reviewing.  (Though I will say - politics does influence where I will spend my money.)  In some ways, a Starbucks is a Starbucks.  At least if you are at a Company Store and not a Licensed Location (of which there are thankfully few.) However, in the world of "the same" this location stands out as wonderful.  The Baristas are uniquely friendly.  They also do seem to try harder to be helpful than at other locations.  I have on a few occasions needed to speak to a manager, not for problems, but to get information, and the woman who seems to be the store manager is knowledgeable and responsive.  She also seems to have a good rapport with the staff, which may account for the quality of service.  The store, and specifically the restroom, is always clean, and when I have found it lacking in a paper product, they are quick to fix the problem.  Although the lines can be long, service doesn't dawdle.  On the other hand, when it isn't busy, staff is happy to chat about coffee, or movies, if you are interested and don't interfere with their work getting done.  There are plenty of tables, and four areas which have power outlets, which is more than most locations, so it is truly possible to be there and get work done.  It does get crowded at times, especially during exam weeks at Drexel and Penn, but I have only once, out of many visits had to leave because I couldn't find a seat.  This is not the ONLY Starbucks in Philly that I like, but it is my favorite!   On a Company-wide note - they now use NO High Fructose Corn Syrup in ANYTHING.  Desserts are back on my menu!

Came in for the 1/2 off Frap Monday. The place is packed, but still got great service, and the place is clean.  Other than that, it's a Starbucks. Pretty much it's all the same as the others.   Knowledgable nice people, what more do you need?

Not all Starbucks are created equal.  * Many have tiny, nasty, difficult parking lots.  This location is in a plaza so parking is a breeze.  * This is a busy location and after you place your order you need to wait for your drink to be made.  The area for waiting here is very small and I get mildly annoyed when it's crowded.  * The people here are great.  * Starbucks has partnered with a new bakery and they are rolling it out all across the country.  It's called La Boulange and they are from Fan Francisco.  It's easy to tell if a location has it because their signature color is pink.  This location was just rolled out so you'll see lots of different tasty treats.  I stop in here most weekday mornings and overall they're great!

AVOID THIS LOCATION AT ALL COSTS!! I was so excited to have a newer, updated Starbucks in the area, but that excitement quickly disappeared after the first few visits. I always use the drive-tru, which is supposed to be quick and convenient... not at this location! The few times I purchased coffee at this location I spent an excessive amount of time (always 10+ minutes, once almost 20 minutes) in the drive-thru.   Yesterday, I was driving by and saw a very short line, and decided to give it another go. My mistake. There was no line, only one car that pulled up right before me. They quickly ordered their ONE DRINK, pulled up, and I ordered my ONE DRINK. From the time I placed my order it took 15 minutes to get to the window. The line of cars behind me was now at a whopping 9 count. The time it took to place two coffee ordered the line quadrupled in size. Let me repeat that... I WAS THE SECOND CAR IN LINE, AND IT TOOK 15 MINUTES TO RECEIVE MY ICED COFFEE. Thank goodness I wasn't that 9th car in line, they're probably still waiting for their coffee.   EVERY time I come to this location... I wait... for a very long time. It's an ongoing problem that seems to not be given the attention by staff that it needs. It's a shame because it's a beautiful and convenient location.
Literally the worst Starbucks in Tampa. Inefficiency and wrong orders are all they seem to serve.

Urban concrete everywhere  Buildings rise and traffic swells.  This starry cafe is a break in the melee. Not much vibe or 'culture' but does the trick nonetheless.  Crooner Jazz music  on heralds a different, nostalgic time.

What a mess. You can't park in most of the parking spaces because they're blocked by the drive-through. I called ahead and waited 25 minutes and still only got half my order before I absolutely had to leave. I am told that it's always like that. Total mess.

First time coming to this location and Nikki was amazing, she was so sweet and polite when taking my order. Also liked the fact that their drive thru was pretty quick. Most Starbucks take a little longer. Nikki just made this overall experience really great. Loved her, oh right, the drink was delicious!

The first thing you notice through the door is the litter covered floor.  Walking up to the check out the register and the area around is dirty and dusty, 2 people answer one persons enquiry while customers wait, then do the same with the second customer. Time to get served no apology for the wait  Frappe-chino and a cake really nice pity about the surroundings go to the self service area bins so full they are overflowing and dirty

I get my coffee here every morning.  They have been consistent with their service and quality of the drinks I have ordered.  Starbucks has figured out their drink, the taste is the best out of all the coffee places in town.
7 employees working a few customers and not one clean table. In addition to that there is a nasty odor that make you want to leave. No excuse for this!

Came here after work for a half price wake up jolt. Service was quick and polite through the drive through. The lady was very soft spoken and hard to hear. She was efficient and friendly while lying and drinks came hot as we ordered. The drinks my friend and I ordered both had sleeves and even had the stop caps on so they wouldn't spill in the car.

This location is in a convenient location, well staffed, but they seem to only care about their drive thru. The interior has the worst furniture, and their counter service is lacking in friendliness and speed besides a couple taller thinner gentlemen whose names i forget. This location may as well not have an interior.

There are two national chains that seem to garner their share of "haters" both for legit and not so legit reasons - Chick-fil-A and Starbucks. Chick-fil-A has obvious concerns politically, while Starbucks seems to garner hate mostly because it's the "big brother" type coffee house that has replaced local, indie-tinged coffeehouses nationwide.   The truth is, as evidenced by sales, that most of us still go to both places. Chick-fil-A has, hands down, the best customer service in the fast food world while Starbucks is so woven into the fabric of our culture that many people don't say "I'm going to grab a coffee." They say "I'm going to grab a Starbucks."   I generally try not to review a lot of "chain" businesses on Yelp, but there are times when it's warranted. This is one of those times.   This Starbucks is located directly on Monument Circle in a building my father once helped renovate. It's on a busy corner and has two entrances.  Both entrances have older, wooden with glass-filled doors that can be a bit difficult to open. They also both have serious lips at the door that can be a bit difficult in wheelchairs. Unless you're downtown already, there's little chance you've come here as a destination because parking downtown is difficult and largely paid. Because this is Monument Circle, a wheelchair user has to also deal with the cinder block sidewalk with certain blocks that seem to always be loose.   While I'd visited this location before, the truth is that even as someone who works downtown I seldom go as far as Monument Circle from my nearby Government Center office.   Over the course of a fundraiser/awareness event that lasted around 4 days, I got to know this Starbucks very well as they became involved, almost last minute, in supporting my event.   Over the course of four days, I was permitted to use their restroom without a purchase (I was staying on Monument Circle 24/7; so this was a big deal). I was also provided water periodically and, a couple times, alternative drinks. The regional director for Indiana also ended up supporting in other ways.   This location, which is pending a needed renovation as the inside offers no seating but does have window counters, Is a frequently busy location that attempts to manage the chaos in a structured, occasionally stringent way. This area also is frequented by members of Indy's homeless community, most of whom have developed a sort of peaceful co-existence with Starbucks and its mostly compassionate employees who are still tasked with ensuring the business remains safe and responsible.   Over the course of four days, I witnessed employees who were unified, enthusiastic, fun, efficient, and effective at their jobs. I witnessed a manager, Dawn, who remained aware of what was going on and worked to keep people on task while creating a fun, caring environment. As someone whose work they were supporting, I was greeted by name with enthusiasm, concern, and warmth every single time I entered the place.   Every time.   The drinks I ordered were always delivered as ordered, while the one time I special ordered a food item it was also delivered as ordered. The restroom, which is now operated by code rather than key, is a one-user restroom with appropriate hand-rails and a motion sensor hand-dryer. Even when I was really tired, it was easy to use.   There's no question at its most crowded that it can be difficult to navigate in Starbucks, though for the most part I never had difficulty and found most of the customers cooperative with my mobility.   While I don't know the details of the upcoming renovation, my understanding is it will make for a roomier, more hospitable experience. Starbucks also offers outdoor seating when it's weather appropriate. However, for a stellar experience over a period of 4 days and warm, enthusiastic support of one lonely activist's endeavor to make his community a safer place for children I'm giving Starbucks a well deserved 5-star review.

When to this Starbucks after having lunch at Pumpernick's restaurant.  There was no line. It was happy hour. My daughter and i were able to get a drink for the price of 1.  Great deal! My drink was made as a requested. I will continue to go to this Starbucks  when I have a craving.

This message is to management, if you even care. For god's sake, please please please hire more staff! It is unacceptable to have three people working in the store at 7:30am when the drive through line is 15+ cars and the line in the store is 10+ people long. It is true that I used to live in the city and am used to a 2 minute visit. Spending 10+ minutes in this Starbucks just tops the list. I thought maybe it is just a one time thing, but it is consistent every single day and that is why I go out of my way to avoid you, Horizon Blvd Starbucks. I thought a mega giant like you would have some standards...

I go to this starbucks occasionally after nightshift at the hospital. A few months ago at Christmas time I ordered a new menu item "Holiday Flat White" which I read straight off their menu on the wall behind the counter. When the cashier told the barista my order her initial response was "I don't know how to make a f*cking flat white" right in front of me. I received a drink none the less. Today I decided to go in for a green tea latte. I've had these before from starbucks and also have matcha at home which I prepare my own lattes with. To my astonishment their "matcha green tea latte" said it was over 400 calories. I noticed another menu item that said "matcha soy milk latte" and it read around 200 calories so I thought I would try it. The cashier was so rude and insisted I was wrong and said "it's the green tea latte. Matcha is green tea ma'am." Even after I gestured toward the menu board, she looked up at it and STILL insisted they were both the same thing. Did not even ask the barista. I wasnt arguing over tea after a 12 hr shift so I got the matcha green tea with coconut milk. Terrible customer service.

This is my favorite Starbucks to go to in Santa Barbara!!! The staff is what makes this particular Starbucks so great. This location can definitely get pretty busy at times, but the staff are always so friendly and provide wonderful customer service regardless of how busy they are.

Love this little starbucks! Every time I come here there is always the sweetest girl at the drive thru window. Never had a problem here or waited very long for my order. My only complaint is that the parking lot is designed poorly and if people are trying to back out they cannot because the drive thru line is blocking them. Besides that it's probably my #1 favorite starbucks in Saint Petersburg. Happy coffee sipping!

I go to this Starbucks almost ever other morning and I get the same drink every single time which means I pay the SAME price every time. I ran in this evening for my SAME drink with the same amount of cash I use every time I'm there. The manager "Bree" was very rude not only to me but to the family in front of me. She stated that my drink cost $1 more than I normally pay. I told her I come here ALL the time and I can show her how much I pay for my drink every single time. She stated" well this is what it cost"! Very very rude service.

I am a regular at Starbucks.  5 days a week I was going for my morning breve latte, that's about $5.25 a day, times 4 weeks, that's  $105. a month.  And However, if my husband and grandkids are with me it is much more as they usually want something to eat and often times a thermos and reuseable to go cup! Recently, I went in and upon ordering my coffee I discovered it was twice the usual price.  When I questioned the person behind the counter about this I was told "yes prices had increased", "by double?" I inquired (almost just paying it).  When another employee looked at the register and asked if I ordered one or two?  I said one.  He realized I'd been charged twice.  He apologized explaining the girl on register was brand new.  No worries.  We are all new employees at one time or another in our lives and mistakes are made, and then easily rectified.   I was told by this young gentleman that they made the correction (although I was never given a receipt; my mistake but I have literally been coming here for 6 years and didn't think it necessary).  When I arrived home later that day I was on line with my bank checking my deductions when I noticed that yes, Starbucks had charged me double, and then took it off.  However, I noticed that they than charged me twice for the same drink showing two separate transactions.   Again, knowing the girl at the register to be new I realized she simply made another error.  I decided I would call Starbucks to see what they could do about it.  I spoke to "Danni", whom I assumed to be a young manager.  I explained to her what happened.  She remembered me, as I said, I'm in there a lot.  She told me to simply come back when she was on duty and she would give me my next coffee making up the difference.  I told her that would be fine.  However, when I went back she wasn't there.  I explained to the cashier what happened and she told me to wait for Danni to come back in.  So I did.  Today Danni was in.  I went up to the counter and brought to her memory what had happened.  However, on this occasion she told me I needed to prove it with my bank statement.  Now I'm standing there feeling embarrassed asking for a refund for one cup of joe, while at the same time being looked at and spoken to as if I was just trying to get a free coffee.  Someone needs to speak to these young people about customer service, especially the regulars.  Not only did I spend what I believe was a nice amount of money on coffee and other items per month but I was also a generous tipper.  The only person whom I ever met at this location who knew anything about customer service was the night "Brandon" who has worked there for quite some time.  He is simply heavenly to deal with and knows the value of a repeat customer who has been nothing but kind and understanding towards your staff.   I am not saying I'll never be back but it might take a while for the sting to subside.  I'll also insist upon a receipt each and every time.  If you can't trust me why should I trust you?  Receipts should be mandatory, not asked "do you want a receipt" , if you are going to treat your customers this way. Today my star rating is going to be very low.

I'm a die hard Starbucks fan. The employees and the managers here are super friendly and pretty fast for how busy they are almost all the time. They do have a great location for before and after work for a lot of people.

Management here is terrible. Most of the baristas are very kind- but most of them tend to ignore your presence, and argue with you if your drink is incorrect. The store is usually always dirty, and the store manager argued with a lady who saw a barista sneeze in his hands and proceed to make her drink. People on heroin sit by the door, slouched in their chairs, drooling- people smoke on the patio- and I've seen baristas yell at people for coming in to plug their phones in. Only a select few seem to know how to make beverages correctly. Also- often there will only be one barista, doing all of the work by themselves, as other workers chat to the side.  It's very noticeable that Barry and Will are always working hard, and well.

Sat for several minutes at the window just waiting on our 2 coffees today at 1pm. I made a comment about how long it was taking to my company, as it was surprising. The gal at the window was just looking at her computer doing nothing. She FINALLY grabs the beverages that have been sitting next to her computer for the entire several minutes. I asked her if they had been sitting there all this time(they were!), she said "No." ... I responded with, "Yes, they were.... why?" To which she decided to ignore me rather than give any attention. A simple apology would have sufficed. But as I asked several questions after the fact and she completely acted as if I didn't even exist... unacceptable. Ignored. Completely unacceptable. Toodles, Starbucks.. you have reminded me yet again why I opt to take my business elsewhere.
This is a decent Starbucks. I was surprised at how large the parking lot was, but every single spot was still taken and I had to wait for someone to leave. Looks like they need to expand, to me!
Do not go here on Saturdays earlier afternoon because they have only one person making drinks and it took forever!!!! I couldn't believe how long it took to get a Frappuccino.

In the early hours when there is absolutely no one in the mall, service is great. Now ordering anytime once the mall is open is a waste of your time....the wait is always 10 mins or longer guaranteed. During this visit though i wanted a Caramel Ribbon Crunch Frap (which is amazing) and was clearly available to order based on the signage. How, after waiting in line for 12 mins i was told that they did not have the ingredients needed to make this drink. ..confused i was but I opted for the basic Caramel Frap seeing as though i had no other choice after waiting for something i really wanted but clearly could not have.   So get your fix in the early hours or try the court Starbucks its less foot traffic on that side of the mall so it may be a shorter wait.   Otherwise you'll spend your lunch break in line waiting to be served. Which is no way to spend your break if I must say so myself.   I gave 3 stars because they are nice and make good drinks....when they have what you want of course

Slowly but surely I'm going back into a normal routine. And part of that normal routine sometimes involves a drive thru for coffee instead of a scale, grinder, and French press.   This Starbucks location has been closed for about a month due to the pandemic and prior to this it was probably my favorite local 'Bucks. So I'm glad to have made a return today. The baristas are generally very friendly and always very efficient. They do a great job keeping the drive thru moving quickly. It's particularly important that this drive thru moves fast because this parking lot is a nightmare. My coffee order was placed and received in a total of 1.5 minutes. I mean...that's impressive. The cafe is very small and so this is an in-n-out location primarily.   I'm glad to see the baristas are all wearing masks right now and that they have strict procedures to maintain social distancing compliance.    Also super cool to find that Starbucks has extended redemption period for birthday rewards! I got to use my birthday reward to treat my husband to a ~ridiculous~ ~indulgent~ ~dessert coffee~ (iced venti caramel macchiato) weeks after my bday.   That's right, babe, you just got callllllled out.   **Please note my husband is still a manly dude that mostly drinks black coffee. He appreciates the subtle nuances in beans. He is a particular connoisseur of beans from Latin American countries and his favorite way to enjoy coffee is in an aero press. But yes, he digs an iced caramel macchiato sometimes.   It's fun to feel safe going back to Starbucks again!
Delicious drink.... mobile ordered was ready soon as i got there   Baristas were friendly and greeted me when i came

Well, Starbucks is Starbucks...for the most part.  I have never had a bad latte here or anything like that.  But I am more concerned about the parking lot situation at this particular location.  It constantly causes issues that make you think to yourself, "CAN'T THERE BE A BETTER WAY?"  And then you cry out in agony.  The situation of this Starbucks is that you can only pull into it if you are turning "right."  If you are turning left, then you have to turn at a stop light and then pull into the parking lot.  Well, this is quite the issue because you are creating a situation where you are pulling head into about everyone else...the entire drive through line...which is so tightly wound up in that tiny parking lot that you can't even swing into a parking spot half the time.  Which, for that matter, there aren't that many of.  Even writing this review stresses me out.    This Starbucks location is also tricky because it is quite small inside, seating-wise.  And yet, every business man or woman without an office is determined to come here and take up an entire table to him or herself for a few hours at a time while drinking "H2O" as their cups are labeled.    So here is my constructive advice for visiting this location:  make sure you are not someone who gets anxious in awkward parking lot situations and if you are planning on coming here to actually have a coffee in the store, beware, there may not be room for you.  But, I mean, my favorite White Chocolate Mocha is just as good here as any other Starbucks.

Another good Starbucks! What I love about this Starbucks is all the windows and natural light that shines through. It has lots of seating, with tables and comfy chairs for those that want to relax and read a book. It is pretty busy because of the location, but I always find a seat. This Starbucks also has a drive-through, and has good service too!

I try to avoid this location as much as possible.  I love Starbucks  and will frequent many locations but this one is sooooo horribly slow.  Not sure what's going on with their management here.  Unfortunately it's one of the closest to me so at times I'm stuck going to it.  Don't go there unless you want to spend 20+ minutes.
Being ignored for quite a while when ordering. No one else was in the line at that time.

Lots of workspace. Plenty of room to spread out.  Starbucks evenings offered here.    I have not tried anything from the evening menu, but now that I know this location offers it, perhaps I shall one day.  This location also offers a drive thru.

My DISABLED child came home and told me today that the lady at Starbucks at the Mall was very rude to her when she was there yesterday. She (not sure what her name was)  made her feel so rushed to give her her order. And was very nasty to her. This is the most disgusting behavior, and whoever you are you should feel really ashamed of yourself.  And maybe find a job where you aren't in SERVICE.
This is the Starbucks in the Lawrence Park Shopping center and it is fine.  Bathrooms are closed due to Covid and the service can vary based on who is working.  No issues.

I just sat at the front counter for over three minutes without being greeted or spoken to.  In the meantime, five employees ran around taking care of drive through customers.  I was the only customer at the front counter.  I can get that at mcdonalds.
I really enjoy this location .  I find that is always a fun energy in the store and provides me with an amazing customer experience. Go Starbucks !!!
The rumor is that this Starbucks is the busiest in all of Tennessee. I believe it. Every time I come here it's extremely crowded. It is a great location, but come with time to wait.
Katie made my drink perfectly in record time and was very friendly even at 4am! Great service!

This location is in Downtown Indy where you can only get faster service at the at the race car track (yes - I did use a racing reference).  Service - fast and friendly Cost - well it is starbucks and pretty standard Atmosphere - Friday Evening - very crowded and lively (good thing)  Enjoy a cup of Joe today :-)

This is a great Starbucks location, with plenty of indoor and outdoor seating. The new CITRUS GREEN TEA LATTE smells like a lemon drop and tastes like some lemon gummy is lost somewhere in the bottom of your cup. It's not bad, if you like that sort of thing. They didn't skimp on the green tea, it's got lots of great green tea flavor.

I'm definitely not a regular at this location but everyone in line behind me was! The staff was very friendly and I received my drink right away. The cashier started ringing up all the "usuals" for those behind me and knew all their orders. Inside was tidy and clean. There's also a little outdoor seating area. Main issue with this location is parking/maneuvering around the lot. Very small and tight location- I took the last front parking spot! There is a drive thru but it's a little tight behind the building. Make sure to note the very uneven pavement in the back and if you are leaving from a parking spot, you may get stuck in part of the drive thru lane :/ This Starbucks location is a little difficult to get to if coming from the interstate because of the left turn without a light but if you're headed to 465, it's a super easy right turn to get to it.

This Starbucks has been a life saver for me. It's so easy to get to and just as easy to hop on the interstate. The drive thru line can get super long though, especially during the morning rush hour since it's right off the causeway. Mobile ordering is the way to go!

I love this Starbucks. They're consistent and fast. The girls (and I say that because I've never seen a guy working) are the sweetest, super accommodating, and go out of their way to take special requests.   This is a super busy location during the day during the week, so I recommend mobile order. I've never done their drive thru so can't speak to that.
Only medium size by Starbucks standards. Cool Portland Brew coffeehouse nearby on Murphy Road at West End is preferred alternative.

Stopped for a quick lemonade on our way but like someone mentioned in their review, if you are in a hurry, don't trust their driveway. It's very slow and even if our drink was ready a while ago, no one was there at the window. This was the same for the two cars before us.

The checker was a young girl the only one that was not friendly. Because of her they get the one star. The food is always good at Starbucks or for the most part But her attitude and lack of customer service was not good. The rest of the people working there nice.

wow, i haven't been to starbucks in months and i just went this morning and the drink was ok at best but the service... i was helped by a lady named Brooke and she shined a warm ray of sunlight into my soul and cured my ailments with her warm smile and kind aura. love that, will come again

Horrible!!! For the amount I pay for a vanilla chai latte! It tastes like crap compared to the other Starbucks. I come here every morning before work. Looks like I will have to find a new spot worth the money!

Located on a corner so it is easy in and out on a busy street. I opted for the drive thru as I drive a huge truck and the parking lot spaces are on the smaller side. The drive thru was a squeeze too but I made it without any mishaps. Ordered my cold brew and they had it ready by the time I made it to the window. Staff was decent and prices were normal for The Bucks.

I love Starbucks because of their consistency, yet this location needs more training on how to make their drinks to Starbucks normal standards. For some reason their espresso is incredibly weak and the color of iced tea... when I asked about why it was so weak they offered extra shots of espresso but again, it barely tasted like coffee. They need help.

After a boozy lunch, we stopped by the Starbucks for some afternoon beverages.   I rarely drink coffee, so I ordered some Ice Tea Cooler thing. My guy ordered a Trente Americano, all the funny complications to his order he loves. I think it's pretty funny.   I was confused to whether the new size Trente, was 30 ounces or 30 shots. Although I'm a complete dork when I'm buzzed, the barista enjoyed my humor.

About the worst Starbucks ever. Three employees and they all seem to be doing things that do not include taking orders and making coffee. We were second in line and it took five minutes to take our order. Oh wait, we left. Went to chick fil a and had our order taken immediately.

I can't believe we are reviewing Starbucks. But then again, we also reviewed Wal-Mart and Super Fresh (ah, local Philly flavor!).  I personally don't like their coffee (too bold), but I suppose Starbucks is good for those who drink Coca-Cola Blak (that's caffeinated caffeine for those not in the know) or for those who need to be hit in the head with an anvil to wake up in the morning.   BUT, Starbucks' Iced Black Tea in the summer is incredible. For colder months, go for Pumpkin Spice!

Convenient and hut like with a drive thru. The outside seating is sparse but the drive thru is quick. I go here often whenI am crunched for time and they have always been speedy. Bonus, have yet to get a messed up order here :)

Stopped by to grab coffee and saw a familiar face behind the counter. Tyler worked at another coffee shop in town my family and I used to frequent, and needless to say we were all excited to see a friendly face at our new regular coffee stop. The entire staff was working so hard during what appeared to be a crazy rush, but they all had a smile on their faces. And the same could be said for Tyler. He was working just as hard as we always remembered, and we can't say enough about his attitude, politeness, and eagerness to help.

I may be a little bias since this is my home(I'm a partner here), but our store is absolutely gorgeous. I've worked for the company on and off for about 12 years and I've seen/worked at a ton of locations, but this store tops them all! Come by and try some Nitro Cold Brew and listen to us tell lame jokes! You love the coffee and we love you!

The interior is extremely nice... That's the only thing good about it. My boyfriend and I are both partners and we often check other Stabucks in the region. This one was by far the worst, extremely disappointed.  Asked for a syrup Creme frappaccino and the girl had no idea what I was talking about and punched in a coffee frappaccino. The guy that was working with her had to find the button for her. I then asked for affagato style, meaning shots poured on top of the drink... Her explanation? "We don't have that." ......? Girl, clearly you guys have that, you just have no idea what you're doing. Asked for extra caramel drizzle... Didn't get extra... She asked me how to make the drink and asked if she put frap roast in the drink after I told her it was a Syrup Creme... (Partners and Starbuck Regulars will know what I'm saying)  My boyfriend got a java chip frappaccino and it tasted like a mocha frap. There was barely any frap chips in there and tasted like there was no frap roast/ coffee in there as well... And she didn't put mocha drizzle...  Thought this would be a good place to go to after a long day, didn't think I would have to tell them how to make a simple drink...?  Bottom line, BEST worst Starbucks ever.

I have been going to this Starbucks for 7 years. SEVEN! I have no complaints regarding my drinks. I order a variety of concoctions, so it's never the same thing for me. They do a good job and even when I have to tell them how to make it, they typically do a great job. So drink-wise, they get 5 stars...BUT, the slightly more mature gentleman that they have working the window has absolutely horrible customer service skills! Whenever I great him, he never says hello back, he just gives me a total for my drink. He has never said you're welcome even after being told thank you (which I do every time). Today, I asked for my receipt and he said the receipt machine isn't working very curtly while never looking at me.  I can absolutely understand having an off day. We all have them. It's not great to have them when you work in the customer service industry, especially when you're dealing with hundreds of customers a day, but it can happen. Unfortunately, he has made me go out of my way to a different location because of his rudeness. I don't know if that's his natural personality and demeanor and he's like that with everyone or if he chooses to be like that only with people that are not of his skin color.
