But, whatever the reasoning is, his customer service skills need to be addressed. I have never had this issue in Avon or at the one on 56th St. and Lafayette Rd. It's only him at the 10th St. location. I'm even considering calling and making a verbal complaint, because no one should have to be subjected to that.

I just left this Starbucks and plan to never go there again! I waited in line almost 15 minutes with just 2 single people ahead of me. The girl at the register couldn't seem to figure out what she needed to do and no one helped her until the very end, and she had to ask! There were 3 others working and no one seemed to care that there was a line. Also, we got in line and there was a person down at the end of the counter that I didn't even see. My husband ask if she was in line and she said she was.(she really wasn't)  Come to find out SHE was an employee. Now I know you make the employees act like customers when they have their breaks, but really?!?! 15 minutes is way to long when the place is not busy.  Then the tall guy with dreads copped an attitude.  I mean,seriously...

Thanks to the chai latte I am now officially a Starbucks addict. I love the fact that this one is so close and a drive-thru.   I'm taking a star off because the parking lot is a mess and frankly kind of dangerous maneuvering your car around there. Another star off because even if I'm the only car in the drive-thru lane it can be a little slow.

Nice location, right by the Whole Foods and the new Atlantic Books outlet.  The bathroom is clean and private, the service varies from time to time but in general it's good.  There are a ton of windows and four big comfy chairs, as well as a sitting bar.  There is also outside seating.  The best thing about this location is that someone is always dumping their newspapers in the communal bin, so no need to pay for one! :)

I love this Starbucks. It has a healthy split of clientele with middle age people like to college students studying on their laptop for classes at UNR. It's in a good neighborhood northeast of downtown Reno off I-80. The employees at this Starbucks are very knowledgeable about the drinks and are helpful. They also are very nice to the customers here. The place is very clean. It's usually busy day and nite. There always a few people in here. My favorite Starbucks in Reno!!

This is one of my favorite Starbucks locations in town. It's a newer location with clean interior and an upgraded drive thru with video screen ordering which makes it easier to see that your order is correct. The parking lot is fairly small but there is street parking on Sidney right next to the store. I generally do mobile ordering in the evening before work. The evening crew generally greets me by name and knows how I like my drinks. If they are out of a food item I ordered by the evening they have other suggestions ready for when I arrive. This visit I went in the drivethru at noon during the week and got a Frappuccino as a pick me up. The service was prompt and the guy working the window engaged me in conversation while I waited. I will definitely keep using this Starbucks as my preferred location.

I happen to spend a lot of time and money at this Starbucks, every time I go I am amazed at how slow the drive thru line moves. The lady baristas are ok but Jay and Guy put them to shame with their impressive customer service skills. Drinks are inconsistent but beggars can't be choosers.

The online hours state 10pm closing.  However, we literally honked at the 2 baristas in the store.  We were completely ignored even when we got off the car and stood at the door.  Not even a gesture to say they were close.  So much for genuine friendly service.

When you have a drive thru line out to the street, you don't sit here hanging out the drive up window socializing with every customer. Its discourteous to everyone else in line, especially when people downtown are trying to get to work.  This place has numerous other faults as well for such a busy location. They don't fill up the drinks all the way and they've forgotten my food order inside twice so I had to wait double the time when it was already way too long. I'm done with this place. There's so many other good coffee shops in town to give my business to.
This location has amazing uplifting signs posted along the drive thru... so grateful to see happiness and joy.

Normal walk-in starbucks... Staff was very friendly... Drinks were made fast but with care... Had a great convo with the bristia about differnt coffee's we liked and she suggested some of her favorite that I might like...
Not sure what has happened at this location. The service is extremely slow. My order is always wrong; whether it's the wrong size or the wrong milk..... won't be coming back.
We have team meeting here all the time! It is quiet and relaxing! Thank you staff! Honey Real Estate Team
This Starbucks always gives me excellent service. I come three times a week and they workers are so nice

I'm addicted to the  1/2 blueberry black tea  1/2 lemonade this summer. I love it. I get it without the cane sugar and it's not too sweet and very refreshing. I stopped into this location for an afternoon pick me up. The drive-thru is always busy here! I'll be back!

(Disclaimer:  Sorry for the length, I'm heated/venting!)  I normally would give this store a 5, but I had a TERRIBLE experience here this morning because of the shift? manager.  I had called earlier to advise I was walking up with my dog from my neighborhood and asked if they were still bringing out online orders for curbside pickup.  I was given 3 options on the phone (from the woman who answered).  1) I can come in with dog to place my order and wait outside for the beverage.  2) I can leave the dog outside and come inside to order/pickup.  3) I can go to the drive-thru window.  I chose #1, because in the past I've been told you can't pick up orders in the drive-thru, as it's disruptive to the vehicles in line. When I walked in to place my order, everyone seemed friendly.  No one else was in the store, except for employees.  I forgot to put my face mask on, so as I was ordering my drink, I quickly took it out of my pocket and put it on.  I was redeeming a birthday reward too, as my birthday was 5/19.  The person ringing my order made a mistake and called a manager (I think her name was Meghan (blue/turquoise hair?)).  She had to do a complete refund, which was fine by me, I was in no hurry.  She did the refund, and when Meghan rang the drink up again, she claimed that my birthday redemption was nowhere to be found and it may have expired.  I told her the reward gave me until 6/2.  She argued with me that they typically expire within the end of the month of the birthday.  I went to show her my screen with the expiration of 6/2 and she brushed me away saying "Just forget it, it's fine."  I think she realized it was still May (May 30th).   While they were making my drink, Meghan became increasingly passive-aggressive.  She told me that unless my dog was a service dog, I'm really not supposed to bring him inside.  As I was telling her I figured they would have told me that immediately when I walked in the store, she continued talking over me, telling me next time I need to use the drive-thru.  I had been in there for about 5-10 mins at this point, and no one said a word to me about the dog, until that moment.  I said I would be happy to wait outside, and they could bring the drink out to me.  She told me to go outside, and that next time I need to use the drive-thru.  Very nasty tone and demeanor.  I don't know if she was upset she had to do the refund, that she was wrong about my birthday redemption, or if she was having a bad day, but her behavior was disrespectful, and frankly unacceptable.  I'm a local resident to Rock Hill that's supported this location ever since I moved here, even through the pandemic.  No one deserves to be treated this way, but especially local folks trying to support a local business.  Oh, and they never brought out the pup cup I asked for either.  So my dog gives them 1 star too!
Love this place: the location, the window views, the snacks, the staff and the service provided, the variety of newspapers. And my drink is always right! ... Love Love Love
Tori made my morning brighter. She was  very sweet and cheerful. This location is always fast, efficient and friendly. Thanks all!!

Got a strawberry Frappuccino. Delicious as always. I also found the staff to be friendly to my kids. It gets kind of crowded in this Starbucks, but it wasn't too bad this evening.   Great place for. USI was meetings over coffee or tea. Especially if you can sit outside.

I was so excited that they were moving because that would mean adequate parking and an easier in and out. I didn't realize that meant that the fabulous staff at the train station location would be gone. I've gone in several times and each time the staff has either been rude or messed up an order. Unsweetened black tea is not a difficult drink, but they manage to. I'll be going to Exton, which is saying a lot because they're slower than molasses in February.

This Venti Medicine Ball Tea is exactly what I needed! I encourage anyone with cold symptoms to try it.  I ordered mine extra hot and it was perfect!  The customer service was friendly and expeditious
This is a great little Starbucks in a busy shopping center. The baristas are always knowledgeable and helpful.

Ok, admittedly I'm not a huge fan of Starbucks and initially I only came in to use their WiFi...but the staff is really what won me over. Everyone that works there is super nice and if you come in regularly they make an effort to learn your name get to know you a little bit, which i like. They play good music too. And their passion iced tea is delicious. Hi Mrs. Deb!!!
Really good coffee and food but the drive thru workers are always rude and they always get our order wrong.

I like the coffee, but the line of cars going all the way around the store is a deterrent for going in.   You get boxed in when you park and it can be difficult to get out, depending on whether or not drive thru patrons are so busy looking at their phones to see you or not.   I won't go back.   I've tried three times.. too much of a zoo for me.   Stupid plan for drive thru!!

The staff here is great. They are always friendly, and helpful. It's one of my favorite local spots for a meeting, or just to work when I need to get out of the office for a bit. I wish they had more seating, which is the reason for the four stars.  But overall, I think this is the best vibe of a Starbucks in Fishers. As far as the drinks...it's Starbucks. They may not be the best or most creative, but they are consistent every time.

This location is the Starbucks closest to home - so when I do have a hankering, this is where I usually end up! I've always had a good experience here. The drive through can get very backed up during peak times due to limited space, so I usually order ahead and park/run in to grab my drink. There is a small but nice outdoor patio that has a great mountain view. The location further north on Oracle is much bigger, but I prefer this cozier shop that is far more manageable and well run (at least in my experience). Thanks, crew!
This Starbucks starts sweeping and taking all the food away an hour before closing.  I don't like this.  Why come if they are closing at 10pm

This is the best Starbucks experience I've had. The young man that was taking my order was so nice. I wanted to try the Matcha creme Frappuccino, but was unsure if I would like it. He talked to me about it for a minute or so and I agreed to try it out. I'm glad I did. It was really good. After I had started drinking it, he got my attention and checked on me to make sure I liked it. No one has ever done that at any Starbucks before. He was a really nice guy with excellent customer skills.

Girl in drive through window dropped the two quarters I put in her hands, asked me to get it for her when clearly i had no room to open my car door but I attempted.. ended up just giving her more quarters I had instead. She was not very friendly. I asked for my frap to specifically be made thick with extra ice and it was liquid):

I just wanna give an All star review for your drive thru Lara! She was so awesome and pleasant to strike up a convo in line while I was waiting. Super quick and made my order perfect. Great team today :)

I've given this location several opportunities to change my mind. But every time i come they disappoint me. I'm a pretty basic coffee drinker, just a basic java chip trap is what I get each time and every time they have made it has been terrible. From a compete mess covering my hands in chocolate to completely watery, or flat no chocolate. The only reason I even placed two stars is because of a funny gentleman running the drive threw admitting his faults and making it laughable. The rest of the staff I've dealt with im lucky if they even make eye contact with me and I'm a pretty friendly person so I'm surprised.

I love this Starbucks so much. The staff is ALWAYS so friendly and wonderful. They know our orders in+out and can recognize us in the drive through. I feel at home there and the workers seem to enjoy being with each other which I absolutely love and is so important to me to see them having fun while working. I really do love them all and consider them as friends. I always say bye when I leave to everyone and hear a chorus of "bye Sarah" while I'm walking out. One of my favorite places to be. I love you guys!!

We stopped in for a iced tea on a very warm day.  Customer service was good.  My tea was good as well.  The store felt very warm inside, and we ended up going out and sitting in the car with air on to cool off.
A Starbucks. This is a go to Starbucks when there are holidays or you are up early. Yes they are open real early and often.   Clean.   Friendly staff!

The last couple of times I've been here I went through the drive thru , hours said they were open and no one answered. Not sure if they've changed their hours or if someone is being lazy. This was on a Thursday at 9:20 pm. It's the second time it's happened.
I waited in the drive thru line for over 30 minutes.  You can clearly see in the windows that the employees are just chatting away and not making the customers a priority.
Slooowwwww. Make sure you place your mobile order 35 mins in advance if you want it somewhat on time.

It was a Sunday morning. 20 plus people in line. Tyler was the at the cash register, slowly walking and talking and acted like he just had a huge breakup or something . About 3 minutes just to take an order and payment. Severely unhappy guy not even trying to go a little faster for everyone waiting in line.  The barista making the drinks was faster and trying to speed things up, but her team member was holding her up.  Unfortunately there isn't another choice for coffee in the am inside the hotel.

We stopped in here to quickly warm up with a cup of coffee. I always am nice to people serving me, especially in big cities, because their jobs are probably stressful. Plus, unless you were raised in a cave, you should just say please and thank you regardless. Even with that though, I'm bummed the staff was terse and unfriendly. When I asked if they had decaf coffee they almost seemed angry I asked. Soooo awkward.  I've experienced better because the layout didn't seem conducive to this location either. For the size of the space, the tables were oddly placed and everyone was packed in like sardines. The people next to us were doing some ODD things and we were exceptionally close to them. While the coffee was fine, just consider getting in and out. Its the best route!

I have only been to this Starbucks location a few times and as I recall each experience has been bad. Today was the same. I went through the drive through and placed my order of an Americano & Brownie. When I got to the window the barista held out the scanner to read my card as she was talking on her headset to the next client in the car behind me. She did not greet me - she was busy helping the client After me. Once she took a break from the drive through clients After me - I told her I wanted to get my brownie for 50 points. She said ok with a displeased look & said she'd have to void the transaction and ring for the 50 points. That took a few minutes & a manager had to come over to assist with the void (the manager did not greet me either) during the wait time she did not greet me, did not make small talk & was not pleasant in any way. She had handed me the brownie when I pulled up but as yet not given me my coffee. I asked for the receipt when she said she had to void the transaction - and when she got the receipt and handed it to me (she was on her headset talking to a client) she seemed finished with me. I said "I still need to get my Americano" - she said very rudely "give me a moment", in fact she'd had SEVERAL moments. A minute or so later she handed me my coffee without a thank you. I do not plan to return to this location.

This place is awesome!! It is my birthday and  I made a special request! They went over and above to accommodate it! No hassle , no complications  the late night crew that worked 04-24 deserves the recognition!    I'm a stickler about my Starbucks and if it wasn't so far from home it would be my regular! I will definitely stop by on my way to Tampa!!  You go Starbucks crew!! You have a new fan here

when I visit this starbucks, if I take the drive thru, I sit in line for 30 minutes, then another 5 because they dont make your drink til you get to the window to pay  I walked inside today and myself and about 10 other people have been standing around waiting for our drinks for 20 minutes  this starbucks needs restaffing, badly.  every time I'm here it's like it's the first day for everyone working

Funny how we're writing A review on a chain that prides itself in consistency. The day before coming here I had gone to a different location and had a wonderful peppermint chocolate holiday frappe. However, I had a Vente frappe here with the same peppermint chocolate flavoring and was sick the entire evening with some kind of stomach bug. I don't know what they put in their coffee or possibly just dirty fingers is what my partner and I were thinking, but it wasn't a pleasant experience. What is more.... The flavor on my partner's drink which was the chai latte had to be doctored up. I thought to myself it's crazy that we spend five dollars plus onto drinks that are on sale and have to come home and add ingredients to make the flavor at standard. Probably better to order inside then to go through the drive-through as you can taste, watch them prepare it and ask for any extras. At least they do offer to redo the drink as she had way too much cinnamon and her latte. Luckily the cinnamon was on the top so I could skip it off before I had to add the extra honey to sweeten the mix.  AND the seating is so sterile. Nothing comfortable. Just hard wood chairs and a HUGE desk like table with metal military style stools.
Rude employees at the window. Horrible beverages and overall a DISGUSTING adventure at SBUX

Not much to review ... it's a Starbucks; what can you say, except to be glad they have one in the mall! You get your drink, and hope it's up to par. If not, they'll make you another one. So relax, and hope they get your drink right. Also, prices are a little higher than other stores, the barista couldn't explain why. But that's ok- maybe they're a privately operated.   There are few tables and chairs, so it is convenient to stop, have coffee, and strategize shopping plans.   They use the app, and it's convenient.

If your into a good cup of coffee then STARBUCKS is the perfect place for that. And a good breakfast sandwich to go with it. Personally I go to 282 Los Altos Park way. The staff is amazing they will literally go out of their way to help or please you. I highly recommend the Chicken Dubbe smoked Bacon!!!

Good service! This review is based on a drive-thru experience only. I was served quickly, the staff was friendly and the coffee was very hot and fresh. I didn't get anything fancy today just a cup of Pike...

Great location for coffee among all the restaurant bars in St Pete. Nitro Cold Brew is the best! Seating inside and outside. A little roomier inside than some. Tables could be cleaner. Wasn't very busy.
A road trip stop en route to Orlando and the team was not prepared for a crowd. Inexcusable for the lack of preparation and urgency in the current staff here on this day.
This Starbucks is convenient if you're in Manayunk.  Staff have always been nice, and I've had my fair share of coffee drinks here.  For you drivers out there, street parking can be a royal pain.

The cashier was extremely rude and and refused to help us when our corporate cards chip didn't work. She said she didn't know what to do and refused to help. I was after I told her we would go somewhere else, she stepped up to help. How rude. I won't return to this Starbucks again.

I love most of the partners who work here, but honestly there is a lot that could be improved upon. First of all, this Starbucks is ridiculously busy, all the time. It really seems like Plant City could handle another Starbucks, maybe closer in town, that catered more to locals. The employees here are always so busy that they don't always have time be super friendly, like you expect at Starbucks.   Most of what I have to say is not the employees fault, but maybe their managers and regional managers. It is great that they are open 24 hours, but even that doesn't seem to give them enough time to clean. Their bathrooms are used like a truckstop bathroom and seem to get cleaned just as often.   As I stated, please know that the employees here are generally very nice. Please send them help Starbucks gods, they need it...

Worst customer service at a Starbucks I've ever experienced. The servers were rude to the customers. I forgot to ask for a water when ordering and was told to get back in a very long line to get a water. Employees were talk amongst themselves instead of paying attention to customer. Server asked what I wanted three times because she was not paying attention. I will not be returning.

Came before 8am. Great service at the register but terribly slow at making the drink. I ordered an iced green tea. I waited over ten minutes. Will go out of my way to another location versus coming back.

I've been to this Starbucks so much it isn't funny. Usually they are fast and nice, but this particular visit TJ was a little rude. She had a bit of attitude when I asked her what the largest size was called. She exhaled in annoyance and said the name very flat. Then when we went to pay I told her we had a buy one get free and she made her voice high pitched and said, "okay, I'm going to have to scan it twice" as if it we're going to have to be a problem. Overall though, I love this Starbucks. It's convenient and my go to for sure.
Three $8 drinks Not even close to being made correctly in three days. This Starbucks has gone from being the best to the worst SAD STORE
Just went to this location for the first time today. They were quick and friendly. Lots of places to sit too which is awesome. Keep up the great work, Starbucks!
If you want pay full price for a half a cup of coffee this is the perfect Starbucks for you! If there was an option for 0 stars I would have done so.

Let me start this review by saying Fishers and the Greater Indianapolis Area needs more late night coffee spots! I've bounced around several college towns over the last 16yrs and it baffles me that when I'm feeling a late night need go study, write, and just get out with a friend and talk over coffee, my only consistent late night option is IHOP .  That being said, this is a...drumroll please...Starbucks!!! You know the menu. It's consistent. However, why I give this specific Starbucks 4* is because I love the vibe/atmosphere AND is it open until 11pm on Friday and Saturday night! Most of my study time happens in the evening once my kids go down and it's nice to have a place in Fishers to go. The baristas are really nice, the menu is consistent. There are plenty of tables and outlets to go around. And it's open late(r)!

The team was kind and the drive-thru time was nice and short even with it being full. We especially appreciated the kind worker at the window handing out drinks. Our peppermint mochas were also made perfectly.  We passed through and needed a caffeine fix. It was a perfect location right off the highway between STL and Chicago.

The service here is horrid. I have not visited this location in a while because of it. I gave it another try today just because and now understand, it is what it is. This location hires the most unpleasant cashiers available.

I can see why this Starbucks has a higher rating than some of the others. It's quieter and therefore you get a lot more customer service from the baristas. Our drinks were made perfectly. That's not always the case.  There's a nice little patio area and the inside is a little more cozy then some of the other locations. Also, it's a lot cleaner since they're not so hectic. If you're a drive-by girl guy, they have that too. It's pretty easy to access it off of Oracle, and be right back on the road. I like to hang out if I'm going to enjoy some coffee. Give it a try, you might just like it!

We always get friendly and professional service here plus properly made drinks. We buy a hot chocolate for my wife's manicurist each month and sometimes an espresso for myself. Thanks for being on point!  Life is good with espresso!

I've never been the one to review restaurants but Ive never been to such a disorganized Starbucks.  My order was completely wrong, they forgot to give me my other items and when I spoke up about it they made it seem as though I did not pay for that item. I was just in complete astonishment. The cashier was rude and did not greet me as I approached the register.  I am so disappointed. I will not return here.

This is my favorite Starbucks because the baristas are always excited to see me. They always make my drinks with love. The store is a very busy location and I've noticed popular times of day are early morning and after school so I avoid those times of day if I can. My go-to drink is a sweet cream cold foam cold brew.
I go to this Starbucks almost twice a week and they are so awesome. The customer service is wonderful and they are always always super friendly.

The staff here are great! I've been coming to this location for over a year now, and I haven't had them ever get an order wrong. They always are very friendly, I've had the drive thru sing to me as I've approached the speaker multiple times. It is a very large location.  Unfortunately it is an extremely busy location. Drive thru lines going back to the street are not uncommon. Due to the volume of business they can be rather slow.  Overall, it's my favorite Starbucks in the area, and I find it worth the occasional delay due to how busy it is.

Went here today went thru the drive thru and had my grandson who is 4 months old with me. Ordered 2 blacktea lemonades sweetened.  Drinks were not sweetened  at all. Had to go in to fix it and instead of making new drinks  the guy opened  the kids and added more sweetener. Poor service

Drive thru is not slow which is a game changer! Lol The girl was so nice that when she saw my dog she offer us a puppuccino without even asking! Great service and nice location. Loving their Peppermint mocha latte!
As soon as I woke up...I walked towards the window...pulled the curtains apart...rubbed my eyes....AND BAMMM..Starbucks.  I knew what I was having for breakfast.  Woo hoo!

I want to first start off day that this store and the employees there are very revolting. The bathrooms were dirty, the bar was super messy and I being the customer seeing that makes me not want to go there ever. I have went there only one time and will never go again due to the fact that there are very rude and unprofessional employees there. This one employee made a huge big deal behind my back and a friend behind our backs. This employee name is Xander. He has had made various accusations about me and my friend having a fetish with transgenders. First off that is very untrue and that we had no idea this employee was transgender. He should be employed at this store or any Starbucks. He has been so rude at the times when me and my friend was there. Aside from that the store was very comfortable to do some studying but besides that Xander should not ever be employed at any Starbucks. He has worked at the Tyrone Starbucks and had accused everyone at the Tyrone store that they were out to get him. I hope someone sees this and have a talk with this person. WARNING!!!!! IF YOU GO TO THIS STARBUCKS PLEASE BE CAREFUL HE MAY BRING SOMETHING UPON YOU.

I visit this Starbucks a couple times a week--I only use this one because of convenience on my way to work. It is consistently the slowest Starbucks I have ever visited. It often takes 10 minutes or more in the drive through. They are in absolutely no rush. In addition, half the time I order they have run out of whatever I'm ordering. If you are crunched for time, you may want to drive out of your way to visit another--you'll probably save time in the long run.

Those of you who follow my reviews know that I am not a big fan of coffee. In fact the thought of knocking back a mug of percolated, roasted Columbian bean juice pretty much turns my stomach. No matter how much I might like that early morning blast of caffeine to clear the cobwebs after a night of sharing a bottle of Caymus Special Selection Cab during dinner, I just can't handle the taste of coffee. Nope, sorry, it ain't gonna happen!  Now that we have that out of the way I'll continue with my review....  So there I was, innocently minding my own business, as my friend and I strolled the sidewalks of SOHO one afternoon, when she suddenly perked up and gushed, "Oh look.....a Starbucks!". She used her feminine trickery to convince me to stop at the store so that she could order a mega-cup of Grande Mocha Triple-shot Soy Milk Salted Caramel Flan Latte blah blah blah while I ordered a simple hot green tea. (How in the hell does anyone remember the name of a drink that must take 5 minutes to write down all of the ingredients?) Oh, and she also wanted a low-fat blueberry whole-grain yogurt muffin with honey ("because it sounded yummy").  We found a quiet table in a corner of the store, and sat down to chat while waiting for the barista to call the name on our order. (I had momentarily pondered giving my name as 'Beelzebub', but I decided to behave.) Perhaps I was captivated by my date's lovely smile, or the friendliness of the folks behind the counter, but I began to calm down and enjoy my time inside of this coffee shop.  The place was jam-packed with people typing away on their laptops and conversing on their cellphones. There was a group in the corner playing some sort of word game, and every so often they would shout out a random off-the-wall, and generally inappropriate, word and the whole shop would bust out laughing. Another group was playing a board game, and so the mood was very festive.  As I sipped my tea I took in the décor of the shop which included vaulted high ceilings, huge plate-glass windows, tables and chairs which could easily be pushed together to accommodate any-sized group, a large outdoor patio where the locals sat with their four-footed furry friends, super-friendly folks behind the counter, and everyone laughing as they worked or played games or simply chatted away. The smells of the various drinks and food items was pleasing, and I am told that the muffin was divine, though I was threatened with a fork stab to the back of my hand if I even thought about reaching across the table to try a bite.  The folks from Starbucks love to say, "Wake up and smell the coffee -- Starbucks is everywhere," and rightly so as the world's #1 specialty coffee retailer has more than 16,000 coffee shops in more than 35 countries. They've built a massively-successful brand, and you have to admire all of that work and the quality of their products.  You'll find this particular Starbucks located along South Howard just south of Swann Avenue, and in easy walking distance of several popular restaurants and neighborhood watering holes. The shop is open at 5am each morning, and stays open till 11pm on weeknights, and then till 12 Midnight on Friday and Saturday nights for your late-night Java fix. There's a huge parking lot out back, so plenty of room to c'mon down and meet your friends.  I'll never be a coffee drinker, but I am a huge fan of this neighborhood gathering spot and the service that they provide to the local community. Oh, and they have hot tea and cocoa to keep me happy!

This Starbucks is great. They are fast and always polite but we have had some trouble with a few of their workers recently being mean to my mom and myself when we order. They have said things under their breath or laughing behind our backs.   This does not happen every time we go though. It's a standard Starbucks and doesn't really stand out on any special. Just know that they may be a little slower due to the popular choice of drive-thru there.

First negative review for Starbucks. I will be contacting the store. To begin I am not an avid Starbucks drinker or coffee drinker. I go occasionally for their frappes or iced teas. I am currently pregnant and drinking a lot of caffeine is not recommended especially if your body is already not used to it. I went today craving an iced coffee and request a decaf iced coffee. They said they don't have that and instead recommended an Americano. Again, I am not an avid coffee drinker so I'm not familiar with this drink but it sounds like it has a lot of caffiene. So I clarified that I am pregnant and cannot have a lot of caffeine, is this drink low in caffeine. The girl said yes. So I pull up to the window and I clarified once more that here is minimal caffeine and she said yes our decaf is minimal caffeine.   I get to work and take 3-4 sips over the course of 30 minutes. I begin to feel a head change and quickened heart rate. I have not had much caffeine at all these past few months so I begin to worry. I look up the details and turns out Americano is espresso & Starbucks no longer offers decaf drinks after 12pm and their "decaf" is watered down espresso.   I am completely upset by this for being misled. Fortunately I barely drank any but caffeine can really harm your pregnancy is you have too much especially when you are not a regular coffee drinker. This was not ok.

I tried to do some work here today and I couldn't even with ear buds on.  Maybe I should have brought my larger surround sound headset instead.    One of the reviewers who said that there a lot of ladies from nearby who like to talk really loud and obnoxiously to draw attention to themselves or talk loud on their phone inside was spot on.  I left this afternoon after about 30 minutes after this couple of bleached and Botoxed middle-aged women with their trendy yoga pants were way too loud at the table next to with a seemingly never-ending torrent of petty complaints, gossip about other women and their kids, and name-dropping.  I came here for some coffee and to get some work done.  Not a headache.

I will NEVER go to a Starbucks again!!! Constantly screwed with their erratic app. Starting to screw up a lot of orders. And their practically inaccessible to communicate with. NEVER follow through on committments...... I really loathe this company and have made it a personal mission to dissuade as many people as I can to ever support this company

My wife and i went here this morning. There was an obviously "disturbed" man sitting at a tall table near where we sat down at the large community table. I quickly noticed he was staring first at my wife and then at me. I looked away and when i looked back he was still staring. We moved to a far corner of the store. After awhile he stopped staring and began having a nice conversation with himself and making odd hand gestures. Yes he is probably harmless.  My main complaint is that when we brought this to the store's attention the response we received was "oh yeah.he is a regular here.Its his safe zone and he is harmless" Very nice and caring. However i think when you are a paying customer (and we have been going to Starbucks for over 15 years here and in Seattle) you are entitled to a peaceful environment. I thought the store response was inappropriate and unprofessional. We told the store we will not return and they seemed unconcerned to say the least. I have seen similar situations in our Seattle Starbucks handled professionally and in a proper safe manner.   This place needs some customer service training/upgrades for sure!

I hit this location up on my way to work 5 days a week.  I love the drive-through staff.  They are funny, upbeat and always get my order right.  Unfortuantly I cannot say the same for the weekend staff.  I sat at the order center for 3 minutes before someone took my order last Sunday and then waited 5 minutes for them to acknowledge me at the window.  I finally gave up and drove off.  I know that you can't have everyone work 7 days a week, but I would suggest some cross training between the week day morning crews and the weekend crews.
Never answer the phone, also they are very rude and ugly every time I go there. I don't recommend going to this location.

This is a fantastic Starbucks in the heart of Downtown Franklin. Good line management, friendly baristas and clean store. Excited to see what the remodel brings. It is a great Starbucks but if you are visiting and want something local walk down the same street to Frothy Monkey for an amazing local business that has a coffee stand inside their cafe/restaurant.

I have been going to Starbucks for so many years that it's almost a daily event for me and my hubby!  I'm so glad they are open thru this awful pandemic!  You know what's funny:  I don't like coffee at all. The only way I drink it is if doesn't taste or smell like coffee!  I actually feel bad for the employees who take & prepare my beverage, lol.   I am so picky. My order is always the same and EXTREMELY specific. I literally specify exactly how I want it and considering how expensive they are, I believe I should "get what I pay for" (I'm not trying to be difficult, I just w as want what I want, ya know?)  So, I politely say, "Hiiiii, Can I please have a HOT Venti Sugar-Free Carmel Macchiato with 9 pumps of your Sugar-Free Vanilla Syrup, just 1 Shot of Espresso, 2% Milk & 4 Splenda with a large Straw".    Now if you've read my reviews, you know that's just my personality. I'm not rude at all, I am always super polite (unless you're rude, lol.   Now, I always tell my hubby to wait a sec before pulling away/leaving until I sip it to make sure it's right bc if not, I'm going to send it back & ask them to make a new one.   I mean, for what we are paying, we deserve to have it right,  Right?  Yup.   So, I highly recommend that you go to Starbucks and get a Yummy beverage made just for you to your specifications!!  I Love, Love, Love Starbucks Hot Carmel Macchiato

Great Starbucks with awesome staff. The coffee is always fresh, and it is served with a smile (seriously). The only thing that keeps this one from being a five star in my book is how small the store is inside. It's a good spot to grab a quick cup of coffee, but not so great to chill for a while.

Kindness!  One of my kids cut their knee on the monument and the staff was amazing!  Not only did they have a Band-aid, but they gave each one a cup of water!  Great staff and they found the time to care while serving customers!  Busy place~awesome location!

Great drinks, but the service here is SO SLOW. I have gone inside and through the Drive-thru and there's never any sense of urgency from the baristas. I feel like they take their time and don't consider the customers they are serving. It's just a bit frustrating when you're trying to get to work.

This Starbucks is super convenient to me but parking is not ideal. This location is usually very busy so tables are prime real estate. This location is also highly frequented by the homeless which is not usually a problem unless they are soliciting money. The staff is usually friendly and courteous but this location is never very clean. The floor rarely looks swept or mopped. The bathrooms are not maintained very well throughout the day either.

My favorite coffee place, they stay busy but they are always FAST and get my order right! Great staff, The employees are so FRIENDLY and really care about doing a good job. If you ever can't decide what to get , just ask one of the employees, their faces just light up as they pair you with your perfect drink!

There was a customer inside Starbucks that had a dog the entire time I was there... when I was getting my drinks another customer walked in with a dog. While I was heading to my car there was someone else walking towards the Starbucks door from the parking lot with their dog.   NOT a single employee asked if either customers dog was a service animal. Honestly it's gross... you are a retail food facility! Completely against Santa Barbara county health code regulations. In a matter of 10 minutes 3 dogs. Maybe I should put a call into The county health inspector.

Let me tell you about the tea!! I quit drinking coffee and pretty much all caffeine in April of 2015.  I still love the aroma of a coffee shop, but I have to find an alternate beverage. The barista was very helpful in assisting me with my choice of tea.  There is a menu card and the ones with 0-4 black dots beside them indicate the caffeine level.  I ordered the Youth Berry.  She puts 2 teabags into a grande cup.  It was ruby red in colour and smelled heavenly.  I'm not a huge fan of açaí  berry, but it was surrounded by enough other stuff that it was merely enhanced, not overpowering. Service was swift and friendly.  It was steady traffic, but not crowded.  Miss Laini and I had a lovely visit :) One thing with the ladies washroom.  We are not sure that the locking mechanism works.  We did not actually test our theory.  Just a hunch.

Used to be my favorite SB to go to! Now the staff changed or management or something because getting a drink in the drive thru is about the most frustrating thing u can imagine. I have been in like for 25 minutes before! Don't let the number of cars in line fool you. That doesn't seem to determine your wait time. I hate that it has declined in efficiency and staff friendliness. There are other SB around and actually have had more success getting out of my car, walking into target getting the drink and leaving than sitting in the drive thru line at this one!
One of my favorite Starbucks in the city. Employees are always nice and fast with drinks. If the drinks aren't good they will remake and give gift card.

I love this Starbucks location! It's definitely roomier than most, with plenty of varied seating options in close proximity to outlets or with outlets built in to the furniture itself. There's also plenty of "group seating" that would enable larger groups the space to work, study or meet.  Service is always fast and professional, and in the 10-12 times I've been here, I have only ever had positive experiences.  The space is super clean and shelving is all well organized. I can tell that the management and employees take pride in keeping their location in beautiful condition.  The walls are decorated with St. Louis themed memorabilia and the lighting fixtures are super fun. I really like the way they worked with the unique building space to create an open, welcoming vibe. This is also one of the few Starbucks locations in the area that stays open till 10pm, so if you are in need of a late night study spot, this is it!
Visiting from Atlanta the service I received from this location was by far the best! very kind and helpful! Will be back soon.

Returned back for a meeting and was surprised that this Starbucks has done away with the tables and chairs. I asked a barista what's with the change, she said that this location was a type of special cafe that's been used in France. I'm pretty sure that management has done away with the tables and chairs so that they can deter the homeless guys to escape from the cold and irk customers. The standing thing is just too awkward and it really doesn't give you much room to have a meeting with coworkers. Your best bet for studying, meetups and meetings is to go to either the Mass Ave. location or the one near Whole Foods.

Reminded me why I never go to this location.  Was ignored by staff so they could talk to co workers.  They actually told me I would have to wait until they could finish their conversation.  The girls behind the counter were talking so loud to each other the entire 2 hours I was there that multiple people commented about it and left.  They were literally screaming about gossip.  Totally unprofessional and distracting.  Serves me right for even coming here.

RUDE!!!! The girl taking my order was plain rude. I explained to her how I want to order food and a coffee however my card is never accepted at this location. I go to other starbucks' almost everyday. She said "that's not my fault we can't give you anything then" and pretty much shooed me away. I was so upset never coming back here.

Date of visit 04-24-2019:  I stopped into this Starbucks for a drink after driving out to Tampa for dinner celebrating my birthday.  I usually try to skip & forget that one day of the year that references to my age and that time waits for no one.  I walked into this location and was immediately greeted by 2 or 3 employees with a friendly "Welcome to Starbucks", which I have never experienced at none of the other locations! It was around 11pm and coffee shop was moderately busy and most of the seats were taken by folks actually drinking their coffee and talking to each other and not distracted by some form of electronic device.  I walked to the counter and explained to the employee  (I think it was Amy) that it was my birthday and I wanted to place an order for a Java Chip Frap. She wished me a happy birthday with a smile, asked for my favorite Starbucks Gold card. Other employee's heard her and also made a point to stop what they were doing and say happy birthday! I think its the most happy birthdays that I have ever got in one day :) ! It only took the employee a few minutes to get right on my drink making it and in my hands with another "I hope you have a wonderful birthday", which I really appreciated!  The store was very neat and the floors looked like they were just mopped, hard to believe with the amount of traffic that was in the store. The counters were stocked and everything was in place. If I didnt live 30 minutes away I would make this my new favorite Starbuck (Store cleanliness 5 stars). The Ambiance was really nice and laid back, there wasn't any loud music and the other customers didnt have to yell to talk to each other. I got the vibe that I could sit and just chill which I will make a point to do next time I pass by! Thanks for the great experience.

Disappointed by the staff (twice). I wasn't given my receipt and they charged me for the wrong size. When I asked about it, the barista said, "oh you wanted tall?" and that was it. Didn't offer to correct my order at all.

Interesting location! And interesting how people here look very wealthy. This is a great addition because there is a Target, Petsmart, and bank here. I came here because I went to my nearby apartment to pick up some stuff and dropped off my dog at Petsmart to get groomed. Now it's the waiting game.... so I decided to shop and then cozy up to some coffee here at Starbucks. Everything is clean, spacious, and bright! The ceilings are so high up, it makes the place feel more roomy. It's busy here, but not too busy. Staff here make sure you are being helped as well. Bathroom is very clean and well-lit. They might need more seating, but that's all I can say for now.

I'm not usually a Starbucks guy.    I wanted a coffee and was on the way to visit friends; if I showed up with one coffee, I'd have to put up with sad puppy dog eyes, so I stopped at the local Starbucks.  There really aren't that many other options in the suburbs of West Edmonton. I actually think the coffee and the espresso are pretty average and certainly not worth what they charge for it.  But, this stuff is way popular and well branded.  I mean walk into a real Italian café and try that Tall, Grande, Vente thing and watch the retarded child look you're going to get.  Only Tall means something to a European barista and it ain't what you get a Starbucks.  A "tall" coffee is a single expresso made with twice the normal amount of milk as a "short".  It's not a "size"; it's more volume because you're asking for more milk versus the more common "short".  Similarly at a bar, you can ask for your highball in a "short" or "tall" glass; same drink, more mix.  If they get that much wrong, how good can the coffee really be?  The really annoying thing is that this location has a drive-thru window.  Who's brainwave was that?  They have customers both inside and outside and one barista.  So, guess what?  There is a long suffering line in front of the counter and one snaking around the building.  Additionally, you have no real sense of where you are in line or when you can reasonably expect your order to come up.  The staff look stressed out; the bartista definitely is and there isn't a customer with a smile on their face for a good thirty paces.  Oh, what a good time.

I've been coming to this Starbucks since they first opened. I have had both good and bad experiences, albeit mostly good. I've often heard this is one of the busiest stores by volume in the country due to the very busy drive-thru. That being said it typically takes at least 15 minutes to get your drink of choice unless you show up at an off time.   About a year ago they started offering the coffee pressed from their new Clover machines. Around the same time they remodeled the location. They increased seating, albeit at a cost. The comfy chairs are in shorter supply. This is primarily the reason I've deducted a star.   The location needs to expand. Realistically they should have double the space with more comfortable chairs and at least another cash register.   All in all this location is a quality Starbucks with good service in a great location.

Went here just before closing and were happy to find it still serving. Ordered a nitro and frap and two water because we'd just changed a flat. They forgot the waters and didn't mix the drinks. We asked for a straw to mix the drink but they said they were closed now and couldn't open the window to give us a straw. We went to Luciana's for straws. Love that place.

This is one of my favorite Starbucks. It is a convenient location. Lots of businesses we use are close by it. The one down side about the location is that it's busy so it is sometimes hard to park. The drive thru is accessed between buildings and could be easily missed.   The workers, however, are always friendly and helpful. They are quick. They are always efficient. The store is near and clean. I stopped on Christmas Eve while out running a last minute errand. I was nervous about stopping on a holiday like that. The drive thru moved quick and the workers were more than friendly and made my Christmas Eve great!

A busy morning at SB but everyone is still friendly and wearing smiles.  Place is clean. Just here writing for a few hours.  This really is a new space and bigger than other SB.  Thanks for adding this location.
Wow. The worst Starbucks ever. A 33- minute wait and two of the three drinks were wrong.

Across the street from Golds Gym. Don't blink or you'll miss it. Numerous wall outlets, a plus for us battery life challenged. Quite an impovement over many stores with only one or two outlets! Counted 4 on the west and south side for 3 tables.

Although there are several Starbucks locations around the country that are open 24 hours now, this location will always be the original "24 hour Starbucks" to me. Located conveniently right off of the I-4 at the Thonotosassa exit, this place is a must stop if you are making that typical trek from Orlando to Tampa or vice versa. Why, you ask? Well, because there is delicious coffee and tea, clean restrooms, and delectable pastries. Really, what more could you ask for? There is even a drive-thru for added convenience!   The size of the Starbucks is ideal--room for casual seating and lounging, but not so huge that it's overcrowded and crazy. I think the drive-thru option helps prevent that. Also, the location right off of the I-4 (note the GIANT Starbucks sign over the highway), makes it easy to pull in and out of. I've never experienced any problems here, and also I associate it with roadtripping across Florida, so...5 stars!

SIGH.....for such a great location this Starbucks borders on mediocre.  The WIFI signal kept stopping and starting, by far the WORST WIFI I've encountered in any Starbucks.  Staff was somewhat friendly, could use some more classes on proper public service, other than that nothing outstanding at all.  If you're in this part of the city and want a drink, sure stop in.  If you're looking for a place to do some surfing or working online while you relax with a coffee, stay home.  WORST WIFI EVER.

Rude, stupid teenage employees. I asked specifically for a pour-over, because their coffee was stale. Kept telling me it would take 10 minutes. I finally said, never mind. And walked away, and she says, whatever

Pros- They had good service and free samples ( I had a chili latte of some sort that was quite interesting)  Cons- The parking is difficult and limited. Also, it is basically located a few yards from a busy highway on ramp, so getting out of the lot into traffic is a little busy at times. I just went out the exit towards the local roads to avoid this problem.

The first time I came here was during the morning rush and the line was REALLY long, but I needed my cup of coffee. Lol. Even though the line was out the door it went by pretty fast. The Barista that made my drink was so nice! She called my name as soon as my drinks were ready (I ordered my favorite holiday drink: Grande Nonfat Caramel Brûlée latte & Hubby's Venti Caramel Frap with extra caramel) and she even told me if I didn't like it or needed more caramel to just let her know. But it was perfect! For years I've been going to Starbucks and no one has ever told me that especially when they are super, super busy. I could tell right away the baristas working here love there job. They have the best customer service and value there customers.

This location never understands how to make drinks with any other milk other than dairy.. they get confused and just put dairy in it anyways... for the pumpkin cold cream all you need to do is add the cold brewed coffee, the vanilla sweetener and then foam almond milk.. you do not need to add dairy at all. The person getting the almond milk just wants the almond milk splash, we don't want a lot of milk and like the drink not as sweet. What is so hard?  just don't add dairy milk...
Got to the store 7:55pm - realize only 5 minutes to close, but the Barista told me they could only make me a cappuccino because they were "out of coffee".

This location is consistently unavailable for ordering ahead during a time (pandemic lockdown) when you can only order ahead. They got my drinks right the two times that I was able to order of the six or more times that I tried.

Wonderful! Our dogs eye got scratched by a cat, so we took him to a local animal hospital. It took a long time to get him (3 hours), so we got some Starbucks to get us through. We stopped by, and this one lady was SO nice! She sent us prayers for our sweet puppy, and was very polite! Our food and drinks were made in under a minute, and the service was amazing! Would recommend going here!
Kept getting my order wrong. Charged me a second time for one of my drinks. I wouldn't recommend anyone I know to this location.

I rarely go to this Starbucks location but it was on my way to Mass Ave and I was trying to complete a bonus points challenge. My New Years Resolution was to give up Starbucks. So far  - it's not going that well! I got in the long drive through line that moved Very Slowly. The car behind me bailed out. But of course eventually I got up to the window and got a cold brew drink ( to check off another bonus stars requirement) and used 50* to get a sweet treat (pumpkin bread - it's delicious). The barista was very friendly and both my drink & the pumpkin bread were good.
They are always out of food items. Don't follow your orders. Rude when you ask a question. Super excited about having this new Starbucks but hate the service:/
Great staff in a very busy part of town.  Good job SBUX.

The Goods Cannot function without a latte. Leboulangerie is the new bakery and it is much better than before   Ambiance 1. I like the faint lights in the ceiling kind of rustic chic  Service  1. These people know me all the time!!!  Remember me by name!!  MoJo 1. Anything drink, I love them all but sugar free coffee is my fav

I've been hesitant to leave a review because I know every company has their bad days, but now it's been 5-6 times and I can't dismiss it anymore. I stop at this location's drive thru on my way to work most mornings and quite few times I'm greeted with, "What do you want" instead of the usual "Welcome to Starbucks.." greeting. I typically order multiple things, and many times I've been cut off after the first item in my order with, "Alright your total is ___, pull around" even if I wasn't finished. After getting my order at the window, I rarely get a "Have a good one" or anything, they don't say a word. No smiles or anything. I just feel like I'm bothering the employees by being there. They all look irritated. I'll probably start going to the one closer to my work from now on.

This is the best places for food ever I come here whenever I can someone to take me I'm 9 so I can't drive but if o could I would drive everyday even though it takes 30 minutes to 1 hour. Best place ever

Good morning Yelpers. So for those of you who follow me you know that coffee is a must for me every single morning. Typically I go to the Starbucks near my home but today I had an early errand so I stopped by the Starbucks near the Brandon mall.  This is a very, very nice Starbucks. not only was the area inside clean but there was an area outside, a very big area, that was also well kept and clean. they have a lot of umbrellas outside to keep those who want to sit out in the Sun shaded.  I was very impressed that outside there was no trash in a sitting areas and all the tables we're wipe down and clean. As with all Starbucks I was greeted when I first walked in and my coffee was served quickly and it was just the way that I like it. I will definitely be returning to the Starbucks.   :-)

I love Starbucks but this neighboorhood Starbucks is as good as it gets. The employees are friendly, the atmosphere is peaceful and the product is wonderful. Next time you make a visit to the Delmar Loop check it out and get a Vanilla Bean Frappicinio with Carmal sauce.

always super sweet and actually makes drinks in reusable cup and doesn't make it in plastic then dumps it into reusable one also never had a drink made incorrectly, but a great place to spend your time studying or working!!!

You have one of the rudest kids employed at this location. Slamming things like a child,  then places an empty cup and says NOTHING.. 10 min later I walk up to the register to see why my order is incomplete and all I get is oh we're probably out of that.. um ok then COMMUNICATE to the PAYING CUSTOMER.  Rude and unprofessional.
Worst Starbucks I have ever been too every time I've gone service is always good but the drinks are terrible tastes like burnt coffee every time

Definitely not your normal, everyday Starbucks. This is unidenified flying coffee experience! Even the interior looks different. I'm not sure what it's like in their drive thru, because when I make the drive out here, I have to go inside and sit for awhile. The ambiance is perfect. There's tons of room for the students from SLU & surrounding universities to come and study and...*drum roll* THEY HAVE A CLOVER. Oh my goodness, when this store opened with a Clover -- I literally screamed. It is the best tasting brewed coffee you will ever have. Unfortunately, that is reflected in the price. Around $3-ish for a tall? Kind of makes you feel bad when you put your creamer in.

I would like to give Starbucks 5 stars because they are so nice and there service is great and there drinks and food is magnificent it's practically to die for the store is always clean and tidy and it's so nice and cute and a great and quiet place to do homework or work I totally recommend going to this Starbucks
Joe the manager here has turned this place around!!!  He understands customer service and has taught his people well!! This store is now on fire and no complaints ever!

So, let's start this off in the proper context. I'm not the biggest fan of Starbucks coffee. I think it's a bit too acidic, and I prefer a generic breakfast blend over anything they make.  That being said, this location has great staff, it's clean and I have no complaint other than my coffee preference. I generally rate Starbucks as a chain, so 3 stars is what I expect I expect if any chain. This place gets 4 stars, so that tells you it's cleaner, friendlier and more worthwhile than other locations.   Order, add Splenda, drink up and enjoy!!!
While I am not a coffee drinker, my wife loves the place. She also buys 2 bags of coffee and has them grind and blend them together.

They sold me not one, but TWO moldy protein boxes and that's supposed to be a mango dragonfruit drink. "We're out of the dragon fruit but it's just the same stuff." Server said. But if I'm paying for a $5 drink, I want the fruit. And I want my overpriced apples to be edible.
I don't know why I keep doing this to myself. This has got to be the worst and slowest Starbucks in the city. These so called baristas need more training.
My only complaint is that they NEVER have their drive thru open. I don't understand why because when I go in it looks like they have enough people to cover it. ‍

I love this Starbucks location for many reasons. If you are a fan of their coffee, you know your drinks and food will be consistent from location to location. But this location has a few small things that make it worth the drive to!   The parking lot is massive. This is helpful for anyone who likes to go inside in the mornings or weekends.   This location has a drive thru! Thank goodness because I do not like to go inside on my way to work everyday because my morning is busy busy. So the drive thru option is crucial. It has a large screen where you can see the items you have ordered and see the menu. Very nice!   I also love how large the interior is! There are so many tables and seating areas to choose from. Very easy to grab a book or your laptop and enjoy a table to yourself.   The people who work here are also lovely and friendly. They get to know their regular customers and if you aren't a regular you will be soon!

No there's not a drive thru however... this location offers pleasant, efficient service. Plexiglass ordering for safety, indoor & outdoor seating options along with 6' distance placards for a gentle reminder that people should do the customary and respect space.  ~Christy

A sense of urgency is not something the employees of this particular store possess. I've placed mobile orders on three separate occasions in the past few weeks and (knowing they are always very slow paced) even gave it an extra 15 minutes prior to showing up to grab my drink and STILL had to wait an additional 10 minutes or so. Not cool.
Slowest Starbucks I've ever been to- 2 cars ahead of me and I waited in line 20 minutes!!!
Has gone so down hill. Drinks are horrible every time and management sucks. Something needs to be done asap.
If you're open until 830o, don't lock the doors at 8p and make people walk through the drive thru to get a drink
This is the third consecutive frapp I've had here that is watery. It's literally cold chai tea with whipped cream on top.

Terrible customer service. You ask for blond they give you medium roast thinking you won't notice. After complaining, they just gave me attitude rather than fixing the problem. Only after I contacted the head office after the 3 or 4th time they've done it, only then i was satisfied. Usually don't go to that location bc of that but sometimes I have nowhere else to go.
Getting a 5 star here. Super nice atmosphere. It's also very neat and clean! Polite baristas and delicious drinks.! No complaints on my end I'll definitely be back to this location

*CAUTION* The cars speeding through this cramped tiny parking lot to get to the drive thru are going to hurt or worse the customers coming from or trying to get back to their cars. There must be a resolve. TRAFFIC BUMPS ???

3/01/19 - Eddie's last day. He has been a plus plus for this location. He will be missed immensely. ;(  2/12/19 - So disappointed to hear that our wonderful asst manager Eddie The Arizona Pavilions location will be leaving us.   1/11/19 - VERY disappointed that they didn't have my favorite sandwich today, chicken and artichoke  11/20/18 - So disappointed today. Not one sandwich in the cooler at 11:15 am on   Once again we are here. Our favorite place. Love our Baristas. Jessica, Treigh, Caleigh, Alex and Taylor. If I've forgotten anyone please forgive me. So many great people work at this location. :) And of co

My wife came in to get us coffee so I can't really judge this place I guess. Still the coffee we got was good had no problems with it. The pace is small though and if you don't like dogs probably not the best place for you.

I go to this location multiple times a week. Baristas are great. Coffee is consistent. The drive thru line moves fast. Everything you need/want from a Starbucks. Not sure why other people are giving great reviews and then leaving 4 stars. You people are reaching hard for something to complain about. Go somewhere else. It only makes the line shorter for me.

So , I order a peppermint mocha iced coffee, not a Frappuccino . I have ordered this drink many times over the holiday seasons . In Brandon , Tampa , Orlando and Jacksonville locations . Never had a problem.  When they gave me the drink it was light in color heavy cream vanilla with clear liquid on the bottom. Clearly not a peppermint mocha but I take a sip to see what it was, and it was just pure mint . I definitely didn't taste chocolate and I definitely didn't taste any coffee. So I went up to the cashier and I said "hey this is not my drink I ordered a peppermint mocha."  She then told me that was my drink and I pointed to the picture to point out that it was not a peppermint mocha . She said it was .  Unfortunately I had to go to work so I couldn't sit there and argue with her. I threw my drink out on the way out, it was a un-drinkable. One of the most disgusting drinks I've ever had. So I basically just gave  Starbucks free money. Please do not go to the Starbucks unless you just want to give away your money.

Ample parking, ample seating and friendly baristas make this cozy Starbucks feel a little more local. Good spot to take meetings and get some work done. This Starbucks has all the same bells and whistles as other but with a friendly crew.

I am a Starbucks junkie-I get a quad-7 shot latte daily. In South Tampa, this is my fav location. Barista's are awesome, know you by name and drink. They are friendly and make it so easy to come back for more. They know ALL of my drinks-my substitutions for my seasonal drinks, my usual all year round drink, and my lunch drink. No questions asked-they have it already going as I am walking in!

My absolutely favorite Starbucks!! It's is always so clean and everyone is so friendly!! If I could give this location more than 5 stars I would!! There is a man working that was so kind to me last week it made my day, I think his name might be Joe, but love love love him!!
I go to this location on a regular basis, the staff is consistently friendly and helpful! Definitely recommend this location

Surprisingly comfy and cozy Starbucks. Lots of seating and two long tables. Half the customers are using laptops so very good place to work. Some outlets along the wall. 6 red chairs in the corner near the windows.   Restrooms to the right of the entrance.

I ordered a traveler for a meeting. A traveler is a cardboard box full of delicious- whatever you want. I get the blonde roast cause that's what those brainy people like. I've worked here at the TGH for 17 years and love that there is a place like this here. It's a safe haven for employees and patient family alike- stepping into another world for a break from life.   I called at night to order my traveler. It wasn't ordered in the morning so I was late to my meeting. Made me look bad, but I still love this place.

This is my most frequented Starbucks due to the fact that it is close to my office, but I also really like the staff! Everyone who has helped me has been extremely kind! I'm that girl who is known to have full on conversations with the staff members when the restaurant is not too busy! My only "complaint" is that they use stickers to mark your order on your cup rather than in hand writing with a Sharpie! The Sharpie makes me feel special for some reason. Haha!

I never get what the fuss is.  I hadn't had Starbucks in almost a year, but was jonsing for their java chip.  This happened during wonderful Hermine so daily I went there to find every location closed.  Finally on my 3rd day, I was in luck!  Following my order, my sandwhich was really nothing spectacular and I ended up tossing half of it (had no taste) and my java chip had no chips!  Tasted like a mocha frappe.  Definitely not worth $10.  If rather get one from McD's if I opt for something like this again

I've frequented this spot in the last few months because I've been working at a near by office.  The thing I like about this store is that it has a drive through that typically moves at a decent pace.  The bummer is that I order the same thing every day and it is never made the same. In addition I get charged differently for the exact same order (if I don't mobile order it).  Pretty much a decent spot that does have some outdoor seating.

Fairly rough experience here...  it could be pages of me explaining about the experience but I will save you the time.  The moral of the story is that I knew more about ringing up my fairly simple order than the guy working their did.  Sad but true.  If this hipster is working... I'll just about face.

Drinks were good. I had to wait to be acknowledged by one of the 3 baristas. They saw me, but continued doing what they were doing before finally coming over and greeting me to take my order.  Hospitality is one of the things Starbucks is known to deliver, so it's pretty much expected.  Otherwise, like I said, the drinks were good and the place was clean.

This Starbucks location is a franchisee located in the Bridgeton Target. I frequent this Starbucks when I am on a Target run and unfortunately it's the only one located on this long busy business strip.  There is an obvious difference between this franchisee Starbucks and other company owned Starbucks. They never have all the same pastries, wraps and salads that you'll find at a company owned Starbucks. They also receive limited quantities for seasonal drink additives so when they run out, they run out and they aren't able to restock.  Getting a consistent drink here is really hit or miss. The Associates here are not trained like Starbucks Baristas; so one day they may be stocking, one day they may be cashiers and the next day they may be here at the Starbucks counter. Over the past year I've become familiar with at least two ladies that work here often and when I come for an afternoon run and they are extremely helpful. I no longer have to state how many pumps of toffee-nut or mocha I would like in my drink (keep in mind you never have to do that at a company owned store because they are trained on proper usage and only deviate per customer requests). This location has lots of the regular Starbucks swag and seasonal coffee. Now you can get the 10-cent discount on your drink purchase when you bring in a travel coffee mug.  All in all, in a pinch and needing a legal addictive stimulant - this place will do.
Pretty good Starbucks but they are busy so I think some things fall through the cracks. But all in all a good spot with hot coffee.

Today, my boyfriend and I went to Starbucks. We had to repeat our orders 3x before the lady took our order. Then she never took our name and had a poor attitude. Our orders were wrong. We asked for iced coffees with almond milk, was charged for such and given hot coffee with regular milk.   It was highly irritating to have to repeat our order and still have it completely wrong. We couldn't wait to get it fixed since we had an appointment. It was just frustrating
I tried to give them a second chance. They are very rude at drive thru and my drink was incorrect. Save your time and go across the street to Wawa to get your cold brews and espresso

So my whole family including myself are all gold card rewards members went there 2 days ago and bought a iced caramel macchiato and a sandwich. The sandwich was cold and the girl made me a new one and the new one she double cooked, so it was dry as can be and hard as a rock on half of it!  My drink was made wrong completely but since I was in a rush I just accepted it and went to work very pissed off, we've been going there since the day you opened. And we go there all the time like 5 days a week very upsetting lately we've been coming into problems with our orders we're very picky about our coffee. I worked at the original Starbucks in Seattle for five years and this location Falls extremely short of being consistent in a good way!

I rushed to Starbucks because it said they close at 10pm and I get there right at 9:30pm and they tell me their closed. I tell the barrista I thought you guys closed at 10pm and she said on Thursday's we close at 9:30pm they need to fix their hourly schedule I'm highly unsatisfied and disappointed.

Generally speaking, the people working at this Starbucks are friendly and get your drinks just like you asked for them, so I've never had any complaint. Up to this morning I would have given 5 stars. But this morning the male server who handed out my drink at the drive thru had the filthiest finger nails I've ever seen. No food service worker should ever have such nasty looking hands. I couldn't find a way to comment inside the Starbucks app, so here I am. If it happens again I'll stop going to this store.

Worst Starbucks EVER! Every person that works in there is slow in the head. It's almost like they intentionally get your order wrong. When you ask for something toasted they barely warm it up...much less actually toast it. And who ever the blonde girl is needs to learn to mind her business. Lucky I've changed my life.

This Starbucks is very nice! In general I like Starbucks and it is consistent with others. My drink is always perfectly hot. When I order to-go from my app it is always waiting for me, which is super convenient!   I also like the outdoor seating. Although it's on a main road, they have a rustic wooden fence blocking the view of the cars going by. I always appreciate that they give free water if you ask as well, no purchase necessary!   My only critique is the price, obviously. I don't drink regular milk so I pay +$.60 for soy which is delish but makes my tall drink $4.33. That's a lot for a latte. However, I know it's a luxury and I have learned to expect it, which is why I still give it a 5 star review.   PS pumpkin loaf rocks my world
It's a simple little Starbucks right off the highway next to a gas station so very convenient when you're headed through in just need a quick cup of joe.

The service here is fast and the beverages have substance! The MetroBus is directly across the street! This place is open until 10 PM! I am going to make this one of my regular stops! I have three fave Starbucks here in St Louis! The one on 6th and Olive, the one across from Tower Grove Park and this one! Be of them have free Wi-Fi, good work spaces, a great casual surrounding and beverages with substance!

I am getting my quad shot expresso macchiato there for last ten years, baristas change few times but they continue to remember my drink and have it ready some-times before I even have a chance to pay for it.

I love Starbucks but this is the slowest Starbucks I've ever encountered. I've mobile-ordered multiple times and the consistently were unaware they even have had a mobile order. The drive-thru is always long and it always takes 20min to get a drink.

So I decided to venture away from home to tackle some homework this afternoon, and a good friend recommended that I check out a Starbucks in a Chapters location. Lucky for me, there's one right by my home in my beloved Chapters which I visit often for new books. I was able to snag a table right by the window, and ordered the toasted graham latte. The ambiance was lovely for my entire visit. Not too loud, as most others were also doing homework or reading, and some pleasant music was playing in the background. At one point a couple of gentlemen struck up some music on their guitars, which added an awesome vibe. I also appreciated how many electrical outlets there were, although my prime table unluckily was not  close to one so I couldn't use my laptop. But I'm not complaining.  My only wish for this spot was that they served meals, because I only left due to hunger!
They made they drinks pretty fast, and some of them were friendly. But they didn't have any table and chairs left when we went.
This Starbucks was filled with chandeliers... It was so fun and funky!!! Great decor!  This is right off the lobby in my hotel which was very convenient!!!   Love downtown Indy!!! For sure!
Lorraine was so sweet at the drive thru and was extra thorough even though they were busy. Drive thrus have been crazy because of covid, but Lorraine really was such a ray of sunshine!
Not enough staff to handle online pick-up orders.   Pick- up order times are not accurate. I would skip this one.
Must be one of the slowest Starbucks ever, every time I have been there. Ridiculous! Hire some more people for goodness sake!  Other than that, your typical starbucks.
Nice service but this location is filthy. Dirty tables window sills floors. Great goin.

The customer service from the girls here is great!  Taylor, Grayson, Michelle,  and Gabby all work so hard to make sure they get it right.  I had a real "karen" in front of me complaining the other day and Taylor dealt with it with grace and class,  which really,  they don't get paid enough to put up with a bunch of crap but she was nice about it anyways.   Great work girls!

Ann is one of the nicest people I have ever met.
