Starbucks is a place where a lot of people go to have conversations and particular people go to be specific and complicated to feel better about themselves but Ann made everyone feel like they were special, including those annoying caramel frappacino/caramel machiatto with extra caramel people. I was so incredibly impressed with her service and care. She is an amazing person and I hope she gets promoted to the highest position because she is what every Starbucks employee should strive to be, up to the highest position. Hire more people like Ann and your company will be nothing less than the best.

This location replaced the Starbucks that used to be up the street at Clearwater Mall. This location is very spacious and lets in a lot of natural light during the day. It is a little dark at night but still manageable if you're trying to do work here past sundown.  The baristas here are very efficient and I had no problem with my drink. The only gripe I have here is that the parking lot is tiny and super awkward. The drive-thru also snakes through the middle of it so getting in and out is also a bit tricky. I had to park at the adjacent restaurant's parking lot.  All things considered, it's not a bad Starbucks. It's very new so everything feels clean and sleek.

Just. So. Bad. I have numerous stories of nonsense with this store. But, today's shall be the feature. I order a dark roast drip coffee. Literally all that needs to happen is a cup needs to be placed under the spout and then, upon fillage, a lid must be applied. I waited for about 12 minutes for a MOBILE ORDER. No one checked in. I had to ask twice before the barista realized she was not, in fact, brewing any dark roast. The explanation was that they dump regular coffee after 11am. Welcome to crazy town. In summation, be prepared to wait bc this excursion took 20 mins beginning to end.

Hands down the best customer service in the industry right here. My wife and I have been coming to this location  for years and always have an incredible experience with the employees when we come in. Huge shout out to Parker for always being friendly and always making the best drinks! This will always be our coffee spot.

Came in late night, around 11:00 p.m. landing from a New York flight. Everything to eat was closed except for a grab and go store and Starbucks. Was greeted by 2 wonderful ladies who gave outstanding service and had the whole transaction done and my sandwich warm and ready for me to eat in under 5 minutes. Some of the best service I've received that any Starbucks, surprisingly!   What I liked most about it though? Prices are kept reasonable despite being inside the airport -- not like many other outlets that jack up the prices once your inside a terminal.  I think Starbucks will now be my place of choice to grab a reasonable, delicious quick meal before or after my flights! Very happy camper.

While the drive through has always been really fast, the store employees have always seem cold and rude...by far the most unfriendly Starbucks staff I've had to joy of meeting. The blank stares or annoyed looks I've gotten anytime I've greeted them good morning or said thank you does not make me want to return, period.   I get that it is a busy location and I truly wouldn't care about their lack of hospitality if the quality of my orders were okay..but I've gotten stale pastries, watery iced teas, and incorrect coffee orders there the handful of times I've visited. If I'm paying $5-6 for a drink I would love to not feel like I'm annoying the staff with my existence while still getting a bad order.

I'm not really a Starbucks type of person, but we received a gift card as a anniversary gift so we stopped in while at the mall. The cashier was cool when we told her we didn't know the lingo lol. It was a bit much just to order a drink and donut. However it didn't make the experience unpleasant. It's a nice area inside with a fair amount of seating, it was clean and I'd go back to Starbucks...if it was my choice of coffee stores.

The service and staff at this store is unmatched. We had a great first interaction, great conversation with the barista at handoff, and then after I spilled my entire coffee in my car, they provided a wet and dry rag and gave me a replacement coffee. They were so sweet and helpful.

WHERE IS KRISTINA!?!?!  My favorite ever barista/manager is MISSING!  Okay.......I'm sure she isn't missing.  I'm sure wherever she is she is fine.  I just have not seen her in a long time and she always makes my drinks 100% perfect.  Maybe I just go in at the wrong time now.  I'm sad.  What I will say is that this Starbucks (as the previous reviewer said) is consitant.  They make great beverages 99 times out of 100 and on the off chance that it is not right it's because the person who made it is very new and they fix it for me without any problems.  I've never had a problem with this location.  Great drinks and quick lines.  In and out super fast.  I find it funny that I can park in the Safeway parking lot and go in to order and get my beverage and get back to my car faster than I can get my drink in most Starbucks Drive Throughs.  Cheers! Stay Caffeinated!

Since starting law school, my obsession with Starbucks has gone to another level. I go literally everyday. It's a problem, lol. But out of all the Statbuck's in town, this is the one I frequent the most.   I love the drive-thru! I've been told I have a complicated Starbucks order, lol, but the staff here are always so patient and gracious.   Sometimes the drive-thru can go slower, but it usually never takes than 5-minutes to drive in and out. Perfect for my early morning coffee runs before class.   The only problem is the parking lot and drive-thru are somewhat tight, which I've seen makes it more difficult for larger trucks. But, it's seems manageable.   I've never studied inside this one, I usually study at the one in Wydown because they have large tables, but there appears to always be a table available. And again, the staff here are so nice!

Love the drinks. Love everything about it. But when I go in to get my order I sometimes get a eye roll at because I asked for a straw or a water. This happens every time  I go in there. But the rest of the staff are very nice and cheerful when I ask for a different drink.

Baby can't you see I'm sipping A store like you should wear a warning Its dangerously... Delicious  There's no escape And a long wait But I need a sip Barista give me it Your amazing I love it  Price too high, why can't it come down, Losin my money drinking all around But I need you now!  Oh, the taste of a caramel macchiato I'm on a ride Sbux is toxic, I'm slippin under With a sip of your poison paradise, I'm addicted to you, don't you know that you're toxic And I love your friendly service, don't you know that you're toxic

Must have come on an off day. I went through the drive thru and was the fourth car in line. I waited and waited and waited. I was the next car to get my drink when the car at the window finally got their drink after waiting a while, sent it back and then the waiting began again. I had to get to work so I left the line and won't be back to this location again. Idk why I wasted as much time as I did waiting ‍
I walk over here at least once a week since I work nearby. The staff is great and they get through the line quickly! My drinks are always tasty. No complaints here.

Drive thru here kinda sucks, that's just a location problem. I usually go in.  Service has always been great  but I find their drinks taste weak and watered down. I usually get a triple shot latte.. that shouldn't taste weak!

So I placed an online order and when I went to pick it up, that's always great to be able to customize and place your order on the app.  So when I went to pick it up, it was already, but I did ask for a cup with ice, there three gals working behind counter.  As the gal is serving the ice I notice that one gal, has her hair loose, a kind of ponytail but lots of hair coming out from it, and she keeps playing/touching it with her hand.  It's just so not right!!!! Specially or someone handling food/drinks it's just unsanitary!!!  honestly during Covid and all she needs to keep her hands super clean, why are sanitation rules being enforced?  My drink was a cold drink which was a different gal doing, or at least I hope it wasn't the girl with her hair all over the place. Not going back to this location for now.

Without question, this is the slowest Starbucks I have ever been to. I have now been standing for 20 minutes, awaiting two drinks that I have already paid for. Given the expected holiday demand, this location is extremely inefficient. FIND ANOTHER LOCATION!
I really like this Starbucks.  The staff is friendly( especially Rebecca) and the service is great.  I go there everyday.
Very great customer service! The staff was friendly and warm welcoming! They try their best to make your day happen!

I'm not a Starbucks hater. Usually, I'm happy with Pike Place Roast, as reliable a franchise product as a McDonald's hamburger. But not today. I'm not sure what happened. Based on the taste, I'd hypothesize last night's pot was rewarmed, or fresh water was run through old grounds. The petite vanilla-bean scone was much appreciated, a good way not to overindulge. But the PPR was the worst I've ever had. Forced to go to one star -- couldn't finish it, had to pour it out. Hopefully a onetime aberration from what has been a fine Starbucks location.
Really nice manager and staff seating and wall outlets are really not too great but really isn't up to the staff. Lots of parking and easy to get in and out.

This is the worst Starbucks I've ever been to. The service is ALWAYS painfully slow and the drinks never taste quiet right. It took about 20 minutes just to get a bagel one day and they weren't even busy. The iced coffee never tastes fresh and they never get the syrup to coffee ratio correct. Don't even try the drive through. You will be waiting for an incredibly long time.

Very slow, always busy and the drive through always seems to be lined up through the parking lot. I should stop coming here because the last two times I waited 15-20 minutes in the store for one drink.

Not my favorite location. Ordered my drink in the drive thru. Pulled up to the window, no hello, no how are you... I was pouring out a diet soda on the ground. He talked to me as if I were his small child in a rude voice saying "next time can you not do that. It attracts bees and I have to clean that". Luckily it was diet and bees don't care about aspartame.  I would've been understanding and apologetic about that if he had been kind from the start. I believe his name was Andrew. Those are the only words he said to me the whole time at the window. Yikes.

I usually go to this Starbucks all the time and the line is always long and was today. A man pulled out of the line in front of me and when I got up there they almost gave me the wrong food but I said something and she still charged me for his order even after I said my bill should be lower. I didn't wanna even argue since I just simply asked why it was so high for a coffee and Pastry. But I paid $5 more even after trying to fix it. I was just aggravated and over it so I left. Probably just going to go to the other one from now on, this isn't the first time this has happened.

I was here for the free coffee special, it is a decent size store with plenty of parking.  It was packed.  The ordering line is long, but the staff is very helpful and efficient.  The waiting time is 15 minutes but is understandable.  I do see the  barista got frustrated with the workload, however, the drinks that I got didn't drop the quality.  11/01 - Update: The service is great every time I am at this location.  I got the free drink and I loaded up my drink to the limit.  No complaint, no fuss.
They plated my cheese danish - the first of 8 Starbucks in the area to do that.   Great service and very friendly people.

Worst customer service ever!! Not sure what was going on with the cashier but regardless customers deserve to be treated with respect. Something was going on with the phone order system and wasn't working properly so when I placed my order I wasn't aware that my order went to the store on mass ave instead of this store (I live in the 360 tower, so when I order it automatically goes to this store). I was standing by the door with my puppy for my order and the cashier rudely stated that dogs weren't allowed in the store and that the online system wasn't working. She asked what I ordered and asked that I show her my receipt- all this over a grade coffee.

I have been there multiple times and the managers have treated me horribly. I didn't want to write a review about it but recently I've tried my luck again and ran into an issue! The barista Moe really helped me out, how is it that a barista is way more professional and more for helpful to me than your management? I recommend you have kind people that are good with customer service in a management position rather than people that refuse to help you. Thank you for the help Moe I will be back because helpful barista's like you!

Super service since reopening after a small remodel at the end of May. Today I tried the Spinach and Feta Wrap. It has egg whites, diced tomatoes, feta and spinach. It would be better with sundried tomatoes. The wrap was a bit drippy from the diced tomato. Sundried you wouldn't have that issue plus you'd have a bit of sweet with savory. Get on that Starbucks chefs.

I'm a reglar here but they've messed up my drink twice by putting milk into my latte instead of soy! My stomach was destroyed today. This is dangerous! People order soy for a reason! I realized this too late. I always get ventis, too.
The staff is rude and the location has no power outlets. How does a coffee shop not be equipped for tele commuters? Do not visit this location!

This is my favorite Starbucks. That being said, I favor this one because the evening staff is great! All very friendly & awesome. They make my drinks perfectly, & I'm always pleased. The only reason this is not a five star review is because of the day staff. I have been there a few times & the same girl took my order via the drivetru & when she handed me my wrong drink, I would tell her that I didn't order this. She would roll her eyes & then take the wrong drink back. Look, if I'm paying the money for a fancy expensive drink & you hand me an iced coffee, I'm getting ripped off. Overall, love the night staff!

It's a little heartbreaking to see the parking lot in such disrepair after this winter... it was never bad before, but now you need to weave your way around potholes and piles of crumbled blacktop.  The service here has always been amazing. Their regular managers and baristas are friendly and very accommodating. Sara takes the drive thru orders with such pep that it brightens my day before the first sip of coffee.  I can't tell you how many days I have gone to this Starbucks and spent 3+ hours working on lesson plans or grading. I know every Starbucks seems the same, but there's just something about this store and the people that work there--I always get more done!

If only no stars was an option...employees move slower than slow. You will grow cobwebs waiting for your drink. And when you do get it, God forbid the drink is made correctly. Having worked at a busy nyc starbucks location, this place would never make the cut, and it's in a slow paced philly location. Management is doing a bang up job with this one! Never coming here again.

This Starbucks is the worst Starbucks in the Tampa Bay Area!  They close whenever they want... I've tried coming multiple times, and it's been closed during their "business hours".   Also they are the SLOWEST ever. I waited in line 30 min for our order. When I got my order - they handed me my drinks in a drink carrier. In the carrier were to VERY HOT americanos... neither of them had stoppers in them. Sooo the HOT americanos were leaking naturally- and extremely hot water spilt all over my legs burning me.   The girl in the window told me it was her third day. Makes sense. Because there was 0 customer service... and clearly she had no idea what she was doing   Absolutely ridiculous.

Dropped by this Starbucks drive-thru to partake in their new Toasted Graham Latte, which by the way is pretty good.  I didn't get the opportunity to go in to the store, so this review is based solely on my experience in the drive-thru; I found the employee to be quite friendly as with efficient, barely had a wait for my drink to arrive.  One thing I did notice was how small the parking lot area for the Starbucks is due to the other surrounding businesses, so it can be a bit of a pain when you're trying to exit the drive-thru and there's a variety of people trying to park, find a spot or get out of one.  Nevertheless, I'd definitely come back!

Friendly one-man staff, and great outdoor seating with a nice view... what more could you ask for. Stopped in after finding out that my flight got delayed. Hard to find even with GPS. almost passed it. Ample indoor seating, clean, and quaint. The employee didn't seem racist... but then again we didn't sit down first, we ordered so I guess we'll never find out if they would have called the police.

Terrible place, go to the Starbucks in Fredmeyer.... Always a wait because they usually can't give the customer the right drink or food. Just go to the one inside Fredmeyer it's faster and more efficient than the one near McDonalds....

Coffee is coffee to me so maybe not fair to judge but this location of Starbucks just had too many people that seemed to be using this as their early morning place for hygiene.  A small line at the bathroom and the place was a little unkempt compared to the SB I went to about a mile away the previous morning.  This is a good location.  Very convenient and did not have time for the extra distance.

When I came in they were short staffed and incredibly busy, when I went to get my drink another person took it and ran off before I could stop them, but the barista saw and immediately remade it for me. A long wait should not negate human decency.

I've frequented this Starbucks for many years due to it being close to a neighborhood where I used to live. The staff is extremely friendly and efficient. The drive-through line can get pretty long, but employees are usually able to work through it fairly quickly. The coffee is always delicious. I find that this location is out of some food items frequently, so that is a little frustrating. I'm not sure if that is due to busyness or the store not stocking properly. But overall, this is a great store.

Nothing I can say about Starbucks that has not been said already.   The coffee is excellent and the service is wonderful.  On this particular day,  I purchased an ice tea because it was hot and was walking around downtown Indy.  The ice tea was refreshing.  I can always appreciate Starbucks whenever I have a craving for coffee.   It is terrific and makes me feel good.  A great pick-me-up in the morning!

Staff worked slowly and managed their workflow poorly. They took several orders from different customers and started making them all, but they waited to give the orders until they completed everyone's. The cashier took our order and my wife wanted to change the size of her drink. The cashier became irritated with us for changing. This would have been understandable if we had paid already but all she had to do was change the order, but she was clearly bothered by this extra work we demanded of her.   Lots of untrained and inefficient staff with poor customer interactions.

I did an online order. I arrived there 10mins after I placed my order. They were busy so I waited when I looked at the time, I had waited already 10mins. I went in line and wait. Once I spoke with cashier, my order had not been completed. They said they would take care of it. In total, it took me 30mins to receive what I ordered. I waited because I did not know how to cancel my order from online and get refunded. When I left, I realized my order was incorrect as well. I did not return back since I did not want to wait anymore additional time. I do like Starbucks, but I will not be returning to this location.
Located inside the Safeway, it's your standard Starbucks, as it should be. You can grab a coffee while you shop or grab one one the go. Convenient for Westsiders.
Got a vanilla iced latte with oat milk, a chai tea late, and a caramel brûlée latte. They gave me an extremely small cup for the latte and it was still about $4. The cups were also sticky.

This location is amazing  The employees are the BEST OF THE BEST Top Notch They strive to do their best at all times When they are unable  It is someone had an emergency  Or they are low on the things they need to prepare. Our orders, please don't take it out on them. They truly try to do their best. They work back to back shifts  Sometimes as well  Please don't make cry  Like a person I know did  Love and live our best lives now

It is 8:15am and the store isn't that packed. The parking spaces are very few and people have to park illegally just for a fix of their Starbucks. The staff is also few, and SLLLLOOOWWW.   Why Starbucks? Why?! Even when I come at other times of the day, parking is awful. Wifi is slow.

As much as I try to make coffee at home, some days you just need Starbucks. I knew I had a busy day at work today so I decided to stop for a pumpkin spice latte. It's a little earlier than I usually get one in the season but there are no rules anymore so why not.  Pre-Covid, I used the app a lot and would send in my order as I was leaving my house and it would be ready to go when I arrived. Starbucks was closed for for awhile and then finally opened back up, but I have just been using the drive-thru since then. Both ways are equally quick and I get in and out quickly.   The staff are very friendly and genuinely seem like they want you to have a good day. I would say that is true for all Starbucks locations but this is the one I frequent the most.

Iced coffee + almond milk = I'm awake and don't have the bubble guts! What a combo!   This location has very high prices because it's inside of a hotel in the CBD. I had a $3 gift certificate and didn't have many options.

A coffee shop is only as good as your last visit. It's Saturday morning, I have been sitting in the drive thru for 15 minutes and haven't moved. I think they are waiting on Juan Valdez to roll up with his donkey with the next shipment of beans

Second time coming into this Starbucks. I try to stop in this one over the others downtown. The staff is so kind and my drinks are made quick! I'm writing this review because I tried the Tampa Sunset and it didn't disappoint! It's a great summer tea option!

Meh, this location is just ok. The staff is mediocre and move at their own pace. It seems that they're not really there to get people in and out. One person can be responsible for taking orders and making drinks while another employee stands there or looks through a binder behind the counter. Since I work in the mall this is the most convenient location that accepts the Starbucks gold card so I can earn my stars.

2/12/19 - So disappointed to hear that our wonderful asst manager Eddie at The Arizona Pavilions location   1/11/19 - VERY disappointed that they didn't have my favorite sandwich today, chicken and artichoke  11/20/18 - So disappointed today. Not one sandwich in the cooler at 11:15 am on   Once again we are here. Our favorite place. Love our Baristas. Jessica, Treigh, Caleigh, Alex and Taylor. If I've forgotten anyone please forgive me. So many great people work at this location. :) And of co

Edwardsville opened another Starbucks next to the high school. Smart choice for a location!!.  Went inside for an evening jolt of caffeine.  Hubby wanted dessert; real slim pickings on that. He opted for the banana bread ("It's good" he said).   No wait for service; lots of customers inside all on their laptops.   Possibly college crowd since SIU-E is nearby too.   Had to ask for creamer since there wasn't any but that was no biggie.  We sat outside and there was plenty of seating outside as well as in.   Think I prefer this location to the older one.

This place is wonderful the staff is quick. This is one of the smaller store but they had 3 people working or even a long line is gone in a few mins. They know their coffee and the store is clean. It's starbuck so the coffee is always good.
Thank you Jaiese for an awesome macchiato and the joy of seeing your epic name!! Loooove this Bucks!!

This is the worst Starbucks ever! I've ordered twice from the app. Both times I had to wait at least ten minutes to drink my drink even though it was already made and sitting at the side. Each time at least three people made eye contact with me, but didn't offer to help and I had to be really assertive to eventually get my drink. This morning my order was wrong, but I didn't have time to ask them to make it again because I had waited so long to get and was on my way to work. What a bummer they hired such sucky people. Usually the people at Starbucks are great, but at this one their terrible.

Starbucks usually have their acts together. Morning prime time and things are humming. This Starbucks, people standing around looking lost. Long line with nobody being helped.  Coffee that is mundane. With so many great coffee shops in town (shout out to Handlebar), why did I come here?

The customer service provided by the brothers and sisters of this location is unmatched. It's always a memorable experience when Lenny, Kyle and Batman are working. Nothing but laughs, good energy and good drinks. Thank you for going above and beyond for your customers and adding value to their lives. I'm sure I speak for everyone else in saying, we appreciate you!
Fast although unorthodox Starbucks service...the service was masterful. Yes strange but good.   The rest you know.

This was the worst Starbucks service I've ever had. I waited for five minutes in line for someone to even acknowledge me and take my order. The barista on the cash register was unable to pay attention to my order, and thus put it in wrong. He argued with me over the contents of the drink, which I ended up being right about after confirming with a second barista. Then I requested to use my birthday reward, but again he wasn't listening and proceeded to tell me I didn't have enough money on my card. After I repeated myself 2 more times he understood. Then my drink was made wrong because he didn't put it in correct in the cash register. My drink was remade by the second barista and even then the drink was incorrect and tasted horrible. Will never return.

I came to Starbucks more or less everyday for 3-4 years. Just came today after a long time. Look like nothing have changed. Terrible service! It's an busy location. Right on USciences campus. There is always a long line. I don't mind the line however, staff could be a bit more friendly.
located on the Southeast corner of the Circle downtown, a great place to people watch & like us, as Falcon heads, watch the Falcons in the Key Bank building, Eric was very helpful & friendly

This is the most personable Starbucks crew I've ever met. Pretty much everyone is extremely great. The only reason I didn't give a full 5 stars is one employee I believe named Sally is extremely rude. I come in almost everyday and am always extremely pleasant to her saying thank you and all I get is a stank face and ignored. Aside from that these people are amazing. Would recommend highly.

Fortunately, coffee drinkers are addicts so they put up with a lot to get the coffee.  Very rude workers.  Petty.  No customer service.  They don't even try to be decent.  Convenient location is best attribute.  Usually there's parking, but wow, putting up with the disrespect and rudeness of the "help" is very hard.  90% of customers are using Apps.

The drive thru is a thing of convenience. Or so I thought. I am assuming the new people are on the drive thru. I ordered a hot chocolate & a chocolate chip cookie.  The young man at the window appears with hot 2% milk. And no cookie. So I told him. Sir, that's not my order. He reappeared with a cookie. Hmmm, I just took the cookie paid and left. Maybe things will improve once they get trained properly. On the bright side noone was rude to me. Oh it's gonna be a lovely day.

Hands down the best Barista Partners in Tucson! The most courteous, hospitable, reverential team.  Oh...yea...the other reason I come to Starbucks. The coffee is pretty remarkable. Always accurate and the Team always goes the extra mile to put a smile on their customers faces.

This is a great location. I really like it here, and I would love it if they had more seating. I always order to go and my drinks are perfect. If anything isn't 100%, they correct it immediately. The staff are friendly and efficient and the location is pretty clean.  Also, if there is a promotion going and the place is packed, I usually get my order in minutes. Product shelves with their mugs etc are well stocked, plenty of treats and sandwiches, and I don't think I've ever seen them run out of the iTunes freebie cards If you need a place to sit, you may be out of luck. The tables are usually filled with patrons from local businesses having a lunch meeting or students catching up on some projects and collaborations before catching a bus at the transit centre. Always reliable and consistent, this location is a regular stop for me.

This is my favorite starbucks location BY FAR. The cafe is always clean, I always get my drink in a timely manner, and it's always correct. Even if they mess something up, they're always more than happy to remake the drink. The staff here is wonderful as well. Everyone is very friendly and always try to spark conversation. Emily and Savannah specifically are the nicest baristas I have come in contact with at ANY Starbucks. I live in Collinsville and always make the drive to this location because it's just THAT GOOD!

I drink Starbucks almost every single day and typically I have had the pleasure of interacting with only friendly staff members. However, after visiting this location over the weekend, I will never return. Two different employees were so extremely rude during my visit. The one taking my order had an attitude with me like no other and when i asked for something, instead of being polite and telling me they couldn't give me a cup of whipped cream for whatever reason, she stared at me and said "No" so rudely and then turned away like I wasn't still standing there waiting to pay. The woman handing me my drinks then glared at me and asked me "What are you still doing here?" And when I simply said I'm waiting for the rest of my drinks, she rolled her eyes and huffed. Keep in mind, it wasn't busy in the store and I had 2 out of my 3 drinks. Starbucks, the employees at this store need a refresher course on customer service because it was absolutely appalling.
The address is actually 125 E Swedesford Rd...it is in the Gateway Shopping Center. It's great! The service is super friendly and there is a ton of seating. Everyone should go here.

Unfortunately not a very good Starbucks for how convenient this location is for so many people. I always forgive the longer than normal wait time, but  most of the time they skimp on the matcha I pay $5 for and give me a cup of warm milk where I then pour my own packet of matcha in to make it worth drinking.  And secondly when I end up waiting an extra 10 minutes stuck in the drive-thru bc of 1 car's order ahead of me, I don't get any "sorry for your wait" or "this is on us" which I've gotten at other Starbucks. When I hear at least some compassion for the ridiculous wait at other locations, I am absolutely still happy with my order and proud to revisit that location in the future. Never seen that customer service at this Starbucks location unfortunately. It's very disappointing because they get so much business, they should have a 5-star rating.

Recently I've seen an improvement in service. I also have noticed several new employees and so am happy that management must be listening to feedback. The service levels have continue to be positive and I am now Updating my rating. Keep up the customer focus......it matters!
25 minutes to get 2 coffee drinks!!! I get that they are busy but WTF?!! That is absolutely ridiculous!! At least that made our drinks correctly.
We love to check out independent coffee houses but sometimes you end up with a bad cup. Starbucks is always consistent, it usually comes out just right.

Apparently spending close to $200 a month isn't enough (yes it's an addiction) to get a refill on your ice coffee. Apparently, It's against company policy to get a refill unless you are finishing your coffee in the restaurant. Someone might want to tell the lippy manager that  we are in the middle of a pandemic and people don't like drinking their coffee in places where dozens of strangers come through every hour. Instead of being a corporate robot, maybe be a human instead. Or use your common sense, if you have any.

The Webster Groves Starbucks team is really great when it comes to making up for mobile ordering errors.   For example, this morning I ordered a drink and bistro box, but when I came to pick up they were out of bistro boxes (the app hadn't updated yet). The team offered me to pick out a different bistro box and also a breakfast sandwich on the house since the bistro boxes that were left didn't have much protein. This is much appreciated!   Other times when an order has had mishaps, the Starbucks team gave my husband and I $4 Next Drink's On Us cards.   Webster Groves Starbucks is really accountable for mishaps, even when it's not their fault. They are always friendly and accommodating, and I appreciate their hard work and great service. Thank you!

Bottom line, the slowest drive thru with absolutely no excuse. All times of the day, no matter how many customers. Slow. They never apologize for it either. Even when it takes a solid 30 minutes. Idk why i keep trying this one, in fact I just left the drive thru after waiting for 15 minutes not even getting a chance to order, with the same car at the window since I arrived.
One of the better Starbucks in the area . There always friendly and talk with us when I come in with my daughter. Then even make suggestions because she likes to order off the secret menu .

The drink was decent and employees were pleasant but I find the Starbucks "policy" bothersome and downright rude to not allow customers to sit indoors. Why open up the inside then? The sole reason I go to Starbucks (when I do go) is to work and grab a coffee, which is what coffee shops are known for. But instead you let people in and out but you tell a pregnant customer she has to sit outside in 95 degree heat "outdoor seating" when there is no one but employees inside. This is just proving that the establishment is uneducated about what is actually going on and there's more policies with no science backing it.   They would rather have a pregnant woman have a heat stroke while all their employees congregate in the same 10' of the store to follow "cdc guidelines".  This shows me that Starbucks cares more about an image than their customers health and well-being. I very much regret spending my money here.
They took away all of their indoor seating. That really too bad. Options were important and their window seating was a good thing.

The billboards say open 24/7 and they mean that, as I've stopped here on many an odd hour and holiday. Great drive through service and employees who are very friendly despite those odd hours and holidays, too. Only reason I'm holding back the fifth star is that the logistics when leaving the drive-thru are a bit annoying but if you are on that dead stretch of I-4 between Tampa and Orlando and need that caffeine fix, seeing their sign on the horizon over the interstate is a beautiful thing.
Mr. Paul was very unprofessional by telling "idk what does the sticker say". When I asked if the order pick-up drink was mine.

This is the worst Starbucks in Nashville. Drive thru or pick up you will be waiting at least 20-25min. Extremely SLOW service. They are also always out of supplies and food. So don't get your hopes up when you're craving something

THEY DONT LISTEN TO CUSTOMERS. This was my go to Starbucks for so long. I go here multiple times a week. They have been messing up my order for so long now but I keep giving them a  chance because i think they will get it right. I only get an iced coffee with 2 pumps of white mocha.... how hard can it be? Today the barista decided to add 4 pumps or maybe more. Usually when I  ask for 2 pumps of anything it says it on the sticker but not today.   There is literally a mountain of white  mocha because they decided they wanted to add more. Can't even drink it now because it is way too sweet..   LISTEN TO YOUR DAMN CUSTOMERS!!!!
Always hit or miss, but usually when Tia (?) is working my drinks are perfect.
This location is always extremely slow. Nice staff but need a better system to get customers in and out. Each time I visit I wait for almost 15 minutes.
Leiah (?) was so sweet! She definitely made my day. The drive through was fast, and my pumpkin spice latte was delicious!

Quality location, quality staff (yes, Nate, I'm referring to you!) A bit busy on weekends but that's Suburban Square for you. Only thing I'd change is making it open later, especially on weekends. A girl needs to study!

Great service - always. This is a very busy location in the heart of the Brentwood business district. Lots of folks coming and going in the morning, both on foot (from  adjacent parking lots) and in the drive thru. Yes, it's difficult to get in and out of, and it's small, just like every other location I've visited. But mobile orders are consistently ready in 5-6 minutes, and the order is always right. That's what matters most.
Extremely long wait but grateful for the drive thru. Staff are pleasant. Got my drinks right.

It took forever to receive my caremel ribbon crunch frap! By the time I got it, it was watered down and unsweet. I wanted either refund or them to remake it, but the line was too long. Totally unsatisfied and will never choose this Starbucks location again.

5/5 Stars.  Why?  I walked in here today looking for some refuge from the scorching STL heat/humidity as well as a temporary respite from waiting around for Izakaya Ren to friggin' open! (Who takes a "Lunch Break" from 2:00pm - 4:30pm anyway? I guess they just don't get enough biz between those hours to warrant staying open. Understandable.)  Anyhoo. I walked into SBux, on my phone, looking like a rambly idiot as per usual. I hung up and skittered over to the counter where a very peppy woman wanted to take my order. Oh, and believe me when I say "wanted." It was like she was born to be friendly and serve coffee or something, haha, IDK. Not trying to be condescending here either. She was SO helpful. I remarked that I've been wanting to try a "Cake Pop" for a long(-ass) time now and that I wondered how many calories exactly is in the Birthday Cake flavor. (Mostly out of curiosity because they have an advert that boasts each one only being inbetween 170 and 190 each. So it was almost like a bizarre urge to play the Price is Right only with calories and cake pops!) She hurried around the counter to the customer side to grab me a brochure with all of the calorie contents in each food item at Sbux (forgive my crude acronyms) and pointed out each one to me. Wow.   I settled on a Birthday Cake Pop (which she attested was their most popular flavor) and then I inquired about getting a Tea Latte. I haven't really been to an Sbux in a long time (as you could have probably guessed) so I had no idea what went into a "Tea Latte"....she immediately grabbed the cards on how to make each drink and rifled through them. I told her it was okay and she didn't have to but she said it would be good for her to touch up her drink making skillz anyway. (O_o;)  She even accommodated my bizarro request for wanting it made with sugar free syrup and low fat skim milk. (Haha I am such a jerk...or rather my diet is such a jerk.) When she asked for a first name for the cup, I told her "Kate" and she even made sure she was spelling it correctly, "With a 'K', right" Omg! So adorable. :]  It took the other girl a little while to make it but I wasn't in any big hurry anyhow. When she called my name I came over and she apologized profusely for taking a little longer than usual to make the drink and even offered to completely re-do it if I didn't like it. Holy cow. Yeah, I was pretty much floored by this point.  The drink was amazing, and the cake pop was sooo fresh. My mum had warned me about getting a stale one once so either I got lucky or....maybe this is just the Starbucks from some bizarre parallel universe where big corporate chains randomly decide to bend over backwards for their customers. Even the interior was sparkly clean, neat, smelled nice, and was relatively quiet for a Starbucks. Damn. Impressive. It looked kinda new too, maybe that's why. There's only one other review for this location so yeah...I am not familiar with this part of town.  All in all, good experience. I can't believe I just wrote this long of a review for friggin Starbucks, people!!!  (The girl who made the drink even smiled tenderly at me on the way out....she was cute too....maybe she fancied me? Ooooh.) :D *is  creepy*  Edit: Is it weird that I'm finding myself wanting to go back to just this location? @__@; maybe if only to flirt with the cute blonde gal who made my tea latte, or maybe to enjoy atmosphere again! But I am secretly afraid that if I keep coming back, it will lose its magic, like a little kid at the city museum. *weird* xD

Skip this location unless you enjoy being ignored, talked down to, and once again, ignored. Terrible service and they didn't even fill my expensive coffee to the top. Got two stars because the drink tasted fine after the ten mins it took to prepare it. I was the only customer there. Glad this app exists so I know where NOT to stop on the interstate. Think I'll go to a travel center next time. At least there, I expect bad service.
No outlets at all so find another place to do schoolwork. Bathrooms could've been cleaner.  Coffee is alright.

My favorite starbucks location! Every single employee is so kind and especially the tall kid named joe! He remembers my drink every time I come in and I really appreciate that since I come in four times a week. Super amazing and really appreciate this Starbucks location!

This location is very slow. While waiting 15 minutes for a Bacon Gouda sandwich, I noticed the staff is disorganized, little to no structure. What surprised me the most was the amount of staff behind the counter should not allow for slug moving service.
Third time we've been given the wrong drink at the drive-in. Just Awful! They can't get it right.
Absolutely love this location and everything about it. The staff are awesome, truly a great warm welcoming environment.
Coffee is fine but no use sitting down with a laptop since the WiFi is absolute trash.

Parking here is murder. They only have 4 spots which are almost always occupied. This is easily remedied by parking at the CVS across the street or on Penn Valley Drive. Both are fairly reasonable options.   Other than parking issues, this is a great branch. The lines are small, the staff is quick, they have a few tables out back overlooking Lake Afton, and there's also plenty of free seating available inside.   It can get crowded from time to time, but overall, this is a nice, pleasant place to get your AM coffee.

This is not a very good Starbucks!  They don't update the app when they run out of bakery items.  So you order ahead and when you get here to pick up your order they say, I'm sorry we have run out of that.  What else do you want?  Nothing!!!  2 stars is all you will get out of me.  Blah!  Update your damn app!!!

Stopped in for breakfast before work. I got the impossible breakfast sandwich. It was nice and warm but the corners of the bun were hard as if it had been overheated. I didn't get a drink since I was headed to the office where coffee and tea were available. I'd like to see truly vegan options for sandwiches.

I come to this Starbucks every day and they're always very nice. I work down the street so I place a mobile order and it's usually correct and everything is ok.  Tip: every other Starbucks has a sign or a bin that says "mobile order pickup" and they don't. After 2 months I still stand there for a while waiting or they hand me one thing and forget to give me the other so I wait for that.  Typically everything is great but since I order through mobile and leave with it, I have gotten back to my office to find that they didn't warm something or they gave me cream instead of coconut milk. I don't have the opportunity to say anything after I've left so I would just ask that you pay closer attention to each order. A bad coffee can ruin someone's day when they don't have time to take lunch.
This is one of the best Starbucks I've been to in a while! Friendly staff and the baristas were super helpful and had great recommendations. Whatever this location is doing - keep it up!

Every time I visit my orders are ready to go. They make sure the items are packaged correctly ie the labels are correct # of bags in order. They are friendly, verifying the order is correct so you're not grabbing the wrong items. They're always busy when I am there and it never seems to change their mood. Keep up the good work.

I love this place. You smell the coffee aroma the minute you get in. They have very efficient staff. They are very quick and very friendly. Very cozy place especially in winter time love to spend time there with my colleagues at work.

Dear Starbucks in Suburban Square,  Come on.  Are you really so hard up on cash that you need to charge for wireless?  And why are your tables so small--and round, to boot--that they discourage camping out and working all afternoon?  I must admit, I'm irritated.  I love my frappuccino lights in all of your flavors--I'm a loyal customer, I swear--but I also love coupling said drinks with long afternoons of steady work, preferably in an environment devoid of teenage girls screaming about lip gloss.  Just saying.  Love, Shawna

It's 40 degrees in Philadelphia. Why do you have the air conditioning on now? Is that really what Starbucks corporate wants? What a waste of energy. Not very good as a corporate citizen. Besides the fact that you guys barely recycle (correct me if I'm wrong).   I guess you want to freeze us out of your location. What's up with that?

I give up on Starbucks.  A few weeks ago, I ordered a mocha.  I waited for 15 minutes while the barista stared at me every chance she got and completed three other orders of people who walked in behind me.  The only thing I different that day was I was wearing a USO cap with a Marine Corps pin on it.    Only when I went to the register for a refund did she hold up a cup a and said she was making it.  I sent an email to Starbuck customer service and have not received a reply.  Now when my neighbors give me gift cards for watching their pets, snow blowing driveways and everything else neighbors do for each other, if they insist on giving me something I will ask them it not be a gift card from Starbucks.  And I won't be getting Starbucks gift cards for the service men and women when the USO has events.

This has got to be the slowest, most incompetent Starbucks I've ever been to. That says a lot, since I've visited hundreds. I waited in the drive thru line for 35 minutes for one item, and no it wasn't during rush hour or anything.  Actually still in line! This is the reason I typically visit the Goodlettsville location. Should've known better.

PROBLEM Music was so loud I would characterize it as rude. The store is not clean. Not a very good Starbucks.   SOLUTION Hire, Train, and Mentor good people. It doesn't take the clientele to point out filth and loud music. It takes common sense and client centric leadership.

I have just left this location, and the service was spectacular. I was so pleased with the way the staff had handled my order. My coffee was perfect, even though they were incredibly busy. I cannot say enough good things about the 8 AM staff.

The people really weren't that nice. I would recommend going here though because i have had better experiences. But today, the lady made my father and I's drink horrible. It taste like water and has no flavor what so ever. And for the price we pay i would expect way better, especially since it's happy hour today and it's a more popular day to go.

New location? Was nice to begin with, now same experiences as old location, super slow, not very nice. Cater to drive thru so mobile orders not even worth it. I miss the Maple Glen location so badly!!!

I am disappointed to find such a terrible Starbucks in such a beautiful place! I worked for Starbucks for a couple years in college and I am a very frequent customer. The employees working here were extremely unaccommodating as well as fairly incompetent. In addition, there was a strong smell of sewage inside which was a little unsettling. There are plenty of other options in the area so I would suggest you avoid this particular one. Rude, unfriendly baristas are not acceptable!
Horribly slow. I would have left but I was let trapped on the drive thru line. Never going to this location again

Friendly and fast service, super helpful staff. Why aren't there organic options yet Starbucks??  For both coffee and creamer selections? I had a lot of stars built up otherwise i'd go to a neighborhood organic coffee shop.
Excellent service. Come here very often. The people working behind the counter have already memorized my favorite drink and have been very attentive.

MUST READ: Starbuck's CARES about YOU! I unfortunately was in accident out front of this store and after the reports were taken and the cars towed away, I entered to store to try and get warm and truthfully process all that had happened just a few minutes earlier while I waited for my wife to pick me up. A cup a coffee, I thought, would be really nice so I went to order my usual... As per normal, the employees asked how I was doing and I shared that I had just experienced an accident and was waiting for my wife to come get me. They were generous enough to listen and offer their support as I explained the situation. To top it off, they offered the coffee on the house. Yes, maybe just a free cup of coffee but man it meant more than a 20oz caffeine boost to me. Thank you to everyone in the store who were so generous and if you've lasted long enough to read this review, I hope you'll consider Starbucks for your next coffee craving..

This Starbucks is always slow, and my order is usually wrong(today they added smoked sea salt to my honey latte, I did not order salt on my latte). Disappointed customer, the location is ideal however the service is subpar. I would recommend going to the Lindbergh and Lemay location instead, they always get my order correct and are way more efficient!
This location is constantly closed due to staffing issues caused by COVID. You never know when it's going to be open.

Honestly they're my favorite Starbucks in town. They always know how I take my coffee and they do a decent job.  Seriously, what more can you ask for?   Sure they're not anything fancy, rustic, or locally owned, but they're a cup of coffee when you need it.  Old reliable. Like an old pair of sneakers. They get the job done.

This Starbucks location has simply become more and more infuriating. I like coming here after I do laundry to watch some netflix or surf the internet during loads....unfortunately the internet connection at this location is such an embarrasment, I am very surprised that no other reviews have mentioned it. There might well be no internet - I can't watch netflix, it takes 60 seconds to load a standard website, videos don't work, its absolutely pathetic. I am actually writing this review from the PJ's next door. Perhaps nobody else has complained? When I look around I see everyone on their computers in word documents but nobody trying to actually use the wi-fi so perhaps that could be why - but to the owners, simply pay for more/better bandwith, it's worth it despite your obvious conclusion that somehow its not. I will not be returning unless the internet actually works. Village coffee on Jefferson also never has this problem ,but again I'd love to come back since everything about this starbucks is great but for the nonworking internet.
The staff at this Starbucks is always very friendly! Drinks are consistently good & made quickly. I highly recommend their new blonde espresso.

We had a terrible experience. I was with my friend to order drinks on our way to Orlando. First time at this Starbucks. We had Lucien as our cashier. She got our order wrong and forgot to put in one of our order. Our order seemed to overhwhelm her significantly despite asking for no modifications except oat milk instead of regular milk. She was either very soft spoken or irritated. Took 5 minutes to just get our order placed. She didn't even confirm our order. After the order, only two out of the three drinks came up. One of the drinks was wrong. So we had to ask to have redone. Then, we were waiting for a long time for a third drink watching other customers who came after us get theirs. When we asked if they were making our drink, they said they never got the order. We had to bring it up to another barista who was a bit older with white hair in a ponytail. He was sweet and helped refund us. On a side note, the bathroom was not very clean. Toilet paper all over the ground. We are never coming back.

I'm a HUGE fan of this Starbucks. It's the closest one to where I live, and the workers there are ALWAYS friendly. I always feel like I'm talking to an old friend, even though I typically only go a few times a year (I can't stay away from the Pumpkin spice frap...YUM!)  I've been through the drive thru a few times, and they have doggy treats in the window for your furry friends. How sweet! :)
People are friendly, the place is always clean, and it's a Starbucks. So you know the menu.  Stop by if your thirsty, they have a drive through btw.

Unfriendly service. I also suffered a concerning drink quality issue yesterday. As Starbucks Corporate is trying to address these issues and has quickly refunded my purchase price, I won't elaborate. It was just... very unfortunate.  I'm a Starbucks devotee. This is the closest Starbucks to my home but not the only Starbucks nearby so I'll just avoid this one... forever.

If I could give them less than one star, you bet I would. I've never had anything but problems at this Starbucks. While I've only been there twice during travel, I believe it is the worst run Starbucks I've ever been to.  The first time: no cars in the drive thru, almost no one in cafe, yet we still got sandwiches that were burnt and bitter shots in our espresso drinks. I mean, if they're that slow, they can probably check my Bacon Gouda sandwich to make sure it even has bacon...Why is quality so crappy when the wait is nonexistent?   The second time: just stopped by and had a huge line for a wait. Now, I understand that, it was a busy time and that's out of their control... but we ordered with no difficulty (and no screen to double check our order) and assumed everything would turn out okay... I was wrong. We ordered a triple venti cappuccino and instead got a triple venti americano which is MUCH different. You guys can at least double check the order at the box or at the window to assure the quality of your product, it's like your job or something.

I never have coffee at Starbucks, it's too strong for me. I'll usually opt for a chai tea or a latte. My boyfriend is a Starbucks fan. This one was fairly standard for the chain. No better, no worse than any of the others. We had a drink to wake up and wait out rush hour before heading on our way.

The drinks are usually decent. The customer service however is lacking considerably. There was a younger guy at the window today who seemed bothered to do his job. I was in the passenger seat so I wasn't paying much attention, other than to grab our drinks. We drove off and I mentioned his crappy customer service. My boyfriend started laughing because apparently he was playing on his phone. Seriously? At the drive thru window? Extremely unprofessional.

This place has completely impressed me! I used to always stop at the Starbucks in Lakeland off exit 33 on the way to Tampa, and this one is way faster!!!! I always stop at this location now, and yes, the drive through is a little slow...but the other location's drive through wait is 30+ minutes! They got my vote here!

The low review is primarily due to the service that has gone significantly downhill. I usually stop at this Starbucks pretty regularly before work. This Starbucks has the SLOWEST drive thru of any of them in town. Yesterday after the line not moving for 15 minutes, I just left. This morning, there weren't many cars, but enough that my drink should have been ready when I pulled up. Instead it was another 5 minutes after my card was processed before I was handed my drink. "Just go inside" you say? It isn't any faster, and I've had them put my order on the drive thru counter. It took me 5-10 minutes to get someone's attention to grab it for me. And the point of a drive thru is for it to be quicker than getting out of your vehicle, walking inside, waiting in line, ordering, waiting for your drink, and then walking back to your car, getting in and driving away.  They need to take the service in the drive thru (and inside) up a notch.

Customer service is usually pretty good, however Starbucks says they care about their employees but then start them off at $9.25/hour when the company has the ability to pay a living wage. PAY YOUR WORKS MORE. ESPECIALLY DURING A PANDEMIC. YOU ARE NOT ESSENTIAL.

I've been here a handful of times. I've driven through, but they are usually backed up and so I ultimately decided it was nice inside and my daughter and I enjoy sitting and hanging out. It's clean and the staff are friendly, and they even seem to have more food items on stock then the surrounding Starbucks in the general Tucson area. I literally searched for the sugar plum pastry everywhere and couldn't find it and came here and got it two times here in a row! They are all out now though (limited special item). One day when I arrived, there was a mishap and they forgot all about my drink. I waited patiently, and finally went to remind them. They were apologetic. I was not in a hurry, if I had been I'd have been annoyed probably but it wasn't a big deal for me. It did however make me decide to come a couple more times before reviewing them to make sure I didn't rate them poorly if they didn't deserve it. I have since maintained an otherwise good experience with them, so I feel I can give them the 3/4 stars. This has been my new favorite location. I think that's for personal preference really. They are generally busy and a little slow though, so don't stop here if you're in a hurry and you'll be alright ;)

If you want lukewarm coffee, go here. I asked for a new cup of coffee and the barista suggested I get less creamer and it was still lukewarm. I also had to wait 5 minutes for pike roast (which is available 24/7 and never takes that long). Go to the Starbucks by center grove instead, more friendly too!
Great job keeping up during corona virus limited service.   Had drive through yesterday. Fast and friendly.   Order ahead was correct and on time. Pick up was easy.   Good job guys!

No Carmel or Brown Sugar syrup, so they can't make the drink they're currently advertising...HUH?   Also, I can't scan my Starbucks rewards to earn points because this location inside Target is not setup for this capability...not really sure why not and def. disappointed!  The Macchiato also tasted bitter - the sugar helps to cut down on it, but I could def. taste it and it didn't go down smoothly like other ones that have been made for me.  The customer service was really good besides the shortage of syrups to not be able to make the original drink I wanted!  I won't be back - I expect better for a coffee that costs +$5.00!  No dining at this location because the chairs are still up on the table.

This is a great location to meet up with all my friends in Millwoods for a nice Starbucks drink. Staff here is always friendly and they always get your order right. They are really quick at the cashier and they are also knowledgeable about the drinks.  I always find a parking spot in front of the shop.   It's kinda crowded near the front of the store, but as you move further to the back of the shop, there is a lot more room. I like going there with 4 friends because there is a spot with 4 really comfortable couches near the back corner. We have great conversations there.  I find it pretty noisy here so this wouldn't be a Starbucks that I would bring my book to sit and read.  Sometimes if you go when they are about to close, they give you free pastry from the display case.
Not a good Starbucks. Staff doesn't know their own coffee and refused to do pour overs for anything but the three they already have brewed. Maybe they'll get better...

I get it, you get busy. That's normal. It's how you handle it that matters. 25 minutes for a coffee is unacceptable. What's worse, when people complained to the manager, they were dismissed. The young woman taking order would say she had no idea how long it would be! You better have an idea!  You know when the orders placed, you know when the name is called. Step it up a Cville!

Again, it's a Starbucks.  This one is located at 75th and Shadeland on the corner of a little strip that also houses the popular Caplinger's Fish Market.  Inside is VERY small as is the outside so seating is limited.  Great for a drive through -- you have to go around the other end of the strip mall by Donatos to access it.  The staff at this location is pretty friendly, too.  Especially John in the drive through and a gorgeous gal inside with a really unique name that I can't recall ...

Closed inside seating but there is seating outside. They have quick and friendly service, and a drive through as well. Hopefully they will open the indoor seating soon because it's a very nice, clean and relatively new Starbucks. My pumpkin spiced latte that I was craving was perfect.

I've been here 30+ times and have never been treated so rudely. Rolling eyes at me when I reminded them (after they handed me my drink) that I asked/paid for a shot of espresso in it. Then just pushed my coffee to me across the bar almost spilling it.   2 cars in drive thru and nobody inside and they look like they have no idea what they're doing.   I won't be back to this one.
The staff at this location is very unfriendly. The girl with the giant plugs and dirty hair is extremely rude. I did nothing to deserve being treated so poorly. My family will not be back.

I wait in the line 32 minutes to get my order and when I order they told me everything I order they told me they didn't have it and also all the time  Different price they charge me and also they are fucking so mean and when they give me my Order all of it ice that is fucked up really fucked up and also the fucking drink it's just like a water taste

Wow, this SB location definitely has an efficiency problem. Average wait for grande cappuccino = 15 mins minimum. 5 of those minutes may just be waiting to order once you've reached the front of the line. I cringe at watching all the slow moving wasted low energy. If you are in a hurry, go somes place else!

First off, I should admit: I don't drink coffee. (Whew, I feel better putting that right out there) But, I have a wonky schedule, which means at 6am, I'm looking for supper-or something supper'ish. Starbucks, as I'm finding out, has a great hot menu. Today I learned: the impossible is possible. The impossible sausage, egg and cheese sandwich is really good. Tasty-yes-great texture, not greasy-and the roll it's served on is divine. (Oh, the crunch!) I'd eat my shoe if it were served on this roll! I will definitely have the impossible again. And bonus: it's also filling.   I'll be back to this location, too. At 6am on a Saturday, there was no wait at the drive-thru and the service was amazing. (She didn't balk at the Elmo pj pants I was wearing) Yes, it's across SR 60 from Brandon Mall and Target, in the busy Michael's/AMC theater parking lot-but early in the morning, it's a breeze. They have tons of parking and a little outside eating area. For $10, my Saturday is off to a good start.

This Starbucks consistently messes up my order. The service is also regularly slow. I gave it one last chance, but after several things I tried to order confused the barista, I gave up. We left without buying anything and are going to Chickfila. That will be the last time I ever go to this location.

Great location (meaning it's right downtown St. Pete) and really have to love almost any Starbucks! The employees are really nice and does a good job. However it can be hard to find a parking and this Starbucks is pretty crowded at most times (downtown, huh). There are only few nice tables and they are taken for hours by people who sip on a drink and study on their computers. So it's a no no for finding a nice table, but yes yes for getting my morning coffee... until I get my own Starbucks machine on Black Friday sale for $59. Yeeeey:)

Okay. I don't normally give 1* to establishments unless I'm really irritated. Just isn't worth my time or that of the place I'm reviewing. This is different. I have had many nitro cold brews at various Starbucks in the area.  I order a newer item on the menu which is the nitro cold brew. This is essentially a cold brewed coffee from a keg. I tried it and instantly knew something was wrong. It had was lighter in color and at first glance I thought it might have had creamer. I pulled around and voiced my concerns and was instantly belittled for my knowledge of nitro cold brew. It was my fault?   In reality, the coffee wasn't as strong as I was used to for a nitrogen cold brewed coffee.   Point is, if your product doesn't live up to a customer's standards. Don't make it their fault. Talk to them and make them feel like their opinion counts.   Peace

First of all, we were not greeted by the person at the counter. We could not understand a thing they said because they mumbled. When my mother tried to reload 20 she went to use a 100 to pay and they couldnt take it because it was too big of a bill which is beyond my understanding. And there was no apology no trying to reconcile and no "sorry about that enjoy the rest of your day" terrible customer service.
They will never get your right !!!! Worst Starbucks in Tampa . I have a milk allergy and they always mess this up even when I tell them this ! No foam? You will get EXTRA !!!!

Manager Danielle was amazing ! She went above and beyond for every customer she came in contact with ! Definitely an example of how managers should be ! Customer service is not taken serious these days....she u Gave me hope ! Lol

This message is for the management of this location. Please, please!! Retrain your baristas and take the time actually to try the coffees being served out at this location! You need to be in the customer's shoes, so you know what we are experiencing. I have been coming here since opening, giving chance after chance.  I don't know what exactly has gone wrong with this newer Starbucks. But every single time I go, there is something wrong with my drink. May I add, I don't order anything complicated. My craziest drink was a simple soy latte, for example.  I just went through this drive-through today, and my drink tastes like diluted, watered-down coffee and a splash of soy milk. Not worth $6. I've been ordering the same drink for years at multiple locations throughout the USA. But this location can't make the drinks right.

Worst Starbucks I've ever been to!  They can't seem to get an order right!  I am a regular (I know, stop going if it's so bad) since it is the closest Starbucks to my job.  At least 50% of the time my order is made wrong.  It's not a hard order - Peach Green Team Lemonade.  It's on the menu.  Maybe they should hire some people who can read what the order actually is!
If I could select 0 stars, I WOULD. The service was absolutely freaking horrible. The servers/baristas SUPER RUDE!!!! Never will I EVER come to this sorry A-word establishment EVER AGAIN!!!

Starbucks must be doing well when they place two Starbucks across from each other.  This Starbucks is located inside the Marriott hotel and is located right across the street from the Sheraton Starbucks.  This Starbucks also closes early so make sure to get here before 5pm. I believe this Starbucks also has the collector Starbucks cups (has the city of New Orleans printed on the outside).  Lots of seating and relatively fast service.  No use having your Starbucks gold card here though. No free soy or syrup choices.

Would be 4.5 stars... but I will happily round that up!   We went on the day we visited Longwood Gardens. Since we left late morning, we had to find a place to eat for lunch before visiting the gardens. Considering I'm a Starbucks, we decided to eat here. This location is just a few blocks away from the entrance to the parking lot of the Gardens.   Easily located right off of US-1, this Starbucks is part of a strip mall, known as the The Shoppes at Longwood Village. Hey, if the shoppers need their caffeine fix for buying clothes or groceries, they certainly have this place for that! If you see the sign for this strip mall, then you'll know that Starbucks is in there. This, however, is part of a smaller building, which is separate from the main building where other stores (including "anchors") are attached. Because this parking lot is huge, finding a spot was never a problem.  We walked into the store. It was small, but not the smallest that we've ever been to. They had some areas for you to sit at, but it's never enough to accommodate a huge crowd who wants to sit down and unwind with coffee and/or food while accessing the free WIFI (which was how I checked in here via Yelp! LOL). At least the wooden chairs were okay and the table was clean.  The people behind the registers were nice, and our paninis were toasted up without issue. We also got some iced white mochas too, and they were good, especially on a warm day. As we were eating, a lady from behind the register approached us and offered some samples of two of their seasonal items. One of them was a Michigan Cherry Oat Bar, and that tasted really good! Another item was a small berry drink. I don't remember the exact name of it, nor didn't I care for it as I thought it was just okay. It's not often I get free samples from Starbucks since I was being a patron, and because of this generous act, the full rating is warranted.   Overall, the store was nice and customer service was flawless. I would say that this is as good as it gets. Woo! :D
I've never had any issues at this Starbucks. They are always friendly and I never wait long for my drinks. At the end of the day, it's Starbucks
Worst Starbucks in Nashville. Slow service, overly busy, and the lattes and mochas are very bland tasting. Very clean store though.

Staff is very disorganized. They ignore there customers and continue on with there own personal conversations. I know there a busy location but they take forever to get you your drink. Might need to reevaluate there staff because they don't seem very proficient.

Starbucks continues to be the bane of my existence. The thorn in my side. The ultimate example of everything that is wrong with chain restaurants, and the suburbs.   While I do enjoy their coffee and willingly pay five dollars a cup for coffee, water, and milk. I would much prefer going to a mom and pop establishment. However, the problem with the suburbs is there is a severe lack in mom and pop coffee shops. But I guess the upside to chain restaurants is that you can always expect the same quality where ever you go.   All my ranting aside, this is a good starbucks. They have a nice outdoor patio, my order is always correct, and they have a drive through which is always convenient. Be warned, however, this drive through with its proximity to Rt. 1 and the turnpike, can get very crowded in the mornings.

Shopping used to mean shopping. Now it means driving to the store, having a Pavlovian reaction to the Starbucks sign, drinking coffee for 20 minutes, and then shopping. What a world.   I love a Target with a Starbucks. The alternative are the Target branches with the Cafe and those are terrible. The Starbucks branch here is quite good. The barristas do a great job at moving the big lines along and the coffee is the same quality as you'd get at any stand alone branch. They also have Target specific cake pops which my kids absolutely love.   All in all, good stuff!

I go to Starbucks at least ten times a day. Many times I want to take my home office to this location. The music is so loud one would think they are in a bar. I politely asked if they could lower it and the manager was so flipping rude as he always is. His response was no as it pre set. Funny as two weeks ago even one of the employees lowered it as she said it was so loud! I don't even know if he is the manager. He just acts like it which is quite entertaining, This is the only Starbucks where a few employees always look so angry! Thankfully corporate called me back and even they heard how loud the music was.  This is the only starbucks I have ever been to( I go to a Starbucks in every state and country when I travel) where some of the employees are about as cheerful as grumpy the cat. I will say some employees are so sweet but there are a few in the morning  who need to realize they are only serving coffee...not saving the world;)

Probably one of the busiest Starbucks I've ever been to, but it was well worth it.  About the only negative I can see at this store is their lack of two public restrooms.  Oh, they have two restrooms, but one is reserved for employees only.  The atmosphere inside is terrific and kitschy like most locations and as packed as it was, we were still able to find seating for my wife, myself, and our niece.  Waited for several minutes for our mobile order and never heard my name or saw anything like what we ordered.  I approached the counter and asked if the app had selected the correct location.  The barista was shocked and told me that it was in fact a location far on the other side of town (Kino and I-10).  Turns out on our recent trips to Shreveport, LA and San Antonio, TX we had made stops at that SBucks, so the phone app defaulted to that store rather than the one we were standing in a few minutes earlier.  The barista asked to see the receipt, quickly jotted down our order, and promptly made our 3 drinks.  So amazing.  We thanked her profusely and then called the other store to let them know so they could gift the other drinks to some of their patrons.  Impressed to say the least.  We'll be back and I'll check the app a little more closely before ordering next time.

Horrible, my daughter and I had just got done at the hospital for her foot, and the cashier just stared at her. The Cashier ran my car for all of our food and then smiled at me and told me my card was declined. My card is a debit and has plenty, I feel like we got judged because we went feeling good and I wanted to treat my daughter to a strawberry shake (she's only 6 yrs old for crying out loud) my hair a mess lol, but really not serve us and make up a silly excuse all the machines are not working the cashier politely explained. She made faces at me when I asked for extra whip cream on both of our shakes and was just rude like she didn't want to help. I hope this gets noticed asap. I feel bad for sick lik kids or kids who get judged too soon by adults that are getting paid, because there is a hospital and this is life..you would never expect horrible service or rude behavior but I suppose it happens and it happens here. :/

The baristas in the mornings are rude and unfriendly.  I've come to this location a number of times sporadically over the past couple of years hoping to be treated cordially (nothing more required!).  Each time the gal or the guy take the card/cash and then plop the change/card on the table without so much as a thank you or a smile.  It's common courtesy to hand back the change or the credit card to the customer.  These rude idiots never do that.  And, I've watched them treat others similarly.  The management at this location leaves a lot to be desired.  Maybe I'll try in another 6 months and see if they've hired better employees....

I overheard the barista literally complaining about making my moms drink/making fun of it. So I said "well that's the way my mom likes to drink it." She immediately got defensive.   They also put sweetener in it and claimed "they changed the recipe so there's no sweetener" and were incredibly rude after I called her out for complaining.  I never get angry toward people working in the service industry and that really got under my skin. I would not recommend this Starbucks.  Try not to complain about people's drinks in front of a family member next time. "Have a really great day!"
fucking idiots only filled my drink up halfway. i ordered a chai tea latte with light ice ??? NOT LIGHT DRINK DUMBASS. AND IT STILL HAS A SHIT LOAD OF ICE

I love my Starbucks on 10th! The employees are already friendly and fun. We've done business meetings here, come through the drive up, and just hung out with our kids between sports.  The staff takes their time to get it right but also understand coffee drinkers need that caffeine stat ha! Great job!

The employees there act like they pay for supplies out of their own pocket. If you ask for a drink with light ice, be prepared to get pushback about it and how your cup will not be full since you requested light ice. No other Starbucks that I've been to has ever done this other than this, yet they do this every time. Paying $7 for a cup filled 2/3 of the way because you don't want so much ice is a joke. Not only that but they will close whenever they feel like it & tell you it's because they "were slammed today," even if it's still 15 minutes till their posted close time. I recommend driving 5-10 minutes to Green Valley or Tucson location instead.

I came here to grab breakfast sandwiches and coffee for 4 people. The cashier did not understand that I just wanted egg and cheese sandwich without any meat. It took good 5 minutes to get that straightened..  Anyway, latte and the coffees were great! Pretty good and quick service otherwise!

This Starbucks will make you late for work!  I've spent as long as 20 min waiting in the drivethru.  I even tried going inside since there weren't too many people.  I walked right up and ordered and it still took 15 min. Leave yourself plenty of time or find another Starbucks.

I've yet to have good luck with this location. Usually, I go through drive thru which is a 20 minute wait minimum.  Today, the drive thru was closed so I went in.
