Between waiting in line and receiving my drink, the wait was also about 20 minutes but wasn't bad considering the volume. The problem is my drink was wrong and I ended up tossing it in the trash.  So I basically waited 20 minutes to throw $5 in the trash. Coffee is a rare treat for me but I've come to realize that it usually just sours my mood to come here.   My order was a venti sweet cream cold brew - 2 ingredients essentially. I got black coffee with a cold foam.

This Starbucks is one of the cleanest and friendliest in Reno. I like the drive up window, it makes it easy and convenient to get a coffee or tea on the way to UNR. The staff is always really friendly and after ordering at least 30 drinks, I have not had a bad experience.  The major downside is its not a Peets.

Just witnessed an employee roll a trash container full of trash through the store 3 or 4 times while he asked customers who were eating and waiting for their food to move out of the way while he dragged trash in a giant rolling trash bin through the store! Do not order from this place, it is unsanitary, and the employees are so lazy they can't be bothered to pick up a trash bag, and take it out through the back LIKE EVERY OTHER RESTAURANT IN THE WORLD. Someone should call the health department and report them immediately.   The people who helped us at the counter were very nice, and they don't deserve to be represented this way by lazy employees.

There are no other words i can think to describe this Starbucks other than they are AWESOME! I feel extremely welcomed every time i go there! They know my name and the people are just super sweet and kind! I love it!!!

This Starbucks is one of the busiest you'll see in Bucks County. It's THE hang-out spot in Newtown. Starbucks is Starbucks, but at this one, the staff couldn't be nicer and, more importantly, they get the drinks right. There's a good amount of seating for the space for individuals as well as groups who'd prefer to sit at larger tables. The only gripe is that it attracts the teens late at night, so around that time it can get a little loud/annoying. But as long as you hit it before 8, you're in the clear.
Kelsey was genuinely the nicest person I've met all day and it's 7pm. Thanks for making this place stay open an extra hour so I can get some work done. I'll def be back :)

This location has always had quick, accurate service. One time I was feeling sick and had a particularly bad day, so I thought I'd go to Starbucks for a drink and a small treat. That's a big deal to me too because I only go to Starbucks maybe 5 times a year. I reached in my purse to grab my wallet and realized I had left it in my gym bag. I told the barista that I'd have to go home to get it, and then he just told me he'd cover the order himself. I almost cried I was so happy! Seriously the nicest thing and exactly what I needed that day. Will definitely be returning to this location again and again.

Consistently great Starbucks location. Even though there's a good amount of turnover, the baristas are always top notch. Service is routinely fast and friendly.  The thing I like most about this branch is that they take the time to make my drink properly. I often order long shots, but many locations don't run the shots that way, despite my instructions. I'd say I only get long shots about 10% of the time. Anyway, here they always let the shots rip for the full amount of time and you can absolutely taste the difference. Good job for never cutting corners on my drink.  Five solid stars from me. I really hope we can move past the pandemic and get back to sitting and drinking our coffee in the shop. It's really a great place to do that.

I'll give at least 2 stars to any place that caters to vegans.  I'm not crazy on the service at this location, but it happens to be closest to me.  As for Starbucks as a whole, I am thankful for the "use your gift card for purchases and we won't charge you $1.20 to get your latte with soy and a flavor shot for the rest of the year after your 5th beverage" deal.  Comments on parking/drive thru - it get's INSANE here at times, it is an incredibly small lot and if the drive thru is backed up you just have to wait it out, there is no passing the line of cars to leave the establishment.

Worst customer service from rude employees. We waited in the drive-thru   for the three cars in front of us to order their coffees and when we got to the speaker we were rudely told by a girl on the other end that "we're closed, and we can't take your order". Not only were we not served, but we were made to wait in the drive-thru behind all the other cars getting their orders because there's no way out of the drive-thru once you're in it!!!!
Skip this place in the morning.  Definitely skip the place for a mobile order.  Their process needs to be analyzed for a more efficient way to support the demand... hire more people!!!

Went on a bagel run to another establishment , but I had to have Starbucks.   Still no seating inside. This location is geared to keeping drive thru moving, so I think the drive thru is quicker than walking in and getting it at the counter.

I love Starbucks tea & get it often @ this location. I don't understand why this location is extremely slow. It doesn't matter the time or the day there is always a line. They r not very friendly @ this location either. Therefore, I had stopped buying Starbucks tea. I recently started back hoping things got better its been about a year. No luck. I went this morning around 10:25am there was a line wrapped around the building & flowed into the side road where traffic is pretty busy. I parked to go inside thinking it would b quicker. It was not. A large group was waiting inside. After waiting to place my order I asked the very young cashier who the  the manager was & he said he was. I said to him, I just want to let u know there is a line around the building & is even flowing in the side road of traffic.  He said, yes we are busy and asked what I would like to order. Very Disappointing. Ive never had this bad service at another location.
I'm a huge fan of Starbucks and this was a great one to stop in. For it being in such a crowded tourist spot the staff was friendly. Really enjoyed my latte.
Just not enough mature staff ready to handle higher volume OTC & Drive-Through operations.  Not bad people/service, just too slow.
SLOW and still get order wrong lol gave second chance. Never again. 30 mins in line!!!!!
Absolutely the best place around!!  The customer service is amazing. Only had one bad server, but for the majority of the time EXCELLENT SERVICE.

Best Starbucks experience I have ever had! The customer service here is phenomenal! Every employee I have met has gone above and beyond to  meet our expectations and makes sure every customer is happy! Wifi is another story, bad service connection, and some days I cannot get any Wifi access at this location on River.
Love Starbucks.. but I waited nearly 7 minutes for a tall blonde roast. Store seems understaffed.
The slowest drive through ever. Everytime I come here. Coffee is good staff is friendly, but for some reason with only a few cars it always takes longer than 10 minutes. So GO INSIDE is my advice

There is always the longest line at this Starbucks. Plan on being in the drive through for over 10 mins, and probably closer to 15 mins. The employees are extremely kind and personable, but the service is extraordinarily slow. Also, they seem to be randomly closed at times for no particular reason.

I tried the Bacon, Sausage, Egg and Cheese Wrap today. What I did like...the wrap was hot, the bread/wrap itself was good and flaky, it was full and it did taste good. What I didn't like... the sausage was all down in one end, the bacon was one strip of half cooked bacon ( bacon crumbles would be so much better ). Still great service and my Trinta Black Tea gave me my caffeine boost for the day.
I go out of my way to come to this Starbucks. I have two others closer to my house and this one is the best.  Besides the drive through, the drinks are consistently perfect. And very friendly staff!

Sooo, let me preface this by saying I love me some Starbucks but this location is hit or miss and is probably one of the more difficult locations to maneuver in and out of. You are battling traffic from three different establishments and it's just dangerous. If you are ever in a hurry I definitely wouldn't take my chances because for some reason this location takes awhile to get your order done. Sometimes you appreciate the extra time and others it's like you leave wondering why it took so long for you to get something you didn't order. Ahh well, the employees have always been very nice and extremely pleasant but maybe they are just overworked? Not sure, but I can say I just got my pumpkin cream cold brew and it is extremely tasty so today I'm appreciating the wait. If you're going to stop in just plan accordingly!

4 out of 5 visits they're out of at least one thing my girlfriend and I order. Totally under-prepared store and almost a waste of a visit when you have a taste for that one thing you've had on your mind all day, but once again they're out.

I definitely wouldn't go to this particular starbucks to study, very limited parking. Starbucks is starbucks, but the baristas here have been kind of screwing with my orders! Iced macchiato and it tasted spoiled. Next time got the very berry refresher and it looked much lighter than usual and tasted almost like watered down fruit punch -- really watered down. Not too satisfied with the last few drinks but, its Starbucks. I'd just suggest going to another location instead.

It really shocks and saddens me that this Starbucks is rated so low! I have been going to this Starbucks almost everyday since it's opening. Back in the earlier days, my grandmother and I would go before I would go to school and the baristas knew both of our names as well our drinks. Ken Whan was the manager for years and just recently retired. He had numerous articles in the paper about his phenomenal customer service which would make customers always come back to this location. Even though he is retired, this Starbucks still has a great reputation and those customers still come back because the baristas are still great.   This location has a really nice patio space in the back and comfortable and open seating due to the large windows. Seriously people, this Starbucks is awesome!

Gotta love having a drive through.. it's a big PITA to get out of here though - save yourself the trouble and go out at the light.  Even turning right on Lindbergh is dangerous because of a poorly placed row of bushes blocking your view of oncoming traffic.  This place does get pretty busy in the morning but with the exception of one day when BOTH of their fancy machines were broken they're usually pretty quick.

I like this location. Despite the fact that it's a popular spot and sometimes the drive thru line is so long it's expanded into the parking lot, I'm never waiting for long and the workers are always moving quickly and keeping the line going. If I have questions about anything, they are usually friendly and more than willing to answer.   I never have an issue with quality of food or drinks. If you're a Starbucks fan but don't want to wait forever for your order or get out of the car for it, I'd highly suggest this location.

I stopped in while passing through town on my birthday. I had to wait for a friend to go to lunch. I used my birthday drink reward and the barista who took my order was great and asked me if I wanted a larger size, extra shot or anything since it was my birthday reward.  I also ordered an older drink no longer on the menu so she helped the barista making it remember the recipe. Really outstanding service, which is what I usually get from Starbucks anyway. This location is very cozy inside and out. There's plenty of comfy chairs inside for reading or chatting with friends, plus great work spaces. Outside there's a spacious patio with covered and open areas. Even in January it was warm enough to sit and enjoy the sun. Plenty of comfy chairs and tables outside as well. Really just a great location for working or kicking back with a book or a friend.
Unfriendly staff, bad customer service, and the beverages don't taste good. Unfortunately, most of the Starbucks in Clearwater are this way.

Put in a mobile order and the store was closed. If I had time to wait in a drive-thru line that wrapped around the building I wouldn't have put in a mobile order. If you aren't open, don't take mobile orders or put a cancel order option on the app

I hate writing bad reviews for places but I think it's time. This is the 5th time that I've been to the Starbucks because it's closest to my house and every time I go it's something different. This time I went in there was no wait there was nobody even around and ordered my drink and then a small Rush game and I waited 15 minutes to get my drink which they had forgot about. I had to stand there and watch everyone else get their coffee. While I was in a hurry. Other times they have been rude to my boyfriend about the order they have messed up on our coffee. I realize that it is a busy company but it's also good to provide good customer service which has been lacking severely at this establishment. I am immensely disappointed. I feel like they are lacking on friendly fun people skills.

I come here almost every day on my way in to work and the staff is always so upbeat and friendly. I think that Starbucks coffee in general is consistent so I like having the go-to standards and I don't stray from my order very often to explore the menu. I will say that on the days I can't leave work for lunch the protein boxes are life savers for me.  Drive through or inside- the drinks are great every time, the place is clean and the service is friendly.

After receiving terrible service at Dunkin Donuts with our food, we came to Starbucks to order drinks. The attendant who  Ibelieve was the manger George took our order and he was amazing ! Answered our questions , listened to us , and explained the difference so we made sure we made the right choice !   We always appreciate great service ! Thank you !

I'm usually a local coffee shop kinda gal but every now and again I go into a Starbucks because they are conveniently located. What I generally enjoy about Starbucks is their speediness and consistency throughout the business. This Starbucks is certainly the exception to the rule. It's taken me four years of working in the green hills mall to write a review because I always hoped that an improvement was just around the corner. Always understaffed, sloth like and poorly stocked. What's going on with this shop that's different from the others? Can some one figure it out so we can all stop being late to work while waiting thirty minutes for our grande drip brew.

this is ridiculous. I come to this Starbucks all the time and and these past few weeks I've had countless problems. I ordered a pink drink with dragon fruit inclusions and this is what I received. The coconut milk won't mix with the refresher and I got 2 pieces of dragon fruit- literally. The staff used to be very considerate but seriously how do you mess up one of the most popular drinks and still serve it ?!

We stopped by this downtown Starbucks location early on a Sunday morning before heading out of town. The cashier was super friendly and personable, which is always nice. The shop was clean and our orders were quickly served up. There is a drive-thru here if you don't want to park. Great visit!

Horrible service with ridiculously long wait times. Will never come back. I waited in the drive-through line for 20 minutes with only a few cars getting through. It's easier to drive to a different location than to wait in line at this one. The manager needs to retrain his drive-through staff.

I love Starbucks.  Coffee is always reliably great.  Teas are really good too.  The location however is the biggest downfall.  I wish it was a self-standing store.  Not a huge fan of Target but will go for Starbucks.  Service has never been bad.  Store is often crowded with people just sitting or eating pizza from express pizza place next door.  I prefer a more quiet environment without all of the store traffic.

Usually the best service and kindest people, they were shut down for renovations and just reopened, went today with my 8 yr old daughter and ordered her a pink drink, the lady at the window working named Michele had an attitude and asked if the drink was for my daughter and I told her yes it was and she gave a dirty look and told me you know that has caffeine in it don't you?
I encountered the NICEST woman ever bless her heart. great energy and passion for her job and really made me feel special. Thank you to the barista with short pink hair, you are a godsend

This Starbucks is spacious and well laid out. The drive thru is an added convenience. I visit several times per week and the staff is always courteous. I prefer espresso and related drinks and am always pleased.

This Starbucks is always busy, but they know how to get customers on their way in the drive thru. Every employee I have talked to has been super nice and my drinks are always perfect!  I always pay using the Starbucks app so I don't have to mess with my debit card. I love this location!!!

I've only recently gotten into Starbucks, and while I have a favorite location to go to, it's near work and I thought I would try this location near my home so I could enjoy my drink during my commute instead.  For this location, they never have skim milk out and the baristas fill the coffee to the top. I have a very simple brewed coffee order and just ask for a little room to add my milk, and they can't seem to comply.  There are better locations on the west side than this one (Avon, Brownsburg). This one is usually not busy in the morning, which probably should have been a tip-off.
Worlds best starbucks and even better people. My poodle is also a fan of her daily pup cup.
I've been to many Starbucks in my lifetime and this is by far the slowest Starbucks I have ever been to... inside and drive through.  I've been here multiple times and it's always the same.

Love this place I used to hate coming here because their customer service was terrible and my coffee tasted burnt idk what happened but I have been coming here for the past 3 months and it has been amazing every time! Amazing customer service every time I have came and the workers look happy to be there whatever changed makes me def wait even through the long wait lines and the best part of all is after my awesome experience my coffee is sooooo yummmmmy I can taste it! So yes def after giving it another chance it was absolutely worth it! I'm a loyal Starbucks customer again!

I just redeemed stars for a cookie on 5/21 and the barista used 150 of my stars!! Under the new system it's only supposed to be 50 for a bakery item. What a waste! Somebody needs to do some training if the customers know more about their new programs than the employees. I've had mistakes here before such as charging my card when I asked to use a reward. They are chronically understaffed as well- poor management. *This review is for the Target location
Absolutely awful experience ever time we come here.  Burnt shots, messed up drinks, the list goes on and on.   I would recommend avoiding this place.  Horrible Starbucks location.

This went from being a favorite Starbucks of mine to least favorite with a recent remodel. Not sure where the direction came from but it was totally misdirected. Instead of being that  unique community feel, the lack of comfortable seating, outlets and stark design makes you want to get out asap. Too bad for the loyal regulars.

Convenient location near the hotel. It is quite busy so the lines inside and out will be long. Today we got lucky though, in and out in 10 mins. Sometimes they are understaffed so be patient and nice. The kids work hard in getting all orders on time. Once you enter you are greeted with a smile and they are all nice. While walking back to our vehicle, I did see some customers with their pups and they were having their puppychinos .  Don't forget to leave $$ tips. I recommend this location :)  Overall  Clean Nice friendly staff Most of the time busy  Good location

I've always experienced fast and friendly service at this Starbucks location! I often order from here from the mobile app and have my assistant pick it up for us. It's always ready when she arrives.   I will sometimes go through the drive-thru as well and again, they are very quick! The parking situation is a little cramped, and I can't exactly review the inside of the store, since I've never been in it myself. The five stars are for the staff and the quality drinks they create. I have another Starbucks closer to my home and I will drive the extra couple of miles to get to this one. Not once have they messed up an order, when the one on Prater-Sparks Blvd have, repeatedly.   Also they are always well stocked when it comes to bakery items. Not once have they been out of whatever I've wanted.

The people behind the counter here are always pleasant and nice to talk to.  The layout might be a little cramped and it's not really a "cozy" starbucks...but Starbucks is starbucks...oh and parking can really be tight as well.

Morning treat. Store is in a shopping center. But the actual store is not as clean. Alittle disappointed because of COVID store should be kept even clean due to the cdc guild lines.   However they made my drink perfect to my liking. Actually all the three drinks we ordered were good.

I went inside ordered two coffees and two sandwiches spent 21$. They gave me with the sandwiches within five minutes as I wait and watch several customers come and go and get their coffees the lady asked me what am I waiting for I have said 2 double tall white chocolate mocha 's. As I wait five minutes still no coffee's and customer's still getting their coffee's as I wait. So I left sandwiches and say I will not return. It's as if they acted like I wasn't even there.
Efficient, friendly service. Always willing to accommodate a special request for the barista. And their skills at an iced cappuccino - heavenly! Real frothy foam atop creamy cool espresso.
Very slow service. Baristas were constantly talking instead of making drinks; taking 15+ minutes for 2 drinks with little traffic. Makes me wonder how long it would take it the morning during a rush.
Standard starbucks but with drive-thru, free wifi, and an amazing, well-spaced outdoor patio.  It's a great place to do work outside the house during COVID
I often drove through and every single time the lid was loose, and coffee ended up all over. The quality of the coffee is bad and lack of consistency. I will never come back!

Caffeine not Caribe. Stupid spell check.  Well, fast service, friendly and I get Fry's points. What more can you want.  If you are near, stop on by, they have s few tables and chairs where you can sit.

I spoke with a manager named Carol and she was very nice. She replaced my order and made it right. Like I said this is a great, beautiful and spacious Starbucks and I hate giving negative reviews when people work so hard to get everyone their morning coffees. So I am happy with the way Carol treated me as a customer and replaced my order for me. Thank you.  However, when my daughter went to pick up the order she was treated rudely by the same employee I spoke with the second time they got it wrong. I did not get her name but she is a very overweight woman with a nasty attitude.  The order is a simple order: Venti Iced Coffee Regular Splash of Heavy Whipping Cream 6 Pumps of Sugar Free Cinnamon Dolce 6 Pumps of Sugar Free Vanilla Cold Foam Cinnamon Powder  Easy. Maybe she shouldn't work in a coffee shop if making coffee is too difficult. Unfortunately I will be choosing a different Starbucks.

Most unorganized and dysfunctional Starbucks I have ever been to. As a gold member, nothing makes my day more than my afternoon coffee. Workers are rude and appear as they don't want to be bothered, staff is overwhelmed with the massive drive through line and there have been several times they have told me they can't make my drink due to them being out of certain things such as cold brew and sweet cream. Not your typical Starbucks that's for sure... something is always off at this one! Very disappointing!!!

I always come to this starbucks because I live on this side of town. The people there are super friendly. Compared to the other Starbucks I been to throughout Tucson, they are the only place that make my drinks how I want it. Everywhere else I have been don't make it quite right. Plus the people in the Safeway are friendly people. I would recommend grocery shopping and the Starbucks kiosk there anytime.
Rude as hell the worst service didn't shake drink didn't even use manners I work in service I understand how hard it can be but the experience I had was ridiculous rude

Came to this facility this morning asked for a trenta pink drink no strawberries 4 pumps of vanilla . I got 4 scoops of strawberries and vanilla! The berries get caught in the mouth piece and the straw! So I came back this the facility this evening order same one even said please no strawberries explain what happen this morning I get my drink there was a strawberry in I said no strawberries she said oh it just fell in it like no bug deal !!! I will go else wheee ! They need help the line was so long there was 4 ahead of us took 47 minutes have a great day

I come here all the time. Most of the time, the staff is on point!  A few times Ive had one particular young lady at the drive thru who just doesn't understand coffee, money or customer service. Tonight in particular, I ordered a dirty chai with an extra shot and she told me I was ordering a made up drink...   Ummmm...no. That's a very common drink served at most coffee shops. It's literally on their menu.    When she went to hand me the drink, she just shoved her arm in my car and didn't say a word. Just hoped I saw her. It's very weird and she needs training... Everyone else is great. Just not her....
Got the pink drink which is a must! Friendly drive employee and no wait! Whoo hoo! Right behind Tijuana Flats

Horrible customer service. There's a girl who works there, she must be a manager, she is so rude. She always has her hair tied back tight and has painted on thick eyebrows. Please fire her. My friend kindly asked for her to correct her drink since they put sweetener in it on accident & she made it seem like it was her fault. Took it back and just handed it to her without saying a word. No smile from her. No personal interaction. And this is my weeklong observation of going there twice a day for 7 days straight.

This location of Starbucks is the best!  I've been hopelessly addicted to Starbucks Coffee for well over 10 years. I love how fast I can get a coffee in the morning on my way to work. Either by walking in or drive-through, they haven't ever messed up my order yet. I used to live on the other side of the city and I seemed to wait forever in the morning, but not at this location. They have extra staff and they almost know how many people to expect.  Also I love the FREE WiFi that they offer. It's nice if I'm having a stressful day and just want to sit for abit on my computer and read/send off emails. The atmosphere inside is very calm and relaxed.

I'd give this particular Starbucks a pass, if I were you.  The wait is generally quite long.  I've waited up to 10 minutes at the drive through where, unfortunately, you can't get away once you're stuck there.  Today, with the drive through line of cars hitting about 8, I went inside.  There was no one in front of me in line so I thought this would be good.  However, I waited at the counter for about 2 minutes with no one coming to take my order.  After that I just left, thinking that if it is more than 2 minutes to take my order, how long would it be to actually get a cup of coffee.  I wish I could cash in the Starbucks gift card I got - so not worth it.  Also, I've gotten an inedible breakfast sandwich here with my coffee once in the drive through.  The muffin-and-ham sandwich was still ice cold in the middle.  So, give this place a pass and hit one of the non-chain coffee shops nearby.  I'm definitely pretty annoyed with this store and won't bother going back.

Hands down, the friendliest Starbucks I've visited.  I'm proud to call this place my neighborhood coffee spot.  The drivethru is my go-to for extra tough mornings.  It's like the staff knows that mornings are rough and they are extra nice because of it.  The patio is big with quite a few tables.  Inside there's plenty of space for everyone.  Don't forget to check out the sale table near the bathroom.  I love unique coffee mugs!  My favorite drinks are the white mocha latte and pumpkin spice latte (always with an extra shot to balance out the sweetness), and the passion tea, iced and sweetened.  If you need a quick meal, the protein box and the quinoa salad are both tasty and nutritious!

This facility is large, and spacious.  However, the staff is horrible.  For almost 2 minutes today April 2, 2017, three employees were playing "pat-a-cake" literally at the cash rap. (I have several videos of these employees doing this). I would be happy to provide these videos to management.  How in any way, shape, or form is this appropriate in a packed restaurant/coffee spot where people are studying, working, and conducting meetings?  There did not seem to be a manager around to make sure that the employees were doing their jobs, and not goofing off while customers became onlookers, and people were waiting in line.  I will be returning to study, but management really needs to get their act together, and make sure that their employees are doing their jobs, and not making this location in Lionville the laughing stock of the area.

5 stars for the beverages and ambiance (we've all come to enjoy and know Starbucks for their drinks which are amazing)  2 stars for service, this is not the first time I've had issues with service  I came in and stood at the register for 5 minutes as "Bee" and another employee looked at me. After awkward contact I was finally asked me "If I had been helped"..I was the only person in line, and 2 other people were seated in the store.  Took 5 minutes to get a cold brew with cold foam, which came out wrong the first time but it was delicious ‍ obviously or I would have took my business elsewhere.
Don't order unless your already in hospital. Security said you can't go inside Unless there for a patient pickup. I called and no one answered to refund me. Horrible.

This was a welcomed surprise to have a Starbucks café inside of TGH. Usually the hospital Starbucks locations are more of a kiosk, but not this one. I was happy to see all the seasonal pumpkin spice offerings even though I was mostly interested in getting a cool refresher drink after being in the West Pavilion waiting room which seemed like forever. Okay well, it was really just most of the day. The mango dragon fruit refresher was exactly what I needed, and was just as good as I expected it to be. This location appeared to be fully stocked with grab & go items as well. I was there around 4 pm and only a few people in line.  If you happen to be in the West Pavilion, just take a right at the Golden Tree of Life and follow the signs to Starbucks & McDonald's. You can't miss it. It will be on your left. If you're anywhere else in the hospital and don't see the signage, then just ask one of the many friendly TGH staff members. They will point you to the right direction.

Very disappointed in this Starbucks The service was good.  The drinks had very little flavor. Ordered a vanilla frap with a shot of expresso. I ask if they put a shot in. I think he was offended. The next drink was a Carmel frap with no whip same-thing weak flavor and with whip.  My compliments to Starbucks at 96 th street they know how to make it.
Great place, quite, clean, fast service. A+

This is a great Starbucks. They usually have a line in the drive thru, but it always moves quickly. I've never had a bad experience at this Starbucks. They people are always friendly, service quick and accurate.

Rude and efficient employees. It's an airport. Everyone is on a timeline. There were four employees, two of them basically doing nothing, and the one at the espresso machine in between drinks because they're moving customers through so slowly. Meanwhile, the only cashier taking orders, takes time to go actually fix a drink. No one steps up to the cash register to take an order for the wait on someone else. so the long lessons around the corner. Plus every single one of the employee individuals had an attitude. I'm also quite sure that Starbucks could give a hoot

Came in the drive through today to order a dragon drink with foam on top. I guess they forgot to turn the mic off because they started laughing at what a ridiculous drink it was and how "people just order things just to order it" maybe you should double check if your mic is off next time mrs. Haylee

This is the Starbucks located in the lobby of the hotel we were staying at for our annual convention.  It has a large seating area in the back with many tables for meetings or just comfy chairs to sit in.  During my convention, I would duck down here to use their wifi and get some work done while I was on lunch break.  This place has the same great drinks that you have grown to love and expect from a Starbucks.   Everytime I came in here the place was spotless and the staff was extremely helpful and friendly.  They even remembered me from the days prior.  It was very welcoming.

It's Starbucks you know the chain you either love it or hate it....I'm a HUGE fan.   This one has all the perks *drive thru  *outdoor seating  *full offering of bistro boxes & food options -- I've done the leg work not all Starbucks in Reno carry the Edamame Humus Bistro Box  *Free wifi *Lots of indoor seating options -- bar seating facing the window, 3 bistro tables & large communal table.    Linda is my favorite barista here!  Actually they're all pretty nice!   Yup it's pricey but an addiction is an addiction!

Waited 30min on a mobile order. Was ignored by a couple ladies when politely asking why my ticket was crumpled on the counter (after politely waiting 25min before asking after the mobile order). Never again.

I love the location and drive thru option of this Starbucks but the coffee has recently been less than satisfying.   The atmosphere is pleasant and clean. The staff are friendly and polite. The drive thru is clutch. Food is meh-right. The coffee is unpleasantly watery like there isn't enough coffee beans for the brew. Add more beans and this place will shine again like it did in it's hey day.

This Starbucks never gets drinks made to order, the drinks are always super watered down and they never taste good. Feels like a "half-assed" Starbucks, including employee effort, overall food, and customer service. You definitely don't get what you pay for. Go to a different Starbucks.

Avoid at all costs. This location has experienced management and staff shifts recently. It used to be a really great coffee shop now it's become a McDonalds with constant pestering to upsell. Not to mention its filthy.
Very disappointed to see that they found it necessary tO cover up and blank plate every single plug in the place. Never have I run into this issue at any other Starbucks.

This Starbucks is different from a lot of Starbucks I have visited in the past. For one, it is very diverse and the staff seems pretty friendly for the most part! It is also rather small, which makes it rather difficult to get in and out of the store/parking lot.   As far as the coffee was concerned, my Cafe Mocha Latte was amazing, perfect amount of chocolate and just enough espresso! I have had this drink at other Starbucks before, but the one I had here has to be the best!  The staff is usually very kind, and helpful! The only con is that the store could be a bit more cleaner, especially the condiment bar, other than that.. thumbs up!!

I am always hesitant to write reviews for chain stores like Starbucks.  There isn't anyone coming to a Starbucks who is checking a Yelp review first.  Almost always, folks are headed to Starbucks because they need their legal fix of caffeine and know that Starbucks is a international consistent supplier.     This Starbucks is no exception.  The staff is fantastic, I have always had great service here, and well made drinks.  I am a big fan of how Starbucks treats its staff (and have been for a very long time, they have treated several of my friends very well and earned my loyalty a long time ago).  It is nice to arrive in a shop and feel welcomed and like the staff are happy to see you.   But, the layout of this Starbucks leaves something to be desired.  I like Starbucks as a workspace.  It is usually a great place to zone out and focus on my task at hand and still be around people.  All of the Starbucks around here seem to be moving towards a shared workspace model, with one large table with several chairs.  This encourages co-working at the table, and leaves more room for lounge chairs so people can be social.  There is a lack of open space, so they can't easily add more tables if they wanted to - so I guess they have done the best they can with with what they have to work.  It is a free standing store and can't really expand any further- it also has a drive through.   I visited this Starbucks today because the Starbucks by campus on Central has remodeled and significantly reduced the aviable space.  They have added a co-working table there too.  At least this one still has more space for people to lounge and be social.  Central seems to have forced everything to take place at the co-working table.   It is probably a choice from corporate Starbucks to reduce people loitering in their shops (which started before the recent troubles over race and kicking people out up north).   Helpful tip- get a Starbucks card or app.  It is a pre-paid card and lets you discounts and free drinks.  If you are planning to loiter it is a great deal - because it makes the normally $.50 refills on ice coffee or tea free.

Like most Yelpers have already mentioned, Starbucks is going to be Starbucks. Sometimes, you'll have an exceptional experience, but more often than not, you'll just be here to grab what you need and leave. The Starbucks inside this Sheraton on Canal Street is no different, but luckily this hotel actually has a Starbucks.  Recently, I stayed at the Sheraton for a conference and was annoyed by the fact that we weren't offered breakfast. Luckily, this Starbucks saved my life. Open very early at around 5am, you can definitely turn to this Starbucks for your snack needs. This Starbucks has your usual coffee, bananas, desserts, breakfast items, as well as water.  However, there's several cons worth mentioning at this Starbucks. For one, the space is small. On high traffic days, you'll be waiting a while just to order and you'll be lucky to find a seat. Also, I'm not even sure there's Starbucks WI-FI, but at least if you have a room, Sheraton WI-FI reaches you here?  But the worst thing is that prices for their items are ridiculous. Not sure if it's hotel surge pricing or competitive corporate pricing, but I was so annoyed at the fact that I had to pay $6 for a stupid parfait and $6 for a fruit cup. That's crazy expensive! But at least Starbucks is here if you do need food (shrugs)?  Overall, I am grateful that the hotel has a Starbucks. It offered me water, food, and a place to relax when I needed it. But prices are so high, the space is small, and there's no WI-FI! I give this place a "tolerable, sufficient for sustenance" rating.  Eat well and prosper,

Pretty typical Starbucks. The workers were nice, the food and drinks were good. It wasn't the cleanest location I've ever been to, but it wasn't atrocious. The parking lot is pretty busy. I recommend parking near one of the others stores or you may get blocked in by the vehicles in the drive through.

Best customer service ever! I rely on this Starbucks 4-5 mornings a week to get me through the day. I work 3rd shift and then 2 hours later e-learn for 7+ hrs with my 1st grader. Between work and e-learning I need caffeine to recharge. Every experience has been pleasant. Zeb is a great leader with an amazing team. Whether I order online or in the store they smile and call me by name when I walk in. My order has only been incorrect once in 3 months and they quickly fixed it with an apology and plenty of caramel sauce. You really won't find a better place to get your caffeine fix or baristas who still somehow like people. During this pandemic they have also been the only place I have felt comfortable going. Everyone wears their mask properly, there is no dine in seating and they work quickly so you are only inside for a moment. I love this Starbucks.

Literally waited in the drive thru for 12 mins at this location off Speedway and Pantano. I would have gotten out of line but I was blocked in with cars behind and in front of me. Ordered two grande coffees that came out to 12$, defiantly more than I wanted to spend. The staff wasn't rude, but wasn't the friendliest either. I ordered two medium Carmel Macchiato's that were pretty dang good so I'll give them that. I may return to this location in the future.

This is my very favorite very starbucks. I study here as often as possible. The only thing I would say that there are limited seats with access to outlets. The staff is amazing. I recommend times 100.

I love this Starbucks.  It's my go-to attagirl prize for getting my grocery shopping done at the nearby Walmart. I've always received prompt, friendly service and it's nice that they have a drive thru.
Love this Starbucks!They're nice and know their menu!my drink is a bit complicated but they always know what I mean and make it perfectly!

I really like this Starbucks. 1. it's right by my house, 2. the staff is typically kind and sweet, 3. the wait isn't too bad. The only downfall is that sometimes my drink is spot on and sometimes they don't blend it well enough so when I get home I have to mix it myself. I always do the mobile ordering and they're on top of that! One of the perks of Starbucks. Definitely a good starbucks other than the drink mishaps from time to time.

Look it's a really good place and their food is really good plus their drinks are amazing but some of them are just pure chocolate and sugar and others are just coffee which is really good I like coffee but here's the thing not only that it's extremely pricey but that doesn't stop me from getting a five star review because it's just so awesome highly recommend it just I wouldn't go there every single day or you might not have any money

They coffee here is good, like at all Starbucks. The offerings to eat is quite limited. What is astounding is how indifferent the people who work here are to how loud the music is. At times it is impossible to talk to someone on the phone without using the speaker phone. I have had to talk to people who work at various Starbucks about this, requesting that they turn the music down, mostly with bad results. They say they will gladly will turn the music down, then they don't. And they will not turn it off. I can only surmise what the reason is for this is: They think they are a music venue, and the music they are playing is simply too important for people not to be exposed to it.

I have been meaning to post a review about this Starbucks for a while. I have to say every time I go (usually during morning rush on way in to work) the staff in the drive thru is always super sweet and friendly. I don't think I have ever had someone not cheery and pleasant. Especially with Covid it has been so nice to have such lovely people. It is a credit to management that they do such a good job hiring and encouraging good behavior.

They're so friendly at this location and up until lately my drinks have been great. But the past two times I've ordered a hot caramel macchiato they've forgotten to put in the vanilla syrup. I put some sugar in it when I got home and it was ok, but I should t have to pay $7 for a drink that's made Incorrectly. They did fix it, but the second drink I got was still wrong and I didn't have the heart to throw another one away.

Speedy service, Keep it up baristas! always very friendly and professional.  A little facts on Starbucks...originally, Starbucks offered only two sizes: short (8 ounces) and tall (12 ounces). When the company added grande, venti and trenta, they kept the old names for the original drinks.  At most Starbucks around the world, the size options are: short (8 ounces), tall (12 ounces), grande (16 ounces), venti (24 ounces), and trenta (31 ounces).  Grande is Italian for 'large,' venti means 'twenty,' and trenta is 'thirty'.   After a trip to Italy in 1983, founder Howard Schultz became obsessed with its coffee bars and wanted to introduce Italian names. As customers demanded larger sizes, 'short' was taken off the menu board so the larger sizes of 'venti' and 'trenta' sizes could be added Tall ended up becoming the smallest sized drink on the printed menu.

BAD HORRIBLE WORST!! The service at the drive thru was so slow, I thought maybe they were being robbed. I paid over $4.00 for a drink that I never received. I knocked & knocked on the drive thru window. They never returned. Believe me, if I didn't require help getting in & out of the car because of a leg injury, I would have gone in. I did call corporate to complain. They said they'd call back. They never did.  NEVER pay until your drink is ready.

A bit slow (occasionally) with the drive through in the morning, but other than that - they're a keeper. Two thumbs up for their happy voices streaming through the microphone at 7:30 in the morning. It's just what I need! I'm not a morning person. I'm not. So, when someone else can force their happiness upon me AND hand me a coffee, we might as well my friends.

I recently came to this location and ordered a double espresso with a splash of milk. When we received the drink, it was cold. We went back in to get it replaced with a hot one and the workers claimed it was because of the milk that it was cold. When the remade it, with comparably the same amount of milk, the thing was actually hot. Which leads me to believe it was a cup of some old shots of espresso they had sitting around. I'm glad it was replaced, but the deceit, customer service and quality of the original drink is offensive. Any who, was happy to let this go. But today I came to this business and saw that they are completely closed, doors locked, over half an hour before the listed closing time on the door and online.  Sooo yep this location is caca, avoid at all costs.

My favorite Starbucks especially cause it's the closest one to where I live! Surprised I haven't done a review sooner since I come here so much. Staff is usually friendly & they do wear masks. Cafe dining closed as of today and only the drive thru is open. So the drive line was longer than usual but this one is pretty busy in general thanks to the close proximity to the U of A!

This place is so unbelievably slow it is ridiculous. I went to their drive through on my break for a tea.. it took thirty minutes to get through with 5 cars in front of me. I would have left had the woman in the car behind me not refused to move. Late back to work from my break and I am never coming back here again

Ordered ahead and still waited over 20 minutes for my food item which I never received. I asked several times and was told "it's coming, we are really backed up". When people behind me received their items before me, I had no choice but to leave without my sandwich as I was late for work due to waiting 20 minutes. Starbucks has gone so downhill

There are always super nice and they know what they're doing unlike the one on Southport and 65 .. This location is always busy the Drive-Thru lately but they're always SUPER friendly here at this location.. It's right around the corner from where I live but again super friendly.
Ordered a few Cappuccinos with Carmel, a pink drink for everyone and a pupaccino for my puppy. Each was made very well and my dog loved his little pupaccino.

dead roaches on the floor, rude barrister behind the counter.  Ended up walking out after waiting 15 minutes and not getting my drink (and there was no one else in the store and the drive through was not busy).  The best:  the rude employee had the nerve to flip me off on the way out the door.  To the short white girl with extremely short black hair, watch out, you never know who a customer may know at the corporate office!!!! Just saying.

Spent all morning here. Was busy when I arrived and somebody was willing to let me sit next to them on the table they weren't using - moving some documents so I could work on some stuff as well. Free WiFi and and I was able to plug my laptop into the outlet on the wall. I have a giftcard on the Starbucks app and was able to get two free refills! Will definitely return. Hopefully there are some places to sit outside in the summer!

The service here is absolutely terrible, I will not return. I have came here like 7 times now and gave them the benefit of the doubt because they are a new location. I've ended up in the drivethru line stuck for 30 minutes 2x when there was only 2 cars ahead of me when 1 order each. It's wild how long it takes to get through when they are not short staffed.

There isn't much particularly noteworthy about this Starbucks location.   It's as modern as it needs to be, it's as cozy-warm as it needs to be, the baristas are super-friendly, but it is a little on the smaller size as far as seating is concerned.   There probably is enough seating for this inconvenient location, which is at the end of a strip mall on the frantic-busy Germantown Pike. You just gotta be quick to enter the parking lot at the right spot, and not drive past this location; else you'll have to make a round-trip back to access it again!  My drink was made perfectly, and the barista went so far as to add a little sprinkled fairy dust on top of the whipped cream - something I haven't had happen at any other location; that just added a little extra zip that made me smile, which made for a completely enjoyable visit.   The baristas are hipster-cool here, so I shouldn't have been too surprised!

I did a mobile app order today and ordered to the wrong location. I showed up here and when they couldn't find my order and I realized what I did, I was mortified and offered to pay in cash for my order due to the giant line behind me. They were so kind to me and told me it happens all the time and they would take care of me. I was blown away. They all joked and laughed with me at the window while they made my order and really brightened my day. Thank you for the excellent customer service!

The people here are always amazing, polite, and amazing. I have never had a problem with them unlike other Starbucks where it's an issue just to order a drink. The manager is super nice and ready to help. The baristas are on point with everything they do including friendly service and attentive actions. I trust them to do it right every time. This should be the most popular location in town, if it isn't all ready.

I love Starbucks. The selection of beverages that warm your insides just bring me pure joy. One thing in particular that I do appreciate about Starbucks is how tailored each individual store is to the location the store is located - this location just so happens to be located in the heart of SLU campus and is a pain to get in and out off in a jiff.   The service: Impeccable  The quality of beverages: Outstanding The location: Just okay.. Parking: Let's just say you may have a better chance finding a needle in a haystack verse finding a parking spot.  The circular building that the Starbucks is located in is a part of the community but just not conducive for customers trying to get in and out of quickly.  Will I still be back?  Heck yeah, of course as I still need my espresso.  Cheers,

I came here with a group of friends today. I was the first to order out of 7 of us and my drink was the last to come out. I have a cold  so I ordered a hot chia tea latte,as I ordered I said hot not iced three times to clarify, but I received an iced chai tea latte. My friend ordered a pour over coffee, after ten minutes of everyone else having their drink he finally said something to her. She apologized and started the drink. He waited another ten minutes and finally said something again and she said she had once again forgotten and then finally gave him his black coffee. It took twenty minutes to make a pour over. I would understand if it was super busy and chaotic here but we were the only ones inside and there was no one in the drive-thru.
This is my local starbucks and it's always quick and I get my drinks made perfectly! Sometimes there can be a big rush, but try to go there throughout the day and you'll avoid it.

So we take the dogs for a walk, as allowed, and visit this location in North Meridian as we often do. Walking across the parking lot I call out to the worker emptying the trash and ask if they are open.  She replies they are but not the cafe.  As we are at the sign for the drive thru we inquire whether to walk to the window or order at the sign.  The same worker says they won't accept walk up orders. I realize this is a new situation for all but how about a little adaptive effort.  I can't order in the lobby and I can't order at the window.  Poor customer service, Starbucks at 96th & Meridian. Adapt as we all have to and show some support to those getting a little exercise by walking. Would a bicycle rider also be denied service? If this is a store manager's decision I hope a regional manager would provide a solution.  If this a corporate policy then shame on you for not "thinking through" how to continue to serve all customers.
Quick service and pleasant staff. Coffee and food were good and served quickly!  Got a double chocolate chip frap and bacon & Gouda sandwich.

During this COVID-19 pandemic, I have found this Starbucks location to be the best in the area for continued efficient service. They have become my favorite to go to.  I frequent many of the locations in the area and this location is consistent in the quality of their drinks and the speed at which they can get thru a long drive-thru line. They have also been prudent in handling the way they hand out the items in the drive-thru window. Pretty much all of the baristas I have encountered there at the window have been friendly and diligent.   Earlier this week, I had an opportunity to order inside. They had the tables marked accordingly as to where is open to sit and where isn't. There is also a small patio in front. We chose to sit there. There are about 3 umbrellas you can sit under out there.  I will definitely be back!
Ordered Flat White. Served in a cold latte cup. Tasteless. Trash on the floor. Trash can overflowing. Gangster music playing the background. This is Starbucks in Webster right?

Fired my 3 employees for "violation" that didn't actually break any rules. I will not be using this coffee shop anymore before work, in a pandemic where businesses are stating they're desperate for workers I feel they need to see their employees as humans and not machines.

If my memory serves me right, this Starbucks used to be a Long John Silver's! That might explain why it's a lot more spacious than a lot of the other Starbucks locations that dot US 19. The inside is great for getting work done as there are tons of tables and lots of space. Plugs are easy to come across and there is a lot of natural light. They also have a pretty big patio area if you prefer being outside.  This Starbucks is extremely busy but it is also well-staffed so the employees and baristas can get everyone's orders out pretty quickly. My only gripe here is that the building itself is built a bit too close to the intersection of 34th and 22nd so it feels like a game of Frogger trying to get in and out of the store. The store itself doesn't interface too well with the surrounding plaza either so parking and getting out gets a little awkward. Definitely not a dealbreaker though!   This location is definitely one of the more work-conducive ones in my book, I'll be back.
Decent Starbucks but definitely would be better if they had a staff that actually knew what they were doing and you didn't have to explain to them what kind of drink that you wanted....

Only Starbucks that can not process a simple order and hand it to you at the counter - no there was not a line- The simple order was a venti Pike Black poor the coffee and hand it too me. This is not the first time but my last time here.

Because Starbucks has the same drinks...well... everywhere, I base my Starbucks reviews on customer service and ambiance/seating areas. As someone who likes to use Starbucks to get writing done, this location is pretty okay.  Late Saturday afternoon, is a little less crowded than going on a Sunday. There are plenty of seats, although I wish there were more electrical outlets. The weekend lines tend to be long, but the baristas are friendly, and they are usually pretty good at getting through orders fairly quickly, even when busy.  What I don't like?  It's always FREEZING in the seating area of this location.  What's up with that? I mean, I always have to wear a heavy sweater or hoodie no matter what the temperature is.  I guess that will come in handy during the summer though when it's hot.

I ordered an iced latte and got an iced coffee. I asked if it was supposed to be a latte and the barista looked at the sticker and said no. I assumed I mis-ordered and took the drink. I took a sip and just randomly looked at the sticker, IT SAID LATTE. So I took it up and asked if I actually could have the latte I ordered and the barista seemed super cool about it. When my order was called , it was a hot latte even though I ordered ice. I didn't feel like wasting another drink, so I just dealt with it. I've never been inside this Starbucks, it's clean and spacious but I most definitely wont be heading back to this location anytime soon.
These people refused me service and I saw them spit in some poor mans drink! Please consider ANYWHERE else to go this place is disgusting!!!!

Worst Starbucks EVER.  Fourth time thru and not one time have they had the ingredients to make a smoothy. No one time have they had a breakfast sandwich I ordered. I'm done with that place. I've given them plenty of opportunity to turn my opinion around.
Friendliest Barista's around!! All employees at this location are phenomenal!! They always go over and above to make their customers happy!!
Worst service I've seen. No toilet paper, no coffee lids. Took forever to get us our coffee and it's always filthy, not coming back. Ever.

There is always a very long line at the Starbucks it seems like. If you go here you should use the mobile order app, and hopefully it will be ready by the time it arrives. The layout of the store is awkward for the long line.

Relative to other Starbucks, this one is generally okay- nothing horrible, nothing great.   The building doesn't look like a typical Starbucks, and the first time I went here, I actually drove by it. It's easy to find parking, and the place is on the small side- about maybe 7-8 tables plus a few chairs inside, and then an outside patio for when it's nicer out. I've always been able to find a table inside, though. The service is decent, the line fluctuates between quite long and empty, and the noise level fluctuates between quiet and very loud. Service is okay, speed of drinks is okay, food is okay. The bathrooms were clean, and they have free WiFi.   Generally a decent, middle of the road Starbucks, but it's main advantage is its location right on 202, near the KOP mall.

The red headed gentleman was great & pleasant to work with.. however the female on staff was not very nice at all. (Dark hair with grown-out shaved sides) Marching around like she had somewhere else she'd rather be, tapping her foot at the front door at 5:59p to ensure we were out of the facility at 6pm on the dot was a bit ridiculous. Didn't budge with a response when I smiled & thanked her for her patience (which was premature & completely unwarranted) & truly made us feel unappreciated for even spending our time & money in her store during our study session... we will not be back. I'd rather drive across town to a different location than to risk feeling that way again. I'm almost positive this girl was management & that makes me question the type of personality Starbucks hires. Promote the young redhead guy! He's adorable & personable :) xo

Very good Starbucks right in the heart of Carpinteria. They were super busy but I got my drinks FAST!!!!!! I was impressed that the barista used my name several times during the transaction and she took the time to clarify my order.   My drink came out perfect and just how I ordered. Thanks for another lovely visit Starbucks!
Closed at 5 on New Year's Eve! I get closing early but not at 5. Lost my business for all of 2022

This is probably the worst Starbucks I've ever been to - but that isn't the fault of the workers there. I feel quite bad for them. It's always so incredibly busy and they are ALWAYS short staffed. I don't know if this location is just trying to save some payroll or what - but management needs to reevaluate their staffing needs. Kudos to the staff for dealing with it

Usually come there often, but service was beyond rude didn't even greet me, be honest didn't recognize the girl serving too, spend most of the time talking to coworker so just left. Time is money and if I'm not getting proper service definitely not giving my money.

I waited a long time to get one drink and my order was not correct. Please hire more baristas and focus on training and speed. I wish there more Starbucks in this area. It is right across the street from wawa, so this Starbucks really should try to improve.

Stopped by to rock a iced venti white tea w 1 pump of liquid cane cuz it's just crazy hot out already!  My barista, Shelby was moving at the speed of light as her hands blurred w speed. I've been to, some would say, too MANY coffee shops but this particular Starbucks location rocks. Fast, clean, and really friendly staff. Nice change!

Having a full functioning Starbucks inside of a hospital was a great idea. There long lines in the morning but it moves really quickly.  If you go to Starbucks, I would suggest getting the app because you can get points on your purchase and eventually get free coffee.  You can't redeem your free drinks at this location.

Needed a couple coffees and maybe something quick for breakfast. Walked to Starbucks and I've heard all the hype on television about them not throwing anyone out. I guess I figured how big a problem could it be.... then we went here. I ordered two coffees and my wife left it up to me, and I looked around. There were NO open seats homeless "patrons" were taking all the seats. I looked down and saw a bag with bugs crawling all over it. No, not eating here and if Starbucks is into the homeless more than coffee and pastry customers maybe they should open shelters. Expensive coffee and not a seat to drink it in... great business model, I wont ever go back.

Very disorganized. Got up charged, wanted a black coffee and some egg bites: should've been around or under $10 but got charged $16. Then they forgot about my coffee and I had to remind them 5 minutes after that I just had a black coffee that they literally keep on hand and just have to pour it. And then as soon as I picked it up, spilled all over my hand. So good luck at this one  egg bites were good though.
We are from Pittsburgh and we are visiting Nashville for the weekend. We had a huge order for our party and Allison gave us the best service that we've ever had at any Starbucks!
Rude Baristas used to be a fun place to go to a couple years ago now I  don't even look forward to going there anymore

Let's face it: Starbucks are like Walgreen's - one on just about every corner in town. For the coffee lover, that's a good thing because you know you are never far from a hot cup of java. The thing that sets this particular Starbucks apart is the congenial, helpful staff. Often I stop in not for coffee but because I'm having a bit of a down day and need a lift in spirit.   With very few exceptions, the baristas here always seem to enjoy helping their customers...and that's something you don't see every day! I know that no matter what the situation or time of day I can count on Sandy, Shelly, Emily, Heidy, Patrick and the rest of the staff to not only put a smile on my face and a tasty drink in my hand, but to make me feel valued as a customer and a friend.  A tip: For value and the best tasting cup of coffee you can get at Starbucks, treat yourself to a French Press. To me, that is the only way to discover the true flavor of the many coffees available. Be sure and ask your barista about food pairings to go with your coffee too. It is amazing the flavors you can experience when food and coffee are properly matched.   Now for the drawbacks. My past couple of visits here I've noticed a disturbing trend: one of the managers (at least he acts like a manager...I can't be positive) has a bad habit of talking down to the rest of the staff and tends to be a bit of a tool. From some of the comments I've heard it seems like he may have some unresolved anger issues. (If he weren't of above-average stature, I'd say it was a Napoleon complex.) Not sure what his deal is but it makes me wonder about what kind of people skills Starbucks requires managerial candidates to have. While it hasn't been enough (yet) to make me not return, it's getting really close.

This is not the best Starbucks but they get four stars for having quick service and the standard Starbucks fare. If you get there early in the morning, there is typically no line at the drive-thru.  The parking lot for this Starbucks is horribly designed and the drive-thru is even worse. It is especially at the apex of a U-turn. If you have a larger car, it is impossible to get close to the callbox. I have seen lines here back up to ten cars. The wait can be long, so if you are in a hurry, this is not your spot.  On this particular visit, I went inside to avoid the drive-thru line and ended up leaving before placing an order. Starbucks could probably put another location in Ambler. This one just gets too much traffic..
Fast, clean, not over crowded, professional area, lots of outdoor seating and plush chairs inside. Great for business meetings.

I love the service I get at this Starbucks. They always get my order perfect. I love bacon Gouda sandwiches and it's always prepared just right. My daughter drinks a mocha frap with hazelnut and roast. It's yummy. Go get one for yourself.

So just your regular Starbucks coffee shop with the usual stuff. I spent a whole afternoon here doing some paperwork after a weekend of working overtime in Tampa.  Baristas were polite and attentive. This shop was always crowded because it is near USF.  Store did look like it needed som TLC and floor was dusty.
It was good. Really like the vibes I was getting! Was busy, but understandable because all the drinks were heavily modified, I like my drinks simple. Please support our baristas by ordering simple.
Avoid during the mornings.  Wait time in drive-thru typically is about 25-30mins.  Either it's the only Starbucks in a 15 mile radius, which isn't, and super busy or it's super slow.

I love starbucks! This location not so much. Stopped in thinking it was going to be fast. Ordered my venti iced latte with coconut milk and paid.  While i was waiting i saw people cleaning because they were not busy. About 5 minutes pass and my order wasnt even started. I ask if they got my order and they were like no, the machine didnt print it out.  After that my order was made. I didnt get my good vibe in this starbucks. I will return cause it a good location. Its off the plant city exit. Easy in and out of the interstate.
Starbucks corporate!!!!!!!! .  You really need to come help re-train the people in this store.   Short staff and lack of experience

I'm pretty disappointed in this place. I come here often and today I order an extra hot chocolate and they handed it to me and it felt pretty empty but I didn't think much of it. Once I drove off I lifted the lid and my cup was only half full?

So I normally prefer to come to this Starbucks location. They tend to be friendly and quick. I am a sucker for good customer service. I came in to the store because the drive thur line was long. Like out in the street long. There was one group of three guys in front of me. They took their sweet time ordering and the cashier was falling all over herself in helping them. I dont mind it if you display that some level of helpful to everyone. Then she was so busy talking to another employee before she even greet me with a "we will be right with you".

This location might be the worst Starbucks ever.   Our orders are routinely incorrect.  And our drinks are routinely under poured.   The employees are usually rude and really seem like making orders is a pain for them. Maybe you should not be in a customer service roll.

This is one of the worst starbucks i have ever been to. who knew that you would have to mobile order an hour before you wanted your starbucks. I just waited 30 minutes to get my hot tea. They never printed out the mobile order receipts, people were coming and going who were in line. I believe starbucks noted that mobile ordering is for people who are in a rush, they just made me 15 minutes late for class. This starbucks needs to get it together or they are going to lose customers.

I'm sorry; I know it's still COVID and they're a new franchise who may be training more folks, but this Starbucks is the WORST I have ever experienced in town. The people are so nice, but there is a massive disconnect between ordering and receiving your coffee/drinks. Even the busiest shops in Franklin/Cool Springs turn around customers quicker with double the crowd.   We've tried to order in-store, online pickup, drive thru - every option comes with a minimum of a 15-20 minute wait as they all run around and forget who's order is actually finished and who's needs making. Really want to like this spot, but with other coffee options nearby, can't see us keeping up with a place that holds no value on decent service. Hope it improves!

Normally this small Starbucks is excellent. However, this morning we had to wait 15-20 minutes on two orders. A pumpkin spice latte and an Americano.   I finally asked the register person to credit our order.  He said "sorry". The basic root of the problem was a slow, untrained barista who seemed to go even more slowly after I complained about time.    So, I'm sorry to say I cannot rate this location higher than a 2
Caramel macchiato paired with the chicken sausage and bacon biscuit.   Pure yummy!  Mic drop!!

Walked in at 8:25 was told sorry we are closed. I asked what time do y'all close... the girl said 9 when I told her it was only 8:30 she said sorry we are closed as I said. She walked me out and locked the door behind me. 11/06. Rude as all hell! Closed over 30 minutes early and was nasty.
Line takes forever in morning. The drive thru people are very rude sometimes when you ask about the products they offer.

Of course we all love starbucks!  But lately I have found that my caramel mochiato doesn't taste the same as sometimes they don't have all the ingredients. Also, the location that I go to is the one on Town Center. I would order food in advance, go pick it up to find out that they didn't have what I ordered and I had to find some kind of alternative; then wait for them to prepare it which is what I wanted to avoid in the first place.  I wish that they would have called me in advance to let me know what they don't have in my order to spare me the wait.   I learned my lesson not to order in advance, but on site or drive-thru.

Just going to say that you guys need to get a bigger stock of food supplies. Every time I go to this location my usual drink doesn't taste how it should. I'm guessing their syrups aren't remixed everyday for good flavor. Today I even went back to get it fixed and it still came out bitter and like it's mixed w other things, but they insured me it was the right ingredients so I don't know. I'm just disappointed, this is my go to drink and I know exactly how it should be. And the past 3 times this location has made some funky drinks.

First time at this location in an area called The Loop. It's a half mile walking distance from Washington University Danforth Campus.   A FREE birthday reward was redeemed for a chocolate chip Frappuccino. Staff was friendly and drink yum! It's a small dining room but I found a clean table. The area was overall tidy. However the bathroom hallway was full of boxes which was unattractive.   I would return. 3.5 rounding up.

This Starbucks is trying it's best.   It's drive-thru is probably the busiest drive-thru not connected to a McDonald's of Chik-fil-a in Greenwood. They work hard to get your order correct, fresh, and to you and if they are achieving that they are successful in my eyes. They've been of consistent quality as long as I can remember and the pandemic hasn't put changed that from experiences!

This isn't just your average Starbucks. Walk through their doors and feel like you're home. I will go out of my way to see my favorite barista's at this location, they have been known to put a smile on my face even during a rough day. It's more than just my coffee, it's a cup of sunshine. A little cheesy? Yes. But to top it all off, if you are not as into the feel good stuff as I am, they make a perfect drink here.

When you order a hot coffee drink (Hot Venti Americano - extra shot) at nearly 5 bucks, you kind of want it at the right "hot" temperature. At least I do. This clearly wasn't. Lukewarm at best. I brought it up nicely and I was met with an insistent recalcitrant attitude. The young girl offered to add steamed cream which I never asked for, nor did I want. I would avoid this Starbucks until they get the issue taken care of. Turn up the hot water temp! Easy fix.

Always a great Starbucks! Their team is super friendly and knows many regulars by name. They always seem to get drinks out quickly no matter how many people are in line and the store is always clean and well stocked. They even have Cold Brew and Nitro Cold Brew on tap!
High priced dish water. For what they charge you would think they could turn out a better product. Yuck.

Waited 40 minutes and paid 6 bucks to get a cup of iced milk instead of the shaken espresso I ordered. I typically wouldn't leave a review, but this has now happened to me multiple times. I will not be coming back.

The drinks at this Starbucks is so watered down and tasteless, compared to the other locations..  We live about 4 blocks from the Mesa Location, but either drive downtown or go to the La Cumbre one by Macys to get my Chai lattes. The Frappuccinos there are no different. It sucks because it should all taste the same .. I suggest you guys make your drinks and go Compare with other locations to see what I mean. This has been the case every time we give this place "one last try"
I absolutely love this Starbucks location, the baristas are always super happy and helpful! Thanks for being a positive experience always

Every Starbucks is the same bland, boring, sterile yuppie infested, "I'm in a rush, get my latte quick" type place.  Awful Coffee, lousy rude service, terrible sandwiches, overpriced everything.  I avoid it always unless I get a free gift card.

Not a drive-thru location, which is always disappointing. And this location is especially small inside w/only 8 small tables and limited seating.   The staff was fairly nice, but the reason for the two-star rating was an epic fail on this location's part...how the hell do you not have any dark roast brewed? And then not even offer to brew some!? WTF?   I desperately needed caffeine and had already walked in, so I settled for the Pike w/a shot and 4 splenda. Let me just say, that was the worst $2.71 I have spent at an SBucks to date and shame on this location for not having dark roast and denying me a well-intentioned caffeine fix!

The customer service needs to be improved, our order was missing food and when we called to ask them They were very dry and rude. It sounded like they didn't care that THEY FORGOT TO PUT OUR FOOD IN THE BAG

As far as Starbucks go, this one is a gem. Especially for being located inside a mall, which usually spells doom for good service as well as good manners.  I come here so often, I'm pretty much a regular and have never had a bad experience. The staff is always friendly and the ones I see on a regular basis have a great sense of humor.  In fact, the last time I was here, the barista made my drink the wrong size. However, she remade it on her own, without me realizing that she had even messed up in the first place, let me take both drinks and gave me a "free drink coupon" for 'messing up" my order.  Doesn't get much better than that!

The absolute best Starbucks in Brandon. I have noticed they receive major slack for the time it takes to receive your order but what do you expect as it is in a central area of Branson. Other locations are tucked away or away from major traffic but this one is busy ALL the time. I've walked in tons of times and they welcome me by name. The managers are always available to speak to and the staff have no problem in correcting your order. I can tell you depending on the day I may request extra pumps of flavoring and they quickly respond. I have become very patient due to the personalities of the staff within the location. When I am super busy, I order pick up but the experience becomes different. The staff always ask me about my day or compliment something about me. They exude exemplary customer service that I can't help but to return over and over. My favorite person is Johnny. Sometimes I come order something just to see him not because the coffee is well as you know amazing. He simply makes my day. His smile brightens the room and he always checks to make sure my order is correct. I truly appreciate the time he as well as others take in making my coffee. I always look forward to my next purchase. Please go in and say hi to the staff especially Johnny and you may update any negative reviews.

I've been going to this Starbucks for at least 5 years. I'm not sure why I've never wrote a review before. ‍ This Starbucks is the friendliest on the Illinois St. Louis metro side. Since I'm a regular, they all know my name and greet me with it. The only other place I get that is Target in Edwardsville, but I worked there.  They are all super friendly and hold good conversations- when not busy. I think I've only had two issues in the years I've been coming here, and that is a lot when I'm a regular. They corrected the issue, without hesitation, and were apologetic about it. I've seen this with other customers too, not just regulars. Good food, good drinks, and good baristas.
