went out of my way to come here. Sat in the drive-through for 8 minutes. When I get up to the window, I sit for another five or six minutes. Then they tell me The espresso machine is not working. Then asked if I wanted something else. I said no, I went out of my way to come here to get a latte. She said sorry about that and gave me my money back. I just sat there for 14 minutes and my time was not acknowledged. They didn't offer me a free drink, free food. A gift card. Nothing. Justa "sorry about that". Just left me super annoyed that I wasted so much time for nothing.  I know many other Starbucks would try to compensate for some thing so serious but not this one I suppose.

My drink was good, but the person at the window put out a weird vibe that's sticking with me. I'll shake it off, but get some social skills.  You might just be the only person someone gets to see or speak to today. Just be nice... maybe smile.

By far my favorite Starbucks location in the New Orleans area! The service is usually very fast and if there ever is a wait, I'm never in line for more than 20 minutes. Every staff member at this location is very accommodating, energetic and friendly! Caesar is always a pleasure to see in the mornings when picking up or placing my order. Also to mention that this location and their baristas are ALWAYS consistent! My coffee always tastes very good. Thanks N. Causeway staff for always making my mornings better!

I mean, it is Starbucks so you can't expect a whole lot. When I entered the store it was quite messy and nobody acknowledged me. I stood at the counter for a few minutes before I was asked for my order. I chose a peppermint mocha with whipped cream. The wait time was about 5 minutes, not too bad. The milk tasted burnt however. Bleh. I was going to ask if I could have ny drink redone but the barista at the bar seemed in a bad mood so i didn't wanna make things worse.

They did a renovation which is nice because it doesn't look very dirty like it used to. But the people working there especially the old guys are still very rude and not friendly. I am not against old people working but they need to be nicer! Anyway. They also started putting locks on the bathroom doors and refusing homeless people using the bathrooms. They are people too. They just need to use the bathroom. Just ask them to not stay in there forever. It's not like they are the only one who would mess up your toilets!!!!!! Not sure about other Starbucks but this one is very close to where I work so I come here a lot and I see too much I gotta say!

This is literally the most poorly ran Starbucks I've ever encountered. The staff is extremely friendly, but they never seem to do anything with a purpose, even if they know there's 50 people in line. I've often seen them pause for a few moments to chat with no sense of urgency to get customers through.  While this would be normally infuriating at any location, more so at an airport. If it's a staffing issue, then please staff better, especially during peak hours.  But it's never okay for it to take 30+ minutes to get to the cash register. Wait is to be expected to receive your drink. But to order? That's simply not caring.

The drive through is incredibly too long every time I'm here.. this time I was in line 30 min. It's just coffee what takes so long? NormAlly it takes anywhere from 15-20 min in line and that's a good day....

I am a huge Starbucks fan and was thrilled to see that this one was built right between my gym and my home! I went to get coffee the first day they were open and have been back several times since. I was disappointed on my first visit to discover that the employees were not as friendly as all the other Starbucks that I have been to. I decided to give them the benefit of the doubt since they had just opened and were possibly unfamiliar with the store. Unfortunately, this unfriendliness has not changed on any of my return visits. Until now, the quality of the coffee was just as good as any Starbucks so I just overlooked the unfriendliness, but today something happened that forced me to write this review.   Today I ordered a latte Macchiato with an extra shot. I told the cashier my name, she wrote it on the cup, and I went to wait for my drink. Christin the barista, called my name when it was ready and announced my drink but it was not what I ordered. I politely asked, "what did you say"? She did not reply, and my partner said he couldn't hear her either. I leaned in and asked again politely "excuse me Miss, what did you say?" She looked at me and impatiently and said the name of the drink again which I still did not understand. I smiled and said "well that's my name but that's not what I ordered". To which she replied petulantly "well then I guess it's not yours". She then condescendingly proceeded to explain to me the difference between what I had ordered and what she had made. So I replied again politely "well you can understand my confusion since that is my name and there's no one else here waiting". She snapped "yes of course".   Nothing else was said, she went about her business, and then after about two minutes took the mistake drink and threw it away. In reality, she had made an error and was making me a new drink. Shortly after, she gave me the correct drink and said barked "here you go"  I'm not sure why, but for some reason the management of this Starbucks tolerates unfriendliness and bad service. This Starbucks is conveniently located for me so I will continue to go there, but I will recommend that you be ready to be treated poorly when you get your coffee.

My go to Starbucks! I love this location because of the location itself and the always friendly service. I'm always greeted whenever I walk in the door whether it is busy or slow inside. When the weather is just right, the tables outdoors are the perfect spots to study or to hang out.   I recommend anyone who frequents Starbucks to utilize the mobile app rewards. I love the mobile order feature: place your order ahead of time and just pick it up if you're on the go!

Don't go to this Starbucks. Pregnant with my third kid and barely sleeping with bad pregnancy brain. I stopped by the Starbucks while walking my dogs because my daughter was hungry. I went inside to get her a snack. Being human and a tired, sleep deprived mom I accidentally walked off without my dogs. Realizing this shortly after, I called Starbucks in a panic & about in tears and told them I was not far away and was literally sprinting back to the store.  I asked if they could make sure no one steals them or anything. They said okay I see them and sure. WELL After this they called animal control and the sheriffs department on me. The sheriff told me after seeing my dogs she realized this wasn't really an appropriate call & that I was obviously a sleep deprived mom. She said she could tell I felt terrible and that my dogs were extremely well behaved and well cared for. She then told me to leave before animal control got there. Luckily, the sheriff was so understanding and kind. I will never return to this location. In the past they have done annoying things like mess up my drinks etc but I understand that mistakes happen and have never gotten upset. Now, I'm MAD. Support a local coffee shop instead. I am so easy to please this is my FIRST negative review. I usually only review things I love.

They are very, very busy here during peak times of the day. Unfortunately, I don't think the new staff at this location are prepared for the kind of rush they are receiving.   The drive-thru is always insane, and the cars in the drive-thru end up blocking spaces for people that want to park. Someone should really look into that because in the morning you end up with a huge traffic jam in this parking lot which leads to issues on Speedway (which itself is already heavily congested in the mornings). There needs to be some sort of order in this chaos.   Drinks, even if ordered 10 minutes ahead on the app, are slow to appear. I've had several times where I ordered 10+ minutes ahead, walked in, and still ended up having to wait for a drink. That would be acceptable, I guess, if every time I showed up my drink was correct.   On two occasions I have come in and waited for a drink only to have them put whipped cream on it -- which I specifically have them take off. The little sticker will even say no whipped cream and it appears. The first time this happened, the barista immediately noticed and remade my drink from scratch. It was appreciated. She gave the one with whipped cream to me and I passed it along to a co-worker as I have dairy issues and can't even have whipped cream on my coffee without tummy upset. This barista, I unfortunately did not get her name, gets a STELLAR 5 STARS!    The second time, I had to tell the barista that I didn't want whipped cream. She seemed annoyed, but saw this on the sticker, and took a spoon and scooped the cream out, then gave the drink back. A huge portion of the cup was whipped cream -- much larger than normal. I told her I wasn't trying to be a pain, but I'd like the drink remade since (1) I cannot have dairy and (2) almost 1/4 of the cup had been whipped cream. It was not my mistake and normally anywhere else they'd be happy to accommodate this request. The grumpily agreed, kind of snatched the cup back, and remade the drink. It was just poor service -- I understand them being busy, I get the frustration, but when I'm paying $7-8 for a latte I would prefer it to be made properly. This barista reduced my review to 3 stars and has made me question ever coming back.   This location is one I won't visit anymore unless I've somehow made an error in judgement and forgot to stop at my regular location(s). There are a lot of Starbucks locations and I won't make an effort to patronize one that won't make an effort to get my drink order correct, and in the event there is an error correct it without sass.

This Starbucks is a mess. I've gone here many times as it's close to the interstate, the baseball fields, etc. If you have 45 minutes to waist in line, go for it. Their cafe is rarely open and now they even close early on the weekend. They try to be friendly, but it comes off as hollow when they are obviously not trying to work hard to get your order out as right and fast as possible. I don't ever expect a Starbucks to be super fast but this is absolutely ridiculous!
They fired three good employees never going back... who fired them and whyyy. so lame.

I normally have good experiences with this Starbucks but not today. I ordered 2 strawberry refreshers and for some reason there's a fizz in both of them and it tastes old which makes me more worried. I'm trying to call to let them know and NO ONE is answering the phone I called 5 times already no answer. I don't spend $5 on a drink for it to taste like shit Starbucks !!!!
Didn't have my card and only had $100 bill, young preppy dude was acting like a baby and would not take my $100. Maybe put a sign up Starbucks. Not coming here again.

Waited in the drive thru line for over 30 minutes at this location which is by far the LONGEST I have ever waited in a Starbucks line.  This was in the afternoon on a Monday... not even what I would consider to be "rush hour".  Extremely frustrating!!

not so good experience. i waited a long time in the drive through. the lady that took my order sounded annoyed that i didn't know starbucks terminology. i asked for my drink to have extra cream and sugar, and an extra shot of espresso in it. at the window i overheard her say to her coworker "wait that was supposed to to have an extra shot of espresso. oh well". and she gave me my drink. i know what i heard, so i asked her if it has extra espresso in it. she hesitantly said yes. also, i asked for extra sugar, and in return got no sugar. there was no way i was waiting in that line again so i had to take the bad coffee and go.

The worst Starbucks experience I've had in a while. NO parking at 3pm, lot of people working inside at tables. I had to tell the barista my drink order 3 times (unsweetened green tea, light ice...not that hard!) and requested that they use my sweetener packets and they said it wasn't a problem.  I went to the restroom and when I came out, my iced tea still was not ready (no one was in front of me in line and no drive through customers). There were 4 total baristas and they were all talking to each other on their headsets about someone they knew in high school. Social hour apparently.   When I finally got my drink, my sweetener packets were floating my in drink. I was shocked and audibly said "seriously?!" And they looked at me like I was crazy. When I said my sweetener was floating in it, they offered to remake my drink but seeing as I still had a 2 hour drive, I wasn't interested in waiting to get it remade.  Needless to say, I will not be visiting this location again.

Only been here a handful of times. The oatmeal and breakfast items are good and the blonde roasts are never burnt. So by chain coffee store standards, this Starbucks deserves 4 stars.   For me, I don't think there are many tables in open areas for studying especially because I don't like the claustrophobic tables in the back by the restrooms, but they have a cute little area with couches if you want to catch up with friends!

went with a group of friends during a monday around 4pm. one friend ordered an iced caramel macchiato and there was no extra caramel and the drink was already mixed (the milk wasn't separated like it usually is when served). the same thing happened with another girl who ordered a similar drink. another friend had no caramel drizzle on the top of her drink, even though it was on her receipt. when i ordered the girl informed me that they were out of grande cups. she basically led me to believe that they couldn't just make  grande drink in a venti cup, so i had to get a tall. when i got it the cup was only 3/4 of the way full and half of the drink was foam. i couldn't even get to the actual liquid. definitely do not go to this location. i give 2 stars

This location never lets me down. They always have my togo order ready within moments of arriving. I totally avoid the drive thru line as you probably already know, they are always long and I have no patience. Going inside almost feels like a life hack. Always friendly, consistent and efficient.

This is your typical Starbucks, overcrowded, standard menu and awfully long drive-thru line. The service is exponentially friendly and very patient.   Tips:  -Order inside. I was behind three people and still got my coffee within 5 minutes or ordering. Beats waiting in drive-thru line! -Avoid around the 9am hour. -Get your order to go. Seating is spotty.
The staff is amazing! Always so polite , on point and just all around super friendly ! Coffee always taste great and food is fresh! Store is clean and comfortable! Thanks

My little sister and I frequent this location and we love it. We always go through the drive thru and we have never had a problem. The people who work the drive thru are extremely polite and nice. Great job to whoever does the hiring there. The coffee is delicious as it is most Starbucks. Quick and friendly service every time always equals a 5 star review from me! Great job!
Amazing this store is so funny. The workers laugh over everything I say! Come here! Pleaseeeeeeeeeee come here rn the people will love to assist you :)

Maybe it was just an off day, or maybe it was because of training new people, but this was kind of a confusing Starbucks experience. I guess I might be spoiled, because most Starbucks locations really get the concept of the "customer experience" right.   A quick stop in for a nice chilly frappuccino on this hot afternoon started out a little bit strange when I was the next customer in line, and an employee standing at an empty till caught my eye and smiled brightly. I started to walk toward her and give her my order, and the employee at the other till, who had been helping the customer in front of me, broke off to reprimand me for trying to cut in front of that customer! I felt quite taken aback at the intensity of his response, to what was obviously a misunderstanding. I could barely even get out the words, "i'm so sorry, I thought she was asking me to come up and order."   When it was my turn, for real and not for pretend, it became apparent that she was a brand-new employee, being trained, and thus it was no surprise that it was a slightly confusing process to get my card reloaded, my items run in, and finally paid for (no big deal in itself; we have all been learners at something, at some point in our lives). Perhaps this brand-new employee also learned that it sends a mixed message to customers waiting in line when you make eye contact and smile at them, but you are totally not ready for them to actually come up and order...  However, when I picked up my drink, it had not been marked correctly, so it wasn't quite right. However, it was close, so I just took it and left; I clearly needed to consume some of that caffeine, before trying to wrap my brain around any more confusion ;)
This is the filthiest Starbucks I have ever been in.  Hard to enjoy a cup of coffee when your surroundings are just gross!

After six weeks Tucktaway in quarantine, and not being able to find a Starbucks anywhere... It was like finding gold to realize the location had finally opened up! It's amazing how much you miss something when it's no longer there!
Friendly staff and accurate orders. Can't ask for much more from Starbucks :)

Well the mobile order said 10-18 minute wait.  25min in and no order yet.   Not well maintained. Poo service and bad attitudes.   Disorganized and unhelpful  No cans of refresher drinks. Needed to be refunded.   The Starbucks experience has really gone down hill with the over expansion of the last 2 years.  City locations still seem to be able to attract decent baristas but the suburbs are truly lacking.   Maybe Starbucks school is needed

The baristas at this location are incredibly nice and always manage to get drinks out correctly and fast. They always make my day and give a genuine smile and quick conversation. Wish I could give more than 5 stars!!

The last 2 times I have been to this location I have gotten a grande and both times they have put a venti cap on my drink. If I move to straw it comes off, and keeps falling off making my drink spill all over me.

Most staff was disinterested, boarder line rude. Coffee was ok. Not sure why Starbucks corp doesn't check reviews and fix problems. Luckily there are other alternatives now so it's easy to avoid visiting Starbucks again.

I live in southern Indianapolis, but work up here in Fishers. When I tell you that I haven't gone to any other Starbucks besides this one (with the exception of when I travel) in months, I am being entirely honest. The employees are so kind and welcoming. Everyone is very patient with me, as I am not much of a coffee drinker. Today I decided I needed a "pick me up" after spending yesterday put on the water and working all day today at work. I ordered myself a green tea, an impossible sandwich, and ordered my manager a peach tranquility tea. I picked up my mobile order in the drive thru and when I got to the window, Aaron was there to greet me. Aaron was a new face for me at this location, but he/they did a wonderful job. He/they (apologies if this is not your preferred pronoun!) double checked my name and items. He/they handed me each item as he/they told me what each one was and he/they was extremely kind. I left with a smile from ear to ear because of the kindness Aaron had showed me at the drive thru window. I know these reviews sound silly, but as someone who also works in a setting where I interact with customers daily, I truly value when I visit an establishment and the employees are kind. Honestly, the smallest bit of kindness can go such a long way and today is that much better because of the kindness Aaron showed me.

This is the starbucks you go to if you're trying to get someone to make you a super complicated drink- the absolute correct way and without an attitude, even in the drive through. I always order off menu extra complicated drinks that literally make these poor people have to do math in order to concoct these beverages and they still do it with a smile.

Probably the worst Starbucks that I have ever been to. The 4th of July AM cashier was about as surly as they come. The "flat whites" were awful. The store is cold and uninviting. Really just a hangout for the homeless. The manager needs to check out the several far superior coffee establishments close by like Green Star or Handlebar. Starbucks has fallen far from its original concept. It is really just a fast food chain now. And not a particularly good one at that. So long Starbucks at State and Victoria!

Upon picked up the cup, it's so light.  Opened the cap and only saw the cup was 1/4 full.  I asked is it a Grande, the barrister said yes and seemed like this is not the first time, I was immediately offered to make a new cup.  The next cup was way more. Other than that, the service is excellent.  However, I may get a new title as 'male Karen'.

If no stars was possible that's what they would get....this Starbucks has the most incompetent staff in town.  I consistently get the worst drinks from them as they can not follow directions.  Not once have they gotten my drink order correct.  I have even ordered simple drinks they should not mess up like a green tea latte and they do not add enough of the mixture and I end up with a milk drink essentially.  So incompetent!

The incredibly friendly and attentive staff here bring the rating up to three.  If it were not for them, I'd give it a one, because this is not a particularly enjoyable Starbucks location.  St Pete, doing its best to be a 'big city', has a decent number of homeless folks in the vicinity of this Starbucks, and they are constantly coming in, spreading their belongings out, coughing without covering, etc.  Pinellas already has a Hep A issue, so I'm sorry, but I don't really like homeless people in my chosen eating establishments.  The bathroom has a pointless combo lock on it, and I say pointless because as we all know Starbucks policy is to no longer ask if someone is a paying customer, so they just give the code out to anyone who asks anyway.  That of course has lead to a disgusting bathroom I won't use.  Next up, the non-homeless expensive condo population of St Pete, who are also in close vicinity.  That brings in a combo of either yoga pants and 'service animal' dog as an dress accessory, or male with AirPods talking about their next awe-inspiring business deal way too loudly.  Finally, the place is kept ice cold and music loud, probably to encourage people to not stay too long.
Very good customer service, always friendly. They also give our dog treats sometimes if you bring your pet through the drive thru.
I came In today and the gentlemen at the window was so kind to me . I have a migraine and he even asked me if I wanted a cup Of ice. So genuine and caring! Thank you.

I always drive by this location and forget it's a Starbucks. This location is in an older looking brick building, bit different from their usual look. Most of the time I don't see many people here, if any at all. Decided to swing by since the other location on 116th, closer to I-69, had horrible service. I went through the drive thru. The barista was super friendly and quickly made my drink exactly how I ordered it. Will definitely choose this location over the one closer to I-69.

I know, it's Starbucks. This really is the best one. Everyone is super nice and thoughtful. Even when they are super slammed and people are crabby, they still smile. They love making reccomendations and are enthusiastic for the job. Parking can be challenging and it gets super crowded. It's a great spot to sip your tasty beverage and people watch
This starbucks sucks it never has anything in stock and is so dirty no caramel or strawberrys and ive visited this Starbucks 4 times in the same month not once did they have what i wanted

Most unprofessional business I've been at in years. I was overcharged and my coffee was made wrong. I'm 6months pregnant and had to walk back to the Starbucks in the snow, I get to Starbucks and tell the girl my coffee was made wrong and I ask her to please fix it - she tells me "No" and tells me she made the coffee right the 1st time. I have my receipt and my full coffee in my hand, and I tell her that if she isn't going to fix the coffee then I want a refund, and then she tells me she is going to refuse me service bc I am with a man who is a racist... I was like "I'm here alone" and then she says that she seen me with a racist man when I ordered my coffee the 1st time so she doesn't have to fix it or refund me my money... WTF? I'm so mad I am shaking. I paid $8.17 for a coffee that is undrinkable. I told her I was going to call corporate and asked for her name and she took her name tag off and called the casino security on me. (This Starbucks is inside the casino) The security guards thought she was in the wrong and they were only concerned about me and my baby bc I was crying tears of injustice at this point. So I walked back home with no coffee and no refund. I'm high risk pregnancy and I'm not supposed to have stress like this, bc my blood pressure goes up and I start spotting. I can't believe that an employee would go so far out of their way to bully a pregnant woman - if I end up back in the hospital over this then I'm calling an attorney. That worker had no right to bully a pregnant woman just to get out of having to remake a coffee!  PS. My husband is black AND I have 2 kids who are black and I'm pregnant with a black baby! I'M NOT A RACIST

Not really a Starbucks fan, but stopped in here yesterday cause I accidentally missed the turn for Dunkin Donuts and my girls suggested we stop in here. The prices weren't as high as I remember, but I definitely prefer Dunkin Donuts iced tea over Starbucks.  Four stars though because the service was good and the place was hopping.  Just not my personal choice for coffee or iced tea.

This has got to be one of the worse Starbucks ever. We had to report the order several times and she still got it wrong. The speed from ordering to getting the drink wasn't too slow but the order processing was painfully slow. Double check your order before you leave
Love this starbucks, know me by name and know my drink. Definitely feel like I am at a "barista". Thanks everyone for making me feel so welcome

I go to starbucks very often. I have one near my home on Gandy, but after one visit I always favor going to THIS starbucks. Mainly due to their fantastic costumer service. One star employee that I'd love to point out is Niki. She ALWAYS greets me with a smile when I come to the drive-through window and ALWAYS has the best snack recommendations. She is a pro. I highly recommend coming to this starbucks. Thank you again Niki.

I'm really not sure what all of the complaints here are about. It might be because I purposefully try to avoid Suburban Square during high-traffic times (like the middle of the afternoon on Saturday) but I've never had a bad experience.  True, the line can be very long sometimes but that's to be expected for a coffee shop in a popular shopping hot spot. In my opinion, the service is very quick and efficient. It'd be nice to see more of the employees smile, but they've never messed up any order of mine.  I've come here to work on assignments for graduate school plenty of times. I have always been able to find a seat. Plus, there is free WiFi. What's not to like?
Everyone is very nice and helpful, a good place to study as well!!!!! Good at making drinks too

I go into this Starbucks all the time, because one its close to home. Two its convenient. The employees have always been so nice and sweet. This morning they weren't either. I know at times you can get busy but that does NOT mean you have to be rude to customers. They had forgotten my piece of bread i ordered which was no problem at all, It was the fact that when i had my mom go in and get it they first ignored her didn't ask if she needed help, and then when she had asked for the piece of bread we ordered she got snippy with my mom. There was just no reason for that and I was completely shocked when she told me that because usually they are so nice. AND we had just gone in there yesterday morning.

I placed an order for a iced breakfast tea with 5 pumps of sugar via the mobile app. It took me a few minutes to get to the store and I walked up to see if my order was ready, however I did not see it. I have gotten this drink many times, so I know what cup to look for. I stood waiting for my drink for 10 minutes. I didn't say something immediately because I did see they were busy and perhaps they were brewing tea. I finally decided to ask if maybe I am at the wrong store. I was asked for my name, and I was advised it was there but maybe they made it wrong... and they did.  No one apologized, no one seemed to be worried why I was just standing there. I watched them make my drink sloppy and in a hurry and of course did not do the right amount of sugar pumps. When my drink was ready, it was just handed to me..no words, no apology. I left feeling extremely disappointed.

I have been to a lot of the Starbucks around Reno, Sparks, and Carson City area and I have to say that this location is one of the best. I went in yesterday looking for one of the drinkware cups that I had been wanting and finally found one at this location. The girl who was working the counter was so friendly and I really enjoyed being able to chat while waiting for my drink to be made. I noticed that all the staff seemed happy and had such positive energy. I came back again today to get another cup and the same girl was working and was training a new person too. She was just as helpful and friendly as yesterday and even though she was training she made a point of taking a couple minutes to chat and made me feel happy and welcomed in this store. The location has a lot of parking since it is located in a nice shopping center. The only downfall is this store closes at 6 pm which seems just a little early for a city like Reno but maybe at this location they do not get a lot of business in the evening. I do not mind driving a little out of my way to get such friendly service.

I'm a coffee snob and I make sure to go out of my way for this Starbucks. Not all have the Reserve roasts and the Clover Brewed coffee, so take advantage when you are near here. It takes a bit longer for the clover brewed coffee so be patient. I get the reserve roast clover brewed so to enjoy the best of the best. The customer service here was also outstanding.

By far my favorite Starbucks location in Reno. Consistently fast and friendly service, with very easy access to parking. Many Starbucks parking lots are a nightmare, but not this one because they are in a large shopping center area.   I almost exclusively use my mobile app to order and pay ahead of time. During COVID, the pick up experience between different Starbucks location has been frustratingly inconsistent. Some locations even make you stand in line to with people walking in to order because they keep prepared drinks behind the counter. This location has by are the BEST system! Right when you walk in the door they have an organization system. Your order ticket is placed in a bin with your name on it and when ready, they place the order there was well. Very impressive. Wish more locations would use this system. There are several Starbucks locations closer to my house but I will drive out of my way to visit this one because of the convenience and service.
Nice SB's. Clean with friendly baristas. Ample parking out front. Only drawback is it has no drive-thru. Conveniently located in shopping center with a grocery store.

I've got no complaints at the Starbucks. They always try to be as efficient getting the orders out in a timely manner. This space is tiny! Sometimes it's rather busy on peak times like the mornings. But as I said they work very hard to get it done.

I used the drive thru today and ordered an iced lemon pound cake to go with my coffee. Received a blueberry pastry instead and didn't realize it until I got home. From now on, I should check to make sure what they said is in the bag is actually correct.

Standard S'Bucks product and service, with a friendly staff.  Lot (and different kinds!) of seating that will really look great once COVID stuff is over with. Definitely one of my favorite S'Bucks interiors to date.

this establishment remains very clean and orderly. But the attitude from some employees at this establishment ruined my experience.  I ordered a mocha frappachino and a ham and cheese croissant. And a couple other food items and drinks for my children.. A worker named chole gave me attitude the whole way through as I ordered. At the end of it, she rudely commented are you done yet?. She made unpleased expressions at my children and I overheard her telling her other coworkers about these Mormon moms and their children. I did not hear it very clearly but I know for fact it was not very nice. I just wanted to enjoy the afternoon with my children, but people like her should not work in the food industry.

Two days ago I was came in for an iced coffee, and while nothing was wrong with my drink, there was something terribly wrong with my service. The girl taking my order was not over friendly, and not outright rude, but less enthusiastic than I'd expect. After almost sliding my card before knowing it had a chip reader, she snapped "Hold On." at me, which kind of threw me off because as someone who works in retail, I would never treat a customer with that tone. But this is the real kicker- while my receipt is printing, I notice she begins looking down and what do you know, she pulls her phone out of her apron behind the cash register and STARTS TEXTING WHILE MY RECEIPT HAS ALREADY PRINTED AND I AM STANDING THERE WAITING FOR HER TO HAND IT TO ME. I will also mention that it was around 5pm and there were about 4 people in line behind me, so she stood there texting while we all waited for her service. I was appalled and actually couldn't believe how dumb someone could be to do that, right then, on the job.

I've been to this location 3 times.  Each time the service was slow.  My last trip I got a trenta ice coffee with 2 shots of espresso and it cost me $20. Why?  I paid with a 20 and asked the barista to put it on my starbucks card and then take the drink off of the card.  He spent a lot of time doing this and then said the register wasn't printing receipts.  I've never had a problem before so I told him that was fine and drove off.  The next time I tried to use my card I had a 0 balance.  Where was the $14+ that should have been there?  I called the store, the manager was too busy to speak with me and I left my name and number.  She never bothered to return my call.  That's fine, I won't return to your location, ever.  I emailed starbucks.com and got a portion of what should have been there.  I'm definitely exploring other coffee options now.

Great Starbucks, they make some pretty good secret menu drinks. Google it and they are more than likely to make it for you here. Service and drinks are well made here but there are some flaws to this Starbucks. The seating area has like no windows. I felt like I was sitting a box. The restroom lines are usually long and restrooms are sometimes out of order. Many of the tourist who come visit New Hope use Starbucks as a public restroom.  Verdict - Great location and if you wanna try some whacky secret menu flavors, the people here can try their best at making those drinks. I tried some cotton candy drink and it really tasted like cotton candy. There is also outdoor seating here and Lambertville is right across the bridge. It's a good corner spot to take a break and enjoy some refreshments.
I don't typically review large chains like Starbucks but this one deserves it.  The staff here is beyond friendly. Highly recommend.

If you like to wait for coffee with an attitude, this might be the Starbucks for you. Too bad the usual happy SB attitude is not present here as the location is good. I'll definitely stick to the local establishments in the neighborhood from now on.

This is our second time here in two days. To best describe it layout wise, it's long and narrow. The whole layout of the prep and kitchen area is a lot different from any Starbucks I've been too.   Yesterday, when we came at 10am, they were struggling to stay afloat with the massive drive thru lane and the line inside. We had ordered a bacon Gouda sandwich and were told after ordering that they didn't have any left. We had also ordered a warm plain croissant and when my niece bit into it, it was still frozen in the middle. So she was gifted a chocolate croissant to make up for it.   Today, we ordered 2 bacon Gouda sandwiches, mocha, java chip Frappuccino, strawberry green tea & a passion fruit lemonade. They were quick and efficient and got everything right. They didn't have any passion fruit for the passion fruit lemonade so we opted for strawberry instead.

I remember the days when the people at Starbucks were fun, happy, and cheerful people. Now, they don't say hi, or thank you, or even smile. Not that I'm THAT old but, ah, the good ole' days. Just so I'm clear, the young girl who helped us didn't say hi, or thank you, or not even a smile. Really, really starting to hate the entitled employees found in more and more Starbucks...  And my lemon loaf is hard on one side!!! Ugh! Lol
Alia, Ty, Jared...love you guys fir the warm greetings on my Starbuck's missions.

Absolutely terrible service. It took them 25 minutes to make one drink. No apology. Whoever their manager is, he clearly doesn't understand that if he's going to bark at others for not wearing masks, he needs to learn how to wear one properly himself.
EVEN TIME I come to this Starbucks there's a 30 minute wait. How and why does it take 30 minutes to make a cup of coffee?

Starbuck's definitely generates more crowd in G-Hall especially in the morning when students are half awake. Friendly baristas. Sometimes they get the orders wrong but everyone makes mistakes.  Most students order drinks to go. Getting rid of that unnecessarily large dark wood rectangular table could really alleviate the traffic. Not many people would sit there anyways

The employees here are so nice and go above and beyond the norm of customer service! Kudos to Jacob he definitely deserves a day off with double pay; never fails always greets us at the drive thru with kindness and energy; I wish I lived closer to this location for he is by far the nicest barista  (I visit Starbucks quite a few times per week) He should be considered a trainer. Tysm

Always a Awful negative attitude towards its ordering customers.   I asked for a chestnut praline Latte . Which should come with whip cream and dah praline sprinkles on top of whip. You think I asked for a bag of gold and a Bitcoin. Only to be told in a snobby tone. IF WE HAD IT I WOULD HAVE ADDED IT.   Get over your snobby tude, I don't read minds.

Not the usual friendly service here today. A grumpy barista this Monday morning. Ordered my usual grande peach infused iced tea on the app but it doesn't allow for the fact I usually have them pour it  over a Trenta cup of ice. Somehow the barista was not happy when I asked for Trenta cup of ice. Sorry to spoil your day, but on a hot summer day the ice melts fast and my tea gets  warm. Guess I'll continue to use their app but keep my own cup of ice in the car to pour my tea over.
They make the most delicious venti soy sugar free decaf latte ever.  The people at the register are so nice; the people actually MAKING the drink?   they can be salty.
I received such pleasant service and the interior of this Starbucks is spacious and clean. Definitely a spot I would return to.

Today is my birthday...I have the Starbucks app and have entered my bday in before so I know they have it....I went in and asked how I get my free birthday drink and they said I have to get an email...i checked and I was like ok but I didn't get an email can I just show you my license I promise today is my birthday... no... no free drink for me. Even though many ppl have told me they have showed Starbucks their license and gotten a free drink on their bday....Also worse than that I ordered a drink I order all the time it's the dragon fruit refresher... paid $6 on my birthday and today it tasted like pink sink water.. had zero flavor rad... thanks guys

Every time I come here, this place always messes up my order. Also, I am continuously baffled how long it takes to just pour a black coffee out to make an iced americano. A tall house coffee shouldn't require a 10-minute wait.

Quick service for being so busy. I lose sleep over the fact that every starbs I've been to gives me a different VSC nitro cold brew. Sometimes it's sweet, sometimes its mellow and fantastic, and today it's so bitter my skin melted off my body and fell into a puddle on the floor.   I did not clean it up I just went to my car and slammed my coffee. Choked it down. I'll try again another time.

4 stars for excellence! Minus one only because there isn't a drive thru, however their customer service is great, they only had two people working and yet they made the drinks and prepared/collected our edible items quite rapidly, I was impressed. The area was clean, and they had nice options of cups and coffee for sale. I liked this little Starbucks in the grocery.
Fast friendly service. Clean interior and exterior. Highly recommended. This store is easier to get to and more convenient than I4 store

Got the venti mocha cookie crumble frapp & the FREE nitro shot they are offering for today only! Frapp is quite tasty!! Love all the cookie crummiez all throughout the frapp. Nitro shot, well, eh it was just ok. Asked gal what it was & she couldn't really tell me other than what the description said on the board. But, it has a very strong coffee taste.
Service was absolutely terrible spent. Spent 10 minutes being ignored by a staff before someone offered to take my order.

I swear my drink is different here every single time. The staff seem young and they definitely cut corners when making drinks. My Frappuccino had a layer of chocolate sauce at the bottom of the cup. I asked why and the barista said they pour the ingredients into the cup first (which is fine) prior to blending and that the chocolate pumps must not have blended. This is not the first time this has happened. It's just gross - who wants to slurp up chocolate sauce at the bottom of their drink - I didn't order a sundae at an ice cream parlor!
Currently struggling with staff to the point they do not have decaf because they not brewed it yet. A staff of 10 is not enough to operate the demand

This is a small, high volume store. There's not room to hang out inside but they do a good job of directing traffic and getting out mobile orders quick. Staff is very friendly and will start welcoming you by name after a couple mobile orders. It feels clean and safe during the pandemic. Fellow patrons - we can ALL wait outside until we see our orders come up. My favorite beverage is the cold brew with cinnamon almond milk cold foam.
i ordered a green tea with no water and was intentionally given a green tea completely watered down. My whole day is ruined. Will never come here again

This is the third time I've been to this Starbucks in the last two weeks and this is by far the worst Starbucks I've been too. The customer service here is horrible and I will never come to this one again!

Love this Starbucks location. I stop here 3-4 times a week before class and my mobile ordered coffee is always ready and made exactly as I ordered. Friendly cashiers and super clean. It's nicely located within Villanova's shopping center. No problems with parking either.

Stopped here over the weekend for a pick me up.  The drive thru line was ridiculously long with over 8 cars waiting but rather than wait I took my chances and went inside.  I'm glad I did because there was only 1 person in line and they were already being served.  I ordered a grande iced coffee with skim milk and Splenda.  It was prepared well and tasted exactly how I expected.  The cashier was friendly and made small talk.  When she heard my dog was in the car she offered  me a free Puppacino .  For those who are unfamiliar with this a Puppacino is a cup full of whipped cream for your furry friend.  My girl Bambi was a happy girl!

I don't mind a trip to this SBux at all. The staff is always super friendly and totally fast at making my drinks. They call me by my name which might seem cheesy but it's a nice touch, and makes my overall experience feel more personalized. I love the little sketch of the new Sbux "coming soon" to Forest Park & Grand that they have on the wall (the old Del Taco) which I thought was cute. The cake pops are always fresh, and the coffee is never stale or burnt tasting. It's conveniently located near a bunch of different places I frequent off of Olive Blvd. (mostly doctor offices) so yay for good location and stress-free parking, although the lot is somewhat small, I have never had issues parking there.
This location is awful. You have to double check everything in the drive through. These people could fuck up a wet dream. If you want sbux, find a different location.
Clean store friendly staff.  Cheerfully added cash to my Starbucks card, and had my food order ready before I finished putting cream in my grande.  What's not to like?
Great Starbucks! The baristas are very nice and the place is very modern and clean. Mickayla was so sweet and even brought us our drinks to where we were sitting.

Baristas at this location are great. Customers? Not so much. I felt as if I was amongst a group of zombies Mostly everyone was wearing earbuds and talking so loudly that I had to retreat to my car for some peace and quiet. And some even more thoughtless ones apparently felt no need to cover up while sneezing! There was not a quiet corner to be had inside and I found the lack of consideration to be appalling. My coffee was delish. Too bad I couldn't enjoy the ambiance which was spoiled by a bunch of bratty, inconsiderate millennials!

I don't frequent this location much, anymore, since we moved. But when I do, I rarely have any issues. They're nice and check on things before you order them, to make sure they're still available.   They always roll out the new menu items so I'm able to try all of the yummy stuff Starbucks has to offer.  I gave them 4 stars because they get packed and backed up at times. During the super busy times (before and after work), expect a wait. They try their best to be efficient so be patient. There's always the drive thru but the way it is set up, it wraps around the back of the building and spills into the parking lot, in front, making it somewhat of a nuisance.  I'll be back if I'm in the area.

I am a lover of everything STARBUCKS!  However, the biggest downfall of this location is the fact that EVERYONE GOES THERE!  I used to attempt stopping at this Starbucks in the morning on the way to work and it was a terrible idea.  I would challenge myself to get up earlier so I didn't have to wait in such a long line.... still a fail!  The worst part is the setup of the parking lot.  Instead of having one clear cut line, people approach the line from different directions so it seems like you may be getting the short end of the stick (depending on which side you line up on).  I have also tried to hop out of my car and go inside.... Ironically, it took me longer inside than it would have taken me in the line.    Nothing against the service but because this is at such a busy intersection, it is never a quick stop!

I absolutely adore Justin and Abby at this location. They are so sweet and kind to everyone every single day, and constantly portray an uplifting attitude. I look forward to seeing them each and every time I come to this store.

I just recently went to the new starbucks that opened near McCaffreys and I went through the drive thru. I ordered a drink and a panini and as soon as I got to the window, my drink was ready and my panini was out only a few seconds after that. The panini was cooked perfectly and wasn't cold. I hope they can keep up the fast pace unlike the other starbucks in town where you wait 10 minutes to get a grande drink.   update: at the drive thru i asked if they had any bacon and goudas. they said that they ran out to witch i said oh no what am i going to do because i was sad they didnt have any left and told the man its ok have a nice day. I continued driving and a man popped his head out the window and said "i heard screaming" and i said no i was just sad there were no bacon and goudas left and the guy said "yea thats not out fault" like hello i know its not your fault sir. he didnt smile or laugh and just gave off a bad vibe.

Shannon's genuine smile and happiness turned my whole day around. Her hospitality is a hard find in the service industry during Mardi Gras when we all (myself included) are especially stressed, tired, and unappreciated. You go girl!

11/24/19 - just wanted to give Christina the thumbs up. Very sweet and hard worker. Made sure everyone was taken care of while cleaning up the atea  My experience today was less than wonderful. A young lady took my order then got her food and drink and went on brake. I had to ask another barista for my drink. Then I asked the first one where my sandwich was. She pointed to the barista at the register and said she'll take care of it. The other barista had no clue as to what I ordered. This has never happened here. I quite disappointed.   3/01/19 - Eddie's last day. He has been a plus plus for this location. He will be missed immensely. ;  2/12/19 - So disappointed to hear that our wonderful asst manager Eddie The Arizona Pavilions location will be leaving us.   1/11/19 - VERY disappointed that they didn't have my favorite sandwich today, chicken and artichoke  11/20/18 - So disappointed today. Not one sandwich in the cooler at 11:15 am on   Once again we are here. Our favorite place. Love our Baristas. Jessica, Treigh, Caleigh, Alex and Taylor. If I've forgotten anyone please forgive me. So many great people work at this location. :) And of co
This is the slowest Starbucks ever. There is only one person working and doing everything. I hate this place. Stay away
Best Starbucks I've had in a long time! Service was excellent! Drink was soooo good! Thanks for a great experience!

I was on my way home from work. I was singing 80s music-with gusto-and suddenly had to have a Starbucks spinach and feta wrap. Warm and cozy, savory and tangy, ooey and gooey-it had to be done. So I turned down my "totally awesome" throwback tunes long enough to hit the nearest drive-thru... right there in the 1600 block of West Kennedy. Ohhh, I was not disappointed. The wait was short, the service super friendly and the wrap, so tasty and satisfying. It really is a nice combination-perfect for morning. It's six hours later and I'm still getting energy from it (and still humming "Total Eclipse of the Heart.")   Thanks Starbucks!
The nicest workers of any Starbucks I've ever been to! Always ask how you are at the drive thru and chat with you at the window if there's time. They always make my day!
Today I ordered lemonade. There were strange stains on the lid. And On my last two visits the staff was very rude

You will wait for a very long time. And you may even wait in the road  I have been coming here consistently for months (really not sure why I keep coming back)  By the time you get your mobile order, all the ice will be melted.  Weird overflow lot  Good luck at this location
Great location with outside seating. Staff is friendly and service was decent even though this location gets very busy in the mornings. Enjoy your next cup of coffee at this Starbucks location!

Tried a new breakfast sandwich today. Double Smoked Bacon Cheddar and Egg since they were out of the Spinach Feta flatbread wrap.   It was pretty good. 500 calories vs 290 so it should have been good. The croissant roll was very flaky and soft. The bacon was not near cooked enough for my taste but I like crisp bacon not stretchy.   I would probably get this again when out of the wrap.

Wow, they cant properly fill a cup of coffee.. unless filled to the tiptop as high as possible. The temp of the coffee is also way too high, McDonalds burn your genitalia's hot. Not to mention slow like molasses. Terrible.

It's a Starbucks, it makes and serves Starbucks drinks.  If you've ever been to a Starbucks then you have a really good idea as to what to expect.  And if you haven't been to a Starbucks, then welcome to Earth  It's in the heart of downtown Indy so parking is at a premium and there isn't a drive through.   I was able to order and pickup my drink in a very reasonable amount of time.  They got my order right so I really have nothing to complain about.  I'll totally swing back by here again the next time I'm downtown.

I recently moved to the area and this place has become the favorite part of my work week!  Nova, Shelli, and Derek are always there with a smile and checking in on the latest and greatest with me and the other "regulars". They're seriously the sweetest!  The only thing I'm not a fan of parking/entering. The drive-thru causes a mad house so it's been a challenge to get in and out without some fast and furious type maneuvering.

I absolutely love the baristas at this location (as of 12/26/2018) they have the greatest attitude I've ever experienced --- and on top of that they have your order ready within seconds. I feel like they all deserve a raise (I love their dragonfruit lemonade refresher)  I will fight anyone who gives them grief, a bad attitude, etc! These baristas must be protected from evil
Awful product and service.    12 bucks for this crap.   Will never come back to this place again.  No one even acknowledges us during the long arduous wait.  And the fraps were watery and lame.

Absolute horrible customer service it's like COVID it is not even a thing, there are so many customers without masks inside and outside. Never seen an employee clean a table. The employees are so rude. They give the wrong drink and then when you say "I'm sorry this is incorrect" they act like it's the biggest inconvenience and don't switch it even though I'm paying six dollars for a goddamn shitty cup of coffee. I LOVE Starbucks but this is NOT it. The only reason I went this week is because my at home coffee maker was broken but I'd rather have the horrible dish water Dunkin Donuts serves then waste my time and money on this specific store. My favorite Starbucks was at the university of Miami campus and it was SOOOOO much busier than this Starbucks will EVER be and they had nicer employees who actually cared about giving the CORRECT DRINK TO THE CORRECT PERSON. I have been a Gold Card member since I was 15 in 2013 - I have spent on average $10-15 on coffee a day, I have a severe caffeine addiction and I can't drink the crap they served me without gagging. I'm so appalled by this horrendous establishment. It ruins the name of Starbucks.
Parking can be difficult during morning hours...drive-thru is your best bet!

Updated review. Only three stars today as the pastry display case was pitiful, to say the least. The barista explained to me that they are having great difficulty getting their pastry orders, especially the pumpkin scone order. The Starbucks advertising for the season talks up pumpkin, pumpkin, pumpkin, and yet they have nothing in their stores. Someone has obviously dropped the ball major big-time on this one!   Nice SB's. Clean with friendly baristas. Ample parking out front. Only drawback is it has no drive-thru. Conveniently located in shopping center with a grocery store.

Came here for drinks today (2/23) and it was probably the worst Starbucks I've ever been to. The guy working the drive thru with the tattoos of naked body parts was so blah, rude and like he didn't want to be there.  We ordered an iced passion tango tea lemonade and the order came out wrong so upon telling him it wasn't what we ordered he genuinely seemed annoyed at us for his own mistake, no apologies and huffed and puffed away for the correct drink. After the correct drink comes it is literally watered down so much it tastes like water. Our other order was blonde espresso iced with sweet foam and no exaggeration the sweet foam took up 3/4 of the cup with only 1/4 of coffee. It was all gone within five sips.  Super disappointing experience from the service to the drinks. Would never return to this location and would definitely not recommend.

There's a lot of Starbucks near by, but I think this location is my favorite. The baristas are sweet and never mess up my order. When you walk inside the building, it's spotless and not too crowded around 7pm. It's a good spot to study for an exam.

Coffee is a hit and miss now at first it was pretty good and then a couple weeks couple weeks went by they hire some new guys there and it's just the coffee is cold now like they don't know how to make it in there horrible attitude it's too late these two new guys are horrible one of them was like a stupid mask that looks like a face

I'm not sure anymore about this Starbucks....  I stop by almost every morning before work, but here lately I've waited forever in the drive thru just to be told they just opened and two of their employees called in.  Then there have been times when the drive thru and front door is blocked with chairs on the outside...  I'm ‍ confused, what is going on at this location?  Ending on a good note, they always make my drink awesome!

I have to change my review. We waited in the drive through for 20 minutes. Definitely not coming back to this location anymore. Even the person taking the order was super slow. Who has time for that?! No thanks!

Pissed! I went in today to get a Caramel Apple Spice and a Double Bacon breakfast sandwich. They were out of the sandwich as it seems they always are, and I received the wrong drink!  I got a Apple Crisp Macchiato, and even that had zero Apple flavor! I didn't notice until I got home relaxed and ready to enjoy my breakfast.  It was a total disappointment.
The worst coffee place in all of Tennessee. Even when you place an order on the app for pick up it takes more than 40 minutes. Get your shit together!!

Drive thru-only extends line even after rush hour blocking entrance to park and pick up. Made the mistake in picking up my drink inside option on the app. Should have ordered from in Target instead next time.

This must be a very busy story as it's on the main strip and across from the mall but they have yet to allow indoor seating or open the restrooms to the public.. how are we supposed to wash our hands without the restrooms?  The staff inside was very friendly, there was no wait for my drinks.

Hey do you want the worst Starbucks experience ever?! Then definitely go into the Starbucks at the Doubletree hotel in downtown Nashville. There were 4 employees and about 6 people in line. Not only was it the slowest Starbucks but it was also the least friendly. Hey gals, you're in a customer service role, guess what that means you're basically an actor. So when I asked "Hey how are you this morning?", before ordering my drink...it's you're job to pretend like your day is going well-not roll your eyes and ask what do you want.   Also how many baristas does it take to make a grande toffee nut latte? It takes 3. One to make the drink, one to play therapist as the other slowly makes the drink, and one to quietly say the name on the order when it's done....so quietly the other customer and I had to make sure we were getting the correct drink. I completely understand talking to your co-workers but when there's a line of drinks waiting to be made and food being ordered, leave the personal woes on the back burning and do your job.

Well, I sure hope I'm not letting the secret out, but this Starbuck's is a hidden gem. The looks of it are deceiving as it appears to be a run-of-the-mill strip mall location without a drive-thru, but it does indeed have one--you just have to drive around the side of the building. Because it hides out, the crowds are virtually non-existent. Also, they get the orders right every single time.
Best Starbucks on this  particular road trip across county.  They have excellent customer service for EVERYONE... they  also have free grounds for the garden. !

Friendly, fast service, drinks are as expected. Limited seating that isn't always neat or clean, but remember this Starbucks is in a Target so they have a zillion people with carts and little kids going in and out constantly.  They're very fast and I've never seen a huge or slow-moving line, which is nice because i usually want to get my chai and get the heck out of there.  I'd recommend going before you do your shopping if you want to sit and nurse your drink or snack, as the sitting area really isn't large enough to allow lots of carts and bags unless it's basically empty. One or two small bags are fine, but if you've stocked up for the end of the world, put your stuff in the car and then come back in.
Light ice means less than half. Since it was a mobile order, I couldn't have it remade.

Very poor customer service every time we go there. Went once earlier this week & both of our drinks were wrong. Gave it another shot during a less busy time. Both drinks were still made wrong & the young girl at the window was quite rude.

This place is amazing. Always so friendly they're always busy but working hard, they know me and my drink by name even though I'm just one of many customers that come in there. It is definitely my favorite location in town
I don't know why we come here anymore. This location is always dirty.

I go to this Starbucks when I'm heading to the Eastside of Tucson and I don't know why I still go. The employees are so rude when you are nice to them and no one every wants to make your drink right and it's lazy. I order the one same drink with nothing special just want it the way it looks and made the way you where taught during training and not give out half assed drinks.  . .

I love Starbucks I really do. But this location has gotten my order wrong multiple times. This location is close to my house but now I probably will just go out of my way to another location because my order keeps getting messed up

There are 2 Starbucks less than 0.5 miles from this one skip this and visit another location. Coffee is never as ordered. Ordered coffee with 2 extra shots. Was given a grande cup of steamed milk. This type of screw up happens every visit. Spoke to manager numerous times. She should be fired. Staff is poorly trained and supervised and is far too busy socializing to do their jobs.
Would not recommend this location to anyone!! Very rude and unfriendly staff. The worst Carmel Frappuccino I have ever tasted.

Thank you, Starbucks employees, for continuing to let us get our Starbucks fix during this hard time! This location is now drive-thru only, allowing us all to stay safe and have minimal contact. The people working are friendly and driving here is my big event for the day during this quarantine! I have been coming here for a long time and they are always quick and efficient, even when the drive-thru line looks super long. I hope they can remain open as long as it is safe for them to do so! I love my Starbucks and I love this location!

Meh, way too busy. I go there when I don't want to get out of my car and go into a good coffee shop. I either get really nice baristas or the complete opposite. Good place when your craving a sugary coffee beverage.
My favorite Starbucks location :-) baristas are the best!!!! Service is awesome :-) This is my first time checking in (on Yelp) but my colleagues and I are here at least 2-3 times a week.

There's 2 Starbucks in this mall but I'm pretty sure based on these reviews that this is referring to the one near Macy's.   To start, this Starbucks is just dirty and disorganized. There is barely enough room for the lines to go between the meager seating.    Pros:  Couldn't find a food court and the only nearby alternative was Legal Seafoods so I'm lucky this place served real food like sandwiches and oatmeal  Cons:  The wait is quite a bit, probably half because people are not sure where to line up and what is going on in there. The line actually forms to the left side, which is funny because you enter from the right side of the store and there's already a clump of people there who are actually the people waiting for their drinks.  Prepare to fight for a place to sit, along with a clean spot on the tables.  Confused and slow employees, but to be fair it was the holiday season This one's not really their fault, but probably by proxy, all the patrons there are pissy and grumpy and territorial about the line. Probably mostly because they were in the line for so goddamn long.
Still not sure why a 3 star rating overall. They are still a 5 from me. The drinks are always awesome and we get great service.  Got the Mocha Cookie Crumble Frap this time. So good
Always great service, have came to this Starbucks plenty of times. Overall just a great local Starbucks.

I visited this Starbucks location several times while in Indianapolis and I was very impressed! The atmosphere is always clean and organized. The employees are very friendly and made my coffee perfect every time. The service is quick. There are several seating areas with a good amount of space, including an outdoor seating area as well. I really love this Starbucks location and highly recommend it!

One of my favorite Starbucks because the staff are amazing. Coffee-deprived people seem to forget that these are human beings behind the counter who are also tired, but are working like crazy to make your 5 frappucinos and skinny latte.   I have encountered a day here where we waited in line at the drive thru early morning and it took a bit longer than usual, no complaints from me. This place is always busy in the mornings and has two lanes merging into the drive thru. When I got to the window they comped our two drinks. Another time I asked for a non-dairy drink and they accidentally put a dairy topping on and they remade my drink and comped a new one for me. One last story! My boyfriend forgot his wallet and we realized too late and they said to just pay it forward next time and gave us our drinks for free (of course next time we stopped by we paid for those in back of us)!  Make sure and sign up for Starbucks rewards for a free drink on your birthday and other discounts!  Great starbucks with awesome partners. I have been going to this particular store for about 2 years since I moved to Tampa!
This Starbucks empties trash and cleans the shop.   Staff were super nice and efficient.   Too rare at Starbucks.   Made my morning.  Thanks.

This Starbucks location is the one I probably frequent the most.  I am here almost every Sunday morning in the drive thru.  The employees are friendly and my drinks are always delicious.   Even with my picky order that seems to always change they are patient.   I also keep the corner of the bar company several times a month while I read or crochet.  It is nice to see the frequent visitors and hear there order stated before the customer even says it.  I have seen drinks remade without complaint when it is not quite what the customer wants.  The employees are constantly cleaning during any down time and seem to really care about the store.  I will continue to be here often.

I don't know who is in charge of this place. They want to charge you a premium price and yet always have ti substitute items on your order. It's a simple fix, you know how much you sell a week, so order what you need. Really is no excuse to be out of things. Not that complicated.   Example. Let's give you the smallest straw since we are out. Even though the cup is bigger then the straw. While still charging you full price.

During the pandemic I have been going to the Starbucks at 5970 W. Arizona Pavilions Dr., Tucson, AZ 85743 most Sunday mornings. To avoid the long lines at the drive thru I have been getting up at 4 AM to be the first in line when this store opens a 4:30. The baristas are always so friendly. I always ask for a pup-a-chino for my dog, and a Grande, non-fat, no-foam Latte for my wife. They always top the dog's treat with five calorie Milk Bones! Thank you, Arizona Pavilions Starbucks, for making the pandemic tolerable!

One of the best locations in Indy! The staff is very friendly. I stop in almost every morning and I have a complicated order-- but it's made very consistently from day to day.   The one drawback to this location is there is a large homeless problem in this area of downtown and so almost always there are people asking you for money as you walk in or out. But that's completely out of Starbucks' control.   I pass 3-4 Starbucks on my morning commute but I choose this one because of the friendly staff.

Poor management! I waited 20 minutes for my order placed at 8:30 on a Saturday morning. To be fair, they were slammed with orders, so that wait was not terrible. What moved me to give one star is that apparently they were "all out of " a number of things posted on their menu on the wall. I ordered an Impossible breakfast sandwich, which they were all out of. I asked if I could have just egg and cheese and they said they would have to take the meat off a meat breakfast sandwich. I'm not an uptight vegetarian, so I said OK. They charged me the same and did not offer extra cheese or anything. During the 20 minutes I was waiting for my order, I overheard three people being told they were "all out of" what they wanted. When people ordered food, they were told they would have a long wait, and did they still want it. Most said "no" or ordered something else. I would say they should hire enough workers and have enough product so that customers are not discouraged from getting what they really want.

9 out of 10 times they always get my order wrong. Once or twice I can see, but it's almost everyday. If there were another Starbucks around I would switch over but nothings as close to my 10 step walk from the office.

I don't know if the employees feel this way, but this starbucks seems very organized! Orders come out fast, the drive thru line moves quickly, it's pretty easy to get in and out of parking if youre picking up a mobile order inside or if you just came out from dining in, and the employees have always been helpful and kind

It's nearly always too busy, which I suppose is just a thing to deal with.  When not too busy, it's a lovely place to sit and enjoy a tasty beverage with a friend or with a book.  They have a deck so in the summer, they double their seating capacity so long as the mosquitoes aren't biting.  I've been there several times and have received adequate service.    Yesterday, I wanted their egg-whites, feta, and spinach sandwich.  It was unavailable and so the person apologized and then removed the sample from their display -- this tells me they care about their customers.  The fellow making the drinks needed help, though -- there were 5 people waiting for drinks by the time I joined the line.

This Starbucks is SOOOOOO disorganized. The staff behind the counter talk to each other instead of making drinks and leave you waiting at the pick up counter with your foot tapping. I asked what teas I could choose from and the  girl just said we have green tea that's all I know. I asked for earl gray and I was the only one customer waiting for drink besides the drive thru line which had 2 cars.... needless to say, I waited for an uncomfortable amount of time to the point where other folks walked in, ordered and got their drink before me. I just asked for tea!! They definitely forgot my order. Not cool! I had to grab the girl's attention to ask where my tea was. I said, "do you know how much longer on the earl gray?" And she says, "no" and I wanted to say, "well then who should I ask to get my tea?" But I held my tongue. Then I see her drop a random tea bag into a cup and quickly fill with water and push towards me. I thought they typically sweeten with some kind of syrup but I guess not for me or not today. I seriously think they need to hire and train folks not to be so disorganized even when things are slow. Just tried the tea and wow it is bland and tastes all wrong. I looked at the tag and it says "English breakfast" so I'm definitely not happy with the service but I already drove away bc I was so ready to get out of there. Next time, get the order right and don't forget customers.

I'm a pharma rep and am always ordering a lot of drinks when I go in. Today this location was especially busy, but the baristas were still so kind and helpful despite the fact that I had to place a 17 drink order. They worked quickly to get me all my drinks and make sure everything was right. I appreciated their pleasant demeanor and helpfulness!

This morning I placed my order on the mobile app which is normally very convenient but today I somehow accidentally sent my order to the keystone store which is way out of my way. When I got to the Robb store and realized the mistake, I was already late for work and stressed but the staff there was very nice and helpful, my made my order quickly and even honored the fact that I already paid to the keystone store. Thanks Robb Starbucks!

Very crowded and hopping Starbucks in hipster Newtown area of Bucks. If you are into people watching, especially attractive young yuppies, this is the Best of the Bucks Starbucks to go to. Seems popular with the college age crowd on the weekends and the working crowd during the week. But If you like a quieter environment to drink your latte, this probably would not be the best of the Bucks County Starbucks to go to. Food case could be bigger and the seating area is small and cramped. (Although they do have a smaller outcave to the side for seating with few extra seats. Parking can be difficult especially on the weekends as it's all off street and always crowded. Bathrooms could also be cleaned more often i found. This location does the job I guess if you just want a Starbucks drink and go but other than that, there are better Starbucks in Bucks in my opinion!

This has got to be one of the worse Starbucks ever. We had to repeat the order several times and she still got it wrong. The speed from ordering to getting the drink wasn't too slow but the order processing was painfully slow. Double check your order before you leave because chances are it is wrong. For the most part the workers are friendly but just slow moving. I also noticed that when making their drinks they like to keep all the milk on the counter instead of putting it away to keep cold

Absolutely terrible service. I purposely drive across town to avoid this Starbucks. Baristas are super rude and don't know how to make the drinks right. This place seriously needs a visit from management.
I love this Starbucks, I can get work done on the computer and have a great cup of coffee, the staff is very friendly.

Great service. Always a welcome warming smile from the team, and they really know me now. Just what I want. The atmosphere is cozy (although it gets cold with the door opening and closing with nothing in between). Has the gingerbread latte. Not a fan of it. Smells like an old wood shed, but tastes like a cinnamon dolce latte.
My absolute favorite starbucks. The baristas are always friendly and very personable. I love all of them and they always make the experience a great one.

Store is supposed to be open till 8 pm.  I came in for coffee at 7:25 pm only to find that they stop brewing coffee at 7.  I've never had a Starbucks tell me I couldn't get any coffee before.  They didn't even offer a pour over.  I've been to starbucks at 7:50 pm and was still able to get a coffee but not at this location.  I'm on my way to the one a couple of blocks away.

I generally try to stay away from coffee chains since there are so many local places in town, but Starbucks always has a good tea selection which I appreciate! (I don't drink coffee)  It's Starbucks - I'm sure you're familiar with the prices. Affordable if you get a house coffee or tea, a little pricey if you get something a little more frilly.   For not drinking coffee, I spend a lot of time in coffee shops doing freelance graphic design work or looking for a quiet spot to read. I'm always trying to find the right mix of cozy and quiet, but with large windows for natural light. This Starbucks does the trick. It's small - but since it's off the beaten path a bit, generally not too full. However, it's never empty - if I wanted to be 100% alone, I'd stay home!   The baristas are always friendly and it's clean. There's one long talk table in the middle if you want more of a community atmosphere (or room to spread out) and smaller tables next to the windows. There's also outdoor seating in the normal months. Located across the street from Walmart and Goodyear, it's a great place to work after running errands or while waiting for an oil change.
Never fails how amazing the team is. Thanks for flipping my day from stressful to happy. You guys are the best

What is special about this Starbuck's is that the service is amazing (yay Paul!) and the atmosphere outside on a nice day is very neighborly with folks talking to eachother and the dogs are all out. The drinks and food are standard. Just wanted to say it's a nice place but check out Chestnut Hill Coffee if quality coffee and espresso is what you are seeking.

My review is based solely on the morning crew, between 6:30am-7:30am M-F. I make a pit stop at this Starbucks location several days a week on my way to work. My schedule is pretty routine and I arrive at approximately 6:40am each time, however the traffic flow of the drive thru is completely unpredictable. Sometimes I am in and out in approx 10 minutes. Other times I don't make it out of there until almost 7am and at that point cars are practically wrapped around the parking lot. The slow service is definitely a down side, however I can honestly say they've never gotten my order wrong nor has the flavor ever been compromised. The quality of their products is always consistent.  It's encouraged that people try to order ahead on the Starbucks mobile app, however the items I order on a regular basis show as "sold out" on the app, so I must wait in line if I'm truly craving them enough.

I'm in here a few times a week during the morning rush. The manager is almost always working the floor rather than passively hanging around - and she's pregnant, too! The staff is always very, very friendly (even when the manager is not working). Keep in mind that this is an extremely popular location, so there is ALWAYS a line in the morning. But I've never had to wait an annoyingly long time for a beverage, and the quality is always great.
Under staffed, stopped for a quick cup of coffee 6:45am, lengthily Line, 2 people serving 15!

I honestly feel bad for the employees working.. it's 5am on a Sunday morning and you only have 2 employees behind the counter? That's ridiculous.. They need to give their baristas more support..
