I'm writing this from a line that's 15 deep.. Might have to forget about getting my coffee or miss my flight.   Horrible scheduling on the managers part but the employees that are working this morning are polite and courteous nonetheless so kudos to them.

Perfect customer service! Drive thru window had fun facts printed for you to read while you waited. The wait was super short even though they were busy. Great attention to detail and the customer experience!

Here all this time I thought I was cool . nudge,wink,...... My son told me to order the grande,carmel frappuccino one day, I was hooked. So, on the occasion I get there at 5:25 in the morning, and they are taking orders, I will order my manly drink, and sip with delight and slurp the whip cream with my straw, when I get to the bottom. mmmmm, :)

Since the addition on the space next door, this Starbucks is the largest I've been to outside of NYC. It's so spacious, bright and clean inside. I love the 2 long high communal tables and tons of electric outlets placed under them. The only complaint I have, which is the same for all Starbucks, is the temperature. It's FREEZING in there. It might actually be colder at this location than most. Recently I was there for 2 hrs, even with a cardigan on, I left a popsicle. I'm not kidding.  Why 4 stars and not 5? A couple of employees could be more cheerful and sociable. They actually look like they don't enjoy working, period. The whole time I was never, I never saw either of them smiled once. Maybe I shouldn't be too critical as long as my drinks are done right.  The parking lot is still small since this Starbucks is in a tiny plaza. There's no changing this since there's no room to expand around this plaza. I usually park in the back in case people scratch or hit my car when they try to squeeze into the small spaces.  Looking forward to hanging at this location more. And I'll be bringing a coat. Ha!

Oh how I love this Starbucks, mainly because of where it's located. Today was my first time to this location. It's been long overdue for Starbucks in the neighborhood. It's very clean, nice decor, plenty of seats (inside and out), overall a nice space to enjoy the Riverfront!   The employees were welcoming and friendly. They seemed very knowledgeable of the drinks. However, they were very busy, lines were long and the service was slow. I ordered the usual, Grande Carmel Macchiato, it did not taste as if it was made properly. I told the barista it tasted funny and they gladly made it again. This time it tasted a bit better lol.   Although I did not have the best service today I will go back to give them a second chance. If you live in the area definitely give them a try. Im sure they'll get better with time.

They have flies in the food display case. Come to find out they are supposed to give you the food that's packaged behind it or in the back. After telling them about the bugs, the reaction the staff had was awful. They acted like they didn't care. One associate said it was because of the vents in the case, as if that was normal and that I should expect the bugs. They tried to then serve me food from that case. No thanks! I'll pass.

Typical Starbucks.  Packed, bubbly service with a smile, yummy drinks.  I always wonder if I will find a Starbucks that does not get the same review from me.  I swear they are all cloned and placed throughout the nation.
This Starbucks sucks. I feel bad because the whole staff is clueless. Only 3 people working and none of them know what the hell they are doing!! Bathrooms smell like shit. And slow slow slow service.

I love Starbucks!  I can drink something from Starbucks every day.  This is the first Starbucks I've ever seen with two bathrooms.  Usually, the men and women share.  Inside is clean, nice and neat.  The service is great, they really go above and beyond for the customer at this location.
I owe Starbuck's coffee shops for my undergraduate degree. I just moved to town and I really like this one. I love the post-it board and everyone is very friendly. I will going back for sure!
Prepare to wait for at least 20mins gift your order. It happened twice, not doing it again.

Would be a great place to get your stuff done but this place became the trendiest "study" spot.  Girls would come looking thier best dresse down.  Sort of how they would dress up and get their faces dolled up for the gym.  I loved hanging out there and spreading my books (that I borrowed from the place by the library) all over the tables until the store manager thought it would be a fun idea to blast the AC on so that we wouldn't park for more than 45 minutes.  We were smarter than that, we'd just come in sweats and Uggs.  Problem solved.

On my travels on Canal Street I'd pass the downtown Sheraton Starbuck's sign. Today, I gave it a try.   It is located at the Canal Street entrance. I came at a busy time. They were fast and too busy to be friendly.   I had a choice of plush chairs, tables, and bar chairs overlooking Canal Street with the Marriott. I chose the bar chairs. I got plenty of sunlight, which probably kept my coffee warmer, and did some great people watching.

An OK starbucks if you're retired and have plenty of time on your hands.  As you'll read in other reviews, the place is almost always too busy.  Exacerbating that problem is a pretty slow staff.  Hopping off the freeway and grabbing a cup of coffee generally takes 20 to 30 minutes.   After experiencing this problem often at this location, a casual mention to the server at the drive up window brought on a free beverage.  Next time, same thing.  And since they are chronically slow at the drive through, I guess I can expect free coffee for life.  Except I won't go back.  Sorry guys, there is no such thing as a cup of coffee that is worth $5 and 30 minutes of my time.

Busy with college crowd most nights, however coffee is excellent and the staff friendly. If you are a regular they remember your name and your usual drink most of the time. Would recommend for a coffee and some time with friends.

Very friendly staff and convenient location.  The only reason this is not 5 stars is because they have got my order wrong a few times.  I guess when you go through the drive thru these things can happen.  I will say they have been correct every time I go in to the shop......
Horrible service. Huge line and the workers showed no sense of speed or intensity. Very lackadaisical. Waited 10 minutes for a tea?
Worst service. They are totally confused. No manager in sight. If you are in a hurry dont even think about it.

Suppose to be open at 4:30am.  Was still not open at 5:00am.  Considering this is the only Starbucks past Terminal A security that really stinks.  Starbucks Terminal A you need to get your act together.

Worst Starbucks ever! Bathroom filthy, baristas idiots, waited 15 minutes for coffee with no line. Had gift card, asked cashier balance on card he said he couldn't tell me, but it was on receipt. What? Never going back.
This Starbucks is the slowest I've ever been to, six people behind the "bar" and almost twenty minutes for TEA go toa different Starbucks you'll save yourself headache

This place is in the Bellevue so it's a nice location right on broad street. It's the first store on the right once you enter the building so it's easy to get to when you are in a rush. The seating is off in the back of the store (this isn't an "open floor plan" setup) so there is sense of privacy as you eat. The staff is friendly.   Downside: it can be packed during rush hour. VERY packed. This is the reason I avoid it in the mornings if I can't spare the extra time. Also, the selection is not large. This store has a limited menu and, despite being in a posh location near city hall, frequently runs out of items that are supposedly on the menu  Not a bad place by any stretch of the imagine. It's just that, in comparison to other Starbucks, it just doesn't compare.

Used to be my favorite Starbucks now I try to avoid it. While they used to be friendly and fast and never messed up my  order... Now they're Rude! Always trash talking loudly near the counter, Slow! And over half the time make my drink wrong but since it took at least 5min to get it I don't have time to correct it.  Wish they were as good as they were a year ago it's gone downhill fast. And a side note the new decor sucks!!

My oatmeal this morning wasn't hot, but it was hearty! And the two tall blonde roast coffees (I needed the caffeine, and blonde roast actually has MORE than a lot of their espresso beverages) made up for that!

Extremely friendly staff and they're efficient. When I arrived, the drive through line was considerably long but it moved quickly. The sugar free hot chocolate was better than expected being that it was sugar free.

Severely understaffed or a machine is broke or both....always super slow yet friendly service.  But when you are in a hurry.... or cherish your time... go to a different Starbucks... specifically on Saturdays and Sundays!

I hate to say anything negative about Starbucks but....this location is so in incredibly slow. Always! Ordered a salted caramel mocha frap but they left off the mocha and the extra caramel that I paid for.  I hate coming to this location but it's right up the street.

Every Starbucks is the same bland, boring, sterile yuppie infested, "I'm in a rush, get my latte quick" type place.  Awful Coffee, lousy rude service, terrible sandwiches, overpriced everything.  I avoid it always unless I get a free gift card.

I have never been so disappointed in a Starbucks!  Every location other than this one will make the drink I like even though it is not on the menu.  I then try the place again only to be asked in auto pilot "how are you?" I reply then inquire of the cashier "how are you?". She then disengages and completely ignores me and pays attention to the guest behind me while she's still checking out my transaction.  If you want to be treated like your patronage matters go elsewhere this place blows!  Another thing to mention is that no one smiles that works there.  Do you want to start your day off with the :-( not me!!  And last but not least their parking lot is awful chances are you will get backed into!

Mobile ordered 5 minutes before I arrived.  Five minutes in store and my drinks aren't ready.  Nobody else is in the store waiting for drinks, only drive through.  Not what I would expect when mobile ordering!

Extremely rude staff at the drive through window. They were out of every dessert I asked for and when I asked what they did have the woman raised her voice and shouted at me. She purposefully took her time and made us sit at the window without acknowledging us while she spoke to other people.
Typical Starbucks, nice location and nice enough staff.  Drinks are overpriced so I don't come here often but I love the passion fruit lemonade.

I love Caramel Machiato Starbucks makes and buy it regularly. Today, however, my early visit to this Starbucks location after my favorite caramel machiato and received tasteless, artificially sweetened liquid without a taste of coffee. I came back to let them know that it was not well made and barista told me that they will replace it. They did. For some unfortunate reason whatever she made for me with her poker face on turned out to be even worse. I did not go back again. You cannot sell coffee for 5 dollars and make me some unknown liquid. Wawa has a better coffee compared to the ones I got today from this Starbucks location. Disappointed...
Their recipe for large peach green tea lemonade is apparently 1/4 liquid and 3/4 ice.  I finished my large tea in a few sips.  Not worth $4.19
I love this Starbucks! The employees are so quick and are always smiling.  My son and I frequent this location often and its always a pleasant visit.

I am bias because this is my "closest to home" shop where they know my name often times & my mobile order is always ready when I go pick it up.  I know they are a bit slower when it comes to the drive-thru (which is why I mobile order) but my order is right 99% of the time and that 1% they'll fix it.  Pam talks to me about my dogs (who LOVE getting pup cups here) and every single worker "good morning"'s me.  Kind and great service!  Highly recommend the nitro cold brew with sweet cream as well - it is the perfect boost in the morning!

We buy a house over here off central and are thrilled because Starbucks is 24 hours and I literally use you 24 hours and now you change that????? Done! Will no longer be a a patron of this Starbucks....
Driver thru employees were very pleasant and patient yet fast and efficient even with a full line of cars .

Since when does a Frappuccino come with ZERO shots of espresso, forcing you to pay almost $3 to ADD coffee to your coffee drink?! Two drinks took 10 minutes to make and one of them was just a drip coffee that I had to ASK FOR AFTER PAYING & GETTING MY BAR DRINK. Not only that, but the "service" is straight up SURLY. While I was there (all 20+ minutes with only 2 people in front of me) I saw 2 women trying to collect their pre-order have to go through the whole rigamarole of re-ordering because their orders were nowhere to be found.  *Edited for clarification; While I know that any Starbucks, at any time of the day, is fairly busy, this was at around noon to 1 in the afternoon, not a peak time. Very few cars in the drive thru & fewer customers inside the store. And I managed a rival coffee shop (also a drive thru store) for 5 years. If our service was ever this shoddy, or our service times were this long, EVERYONE got reprimanded. My visit was an extremely poor, but accurate (if my past visits are taken into account) representation of how Starbucks treats their guests.

Good place to study or hang out, with lots of tables. But even so, it can get very crowded and near impossible to find a seat during the school year, especially on the weekends. Dress in layers as the temperature can get quite cold if the A/C is on high.

Good location, close to my mechanic Buck's Automotive, Walmart plaza, Battery Plus, etc. Very friendly staff, almost always upbeat, and great customers -that's what really counts when looking for good conversations.
Rude and inefficient staff. Worst Starbucks I've ever been to.  Really exceptionally bad for a Starbucks.

Former SS and barista trainer here. I ordered an iced coffee w/milk and vanilla this morning. When I was handed my drink, it was an iced coffee with a splash of cream on top. I took it, but informed the woman working DT that it wasn't made to beverage standards. The only response I got was an eye roll and her turning her back to me while calling out that she needed a break. For a new drive thru, I would expect the team to be much more attentive to customer service than this.
Nice employees; quick service! My drink was delicious.  The only drawback is that this location does not accept rewards.

Worst Starbucks ever. I've never in my life seen Four women work so slow. Literally 40 min to just order. People were leaving because their flights were boarding after standing in line for over half an hour. The manager should be fired and the Starbucks should be closed. Find a new coffee shop.

Rude rude rude. Not what Starbucks represents.  You must not step across The Line before they open, not even to look at the bakery case. Maybe it was just a bad day. Oh yeah, this was 10 min after their opening time according to the website and they still weren't set up.

What's going on with this Starbucks? I've been coming here every day for 2 years and the service just keeps getting worse and worse!! There used to be super nice baristas here, now they're slow and not very helpful at all.

What is all this about dinner here?  Just looks like a regular Starbucks to me.  To be fair, I've only been through the drive through... this place is on the way out of town heading Northeast.    But the service is fast and it's standard 'bucks.      Take that for what it's worth.
I recently had the pleasure of visiting this Starbucks and Amanda was an absolute gem. The team was cheerful, the wait was short, and the coffee was great!
Parking, outdoor seating, drive-thru, WiFi, great coffee-what more could you ask for?

The manager needs to learn how to manage. Employees seem to think playing around and yelling and laughing as loud as they can is acceptable, very childish and unprofessional. The manager needs to understand they are employees not friends. Customers have to yell to be heard over the music and employees. Takes forever to get your order. Very poor service. Like all Starbucks WAY WAY overpriced.

I have been here a few times and I don't know if I've just been unlucky the times that I have come, but the vibe at this Starbucks is definitely different from the other Starbucks in the area. The baristas always seem unhappy and don't greet you with a smile. The young man behind the counter is unfamiliar with how to input orders into the kiosk and when he attempted to ask the other barista about how to input a snickerdoodle hot chocolate into the system, the barista Catie just ignored him. Another time I've been here, the baristas were bantering back and forth with each other trying to pass off the task of who would make the frapuccino I ordered for my kids, another time I asked to have a grande drink split into two small cups and the cashier told me they don't do that - where at other Starbucks they have no issues doing so. Customer service is key and this Starbucks seems to lack it. Food and drinks taste the same as other Starbucks, I just wish they had better customer service and smiled more.

Pains me to give such a low rating, but customer service was poor, product average, and as a passionate coffee drinker, it was almost pathetic to even stay for any length of time. Wifi was spotty, and to top it off, an employee began dusting right over my head as I was drinking my coffee. I left. Baristas were loud and not very friendly.   Starbucks: you can do much better. I know you can.   Fail.

This is one of the bigger Starbucks in the SB area, and also the one I frequent the most due to its location in the Camino Real Marketplace. It's a convenient coffee stop if you're out shopping in the area (Costco, Home Depot, Ross, Sports Authority, Ulta, etc) or if you're waiting for the showing of a movie at the theater a couple doors down. This place is quite popular so be prepared for a longer wait (than other Starbucks I've visited) for your drink...it's almost always crowded (a ton of college students studying), there's usually a line, and most seating will be occupied. They renovated a couple years ago so the place has newer furnishings and a slight rearrangement of the layout, but overall it seems pretty much the same as when it first opened. Their super friendly staff and prime location keeps bringing me back, even if it's not the coziest of places and doesn't offer super fast service.

I walk to this location, which is great, because there really isn't much of a parking lot.  I tend to visit with a regular and he gets much better service than I get. The few times a year that I crave a frappuccino, this place could do the job, but I've been to much better locations.  Any mall I've been to or rest stop, blows this place away in service.

I really like this starbucks, the building is super cute, and the staff is very friendly. I've read some of the reviews and I cannot agree with them. I've never had a problem here and I frequent this starbucks because it is close to where I work. The coffee counter is downstairs but upstairs they have a separate floor with tables and sofas so it's great to sit, have a cup of coffee and read.   My negative about this starbucks is that the parking lot tends to be full and its difficult to get out of it depending on what direction your heading since its a highly traveled road.  I used to be a little bit against starbucks, but the coffee is decent and there aren't many places that serve coffee that is better in my area. If I lived in the city I wouldn't visit starbucks, but being from the burbs, its good enough!
Seriously the SLOWEST Starbucks ever! There's always a ton of people working yet it takes 15 min to get an americano. Don't even get me started on the drive thru.

If I were rating this solely based on the amazing individuals that work at this location then believe me, you'd see the biggest 5 stars possible. These employees get hounded daily by huge lines of obnoxious students. I've seen them be berated, I've seen them when they're flustered, and I've seen the way a lot of jerks treat them. I absolutely love every employee here and hope that everyone else appreciates them.   Since this location is at the UA, and on campus right by the library, it gets slammed throughout the day. There are times where there are lines that go 20 people out the door and the employees here continue to bust butt to get drink orders out. Unfortunately, the UA has set them up for failure by also having an advanced ordering app (not the regular Starbucks app--they don't do that at this location) where while there are 30 people in line, there are then 30 college students in classes also ordering drinks. So at certain times of the day the "in person" customers are getting angry because "tap and go" customers are getting drinks before the "in person" orders. But imagine how hard it is for these few employees to keep up with the 30 people in line and the 30+ mobile orders happening at the same time. Really, UA has set them up for failure because people get cranky and take it out on these poor baristas who are doing amazing work. Unfortunately, I have to deduct a star for the long, long wait times and for the fact that I think its a horrible business practice to have all of these different ordering systems slamming people. But again, still 5 stars for the employees!   They don't let you redeem your stars for a free beverage here. That's frustrating. You can, however, still get happy hour deals and other perks here. I mainly earn my stars here and then cash them out at "regular" locations.   What I --really-- hate about this location is how they nickle and dime you. I switch out the milk for soy or coconut in all of my beverages. The upcharge for this is fine, as I appreciate them offering soy, and I realize that costs them a bit more to make. I have zero issues with this. But what irks me is I used to order drinks with "no ice" and I'd have no issues. The ice makes things too cold for me to drink, but sometimes I want something that isn't hot either. "No ice" is a great way to get something closer to room temp. Again, I've ordered like this several times with no issues (and I've NEVER had an issue at any other location ever). But suddenly I'm told there is a 50 cent upcharge here for "no ice". They're actually trying to charge you to take ice out of your drink. I've just saved you some water, some ice, the power it took to make the ice, etc., and the coffee I'm purchasing is around $6-7 and I usually purchase here 3x a week, 2x a day (that's 6 drinks a week, a loyal customer). And you really, really want to charge someone to take ice out of a drink?!  This "new rule" comes and goes. Sometimes they have it. Other times they don't. But basically, they need to get over all of their extra charges that don't exist anywhere else. Seriously, this doesn't exist at any other "regular" or "franchise" location I've ever been to. At some point, you need to realize you're making plenty of money and you don't need to charge to remove ice. After all, I don't get a discount if I take whipped cream off of my drink...  Employees: 5 stars Long waits and weird mysterious extra charges: 3 stars Greed of this location: no stars
This location randomly closed for renovations.  And then just as randomly, it opened without their Clover machine.  Which was the whole point.  Off to Chestnut Hill Coffee Co.

Tonight was the first time I visited this newer Starbucks. I went through the drive through and the barista was very friendly, however I didn't get napkins when I got my drink. Is it Starbucks' policy to NOT GIVE napkins? Anyways, the drink was delish but getting out of the drive through is tricky, this stop mall is built on like a mini-hill and my corolla didn't like the steep decline to the street.   If anyone at Starbucks is reading this...please for your drive through people napkins!!

I have always walked into this location around 5:15-530am just like everyone else this morning shouldn't have been any different but it was. The two female employees were complaining about how I was able to come in early it looked like they had a ways to go before they could open. They were really rude and if they are working I will refuse to get coffee from them. Why is everyone else able to open the store early and have a few customers but they are not ?

Affectionately known as Charbucks or Fourbucks - we have adopted the slogan of:  "Friends don't let friends drink starbucks."  Save money by brewing at home with Peregrine Coffee (the BEST) or go to La Colombe or Caffeination.
Great staff .. It's what  you expect from Starbucks.  I'm somewhat of a regular due to proximity to home but more often than not a positive experience.

It is a nice Starbucks. The inside has some small more intimate tables as well as a big table if you want to study with a group of people. The service is friendly and the location is nice and safe. It is also open til 8 so it's not 24 hours but at least it's open most of the night.

This Starbucks doesn't have a drive though which already makes it an inconvenient stop but on top of that there are no parking spots for Starbucks customers. You have to pay for parking just to run in and pick up your coffee.

Meh.  At best it's disappointing. At worst, it will make you never want to visit a Starbucks again.  Sadly this is on the way from my gym to work.  If you are lucky, they will greet you with words.  Most days, expect to be a disturbance to the rude and apathetic staff.  I can't tell you how many emails I've written to starbucks about this location.  Most recently they threw a bag of trash from the front OVER the coffee counter because the associate was too lazy to carry it around.    Poorly managed obviously, rude and apathetic staff.  Spare yourself the agony.

Always been pretty happy with this Starbucks - parking choices can be less than optimal during peak hours and drive thru often wraps around the building but that is pretty typical for most Starbucks in the Tampa Bay Area. LOTS of seating options (when dining area isn't closed due to Covid) - this location probably has one of the largest dining areas I've ever seen at a Starbucks!  Only drawback is when they keep mobile order beverages out of reach/unattainable, as that largely defeats the purpose of ordering ahead/efficiently.  Also, recommend ordering via mobile app and running inside to grab it versus waiting in the drive thru. :)

Conveniently located next to and inside (you walk right into) the bookstore. how awesome is that to have a book store and a large Starbucks with ample seating? Well, I think it is :)  This Starbucks has its usual long lines but the wait time was short for us. Overall, it's a great Starbucks with friendly staff and great coffee. Enjoy

I go to this location quite often because it's convenient and they always get my order right. I always get a venti white chocolate mocha with coconut milk no whip cream no foam long shot. I have gone to a couple of the other locations in both directions and it's always been hit and miss. Here it's is has been consistent so even if I am going the opposite direction I will go here if I need a quick caffeine. The staff is always friendly. On one day in particular I went in to get a gift card to do a giveaway. The barista helped me purchase the card and put it on my app so that way people weren't using my main card. It was very sweet.
This starbucks is close to work. They seem to be pretty fast and rarely mess up my order. They don't like to give napkins in the drive through.

Good place to pop in for a quick minute while you're at the mall. It's very clean and spacious. I only took one star off because it doesn't have the deeply comfy chairs that some places have. The service is really fast.
Girls were cold and the coffee was hot.  Sure would like it the other way but hey you can't be too picky these days when it comes to six dollar coffee.

Love the staff. The service is always great. Never had a bad drink from there. But for the busiest Starbucks in the city, they always have the least amount of staff working. 5 staff when there is 50 people in line. And this is how it always is. This store needs to hire more staff.

In terms of the drinks, food, etc., my experience has always been fine, The biggest issue with this place is the size. Parking is an absolute nightmare, the inside is small, and each time I have been in, the baristas seem to mess up (thankfully never on my order). They always seem a bit flustered (probably due to the crowd, but hey, you work at starbucks, its expected), and while they are always friendly, I generally try to avoid this location because it is more hectic and stressful than it is worth.

Horrible service- my husband was ignored by the employee standing at the register/ another employee finally came over to take his order. Waited over 5 minutes for an iced coffee/ no additions. I took a drink once we had started in our way - horrible - it looked like coffee/---tastes like spit -not sure what it is - tossed it  .

At least three times a week they screw up my simple order. A Venti iced coffee sweetened with light ice is what I ordered. What part of that said put cream in it? This is been repetitive enough that I will no longer go to the store.

Great place to stop by while going in or out of Whole Foods, which is just a few doors down!  They had Dark Roast still brewing when I showed up at 4pm today, which is great, since I abhor the Pike Place.  Of course they're always willing to do a pour-over of any coffee you want, but it's always nice to have good coffee already made for you :).
I frequent this Starbucks location in the early morning hours and they always have consistent flavor. The staff is friendly- and HELLO!! Drive thru!! Love it.

The worst Starbucks I've been in. Not sure if I just caught them on a bad day or not. I hope so. I came in around 4:25 because I need the pickup on a frapaccino can give.  No one was overtly rude or anything they just didn't seem to care.   There were three young ladies behind the counter but only one was making drinks. And even the lady making drinks seemed like she could use some help the other two were too busy: one doing inventory? And the other counting money in the tip jar. Even that wasn't too bad but then another came in. She never went behind the counter but she talked like she was an off duty employee. Unfortunately she kept engaging the sole girl making drinks.  My advice is to avoid this place unless you are very patient and have plenty of time to kill.

Yes I drink was awesome today! Great staff and I am truly grateful for them.They can occasionally have some long lines just like any other Starbucks, But they are great at moving that line along and being friendly while doing so.

I have been a Starbucks customer for years and this Starbucks in centerton square is closest to my work so I go there 3 times a week at a minimum. I have been increasingly disgruntled with this location. The layout stinks, the staff are fast but they seemed to have lost their friendlier baristas, and I hate that I have to ask for milk for my coffee because they only put out half in half...I know that's corporate but when I go to the Starbucks on main street they are so much cooler about it. Going to avoid the centerton location as much as possible from now on.

Here I'm in this city again and their is some sort of festival in china town. All the surrounding roads are blocked and the traffic is very backed up. Then I see the happiest sight of the day, Starbucks! Even better a nice parking space right in the front! Unfortunately they were about to close so there was no sweets left. I was able to get my hot tea though. The lady at the counter who looked very tired apologized that there were none! I told her I should not be eating more sweets any way!  It's a nice clean Starbucks with lots of seating and available wifi. Unlike most other Starbucks I've gone to, you didn't need a code or a key to use the bathrooms. It had two stales in the ladies room and was very clean. I just wish they didn't close so early!

Stopped in to Starbucks before we head out to this building for work. No coffee for me today, went with my favorite Green Tea Lemonade for the Win!  When we arrived in the parking lot, apparently we were at the rear entrance, so it's a little confusing set up from the outside. However when you get inside, you witness this amazing setup. Like probably one of the best designed setups that I've been to.  They have this area that looks to have been setup for meetings and getting work done, it's like it's own room. The work benches have electrical outlets and USB ports on them! That is a great setup. My colleagues and I were amazed by this as we hadn't seen any like that one.  It had a great feel to the place, the barista was nice and my beverage is delicious as usual! Man I love Starbucks!
Been here twice and I am impressed that even with a long ass line the whole order a drink and pick it up process is fast and smooth.  Works for me.  Plus almond milk Cappuccino?  Holler!
Very spacious, quiet and friendly staff. Good place to work in the middle of the day on a Thursday.

Well I was just here around 11 on a week day watched 6 people go threw line before me some with multiple drink cashier took in well over 60$ right in front of me I came to the register and ordered my drink was 6.77 I had 6 or 100$ cashier says well I can give you change for that and but you can get a smaller drink ? Well no not what I want or ordered how can such a large business not have change mid day for 100 when I just watched them uptake almost the sum of my change ??
Everytime I try to come here there is no parking so I just keep driving to get my coffee somewhere else.  I wish they would relocate to somewhere else nearby with more parking.

On 07/22/15 around 5:00 pm I was there. Not busy, my latte took forever, the 2 baristas didn't bother making eye contact, vanilla shaker was empty, a fly and a fruit fly landed on my table and table was wobbly! Is this A Starbucks?

The drive-thru is my favorite thing about this Starbucks.  Is there anything better than a cup of Starbucks to get you through an early morning?  I think not!  The drive-thru makes it so easy to hop off 275 and grab my drink without losing too much time, which is huge since I'm always running late!  The parking lot is always packed, but there's a side street nearby for overflow parking.  There isn't a ton of seating inside, but there is a nice big patio area outside.  The staff is always friendly, and of course, the drinks are wonderful!
Slooooooow service. Line out the door and they acted like no one was in line. One register for all the customers was not sufficient.

Okay location compared to Newtown and other locations. Baristas are rude, and that is my only complaint about this Starbucks. Drinks are good. If you don't care about service, you shouldn't have a problem going here.

I always have a great experience here! Now they have an amazing barista by the name of Daniel!!! He is outgoing, kind, respectful, and a really great worker. I enjoy just hearing his friendly voice:)))) Sashah

S-L-O-W-W-W!  Drive up (while still at a turtle's pace) gets more attention than those who brave actually walking in.  And while I'm here, and speaking of walking in,the front door is ridiculously hard to open. While they have a "handicap" parking space in front I doubt if anyone needing to park there could open the door without assistance. Seems like Starbucks could afford to install an auto door device.
The worst Starbucks experience of my life.  The employees were talking amongst each other as opposed to working.  Took 30 mins for me to get my Grande iced coffee.  That is unacceptable.
Too impersonal of a feeling for me. Good if you are cruising through shopping area I guess. Nothing special. Used to have later hours, but once that stopped, I don't go back.

I felt like there wasn't the usual variety here today. I WILL say that in lieu of Cold Brew they offered to make my Cascara Cold Foam w/Regular Iced Coffee which I appreciated. I also had traditional oatmeal, which was delicious as usual.   Overall OK experience but a few negatives: No cold brew. Limited food options. The staff seemed more concerned with when they were scheduling their breaks than conversing with guests BUT the line was moving quickly.
Service is too slow. I often get stuck in the drive through and it seems like forever before I get my drink. Employees are usually nice and friendly, as long as you don't have a problem.

Relocating to this new spot is the best thing they could have done. Not only are all the costumers happy, but I'm sure attracting more. The parking and drive-thru have improved drastically. I feel like the staff has also improved. They were always polite, but now they seem genuinely happy.  The drive-thru line moves quickly, and there is always room to study.
Most unprofessional Starbucks I've ever been too. The 5 girls in the back were listening to their own music and not helping people.

Parking was easy (street), and the place was large and airy. What you'd expect from a newer Starbucks. We entered and immediately felt the positive energy from the staff. Friendliest most welcoming Starbucks staff I've ever come across.  Thank you for making our Sunday morning brighter.

Drinks are fine, but oh my gosh, the service was so in need of good management. I went in on Sunday morning, there were at least ten people in line, several people waiting for their drinks, and only three people working the entire place. When I got to the register, I said "hi"--just got a glare from the guy taking the order. And unfortunately, not even a thank you after placing my order. Waited for 15 minutes to get an Americano.

I love starbucks ice coffee but this place is SO PACKED and SO SLOW and so tiny. And parking is hard outside.  I just avoid this place. There are so many wonderful starbucks nearby and this place is super annoying and I have been here many many times before realizing that it's the same every single time!!

First time to this location, i never have a problem at Starbucks. Stopped at this one due to location and needed to get a mug for a gift. I was looking at the sale items, and noticed one of the travel mugs lids were broken and so I lifted it to take the defected item to the register so no one would touch it or something and the top fell slid off and (it was glass) shattered more on the ground. I picked up the pieces and took it to the barista at the register and she was about to charge me for it. Her name was Rita. I informed her that I wasn't trying to buy the defected/broken item and she told me "but you just broke it" I explained what Had happened and that I was simply bringing it to them so it wouldn't get touched. She rolled her eyes and proceeded to ring up my items and take my order. I purchased a few things, so I asked for a bag and she ignored me. I paid and was waiting for a bag and she still hadn't given one, so I asked again "may I have a bag for my items, please? If you don't have one that's ok." She rolled her eyes and grabbed a bag in a snatching motion and gave it to me to bag my items myself. I told her thank you and to have a happy new year and packaged my items at the pickup area so I could get out of her way so she could help other customers since she clearly didn't want to help me. I hope she will read this and it will help her change her attitude towards the guests that come to her location. I love Starbucks but she was the most unhappy barista I have ever met. Cashier/barista Rita I really hope your holiday season gets better and you can find happiness in your day.

This Starbucks was clean, and service was friendly and quick!  The very big downside to this location is the parking lot. There is a double-sided, one-way lane that hooks to the left into the oncoming lane of a two-way road. The space is very tight, and if the two cars parked at the end by the store aren't parked far enough into their spots, you may have to do a bit of neck-craning to make sure you're going to make it out without a scratch.  Otherwise, I like this SB and would go again.
These girls will make laugh. Very nice and friendly people here.
Hanging out with my friend drinking my pumpkin spice latte. So delicious!  Love this time of the year!

A colleague and I decided on this particular Starbucks to meet for morning coffee. Starbucks are generally pretty much the same, minus the amount of seating each one has.  This particular Starbucks has a pretty good setup. When you walk in there are several easy chairs a few tables and then a couple of high top type tables towards the back. Unfortunately this place is pretty popular, and based on the clientele, it seems like that is primarily due to this location being very close to high school.  We wanted to sit and chat and were lucky enough to find an open table. The staff seemed friendly enough, filled the orders with a smile, and the coffee we chose was made to order and tasty.    I personally prefer local mom and pop type coffee shops. Unfortunately in Tampa there aren't enough of them around so Starbucks is a solid back up if you want to get a good cup of coffee, and have a place to sit and chat. This particular Starbucks is no exception.

I stopped by this location today for a quick cold brew.  While they were out of a couple of things, their customer service was great.  The customer in front of me was rather rude to the staff.  Becca handled herself with grace and came up with a good solution for the customer in front of me.  Great job!

This Starbucks looks/feels like you are at an airport.  Some Starbucks are like this now: long and narrow with very sleek  sandwich/drink displays; perfect ones to just quickly pick up your coffee (etc..) and go.  But,  they also happen to have the NICEST employees here, especially Natalie.  She is so genuinely personable and engaging, while also being efficient and quick with your order. Super special quality.  There are also  two big tables here perfectly good for study, meetings, and sharing.  Little tables too. Easy outlets.  Outside seating.  Seems to have its regulars here too, so that sometimes makes it feel like a warm community cafe despite the cool atmosphere.

Sometimes there is a long wait but honestly the convenience of this location is why it's my favorite. The baristas always seem super friendly and I've never really had any complaints other than yesterday, when I waited in front of the menu to order in the drive thru and after like three mins of being parked there, a barista said "you've already ordered right, so you can pull forward." But I hadn't ordered.    They do from time to time seem a little unorganized but the location is perfect and my drinks never taste strange or messed up so I keep coming back.

This Starbucks serves a purpose larger than keeping me well caffeinated... And providing me exercise opportunities (it's within walking distance).  But I will discuss that after talking about the reasons people read reviews.  It's a Starbucks. If you don't like Starbucks you won't like this one. I am not going to ding stars for what is and will always be a personal preference (for taste reasons or otherwise).   This Starbucks has a drive through. It is also a bit larger yet smaller feeling. By that I mean the baristas have a lot more ground to cover, I do NOT mean there is plenty of room for your massive stroller. Sorry but there just isn't. The pathway from ordering to seating corral (there is seating along the way) is just not that accommodating due to displays and that aforementioned seating along the way  The associates here will remember your name. I find that fifty fifty with Starbucks' employees. They also won't complain if you say the size before iced (one of the baristas at a St Louis Starbucks told me I was wasting her time by ordering in the incorrect order - yikes).  However, they are a bit slower. You can be standing at the register for awhile... There tends to be longer lines. They never have spoons ready (they then have to dig through boxes). But I will accept that and be cool with that as the people are pleasant... And I have learned that rarer amongst drive thru Starbucks places.  I have never used the drive thru, so can't comment on that.  Just the people and the service.  Oh and that thing Starbucks brings to an area that isn't caffeination? It brings some weird sense that an area is "okay." I don't prescribe to that, but rising home values after it being built show people do.  I think people say to themselves "it must be a nice(ish) area is Starbucks would build there"   The sociologist in me says that's gentrification in its finest.
Fabulous service. The lines tend to be lengthy between classes but the staff is always friendly and drinks are made with care. A great addition to Washington University's campus!

It's not my favorite Starbucks, but it's my neighborhood location. The staff is friendly, the bathrooms and seating area is decently clean. It's got a great atmosphere, but its a little too cozy for comfort. They seem to be more focused on the drive-thru than the walk-in customers, but I can't blame them too much. Over all, for a quick stop or a in dire need of a Starbucks hit, I would recommend this location.
We were In the neighborhood & stopped by for coffee.  We ended up getting a mocha frappe & vanilla bean frappe they were both excellent!!Customer service was great also.
Friendly workers, always ready with a suggestion. I saw one worker spend 20 minutes showing customers cups on the shelves. The tea and coffee were delicious as was the cookie. Awesome!

I want to preface this review by saying that we live in the building and want nothing more than this Starbucks location to be successful. However, service is constantly slow and the workers rude. Today marks the fifth time in a row that my order was wrong and the barista on duty's reaction was as if she could care less. The store recently fired the one bright spot that they had, Key, who was let go for closing up early when she was left alone to close (major safety issue and happened way too often). The store is not clean and hasn't gone through the standard updates that all Starbucks stores get on the regular. The manager is never in and it makes me wonder if this store isn't getting proper attention because it has been written off and plans to close. I really hope the manager can start doing their job and get this place on track. The Gulch is a prime location and this store certainly does not fit the bill.

Usually will always get the same thing at Starbucks - coffee with a splash of soy milk. Starbucks policy states that soy is extra for 4oz or more (ie. A latte which is mostly milk, and makes sense) but I am consistently charged extra for the small amount they put in a black coffee at this location... I asked and they said it's company policy (which is wrong). A shame because this is the closest location to me, but I'll go to some of the others on my way in to work from now on.

Yo this place gave me wayyyy to much coffee and too little sweg. I hate that. Sweg is the starting point of all organic life. Whenever I run out of sweg this place never tends to my needs of my ridiculous amounts of sweg that I procure. 2 swegs out of 5

I love this Starbucks at Tampa Premium Outlets! They are so cheery and nice. They are open a lot longer than the usual outlet hours from 7:30AM to 10PM.   This last visit I got their seasonal Caramel Brûlée latte which whip cream! It's so good, & it's just like a dessert to me.  They have a nice outside patio too for some perfect Fall time weather which we have right now.

For the amount of times I repeated my order to the gentleman in the drive threw I don't no how It still got messed it up.I get up to the window he's to busy gossiping with coworker to even acknowledge me and then he hands me a tall cup with nothing but liquid Bc the ice melted already, and mind you I asked for a Venti with ice, So i asked for ice n he rolls his eyes at me and storms away to get it. For the money I spend weekly at Starbucks this service was awful. I'm deff going to go out of my way to find a different one in the area very unhappy with this location as of late.

Out of all the Starbucks I go to this one always has cold coffee in the afternoon so if you're looking for coffee I wouldn't go to Starbucks that's kind of silly because I thought that's what they were known for coffee
We came in to get some fraps as it was a hot day in Indy. The place was clean. It appeared new location built. It's a pretty big size. I would come back again.
The staff are all efficient and friendly. They know my drink and always ask about me and my family. Jocelyn is an absolute sweetheart!!
Don't hang out here long if you need to use the bathroom.  Only 1 at a time for the men.  Awful.
I love this Starbucks!  They are so friendly and prompt with their service.  Great place to meet friends and get work done.

I watched someone maneuver their SUV in the most artful way to not hit someone in the parking lot. Their bumper seemed to be about an inch away from the side of another car that was parked in front of the store. Mind you, this Starbucks for some reason allows people to park in spaces that aren't actually spaces, making the already-tiny lot a complete and utter nightmare. We somehow got a spot in the 15-minute parking area and I was glad that the people in back of us finally moved. It was insane. That is my main complaint. I'm not sure what I would have done without an actual parking spot. Other than that... my frap was tasty and the staff seemed nice.
Great location and service! Barista was friendly, helpful, and made me a great cup of latte.

Mobile ordered at 3:54 and received my tall iced coffee at 4:30. Then the barista with pinkish hair acted as if she could care less. When I told her I had chips with my order as well.  A simple sorry for your wait or would have been nice, it was only black coffee.  SOOOOOO SLOW!

The review only pertains to a custom coffee from the Clover machine. This was a weekday morning not crowded by Sunday morning standards. The barista was busy and doing much multi-tasking. The coffee came to me tepid - well below 140 degrees - and I presume it was waiting for the barista to pour it. The tall size is $4 vs. $1.90 for the regular Pike Place.coffee, so it deserves a bit more attention. Other than being busy I do not have a complaint about the service at this location. I just do not think that any regular coffee shop can handle custom coffee. The Clover machine was built to make it all automatic, and it is now unique to Starbucks, but whether it fulfills its function is something else.

As a University of the Art's student I am ALWAYS at this Starbucks. Let me say, I am absolutely in love with it and its staff. Everyone there is very knowledgeable, and oh so outgoing. Go there 2 times and by the 3rd they know you and your drink.   Shout out to my favoritist barista in the world: Emilie!

I ordered a Tall Soy Flat White , but it looked more like a dry cappuccino . So much foam! Not the first time either. Unfortunately, it's a hit or miss location. Some days their coffee is textbook & delicious, other days, not so much.

Dear Starbucks,  Recently heard a quote that I thought was worthy of the general feel of this and the greater part of your stores.  "Just because you're the best at something doesn't mean you're actually good"-Doug Stanhope.  Well, unfortunately, this is not a time I relish saying "I told you so", but this place.  Gets worse by the day.  I've included my review from November 2014 a  few lines below, and well... I told you so.  Don't rely on my impressions alone.  Just peruse the recent reviews of this spot!  This location, currently, like all in NOLA, is just so subpar by any measure.  When under the original store manager, this place hummed.  It virtually operated on a raised vibrational plane.  Anyone who frequented a couple of years ago knows what Ima saying.  Was the fastest, friendliest, freshest, quality, expertly crafted Siren's snug you could happen upon.    Now?  A factory of lifeless, corporately churned sadness.  Nowadays NOLA's coffee-loving veins are coursing with the lifeblood of far too many superior 3rd waves to even consider this, this...ardently formulaic inferiority.   The only decent Starbucks in the area is the Kenner location on Esplanade, curiously NOT in the NOLA market.    MY ORIGINAL REVIEW FROM NOVEMBER 2014 FOLLOWS:  Although I travel a lot, I consider myself a regular at this location when I'm back home in Metry.  I experienced the old store down the street and all it's immense problems (parking, drive through, etc), and the move to this new location earlier this year.  Great improvements were made all across the board in the areas around: 1) quality of employees, 2) presentation of the store, 3) quality of the products, and most importantly, 4) quality of service and atmosphere.  Well, now.  I sensed something was amiss last week on my 1st visit since around the middle of October.  I went through the drive through and the service was uncharacteristically slow.  And I mean slow to the tune of 25 minutes in line with only 6-8 cars in line.  Had nearly the same experience a couple of days later, and the kicker?  The drinks were wrong, and nearly undrinkable, both times.  This from a location that I considered to be the best around for a proper drink.  So I go in the store a couple of days ago and ask a customer, with whom I've become acquainted, what was going on?  He replies that Jay is gone.    I don't go around offering reviews on personalities, etc., but the loss of Jay as the manager of this store is gonna be crippling.  This man worked tirelessly to get to know every single customer, knew all of our drinks, always asked if everything was perfect, he was a walking coffee encyclopedia, was always sweeping the floors, making sure everything was just right.  I can go on, but you get the picture.  Any of the regulars who knew Jay will know exactly what I mean.  I ask a barista, "Where's Jay?"  Strangely, the reply goes something like, "Don't really know what happened.  I think the management culture makes the store manager's job impossible."  And I'm told several baristas have quit or asked to be reassigned as a result.  Starbucks, I don't know what is going on here, and I don't know the entire story, but if you can't hold on to an employee the quality of Jay, you have serious institutional issues.  I have never met a more caring, resourceful, hard working person as the man you have apparently now lost.  His dedication to Starbucks was obvious within a couple of minutes of talking to him.  It will take a lot to get this store up and running at the level it was under his guidance and leadership.  Apologies if this seems like a soapbox stance, but I decided I should use this forum to voice my displeasure with the noticeably quick demise of the area's only truly great Starbucks location.  Good luck getting your s**t together again.

The people working here have a very difficult location.  Super busy and a lot of type A, not very polite customers.  I've never seen any of them loose their cool!  I wouldn't last one day! :)  They're fast making the drinks.  The line is slow to order, but, it's the customers asking a million questions, not considerate of the line behind them.  So be nice.  It's a great Starbucks location.  Update: Pleeease lower the volume of the music!!!

Service is fine here; my irritation with this location is the fact that drive through traffic can enter either off Pendleton Pike or German Church Rd; one would think common courtesy would  prevail and customers would  take turns merging; they do not. My suggestion is a sign at corner of drive through with a reminder.

This is one of the worst Starbucks I've been to. I asked for a Carmel Macchiato and it took the girl forever to make it. After I recorded it and took a drink it tasted absolutely nothing like the drink.   Be warned if you want a coffee that taste like your usual don't go there. They'll mess up your order with the quickness!

There is this lovely lady named Lisa that works at this location. She was so sweet to me despite them being plummeted by the drive thru patrons. I tried their new iced coconut latte, I believe. She said hey, since its the first time trying this drink, bring it back if you don't like it and I'll replace it. How sweet. I like Starbucks, but I think people in FL must love it. There must be one or two on every street.

Dying to get away from the crowd, my group decided we needed some beverages of the caffeinated kind. The spouse piped up, "Hey, there's a Starbucks down the street". The bestie and I jumped right on board with the idea and off we went, adventurers in SoHo.   We walked in and I smiled. What a nice location! It's very open, with vaulted ceilings and plenty of seating.  Our barista was efficient and friendly, even when I whipped out an online coupon for a free kid's hot chocolate with espresso drink purchase.   Tonight was my splurge night, so I opted for a tall Peppermint Mocha Latte and a Peppermint Brownie Cake Pop. Both were delicious. The barista that prepared our drinks had a dazzling smile and added a flourish to the way she prepared the beverages. Great customer service!   I wish I was in this area more often. I'd certainly stop here to kick back with a coffee.

Worst parking lot layout in the history of the world. It's nearly impossible to find parking here in the morning and the drive thru line is crazy long. I expect a long line at a coffee shop in the morning, but when the drive thru line blocks the way out it's just a pain in the ass.   Baristas are very friendly and move the line quickly once you order, though.

What is important to know about this Starbucks is it's just off I275 easy exit and easy return back on to the interstate. Important for travelers to know. Larger store size, lot's of outdoor seating, drive through and very busy most times. Other than that it's a Starbuck's nuf said.

I really liked this location up until today when they purposely gave me the wrong change back & lied about it! I was the ONLY car in the drive-thru (which neverrr happens here). My order cost $5.73 & I gave her a $10 bill + 73 cents...all she had to do was give me a $5 bill back as change! She gave me my drink as soon as I pulled up & handed her the money...all that was left to do was turn around & hand me the $5 change. Tell me why I had to sit at the window waiting for sooo long that eventually 3 cars had pulled in, ordered & were waiting for a while behind me before she gave me $3 + some coins. I tried to tell her the change was wrong & she kept ignoring me/pretending she didn't hear or see me, even though she was standing right at the open window doing nothing the entire time...so I waited. When she decided to acknowledge my presence again, I told her I got the wrong change back & it's supposed to be a $5 bill. She starts giving me some b.s. defensive speech & I got so fed up that I just drove off. If u want to steal an extra tip for yourself & are that desperate for $1-$2 then just take it & go buy yourself some nice chapstick at dollar general. Also let me just point out that I completely understand having to wait a while in lines because people are busy making orders or having a bad day, that's fine & when they apologize, I tell them it's no problem at all & not to worry about it. That being said, it was at least 5 minutes before the FIRST car behind me even pulled into the drive-thru and there were THREE cars who showed up & ordered before she even gave me any "change". She was 100% just being a petty lying thief on purpose!

Great experience and service.   DEFINITELY try the nitro cold brew they serve here. It is so incredibly creamy and delicious. Its a bit more bitter than Id prefer, but I find most of Starbuck's coffees to be thay way. Even so, give it a try. It's quite good!

This place is always busy and packed but despite the busyness service is very fast and the employees are always nice and on point keeping things moving.   The couch towards the back of the store is very comfy too. My only complaint is the small parking.

Am disappointed with how Starbucks has changed within the last 6 months or so. Not sure if it is management or simple personnel changes? The staff for many years was knowledgeable and friendly. Now you get clueless people at the drive thru window and updates are not being done to the board outside that sits on the grass which usually introduces a new product or drink, and the lines are long due to the lack of speed in making the orders; something that was NEVER an issue before. Also I find that my frappuccinos are getting more and more espresso that has a scorched taste to it. Long gone are the days of when Bryan (asst. manager) and Connie (manager) ran this place. Since they left Starbucks has never been the same. However the crew they had after them did a fine job up to a point and now the crew running the place leaves a lot to be desired. Starbucks is slowly becoming a place that I am avoiding and I find myself not minding the drive on West Esplanade from Starbucks to Puccinos. I honestly find Puccinos has a much better tasting product line than Starbucks - STARBUCKS IS QUICKLY BECOMING A HAS-BEEN!
Always a good place to adjust time after heading westbound.

One word: NASTY.    I stopped in here for a hot chocolate and it was rancid. For realsies. I literally almost threw up.   *Let me clarify, since there seems to be confusion: I am reviewing the Starbucks at the address listed on the review. Across from Target, near DSW on Sierra Pkwy- they made the nasty drink.   I was the only person in line, and it took the lady several minutes to even acknowledge me. I ordered a tall hot chocolate and a slice of the lemon pound cake.   I take a bite of the lemon cake, pretty good- the bread tasted a little stiff- like it was a little stale, but the frosting on the top was good. I wait a few mintues for the hot chocolate to cool while I drive to the mall- and then I take a swig....OH MY GOD. *GAG* There is some really funk nasty after taste. It was downright puke inducing. I can't even really describe it, it was VERY bitter and disgusting and it left a horrible after taste in my mouth. I was like, wtf is up with this drink?   I tried to give it the benefit of the doubt, I told myself it must've tasted funny because I took a bite of the lemon cake before hand. So I take another swig- and it's still this NASTY bitter mouthful of BLECH. I decided that they must've made me the wrong thing. I think, Ok so maybe this is some funky holiday version of hot chocolate and I needed to specify regular hot chocolate.( "Is it impossible to get a cup of coffee-flavored coffee? You can get every other flavor except coffee-flavored coffee! They got mochachino, cappuchino, frappachino, Al Pacino, what the F***?")   I figured since I'm at the mall, and there is the starbucks kiosk there- I'd just double check and see if they wouldn't mind making me the right one. I explain the situation to the guys there and he's trying to figure out what is going on with my drink- he sniffs it and makes a face. Then, being the brave soul that he is, he takes a sip from my cup- and proceeds to gag and shake his head. He announces that the Mocha mix that they put into my drink was rancid.   Neat. Nothings better than a hot chocolate made with rotten mocha milk mix. Mmm. Needless to say, I didn't touch the rest of that lemon cake either.   I will never come to this starbucks again. My stomach curdles just thinking about this place. Ew!     Note: Kudos to the folks at the Meadowood starbucks for making me a new one that was not made with expired ingredients. See my review for that location. :)

I have spent many days here working on my laptop.The staff are always friendly.  Over my 8 hour marathon sessions I would have 2 or 3 cups of coffee and 2 sandwiches. I would often get my water topped up even with out asking. This for me was a very nice touch.  Above all I like the ambiance of the cafe. They update the furniture at the end of the summer in 2013. I preferred the old computer desk but I am growing into the new furniture.

Believe it or not, this has nothing to do with the overpriced, cheap coffee. Everyone knows about that.  It depends. Sometimes there's no line. Sometimes there's a huge line. Sometimes the baristas are great, and sometimes they give me a ton of attitude for requesting they put half and half in my coffee because I'm late and don't have time to do it myself. Like, sorry you have to actually put effort into your job???

This is the only Starbucks location that I've been to that I was told that they stop serving dark/blonde roasts after noon. I almost always order a dark roast at Starbucks and am not a fan of their Pike Place roast. At every location apart from this one, my order doesn't cause any conflict. If an establishment creates strife to make you one of its simplest menu options, that's a problem.

Horrible service! The baristas do not know what they are doing and have zero experience. They have no clue on how to make a latte or a simple coffee. I asked for an iced green tea latte and they gave me a HOT latte instead and had to make it a second time. Still they made it wrong and i had to get my money back. I gave them 3 chances the three times i went there and the service was HORRIFIC. Please do not go there. You will be wasting your time. How do you work at a starbucks and not know what an iced macchiato or green tea latte is? Nice decor, horrible service

This is my first time to this newer location, and I was so excited to see they have a drive thru(through?) even though it's not really set up for it. The drive thru actually goes around the back of all the restaurants in that strip. It's kind of a cluster back there because there is also construction going on. Staff super friendly but my iced Americano was room temperature and had no ice at all :/

This my staple.  However the place is getting run down.  The bathroom is not fresh and the floors are dirty.  Guys. ...close for a week or two deeeeeeep deeeeeeep clean.  Buy new furniture some good lighting and some happy crew and boom!

Brand new. Clean as can be. Updated and modern looking. Excellent service and coffee drinks. Probably the fastest I've ever seen my drinks made and drive thru line move. And one fancy drive thru order board to confirm your order. Not to mention a speaker that sounds like the person taking order is standing at your window, unlike the muffled and crackling experience you get at most drive thrus. This specific Starbucks just became my go to spot. And super convenient located on 301 and a short jump over to the I-75 on ramp means to can get any direction fast after your espresso fuel up!
I love this Starbucks!  It's got everything - nice barristas, beautiful atmosphere.  I come here almost every day and it's always a pleasant experience.

It's a Starbucks, you know what their coffee tastes like (meh) and what their prices are like (high). So I'll tell you why this became my Starbucks.  They recently renovated the seating and it's a GREAT study spot. Reliable wifi, big tables in the middle, a comfy couch in the corner, and seating all alongside the window (read: convenient outlets and people watching). Huge improvement to the awkward tiny circular tables they had before. It can get randomly crowded sometimes (why any family would decide to bring all their children here for an afternoon or why a mom would bring 8 teens to drink frappucinos is beyond me). The barristas are nice. One of them made fun of me for having been there every day for a week when writing the paper that wouldn't die, I appreciated that he noticed.   I still prefer independent coffee shops like Citadelle or Toast, but when I'm too lazy to go that far, this will do. Plus I'm a sucker for that Passion Iced Tea in the summer (get it half sweetened, it's better) and for $1.62 (?) it's pretty good.

I am one of the regulars and This starbuck is my favorite of all Starbucks that I've been too! The barista always make my drink taste the way I Like it! The staff are friendly and helpful! Highly recommended!:)

This is easily my favorite Starbucks for many reasons, the main reason being that it's the most convenient--I love that it's right down the street from the University (where I work and study) and that it has a drive through. Despite the lines, the workers at this specific Starbucks are always quick and greet me with positive attitudes. I've never had an issue with any of my drinks at this Starbucks, and I've been going here since I started college in 2014. I go here at least 4 times a week and don't plan on changing that this next semester.   As for my drink today, I decided to try the new blonde vanilla latte (iced) with almond milk. It was absolutely delicious and the perfect amount of caffeine to get me through the afternoon. Come to this Starbucks and you'll never be disappointed!

Great Starbucks: Fast and good service. My Coffee always comes out right. It's super convent when you have shopping at target to legends to do. If they is a long line it moves fast and you get your coffee within minutes.  Didn't give 5 stars because a they always seem to be out of something.
It's Starbucks . . . nothing really bad to say.  Pumpkin bread is amazing!!

This is a really nice Starbucks. I love the modern look and feel of it since they renovated about a year ago. They also have the exclusive Reserve Roastery blends (only a fraction of Starbucks locations offer these) so if you really like specialty blends/ exotic beans, you can find them here. I give it three stars though, because they are so slow every time I come here. I'd say I wait on average one minute for my drink at most Starbucks locations, but every time I come here I'm consistently waiting 4 or more minutes. Doesn't seem like a lot, but it feels like an eternity when other locations (and other coffee joints) have a much quicker speed of delivery. It's puzzling that they're so slow because they always have 5 or 6 employees in there that appear to just be getting in the way of one another behind the counter.

The approach to this location is a little inconvenient. Additionally servers like Zack today made the experience even worse. He was preparing the coffee orders, whispering names and expecting customers To wade through a sea of drinks picking up each to determine if you properly identified the right. Ultimately, I took someone else's by accidental luckily it was the same coffee.  Ugh.

The service good but here is what I don't like, this is a franchise place meaning there are Starbucks all over the country. I should be able to go to one Starbucks in Phoenix and it taste the same in Missouri but that wasn't the case. The store was dirty and the restroom was dirty. I get it when it gets busy that it can cause a mess but when you walk in and people are just hanging around and the store is dirty that's a problem. The macchiato was not good and pretty much tasted like milk. For a 5 dollar coffee I expect for it to taste better. Was not happy with this Starbucks
This starbucks has a great location , a had a really fresh croissant and a coffee latte . The service is good

I come to this starbucks AT LEAST once a day everyday.  I spend hundreds of dollars a month at this starbucks buying coffee, tea, etc.  Most of the time I come, my order is wrong, the staff is completely rude and disrespectful to the customers, or they take their time to make a drink because they are so lazy and even if I'm the only one in there, it takes forever to make an iced tea.  I really hope this starbucks gets better because its a great area for this.  The only employee that is really nice and is on top of things is Matt.  They also use a computer to put in the orders instead of using the sharpie and writing on the cup and writing your name, etc, i know it's lame but it just seems like a regular coffee store when they don't do the starbucks tradition of the sharpie and the customers name!

I went to this Starbucks location last night for just a cookie or any type of dessert/pastry.  I was craving something sweet, so pretty much anything would've done.  I walked inside at about 7:30 PM and step in front of the refrigerated case to find it empty.  I stared at it for the longest time wondering if this location closed at 8 PM maybe and I'd just missed them putting away all the food for the night or if maybe they'd had a whole tour bus stop by that wiped them out or something.  It was probably 5 minutes that I stood there.  Not one employee ever addressed me or my confused expression at the empty case, and I eventually ended up just leaving and getting a delicious, fresh chocolate chip cookie from the Puccino's down the street.  Out of curiosity I looked up their hours this morning to see when they closed, and, come to find out, they were open until 10 PM last night...  I'm hoping I caught them on a bad night maybe, but I won't be back to find out.

Overpriced, overhyped coffee. So tired of not being able to sit down inside because of inconsiderate people using this as an internet café.  I'm done. There are many other places to have excellent coffee.
Going to Starbucks today was the best! The barista made the greatest recommendation for a special frapuccino and it was just perfect.

Possibly the nicest and most knowledgeable Starbucks staff I've ever encountered!  They made my coffee perfect they made what started as a bad day a very good one! We are so lucky to have them right on the beach it's nice for the people who live here and visitors alike! I will be back and recommend to all! Thanks guys!

I'm a Starbucks regular (twice a day every day). I don't come to this location often because it's not my favorite. They are always understaffed and today when getting an iced Americano with white mocha, the barista didn't even stir it. When I asked to store it before he gave it to us he said they are understaffed and they don't have time to stir the drinks with dairy in them because he needs to wash it. Weird because I get it every day at the other locations and they make it perfect. Why can't all Starbucks be like the Keystone Reno location?!
This is your run of the mill Starbucks.. We went on a Saturday morning .. Slightly slow staff and not the most friendly but they got the job done.  Coffee was served and it was good!

Small, quiet, attached to Hampton Inns.  All the handicapped sports were taken by enormous pickup truck with no stickers or disabled plates, super frustrating. Coffee was hot and strong  just like my husband likes. I like the free water to drink in the car.
A very nice young lady serviced me on 06/08/17, we spoke of Natalie's cakes. Very informational about company who makes them. Thank you for a delightful experience.   Thanks again,
Excellent service with an overwhelming number of people in line for a convention. Kudos. Clean, spacious, friendly.

This Starbucks is one of the best I've been in because the staff is always super friendly, makes their drinks correctly, and the place itself is a smaller shop, so it's a more intimate environment, which is great if that's your preference.  Anytime I come here, I prefer walking in over going through the drive-thru because the people who work here brighten my day and it's a great way to start before I head into work.  I look forward to stopping in on my way to work today.

This Starbucks doesn't have a drive through like some of the newer ones in the area but it does have an awesome sit in area to work on stuff with wifi and lots and lots of seating.  10+ tables, a window bar, couch, and a seating area with 6 or so comfy chairs. The staff is awesome too!

I order a grande bold with a splash soy at each and every Starbucks I go to and this is the FIRST location where the barista/cashier showed me the cup at the counter and asked how the color was!    Now, this was excellent for me because when I say "a splash" I literally mean "a splash" and they usually put way too much.  She put the perfect amount, but the fact that she asked how it was definitely made me happy!

Miserable People. They will take your money but not allow paying customers to use the restroom . They blame the size of the crowd 4 hours before parade time but somehow the size of the crowd is not keeping them from making thousands of dollars an hour. This store manager should be fired. You can put more people on your cash register but not to service your restroom ?
Coffee is good but a grande size is now what a tall used to be.   Not good--lower the price if that's case.   There are many other coffee places near here to visit
It's okay. I use to stop here daily. Just to pricey. 10$ for coffee and a muffin, if the prices went down a bit I would come back. :(

This starbucks is full of racist ignorant bigots !! They are lazy, useless, extremely disrespectful, and I have never and will never invest in this location !!! They rush you out the restrooms, and knock constantly while you are using the r&R. They talk crap outside the door while you are using the restrooms. Poor customer service.

I want to give a shout-out to this location and the great people working today. I recently found out I am lactose intolerant and I am still learning to ask for milk alternatives. I forgot to ask for my drink with a milk alternative and they were super nice about it and helped me get one without milk. Extra points here!

It's a Starbucks, that means mediocre, overpriced coffee for brainwashed hipsters who wouldn't know good coffee from a hole in the ground. There are 5 other Starbucks locations within 2 miles of this one.  I still find it hard to believe anyone would drink this swill, let alone enough people for them to actually be everywhere. I occasionally stop in one to remind myself why I go out of my way to get actual good coffee. People, wake up and stop pumping your veins full of sugar and lacklustre coffee. There are actual good coffee houses in the bay area, you can identify them by the intoxicating aroma of coffee, and a lack of green mermaid.

It's nice to be the "Duke" of the Seminole Heights Starbucks!!!!! The only thing that gives me tears about this SB is the parking and traffic. I love the customers who always park right in front of the "No parking" signs and anywhere else they believe they can. It tends to make getting in and out of here a mess. Other than that this seems to be the best SB location in Tampa..

I love this Starbucks, unlike many of the nearby locations, this one seems to keep their coffee machine clean. I typically get a mocha frappe, often with a shot of espresso. Many of the other Brandon locations including the ones in the mall, do not have a good track record with their frappes. They just do not taste good every time, and for $5 a drink I want consistency.
