I don't know if they're not changing out the frappe mix often enough or not cleaning the coffee machine often enough, (as I get an added shot of espresso.) Either way, if you're looking for an excellent frappe, this is the perfect location!

I LOVE Starbucks but this place sucked today!!! 20 freaking minutes to get a normal coffee. The staff have such unpleasant looks on their faces, and the coffee still was not great... I do NOT want a refund I'm just so fed up with this store and the poor attitudes of the staff. It seems as if almost most of them hate their jobs. I have NEVER said this before BUT don't go to that Starbucks. To give an idea of how much I love Starbucks I would be gold status for the next 4 years if I never bought another cup of coffee past today. Honestly from the looks they were understaffed today.

I come here like every day? Then your going to make me get back in line because I forgot to tell you I needed Caraamel Drizzle? Then pay for it. Your swipe fee is more then the purchase... not happy...
Drinks are always quick and on point. Not to mention Aaron made our morning last week with his singing through the drive up speaker. Glad to have a solid Starbucks close by!

Worst service ever!  I ordered four drinks, I was charged for five 2 of the drinks were wrong. I asked for a refund but the girl in the counter says have to wait for the manager and they don't issue receipt for refund. How would I know that I'm going to be refunded, check my online banking nothing is showing up. I was given a hot chocolate for cafe mocha and double chocolate chips frappuccino for caramel frappuccino. They acted that it was my fault, didn't even bother to offer an apology.

Good new Starbucks, nice set up, BUT terrible staff. I was there when Shannon was running the crew. I was going to stay for a few hrs studying but left early. She kept yelling about the pretzels smelling like dog. Shouting instructions at other employees. Complaining about the floor. Talking really loud in general. Singing loudly. Really annoying. Made the shop and the rest of the staff look like a mcdonalds. And it wasn't that busy and she couldn't get orders right.

Horrible. This is my review based on the last 2 weeks. 1st order: My order was spot on. 2nd order: They forgot the raspberries syrup I ask for. 3rd order: they gave me someone else's order. 4th: I thought I was ordering something simple, but they didn't get it.   I

The staff is friendly here.... but they typically screw up our orders. Today... I asked for a passion tea lemonade with classic. Not a hard order. Didn't get lemonade in my drink. Honestly... I don't pay $4-5 for a drink for it to be wrong.

So this Starbucks is right by my home and so I have been waiting to give it a review.  I will tell you, the major positive thing is the coffee is always hot and I love hot coffee!  The staff is really friendly no matter if you go through the drive thru or walk in, they are terrific!   I have had Ice teas and hot coffee and for both this place knocks it out of the park.  One of the better Starbucks in town!

I love almost everything about this Starbucks. The layout and space available is conducive to studying and/or meeting with others. Service and employees are always delightful. There is ample parking. There is really one sole reason for this 2-star review, their wifi connection is always HORRIBLE!!!!! My music stream is constantly cutoff and My I internet connection is lost. Every other time  I conduct an internet search I get an error message that my connection is lost. It is incredibly frustrating and every time I return to this location I hope it's fixed, but I'm batting 100% here - 5/5 times I've come, this has been the experience.

Doesn't matter whether you come through drive thru or inside, this team is fast and attentive! They have a few new people so sometimes the wait in line can be a little long but the speed in which you get your drink makes up for it! GREAT STORE, GREAT PEOPLE!

These very simple people have a very hard time both listening and making coffee.  I find it amazing that so many people have such a hard time making large coffee with cream and Splenda.   Also generally speaking the cream and sugar are supposed to be in the cup not all over the outside of the cup.

This Starbucks is ridiculously uncomfortable.  No cozy, peaceful places to sit and drink coffee.  With a million doors and this and that and traffic, it's noisy, busy, awkward.    In other words not feng shuid right.  No idea what the interior designer was thinking.

I live right down the street from this location so it's my go to right before work. For the most part the drive thru rolls by pretty quick for how long the line can be. The drinks I get are spot on. The customer service lately is horrible. The last couple times I went, the employees at the drive thru work with no integrity. For example, I paid with a $10 bill total was about $7 and some cents. She only gave me back my cents. In my head I'm like this doesn't add up. So I asked for my receipt and she says absolutely. I'm like you knew you gave me this amount of change right, you owe me two dollars. And she doesn't hesitate at all to give me my two dollars. So she sighs and acting all mad because I caught her. Seriously? I was in disbelief. I know it's only two dollars but that's not right.  Next one is a guy, paid with a card this time because I wasn't trying to get jipped! I asked politely, can you do me a favor and heat this up (muffin). He rolled his eyes at me.. So here I am waiting while I hear this guy mock me with his other co workers.... Ugh. How upsetting. I was furious! Never stepping foot in here again.

I always drink coffee in the mornings to keep me awake in school. The coffee there is AMAZING. I go there almost everyday. The service is fantastic and the pastries are very delicious. The workers there are very nice.They are also very well organized.

The staff was obnoxious. Every time I come in here they are extremely loud and have no regard for any of their customers. I do not feel like listening to the staff sing. It seems as if they have no managers on duty. It's unprofessional. Very annoying to try to concentrate while the employees are yelling at the top of their lungs. If it were other customers being loud around me I would understand. But the staff.......

I start every morning at this location! The staff and management are professional, courteous, and friendly.  New hires quickly understand the atmosphere and all customers appear to be satisfied. I love this place!

I enjoy dropping in from time to time, after dropping my little one off at school. It is always a hit or miss. Sometimes they're on point and your drink is made perfectly and other times they're just so busy they aren't consistent.

Update - Turn on the heat and clean the Phuckin bathrooms! It's not enough just mopping the floors. Gauddam! Gloves mask rag or wipes and wipe the toilets down clean and flush. End of story!   Excellent study spot!!! Spacious and very comfortable. They also have reasonable hours. 10pm close.  Staff is very friendly. Only bummer is, there is no clover at this location.

They messed up my drink this morning, I called and they offered me a free one so I go back in just now ands it's even worse then before! I'm almost positive there's not any chai in here. It just tasted like milk. Very disappointed in my experience here today. Especially for spending $14 on 3 really poor drinks. Not sure I'll visit this location again.

This Starbucks is directly on my way to work, so when I don't make coffee at home I stop in here.  If you get a Starbucks gift card and register it and use that to make your purchases, they send you free drink stuff sometimes and you get some added stuff in your coffee for free, like the soy milk that I have put in any foofy coffee drinks that I consume.

Tracy is the sweetest lady there. I go there most mornings before work, and we always chat and talk. My breakfast is always delicious and made quickly. Even if it wasnt good, I would still go here just for the people who work here. The customer service is beyond compare.

First and only Starbucks where I ordered online and my order was not ready when I came in. Generally not that big a deal but there's only a few people in here and probably 5 to 6 people behind the counter. The whole point of ordering ahead is to grab and go, but I'm still waiting here 15 minutes later watching people who came in after me get their drinks ‍

There is this one cashier that is terrible! She would constantly deny any promos or specials to make her job easier. I walked in to order a Cherry Blossom frappachino and she said they don't offer it. I called later in the day and the person who took my call said they did. I did not walk in on the first day of the promotion either. The store managers need to do a better job educating their staff and have them turn away customers.

Because I love making coffee and home and I have a certain few fave brands I seek when I am craving the joe, I don't stumble into Starbucks often.    On days I am rushing., I am thankful for a drive thru, quick breakfast options & the reliability of a chain coffee joint. Good to know that every Starbucks from the Hudson to the Bay, you're gonna get what you get no matter where you are! Yay!  I just MUST mention this location. 75th & Keystone, I'm gonna betcha that this is one of the busiest in town.   Now to speak of the baristas & drive thru emcees: The staff are young & exceptional.  Previously, the gentlemen were upbeat and friendly; second to last visit, our barista was gracious and polite. Every time, they are QUICK!   Todaaaaay (sing that.), the young lady that took my order literally sang my order back to me and made sure I would have a great day! Thanks! =)

I'm writing a review and a little tidbit from the review prior to mine. This location is wonderful. The crew here is always so nice and helpful and all the drinks are wonderful. Lots of parking and easy on and off access to the freeway.   By the way, Carpinteria has TWO Starbucks... The other location is located in the Vons grocery store located on Linden Avenue.  Check them both out, you get excellent service at both locations

I will have to say if you are in a hurry for a something this is not the place to go.  I ordered a Double Chocolate Frappuccino and waited my for my drink.  Four other people came in after me and they all got their drinks (6 drinks) were all made before I went over and asked when mine would be made.  I was told they were very busy and they would get to it.  Two others received their drinks before the gal asked what I had ordered because she could not find my cup.  She hastily made it and it did not taste as good as I had before.  I will pass on this location in the future.
20 minutes for an iced coffee and iced chai???? Come on Starbucks! Hire more people for your busier locations.

I ordered a cloudy caramel macchiato with super light caramel drizzle, the lady gave me it with extra caramel drizzle, I let her know I asked for light and she got mad. I understand the frustration but if I'm paying $6 for a coffee I'd like it made correctly. She dumped it into another cup and the drink was ruined (no longer foamy like it's suppose to be) I will not be returning to this Starbucks.

I almost always have a problem at this location, the employees never get my order right, and when I tell them about it they make you feel like YOU'RE the wrong one :/ I will say, the lines are always short, and it's in such a convenient location for me, I just wish the employees were a little friendlier and had better customer service. Definitely not the same welcoming feeling I got with other locations.

The line was a little long but other than that this Starbucks was friendly and made my caramel macchiato perfectly! They have everything a flagship Starbucks dies including hit breakfast sandwiches and yummy pastries.

It's Starbucks, same here as anywhere else. This location is open 24 hours but the lobby closes at 12 midnight. I found that out the hard way unfortunately. The lobby is always clean but cold so bring a jacket. This has become a great place for me to come late night to get some work in before bed. Even late at night I feel safe at this establishment. The staff is friendly and committed to customer service. As a non-coffee drinker, I usually get a berry refresher (no water) or a pastry with water.

It's exactly like every other Starbucks everywhere, except it has the additional excitement of having about half of their electrical outlets in the store, distributed randomly, just not work at all. There's no indication of which outlets work and which ones don't, and the employees have no fucking clue, so if you don't happen to be paying attention to which power icon appears in your taskbar, your computer may just suddenly shut down, losing all the work you've done since you last saved.  If they're too cheap to get their outlets fixed, they COULD spend three buck on this [ http://amzn.to/1QcZEN2 ], and then cover the non-working outlets, so that there's a reminder for customers that some don't work, plus an indication of which is which, but that would, like, require that someone test all the outlets, which would, like, be work, so they don't do it.  How much do you value that forty-five minutes of work you just lost because they're too fucking lazy to get their outlets fixed? I figure that five bucks plus a loss of almost an hour of work on my dissertation bumps the price to $$$$, hence my survey question answer.  Oh, and don't think they'll pull one of the five or six people chatting with their coworkers and ignoring the customers behind the counter onto the second register just because the line's out the door.

My favorite Starbucks in the city! They always have my mobile order ready, and it is always hot! There is good room to study or have a small meetings, but sometimes it can get loud. The barista's are all very friendly and I always enjoy my daily midday trip to Starbucks!

3 stars for the quality and the service-  Here's another review of a Starbucks. Since it's a chain the quality always seems to be the exact same and like a lot of Starbucks this one has pretty decent service. They also have a large selection of bagged coffee, coffee/tea mugs to purchase, and delectable food in the case. The bad news is that it's in the mall so it's usually really busy.
too much ice,I did not buy a Starbucks,I actually purchase a Icebucks,a bucks of ice.If I purchase a vanti,I get 3/4 ice,even I purchase a tall size,I get half of the ice.

To say this is a Starbucks is giving Starbucks a bad name! I frequent Starbucks an average of 4 days a week. I ordered an iced almond milk latte and it tasted like burnt wax! I haven't ever had coffee taste like this. Five dollars for something this unpalatable is sad.
Located inside a neighborhood. Kinda out of the way. Better off hunting for a coffee fix off of east moana blvd or kietzke lane.

My local Starbucks. Great staff, very friendly. The store is a bit small if you plan to hang out and do some work / have a meeting, etc. But, they have made the best out of the space. It is also right next door to the best doughnuts in town (Nicola's), so grab a Starbucks and some Nicola's doughnuts and you are SET!

for as busy as these folks are they have great poise & attitude while they grind and serve away to tourists, locals, employees and outside panhandlers   sure its sbux and the rep that comes with it but philz, sightglass or blue bottle is over 200 miles away.   if hub was closer for me there'd be one less review.   the weekends are a mess so come earlier or later  the inside of this smaller franchised space is gorgeous with a tip of the cap to the construction mgmt team.  i like the pictures of their humble beginnings storefront on pike place meets virginia street and the ceiling resembles the fox theater in oakland a bit  daily drip seems to have blonde, dragon and pike in the rotation as one is also able to get their la boulange fix

I worked at this location for over 2 years as well as many others in the area for a total of 6 1/2 years. I can tell you for even being one of the older ones, this is by far the cleanest store in the greater STL area. The staff is still the most friendly group of people every time I go in-even the newbies I don't know! The environment is also a perk since there's no drive through. While living in Fenton, I would drive out of my way to go to this Starbucks instead.

I have stayed at many hotels in my life. Of those hotels, several have had a Starbucks inside. But this is the first hotel I have stayed in that had a full service Starbucks and that is a nice perk.  When people visit New Orleans they often have a tendency to overdo the partying. That leads to dehydration, food cravings and the need for a massive influx of caffeine. I am not saying that happened to me, but this Starbucks was a very welcome addition to my stay at the Hyatt.  Since it is part of a massive chain, you already know what they have here. The sandwiches are a great snack or meal, and the coffee does contain caffeine. Service was friendly, but a bit slow. This could be because of the massive crowds so I cut them some slack, but it does bear mentioning.  If you stay at the Hyatt, come here. If not, it probably is out of your way and you don't care. Move along to my next review.
ordered a strawberry greent tea infusion this morning，i would said it is the worst drink i have tried in my life so far. it is simply just water with little green tea flavor
Garret is so sweet and accommodating! Made my experiance here fantastic! Boy should be in management if hes not already!!! Thank you garret

Expensive coffee, not that great. But what can I say its starbucks, that's what you expect. Save your money go to McDonald's, coffee is just as good (if not better) and lower priced.  Try the hazelnut ice coffee at McD's its amazing.

This is the slowest Starbucks ever. Everytime it never fails. Do they train someone here everyday?? They are never short staffed so I can't put my finger on it. The people are always nice but geez it's always low. Guarantee when you pull up the first thing is , " welcome to Starbucks please wait for a moment and I'll be right with you.  So annoyinnngggg

Sidenote: The best cup of coffee I've had in CWE is at Pi Pizza.   I think Starbucks excels at consistency of product. This location is no different in that the coffee is the same taste as any place else. That said, this is a great location to grab a quick cup and go. ...however, parking is tricky near Maryland and Euclid so this is easier said than done.   I rate this as 2 out of 5 stars comparing it to other Starbucks shops in the area. It is small and cramped and has one unisex bathroom which creates waiting for the bathroom an expectation. On a positive note, the bathroom has always been clean. Maybe the guy in front of you feels obligated not to be a sloppy mess knowing a cute girl might be waiting for her turn.   The business itself does really well given the traffic in CWE and their Apple -esque ability to make people feel special while holding the logo on their cup (myself included as I type away at my Macbook writing this review...).   A coffee shop as a whole and I might give it 3 or 4 stars. The staff is great (as great as all Starbucks staff) and you know what you are getting when you walk in the door. But when it comes down to going to a Starbucks, I'd rather go to one elsewhere.

I went into this Starbucks last evening, to use the restroom. The doors to the restroom were open but I was told the restrooms were closed. I asked if I purchased an item, as a customer, I could use the restrooms and was told I could not. I asked how it was determined that the restrooms were closed, and the barista said he or she determines whether the restrooms were closed and they were closed to me. I went out the door, but promptly returned to ask another question and there was a child coming from the restroom. My only guess, since the store was still open and still serving customers, and a child went in after I was told I could not, is that my brown skin played a part in the decision to deny my use of the restrooms. I intend to follow this up to the highest corporate management. I won't use the  barrista's  name here but I will use it in every letter and email  I write and every phone call I make. This type of response to anyone who walks in a Starbucks is humiliating, and is completely unacceptable.
Let me keep short and sweet. Hands down the worst Starbucks, they can't make coffee & the staff seems to have been hired from the unfriendly stupid valley employment agency.
Stopped here to get some coffee real quick. I was happy they had a drive through. The guy at the drive through made my day with his sense of humor.

I went with some co-workers to use the coupons on Retailmenot for free caramel or hazelnut macchiatos from 3/1/13-3/31-13. Retailmenot is a reputable site from which I have successfully gotten several other coupons or coupon codes before. The coupon has no limit aside from not using more than one in the same transaction.   I used the coupon from Retailmenot at this location and other locations the week prior with no problems. Today, a few co-workers and I went in to use the coupons and there were two employees at the front register. When we told them that we had coupons, the male said, "Oh, we're not taking those but I don't know why, talk to my co-worker." We went to speak with the co-worker and she insisted that they were a scam. The first week the coupons were available they were accepted but then all of a sudden they were a scam? Especially considering the first employee "didn't know" why they were no longer accepting them, it seemed to us as if too many people used them so they didn't want to lose out on more money so began telling people they were not valid.  I doubt Starbucks is hurting for money, however, if they can't afford to have a month long special, they probably shouldn't offer free items with a coupon for a long period of time.

Rude baristas. A lot of teenagers work here and don't really care about their jobs. I go here often and almost always have the same experience with the same workers. They're rude and busy talking among themselves.

This is the Starbucks at Canal Place. It's a lil' location with a few seats actually inside. There are 6 tables on the floor in the middle of the mall to sit at to drink your coffee and/or eat your food. This Starbucks closes for business at 2000 hours (8:00pm). Harrah's Casino is directly across Canal and there's another in there open until Midnight but it is a franchise. This one the staff is great and they do get you in and out quickly. The Starbucks at 700 Canal is much bigger and fortunate for THEM but not for me busier than the other ones. Anyway this is Starbucks. Not much to explain. I do love this location when going to: The Theatres At Canal Place. This is an excellent location.

Daniel took my order and he was absolutely amazing! The customers in front of us were unsure of what kind of drink they previously had that they enjoyed. It was a lady and her daughter, the lady spoke Spanish only. However, Daniel did his best with the help of the customers' daughter translating for him to craft a drink he thought she would enjoy. Both customers left happy and smiling. Daniel then took my order and managed to balance making five drinks over three transactions, while helping as a cash out register for non-Starbucks items, AND he even had some delicious looking strawberry samples ready for people to try. Oh, and he did this entirely solo! He did all this without even breaking a sweat, and had the brightest smile I've seen on anybody. Many words can come to mind to describe Daniel (can you say, "Employee of the Month"?) but ROCKSTAR is what comes to my mind. Keep on spreading those smiles, Daniel, I appreciate you.

This Starbucks by far is the best around. They always get my order correct. Sean and Alex are two of the best barista's I have ever seen!!  They always go above and beyond at this Starbucks! Just want to say thanks!!
These gals make my morning!  They are always incredibly friendly, quick and consistent (even with the new faces from the Broadway location)!! Thanks!
Amazing! I believe the girls name is Britney. I work next door at Ulta in the salon and she always hooks us up with free drinks so nice of her!
This was always my studying spot, the baristas are very nice and they get my drink right unlike other branches of starbucks.

There must be fresh blood in this store, I went back a few days ago and was promptly greeted with smiles. The barista at the register was pleasant and went the extra mile in locating additional cake pops for my two girls. It had to be the pink frosted ones, and she thankfully found two!   My order was made quickly and most importantly correct. I will be back to this location.
Standard Starbucks. Tasty treats and Java but nothing special. Bathroom wasn't real clean and out of TP

Smaller store than most. But it serves its purpose.   Great for hotel guests, nearby coffee drinkers and #stickandmove commuters like myself.   Located in the rear corner of Canal Place, 1st floor, you'll find this delightful coffee shop. It's the perfect size. A person that wants to sit & sip, is able to do so in the adjacent sitting area, FYI, no worries.   This is the PERFECT PLACE for me to pull a stick and move caper like I normally do. Place my order on the app and park, pick up & go #stickandmove #success.   The baristas here are vibrant & full of life in this delightful little corner of Canal Place New Orleans.   I think this'll be my NEW pick up place.

OUTSIDE: Stairs and a ramp that go up to the front entrance. Outside seats and tables to enjoy the nice weather.   INSIDE: Typical Starbucks feel. This location has a lot of seating, and a big long table in the back with stool type seating. Quite a few people on this Saturday late morning sitting inside and outside. However, when I walked in there was no line with one guy who came in after me.  ORDERED: Yogurt blueberry muffin warmed. She used a tissue paper to grab it so I'm glad to see proper sanitary practices. When it was done heating up, they promptly took it out and placed it on the pick-up table. Tall iced white chocolate mocha at first she grabbed as hot and started writing until I asked for it iced. Whipped cream on top because I forgot to ask for it without.   STAFF: The girl who rang me up seemed neutral, not friendly, and not mean. I apologized for not telling her it was iced, but she didn't say anything like "No worries that's ok." She just grabbed a different cup with a blank expression and re-wrote it. I thought she would ask for my name to write on the cup, but she didn't. I'm not sure what Starbucks policy is on that if they only take names when it's busy or what. Seems a bit more personal when they call your name instead of calling out what you ordered.   OVERALL: A-OK in my book. My drink tasted good and there wasn't an excessive amount of ice like some places do. If the staff was a bit more up friendly then I'd probably add another star.

I love this Starbucks!! It was well overdue for the area. The atmosphere is welcoming, love the designing, and the decor. The employees are very friendly, professional and helpful. I was disappointed to find the fireplace not working. It has been a month and  the fireplace is still not WORKING!!! Disappointing especially the days I really would love to relax in the lounge. Hopefully the owners are working on this issue.

BEST STARBUCKS EVER! The Baristas that work here are amazing!! They get my order right every time and even know me as soon as I start to speak through the drive through! Kathy is amazing always so friendly! I hate being away from home and having to visit other locations! Thanks 4 being awesome! Triple Shot Expresso Mocoito With 4 Sweet and Lows!

The drive thru line is always extremely long here.  So today I opted for walking in as I was in a hurry. Only person at the counter, waited 5 mins to be acknowledged. 4 employees behind the counter not one looked my way. I have never seen a crew with less sense of urgency and I have been to plenty of Starbucks. Giggles, chatting w each other and grabbing cups and making drinks SO SLOWLY. No wonder the lines on the drive thru are so long. Pretty disappointed today. Management here needs to keep the crew in check.

Poorly run Starbucks.  Ill-prepared to serve and have waited more than 10 minutes for a tea that they did not have brewed. Regret stopping in this Starbucks, as I could've driven 10 additional minutes and been finished by now. They need a new manager.

My first move, especially in Reno with the whole midtown movement  is to support local first. But, out of location convience I frequent this Starbucks more than I typically would, and the biggest reason why is the staff. The staff here is pleasent, friendly and efficiant without being fake. Sometimes,  thats all I need to bring me back in the door. Otherwise, Starbucks is Starbucks. Great staff here.

This is your typical small Starbucks.  True, it's not "the thing to do" in the French Quarter. But, if you work at the Canal Place mall, at Westin, or in the office tower and frequent this Starbucks, like I do, the staff will remember your name and your regular order.  That's all I ask for in a coffee shop.

I love the stained glass ceiling at this Starbucks! It's located inside the Silver Legacy Casino and it looks out on the gaming floor. I wonder what used to be in its place before Starbucks.  There aren't many seats, but most people just grab their coffee to-go anyway. I went here with my mom for a quick pick-me-up in the afternoon. Nice to just sit and relax.

It's Starbucks, always consistent. The drinks are always on point. The baristas are friendly and personable.  You have to take the good with the bad. And as thrilled as I was for the drive-thru, I'm a tad saddened. The drive-thru has slowed the service for those in-house. I've never noticed this before at other Starbucks with drive-thrus but here I have. They have also been out of caramel syrup for what feels like 2 weeks. You would think with so many Starbucks nearby they'd help their new location and loan some.  I'm not in love with the new location's bathroom. There fan in there makes you think there's a jet overhead. And for a paperless hand-dryer bathroom you expect less paper mess. But the toilet seat covers are hanging outside of their holder, and toilet paper bits were all over the floor.
Starbucks is fine, this Starbucks is terrible. Poorly run and operated. It's our local option, so it would be convenient to stop here regularly, but almost every time we do the order gets messed up.
Im not sure why there are low ratings. I went in today and there were a fair amount of people yet I received my large order quickly. The customer service was great and the food was good too.
Slow service , not enough parking.  Need another register and to always attend both registers.  On average, it takes 12min for a small pike - should never take that long!

Usually I don't see a need to rate chain stores, but this Starbucks is better than others. This is rating based on the particular Starbucks location, not the overall company.  The baristas I've had are very cheerful and are GREAT at making drinks. I don't like watery fraps and the baristas make it very thick but not super icy. When there was a super long line from 1/2 off promotion, they made my drink quick and still delicious.  Very roomy and cool location (great in the summer, haven't gone here in winter yet). However there never seem to be enough seats inside. People sit here for hours. The outside seating usually has open tables though.

Writing this on minute 12 as I sit in the drive through line. For some reason I haven't learned my lesson the other times I've come to this location and had the exact same slow service.   Officially switching to the one by work. Peace!

Good service with a busy morning Barista named Brigette was great.  Positive attitude and giving other employees positive comments during a rush.  Real team player.  Starbucks is made better by great employees like this
Great coffee for a Great price.  I don't know when they will find some coffee that is better.  Specialty drink wise that is.

I am a really big fan of this Starbucks location. The staff is friendly, efficient, a little quirky, and they are already starting to remember my order (despite my only becoming a semi-regular about a week ago).   It's also one of the biggest, most wide-open Starbuck's I've ever been to, which makes me very happy. I'm used to going to Starbucks and struggling to get a spot at one of the four available tables. This location however, has something like 12 round tables (seating two to four people) , 10 large lounge chairs, a bar, a long table with lamps and several side tables. And there appears to always be available seating.   If you're the kind of person who enjoys doing work or reading in a location outside of your office or home, this is a great place to go.

Love the look of this building inside.  HUGE exposed brick walls and ceiling.  Staff was friendly and funny.  I loved that they had the AC cranked!  What a wonderful place to escape away to to cool off from walking around down town!

The service/staff were great; friendly and quick at getting my order.  I love Starbucks. The pastries make me smile, the oatmeal is comforting, and the breakfast sandwiches are cheesy goodness. My daughter is a big fan of the Turkey Pesto Panini.  If I love the star so much why only 4 stars?..the disappointment of the Holiday Panini. It was underwhelming. The sandwich was a barely a resemblance of what is pictured on the Starbucks site.   ***** Site description: A generous portion of sliced turkey breast is at the heart of this holiday-inspired panini. It's topped with cranberry-herb stuffing and turkey gravy then served warm on toasted ciabatta bread. It's a handheld turkey dinner.*****  Generous is a subjective term. The panini was slim..it actually looked as though there wasn't anything inside. The stuffing must've soaked up all the gravy creating a thick paste that birthed a mushy texture. Thankfully the bread was toasted; it provided some crunch.   This experience has me doubtful on trying something new at Starbucks. No worries though, I have plenty of favorites to keep me going back.  *The oatmeal options are so good I often order one for lunch or dinner :D

Horrible service.  The guy behind the counter tee making the drink is moving in slow motion and talking to all the employees at the same time.  Taking forever and no one is in here but me. Extremely rude employees.  Won't be back to this location.

I'm really disappointed with my experience with this Starbucks location. I ordered ahead of time using my app. Estimated time was 4-9 minutes. I arrived in the drive thru in that time frame and was told that they were working on my order. No problem. There were about 3-4 cars ahead of me. I assumed it would be done by the time i got to the window. I get there and she again says they are working on my order. Next thing I see is them START my order of 4 drinks. By now, it's about 20 minutes since I submitted. I tried to get their attention dice at that point I didn't have time to wait for 4 drinks to be made and just wanted my order cancelled but no one came. Then she just handed me 4 drinks with no cardboard holder. I didn't want to be late for my kids' bus stop, so I left as soon as she gave me the drinks instead of asking for a holder. At that point I just wanted to get out of there. Of course, the drinks spilled without the holder. I only have 55 cents left on my card, and I'm done with Starbuck ls. I'll stick with other coffee shops that can actually give me drinks in a timely manner, and have people working there who will actually tell me when there's a delay, and use their brains to think someone might need a holder to carry 4 drinks.
They are fast and my order is never wrong. Very nice clean place to sit and eat or have your coffee there employees are very friendly.

Not my preferred Starbucks but it's a quick in and out thing. Location sucks personally, high traffic area, small parking lot. Sweet staff, quick and on top of things. Everything ordered is always made perfect. They always have my daughters cake pops in stock unlike the Irvington location.

Why in the world is this Starbucks closed 2 hours earlier then posted at the airport when it is busy? There is no sign and when people go to the counter they are waiting too long to then be told they are closed. No wonder they have a two star rating.

I am spending a few days at this Starbucks this week because of the free wifi.... great staff and super friendly... I am originally from Orange County, Ca and I have to say, I was not used to nice, and friendly staff at Starbucks. A big 2 thumbs up for all the staff here.

I have to downgrade them as a result of the past few years of service.  The drive thru has substantially slowed down. The past few visits, scattered across an assortment of times, have all resulted in extended wait times. When the lines are long outside i'm happy to get out of the car and go inside. Even inside seems to have taken quite awhile. The priority seems to go to the drive thru, though when you are in the drive thru it doesn't seem like that.  A recent order was requested with "no whip" and delivered to me with whipped cream on top. That easily doubled my wait time since I asked them to remake it.  I will give it an upgrade for last nights purchase. The wait time seemed far longer than the usual extended wait. When I drove up to the window to pay my drink was handed to me and the employee said "No charge since it took 17 minutes." Thank you for this freebie but I have sat for just as long before with no change in billing and usually an incorrectly made beverage.   I am sure these are a result of the surge in people in the area as of late but they should be adapting for that staffing wise. I tend to chose another location or another chain as a result of these issues.

I have been to this particular location 3 times in the last 3 weeks.  The service is usually slow and not terribly friendly but today was the worst yet.  The girl at the window never even considered smiling, she never spoke even when my order was wrong, and we didn't get napkins  Even though this is now the most convenient location, I will not go back.  Have to find someplace new.  I am a Starbucks Gold level member.
I haven't been to this Starbucks in two years... And I recently moved here. They're the slowest Starbucks ever and continually screw up your orders! They haven't changed in two years!!

This is not a "come hunker down and study for hours" type of location. It's very tiny and seating is limited. In fact, it's not even a good location if you want to hang with friends and catch up. This is really more of a "thank god there's a Starbucks in the neighborhood" location. However, I am thankful as it's quite convenient to my STL life.   Staff is incredibly friendly. More so than most locations - I have great conversations here. My orders are always perfect. And I love their tip jar, it's a poll of sorts, and always fun. Parking in the CWE is a pain, but there is street parking available, you might need to circle a bit.
this place is great however sometimes the line is ridiculous due to traffic. The parking lot is so small coming off the main road it's an accident waiting to happen

I love Starbucks. This location is the worst however. Slowest service. Negates the point of a drivethru and call ahead orders if it takes (my average) 10 minutes to get your order. A location by the turnpike/295 needs to be quicker. Constantly disappointed but still addicted.
Nice staff.  However, the bathrooms are always horrifically dirty and/or closed.  The store could really use an update on their furniture.  It's pretty worn.

There are a lot Starbucks around the area and this location has the worst customer service I've experienced. I'm not expecting to be blown away because it is just Starbucks but I also want to be treated with a little bit of respect and maybe get a smile but I guess that's too much to ask for at this location. They also seem stubborn about giving the discounts from the Starbucks app this is the second time I've had a run in with the rewards on my Starbucks application.

I guess it was ok, I am just use to the staff at my usual SB's remembering me. Spoiled yes. The staff was nice, and the coffee exceptional.  I paid with my iPhone app.  That's always fun. I will go back to my usual Starbucks from now on.

I do not recommend this Starbucks, unless you are visiting in the late morning or early afternoon. The drive thru only comfortably holds a queue of 2-3 cars, and then you are blocking the road around the shop. Service is slow, and the baristas are not as friendly. The attrition here seems high for baristas; maybe it is a rotational Starbucks for training baristas in the area? If you sit inside, it is quite small, but clean. I say here in the winter once, and because of the two entrances, and the small size, I froze my rear off with the cold wind blowing in every time the doors were opened.
This place has the worst parking, entrance & exit ~ they really need to move from this location

This Starbucks is one of my favorites  Cuz it's super close to my work.  I haven't got far to drive  When I need a little perk.   This location is quite large.  Though I rarely go inside.  I usually do the drive thru So I don't have to exit my ride.   Another reason I come here, They have the nitro cold brew coffee.  But you need to get your own.  You won't get even a sip off me.

A bit chilly, they definitely keep Starbucks well air-conditioned in this little spot close to 15th & Locust.  Service was prompt and efficient with the barista and the cashier relatively friendly.  Bathrooms were clean.  There were a couple nicer seats, but the seating in here leaves something to be desired with the little wooden tables & chairs; free AT&T wifi.

Very disrespectful Store Manager that goes by the name of Greg. Can't multi task at all to be a manager, poor manager levels.  Asked nicely to please hold a coffee mug purchase I was going to make for my Valentine's while I was going to get my wallet and he totally ignored me, hey man you look so miserable seems your working for free! Makes me  Feel sorry for the owners of the store you are making them loosing business due to your nasty values.

Holiday coffee beans are currently on sale, so my boyfriend and I decided to pop in and see what this location had in stock. Both of the baristas that were there were very helpful. One offered to check in the back because he had a feeling that what was on display was not everything that they had, and he was right! We ended up getting 2 bags of whole bean coffee and are looking forward to enjoying the Christmas and Holiday blends well into the new year. I would definitely return to this location when in the area!
No line, clean and efficient Starbucks. I find it spacious compared to what we have in NY and they also have a large parking.

I come through the drive-thru every morning for my trenta iced coffee double expresso. Matt is generally at this window. He is so nice, smiling and helps start my morning out right. I am moving to Alaska and will miss the morning Starbucks crew which have always made me smile.

I like this Starbucks sometimes and then others I don't. There are good days and bad, but there have been problems sometimes with orders and sometimes the customer service isn't up to par with the others that I have been to. A few times my girlfriend was given and attitude and one of the cashiers rolled their eyes at her with her snotty attitude. I haven't had many problems I stopped in today and the barista, who was a older gentleman, made my drink was very friendly and is always offering other items to go with my drink. My drinks were always made well and I never had a problem, I know some people have had problems with yeh cashiers and I can't say personally I have had a problem. Good place to stop and grab a coffee, certain people make drinks good and others have made them not so well, give them a shot, love to read reviews!

Staff were friendly , this location is One of 2 remaining Starbucks with a drive through in the area.  So dad that Most of the others are inside stores like Albertsons or Target now and that's super inconvenient when you have a car full of kids or running to work and need to grab a cup and go.  The only reason we stopped here was that we were at a neighborhood business. Cafe mojo was opening next week down the street. I ordered 2 drinks a tall pumpkin spice latte with an extra shot and a vanilla latte with 1/2 toffee nut syrup and 1//2 vanilla ,whip and caramel drizzle.  They charged me $1.00  extra for both drinks so just under $10  One drink was ready and was not full all the way and the other they forgot about so I reminded them and then I checked it and it was not made as requested. It was also sitting in their drive thru window line and I was inside? Not that big of a deal, but honestly they were not that busy !   I would've asked a customer standing waiting if they wanted something else or if they were waiting on something ? I left and we both tried our drinks ( my daughter who has been a Barrista) immediately said this is not even hot enough they must have someone there in training , what a big dissatisfaction and waste of $10 definitely not consistent with other locations.  You would think that due to so many of the locations closing and all the cute little boutique trendy hipsters coffee shops in this town that are booming, that the Starbucks crew would be on their game.

Wonderful Starbucks location. The staff is great and always friendly. I mostly go through the drive through and the employees are always sweet and funny. My favorite was when I asked if they made a different type of drink and the response was "heck ya, and it's super yummie" this response made my son and I giggle. Nice job!

Sunday's afternoon study hall people reading books not consuming anything. Others taking up table for 4 with their text books. Tough if you wanted to have a hot chocolate and a slice of pumpkin spice cake. Luck to have found table for two.

All the coffee-type goodness you would come to expect from a Starbucks in a convenient, just over the bridge into PA, conveniently trafficked, quaint house-type location.  Clean.  Coffee.  Friendly people.  Lots of places to sit inside and outside (big porch.)  It is not Dunkin Donuts.  All good things.    It can be tough to find a place to park (err it has been any time i'm ever there, i'm sure its much less of a hassle during a tourist lull) but if you head right after the bridge, you should be able to find an out-of-the-way street spot as long as you don't mind a little walk back.  For coffee, I do not mind.  During today's visit I was a little bummed that they didn't have a fresh vat of Christmas Blend (my favorite cold-weather coffee) that I could stick directly into a vein of my choosing, but I did manage to bold my day up with a bit of Italian Roast.  If you can get a seat outside, relax a bit, eat some Internet, and watch cars run the red light just after the bridge into town over the Delaware.  Also, if you are an avid collector (and an overall gold card carrying Starbucks addict) like myself, you can grab your 100% official Philadelphia Starbucks Mug (complete list below - yes, there is a list.) and be on your merry way.  Just be sure to actually visit Philly (I have, don't worry) so its real and actually counts.  -----  List!  http://en.wikibooks.org/wiki/Guide_to_Collectible_Mugs/Starbucks_City_Mugs#Countries_.26_Cities_Starbucks_Mug_Series

No ice in the ICED mocha expresso.  I swear it's warm, not even remotely cold.  To add insult to injury, they never called out my drink order once it was prepared.  Probably waited an extra 10 minutes twiddling my thumbs.    It's really shocking because no one else had ordered a drink after me and it was obvious that the drink order was mine.

We have been here multiple times between 9 and 10 pm when the app says they are open and they are closed or they shut off there lights when we are trying to open their doors or go through their drive through.  Please update your app and the hours on your door so that you close according to your hours.
Omg I love this location friendly staff, fast service, cleannnnn. Beautiful drinks are made here. Time and effort put into each one.

It's Starbucks. My default rating for fast food chains is 3 stars, and they didn't do anything special to rise above that. They didn't do anything bad enough to lose a star either, though I was seriously tempted to drop one for the lack of drive-thru. There is at least a parking lot though.
It isn't the best Starbucks shop but they make good coffee. I always get my order to go. They are fast most of the time. They get really busy in the morning especially the drive thru.

All Starbucks are the same. Lots of money for really strong coffee. I get the caramel frap light with soy and no caffeine and sugar free caramel while shelling over $6. What's the point?!  I would rather drink my shakeology and make it into my own at home frap!

I went on a sunday afternoon. Poor customer service for a neighbourhood coffee place that can attract regular customers. They didn't call out my order. It was left sitting on the not visible area of the pick-up counter for at least 10 minutes. The other coffee I ordered....they forgot to make it. The baristas didn't even apologize.   The vibe of a establishment is dependent on the vibe of the employees. At this Starbucks: No customer greeting, no music playing, and unkept creamer counter. It's a sad Starbucks.

My Xmas Starbucks always pay it forward during Xmas and love the seasonal drinks! Never a long line and drinks are always made quickly and correct which means delicious.  Never had a bad experience here  :)
The pumpkin spice latte is back! woohoo!  The only reason I ever go to Starbucks.    This bread called the Lemon Loaf is also one of the greatest things ever.

This Starbucks has been open for about two months now.  I really like the baristas in the mornings - they're great, and know me personally! And they move the line pretty darn fast.   But a lot of the products are still really inconsistent in taste; I've had iced passion teas that were basically Kool-Aid, and others that were basically water. Also, this location will take the Starbucks app for payment, but won't let you claim free drinks earned on your app. In addition, free refills aren't offered.  Because of Starbucks' obvious popularity with college students, there's not a lot of places to sit nearby, and even fewer with plugs. The Starbucks management team seems to be taking up two or three tables every time I stop by, so I have to assume they're working to get this store a little more on track.
I come here to do some work sometimes, and always leave disappointed. Because the internet is split between so many customers, it is very slow.

Very small and cramped.   Workers were slow and unfriendly.   No scanner to pay with your phone...she attempted to type in the correct info like 5 times so I could pay...   Wasn't too impressed.   My drink tastes good though hence the 3 stars.  I did see a really cool Starbucks just up the street though and it is in an awesome and authentic looking building.   So skip this one and go there!
CAPRICIA is the BEST she knows just what I'm going to order and sees me coming lol love her

I like.that they have added some things to their menu .The slow roasted ham and Swiss has.become my new favorite. Catherine the manager is so sweet and always on top of things. I usually get the same thing but from time to time i switch it up and recently tried the BlackBerry Mojito iced tea , boy was it refreshing . I'd also like to give special nod to Avery he's always so friendly and goes out of his way to be helpful. Great job dude :-)

I don't ever plan on writing reviews for franchises because essentially, you get the same experience as far as food/drink quality and service goes, especially when the aforementioned franchise is Starbucks. It's kind of impressive and scary how all Starbucks employees (regardless of their make, model, size, shape, sex, skin tone, etc etc) are almost Stepford-ish in how nice, considerate and generally chipper they are.   A secret (well not so secret anymore) way to test the general amiability of a Starbucks barista is to walk in, look stupefied, and then say the following words: "I... don't know what to get. Do you have any suggestions?" If they're good, they'll smile and give you some suggestions based on your tastes. If they're great, they'll do the same, but then offer to make you something else for free if you don't like what you bought. This is why I love Starbucks and am annoyed by people who hate it. Yes, they're a big faceless corporation, blah de blah, but they got so big because they make their customers feel like they're the only ones that matter for those brief two minutes. But I'm soapboxing now, so I'll cut it short.   This location rocks because I love how it feels like a library with a Starbucks attached. It reminds me of my undergrad days, and even simple things like the bricks in the ceiling, or how there is never a shortage of desks to lay out my books/laptop, or how all of the patrons are considerate and quiet, or even how this location is kind of set a little bit below ground making it feel all... cave-like; all these things just make me love spending time in here. Say hi if you see me. I'm the brown guy with the iDevice headphones on. There aren't many Indians in that location, I mean it's not like there's a really nice private university nearby with Indians all running amok. Oh wait... Ok fine, I'm the Indian guy always wearing flip flops; I know most Indians have an aversion to flip flops.

This new drive in location is beautiful with marble countertops and a sleek modern vibe throughout.   They do still seem to have some of the same issues as they did across the street where your order is not always correct and the employees are not friendly.  I ordered almond milk in my decaf and got regular. The employee who waited on me didn't say thank you or even look at me during our transaction. Not your typical Starbucks.

It's 3pm in the afternoon and the young moron working the counter tells me, they have ran out of freshly brewed coffee.   There is seven people working and they are all dimwits!   Just think this must the only Starbucks in the world that doesn't have any freshly brewed coffee in the middle of the day!  Unbelievable !
Nice roomy starbucks. Plenty of seating. Friendly service. Consistent drinks. Clean and tidy.

The baristas here are friendly enough.  The drinks taste the same to me at every Starbucks, so I'll leave the coffee reviews to other Yelpers.    There are two things that stand out to me about this location: 1) Because of the layout--with the counter in a separate room from most of the seating--you can camp out for hours and not leave reeking of coffee.  2) There are tons of electrical outlets.  I have yet to find another Starbucks in Philly that has both of these things going for it.

This is one of my top Starbucks in Boise. Sure, the parking situation is a nightmare, but the location of this place is convenient and the inside is spacious. It seems almost like it is an old converted Burger King with the indoor patio seating that the old locations used to have. It has more than enough tables and chairs and there is separation so that it doesn't get too loud. The outside area is non-smoking... but then again, all of Boise pretty much is. It is shaded though and away from the main street traffic, which is bueno. I love the location, the service, and the layout. Can't beat that :)
Every time I have tried to come get coffee they are closed. Closed on Sunday and only open until 3pm?  I get closing inside but why close drive thru??

So glad we finally have a drive thru Starbucks here in Santa Barbara, I always hated going inside and talking to the barista and waiting in line. Service used to be quick when this place first opened for the first month or so but it's gotten a lot busier since word got out. Had to wait 15 min in the drive thru line once. Not sure if there was some barista in training who was slow or it was short staffed but I was almost late to work, plus the lady in the car  in front of me decided to make the barista show her all the Starbucks merchandise.

After spending way too much money at Starbucks in my life, this is the one that has made me decide to drastically cut back.  After they made my $5 order wrong (pretty basic - mocha w skim milk), I got a condescending lecture about the Starbucks vocabulary and how to order properly so they dont get confused.   Or, I could just go just about anywhere else.

Open 24 hours unlike to locations on Kennedy and Westshore, but the parking is extremely limited and you're usually parking on a side street. Staff have always been nice to me, and this location is the most convenient to my home in VM Ybor and it's relatively quick and easy access off I-275.

I'm a Seattle girl and sorry to say even for as bad and most Starbucks service is in Reno this one was horrible. Under staffed and rude! I don't have 15min to wait when I'm second to order. She took 5 more orders before even starting my coffee. And her co-worker went on break instead of helping! Won't be returning here ever

This Starbucks is the absolute slowest coffee shop I have ever been to. I have used both the drive-thru and gone in multiple times. Expect to spend at least a half hour doing either. It is not that this Starbucks is anymore crowded than most others- they are just under-staffed, don't know how to quickly make the drinks, or simply slow moving. Considering this is a business-oriented location it's quite surprising.
Love the flavor of eveything they have it's so good and plus really nice people everything us clean and tou can relax

I really like this Starbucks because, when you take the fact that it is right two major highways and squeezed into the area that it is, the people working here are making it work impressively well. I come here often enough that my bad luck gives me a mismixed drink from time to time or a long wait, but it's never left uncorrected in a fair manner or apologized for in a genuine way. I've never sought out for perfection by a national chain, but even though this is probably one of the biggest of the national chains, I find myself impressed with how functional and efficient such a location is.

Every time I visit this Starbucks, I'm disappointed.  Today, it was so loud I couldn't read the newspaper.  Staff was vacuuming the food case - so they turned up the music to hear it - then had to speak to one another so loud that it was impossible to think.  This store needs new management.  I generally avoid it, but it was  the location I needed today.

I like to frequent this place whenever I'm on campus. I want to preface this review by saying that I generally don't like Starbucks coffee. That said, this place is solid. There is almost always a really long line, but the employees are very efficient in getting people through. I usually don't have to wait too long.   I'm typically disappointed in the coffee. I usually order a black coffee, which is just too bitter nine times out of ten. If you are into French/Italian roasts, you probably won't have a problem.   The atmosphere is very much a "get in and get out" kinda vibe. Definitely not a good place to sit and study, as the seating is really limited and very crammed. The plus is that the lagoon patio seating is adjacent and includes amazing views.

Don't usually drink this stuff but they have wine and beer too.  We got a glass of red wine.   I am only giving 4 stars service was great.  Staff was super friendly but the wine was bad.  I guess I shouldn't get wine from a coffee sho
No more 24hours???? This location is always the best bet when my gf craves for midnight coffee (weird habit but cute )

I have been going to this Starbucks for years and it used to be my favorite spot.  Over the past year I've witnessed the manager insult and degrade her employees on several occasions to the point where I've almost said something.  She isn't friendly to her customers nor was she at all helpful when I had my my computer cord stolen there a week ago.  The rest of the staff is fabulous but she's honestly made me change locations and take my money elsewhere.
The employees here are super helpful and friendly. I sat down to eat a PB&J box today and really enjoyed my down time before heading to my next appointment!

1st and foremost this is one of the most beautiful Starbucks, the ceiling is out of this world! We had received $5 coupons when we had checked into silver legacy, so our drinks were basically complementary. The line is always long, but the baristas make it go as fast as they can with out messing up an order. I wish this was my everyday Starbucks !
Twice going here for iced coffee it is either to sweet or not enough. It doesn't come out the same each time this pisses me off my coffee is as sweet as a soda this seams like it was on purpose

Very quick, friendly and prompt service.  Everyone greeted you by your name vs just calling out your drink.  Nice touch.  Disappointed I didn't get my gold card rewards and discounts though.  That will prevent me from visiting again
Always slow and today I saw one of the baristas bitch out a customer because she didn't like how he took his credit card back. The manager was standing two feet away, but ignored it.

Nice, clean and nice size Starbucks! Plenty of seating inside and out! Always a decent crowd. Plenty of parking with a short walk to the doors. This is one of my preferred Starbucks and when in that area I like to stop for a drink and that's where they lose that last star!! I wanted to try the new nitro cold brew and for a grande with a mummy cake pop for my daughter...$7.10!!!!! Seriously???!!! So I believe that put the nitro in the $5 something range!!!!! It was good but definitely won't be buying another one anytime soon!!!!
One of the best Starbucks in Delaware. The only downside is like many others I'll say it could use a good cleaning.

Every time I go to this Starbucks I leave feeling so happy. The baristas are great, always so nice and patient with my modified drink requests. My drinks are always made so perfectly. I was a partner and I became very picky about how my drinks need to be, every time I've gone anywhere but the one I worked at I end up a little disappointed with my drink, but not at this one, here I get a drink that is always just right, and the baristas always have the greatest attitudes.

I have an apartment almost next door, so I thought I'd go down for my birthday to get my free birthday coffee. Well, they don't accept Starbucks rewards... meaning no birthday coffee for me here. Also, the employees were not very nice, definitely not your regular Starbucks employee. I turned right around and walked out. Go 3 blocks down to the next Starbucks, where they accept rewards and the employees are awesome.
Friendly and fast staff. Location was clean and well maintained. Great service and I recommend this location at the mall

The baristas were extremely friendly. They even recommended me certain drinks but didn't recommend the most expensive ones. The service was quick but I wasn't cut off or anything. Great coffee experience

Up early without all the coffee fixings at the casa, sooooooo decided to walk the pooches to the only place open. It's kinda hilarious with the media attention it seemed natural. Cranberry scone on the gummy side but the coffee was absolutely necessary. I like the coffee here, not more than my soul but close. I'd have preferred a local joint but 6:30 am -this is where it's at. Plus they're also pissing off "christians" these days so there's that bonus.

I stopped in for a pound of coffee. Justin overheard me tell someone I would like a cappuccino and he said, "Well you've come to the right place!". I was skeptical because I have walked away from the last three cappuccinos I have ordered at Starbucks. I didn't think he could pull it off but I liked his enthusiasm so I ordered it anyway.  It was the best cappuccino I have ever had at Starbucks.
Extremely sweet baristas! This Starbucks is always so friendly and clean! Drinks are great too.

Eh, store manager didn't want to scan my Rewards so I could get points for two drinks or approximately a $12 transaction...the process felt rushed and disappointed with the customer service.   I got a Nitro Cold Brew w/a pump of Vanilla syrup. It was good - still sweeter than how I liked my coffee, but still enjoyable.  Honestly, wouldn't return and would hunt down the local coffee shop in downtown Safety Harbor vs. Coming here.  Customer service wasn't inviting so I'm def. Skipping as a result of my interaction...so disappointed the scanner wouldn't work to receive points or the fact the manager did the bare minimum to attempt to help me with the situation...No thanks!

Slower than slow today! Four people in the front. One mopping. One guy looking lost. Two other girls going back and forth getting stuff. Looked disorganized. 10 min to get a tall cafe Americano w steamed cream. I also didn't have anyone in front of me. Rational?? Couldn't tell ya.

I like this place. Have come here for years. They moved it a bit up tbe street and added a drive thru. Unfortunately, they haven't figured out how to manage the traffic from drivethru and walkin yet.  Always slow.

A Starbucks, is a Starbucks, is a Starbucks.... and this one is a Starbucks.  That doesn't say much, but simultaneously says it all.  I'm a purist with my coffee and always just order coffee.... no frills.  There should be a separate line for people like me who order, they turn and fill a cup and we are gone.... I don't even need to go to the separate counter to pick-up.  But I digress... this was coffee and it was good.

The service is super slow. They have like 4 ppl on the floor and only 1 person is taking orders. Baristas keep talking to each other when there is a line of like at least 3 people waiting for their drinks. Terrible
Consistently the longest wait times I've ever experienced for a coffee shop. I would go somewhere else if not for the lack of any other competition at the mall. Please increase your staffing.

Ordered a venti iced coffee, and a cup of water. The barista refused to give me tap water because they were "too busy" and "didn't have enough ice". Even when I said I didn't even need ice, just the water, the barista looked very upset and then finally gave me a small cup of water. Paying customers shouldn't have to negotiate for a cup of water.   Also they must've not been making the iced coffee on a regular basis because it tasted SUPER watery. I know this because I used to be a barista, you have to either make coffee and let it cool off, which is the slower method, or you make double strong coffee over ice so when it dilutes due to the ice you have a normal strength coffee. I'm pretty sure they were pouring their normal brew over ice and having dilute completely. Won't be returning here.

NEVER come here on a Monday Morning unless you want to be late for work.  I understand that yeah it's Monday morning and everybody still half-asleep but honestly it should not take over 20 minutes to make somebody an online order yet you want to go in there you're sitting there waiting still and then to the point where you have to ask the person where is my order and they just have no idea all rude saying what's up. Im never coming here again

I'm not big on the corporate chain thang, but I gotta give a shout out to this Starbucks. They are quick, kind, and the one mistake they made on a drink since I've been going there was in my favor. I ordered a tall and got a venti, and there's nothing wrong with that. I do think there are some great local java joints in Tucson, but when I need something consistent and a pleasant place to pop open the laptop, this location does the trick.

My girlfriend and I decided to come to this location to enjoy a strawberry refresher over the other locations because this location is usually so spot on.  However, we order two venti refresher just to be told that they are out of strawberry juice.  Ok that happens, I guess, so we change to passion tea when we are told that they are out of lemonade.  Seriously?!?!  We decide to do do the new cold brew iced coffee and what looks to be a chocolate scone.   I hand the heated pastry bag to my girlfriend sitting outside and ask the barista if milk comes in the coffee. She tells me know but that they could add half and half. I ask for a splash The barista hands me two drinks what look closer to white mochas than coffee with a splash. My girlfriend then notices that the barista gave us a chocolate croissant.  I went to point it out but the girl walked away as I was talking to her about the mistake. My girlfriend then noticed that the scone we wanted was no longer in the case.  We wouldn't have ordered food today, but the scone was something new for us to try.

It's been a long time since I had Starbucks. This location has lots of outdoor seating and so many people on their devices inside that it feels like a library (4:10pm Monday). More libraries would stay open if it weren't for fast wifi in Starbucks.I had to get off for this yelp review though (rarely can I acces this wonderworld on public wifi). Anywho, service was quick, my pumpkin spice frap was sweeter than I remember BUT the last time I had Starbucks was probably harvest/Halloween time last year. It tasted more like caramel than pumpkin spice. New state, new tatse buds I suppose. Worth the $, well air-conditioned inside, music I like played among the tapping and drink machines wirr-ing. Will return if in the area

Check under your lid before leaving, as the drinks are consistently underfilled here. This is not a complaint based on just one poor experience. I work nearby, so I've been here tons of times. I am sick and tired of paying for a grossly underfilled drink. The only reason I'm giving 2 stars instead of 1 is that the staff are friendly, and they maintain reasonable wait times despite frequent long lines. However, at the end of the day it's the product that counts, and it's insulting to not get sufficient bang for your buck.
Worst cup of coffee ever. Tastes like it sat in the pot for 6 hours. If you're going to have coffee on brew, please make sure it's fresh. This is gross.

We like coming to Starbucks.  We buy our coffee here for at home, so when we feel lazy, drive thru here we come.  Good service, helpful and inside always has a nice vibe to it.  Always great in our opinion.  And we love the dark roast blends. Can't remember what we bought last time, but its been our morning coffee.

Great service but my Carmel macchiato was very watery (they did replace it with no questions asked) only other problem is they are infested with ANTS!! Couldn't even sit at the table and enjoy my bagel without ants crawling everywhere.
The drinks were fine but all 4 of my sandwiches are cold in the middle. Not room temperature. Cold.

Easily the worst Starbucks on the planet. Takes forever to get your order fulfilled, they treat you without respect a customer should get. No one takes corrective action to improve service, no one cares.

So.... I waited to visit this location a couple of times before writing this review. I visited Saturday 9.14.19, Sunday 9.15.19 and today 9.16.19. I read the reviews  about how slow the store runs, how long the lines were, etc.   I love Starbucks and sometimes I feel that Yelpers give negative or bad reviews just because they have nothing better to do. (Most Yelpers who give bad reviews have lots of negative reviews on their page)  My first visit to this location was Saturday September 14, 2019. It was around 4pm and the store was crowded. There were at least SIX baristas behind the counter, but it just didn't seem like they were moving "fast enough" to accommodate the crowd of people wanting their Starbucks fix.   This visit, I purchased: a Treinta Iced Passion Tango Tea Lemonade, a Tall Salted Caramel Mocha Frappuccino and two Protein Boxes (PB&J on Wheat|Smoked Turkey). The barista prepared my tea as a Tall as opposed to a Treinta. It was horrible and I waited for such a long time, I thought I was going to miss my bus to work.   My second visit was Sunday September 15, 2019. After Saturday's visit, I was in and out simply because I didn't purchase a drink. There was still a crowd of people to wade through.  My third visit today was in the morning before the mall even opened. I was one of two customers in the store. The barista looked as if he was tired and really didn't want to be there. I purchased two Cheese Danishes and a Tall Iced Passion Tango Tea Lemonade. It took FOREVER!!! I actually missed my bus to go home.  It seems like there is no set time of day that will get in|out quickly (even if your purchase isn't complicated). You may want to visit this location if you are strolling around the mall and have the time and patience.    Also apparently this location is in the hiring process for a new store manager. Maybe they need someone to whip the employees into shape, elevate morale and create better customer service.

I love this Starbucks! All of the staff are very friendly, and one girl always makes suggestions and at least attempts to have a conversation with her guests. Today while ordering a Peppermint Mocha and a Pumpkin muffin she joked about how I can't make up my mind on which season I wanted. She told me how they only get the chocolate shavings for the pepmoch certain times of year, and if I wanted she would put cookie crumbles instead to give it a little extra something. Always very clean here. I'm a happy customer!

So i finally had the perfect breakfast sandwich.  Bacon Gouda..YUM!  So i was just going to have a bagel and I mentioned I would like to have the bread from the bacon gouda sandwich and put pb on it (smaller bread than a bagel).  I told her other Starbucks would leave the bacon not crispy and she said i can get it crispy for you so i told her okay.  The sandwich was fabulous and i wish all the other stores would go out of their way to offer crispy bacon when requested.  The others i have been to have said the sandwich is premade and they couldn't get the bacon any crispier.   Kudos to this one!!

This Starbucks is consistently great.  I registered a gift card online, so I get free soy and free syrup.  If you get a tall misto, your total is somewhere around $2 and it tastes just as good as a latte.  There are definitely ways to save at Starbucks.  A few weeks ago, they had a buy one get one free holiday drink promotion.  Anyway, my drinks are always consistently the right temperature, and every drink tastes like it is made from an experienced barista.  If you ask for a triple-shot venti peppermint white mocha and a scone, your order is going to be around $8, so be careful!  But the staff has always been friendly to me, and most of the time I am in and out within five minutes.

This location can be a little hard to find (2nd floor of the Hyatt Regency -- and it's a little bit of a maze up there) but they have fast service, good wifi, and lots of seating.  It gets busy when there are conventions at the hotel, but they keep up the pace and the line moves quickly.   Tables/chairs can occasionally be a little messy (a few crumbs) but not bad at all.  One of the best Starbucks I've been to!

Drop your (friend, sibling, loved one etc) off at work at 7:30am, drive around awhile and end here.    Order a chai latte (or your own poison), and a crazy good zucchini muffin.  Then, devour the muffin top and the rest, while picking out the walnut gems which is strangely like panning for gold.  Repeat when your budget allows.
Favorite starbucks and a new favorite drink.....green tea frappuccino with soy!!! Excellent!!

If you go on a cold day, prepare to drink your Latte 'iced'!  Everyone around me is trying to bundle up to keep from shaking.  The seating is also limited for such a busy location.  If you have anything more than a very brief meeting, find another cafe'.  Everything else is your typical Starbucks cookie cutter experience.

I love Starbucks but not enough to go to this particular shop again. First time I went the barista got annoyed  that I had three cards: an empty discount card, a gift card with some credit and my debit card to reload my gift card), then she asked me to step away and wait for my coffee. I went to the bathroom to wash my hands and thought I left my wedding band. When I inquired about my ring she said that they wouldn't keep a ring if it was left behind in the toilet!  Next week I came back for a latte and to reload my card. There was a woman behind me and when she placed the order the two baristas just kept chatting away for several minutes while I waited by the cash register. I turned around and headed towards the exit door then one of them says "did you need anything, sir.". I said "nope" and I heard her scoff at me. Like she was annoyed by my perceived rudeness.   I now only go to the one by Castleton, the staff is always friendly and it has a drive-through.
Just another one of these cookie cutter venues.  You ever been to one?  It's like all the others, which is a good thing about chain places...their consistency.
WiFi at this location is slower than a dial up connection for those who know what that is.

They can replace the furniture to remove a bunch of the comfortable chairs to replace them with tables, but can't bother to get an electrician to fix their lights that blink every 10 minutes or so and have for the months I've come here,

I don't usually go to Starbucks as they are usually loud and not the best place to meet up with people. But I did meet up with a friend several times here and it was actually nice. The variety of iced teas are good although they are more on the expensive side. The staff here was very friendly, keep up the great work.

So I absolutely love Starbucks. But let me tell you, I had the worst experience here. I asked for a refresher shaken with ice, but no ice. Literally when I got my drink it was super warm. And the baristas are not that friendly. It's really disappointing seeing the fact that I always go to Starbucks.

I usually have a good experience here, but yesterday when I tried to call in a traveler for today the phone just kept ringing and ringing! I've called before with no problems so I know the number is right, and I even waited and tried again a few minutes later, but still nothing. At least answer the phone and put me on hold while you finish up with the other customers.

I dropped into this Starbucks the other day at the request of a friend I was driving with. I don't usually stop into Starbucks, as I make my coffee at home. I decided rather than loiter while my friend got coffee that I'd grab a bag of their coffee and a breakfast sandwich to boot.
