When I handed the coffee to the cashier he was kind enough to ask me if I wanted it ground. Oops, good question, I didn't even notice it wasn't ground, and I sure don't have a grinder at home. That was a point right there, but then I ate the sandwich and it was surprisingly good!  The biggest surprise was the coffee once I brewed it! I got the medium "house" blend I believe, and I think I'll be buying it again.  This review is based on the quality of the food and the coffee, the cleanliness of the joint (OK, it just opened), and the friendly, helpful staff. You just found a new customer (at age 42 no less).
Repulsed that you allow your employees to work while they are sick. I will be staying away
Ordered a breve latte.  Got something else with half the cup filled with foam.  Staff was busy, but apparently too busy to fix my order.
Typical Starbucks. The line was wrapped all the way through the room but they got us through pretty quickly. Staff were very friendly.

As far as any Starbucks location goes - this is probably my favorite. The staff here are incredibly friendly and boy, are they fast! The store is usually fairly busy and parking isn't usually available unless you get there early in the day, which is fine if you work 9-5. The space is somewhat cramped, but better than it was prior to their renovation. Again - it's a Starbucks, but it has the best staff of any others I've visited in the past and better than others I visit out of convenience.
Friendly staff, quick delivery. I was here for several days at different times, never got dissapointed.

This is one of the absolute worst Starbucks! The employees are rude and condescending! They behave as if they are doing you a favor by taking your order! They pay absolutely no attention to your order and so therefore if I ever got an order correct on the first attempt, it may be a miracle! If they paid half as much attention to the customer as they do to each other's personal lives, then maybe they could get an order correct! I have unfortunately visited this store on more than a few occasions and am still waiting for them to fix the problems. Save yourself the aggravation and go to another location!

They really screwed this one up. I used to love coming here, maybe my favorite Starbucks. Unfortunately the management decided to fix something that wasn't broke.  Gone are the soft surfaces and inviting colors. Replacing then are hard cheap firnishings and a color scheme reminiscent of American Horror Story.  The last two times I went it took over 10 minutes to get my coffee. In four visits I have not been able to successfully connect to the Why-Try (Wi-Fi).  What a shame. Gotta find a new spot.
Prime Starbucks place. Just passing by but the coffee isn't less of what you expect at a Starbucks. Was kinda busy when we came but it was around traffic hour so it made sense.

I only have two stars because I love their coffee.   This location sucks!!!!!   They never, ever have enough people working.  I am a gold member and make online orders in which it tells you that the order will be ready in x amount of minutes.   I stand inside for an extra twenty minutes every time.  It is nuts and unacceptable.

If you've been to one Starbucks, you've pretty  much been to them all. That said this Starbucks has some of the friendliest staff, and plenty of room to sit and grab a drink, work on your computer, read a book or even people watch :)

Unhappy with this Starbucks as of late... They are under new management and they have for starters changed the hours. This use to be a 24/7 location and they are now only open til 2 . They often run out of different flavor syrups and pastries. The last 3 times I have gone they were out of at least one of the things I wanted. It is very frustrating..   I have 4 doggies and they luv puppacino time! The last time I went the attendant at the window was quite rude about fixing 2 for me. The babies always share and when I asked for 2 she just was very sharp with me, was not at all friendly.   This used to be my favorite Starbucks so the changes lately have been increasingly frustrating.

Today was not a good day. I waited at the drive thru at least 30 minutes. Ordered  a venti peppermint mocha Frappuccino..didn't taste peppermint at all..a little too watery and didn't get the "venti" amount. And my tuxedo mocha Frappuccino tasted just likes a regular coffee but a little bittered...very disappointed. One of the drink came out all dripping on the side of the cup and the guy handed to me didn't bother to either clam it up or tell me about it till it got all on my hand then offer me some napkins....

I'm grateful that this place has a drive-thru and for that reason I will be an occasional  customer.    However the drive-thru coffee is usually only lukewarm. I asked about that once and the barista told me it was because I added cream to the coffee.  Hahaha.  I get coffee from the Marlton Starbucks all the time and the coffee is always streaming hot,  with cream.  Oh well.....

I stop in here every morning before work (and have been doing it for about two years now).  The staff has always been nothing but friendly to me.  I have no idea how they're so perky so early in the morning, but it's refreshing.  I always get greeted by name and my drink is started as soon as they see me.  Overall, I have a great interaction every morning!

I am here probably more than I should be. It's just so close to my house and the coffee is always delicious. They kinda have a bad set up and the drive thru line sometimes goes into the street, but if you just park and run inside it's pretty quick. The staff is friendly and the store is clean. They have very clean bathrooms.

Been to this location twice now and I have overpaid for the wrong drink. I usually make chai lattes at home but I was running too late to make it so I decided to stop here on my way to work.  I asked for a venti chai latte with an added shot and they charged me for three shots, making it very expensive. Unfortunately, I didn't realize this mess up until I got to work. A couple days before i ordered a Starbucks double shot, which I was given the impression was an iced beverage but instead I got four shots of espresso and some milk. The people are nice, but then Again, ignorance is bliss. Please re-train your new people or quiz them on their drinks because clearly they just don't get it.
The worse tea I ever gotten from Starbucks. California Starbucks Riverside, California makes the best tea latte.

I'm extremely happy that this has opened in my neighborhood. The place is well laid out with a decent amount of sitting room. It has a community table that's perfect for bigger groups. There is an outdoor area to catch some rays as well  The staff here are great and are quick in getting drinks finished. Even thought the wifi can be a bit of a challenge sometimes, this is one of my regular hangouts.
This location is huge but it's maybe the most popular Starbucks study group locations for all of UCSB. We got our order quickly but seating was limited. Neat to see the college vibe again.

Again my favorite place for my carmel frappe. However I do not know why Starbucks builds one such small areas. This is on the corner of a busy intersections and again not nearly enough room for vehicles.
Why in the world they have three people taking orders and only one making coffee is beyond me. 20 people waiting for coffees and only one person trying to make them all Bad management.

Ok, I admit it, I'm a coffee snob.  What's worse is my favorite is Circle K coffee!  I'll drink Starbucks if I have to I just don't want to feel like a need a cigarette to take the edge off after.  Fortunately they have a nice selection of iced tea.  The staff at this one was on it and kept the place clean.  Free wifi also goes a long way for me.  Thanks Starbucks!

I love Starbucks. I always hit this location when I am in the area. They have a drive thru and have an outside sitting area. The location is clean, busy and easy to find. The parking lot can get crowded but overall it is not hard to get in and out of.  They always get my order correct and are friendly whether I go in the store or utilize the drive thru. The coffee is always fresh and the food choices are standard fare for Starbucks locations.

This is my go to Starbucks location in the area. Every staff member I have encountered is friendly. My order is always correct & freshly made. Mobile order is easy & efficient at this location. The drive thru can be long on some days/times but that's expected. There are 4 other locations within 5 miles but this is the best.

They serve coffee flavored water! Don't mess with me I am from NYC and need my caffeine! But they get 2 stars because their bathroom is the cleanest I have seen on the east coast! It seems the further you get away from NYC the cleaner the bathrooms!

This was my absolute favorite location. I used to live right around the corner, and although I ALWAYS change my order, people recognized my face and were always friendly. There really isn't a LOT to say in regards to anything exceptionally done in comparison to other locations, because hey, it's Starbucks - they want all locations to be as close to the same as possible. What makes a specific location of a chain better than others is the consistency, and the people.  Smiling faces, my drinks ALWAYS tasting the same, the store being clean, well stocked and taken care of... this location is on spot. They battle back and forth between one or two other stores for being the busiest in the district, so yeah, sometimes it can be packed (especially the drive through in the mornings). I'll keep stopping by whenever I'm on that side of town, though. They make me smile.
Amazing staff. Some of the baristas are still in high school, but you'd never know it based on their excellent customer service skills.

Stopped here for coffee on a Saturday afternoon. They had a long drive thru line so we went inside. Friendly, fast service! We sat on the patio to drink our coffee and could hear orders. They seemed to be out of a lot of non-coffee choices but were apologetic and no one was cross. Shortages are due to COVID but what isn't being blamed on COVID? We'll go back.
Ask for Kip at the drive thru.  He makes a fabulous cup of coffee!!!  I would know.  I go way too often.

I paid for two cheese danishes and was handed two paper sacks. I drove away and realized that one of the sacks was empty. How do you not know that something is empty. WTF??? Why would you hand me an empty paper bag?!

I love Starbucks and this is definitely one of the nicer ones I have been to in my travels. But the one thing I can say is that no matter how busy they are it always takes longer for me to get my drink here than at any other Starbucks. I'm just not sure why. Maybe it's just the mood I'm in today. Haha. Staff is super nice though.

This Starbucks inside of Safeway is quick, friendly, and has quite a bit of seating even though it's inside a grocery store. The seating is one long half booth/half chair seating, but a huge group of women seemed to be having a great time when we were there.   It was very convenient for the day we were having. We only had to make one stop on our way to a picnic. I got my frappuccino as well as a fruit tray and veggie tray all in one location.  My favorite drink was done just right and the baristas there were really pleasant.
Been here a few times, and every time I do it's consistent excellent customer service. It's the only Starbucks I like going too.

I love Starbucks, but this particular store was not very nice. They continued to remind the people in the coffee shop that they were closing soon, and before they were officially closed, a worker announced to the store that they were closed and kicked everybody out. I've worked in this type of industry before, and in my experience, good customer service should never include kicking customers out of the store before closing. You can come for the drinks, but I wouldn't come for the service.
This place has the best staff ever.  For a Starbucks they are top notch.  I would highly recommend a visit as they certainly know what they are doing.

This review is for this location and my experience for the last 2-3 years.  I probably went here 7-8 times throughout those years.   And every other time that I come here and order the "Blueberry" oatmeal. I get no blueberries. I usually order this when I go through the drive-thru.   My opinion on this: is that a supervisor or manager there told the employees that if they don't ask for blueberries again then not to give it to them. Because going through drive thru you don't check the bag. Going back to the store is now more of a hassle since you didn't want to go inside already.   This is foxy at best. Not cool. Starbucks doesn't need to save anymore money when they are charging through the roof for drinks already.   Come on guys. The oatmeal says "blueberry oatmeal" on the menu!

This is the best Starbucks ever! I love coming here, everyone always has a smile on their face. The employees always try to make sure everything is done efficiently and quickly. I was particularly impressed with the managers; any time something is messed up they do their best to fix it! I love this location!!!

I know this location is extremely busy in the mornings, but the barista today had no sense of urgency whatsoever. I waited inside for 12 minutes in addition to the 5 minute drive after mobile ordering my drink. This wouldn't be such a big deal except I witnessed NUMEROUS people who were standing in line receive their beverage before me (including a coworker). RIDICULOUS. The second I mobile order the drink should be equivalent to me standing in line and ordering it. Why are people who are ordering 10+minutes after me receiving their drinks first?  Also, the parking lot is a sh*tshow, but there is nothing they can really do about that....  Gave it three stars because I usually do not have any complaints.

Taken a serious dive in quality/consistency lately. Wait times have been longer, and have been more errors with drink orders. Today, went through the drive through, it was lunch time so I expected a bit of a wait.   Got to the speaker, was asked to wait a moment before placing my order. Sure, no problem. 10 minutes later, line in front of me is almost empty, still haven't heard back from the barista. So I call out, they apologize, says they didn't hear their headset beep.  It gets busy, things slip, understandable, I guess. Place my order, they repeat it back correctly. Get up to the window, wait a bit more.   I had 2 drinks. One hot, one iced. They made my iced one hot, and made the hot one a completely different drink. Waited more. At this point I've been in the drive through a good 25 minutes. Not even an apology, just an offer to keep the messed up drinks. Yeah no.   Think I'm just gonna go to the one on Disc from now on.

I've been meaning to change up my study spot.. so I found this Starbucks located off of Rt 73 on Fellowship Rd. There's really no other Starbucks nearby except the one in the Moorestown Mall or about a 10 minute drive to Marlton or Cherry Hill. There's also a limited amount of coffee shops, cafes, and potential study places in the area that have tables/seating and free wifi.   I came in on a weekday around 9am and the drive-thru line was through (and out of) the parking lot. Because of this I was scared there would be no spots left inside to study.. luckily, as I picked up my espresso, a small table with 2 seats opened. The study area was filled with people on laptops doing work.   I had an espresso and all the staff were friendly and welcoming. They did mess up my order slightly, but I was able to get it easily fixed. Just seems like the workers are in a rush (I don't blame them, they had soooo many customers in the drive thru).  If you're looking for a quiet place to get work done, this is not your place. The room fills with sounds from the kitchen, beeping, espresso making fizzing, and individuals on phone calls. This was not a problem for me as I work fine with background sounds. Most likely I will be back to this location since it has great hours and a clean study area.

The baristas here are great with the exception of one. My drink is always on point I can walk here from home, which counts as my exercise  newly remodeled, looks nice but not warm, cozy, and inviting like it use to be. Seating is very limited there's no comfy chairs anymore. Outside seating is good place for people watching. Definitely stop in for a good cup of joe but don't plan to stay, lol.

Being the only Starbucks within walking distance of the Williams Center, these folks stink at getting customers through the line and out the door in a timely fashion!  I just spent 12 of my 15 minute break waiting in a line that didn't move an inch! This is not the first time, they need more staff.

Literally no parking. Like ever. That's 3 times out of 3 times on the no parking. Waited 10 mins and parked in a non-spot in front of someone else who was in a non-spot. Villanova and Haverford haven't even started classes yet.   Literally sitting at this location right now. It feels like I'm in an igloo. It must be set to like 55 in here. I'm not joking.  Open til 11. What you'd expect for a Starbucks. Kind staff. Only place you can really do any work as most coffee shops in the area close around 8 or 9 (at the latest). So their closing time really is a big saving grace.  Points for: open late No points: no parking, it's like walking into an igloo.
This Starbucks is a gem. Inside the Bellevue. Beautiful decorative arts carved into the high ceilings. At a great location. If you happen to be in the theater area, recommend to stop in.

Yelp has not updated the hours for this location. I've submitted an edit to this, but for now here are the CORRECT hours I found for this location. Overall, good service from this location!   Monday 4:30AM-10PM Tuesday 4:30AM-10PM Wednesday  4:30AM-10PM Thursday 4:30AM-10PM Friday 4:30AM-10PM Saturday 5AM-10PM Sunday    5AM-10PM
Basic Starbucks experience. Nothing fancy. Had the regular items. Fairly small seating area. Had an outdoor seating but it was too cold to sit outside today. Friendly service.

This review has 4 stars because I like this Starbucks. It's generally very efficient, despite how busy they are, and the baristas are always polite and professional.   But this review is about a customer. A customer who made it very clear he would prefer the group of females working this morning to call him "friendly Freddie" going forward.   I was there this morning, Monday March 26 around 8 am. There were 4 females working, and the only other customer in line was this Mike Pence looking mofo that I now know is named Freddie. He was standing by the pastry case at first, just standing there eyeing a girl in the drive thru.   He gets to the register, where Mia is attempting to take his order, and he's still eyeing the drive thru, and seems to be asking Mia a question about Madelines, but when she tried to answer he said "oh I was asking Shelby, because she always knows." Literally called this girl Shelby over from the drive thru where she was helping another customer, to ask her where regular Madelines were. Guess where? Yeah, right in front of his freakin face at the register. And you bet your britches he asked her to reach over and grab it from the credenza for him.  By this point, I could not control the actions of my face and eyes, and I can tell the barista at the bar noticed these looks. After this guy was done paying at the register, he still lingered, of course so he could get a good view of Shelby in the drive thru. So I'm trying to take my order, and this guy is still in front of the register, which is now my personal space since Mia is looking at me directly, asking what I would like, and dude WOULD STILL NOT MOVE.   Then he continues on talking about himself and how nice he thinks he is and how everyone working there should refer to him as "Friendly Freddie" going forward.   The way he was acting was incredibly demeaning and uncomfortable to everyone around him. And he is clearly a regular.   The manager of this store needs to address this and let "Freddie" know that it's never okay to treat females as if they owe him something. It's never okay to talk to someone as if they don't exist, and it's not okay to go to the local Starbucks and sit at the bar so you can stare at someone for hours at a time.

So i was planning on going to Barnes & Noble on Dale Mabry, until i spotted this new Starbucks right next to the new Chipotle !! Whaaaat !? How awesome is that... if i get hungry after hours of studying i could just head next door !! So the parking area made me slightly claustrophobic... thank god i am a skilled driver (yeah right !!).  All kidding aside, If you came in from N Dale Mabry you get easy access to the angled parking spaces but if you come from Walnut St. you might encounter some difficulty. I don't know how they would have enough spaces for customers of both Chipotle and Starbucks on a busy day. I love the wall decorations and the different seating options they offer (from lounging spots, long counter height table, to 2 or 4 person seating). The inside looks bigger and more comfy than it appears to be from the outside. Initially, i was bummed about not having the long (non-counter height) table available to study, but the normal height 4 person seating was perfect as well. I sat with a gentleman who was also busy working on his laptop. The noise level was good for studying or working. What really made the difference were the employees that attended me. They did not seem like they were brown nosing, but like they were genuinely trying to help me out, especially when it had to do with the Starbucks Rewards program and Stars. Overall, i would definitely return (if i am ever in the area).

This is Starbucks so it isn't any fancier better or worse than any of the other company owned and operated shops. The baristas and staff are all awesome and amazing. This is the location right next to Dorignac's on Veterans at Wilshire. It is also a drive-thu so you may drive around and order and get it at the pick-up window if you're in a hurry and don't want to leave your vehicle. This is well outside of the touristy areas so you can deal with a normal and regular crowd of people. You'll enjoy this location!!!!

We will have to wait 25 minutes in the drive-through when there's not even a long line. Then when you go inside you got a wait 20 minutes as well. Are they stupid do they not understand the concept of drive-through?
Every time I go to this location they never have my mobile order ready always slow and the Fraps always look like mess. I wish I could post a pic of this sloppy mess

i am not even going to try this location anymore.  not sure about other times of the day, but midday they are the slowest staff i have ever seen.  i am not sure if it's a lack of help or if they just have zero sense of urgency.  i have walked out several times.  i'll take my business to the starbucks in the neshaminy mall--parking + entering/exiting the mall and i will still crush my wait time here.

Today you disappointed me starbucks. The staff is normally great and quick about getting everyones order ready. And with mobile ordering, it saves you time by avoiding the line! However, I didn't recognize the staff today and I received my coffee with blood all over the cup. NOT ok! For today's service I would give you zero stars but I'm giving a three because normally it is my go to location.

Late in the day business meeting. Plenty of space in this small venue for our group of 7. Slow service....I mean....s-l-o-w service with a barrista who was very congenial but kept forgetting what the orders were and/or for whom. Wrote the order on the cup, but not the name...and at times mixed the cups with another patron's order.  The Chili Mocha Frappucino (warm) was excellent. Pastries and other finger foods that might have helped our group a bit were sparse and stale.  Will return if I'm ever back in the area...but hope for better.

Very nice Starbucks in heart of Doylestown. This Starbucks sits in a historic building which adds to the cozy inside atmosphere complete with a fireplace. There is also outside seating overlooking downtown. The service is very good and I have been pleased. Parking can be tricky with a small public metered lot behind the building. There is plenty of street parking if you can find a spot.

Last time I stayed in this area for work (October 2011) this Starbucks wasn't here, so I was pretty damn happy to see it when I arrived yesterday.  The drive-thru was full this morning at 9, but they moved the line quickly and the barista working the window was super friendly. I'm a big fan of friendly baristas.

This is my morning go to coffee spot before the gym! Absolutely love James, he always has great customer service! However, the past few times I've been my coffee is half full! I always order a grande and it feels so light. I didn't take a single sip from this coffee and finally checked it this time. I don't want to sound rude at the drive thru asking for it to be a full cup  like I know there's space for whip, but my goodness!

The coffee here always hits the spot for me! Great service, love the vibe from the staff here. They try to get to know their customers which is a huge plus.  They have great expansive seating areas which is a huge advantage for the business folks and common get-togethers in this busy city. I love the decor here. See a couple of photos I've taken. They've put efforts to match the environment in here to that of the surrounding structures. Paoli has so many historical values and it's such a delight to see business portray some of the fabled history attached to the city.  The only downside I would say would be the Amtrak station next to the shop. It brings in a lot of traffic and significantly affects parking. That however shouldn't deter the genuine coffee lover and happy Starbucks fan from this gem of a place.

This is the slowest Starbucks drive thru. It's the closest to my home and it is always--literally always---a long wait. Even when I'm the second car, it's guaranteed a 10 minute wait at least. You can't get out of line either.
Your employees need to wash their aprons! Some are so disgusting. Otherwise service was okay.

Consistently I was getting a coffee cup that was filled up only three quarters of the way. Mind you, I never asked for any room for cream. Today I finally sucked it up and asked her to fill it up all the way. I was given the silent treatment. Baristas face said it all. Sorry, if I'm paying 2$ for a coffee I would like a full cup.
Fast and friendly Starbucks, better than some, on par with others.

Ben thank you for making our coffee experience so lovely .  I went for an iced caramel macchiato but sad no caramel but Ben suggested white mocha well we got that instead it was great thanks again for a perfect experience

If I could give an negative I would. Super long wait time in drive thru. Only to have my simple drink order made wrong 3 times. And the barista called me rude. Tossed my drink and a straw. And slammed the window. Not the only coffee shop in town. Dutch Bros here I come. Never going back to this location.

This is open for a while now and one thing they need to work on is maintaining the condiments counter.    I'm there often and almost always its out of supplies.   I'm also somewhat disappointed that they don't fill the coffee to the top...   Why should I get 3/4 cup of coffee?   Happened too many times to not say anything about ...

Bobby Barista, you think your funny but your comments are nothing short of being a douche. Customer asked a simple question "What year did this store open" it doesn't require 18 douche like responses. Thanks but no thanks  Philly Resident and Starbucks Client

Went in this morning to try the much anticipated Nitro cold brew coffee. This is the happiest place on earth . The crew there was. Genuinely happy and awesome to talk too. The drink was amazing too. Thanks guys for starting my morning in a great way !

Went in and they had no iced decaf and no apology. Okay. And they never have salt or pepper for their food items like the others do. Claim they order it but it just doesn't come in.Well, call corporate and solve the problem instead of just accepting it!

Any ol starbucks, but super busy with Jefferson students and doctors coming in and out all day long. Not really a place to study because people are always moving around, but a nice comfortable place to have coffee with a friend.

I tend not to frequent this Starbucks do to the horrendous parking, but I was in the mood for some Starbucks so I decided to stop off at this location.   First, as I mentioned before the parking here is bad. There are maybe 5-10 spots at the most and it results in chaos. I ended up parking across the street due to joe filled up the lot was.   While the parking here is horrendous, the staff and food are up to par.   I got an everything croissant, bacon Gouda breakfast sandwich and a strawberry smoothie. They also had a large variety of other baked goods in their case.   All my food came out fast and was very tasty. I loved the everything croissant. It was soft and had a variety of delicious toppings including sesame seeds, poppy seeds and some garlic. The smoothie was solid and on par with other Starbucks smoothies that I have had.  The staff here is very good as they are polite and professional. They also serve the food and drinks very fast. Mike was great!   This Starbucks also has a small courtyard with some tables in the back.   Altogether, this Starbucks has terrible parking, but the rest of the restaurant is fine.
Nice space in sub basement level. From outside, it looks tiny, but it's not.

I love this Starbucks. It's smack dab in the middle of the city and it's never crowded. The staff are always nice. There's a great view of Veterans Blvd. I decompress, sip my Pumpkin Spice White Chocolate Mocha and recharge for the next adventure.   This is a bustling business but the vibe is mellow. I dig it.

Quick service even though they were busy. A good amount of indoor seating. Got no problems with the drinks  They got a nice stained glass ceiling too and even though the Starbucks was in Silver Legacy casino, the cigarette smoke smell was not noticeable.   The best of the experience was the friendly service.

Be warned.  Starbucks on saint josephs university is money hungry.  They will over charge you for anything if they want.  Employees Always on the phone.  Not a real starbucks.  No customer service skills   Go to the other Starbucks on 46th and city Line Avenue or on City Avenue and monument rd.

In my quest to review something everywhere I travel, I had to include probably the coolest Starbucks location I've ever been to. It is in the lower level of what can only be described as one cool fucking historic building in the historic area of Philadelphia. The only downside is that the outlets are spread out and if you are in dire need of a charge things can be a bit difficult.

This one is a staple. Close to school, good hours, and plenty of seating. It's always filled with fellow students, and there's always a few cars in the drive through, a testament to it's popularity. All the employees (that I've interacted with) are very pleasant and the restrooms (for those that care) are well kept!

Like Daniel, I practically live at this Starbucks during the school year. But not just because it is across the street from school.  the pastry and sandwich cases are always well stocked, which is great for when I need a sugar rush with my caffeine fix. The staff are really wonderful--always fun, always friendly, and they always know what to make me.   And don't forget Emilie--the best, most funny, most friendly, most awesome barista ever!

It's Starbucks. What can you say about any Starbucks that hasn't been said before.  So, let me remind you:  First, no matter how long you are there, you're going to smell like roasted coffee when you leave. Not the good kind either.  Second, why is it sooo expensive!?  Third, they do offer sandwiches, wraps, and some other foods, and they are also overpriced. Lastly, there is hardly enough seating for the size of the place-- it's not very convenient if you just want to sit and chat.    The service was fine, and the tea & coffee my co-worker and I bought were fine, too.  I suggest supporting local coffee shops and businesses over Starbucks. But, I can't say anything terribly bad about this place. It's a Starbucks-- what else could you expect?
Our 'homebase' Starbucks, this is a great location.  The staff are all very friendly and just overall good people.  Definitely a fan all around.

This is the worst Starbucks in Reno. The " children" that work there have zero idea what customer service is. The drive thru is always a 20-25 min ordeal. If you have all day to waste, you should be ok going there. If you don't have the better part of your morning to spend waiting for your drink, go to a different Starbucks. I especially like when their attitude starts because the 12 year old taking orders can't hear you over the road noise in the drive thru. Should have thought about that one when you built it, oops! Probably just poor management. They have lost my business.

Starbucks GETS IT!!! So fresh & innovative in their business endeavors.    (this review is 60% about the app & 40% about the service, drink, store etc)   Hello, this mobile ordering is making my life all the easier!!!! Ya'll saw the line, I totally skipped... Bullying huh. Lol. I know, I'm not proud... But I was totally able to pull off a #stickandmove caper!!!   Picture it: early morning, I need a cup of Joe, but I ain't (yeah i said AIN'T) up for waiting. So I pull up the trusty Starbucks app, located the closest store, placed my order & pay, then... Boom, Bang, Bing, I pull up walk in & get my order!!! Helllllo am I happy camper? Heck yeah (in my Napoleon Dynamite voice) Annnnnnnnnnd on the way out, because this Starbucks app is so cool, I can tip the barista, even after my transaction is complete!!!! Way cool.  I found my skinny cinnamon latte to be great tasting & temperature appropriate; quite PERFECT.   Although the Barista & I didn't have much bonding time, the time I did spend with her, mainly her calling my name & me scooping my beverage.  She was pleasant, cheerful & engaging (she called my name correctly & with authority!!!!)   Clean store. Ample parking in this shared parking lot.   This Starbucks was VERY busy, long drive thru line, lots of folks hanging out indoors... The ordering capabilities of mobile app is sheer genius!!!

I moved to this area recently and this is the Starbucks I've come to visit most frequently. This morning I was there, sitting inside enjoying my iced coffee, when a very VERY disgruntled woman began berating one of the baristas about her tea. Normally, I would have just kept my head down and ignored her but this woman was ruthless. She insulted the barista, said things to make her feel small and unintelligent. Pretty much did everything she could to hurt the poor woman's feelings. I've worked in customer service for 5 years and I know what it's like to be treated like a servant when you're merely a "server". So, in case this lovely, hard-working barista ever checks her Starbucks reviews - please know that you're doing a wonderful job, and people like the woman have no business even being out in public, let alone interact with mankind... or animals for that matter. Thanks for all that you do.

I've been coming to this Starbucks since the day it opened. It's my favorite ('cuz it's in my neighborhood). Quality of product is standard. Service has always been good in my experience. Staff is pleasant and attentive.  Much better capacity since they ditched most of their kitsch. Recently they overhauled the furniture, I give that change a C+. However, if you have a group of 8-12, there are 3 big tables that will seat you comfortably.

Two to three times a day customer, I don't visit this location often. Every time I have come here they are out of the product I want. From berries in a drink to yogurt.  Staff is very professional and courteous,  management dropping the ball.

I really don't know where to start.  The service is bad and slow.  The last time i was there they completely forgot my order and I noticed after about 10 customers go by..  I understand mistakes happen so I go again today.  This time I looked to see who ordered before me and I thought  i will get my coffee after her, surely.  Nope, again about 7-10 customers get their coffee that came in after me. I  speak to a staff member and ask where my coffee is and he said it's coming.  He said they  make the brewed coffee first??????????? (I was having an iced Americano)  , so i point to a guy walking out with an iced drink and said , "what about him?  He just walked in and got a drink.. THE GUY SAID,  "WELL HE'S A REGULAR CUSTOMER!  ?????  REALLY?????    THIS IS THE WORST CUSTOMER SERVICE EVER!   It always has been.. Finally, another server, grabs my cup to make it,  stops , puts a pasty in the oven and walks in the back room and doesn't come out.  I had to ask again for my drink.  There are so many excellent Starbucks, that i can't understand why this one doesn't care about customer service.  I'll never go again..  too many excellent Starbucks.  The one on Edmondson Pike, the one on Nolensville Road and the one on Murfreesboro Road. .. all consistent in giving excellent service.

First mistake was obviously going to this Starbucks when there are plenty of awesome coffee shops within walking distance, but anyway....the service was the worst. No eye contact and made me act like I was a problem and a burden. Don't Easter your time   Take a nice downtown walk over to Park Ave coffee or drive to Kaldis Coffee

I usually come to a Starbucks for Frappucinos and the killer cranberry orange scones.  Thought I'd stretch my comfort zone a bit and try one of their refreshers - one of the citrusy ones, which they described as a type of a green tea.  I couldn't finish it.  It was horrible, so sugary and sweet that I almost spit it out immediately.  Me no likey.    Since this store is located right at the entrance of a busy street light, be VERY CAREFUL if you park immediately in front of the store.  People zoom in off Virginia Street and hang a quick right and may not see you if you are slowly backing out of your parking spot.

FIVE employees and took 15 min from the time we ordered one drink to receiving it and only 4 people/drinks ahead. Way too young without an adult supervising. They announced the drink wrong and laughed. While making it, heard the horrible saga of one of them not being invited to a boys party because he didn't like her. They were moving in slow gear while discussing such an important event. No tip for sure and not a supporter of Starbucks employees.
I've always had a great experience here. I like just a block away and they very friendly and quick with their service. There's one large table for bigger parties if that's a big deal for you.
Lines were long and it took a long time to get two ice teas.  One ice tea came and the other too another 5 mins.

I understand the need to train staff, but come on!!! There were two staff here at 5:30 in the evening with 3 people infront of me that took over 10 minutes. Orders took forever because beverages could not be made due to training on the register. I ordered a midnight mocha frap and asked for no whipped cream, yet got it with whipped cream. Simply from the unpleasant wait I left unhappy and didn't even have it remade. As I walked outside I sipped my frap to find out it did not even have any mint in it.  In addition.... there was NO PLACE TO SIT inside due to customers who had long been done there beverages hogging up 2  seat tables to surf the internet.    I'd expect much better at Starbucks!

Starbucks needs to rein in the jackass employees at this franchise. We've been in Nashville for months and we keep giving these bitchy baristas another chance.   They suck! The service, the attitudes and the orders always suck. They actually gave me crap for ordering too much in the drive through when their was no parking and no other options. I'm sorry to inconvenience you with my $50.00 order at your establishment.   These guys are aweful, rude and snippy.

This place is great, it gets busy at times, but there is usually still room for me to set me things down and do homework. It is conviniently close to the mall if you needed to do some shopping and the people here are friendly and humorous!

Good location with a friendly staff. The new store is great and the line moves fast. The coffee is always fresh and the condiments counter stays stocked. I would have given Starbucks 5 stars, but as long as they sell the La Boulange crap, i can't give them a perfect rating.

So I come here quite often and today I noticed on the menu that there was a new "Caramel Flan Latte" so I figured I'd try it. I ordered it and I overheard the barista telling the guy who rung me up that she doesn't know how to make it. The guy then asks another employee if she knew whether or not they had the recipe...Meanwhile I'm asking myself, "Am I at Starbucks or the local 7-11?!" To make a long story short, I settled for my usual order and they seemed rather relieved. I was tempted to give them the suggestion of taking it off of their menu since no one knew how to make it. I'm shocked that Starbucks would allow this amateur move for themselves and their reputation.
The sorriest drive through wait times!!  22 minutes folks with no way to get out, it was the Starbucks Coffee black hole preventing on-time arrival to work.

We are everyday regulars at different Starbucks throughout the Tampa Bay Area. Today we arrived at this starbucks and was one of 3 guests in the entire store, and the only guest in line. There were 6 employees behind the counter, doing various tasks. The ONE task the customer pays them to do however, they did not. And that is acknowledge us and take our order. We stood there like idiots for upwards of 3-4 minutes before anyone even looked at us or took our order. Then, and elderly gentleman behind us attempted at a refill, and stood there for close to a minute before anyone assisted him. Unreal

I love this Starbucks. It's close to UCSB, and it has an abundant amount of outlets to plug my chargers into when I'm there for long study sessions. If you're at the large wooden table, look under the table for a long power strip, you can't miss it! I prefer this location as opposed to the one in Isla Vista for their fast and friendly service, and their amenities like a lot of tables and power strips- that are perfect for students like me.
Be careful! I got robbed here! Don't leave anything when you go to restroom. Starbucks does not help you at all

I've been to Starbucks around the country and the world and have not experienced such horrible service as I did here. After I ordered my drink, I asked if I could get a copy of my receipt and waited there for two minutes. The person who took my order seemed to hear me but stayed in the back and ignored me and the people behind me who were still waiting to order. The barista who made my drink gave me a chile mocha instead of the salted caramel hot chocolate I ordered. When I told her she got my order wrong, she gave me attitude. She then handed me a "new" drink that tasted like a chile mocha with caramel syrup mixed in. To make things worse, the baristas were loudly discussing making drinks for themselves even though the line was backed up, and spoke negatively about the customers.

Super friendly crew, the baristas seem like they have fun being there. VERY busy store but communicate with each other well. Although they're always busy no matter what time you go they always makes my drink right, in and out quickly.
Hey this is a Starbucks. The counter girl was completely cute and very pleasant. Barrista likewise. I try and do local but sometimes Starbucks is the only option. Got my latte fix in rural PA.

I like this Starbucks because of its location inside Silver Legacy.  Being between El Dorado and Circus Circus, it's a nice break away from all the gaming machines and tables.  The baristas aren't that friendly. And the coffee drinks aren't as consistent as expected. To top it off, the prices are higher because it's inside a casino. Yet, I still frequent this Starbucks because I haven't found a great cup of coffee from any casino coffee place.

Starbucks on the WashU Danforth Campus (Bauer Hall-the business school). This means a few things:  1) Changing hours based on the school calendar. When school is not in session, it may be closed or open for a short amount of time. 2) Unpredictable hours. If the school has a special event, it may be closed. 3) lack of seating space during peak hours like lunch time. Though there are a lot of tables, it shares the space with the b-school cafe, which has a lot of undergraduate and graduate students there all the time 4) you can use the app to pay, but can't redeem rewards or order ahead of time for pickup 5) wonderful staff!

This is a fairly new Starbucks location. I frequent it often as there is never a long line or fully occupied. The location fits perfectly in front of the Icon building of the Gulch. Parking is usually easy as you can park on the street directly in front of the location depending the time you visit. The location is very clean and the baristas are friendly and helpful.
Nice folks- small place- parking street. Clean restrooms and in very nice area- across from The Chess Club

I once saw a hipster and a soccer mom go at it in the parking lot. Hipster won because 1. Better use of swears and 2. Soccer mom really was going the wrong [redacted] way. Over-caffeinated in a small parking lot - it was inevitable.

You probably wonder what could possibly make the difference between one Starbucks from the next?   The difference is the employees. The manager, Dave, knows my name and remembers my order as do most of the employees there. The products are consistent with Starbucks, but these employees truly care about the product, which they all realize is not just coffee or espresso. These employees all recognize that the product is the service provided.   Dave, as a manager, built a team of who are hardworking, happy to be there, and take the time to get things right. Usually, the worst moments at a Starbucks are the confrontations between employee and disgruntled customer whose drink was made wrong. I've seen and heard employees make sure the drink they made was exactly how the customer wanted it. If it was wrong, the drinks were remade with a smile. There have been times where I've felt secondhand frustration from the situations unfolding, but the baristas worked hard and with a smile.   I've seen these employees cleaning constantly to ensure a positive atmosphere. I've also seen moments where the team as a whole worked so effectively and efficiently that they had a moment to converse and the topics were always lighthearted and filled with laughter, another sign of a cohesive team. The store engages the community as well, with things like a toy drive, clothing drive and other activities.   This Starbucks is what a Starbucks should strive for; a welcoming environment that ensures your product is exactly how you want it.
Love stopping here. Gary and the rest of the staff are so kind and my coffee is always perfect. Awesome for such a busy spot.
Spacious yet cozy Starbuck with mostly window seatings allows you to enjoy your coffee, contemplating on life, and people watch.

Worst Starbucks I've very been too. I worked as a  barista for Starbucks for 3 years and I've never seen someone with such poor costumer service. The manager named Ally, gave me my latte with no foam and then when I asked for extra foam she threw out my entire drink and insisted on giving me a Cappuccino. While I was arguing that I did not want a Cappuccino but just a regular latte with foam, she proceeded to finish making me the Cappuccino. Only to throw that out and then make the latte (again).  Waited so long to get the drink, had to make a scene, just because I wanted a latte with foam. If I could leave 0 stars then I would.

It's really hard for me to rate a Starbucks.  I feel like if you've seen one, you've seen them all.  They have a very uniform feel.  This location I must mention is one of the larger ones, with amble tables and chairs for those lazy days where you just want to hangout in a coffeehouse.    What I am a fan of at this location is their staff.  I walked in this morning half in a daze with a million thoughts running through my head.  Just kind of going through the motions without any emotion.  I had a rough night, refer to my Animal Emergency Clinic review.  I just needed something special, like a Chai Latte to pick me up and a chance to get some fresh air outside of the office.    The barista/cashier who took my order was very nice and despite my half-baked demeanor, she asked me how my day was going and if a sweet treat from their bakery case might help pick my day up.  Which took me by surprise and kinda snapped me out of it and into the actual moment.  I was glad to be brought back into the here and now.  I did take a gander at their selection.  While their pastries looked very good, especially their bite sized petites.  Their English muffin sandwiches did not.  They were just sitting there, fully made and probably cold.  Everyone working behind the counter was very friendly.  I heard many cheerful Hello's, Thank You's and Enjoy Your Day's from the staff in the short time I was there.    I left with a smile on my face and a hot little Chai Latte in my hand that most definitely picked my day up.

What exactly makes a "great" Starbucks? They're all the same to me; shitty music, hipster and Mac laptops. 4 stars for a really good triple dirty chai, a fresh bagel, lightning fast service, and friendly staff.

It was chilly and I needed a warm beverage for the wifey and I. The baristas here are always polite and efficient. I appreciate that, I want to be in and out quickly. The barista chatted with me while making the drinks and was genuinely nice. I always appreciate that good human interaction that seems to happen quite infrequently now. The drinks were made correctly, tasted perfect, and were done quickly.   Beware of the tiny lot, it is hard to get in and out of here if it is full.

Indie hangout and gathering place for hundreds of delinquent teens on a Friday or Saturday. The time I went there I was on a bike ride through Bucks county and was clad in bright blue spandex racing kit. I was in queue waiting and the guy in front of me was paying for a few coffees with several methods of payment: IE starbucks card, cash and credit card-just for a $20.00 tab. I was patient and when my turn was up next, the barrista (coffee waitress) looked at me and went to the person in back of me and said: "your usual Fran?" It was like I was invisible..I doubt that, as I was wearing a bright blue uniform. I complained to the manager and didn't even get a free cup of coffee.  The barristas are pretentious and only seem to want to wait on regulars. I intentionally ask for a "small" or a "large" instead of using their terminology of "vente" and "grande." With the cadre of teens on a weekend night, I avoid the place.

I stopped by here since I was really craving some iced coffee with the sweet vanilla cream and the Starbucks at the mall ran out! This Starbucks was pretty standard. I can't not love a place that always is air conditioned, has good drinks, and is ideal for getting work done or just chatting with friends. Service here was always great!
Speedy drive-thru, friendly service, ample seating.  Parking lot can be a bit of a challenge as it's located in a small strip mall with limited parking.
Starbucks is okay coffee. I much prefer going to local coffee shops over chains. I don't mind their white chocolate mocha but a good local coffee will beat Starbucks any day of the week.

I'm writing a review here based off of the people that work here at the Starbucks inside st. Clair square mall! First off there are some very friendly people that work here that actually know me by name because I use to be a regular. Things have changed within the past few years. For the better and for the bad. There use to be an older female manager that was very rude and she seemed very snarky. She then left and an old guy with white hair become the manager or a temp. or something. He's the most disrespectful and rude barista I've ever encountered in Starbucks history! He doesn't care about his employees and doesn't help them out at all. I was once there and there were about 10 people in line and only one girl making drinks. The manager just sitting in the back being mad at the world. I gave this place place a 2 star because there are still friendly, cheerful people working at this location. There's also a new guy that started...beware his attitude is very arrogant.
Love this starbucks. Everyone is usually always nice and polite... Thumbs up from a true coffee drinker :)
Best baristos and baristas out there. Amazing service !!!

Echoing Kaela V's review: fast and friendly service. Nothing special stood out from one Starbucks to another but it was a positive and prompt experience.   This Starbucks is located in the lower level of the Marriott Downtown which is popularly used for conferences and attached to the convention center via Skywalk. You'll also find the Champions restaurant in the lower level of this Marriott. As noted by Phil, they offer a full menu of pastries, healthy breakfast/lunch options as well as non-coffee beverages like smoothies and tea. If you find yourself biking or walking along the Cultural Trail, this Starbucks is only one block away and also close to the White River State Park (Indiana State Museum, Eiteljorg Museum, NCAA Hall of Champions, etc.).

From out of town and stopped in for a coffee. The barista Sydney, I believe, at the cashier did not greet us even when my five year old nephew said hello. He just annoyingly smiled at my nephew. Then for some odd reason the jerk off kept staring at me like I killed his first born. Wtf? He was very close to me snapping on him. Not to mention I'm pretty sure he put regular milk in my coffee instead of soy even though I made sure to ask when I didn't see the soy container on the counter. My stomach is all types of tore up. What an a-hole.

I've had better service at any DMV. Posted hours claimed that they open up at 430a but there were two very leisurely employees milling about 'preparing' to open until about 450a. Once they did open (to a crowd of 6 people anxiously awaiting their lattes) the expresso maker was broken. Instead of having an ounce of sympathy or customer service the two employees proceeded to shame anyone who inquired as to what the issue was. Horrendously poor exposure for Starbucks.
It was ok.  Big stuff f God did so discuss icy rigidity d do d TV etc day has u do odd Suzy trust
What's not to love about Starbucks!  Love their frappé in the summer months! Great location, plenty is seating inside and out.  Great place to hang with friends and visit.
Nice Big tables to Study or Work. Staff is nice and very helpful. Outside seating area to sit in aswell.
Slowest starbucks drive through I've ever experienced. I will happily drive 3 miles out of my way to go to another Starbucks. I save time and sanity.

So this review is actually just for one single time that I went to this location... Normally I would give this location probably three or four stars, but today was amazing. The girl that served me at the window in drive-through was so awesome. Her name is Madison, and she lowkey got a lot of information about my current life situation so thank you for putting up with that lol. She was super sweet and said we should be best friends, and I agreed, but unfortunately I don't go to Starbucks enough to see her probably anytime soon so that kind of sucks ahahaha anyway...  thank you, Madison, for being so kind and for giving me a pup cup.

My home Starbucks. The parking isn't great and the dining area is small but the people are extra friendly. All of the starbucks staples and the bonus is the staff who know me by name and my drink when I walk in the door. One of my favorite spots for a small meet up for coffee.

Make sure you order your coffee drinks non-stop or to-go otherwise they'll send security to ask you to leave or order more from the workers who spend their time talking about their teenage personal lives out loud and cussing non-stop. Either that or they just judge people based off of their appearance. Either way, not the usual Starbucks customer service found elsewhere. Also, they charge for refills regardless of your rewards status. Just go elsewhere!!!!!

I love all the people here, they really make everyone feel like family. I drive out of my way to order from this Starbucks because of their efficiency and hospitality. Great place to study, mingle and have your favorite cup! 8)
They have the best chicken salad sandwich, and there salted Caramel hot chocolate is great.... Also the mango green tea is delicious

This particular Starbucks is a bit hidden and hard to find if your not familiar with the area.   Theres a lot of traffic that comes through this plaza, because of the Starbucks.   This entire plaza is newly built and I'm glad they decided to put in a Starbucks.   This Starbucks, might just have the friendliest staff I've ever encountered.  I came in yesterday and today and both visits were awesome! The cashiers were super friendly and warm. The baristas made drinks the way I asked and served them up quickly.   What more can you ask for? I'm thrilled and excited to deal with people who seem to genuinely want to serve their customers. It's great and I'll be making my way back tomorrow!   Keep up the good work guys!!!

Every time I go here they made my order with sugar and claim that they don't add any. I'm very very sensitive to sugar and ask every time. I've gotten it from other starbucks correctly. Here I always have trouble! The blonde woman is always rude to me also

This is my favorite Starbucks out of all the Reno locations. Besides being the closest, it's also open the latest. It used to be open all night then got changed to 11pm, not bad considering most Starbucks locations around are only open until 7,8, or 9pm. The baristas are friendly too! As usual, like all Starbucks-they'll make it right if your food or drink doesn't taste right. If not, a quick call to their Customer Service will solve your issue(s)

This is the worst Starbucks I have ever been to. It was absolutely dirty; there were crumbs everywhere, and the tables and floors were very greasy. Also, there isnt much seating, the layout wasn't done very well and makes the space seem a lot smaller than it really is.

Love this Starbucks for the following reasons:  (don't worry Chestnut Hill Coffee Co- I still love you too). 1. Super Cozy 2. Usually enough seating (but seriously girl on the laptop- it's time you hit the road and give me that awesome chair). 3. Clover Machine (for any of you coffee snobs- you must try this.  You will have another fabulous reason to be a coffee snob) 4. Always a dog chillin' outside with the peeps 5. Friendly service 6. Have always had what I wanted (ie what is on their menu- reference my center city Starbuck's review...) 7. Parking out back/on street 8. Great places to walk with your Starbuck's cup up & down Germantown 9. Cool artwork is pretty much always on display  Thing to Work on...  10. Fireplace- duh!  :)

I am so sick and the only card I have was on my cell phone and it doesn't scan. It has the numbers and the security code on the back. Update your system it's 2019 I am so sick and needed a medicine ball. Thanks for making me wait 29 minutes to get to the register and then tell me I can't order because your computer doesn't allow you to type in a credit card. Horrible

The change they made with staffs are the best that happened since my last review. They are more knowledgeable and more in tune with the upkeep of this place. Baristas are a lot quicker especially during busy hours. So far they haven't messed up my order (not like before) since I am attending classes on base I am more frequent here so I can really tell the changes they've made and i hope this will continue.

Standard Starbucks. Good amount of parking. They have a decent amount of outdoor seating and smoking is no longer allowed outside. Seating inside is also good. The staff is very friendly and there is an open mic night every Thursday if you're into that.

This is the classic traditional Starbucks set-up; it's relaxed set up with comfortable chairs, a fireplace and of course, excellent coffee is exactly the business model that the founders had in mind and the on-going success of this coffee spot while the others in town have come and gone (Bucks County Coffee, Coffee and Cream, etc.) speaks to it's perfect location and accessibility.  I see people complaining about the kids who hang out there (there's really nowhere else in Doylestown for kids to go ... lighten up) and the "dimly lit" interior (it's supposed to be like someone's comfortable living space, not a KMART) but all in all this place is great.  I go there frequently.

Located on the intersection at Magazine and Washington is this great Starbucks location. Being on the intersection, there are plenty of parking options available through out your visit there.   The line is always 10+ people long, no matter what time I go but they usually manage to go through it pretty quickly, which is nice. On my last visit, I wanted to try the Gingerbread Latte and I had a free birthday drink, so when I placed my order I requested both of those, the woman who took my order "okayed" it and along I went. I paid with a different guy who ended up charging me for the drink which was supposed to free, I explained that I told the other woman it was meant to be the free birthday special but he just laughed and said "you have to tell me, not her". Well, she's the one that took my order so she should've been the one to put me through the register too. So that was annoying, they just had no communication between the staff members and how he shrugged it off was irritating.   Anyways, I got my Gingerbread Latte and took a seat. Now this place is always busy so don't expect your ideal seating if you come too late in the day. I ended up moving three times as new, more preferred places to sit became available. The downside to the seating is that there aren't many powerpoint available and Starbucks has to know that people come to work here while they drink their coffee. Not a clever move.   The Gingerbread Latte was just okay. It didn't taste like gingerbread at all so I was pretty disappointed but I managed to finish it off despite the lack of taste.   There is a wide variety of seating inside with wall seating, tabled seating, and a few couch seating, which is nice. There is also outdoor seating but during this weather, it isn't recommended but would give you views of the street which is a nice distraction from the hustle and bustle from inside.   Overall, a beautiful location but the lack of communication and consideration the staff have between themselves and the customers made the visit spoiled.
Always great to find a SB close by when I'm traveling. This location has a drive thur which I like.
I've had this Starbucks a coupleof times and its okay but my co-workers just got back from their daily trip and said this one raised their prices by a lot.

I haven't figured out why this Starbucks isn't rated very well. Now that I've been a dozen times to this particular location I think I can safely say it's met my needs just fine!   I have the mobile app and am always getting different offers to try new things and I generally go for them unless I'm just trying to take a quick shot of espresso and get on with my day. The various offers on the app are to try new things and you're rewarded bonus stars which can later be redeemed for food and drinks. Trying new things and earning free things is a win win for me. Thanks to the offers I've discovered some teavana tea flavors I really like (peach green tea and mango black tea) and the new almond milk and coconut milk macchiatos are fantastic. The pink drink with coconut milk and strawberry acai refresher is a little sweet but delicious and just the right amount of caffeine for me before exercising. I drank one about an hour before a long bike ride and it was the perfect extra boost. The sandwiches are also delicious in the morning when you're in the mood for a quick and hot most-important-meal-of-the-day.   Biggest plus to me is I live within walking distance and I order it on the app and walk up straight to the mobile orders and grab my stuff and am on my way! Blows my mind more people don't do that.   The place is usually pretty packed inside and relatively packed outside so I can see if you're looking for a place to plug-in and work for a while it's not ideal. Possibly hard at times to find work space I imagine in the busier hours.   Once in a blue a milk or something will be out in the little drink prep area and you'll have to wave an employee down to refill but who really gets bent out of shape about that? It's a busy place.   For me the ease of ordering and quality of what I get is all I care about and this location has done good by me!
Cool employees that work quickly and efficiently. Definitely one of the best Starbuck's I've been to.

I have been in this starbucks hundreds of times and not once have they made the slightest mistake.   I cheat on them sometimes with other starbucks around town and eventually return back to them because no other starbucks has such a perfect record of service. Your drink and bill are always correct. The seating is comfortable with plenty of outlets for your laptop.   There is an utlta, petstore, and total wine in the same complex  and a home depot and target across the stress so a trip is here always a productive one.
This is the slowest drive thru of my life .... I know Starbucks are slow but this is horrible.  I was going to give them a 1 but I am being generous

This Starbucks isn't great, but it's not bad at all.   This location has a fairly large parking lot if you play in the back parking. This store has a few spots where you can eat outside. However, they are almost always taken during the summer.   This store is not small, but it is laid out awkwardly. It pretty much goes vertically. Anyway this location is almost always packed, but the staff does a great job of avoiding long lines and holdups for drinks.   The seating arrangements can be difficult as they are usually taken. However, with a little searching you can find some seating accommodations. They also have some very comfortable seats located by their Yuletide style fireplaces. I wonder if it is one of those Amish fireplaces. They also have newspapers for sale as well as the Starbuck's coffee machines.  Finally, the store has a "Bala Cynwyd Happenings" board. There people post local events going on.   The staff here is pretty good and keep everything moving. The drinks they make are the usual for Starbucks. However, one staff member seems to have an attitude problem.   A nice inclusion is that they have Kosher options.   I have being going to this location for years, I remember that nearly 10 years ago there was a large fire. The whole store burnt down.  However, this place is back and I will return.

This is an above average Starbucks mostly for its location and its indoor and outdoor seating area. Parking can be tricky around here especially at night and weekends. This is a popular area.  This coffee shop can get crowded but I have never had to wait very long in a line here nor have I waited long for my drinks.   Enjoying Starbucks outside at this location usually provides some great people watching.

It's 3pm in the afternoon and the young moron working the counter tells me, they have ran out of freshly brewed coffee.   There is seven people working and they are all dimwits!   Just think this must the only Starbucks in the world that doesn't have any freshly brewed coffee in the middle of the day!  Unbelievable !  Big change!  Ok so the place looks great, the new design looks amazing and more functional and the staff seems to be nicer and orders are correct and several times I have stoped for a coffee in the afternoon and they have hot fresh coffee.  Total improvement!

Never had an issue with this at any other Starbucks.  Triple. Grande. Soy. Latte. 2 pumps of chai.  Said this very slowly to the cashier at the register, each item showed up on the screen so I know she heard me and she's charging me for it. She casually leans over to the barista and says, you hear that? Barista nods, cashier just hands unmarked cup over to barista.  I take one taste of this drink, there's no soy, there's no chai, at least they got the triple part right.  But there was no-one else in line, there wasn't a rush. I have no sympathy for laziness, especially when it messes up my morning routine.
Very fast and friendly service. I go here often. The only draw back sometimes is the parking but that isn't their fault.

How long does it take to make a bagel? I ordered in the line? And now my frap is melting because they had me pull over to the front. I didn't even get curbside service even though I clearly wanted drive thru service since that's where I placed my order. I got out of my car twice in this heat while waiting. Ridiculous guys. Wtf.

$5.30 for my small caramel frap.. I need coffee hoping to use my treat receipt Starbucks currently promoted after 2pm special price $2.50, they said nope can't use the receipt plus store own by casino they can make up whatever price they want.... Wat da heck....so expensive

05:00 am, the Barristas seemed harassed and unwelcoming. I'll never ever go to this place ever!! I waited 17 minutes until the opening time, no smile, no thank you no nothing. Gimme your money, get your coffee and just go

My green tea infusion was good.  But this is the dirtiest Starbucks I've ever been in.  All surfaces look like they hadn't been wiped down for a couple days.  The floor was nasty and had debris everywhere (napkin bits, straw wrappers,  cup stoppers).  I didn't even want to touch anything on the condiment stand.  Definitely NOT going back again.

This Starbucks, is just like any other, except-the staff.  The staff here is excellent.  That is why they get three stars.  The location is small, the seating is mostly for one or two people and there are just one or two places that will accommodate a small group.  The location is not easy to get in and out of.  People driving down Wetmore are evil and never let you out and you are in a parking lot filled with Walmart, Taco Bell and Best buy customers--so it is always busy.  At night this area is slightly sketchy, especially bc of the Wally world customers. It attracts an interesting breed, especially at this location.  Don't get me wrong, I frequent this Walmart, but the peeps are seriously, interesting to say the least.  So beware. Oh and it gets busy in the am, slightly less than River and Stone, but there is no drive through here to speed things up.
Purchased a iced coffee waited 25 minutes, and they didnt even had what i asked for try to give me something else, so i asked for my money back this is on a tuesday at 4pm , really?

I try to avoid coming here as much as possible because it always feels so frantic for no reason. The one in the cherry Hill Mall faces a lot more crazy circumstances in a much more confined space and they still manage to function without making the guests feel just as anxious. I've had my order forgotten, once had an employee ignore me as I waited in line and proceeded to ring up another employee who cut in front of me to purchase her lunch instead, and have experienced ridiculous wait times during slow hours. The upside is that it's all made well and there's two guys that work there who actually have an understanding of good customer service.

I walked in and purchased a drink after a pretty rough morning... I wanted some time to cool down and gather my thoughts. There were about 8 people behind me who also ordered their drinks. I stood there... all 8 people received their drink, except for me. I looked at the back counter and there was my half made drink just sitting there. I proceeded to let the barista know. She grabbed the drink without a word and put it in 'line' to be finished alongside other remaining drinks that were being made. I waited another few minutes for ice to be added to the cup, along with a lid. No apology... Nice.

I've noticed I made an unpublished draft about same issue so I'll finish with this.I will say the line if it's at a minimum is sort of speedy. Unfortunately I think if it's just a basic coffee or anything without certain effort is the ideal. I know it is very trying when ordering the matcha green tea. I am very simple, coco milk, light ice.i literally mentioned 4 times Ice green tea latte, light ice and coconut milk. I saw hot cup, mentioned again, I didn't notice any writing but gtl. (Keep in mind my flight has been delayed 3 hrs. )I saw them ring up for gingerbread tea,mentioned what I had. Saw the lady at the register walk to prepare the drink(wash hands nooo). She turned into barista and asked "how you do this"?,"do you add water".my mouth opened to say I want a refund  because I'm not a mean or spiteful person but I've literally sat in the airport with a mechanical and the only thing I was waiting for was this drink. Low and behold one of the other baristas said she'll do it. She started to put milk,I said two more times because when the other mentioned water I said I never had water in it, iced green tea latter coconut milk and light ice. So I told the new barista, coconut milk and light ice please, thank you so much. So she got the coconut milk then I saw the scooper of alllll Ice in this tiny cup. I wish I could've took a photo of how much was left over because it was so much Ice. The drink was so dark you could literally make another with it.  Some can balance and some can't. I didn't say anything. They dropped the drink off and I just snapped a pic of my disgust.  However I remembered this was an issue before. So my mental note of the Starbucks at c17 is to only ask for water, coffe, purchase a snack or gift. Do not repeat do not order anything that will require some do more. What's so surprising that it was 4 individuals there. Why did the individual at the register attempt to make a drink. Plus I did not see a wash of hands.
It's my neighborhood Starbucks- they know my name, they know my drinks, they have a fireplace and great music.  What else could I possibly want?

Yes, this Starbucks is really busy and parking can be annoying in Chestnut Hill but I love this place. Why, you ask do I love this location of a corporate coffee chain? The Clover Machine! For 50 cents more you can get your drink made on the most amazing coffee invention ever. It's this neat little machine that is like a reverse french press. The drinks are made one by one and the beans ground right before they make it. You also can pick your roast when you go with the Clover. I love it because it's personalized, the staff is really friendly, and Clover makes coffee how it should taste - lovingly handmade. Besides, it's also really fun to watch the little cake of espresso rise from the machine.   All hail Chestnut Hill Starbucks for having a machine for true coffee nerds!
Favorite neighborhood Starbucks! Even with their rude hair salon neighbors it still keeps serving great coffee.

9people Bad server Dirty  So disappointing this location.  I was there a couple nights ago around 9:30pm.  Not too many customers but about 10 employees!  I was in a good mood when I arrived because a few were dancing in the customer area (I thought it was funny).  But, then I had to virtually yell to get someone's attention to take my order.  Plus, later we sat in the customer area & the place was a mess.  One person came by, said hi, barely wiped one table & left the rest of the place a mess.  So so disappointing because this is a great location.  And no it was NOT busy.  I will not be back.

Hey Yelp. When the owner of this business complains that I've left a review for the wrong location, don't email me - let me be very clear. This is the correct location. It's by the aloft hotel, which is 558 Fellowship Road. The OTHER Starbucks location is off of Rt 73.  That I start my review like this should tell you something. This location has attitude, and not in a good way. I spend a week a month in Mount Laurel, and this is the only drive thru Starbucks nearby. So I go here. I try to have patience; it's a busy location, it's a crappy location in the parking lot - like other reviewers have noted, the angle at which you enter the short drive thru line cuts into the exit for the very small parking lot (which forces us into the drive thru line in the first place) - and each morning, there's inevitably someone blaring their horn at some inconsiderate behaviour due to the Starbucks location. In any event. Let's get into the Starbucks location itself.  I come to Starbucks usually twice a day. That's 10 times in a week, one a month, for the last 6 months. I've probably had good service - not great, but good - twice. Seriously. And it's not like my bar for good service is high... You know, I'd LIKE to have a "hello" or even a "hello, how are you today? How many I help you?" or hell, even a "How may I help you today?" and then of course, as you're taking my hard earned dollars out of my hand, a "thank you", or a "thank you, that'll be $7.80". That's what I'd LIKE to hear. But customer service has no typical shred of manners in the US it seems, and mostly, I just expect a somewhat pleasant "what do you want", and "$7.80" when I reach the microphone box or window. This location though, has several times had me calm my inner child and put aside notions of parking my car and walking inside to slap the lady who was so incredibly rude to me, or worse, wait for her to get off of work. Of course, we would never SAY these things out loud or do them (I'd hope none of us would!) - but I've had such crappy service at this location that these thoughts crossed my mind. Seriously. As of December 2015, there are 2 younger girls working the line who are just incredibly out of line. "What do you want? Oh, you want it DOUBLE TOASTED? That'll be a 10 minute wait. Oh it's not right? You must not have told us you wanted it double toasted." WTF? It's at the point where I don't leave the drive thru line anymore until I check my food order or sample my beverage - because it's often just not right. I dunno about you all, but I don't want to pay $5.95 for a double smoked bacon breakfast sandwich that has what appears to be raw, but warm, but raw bacon on it. When I order a non fat latte, give me non fat. This location seems to go out of their way to use WHOLE milk in non fat orders. When I order a gingerbread latte, and Starbucks is running the sticker program through Dec 24, give me the damn sticker.
