I don't want to have to ask for it, but worse, when I ask, you give me attitude and grief because you have to stop and get it. Seriously? It always leaves me baffled when Starbucks employees are miffed that they didn't get a tip. I'm sorry. I ran a coffee shop. I WORKED for my tips. I gave good SERVICE for my tips. If I had a crappy day, I had crappy tips - and as with most things in life, you reap what you sow. I'm sorry, but I won't tip you for being rude and sometimes disrespectful to me. You don't know me. What makes you think you're entitled to my cash?  And so, my New Years Resolution is probably one you saw coming a mile away. Screw this location. I think the best way to let THIS business know there's a problem is to stop giving them my business, and stop taking their crap. Drive down to the other Starbucks. it's 5 minutes, which you'll gladly trade off in the wait in the parking lot or drive thru line anyhow - there'll be more locals, which is always nice (I love when you walk into a Starbucks and the staff say "Hi Bob! The usual?") and you'll go along your merry way.

Closest one to where I live. I need one closer. Worth the 10-15 minute drive. $5 is a bit steep for a venti frapp. The baristas got my name right, I wanted it wrong. In and out in 5 minutes. Next time I am ordering a doubleshot because I need caffeine.

I want this Starbucks to be great because of its convenient location, but it's only okay! Plenty of outlets, but bring a jacket because it will be chilly.  This is a rather large Starbucks, and definitely one that a lot of people frequent when they're in the Camino Real area shopping. There are several seats, including a long raw wood top table beside the windowed wall that faces Los Agaves. During peak times, there's probably only 2-3 tables open if you need to work. It's normally pretty cold in here, so bring a jacket if you plan on staying for a while.  The cleanliness of this store is always what gets me. It seems very unkempt and dirty, and that has stopped me from working here a time or two. The bathroom is always in bad shape. I'd recommend using the Camino Real public restrooms outside - they are MUCH better!  But Starbucks is still tempting. When I know I can order ahead from the app and skip a line, access an outlet and work all afternoon, have access to real food and not just pastries at a coffee shop, this is a great option. It's in a great spot, making grocery shopping before or after i work a lot easier. Even still, I much prefer other Starbucks locations compared to this one.

Most of my visits here have been positive however I wanted to mention that last time I got a refresher it was not completely filled to the top. I feel like if I am going to pay $4+ for a drink that it should be filled up all the way. I have had this issues at other locations as well. Hopefully this is something that can be fixed :)

5 stars for this location! They are always friendly and prompt. Today I put in an online order and came and waited and waited for it. Finally I asked, and it turns out, I sent my order to another store! OMG, so dumb of me, but this store made my order for me and called the other store to cancel. Talk about customer service! This made my day. Thank you Starbucks on Michigan Road!

I was in a hurry, I got in line, placed my order. Then waited. The girls served lattes and everything else to the people behind me before even making my drink! She said it was because they're drinks did not require espresso. If I'm not mistaken lattes most certainly do require espresso!! Other Starbucks won't even serve a premade tea before the next drink!! So basically I stood in line for my turn and got cut by five people!!!!
This is the best run starbucks I've ever seen. FAST employees who are also very friendly. Multitasking without seeming frantic. High quality products, clean environment.

This is seriously the slowest Starbucks I've ever been to in my life. I went twice, during a stay at a local hotel, and ended up waiting 20 minutes the first time (after ordering!) and then 32 minutes (timed it) the second time... when I got to the window, I looked around hoping to see someone milking a cow in the corner but was unfortunately disappointed each time :(

The pricing is what you would expect at the airport. Not ever coming to this crappy Starbucks again, because if you have to get hot water you have to purchase something. Never mind the fact that I purchased something from the stinking place this morning.  Avoid at all cost.
Like any other Starbucks, but usually packed with students studying so I can rarely find a seat. Quality is on par, baristas are friendly, and quick service.

Not my usual Starbucks but the service was fast and friendly.  This location has lots of outside seating but is also surprisingly large inside as well.  It was a busy Saturday afternoon when we visited, but our drinks were up quickly and we were off to our spa treatments!

Just had an irritating experience here. Got my food, everything was fine-ish. But... this is a location you go to out of necessity, not excitement. Employees persistently called the wrong name for my order (not a close wrong name, just the wrong name) and then vibed me for not responding to.... not my name.
Went through drive yesterday! Service was perfect! Thank you. Very upbeat and order was perfect.

Starbucks is Starbucks. Love them or hate them. Either way I'm only going to provide my view on the specific location rather than their menu selection because, well let's face it... it's Starbucks.   The baristas do seen to have a high turnover here. Can't exactly speak to what that means here but, if you're lucky enough to get a senior one, they'll hook you up. One time I forgot to ask for my extra shot in my grande skinny and I told them to hold off on brewing so I could get back in line. He said that was blasphemy and dumped a free one in there for me.   So the parking lot, yeah... Usually filled to the max (and then some) with SUVs of the luxury brands. One time, I literally saw two Bentleys parked next to two Tesslas in between a few brand new LandRovers.  It's funny that the rich ones always get the spots, eh? My Honda and I parked across the street and defaced Lancaster Ave-road kill-death to get in there.  I also literally saw a fist fight between a BMW business guy and an angry Irish Mercedes guy over that damn parking lot. Thankfully, buff Infinity guy acted like the bouncer and broke it up. NO JOKE. I can't make this stuff up.   At the end of the day this is an in-and-out (of you ever get it) Starbucks. Not the kind for your laptop or leisure.
The slowest Starbucks. Ever. They're probably still making my coffee. And it was literally an iced coffee. You just add ice.

The worst Starbucks in town!!! Their drive-thru wait time is horrendous. The line didn't move an inch in 5 minutes. I was in the drive for a total of 13 minutes! On avg., the wait time is 10 minutes. The orders are never correct and inconsistent. I ask for lite-ice, I get an iceberg!!! I go at least 4x/week on my way to work, hoping things would change. I even tried going inside, it's even worse! People were waiting for their order, not realizing it was ready because their names were never called or written on the cups. The orders were only labeled with a printout. This location will not be getting another cent from me! This place should be embarassed!!!

They're awesome. Except one worker, he had a name tag that said Jesus. He was very condescending and rude. Sometimes they take five years but probably because some customers order like 5,000 Frappuccino's.

Wow...definitely surprised this spot doesn't have a better rating.  I affectionately refer to this Starbucks as "MY Starbucks" (a la Braveheart with the crazy Irishman..."What do you mean, 'your island?" - "Yes...Ireland...IT'S MINE." The one at the Bellevue is closer but the line is forever and my order is always wrong.  The one at 16th and Walnut has the rudest staff ever.  But this one...friendliest crew no matter what...my drink takes a little long?  I don't even say anything and the manager hands me a coupon for a free drink at my next visit.  Can't give it five stars purely for that reason...sometimes you're standing there in an empty store and your iced coffee takes a strangely long amount of time.  And I miss Cafe Hausbrandt :(.

Understaffed for a hotel this size. Apparently my americano with 4 stevias, two pumps of vanilla, and cream is a "really complicated drink" and requires an apology to the person behind me. Good thing it's just coffee and not something that is actually important and complicated. At least the nailed my drink and by nailed it I mean just gave me blonde roast coffee instead of an americano. It's pretty easy to tell when you don't hear the espresso machine and there is no resin at the bottom of your cup.

I had started this review differently and was planning to go on a rant about slow service and how I've been to other starbucks and blah blah blah.  This Starbucks is big, it's clean and the employees are nice.  They have a drive through which seems silly to me but I know people that like to use it.  All I know is that when I stop in there, it seems like the employees move at glacial pace.  If you're stopping here, plan to slow down a little (or a lot, depending on how fast you normally go)  Other than that, it's pretty nice and well, it's close to my office.
Best Starbucks in Reno. Worst parking lot. Friendly, efficient baristas. Always clean lobby and restrooms. I'm a regular and  love Valerie, Ayla and Cody.
I'd take away a star for the terribly planned parking lot and drive-thru, but it's got plenty of seating and a very friendly staff. Definitely one of the more enjoyable locations around.

I've been coming to this starbucks almost religiously since the start of the year at the same time everyday, 10 o'clock. Everything has been going smoothly until recently. There is a barista with glasses, male, short, dark hair who makes the drinks. He's been filling my grande coffee only halfway for the past 4 times I've been there, today being the absolute lowest amount I have ever had. I do not pay $5 for a grande cup of coffee only to get it half way filled. Idk who he is trying to play but he is so lucky that I was rushing to get to school this morning or he would have had an ear full.   I will make sure to check every single cup I get from this starbucks to make sure they are giving me my moneys worth in coffee. I will also be speaking to the manager to make sure that all the barista understand what a grande cup of coffee looks like.

Let me start off by saying I'm so bummed that my experience was bad here because I was really excited to have a Starbucks close to where I live. My fiancé and I went to get coffee this morning in the drive through. There was only 3 cars in front of us and we were waiting for 15 minutes! I've never experienced something like this at a Starbucks. We waited a couple minutes longer since there was only 1 car in front of us and decided to leave and get dunkin coffee. At least they were fast and we got our caffeine fix! I know you're a new Starbucks, but come on...step your game up baristas!

Disappointed when I got my vanilla bean frap, I asked for extra Carmel and I didn't get one drop of Carmel. The person in drive thru really needs to listen to the customers order. And they didn't have enough green tea!

April 20, 2013 Saturday - pre-breakfast 9:30 AM 2 adults  Atmosphere - 5 stars Food - 5 stars Service - 5 stars  Background - Planning a big breakfast but LabCorp was swamped and we had an appointment in 30 minutes so a quick fix is needed.  Atmosphere - A clean small location and very long. But this made moving around in the place easy.  Lots of tables outside and we sat and drank for a little bit before we had to go.  Food   The wife and I went with just a cup of frou-frou with a slice of pumpkin bread.  I had a Caramel Macchiato as usual it was really good and what I except from a Starbucks.  The pumpkin bread was so moist.  The unique thing about theirs was the pumpkin seeds baked on the top crust to give it that nice crunch.  Service - Small line inside but gone almost as fast as I got it in.  They had two cashiers working this location and before I knew it I had my drink.   Future - I will as I got a lot of gift cards for Christmas still.
only reason i didn't give 1 star is because the young man at the front was so kind to us! and our chocolate croissants were great. I simply ordered a matcha latte and it tasted awful :( sad

The absolute slowest drive-thru service one can find at any Starbucks. I've spent over ten minutes waiting at the window at least three times now. I would be understanding if they said something like our computer is operating slowly, but instead I'm left sitting and waiting without any heads up as to what would be slowing down the process.   The store is clean, well kept and a great place to work from if you can work remote. So two stars for that, but three down because of the drive-thru service is so bad that I don't believe Starbucks as a company would be proud of it.  (Note that I'm not saying time in line waiting, but time spent literally just sitting at the window)
Parking is impossible. Not sure why i even pull into the lot when its backed up on the main road, which is a little dangerous.

Like any other Starbucks - good service, friendly baristas, and aromatic coffee.   What makes this Starbucks stand head and shoulders above others is the "European Bakery" next door - omg the sausage biscuit sandwich, ham and cheese croissant, and cinnamon roll were to die for.   We'll be back tomorrow for both the European Bakery and for the Starbucks coffee.
Love Starbucks! Venti passion tea lemonade with raspberry.....so so good! The baristas here are super nice & know what they're doing.
Great location! Close by to hotels and a huge plus for out of town visitors. Great service, friendly staff.
Great Service and awesome Coffees!! Love to share our Christmas Spirit! Nothing better than Creme Brulée Frappuccino!!

The service here is so nice! The male barista was so cheery and happy, it made the short experience much better. The coffee was made great and I would definitely come back this one if I was in the area. Much better than the Target Starbucks.
Slow service. Be prepared to wait 30 minutes when line is 10+. Absolutely ridiculous. Employees need training with registers and a kick of urgency.

I'd give ZERO stars if I could. The customer service is absolutely HORRIBLE, the "barista" has the worst disrespectful attitude, I'm disgusted Starbucks would even employee someone with such poor etiquette and is far from articulate. The sanitation is absolutely horrible here, the first time I came here I asked if they had sandwiches and one employed rudely said to me "If you want a sandwich you need to go somewhere else we don't have them here." Later to find out they do in fact have breakfast sandwiches which is what I intended on purchasing. My second time coming here the employed were speaking very LOUDLY about how all the food in the casino INCLUDING Starbucks looked disgusting and looked roach infested, I was so grossed out I couldn't believe they were slandering the casinos name along with their employers name. The THIRD time my husband ordered us strawberry refreshers and the "barista" filled the venti cup to the very top with ice, my husband took 3 sips and the drink was gone because there was so much ice, when he politely asked the barista what the issue was, she again rudely stated "That's standard to fill the cup to the top with ice if you didn't want it you should have said light ice." WOW I cannot believe Starbucks allows their employees to speak to customers like this. Especially a gold card holder since 2012 who regularly purchases bulk orders of drinks for her office on top of being cooperate account members at the Nugget Casino I am absolutely appalled at the service, I will be no longer be spending such large amounts of money at Starbucks and will be taking my business elsewhere!

I've been going to this particular location for years. The staff is great. They are very friendly. I once forgot my card and the barista let me take my drink and pay later. So nice of him. I haven't been disappointed going here yet!
This is one of the worst Starbucks in the area. They are consistently slow and regularly screw up your order. Avoid.

This is the newest SB in NWReno, and it has all the things you might expect... and that fancy CLOVER machine. https://www.starbucks.com/coffee/learn/clover  I may not often get a cup from the Clover, but DAMN it makes the place smell better than most Starbucks.  --- Also, very kid friendly, and the crew is nice, even when I show up to meet with my boss, and not my cute kids.

Starbucks in the Cherry Hill mall is very hit or miss. They're either empty or the line is so long it extends out of the entrance to the Macy's it's located in. Due to the stand's small size, there are probably 3-4 employees working at the maximum. But even with 2 on the register and 2 behind the counter it's not enough to accommodate the business of this location. Also, the space where customers wait to order and wait to pick up their order is cramped. It's not uncommon for people to resort to standing outside of the Macy's and try to listen carefully if their name is called, rather than elbowing strangers in the ribcages just to wait for a frappuccino. The drink taste like any other Starbucks. The baked goods are usually fully stacked and the employees are very helpful and nice. I give them props for being able to serve their coffee with a smile given the factors that make this location inefficient. I believe the mall would benefit greatly if they simply opened up a large independent Starbucks store within the mall.

This Starbucks- inside Frys- is the best Starbucks on the planet! The ladies are beyond awesome! Even when dealing with the jerks they maintain their professional demeanor and do their job! Jada and Bre are the best!

This is a great Starbucks --always clean-always organized and a great staff--mainly Kristina who is speedy and makes my drinks perfectly always!  I like that they always have community newspapers available for everyone to read but I don't like the cop who comes in regularly and always take the whole freaking pile of COMMUNITY newspaper and takes them home !!! Hello! The papers are meant to be read there and recycled for others-- not taken with you!  This place is better than the coffee shop up the hill where the staff talks way too loud!
Simply my favourite branch in Chester County. I was a regular customer before the revamp in 2013, but the staff is ever-so friendly.
Barista's are very friendly and knowledgeable about deals and promotions. They also gave great suggestions for new things to try. Very quick service and plenty of room to sit/stand/wait.

After landing in RNO, I had mapped this location as my first stop before heading eastbound on I-80 to Winnemucca.  It's a friendly place.  This is one of those Starbucks that asks your name so they can call it out when your drink is up.  Never give your real name, always make one up like "Godzilla" or something.

No matter if there is 1 person in line or 10, always a long long wait. There are normally 6 employees working but nothing is ever getting done. Today I ordered a iced decaf americano and 6 people behind me got drinks before mine was ready, really? This Starbucks has become my least favorite and I won't be headed back to this location. Tired of the lazy workers.
Can't even get a simple order right. Visited the place twice and both times they messed up my order.

If you've ever been to any Starbucks on earth then you know what to expect here. The coffee is consistent and satisfying (except that one bad mocha I got at the Starbucks in LaGuardia, but I don't like to talk about that...)   The great thing about this location is the customer service, especially in the drive-thru. They're always so friendly it leaves me wondering if the constant smell of coffee and car fumes combine to make some kind of mood enhancer.   I don't branch out much beyond my tall mocha (Ok, fine. Venti.) and the seasonal salted caramel latte, but they're always good enough to make me want to come back for more.

Lack of  basics including heating (it's cold through out even with several layers on and a down coat; even worse is the doors were swinging open or are left open so any one standing in line or sitting nearby really freezes).  Also several times I received the wrong order so staffing training needs are an issue. The customer counter (napkins, spices, etc) was often not refilled. Staff was very friendly though. We never plan to come here again. More locally owned coffee shops nearby are definitely a better use of time, value and money.

Smallish Starbucks located on a corner street in Old City. It's right down the way from the neighborhood UPS store. They take Starbucks cards here and have the full menu so come get your favorite coffee flavored treats! Quick in and out, but few seating areas here.
I enjoy the 24-hours (drive-thru) and late hours (indoor). Free WiFi... Outside and indoor seating is available.

The worst customer service ever. Waited while people who were behind me, got their drinks. Saw 5 orders made that were wrong and had to be redone. One three times!   Starbucks area manager - you had best get over here and do some training.  Unorganized.. unmotivated. Unhappy...

Yelp keeps suggesting that I review Starbucks, so here goes...  It's Starbucks...there's nothing that I can say that hasn't been said already.  Except for maybe this:  I despise coffee.   As my Mom used to say, it's an insult to the palate and an abomination to the stomach.    So, much to my surprise, they actually have a pretty decent tea selection.  Not that I'm an expert...I recently had to take up tea drinking after voluntarily giving up Mountain Dew after a 20 year bender.     Every once in a while, I have a friend who wants to meet at Starbucks and I grit my teeth and acquiesce.  I usually order the plain black tea.  And, to my astonishment, it's pretty freakin' good!  Better than the Lipton that I get out of the Keurig, which isn't half bad either.  The other day, I decided to live on the edge and order the Chai tea, black.  Holy s***...what have I been missing? Luckily the Starbucks Chai tea K-cups are available on Amazon for a reasonable price.   Back to Starbucks at the Park Place Mall...the employees are super friendly.  I'm literally the last person on the bandwagon, I know, but they really seem to go above and beyond.  So yeah, go there...like you don't already know.

Traveling to see my husband who started a new position locally, I found myself at this Starbucks.  I currently have a special mug that allows for free coffee for the month and upon presenting this mug, the barista at the register offered a new type of brewing called Clover.  I agreed to try it.  She was very informative and helpful.  A true asset.  Then while seated at the bar awaiting my new drink, another Barista, Royce, gave me some interesting insight into Starbucks,blends and home brew.  He was funny and very helpful.  Even during an obviously busy time, he made my visit quite enjoyable.  The icing on the cake, the coffee was amazing!  Well done!

It's a Starbucks so free wifi, friendly service, great location that is easy to find.  Parking is a breeze.  I usually go for the mocha frappucino and ask for whipped cream. And I stubbornly ask for a Medium instead of grande or whatever StarbuckSpeak is for a medium cup. I'M SO CRAZY REBELLIOUS.

All downtown coffeeshops were closed, so went here. Got the nitro cold brew. No ice, slightly below room temperature, and zero "nitro". Tasted like very average coffee forgotten on the counter. Wife got an iced peppermint mocha that was 90% ice and tasted terrible. Both were smalls, and bill was over $10. What a joke.
This is a great Starbucks! Large inside area and large area of outdoor seating with an umbrella. There is also a convenient drive thru and lots of parking.  The staff is friendly too.
Needs more staff but otherwise handles the customer flow very well. Very small store so it gets crowded. Would opt for Dunkin' Donuts if you don't want to deal with the line (I did)

Terrible experience at this location.  They gave away my sandwich to another customer and after informing them that I had not received my sandwich 10 minutes after getting my coffee, they showed no urgency and took another 15 minutes to put my sandwich into the heat....

Great place to take a break.  Not too fond of the outdoor seating since it's right on the corner of the parking lot where cars come and go.  I stop here on my weekly shopping trips because it's convenient for me.  Still can't get use to the coed bathrooms.  The servers are sweet and friendly here

I frequent this Starbucks on a weekly basis. At first, the customer service was okay. There used to be several men in the morning who were friendly and helpful. Now its over ran with a bunch of bitches. Sorry for the language but literally that is all that works there. They are not friendly, not attentive to the orders, don't smile, don't say thank you or have a nice day. Just a bunch of bitches. If you hate your job so much, find a new one. But with the amount of money I spend at Starbucks, the least you can do is give me my drink with a damn smile. I continue to go here ONLY because of its location to my work and that it has a drive thru. But seriously, whoever is managing this store needs to get its employees in line. This is NOT how Starbucks  patrons are supposed to be treated.
Not the Starbucks quality I have become used to. My cup got pushed to the back of the line. At least 10 peoples drinks were made before mine that were behind me in line.

Rudest customer service ever. As a frequent customer of all starbucks, I have never been so insulted. A polite query into asking when they open is retorted with a snappy "we open when we open.". Real classy guy.   Wow. Just the customer service I expected at 6am.

Super Crowded and busy.   This location needs to hire more starbucks employees so that they can be more on top of the game. I feel as if the lines were too long and short staffed.   Cleanliness was a-okay. Could be better.   Everyone was cranky and employees were making mistakes since there was too many orders and i don't blame them. The lines were incredibly long and people are cranky for waiting.

Minus 1 star for not giving me a paper protector for my cup. Seriously? You hand me a cup, in the drive thru, over my head, with no "thingy"?  Not sure if it's an Idaho thing to save the environment, but really...    Drive thru wasn't bad, staff was friendly, drink was as expected and it is around the corner from the gym I go to in the morning. I'll be here more often!

My favorite Starbucks in the tri-state area, Don't know why it took me so long to write this review but here I am. All of the service and staff here are amazing including but not limited too; LJ, Kevin, Kyle & Zac. One of the few Starbucks that actually knows how to make my drink perfect every time (Berry hibiscus with lime instead of water & light ice), I go past other Starbucks and go out of my way just to go to this Starbucks everyday and will continue to keep coming back.

I know...high price.but it's worth it hehe..coffee at a top eatery is far more costly and rarely more savory...and really....what is money...when you get free friendly atmosphere, manager, workers, etc.  Yes it's nice espresso/whatever/or mocha and sometimes a nice healthy snack..or a serious brownie hehe lucious for me, and I go to the outside seating and do art work, and share with surrounding peeps and workers for free...its, cool and refreshing for me...

I love this Starbucks!  At my work, Friday morning starts off with a mandatory Starbucks run for front staff employees and I'm always the one willing to take on this task. Usually, I place 6-8 separate orders using each of my co-workers debit cards or cash. However, last Friday one of the doctors made a generous announcement that he would pay for everyone's Starbucks. A list of 30 something different drinks was made and I was off to Starbucks. I handed the gal at the register the list and told her she'd probably need backup. I'm honestly quite surprised that NONE of the drinks came out wrong, everything was perfect!  The employees are always on top of it with putting stars on each of the drinks I have ordered. That way I'm not standing at the counter forcing myself to memorize which coworker of mine ordered what specific drink. We all know at least one person who gets carried away with their upside down caramel macchiato, extra pump of syrup, nonfat milk with one add shot. Lets face it, it also decreases the odds of me stealing another person's drink.  The service is fast and friendly to say the least... I just wish they had a drive-thru!

I've been waiting years for this location to open since it is super close to my job. Now, it's here! The good, it has a drive thru for all those convenience needs. The bad, the inside area is kind of odd to sit and relax in. The drinks are standard, staff seem to be learning all the drink querks, and the Peking is easy. This is a standard Starbucks and I only wish it had better seating options.

We love this Starbucks and come here every Saturday morning. However the recent renovation has replaced the comfy chairs we loved with tall rickety tables. The only way we'll be able to continue coming here is if we can get a shaded table outside. Too bad--we loved this place. I didn't see any other regulars there yesterday. Fortunately food and service is still good.
Place seems grimy for s Sunday afternoon. Not sure if it's the place or people. Lots of young girls using internet but not buying anything.

I travel for work and hit a bunch of Starbucks all around the Philly area. Sure I have my favorites, but most fall into a "pretty good" service bucket. This one in Lionville is consistently worse than others in the chain. They have a lot of regulars. While that in and of itself is not a bad thing, when it detracts from other customers it is. The last 3 times I've been in this location I have been delayed while the cashier or barista have extended conversations with regular customers.

Would it be hyperbole to say this is the worst Starbucks in America? Dated, sterile interior? Check. Unfriendly employees? Check. Abbreviated operating hours? Check. They couldn't even take a mobile payment or give the points. Come on, Starbucks.

This is not the first time I've been to this Starbucks location. This visit unfortunately has been one of the worst with the employees attention to their customers. Mobile order was placed via Starbucks app and since the counter was busy at the time I walked in, I didn't notify the employees that I am waiting. 25 minutes past and the counter was cleared of people waiting so I asked the employee if my order is ready.  He said initially that the didn't get the order. I had to point out to him that the mobile order sticker is still at the printer and not being taken care of.  After 32 minutes of waiting I got my order and a "sorry about that".  I asked the manager to be notified that a simple mobile order more than 30 minute and no corrective action was undertaken.  The employee said "sure" and continued washing his cup in sink as if nothing happened.

Every. Single. Time I come here, the people are friendly and don't rush you to order. Grant it, I'm not taking 5 min to order, but sometimes you may want something different. They are always willing to make something for me to see how I like it. I love coming to this location!!!! I do not drink coffee, so they still give me great options!

New Orleans is not the kind of place to go to Starbucks (at least not in the French Quarter).  This Starbucks (inside The Shops at Canal Place) is testament to why.  Three different visits over five days uncovered a disorganized order-handling/drink-distribution process and some spoiled chai.  Spoiled.  As in bad.  As in fermenting.  As a Gold card-carrying Starbucks evangelist, this store left me nonplussed.  The baristas were nice enough, but I suppose it's hard for them to deal with all the personalities that come along with the tourist crowd that I'm sure they almost exclusively see.  I just didn't feel the love.  Ever.  Here's to improvement!

Was diverted here after the location on Shadeland decided that even though they are posted to open at 6:00am on Sundays, that means they have the lights off & are still prepping at 6:15...while customers are driving away, including an Indy Metro Police cruiser...that's another review.  I pulled up to the drive they, placed my order & was on my way in less than 5 min. Awesome!

The service at this location is sooo slow. The barista that took my drink order was friendly. Then I had to wait about 15 minutes for my drink.  There were 7 people working and only 1 person on bar. Whoever did deployment for that shift needs to have a rethink. One of the baristas was moving breakfast pastries from one tray to another. She could have assisted on bar, but instead the gal on bar gets a newbie to help her. She has to tell newbie everything from how to read the sticker to how to make a frappucino. Why would you put your newbie on bar during the busiest time of the day? Like every other drive thru location, the focus is on drive thru customers as opposed to walk-ins.

I live nearby so this Starbucks is very convenient to where I live.  Most of the baristas know me by name which is a nice personal touch.  I'm in every morning to get coffee, sometimes a paper, rarely something to eat other than a banana, petite scone or biscotti because I'm watching my diet, lol!    The pour-overs here, which I often get, think half caff and decaf pour overs, they're great and far superior to 16/Walnut!  Having come here for a long time, I've gotten to know various baristas and I really do miss David, he along with other baristas are often very friendly, which is so nice.  I also miss Josh who left last year!    John, the manager gave me lots of free boxes when I moved a few blocks and I really appreciated that!    I bought a few Starbucks gift cards for Christmas gifts which were real hits!    I'll be back tomorrow morning!  :-)

I've been searching for a Starbucks on Chinden Boulevard for quite a few years, always assuming I had to drive down Eagle Rd to find one. This time I decided to look on the map and to my surprise I found it hanging out on the intersection of Chinden and Eagle Rd.   This Starbucks has a drive thru that is easy in & out, particularly if you are driving east on Chinden. Super, super efficient, the longest I've had to wait is about 4 minutes on a Monday morning.  Inside, the store still has the brand new feel, with great looking furniture. We are always able to order quickly and get our drinks after a very short wait. Because I order long shots, I always expect a longer wait because a well poured long shot takes time to be perfect. Every time, my order is just right.  You shouldn't have trouble finding a seat here except on a busy morning.

Great morning crew during the week . However they are lacking customer service during the afternoon and weekend.  Today for example I order my regular iced coffee with non fat milk and she hands it to me black. I say it's suppose to have milk in it. .... instead of saying ok let me add milk she argues with me that I never said anything about milk! Mam.... come on just add the dang milk why argue  over it!?
Friendly crew, very helpful and seemed like they were glad I came to see them today.  Place was packed, but hey it's Starbucks in a Sunny Saturday afternoon.
Great service! The iced frappuccino was excellent. 10/10 would recommend this place to a friend.
This is currently my favorite Starbucks the staff is always friendly the coffee is always Starbucks but the atmosphere is always comfortable. Big thanks to Jake
The staff here at this Starbucks are extremely polite no matter how busy they get. I love my hot cafe mocha. I am very particular about it and they get it right every time!

Dude. Can you guys actually do your job. Please. I'd appreciate it. I've been sitting here for over half an hour and still no food. I even went up to the counter and one of the employees just stared at me. If only Jason was here. He does a great job.
Nicest Starbucks I've been to...and probably the only one I've seen a black guy working at. If I actually drank coffee regularly, I'd probably be here every Monday-Thursday I had class.

This  is small location but both times I have been the staff has been awesome! Always super friendly, sincere, and most importantly quick! They could have stocked the front cooler a little but I did take into account I came in towards the end of the day. The best part is it's located inside Kroger so I can have my coffee and shop too! Now we just have to get Kroger some carts with cup holders . Worth the stop.

There's almost always a long line at this starbucks and it is usually packed. Because it is so packed, it is not the cleanest or neatest. In terms of drinks, they are usually good and consistent. Service is typically friendly. Sometimes there is a bit of a long wait for your drink.

I'll just start by saying I'm a total Starbucks junkie! I'm completely hooked on the chestnut praline latte and it makes me so sad that it's only a seasonal drink lol. This has to be my favorite Starbucks in town, though. The employees are wonderful! They are always friendly, fast, and definitely care about their customers. They will do anything to accommodate people that is within their means. Example: even when my beloved chestnut praline goes out of season, they'll still make it for me as long as they have the ingredients for it. They were still making it for me months after it ended! If you want great service, you'll definitely get it here. I should also note that their line moves faster than in any other Starbucks I've been to in town. And boy can they make a great drink! The blonde barista makes a mean caramel frappuccino. Always very nice and clean in here too.

Quick service, must be the time of day usually they a little slower. Quiet atmosphere to work on my studies. The people here ( employees and customers ) are really friendly. This is becoming one of my favorite hang outs.

Staff is very friendly especially Jade... he's my favorite barista! He knows me by name and always prepares my order to perfection.  I've visited all the local SB and hands down this one is the best. Parking can be a challenge during peak times but well worth it. Thank you for the great service SB @ Bryn Mawr

The baristas and staff here are wonderful.  Always cheerful and efficient and such a nice way to start your morning. I just had a "pay if forward" moment when I forgot my wallet and a barista covered the cost of my coffee.  Sometimes it's just the little things!

I had one previous bad experience here and chose to ignore it, as I understand times are difficult during Covid.  Now, I gave them a second chance and went in with a friend and ordered two drinks, one of which was a pike place coffee with sweet cream.  Ms Shay, the barista, calls out "Monse, mone, whatever your name is" and I figured it was me, since my name is Monse. Thought that was rude, but whatever, I'm used to my name not being called out correctly. We wait a few more minutes and I go up and say "excuse me" and she ignores me. I want til she comes back again to call out another drink and I then ask her if I'm suppose to wait here for the pike place or if I should have gotten it at the register when ordered. She looks at me like I just inconvenienced her and asks me what I ordered. I told her again what I ordered and she walks away. I wasn't sure if she was grabbing my second drink but she goes and makes sure I wasn't lying by checking the computer and then proceeds to drop off a drink on the counter and call the precious persons drink still on the counter. I wait a minute and my friend and I go up and ask her if that's our drink and she says "yes" and we grab and walk away. I turn back and she rolls her eyes and nods her head.  If this girl doesn't want to work customer service, she probably shouldn't be working at a high pace company where people have the pickiest orders (even tho ours were pretty simple). Again, I understand they were busy, but that attitude and poor customer service is just not okay.
The crowd is a bit intense for my taste, and good luck finding a seat or outlet. It's best to stop in for coffee and then be on your way.

Fast, Friendly, Drive-thru, New & NITRO COFFEE!    I was very ecstatic about this new Starbucks popping up close to home. I had high expectations & was not disappointed. This location has an easy in & out access drive-thru & they're one of the few stores in all of Tucson for that damn delicious nitro coffee-For now anyway, until other stores rebuild & make room for the machine. The inside of the store is nice & clean with friendly staff. I really love their patio because it has booth-type seating with electric outlets! Perfect coffee escape for a student & anyone who truly appreciates the Starbucks experience!

My favorite STARBUCKS! Stephen and John (sadly John is no longer here, hopefully corp will see this and bring him back) are my favorite...This is only one of two locations that make my drink perfectly...everyone here is always friendly, welcoming and provides great service..special thanks to Stephen for always remembering me and my order, it's ready before I even open my mouth..Thank you all

We've been on the road for hours. We pull over to get coffee from Starbucks. The sign on the door says they are closing at 5pm due to the weather. It's now 4:40 and they locked the doors and did not allow us entry to use the restroom and told us to go thru the drive thru for coffee. Great customers service Starbucks!!!

i pulled up to starbucks this morning at 5:50 , thinking  it opens at 5 am because that's what the website says. i get there and try to order and nobody was answering. i pulled up to the window and the lady rolled her eyes and walked away then came back and held up 6 fingers on her hand then walked away again . also there was 2 cars behind us which shows that this wasn't just an error on my behalf.

I don't know why, but I usually have trouble getting my drink made correctly at most Starbucks. I was out with a friend and we decided to stop for coffee here on a whim, and I was slightly worried. But, my drink actually came out perfectly here. The staff were all very friendly, and it wasn't too busy which was nice. If I was ever in the area again, I would definitely consider stopping in here again.

Coffee is usual Starbucks quality and wifi is ok. But I give it 2 stars because it is, by far, THE coldest Starbucks ( or for that matter, any coffee shop) I've ever been too. Don't know why senior management has dropped the ball here since its been like this for years.  Perhaps a marketing ploy...buy more coffee or leave because its too cold to stay! Note: Don't bring a sweater, bring a winter jacket!

Product is always the same so no reason to comment on it....they can make my tea right,,,too bad half the time I come here I get attitude served with it too. I can do without that first thing in the morning!

My mobile order today. I ordered at 8:06 and didn't get my items until 8:18. Why?  Well they ONLY received my cheese danish order and not my drink. So I had to wait they checked and nothing then I had to go back to my car to get the phone to show the receipt and finally I got my drink.   Mobile orders are supposed to make it easier- we'll not in this case.

Went to this Starbucks location on Friday morning around 730. Both the inside and the drivethru were VERY crowded.  Even though it was very busy I still got my order in a reasonable amount of time. I ordered a London Fog tea latte and it was delicious, as always. The staff were very friendly and attentive, even with it being extremely busy.   The bad part about this location is the parking lot. It is way too small. The drivethru line wrapped all the way around the building. And they probably only have about 12-14 parking spots, which you couldn't even park in them because the drivethru line was taking up the whole lot. If you're looking for a quick stop on your morning commute to work, this probably isn't the best place to stop during the busy hours.

Food/Service: 4 stars  Location/Building: 3 stars  Not your typical Starbucks.  Perfect for those wanting to grab something and be on their way, but not ideal for those planning to work, study, or hang out with friends.  This location is drive through or walk up service only.  There is a very small outdoor seating section.  The drive through wasn't super busy when I arrived, but it was still extremely tight and congested.  I cannot imagine what it might be like during rush hour.    The service was fast, friendly, and accurate.  I had a little issue with my Starbucks card, along with a bit of confusion from the cashier with how to process the order, but it was quickly resolved.

Great place to sit or take-it-to-go coffee house in the area.  Staffed friendly and will go out of their way to remember your name and order.  Although it is small compared to some other Starbucks it is always clean and the service is fast and accurate.  Free wifi and they always have soy milk in stock which is important to my wife and me

Why you guys have to have a bad attitude? The amount of loyalty I have to Starbucks is ridiculous but I am brand loyal. This store may have been having a bad day yesterday but seriously...? Each barista was surly, curt and didn't have any people skills that the service industry expects. I can forego being accosted by a smoking homeless guy as I try and enjoy my coffee outside... but you guys have jobs. If service and being friendly isn't for you... get out. Plenty of other great Starbucks stores to choose from fortunately.

This has got to be the slowest moving Starbucks I have ever been to. Yes, it's 8am but the Starbucks I normally go to has me in and out. Don't stop unless you have at LEAST an extra 30 minutes. It took me longer than that to get through the drive through. Inside looked worse.

The location and atmosphere are fine. It's a mall location. I just feel bad for the girl Rosie. She is miserable at her job and tries to make the feeling mutual. U pick ur job love. U don't have to hate life n people don't want that vibe. #notrosie

I like Starbucks but their rewards are really painful to check. I have to push so many buttons to find out my balance every time.  Aside from that, this location is pretty great. I love going in and out but you'll never catch me sticking around. The baristas are good at making coffee and they are kind. It's definitely a place to check out.

My barista JP put too much ice in my drink. 95% ice 5% pinkity drinkity. Was disappointed in the amount of drink and the lack of berries. He also called me Amy (which was not my name on the cup) but he was nice. The other girl gave me a straw when he didn't, thanks hun! Overall it was a good experience. Thanks JP and girl with glasses!

We stopped here tonight 10/29/17 a long drive and detoured our of our way off I-70 to get a hot cup of coffee to keep us awake on our way to St Louis.   1) terribly slow service, used the drive thru, I was able to run into the restroom and return to our vehicle in the line and my husband had only advanced 1 car length, it wasn't a long line- we were 3rd in line when I left the car.   2) 3 baristas, only one was making drinks while the other two filled up straws and managed the till.  3) my husband and I ordered hot americanos with half/half, his was cold, literally they topped off the espresso with tap water, mine was scalding hot and tasted like burnt drip coffee having sat all day, my daughter ordered a black iced tea and got sweet tea.  Totally my worst Starbucks experience ever... seemed to be the service managing the store tonight.
This Starbucks has the best ambience and layout of any I've ever been to. Plus they introduced me to the Clover machine that is Starbucks reborn. I wish I had this exact same store in Long Beach

The absolute best Starbucks I've ever been to! I think I enjoy the baristas and the setting even more than the delicious coffees they make. I have many fond memories of this place from studying, enjoying conversations with friends and co-workers. I enjoy their clover micro brew very much!
Starbucks South Meadows gets an A. The staff is friendly. They have a nice drive thru and are open late. Love the chai cream frappuccino. Liquid heaven.

Consistently the best Starbucks I visit...and I'm all over the area. They always remember my name and preferences and despite being busy, I never wait long. Whenever I can, I route myself to this Starbucks for my morning (and sometimes afternoon) jolt.
The dirtiest Starbucks I've seen. Restrooms worse than a gas station restrooms. Going to find another place to get coffee  and hand sanitizer.

Best Starbucks around - the drive through is relatively quick and the staff rocks! Even though this location is super tiny and tough to find a seat in, your drink is made right and well every single time. I sadly find myself going out of my way to this Starbucks versus the other West Chester locations just so I can get a reliable caffeine fix!

It's Starbucks, ya' know? And there's nothing wrong with that.   I like this location; in the morning, the west-facing patio is out of the sun's death rays. The staff are usually caffeinated enough to be friendly. And there always seems to be nice people in this store.   It's no Exo, but it's a nice Starbucks at a nice location.
Ridiculous how they took 30 minutes for 2 coffee and it was a mobile order ... don't come here

As I've been forced to travel more, Starbucks has become my in-city lay over after I check out of my hotel and before I head to the airport.   This place is great. It's located in a(n) historic locale (on a cobblestone street(!!). I was all like is this Rosewood/Ravenswood?) The seating is ample and the bathrooms are clean. Not as cool as the concept Starbucks in Amsterdam, but up there with some of the more cozy and quaint Starbucks I have visited.

If you're in a rush don't go here. Yeah it's right by the greyhound station. It wasn't a busy afternoon and my 2 orders of white chocolate (which they made a mistake on) took 17 minutes to be exact, took forever, the two barista was actually having attitude towards each other where the girl was just throwing/dropping things around, I was watching and so tempted to take a video of that occurrence.. Never again will go here..

Cashier was very pleasant.  Smiley and very nice. The barista however was more interested in paying attention to the latest gossip from the three other baristas that were just standing around laughing it up. She set a drink on the counter where myself and 2 others were waiting for their drinks but didn't call a name or say the name of the drink, nor did the cup have any letters on it indicating what drink it was. So I asked if it was the peppermint mocha and she turned to me and quickly said "yeah I think so" (as if I'd interrupted her) and went back to laughing and gossiping. So, I asked another barista if this was the peppermint mocha and she said "I'm not sure, probably." I was completely dumb founded. Not sure I'll be returning to this particular Starbucks.

The folks behind the counter at this Starbucks do the company proud. They are friendly and efficient. If you're getting your order "to go" you'll likely be very happy with this location.  On the other hand, the decor & layout of this Starbucks is disorganized and looks worn out. The furniture seems randomly strewn about the room and there is little here that would make you want to hang out very long. And isn't that what Starbucks wants us to do? Time for a makeover here.
Worst Starbucks I have ever been to.  My daughter's coffee was so weak, it had zero coffee taste.  My husband's decaf was made with instant coffee and hot water. He saw them make it.
This Starbucks is right next to Abercrombie and Old Navy; it is not inside the mall.  There are four couch chairs.  Good luck trying to u-turn out of the parking lot!

Free wifi. Nice seating. Pretty decent baristas. The two black girls had MAJOR attitude but I love my Starbucks so I'll be back. It's definitely wayyyyyy more expensive than my Starbucks at home. I can get a venti iced chai tea latte for 4.23 at home. I got a grande here for 5.81. Crazy!!!

Unlike most, I think Starbucks is quite overrated and too expensive. When my office was nearby I went there fairly often. Now almost never and I wonder why I ever went there. The coffee always tastes burnt and ALWAYS requires "additives" to make it palatable. The pastries they sell are grossly over-priced and mediocre at best. Bread Compny or Eiensteins are always better choices.

I have been here a handful of times and 3 out of 5 drinks that I have gotten were made wrong. I say in the drive thru line and watched the lady read the label that said soy milk ,shake the container of soy and realize it was empty , grab the whole milk and pour it in and hand it to me! When I told her that it was supposed to be soy she was upset and asked me to move up and wait ! I moved up and waited and she brought my drink out to me and pushed it into my hand with so much force that the top came off and splashed on my hand and immediately turned around and walked back in! This drink was also made with whole milk ..... The day shift is horrible but the two times I have went at night have been great!!

I go here near daily so the fact that I haven't written a review shows I have, admittedly, been slacking.  Service is what I have come to expect from Starbucks located in malls. It's hit and miss and not always quality. Drinks taste different every time.   For instance? I hated the toasted graham latte based on the version I had at this location. In reality? It wasn't half bad.  This location also has the unfortunate habit of leaving pastries in the plastic bags and just inserting that into the paper bag/sleeve. This wouldn't be a problem, except when they do that you get the unfortunate experience of seeing the production dates on those pastry items. Last time (for me) the production date was from over two months ago... And no I am not joking.  What else? Some baristas are super friendly at this location - such as the girls who wear glasses... They all seem kind. Dom, Chip, and Clark are stars!  Oh and they now carry Bantam bagels (which were featured on Shark Tank) which are bite size bagels that are sort of like filled donut holes, only with bagels and cream cheese.

The problem is now, the same as it's always been, the manager Natalie.  I worked for her for about 7 months back in 2010 and the same thing I hear from customers today is the same thing as it was back then.  Profits above Care.  As long as you could sell bulk coffee to teenagers for only came in at night to steal wi-fi (yes, let the logic wash over you) you were good.   She's driven numerous good employees from the store, or cut down their hours to bare minimal (A barista frank went from 35 per week to 7)  The parking for this place will never get better because it can't get better, there is no way of expanding the lot, so you have to deal with the privileged parking mommy and daddy's BMW any way they see fit.  Brit is the only reason I'd ever walk back into that place.  One great employee though can't save the sinking ship.
It is great to have a Starbucks in the Silver Legacy.  It is the same as other Starbucks, the real advantage here is the location.
The staff is very sweet. They offered ice water while we sat and was very nice. Really welcoming aura and establishment.

No drive-thru, small location, mediocre staff! They seem less friendly than other stores not because they are snobby, they're just clueless! I guess Starbucks is becoming the job of this decade, as compared to McDonalds of the 80s lol.  It's not exactly a happy store, not the Starbucks culture!
The staff is great here - fast, pleasant and always in a good mood considering the fast pace in the mornings of caffeine deprived customers

Unfortunately, the issue of non paying customers is still ongoing. The only reason I mind is because I am a college student at the University and often go there to buy a coffee and do homework. The non paying customers take up so many seats that there is no space for others like me to sit and do work. The non paying customers take up the entire row of booths, where the outlets are. So, even if I do grab a seat to do work, it is usually at at table without any outlets for me to charge my laptop if I need to. I was also in there once when a homeless woman was shouting heinous racial slurs at a black man. Police woman came to take handle of the situation, but it is not exactly what I want to encounter when doing homework.

This is my favorite Starbucks in the Camden county/ Burlington county area. The staff is always friendly and fast, even at 5:30 am. They always get my order right and will even  throw in things like extra butter for my baked goods at no extra cost.

I dont get to go to Starbucks often, largely because it's not within my route to work.   I had a mocha latte and it was delicious. I wanted something warm and soothing.   The barista was pleasant and made my drink carefully. The atmosphere of this Starbucks was quiet. There were a few groups of people and they were not too loud.  I will return to this location.

Staff was friendly quick and knowledgeable, the place it self was very clean and well taken care of.  They had 5 people working so quick is actually an understatement and the my gf and her sister loved their complicated confusing Starbucks drinks so 5 stars will probably be forced to come here again but all and all great experience.
I work down the street.  In 4 trips, my order has either been incomplete or wrong twice.  Very disappointing service!

I've been going to this starbucks for nearly three years and 99% of my experiences are great. Being attached to a hotel means there will always be lines and the staff handle them with speed and accuracy.  I'm not sure why, but Starbucks coffee tends to always taste burnt, so I still with the various tea and non coffee drinks they have. Matcha latte, passion tea lemonade, you name it and they can whip it up. Freaky limited edition drink? Make it a venti.  Are you craving 12 cake pops and an order of egg whites? They will hook you up.  The only downside about this site is they don't accept Starbucks's rewards or special promotional deals, like happy hour, so plan accordingly.

This Starbucks is in a good location. The problem is the SLOW service! When you walk in, whether there is 1 person ahead of you in line or 10 people, you can expect it will be 15-20 minutes to grab a simple drink. I don't know if it's poor staffing or something else, but it's insane. I used to go to this Starbucks every day before work, and each time I was late for work. I finally gave up. There always looks like there's 3-4 people working in the morning but it's still so slow, much slower than any other Starbucks. There seems to be a lack of concern for customer's time--no urgency. The staff seems friendly enough to the locals that they know and like...and they ignore the others. The staff usually talks amongst themselves which is frustrating when you're waiting for a drink and employees are babbling away together. It sends and unprofessional vibe.   Also, they only have one station for cream/sugar and everything is usually empty and messy. And the setup is ridiculous--where people wait for their drinks is also where you try to fix up your drink. It's a madhouse. They need to seriously redesign that aspect.   Bottom line: for overpriced coffee, try Chestnut Hill Coffeehouse a block further up Germantown. It's quicker and the coffee is better.

3.5 stars. This is my neighborhood Starbucks. I typically grab my coffee to go. It's always busy and staff are always friendly.  I've never had an order messed up at this location, so service is consistent. They have nice big tables, which makes it easy to have a laptop and spread out.
Two of my requirements in life, ULTRA HIGH OCTANE coffee and Free WiFi. Added bonus, very cute barista and close to home!
As of Friday November 18, 2016 they will be closed temporarily for renovations (pics enclosed), you will have to visit other nearby locations.

It's a Starbuck's... nothing good or bad to say.....  It had the usual overpriced and high caloric  drinks... however good they maybe.  Not much to say here.  It's a typical college type place.  Everyone there was young and on their phone .

As is the case with most Starbucks built on pre-existing city lots, it is as if the city officials were so coffee-crazed they tossed out the normal parking and vehicle queueing requirements.  If you park in the lot, your car WILL be dinged or you WILL be trapped due to the poorly designed drive through line.  And for what?  Go inside and there's no place to sit, loud and bad acoustics make conversation impossible, and the pastries they sell are reheated from frozen. On the upside, the baristas are friendly here.

I like this location as it's not on a busy corner like most Starbucks! The space is very small so if there is a lot of orders- there is very little space to stand and wait for your order but I like that I can walk underground from my building to get here. The staff knows my order and are always very friendly! I would not recommend having a meeting or working from here- there is literally 3 chairs in the window seat! But I'm a nice day you can enjoy your coffee in the large plaza outside and soak up some sun!

This is the filthiest Starbucks I have ever been in. Gross. Gross gross.   Toilet was a filthy mess with toilet paper everywhere on the floor. The store itself needs a good scrubbing. Trash cans overflowing. Straws, paper and other questionable detritus on the floor. Sticky seats.   I get that Santa Barbara seems to have a burgeoning homeless challenge. Perhaps that's a contributing factor. I don't know.   I just know that this is a seriously gross Starbucks. There's a coffee bean and tea leaf across the street and an independent coffee house up the street. Go there instead.

Absolutely awful. Messes up my order every time I go there (especially mobile orders). I try to stay away from this particular starbucks because of their poor customer service, however they're in a convenient place which is sometimes easier than driving all the way to SR 56 and Bruce B Downs. I have a dairy intolerance and every time I have ordered coffee they have had to remake it because they ignore my requests for milk substitutes. They always seem to have new staff who are grossly untrained and somehow get upset with me when I try to correct my order.

The barristas at this location are very friendly and completely focused on making every customer feel welcomed and valued. They work hard to assure that whatever your chosen Starbucks beverage, it is served the way you like it. One of the barristas, I think his name is Allen, is especially charming and works hard to make your experience in this store delightful so that you will return often. There are countless Starbucks, but it is the staff at this one that makes it memorable.

I went there and ordered a grande iced cloud caramel macchiato. Then, the lid wasn't closed completely, so all the coffee poured into my WHITE hoodie. However, I couldn't use the bathroom at that time because of some problems. I told an employee who works there and her attitude was unacceptable. She didn't say sorry, all she said was " If u want, I can make u a new one" with a disrespectful tone of voice. In addition, she gave me a ton of napkins. Could I clean these stains only with napkins? I don't wanna think this way but were they racist me cause I'm Asian? That was my first and also last time there. What a bad experience!

My friend and I met here for coffee. The cashier was extremely rude. She was cussing as she rung up my friend and snatched my card from me when I was paying. Needless to say, she is always rude. That can't be good for business. Lets also talk about the guy who took my drink order. It seemed to me like he was hard of hearing. I said "Skinny caramel latte" maybe 5 times and he still wrote it wrong on the cup and before I was even telling him additional things for my drink and he gave it to the barista as I was telling him that I wasn't finished. I went over to the barista and told him what I wanted and he was very nice and apologized because he saw what had happened. We couldn't see a manager on duty to tell this to, so I figured a review would do.

Early morning regular customer here. Baristas are friendly and knowledgeable. Once you're a regular, they have your coffee ready for you almost before you step up to the counter. Lines back up later in the day, and it can get noisy and a bit chaotic, but early morning is nice and quiet.

I've been coming here for a week now during the Interactions 2012 conference and despite he staff being slammed, they have been incredibly welcoming and courteous while serving up my Grande morning crack with a Reduced-Fat Turkey Bacon Muffin. Seriously, bravo to anyone who has to work in the service industry during any conference, I'm its never easy to keep a smile on your face yet these folks manage to do it.

Be prepared: tiny space which leads to long lines.  Friendly and efficient staff.  There are probably better sit-down coffee places in the French Quarter, but this was near my hotel and wanted coffee to go. Still Starbucks is way better than PJs.

Good industrial vibes and generally well made coffee. Wish they poured more and sometimes gets really backed up. More of a staffing issue. But normally empty so it's expected to be harder to handle rushes .
They need more employees in the morning. It takes me at least 10 minutes to get to a barista because the line is so long.

This is unfortunately my local Starbucks. Never personable staff, typically rude, and overcharges my beverage at least twice a week. Coffee can get pricey, but the prices I'm paying are inconsistent every time. Wish the staff would be happier to be there.

Place had 3 customers in there and it still took 20 minutes to get my drink which was terrible. They had 5 people working but only 2 seemed to be doing anything. Very poorly managed location. This is not the first bad experience I've had at this location...but it will be the last as I will not be returning. There is a Dunkin donuts right down the street.

Walked in at 6:30am.  I asked for Evolution pure celery juice.  They have the brand Evolution just not the celery juice in stock.  They said Whole Foods has it.  Well, I went to 2 locations and neither had it.  I looked at Starbuck's website first to see if they carried it and they did.  As I was in here I figured I would order a coffee. I am looking at the menu and nothing looks familiar...I said where is the regular menu? They said they NO longer have it only promotional items.  Well, I don't frequent Starbuck's often and now I know why.  I ordered a caramel coffee regular.  Total $5.61.  It was a HOT coffee and they did not bother to put a sleeve on it for me.  i got so aggravated I threw it away and left!  I will NEVER go back!  Horrible service and everything in between!

I did not realize that a Starbucks could be bad. The coffee I have had at this one tasted like it was diluted. I thought the first time was a fluke and gave the place a second chance. Bad idea!  Some people (my 93-year old mother, for instance) may like their coffee this way, but this is not what I expect from a Starbucks.

One star is even too much! Not a big Starbucks fan to start, but they do have a few types I like. One is Blonde Roast, which they do not make after 3pm, they will make for you put you have to wait. Service is horrible, not properly trained, supplies (stirrers) not stocked. Line is usually too long, need more staffing. Need better management I would assume
This has become the slowest Starbucks ever. The drive through line is ridiculous and slow, inside is empty but just as slow? Now my car is blocked by the drive through line.
Although I am not a big fan of coffee, their hot chocolate & sandwiches are definitely worthwhile. The inside of the business is very well decorated, and a nice, quiet place to get work done.

Probably the worst Starbucks I've been to!  The service was horrible.  We came in and placed our order with no one in line in front of us.  We ordered 3 iced beverages, 1 latte, a donut and 1 hot food item.  The iced drinks we're ready right away (to be expected). We waited another seriously 5 minutes for the latte.  Both lattes were terrible.  They were weak.  The hot one totally watered down.  The 1/2 vanilla almond milk latte was also weak with zero flavor.  After waiting 15 more minutes and witnessing everyone in line behind us getting their hot food, I approached the woman working at the oven and politely asked her if there she had my egg bites because I thought they might have forgotten the order.  The manager immediately butt in and said in a snappy voice that it was coming and they had a a lot of food orders.  I told him I'd just seen everyone who ordered behind us had their hot food already so I wanted to make sure I wasn't forgotten. He then told me it was coming.  I said ok and as I turned to walk away he started shaking his head at me in a snarky way.  My husband witnessed the head shake as well.   If you don't want to be in the food industry, don't work there.  Totally unprofessional.  I'm going to Peet's coffee!
A GREAT place to get some work done! The plugs are plentiful. It's located just off the hwy and... Most importantly... The baristas are fast and friendly.

I am giving this place a review, because I stopped by here to get some work done and had excellent service. Normally I dont rate Starbucks since I go quite often, but this time they deserve it. They were extremely busy and a woman in a camo hat began to berate and tell the barista behind the counter how to do her job. The barista apologized and seemed confused as to what she did wrong, since she did nothing. Instead the woman made a huge scene and said "well I am going to the other starbucks down the street" and complained to another barista about it loudly. So I am making sure to give 5 star reviews on both yelp and google as the lady promised to give them bad reviews, and both my original drink and refill were perfect with smiles and light conversation both times.

I go to this Starbucks at least 4 times a week! They never are very happy! They are always out of drink trays and drink stoppers! Today they told me they don't get drink stoppers anymore! The coffee was running down both cups and they didn't offer to clean off! I will NOT being spending my money at this Starbucks anymore! Very rude and not fully stocks! At $4.80 a drink they need to have the supplies!
Very slow service.  Over priced.  Out of product.  No compromise.  Poor Customer Service - primarily the Manager.  Won't be back.

Great drinks and coffees made to order. Breakfast sandwiches are good too and they are made fresh everyday. Salads and lunch sandwiches are also available anytime during the day. Recommend the lemonade with raspberry it's really good.
I love the drive thru. The old Starbucks up the street was not drive thru and difficult to park.  The staff is sweet. Their faces pop up at the ordering center. And they remember you!

Came in around 4:30PM on a Sunday. Steven took my order and he did very well for a new employee. I ordered a Twix Frapp (since I have a free drink) which is a lot of syrups and stuff in it.   My drink came in about 3 minutes and it's blended nicely. They forgot the java chips but I didn't mind it.

Just came in for a non-coffee beverage...kinda felt ignored by the 3 staff people here . Ended up with a Lime Refresher. My tip to Starbucks: the "freeze dried" lime wheel garnish looks horrible. 86 it!

Very busy store in the morning.   6 team members very busy serving orders.   Bathroom was out of order which was an inconvenience.  Lot of people waiting for orders.    Batista yelling people's names and orders.   Even using the preorder app..... Had to wait a long time for drink.
I've been to many of Starbucks.  This one is great and haters gonna hate.   Keep up the good work and thank you for your friendly service!

It used to be nice to meet friends at a starbucks, sit down enjoy a coffee. Now you can't complain about the prices because all are overpriced however when the service sucks and the place smells like it has a dead body in the back that is a deal breaker.
Best starbucks ever!!! Love the partners and the drinks are of excellent quality!   Thanks for a great experience!

My drinks are always good here but everytime I come here when a specific barista is working she always has a rude attitude with me. The first time I just figured she was having a bad day, but then again today she was rude with me. I asked for a drink and offered to tell her the recipe for it (it's not on the menu but it's literally just a mix of cool lime, lemonade, and passion tea.) and she told me "we can't do special orders because we're too busy with frappuccino happy hour." I asked her "so you can't make my drink? I have the recipe." And she goes "well we're really busy so we'd have to shake it in the shaker. It won't look how you want it to." Which like? I don't care? I just wanted my drink. So I asked again and she asked another barista who told her yes they can, she just had to write down on the cup how to make it. Cool lime refresher to the first line, lemonade to the 3rd line, ice, then passion tea on top of that. It's a super easy drink. It takes like a minute tops to make. I've never had an issue with any other barista but for some reason she's just extra rude. I went again though the other day and the new barista was super nice! She was fantastic and even though my drink was written wrong so I got the wrong drink, I think it was just the way I explained it. When I mentioned it at the end to the barista who made my drink she quickly and happily remade my drink for me!

I work for Starbucks currently.. And my store usually always gets the drinks correct unless it's a simple mistake of it being unsweetened or missing a syrup ingredient but won't change the drink and we always fix the drinks on the spot if we made a mistake, even during peak hours we make it a priority. One day I bought a creme frap for my co worker who was pregnant and waiting for me at a restaurant, while I waiting on my order at Starbucks, I SPECIFICALLY ordered a venti creme frap, so NO frap roast that contain caffeine. My co worker is going into her 9th month of pregnancies, and when I received her creme frap which was suppose to be white in color but it was tan brown. I couldn't tell in the dark lighting whether it was a tinted white or a tan which shows there is frap roast in the drink. And I asked them, "are you sure this is a creme frap?" And the two baristas gave each other a look and said "yes" well turns out after 10 mins of walking back to my co worker there was frap roast in it and she asked me not to go back and make a deal out of it but it upsets me because the cup was WRITTEN correctly! But the Barista's F* up! And this is the fourth time they've done this with my orders!! I order about 3 drinks each time I go but one of the few are always wrong! I asked for caramelized honey frap! I get caramel frap! There is a huge taste difference! I understand it gets busy but I TOO am a barista for Starbucks and I work during PEAK hours and I make sure I MAKE the correct drink! They write the order correct but however makes the drink never makes it correctly! And it's not even the same person! Each time I go it's a someone different who makes it but they still screw up my order..... It's just annoying and since I understand they are in a rush I leave it be but when I asked for creme frap! I EXPECT A CREME FRAP! Anything else is okay, but when someone wants a decaf give them a decaf!!

Rude employees. This used to be a great location but now the employees treat customers as nuisances and are terrible. By far the worst customer service of any of the Starbucks in Tucson. Feels like this location belongs in an airport or LA. Sad and a disgrace to the Starbucks brand. Time to tear this location down. DOWN!

I come here some mornings to treat myself to a nice cup of caffeine and calories. The drive through line can get a little crazy (5+ cars) and inside can get a little busy (also 5+ people) but if you watch the baristas, they are working their butts off! These guys are pretty fast which makes sitting at the bar waiting for my drink that much more entertaining!  Not really a review of the coffee since it's the same everywhere, but I really like the baristas there. Super nice, humorous, and not hopped up on espresso. :)

The staff at this location ALWAYS seem to be in a hurry and angry. I have my order ready. I approach the counter and as I am reciting my order, the cashier rolls his eyes at me! This has happened with more than one cashier. Honestly, is it terribly difficult to write down venti sugar free caramel macchiato, skim milk, upside down?!   Another visit here I had just received a gift card for my birthday via Facebook and was unsure how to redeem it. I asked the cashier who again, looked irritated that I was doing anything but handing over cash. She mumbled something to a colleague about it and the other barista said it could be scanned directly from my iPhone.   The only reason I come to this location is its proximity to my apartment. Otherwise, I'd prefer to travel the extra mile to the nicer, friendlier location.   Get your act together Street Road Starbucks.

Oh, Starbucks. Tampa's embarrassing coffee culture means that Starbucks is usually the place that you have to go for coffee. I sincerely miss the quiet coffee shop atmosphere. Now you have to endure your overpriced, burnt coffee in a loud, obnoxious space. I have liked this particular, Sem Heights Starbucks, but it has become unbearable. The parking lot is poorly designed (as many drive-thru Starbucks parking lots are), and it is so loud inside. I like to read at coffee shops, but it's impossible to do here.

Seriously the best service I've ever had at Starbucks. They made my drink incorrectly by mistake, then corrected it (larger size) and gave me a free drink for the future.   Thanks for a great morning! :)

I like this Starbucks a lot! It's super cute. It has a drive thru. Inside, there are comfy chairs and tons of tables and seats and it's warm. It's a really large Starbucks with enough parking. Seriously can't think of a single thing wrong with this place.
Fast, friendly service. Decent number of tables and power outlets. No complaints here.

One year later...   So it's been almost a year... But I got sucked back into this location today out of exhaustion (and desperation)... The only good point to this Starbucks is the location... Otherwise, this store remains the crappiest in the area. Service and quality flat out sucks.
