Ok, so I kind of had a change of heart once I attempted to leave this Starbucks. I had met up with a friend of mine and it was easy to see from the road - the building, that is. You know how Carmel roads are... with their roundabouts and roundabouts and roundabouts and... you get the point. The Starbucks was right off of a roundabout - a busy one at that. I am a huge fan of any and all Starbucks products, but leaving the establishment was a bit difficult. There was no EASY way of getting back to the interstate. I literally had to stand by my car for a few seconds (staring at the roads) to figure out which way I needed to go. ha May I add, I'm a bit directionally challenged - but even so, I shouldn't have had stress leaving a STARBUCKS.   But, my white chocolate mocha was good.
Seriously the most friendly service at any Starbucks I've been too.  Everyone seems like that want to be there.. which is always so refreshing!

Completely disgusted. This was a terrible experience. Yes, all paid to the extreme lack of customer service. Seriously, if you know me, that's all I need. He literally couldn't take an order as simple as a strawberries and cream frappuchino and 2 cake pops. Then, we get to the window, no speaking and a VERY DIRTY APRON. He was a black man working the drive thru.   - That is all. ‍

So usually I LOVE this starbucks but today was NOT great experience. Today is my birthday and even though I have it in my Starbucks app, I never got an email with free drinks and when I said it to the guy working there, he acted like he didn't give a shit, had a very blank rude tone and didn't even bother to wish me a happy birthday in fact, he basically gave me an attitude over a free drink and still ended up charging me $1.18 because it would soooooooo much skin off a big corporation like Starbucks nose to give someone a whole free order on their fucking birthday. Sorry I asked for so much from you on the one day that's about me, how dare I

Had a nice time here. Came here on a Saturday morning to do some work on my laptop. The staff were nice. They were busy so it was a little loud in there but they did get my order in a timely manner.   I was not too thrilled about the seating arrangement. It felt like you didn't really have your own spot but was not a big deal. Typical Starbucks.

3.5 stars for opening at 5:30 AM.  I won't bore you with the mundane details that everyone already knows about Starbucks.  This very small store is located on the South side of Canal Place and can be accessed via Iberville Street as well as through the 1st floor of the mall.  Most people get their coffee and pastries to go at this location as there are only 4 stools and a counter if you want to sit inside.  However, there are 5 tables outside on Iberville street if you are a sidewalk cafe kind of person.  What I like about this location is that they open at 5:30 AM, which is perfect for me, as my job often has me downtown at this ridiculously early hour.  They also stay open until 7:30 PM which is also a plus for those of us stuck at work past normal business hours.  The staff is very friendly and there doesn't seem to be much employee turnover, as I recognize some familiar faces when I go there.  However, be prepared for a line that sometimes spills outside onto Iberville Street during the morning rush hour.

As a weary lonely female Traveller I came to this Starbucks to get a caffeinated drink  to last me on my drive home (about 2 more hrs) and empty my bladder cause I feel safe at Starbucks and it was is stated to close at 9:30 according to the app arrived at 9:15 and doors were already locked up and why is that. Close at your scheduled hours according to the official app.

These five stars are for the excellent staff that has been brought into this new Starbuck's location.    Starbucks, as a whole generally earns an average of 3 stars, since oftentimes the staff is not as "into" their jobs, as baristas of olden-days... LOL  Their brand quality is very good, and has remained consistent over the years, with the exception of:  blonde coffee (fail) and the flat white (what?, are you kidding? You call that coffee?)  Have I written enough words, yet, Yelp?

This is the first time I've been in the Starbucks and I've been around for a long time. I just never make it to this little stretch of West Chester Pike. But since I discovered the bagel place down the road I figured I'd stop in for coffee to go with my my bagel.  The service here is not great. They're not rude but they're not really friendly and they are kind of slow.    However I have to say that I got one of the best Starbucks cappuccino that I've had in a long time.   For that reason I gave these folks at four-star rather than a three star rating.  Parking here is definitely a lot easier than it is at the other Havertown Starbucks.  I don't think this gets as much traffic plus they share a parking lot with the pretzel company so I think that has a lot to do with it.
Just OK as starbucks go. Decent service - but I have yet to make a connection with a specific barrista.  Bathrooms are locked (you need to ask for a key) and filthy nonetheless.  Good location.

My ice green tea was a bit watered down compared to my usual Starbucks (both in Voorhees). The baristas were friendly though. The space is smaller and had less places to sit and study/work than other Starbucks as well. Can't complain too much though.

Average Starbucks. I have been through the drive thru and inside many times.  Parking lot is always full and sometimes I have to park in the neighboring hotels lot. The employees are friendly, however there are always new people here. Accuracy of the orders are sacrificed because they are always training new people. I am pretty leinient on my scoring because of that.  I am not reviewing Starbucks products on this review because i have done so in others. Their products taste the same as every other one around.

We wanted to sit outside because of the pandemic and also because the weather was gorgeous. The outdoor tables and the whole outdoor seating area is FILTHY. Garbage on the floor, and tables not wiped. I happened to glance down at the  great amount of Starbucks garbage and noticed a Starbucks receipt from two days ago. That shows you how often they clean and sweep the outdoors. Too bad. We were very disappointed. We will find somewhere else to go.
Always cheery and fast. I've never had a wrong order, it's a great way to start the day!

I dread going to this Starbucks.  I wish I could say the reason was inconsistency but they are consistently terrible.   I am not sure about their brewed coffee but their Latte's are bad.  Don't waste your money going to this location.
When my order is actually correct, it's great. 3/5 times it is right. Staff is friendly just not very attentive to orders.

Used to live here and was a regular. On a trip I stopped in for coffee and it was great but boy the service here on this day was awful! The barista was obviously having a bad day (older blond woman) and gave me bad attitude for asking for a drink carrier. She went so far as to make snide remarks to another patron regarding MY choice of sweetener: "so glad YOU don't use that stuff, it's so bad for you"! I'm surprised a scientist works part time as a barista,
Slow. These people are very laid back and slow. Also I noticed the place needs a cleaning. All they do is make coffee. What's so hard about that. Slow. SNAP to it people.

First thing let me point out that the ones on the turnpike do not open till 6am. So when I have to go on set early. I take 5 minutes to drive out of my way to stop here first thing. They are open 24 hours a day. Nice to know they are there when I need my starbucks fix.  They have a few tables outside so it is a great place for me to hang out with one of my dogs or one that is in for boarding school. Busy place so great for training purposes.    I go through the drive through at the SB in Delran a lot and they have outdoor seating too. Just not as good of set up as this one. So when it is nice out I drive a little farther to go to this one.
Nice Starbucks, but I got bit by mosquitoes when I was there, like 6 bites. I don't know if it was the weather or something, but the coffee was nice, they have comfy chairs too. Nice.
One star for being clean  One for being organized  Deduct one for slow pace  Deduct another for the apathy  Deduct another for awkward parking lot - hard to park or get in / get out when busy
Staff was rude and I felt like i was bothering them for my coffee...my coffee was yummy...and it's super busy so i don't know if I'll b back
This is the location that I come to to buy VIA Decaf, that the Longley store never seems to stock. It's the only thing that I buy here but the staff is always friendly and helpful.

This is a review for the Charbucks next to the Wyndham Garden Inn on Route 100 in Exton,  This Starbucks is walk-able from there and literally saved me throughout the weekend of a conference where I was forced to stay at the Wymdham.  I came here for breakfast - got some decent coffee and a breakfast sandwich which beat the heck out of the pre-packaged "Grab & Go" garbage that the hotel was offering.  I alsostopped in around 3 PM during a break in the conference and got a much-needed Macchiatto (sp??).  Thank you, FOURBucks.
Starbucks are generally great, this one is no different. Consistent great service and good Chai Lattes!  Their bacon gouda is the bomb also.
They always do my drink at the perfection point! Thank You!! Perfect Smores Awesome Lattes Delicious Frappuccinos  It Cant be Casuality!

Dear Park Place Starbucks, you guys rock! This is my typical Starbucks when I'm going to work or lunch. They always are quick on mobile orders and I know are typically busy with the mall traffic. Today I was getting an error message on my app. I walked up and their power was out! Apparently this happened to a portion of the mall and they were effected. However even though it was probably not the most ideal for the store and the customers, they made it work for the customers that were there with me for the time being. I appreciate that they still pushed to deliver to what they could, and made me realized that this is truly my favorite location. You guys are great and I hope to see you guys again soon!

If slowness was a virtue these guys would be kings..... Show up on Monday but be thinking about what you'll want on Tuesday because that's how long it will take to get your order. And when you do get your tasteless incorrect beverage you will wish you had gone elsewhere. If you do find yourself trapped at this hellhole try the drive through.... Seems they process 6 car orders per one counter order.... And no that doesn't mean they as quick at the drive through, they just aren't terminal like the counter is..... Avoid avoid avoid!

I'm not usually a Starbucks fan at all but, after hoofing it around the Quarter for French Quarter Fest, I decided to stop in here for an iced coffee to get my energy up. It worked like a charm and I went from dozing to having a pep in my step in no time.  The set up of this store is nice enough and they woman who made my coffee for me was good at her job, in the sense that she didn't mess up my order. Other than that, yeah it's a Starbucks.

Starbucks needs to re think their strategy with license stores. This one is owned by the hotel. They do not half to keep the same standards as real starbucks. I work at one and they don't offer employee discounts and they don't have a scanner to use the card on your phone. What is a simple transaction on corporate owned pos systems becomes a line creating disaster Did I mention the employees are rude? Stay away from any "starbucks" in hotels, hospitals, targets, ect
Starbucks is a chain sure but the coffee always tastes good. The barista spells my name right on my cup every time I come in.

This place is the embodiment of what I like to find in a good Starbucks. Good drinks, good food, fast service time is all the most appreciated. I decided to get a double smoked bacon and Gouda sandwich with an iced black tea, and within 5 minutes, they got my order out. Good food at good price, and the iced black tea met my expectation. I decided to get this drink sweetened, and my decision paid off. I drank the tea without hesitation after tasting the first sip, and sure enough will be back to get another.

This Starbucks is my 2nd least favorite (the least is on Hillsborough at I-275) because it's smaller than most and it's located in a small plaza at a very busy intersection. Most times I'm driving west on East Bay so I'd have to do a U-turn at the lights, then squeeze into one of the tight parking spaces, or drive through the small and narrow parking area to get to the back for the Drive Thru.  Inside is smaller than most, too. There are a few tables and seats, but I don't recall a community table. I believe there's just 1 restroom for male and female and the door is right across from the registers. I generally don't like to sit near the restrooms and would like to see it out of sight.  There's a small patio area in front that I rarely sit in because 1) it's next to the drive thru so there's always cars driving by, 2) the busy intersection of East Bay and Starkey isn't much to look at and 3) most times it's too hot and humid in Florida!  Most times the baristas were just okay, not super friendly or talkative. One time I asked for no ice with my iced drink but I was given ice, but most times my drinks were made perfectly.  I really only stop by this Starbucks because it's convenient; definitely not one where I'd want to hang out, simply because the parking is just too small. Sorry.
My local joint.  Great group and very happy to help and usually have my drink started before I actually order it.

We wanted an after dinner cup of Joe. Starbucks was the closest coffee shop around. We did not realize they were closing within 9 minutes! Did they have an attitude because they were cleaning up? No! They provided great customer service. They have a new fan. I'll be going there when I visit Clearwater next month. Give them a change, you'll be satisfied.
Great Starbucks location-Christine was very helpful and courteous..Great friendly staff and awesome drinks and food

Love this Starbucks! One of my fave locations... Partially cause I can walk there from my house.. Lol.  Staff is super friendly and always happy to accomadate any request. Love sitting out on the patio in the cooler air and watch traffic or chill inside on the couch with friends.  Cake pops are my new fave thing!  Def worth you checking them out!

If you're looking to get work done, do NOT come to this Starbucks. For a solid half hour they were playing extremely loud rock music (complete with a lot of yelling) that was blaring throughout the store. The music was quiet at first, and at a bearable level, and then one of the employees it turned it to the highest possible volume. I looked around at the other patrons there and everyone seemed equally as annoyed. This is probably the 3rd time the high volume of the music has made me left this store. I will not be coming back.
For such a small location, they're always prompt and friendly.  I've never had a bad drink and the pastries are great too.    Target & Coffee - all in one place - what more do you need?

This is our Favorite SBUX. A wide diversity of staff. They ALWAYS have a smile for me and my wife.  Manager's beard grows and grows... night crew are cleaning machines. Place is spotless in the morning. Always gets my drink correct but my wife changes her order often and they Always give her exactly what she orders.

Though I'm not usually a fan of the corporate coffee, I will give this place props for being one of the more cozier places to study at with a group of uni classmates on a weeknight.  They've got a lot of table space that's prime for a few hours of nose-to-the-book cramming, plus they keep this place amazingly clean.  The vibe is down-to-earth enough that you won't have to worry about gossipy Main Line socialites complaining about their latest Brazilian blowout fiasco.  Parking in the lot can be a pain, but be sure to check behind the building and on the street if it looks too full.

This starbucks is very good. No real complaints but not outstanding service. The place always has people sitting in every chair. Dont plan on staying and enjoying your cup of Joe. They have no drive thru. Kind of spoiled with my home base starbucks in bordentown. The one guy that works here looks like the actor jason sudakis

This was our first visit to this location and they were PICTURE PERFECT! It was swamped but they didn't make us feel rushed, bless the barista that asked me 'And what else?' vs the always obnoxious 'Is that all?'. Our order was relatively simple this time but all the drinks around us were pretty customized and I didn't hear one mistake. Also, by the time the rest of our order was done I needed an ice water refill and the bar barista was perfectly gracious and didn't come across inconvenienced in the slightest to handle it for me.  I'm only afraid to go back because this visit was so smooth
Each time I come in the staff is friendly and nice. The store is very clean. I like the new lay out of the store.
Loved Jeremy!! So nice loved him never fire him pls or else I will be in tears also this app is telling me need to write more which is kinda MAKUNG me mad Rn
No stars. This place is the worst. Service sucks , people suck, all around just sucks.

I love coming to study here. The drawback is that a lot of the time it is packed with Wash U students so it's hard to find a spot to sit. The greenhouse is super quaint and I love sitting in there on a winter day right next to the heater. Drinks are good, bathrooms are clean. Parking is rough in this area but you just have to put your walking shoes on. Fun people watching place
9:30 am Monday. Both condiment counters and the floor were a mess. Floor doesn't look like it's been cleaned since yesterday.  Expect more from Starbucks. Mentioned to one employee

I don't usually write reviews but this is the second weekend in a row that I have had Crappy service by Erica. I was hoping she was having a bad day last week. Guess not!  I work in the restaurant industry and if my employees had the customer service this girl does then they wouldn't be working for me! I ordered two drinks, both grande. Mine was made as a tall and was one of the worst mochas I have ever had. I asked if they had any sandwiches left and she said "I don't think so!" Ok. I guess she couldn't be bothered to look. Awful service! What happened.
This is one of the busiest Starbucks in the area. Great people, great service. Ihope the staff is being compensated, because they earn their money!

Love this Starbucks and my gold card. The free birthday, syrups, soy and drink rewards save a lot of $ in my wallet :) This drive through is fast and easy to enter and exit at the light in the morning. Only one I stop at for drive thru from Carmel to downtown.
Standard Starbucks, like McDonalds, you can expect consistent food and drink at reasonable prices. Often jammed with commuters but that is to be expected with the chain.
The people that work here need to learn how to make drinks. I've been there 3 times in one week and 2 out of the 3 times my drink has been made wrong. I feel ripped off! And I'm not going back.

I have recently been going to this Starbuck's more due to the fact all my meetings are very close to this location.  I was having a bad day and they asked how I was doing and I said terrible and they gave me a free drink.  It was really great and kind.  I went there the following day and the barista asked if I was having a better day.  It was great to know they remembered me and cared enough to ask.  I am really impressed by this location!
Very green apron!  Love the crew :)  THESE guys are the best Starbucks in the WORLD!!!

Now OPEN inside Silver Legacy on the casino floor. This Starbucks takes payment through your app phone or Gift card. It doesn't take any internet offers.   They offer the same drinks and pastries like the other Starbucks. The design is very nice with a stained glass dome.  Seating is available in front of the restaurant but still indoors.   Plus side is they are open late since it's located inside the casino. Downside they get backed up and have limited baristas since the space is quite small. This is good for to-go orders and go back to your slot machines, rather then hang around and drink/chat.

Plenty places to sit inside and outside well depends on what time you visit this Starbucks it's in a prime location for traffic being next to Panera Bread company and some other places that has heavy traffic. When I am around this area frequently I always stop in and pick up a nice White Chocolate Mocha hot with whip creme which is delicious.    Always make sure before you leave to taste your coffee just in case it doesn't taste right they will fix it for you if you just let them know if they don't they will never know it taste bad or nasty.  Trust me, I know what I am talking about I been around the states visiting different Starbucks and yes at times the Starbucks Bartistas make the drinks some what strong but they will fix it for you which is nice.
There is a reason this is one of the two locations in Philly with a CLOVER press. Nice atmosphere even when it is busy. Feels like a neighborhood hangout.

I think this is the best Starbucks in the Santa Barbara/Goleta area.  It certainly has the best atmosphere. Feels more like the Seattle area ones with some care put into the interior.  Warm and inviting.  Interesting wall art and a good vibe.  A lot of seating options. Large area outside.  Consistently pleasant service.  Very comfortable, handsome Starbucks. Open late too, which is much more urban feeling.  Great!  A lot of students study here so it can really fill up.
